Table event.fixture {
  fixture_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the fixture record. Primary key for the event.fixture data product and anchor reference for all downstream domains including ticketing, broadcast, sponsorship, and content.']
  calendar_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to event.calendar. Business justification: A fixture belongs to a specific event calendar (competitive season/programming cycle). The calendar entity is the event-domain SSOT for season structure, and fixture should reference it directly. fixt']
  capacity_config_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to venue.capacity_config. Business justification: Fixture ticketing, ADA compliance reporting, and broadcast camera position planning all require knowing the active capacity configuration for that specific fixture. Venue operations and ticketing team']
  competition_round_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to event.competition_round. Business justification: A fixture belongs to a specific competition round (e.g., Week 1, Quarter-Final, Group Stage Match 3). The fixture currently stores round_name as a denormalized STRING, which is the authoritative label']
  conference_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to league.conference. Business justification: Conference-game classification is essential for standings calculation, scheduling compliance, and broadcast rights (conference vs. non-conference games). Fixture must be tagged to a conference for int']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Fixtures incur direct operational costs (staffing, security, production) allocated to cost centers. Finance teams require fixture-to-cost-center linkage for operational cost tracking, budget variance ']
  deal_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sponsorship.deal. Business justification: Individual fixtures carry fixture-level sponsorship deals (\'Official Match Sponsor\', \'Match Presenting Sponsor\') distinct from tournament or round deals. Revenue attribution, activation scheduling, an']
  fixture_away_team_franchise_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the visiting team or secondary performing entity for this fixture. Nullable for non-competitive events such as concerts or award ceremonies.']
  fixture_broadcast_channel_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the primary broadcast network or OTT platform assigned to carry this fixture under media rights agreements. Links to broadcast domain for RSN, national network, and streaming rights activation.']
  fixture_channel_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the primary broadcast network or OTT platform assigned to carry this fixture under media rights agreements. Links to broadcast domain for RSN, national network, and streaming rights activation.']
  fixture_franchise_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the home team or primary performing entity for this fixture. Used for scheduling, standings calculation, and home/away revenue split logic in SAP S/4HANA FI/CO.']
  league_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the league or governing competition body responsible for this fixture (e.g., NFL, NBA, MLS, FIFA). Drives compliance reporting to governing bodies and broadcast rights distribution rules.']
  league_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to league.schedule. Business justification: Fixture-to-schedule mapping is fundamental for game-day operations, broadcast slot allocation, and schedule compliance reporting. League operations teams must reconcile official schedule entries again']
  media_rights_deal_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to broadcast.media_rights_deal. Business justification: Rights fulfillment tracking, PPV pricing enforcement, and blackout rule application all require knowing which media rights deal governs a specific fixture. Rights operations teams report fixture deliv']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Fixtures are the primary profit-generating events in sports entertainment. Finance teams track per-fixture P&L through profit centers for event-level profitability reporting, management accounts, and ']
  season_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the season or competition year to which this fixture belongs. Links the fixture to its governing season structure for standings, scheduling, and league governance reporting.']
  tournament_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the tournament or bracket structure this fixture belongs to, if applicable (e.g., FIFA World Cup knockout bracket, NBA Playoffs bracket). Nullable for regular season fixtures.']
  type_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to event.event_type. Business justification: Fixture currently has event_type as STRING column. The event_type reference table exists with event_type_id PK and contains code, name, description, category, format attributes. This is a classic refe']
  venue_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the primary venue where the fixture is scheduled to take place. Links to venue master data for capacity, seating inventory (Archtics), ADA compliance, OSHA safety checks, and HVAC/BMS operational readiness.']
  actual_attendance varchar [note: 'Verified count of attendees who physically entered the venue for this fixture, reconciled from gate scan data in Ticketmaster/AXS. Used for KPI reporting, revenue reconciliation, and league governance submissions.']
  actual_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The real-world date and time the fixture actually concluded. Used for broadcast rights window compliance, overtime/extra-time tracking, and venue operations post-event reconciliation.']
  actual_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The real-world date and time the fixture actually commenced. Populated post-event for reconciliation, broadcast rights compliance reporting, and performance analytics in SportsCode/Hudl.']
  announced_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the fixture was officially announced to the public. Marks the start of the fixture lifecycle and triggers fan engagement and ticketing on-sale countdown workflows in Adobe Experience Platform.']
  attendance_capacity varchar [note: 'Maximum approved attendance capacity for this specific fixture, which may differ from the venue\'s total capacity due to configuration, safety restrictions, or event-specific setup. Used for ticketing inventory management in Archtics and ADA compliance reporting.']
  away_score varchar [note: 'Final score or result for the away team or secondary competitor at the conclusion of the fixture. Populated post-event. Nullable for non-competitive events.']
  cancelled_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the fixture was officially cancelled or abandoned. Triggers refund processing workflows in Ticketmaster/AXS and revenue reversal entries in SAP S/4HANA FI.']
  confirmed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the fixture was confirmed by the governing body or event organizer, transitioning from announced to confirmed status. Triggers ticketing on-sale activation in Ticketmaster/AXS and broadcast rights activation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when the fixture record was first created in the data platform. Audit trail field for data governance, SOX (Sarbanes-Oxley Act) financial controls, and GDPR data lineage compliance.']
  doors_open_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Scheduled date and time when venue gates open to ticket holders. Critical for access control configuration in Ticketmaster/AXS gate scan systems and fan arrival experience planning.']
  event_classification varchar [note: 'Competition or programming classification of the fixture indicating its governing framework (e.g., league regular season match, cup knockout tie, friendly/exhibition, touring concert). Used for league governance reporting and CBA compliance.']
  event_date date [note: 'Calendar date of the fixture in the local venue timezone, stored as a DATE for day-level planning, fixture calendar display, and season schedule reporting. Distinct from the precise scheduled_start_timestamp used for operational systems.']
  event_tier varchar [note: 'Competitive tier or significance level of the fixture within its season or tournament structure. Determines pricing strategy, broadcast rights activation, and sponsorship inventory allocation. Drives dynamic pricing rules in Ticketmaster/AXS.']
  extra_time_played boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the fixture required extra time or overtime periods beyond regulation. Affects broadcast window compliance, venue operations scheduling, and revenue reconciliation for extended event durations.']
  fixture_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric business identifier for the fixture, used in operational systems such as Ticketmaster/AXS, Archtics, and SAP S/4HANA SD. Serves as the cross-system reference key for event scheduling and revenue management workflows.']
  fixture_name varchar [note: 'Official human-readable name of the fixture as published to fans, broadcasters, and sponsors (e.g., \'NBA Finals Game 7\', \'UFC 300\', \'FIFA World Cup Final\'). Used in ticketing, broadcast schedules, and fan engagement platforms.']
  home_score varchar [note: 'Final score or result for the home team or primary competitor at the conclusion of the fixture. Populated post-event. Used for standings calculation, WAR (Wins Above Replacement) analytics, and league governance reporting.']
  is_broadcast_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this fixture is eligible for broadcast distribution under existing media rights agreements. Drives activation of broadcast workflows in Dalet Galaxy and content rights management.']
  is_neutral_site boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the fixture is played at a neutral venue with no designated home team advantage. Affects revenue sharing agreements, ticketing allocation rules, and broadcast designation.']
  is_ppv boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this fixture is designated as a Pay-Per-View (PPV) event requiring individual purchase for broadcast access. Triggers PPV pricing and DTC distribution workflows in OTT platforms.']
  leg_number varchar [note: 'Leg sequence number for two-legged ties or multi-game series (e.g., Leg 1, Leg 2 of a UEFA tie; Game 3 of an NBA playoff series). Nullable for single-leg fixtures.']
  lifecycle_status varchar [note: 'Current operational state of the fixture within its lifecycle workflow from initial announcement through post-event reconciliation. Governs downstream system activations: ticketing goes on-sale at \'confirmed\', broadcast feeds activate at \'in_progress\', revenue reconciliation triggers at \'completed\'.']
  local_timezone varchar [note: 'IANA timezone identifier for the venue\'s local timezone (e.g., \'America/New_York\', \'Europe/London\'). Required for accurate local time display in fan-facing platforms, broadcast scheduling, and ticket delivery.']
  matchday_number varchar [note: 'Sequential matchday or game week number within the season or competition phase (e.g., Matchday 1, Week 12). Used for fixture calendar display, standings calculation, and broadcast scheduling.']
  penalty_shootout boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the fixture was decided by a penalty shootout or tiebreaker procedure. Used for competition result reporting and broadcast content tagging in Dalet Galaxy.']
  result_status varchar [note: 'Official outcome classification of the fixture as determined by the governing body. Distinct from lifecycle_status — this captures the competitive result rather than the operational state. Used for standings, CAS (Court of Arbitration for Sport) appeals, and league governance.']
  scheduled_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Planned end date and time of the fixture in UTC. Used for broadcast window allocation, venue turnaround scheduling, and post-event reconciliation triggers in SAP S/4HANA.']
  scheduled_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The principal real-world scheduled date and time when the fixture is planned to begin, stored in UTC with timezone offset. Used as the primary business event timestamp for broadcast scheduling, ticketing on-sale windows, and fan engagement notifications via Adobe Experience Platform.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the operational system of record from which this fixture record originated (e.g., Ticketmaster/AXS, Archtics, SAP S/4HANA SD, or league data feed). Supports data lineage tracking and Silver Layer reconciliation in the Databricks Lakehouse.']
  surface_type varchar [note: 'Type of playing surface used for this fixture. Relevant for athlete injury risk management, CBA (Collective Bargaining Agreement) surface compliance requirements, and performance analytics. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: natural_grass|artificial_turf|hardwood|ice|clay|grass|carpet — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when the fixture record was last modified. Required for incremental data pipeline processing in the Databricks Silver Layer and audit trail compliance under SOX.']
  var_used boolean [note: 'Indicates whether Video Assistant Referee (VAR) or equivalent video review technology (e.g., TMO in rugby) was deployed during this fixture. Required for competition governance reporting and broadcast production metadata.']
  weather_condition varchar [note: 'Recorded weather condition at the venue during the fixture. Used for performance analytics in SportsCode/Hudl, insurance claims, and post-event operational reporting. \'indoor\' applies to enclosed venues. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: clear|cloudy|rain|snow|fog|extreme_heat|indoor — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']

  Note: 'Core master entity and SSOT for event identity — any scheduled live sports or entertainment event. Captures event name, type (match, concert, PPV, exhibition, all-star, draft, awards), status (announced → confirmed → in-progress → completed/cancelled), scheduled date/time with timezone, doors-open time, event tier (regular season, playoff, championship, special event), home/away designation, neutral site flag, broadcast eligibility flag, classification (league, cup, friendly, touring), and lifecycle timestamps. Anchor entity referenced by all downstream domains.'
}

Table event.calendar {
  calendar_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the event calendar / season record. Primary key for this entity in the Silver Layer lakehouse.']
  calendar_governing_body_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the organisational unit or external body responsible for publishing and maintaining the official fixture schedule for this season.']
  calendar_scheduling_authority_governing_body_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the organisational unit or external body responsible for publishing and maintaining the official fixture schedule for this season.']
  compliance_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.obligation. Business justification: Season calendars are governed by CBA obligations, WADA compliance requirements, and broadcast regulatory obligations. event_calendar has cba_applicable and wada_compliance_required flags. This FK link']
  fiscal_period_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.fiscal_period. Business justification: Sports season calendars must align with fiscal periods for revenue recognition, budget planning, and SEC/financial reporting. Finance teams map season start/end dates to fiscal periods for period-end ']
  league_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the league or entertainment series that owns this season calendar. Links to the league master entity.']
  media_rights_deal_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to broadcast.media_rights_deal. Business justification: Season calendars are the scheduling basis for media rights deals — deals are structured per season. calendar.broadcast_rights_region is a denormalized deal attribute. Broadcast rights compliance and s']
  season_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to league.season. Business justification: A scheduling calendar maps directly to a league season for schedule publication, broadcast rights window alignment, and CBA compliance reporting. calendar has season_code and season_year as plain attr']
  blackout_dates varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of specific dates on which no fixtures may be scheduled due to regulatory, contractual, or operational constraints (e.g., broadcast blackout windows, public holidays, conflicting major events). Enforced by the scheduling authority.']
  bye_weeks varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of round/week numbers designated as bye weeks (no fixtures scheduled). Relevant primarily for NFL and similar league formats. Used by broadcast schedulers in Dalet Galaxy to fill programming gaps and by venue operations for maintenance windows.']
  calendar_status varchar [note: 'Current publication status of the fixture calendar. Controls downstream visibility and consumption: \'draft\' is internal only; \'published\' is the official release to broadcast, ticketing, and venue operations; \'amended\' indicates a post-publication revision; \'suspended\' indicates a temporary halt; \'archived\' is end-of-life.']
  cba_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the season schedule is governed by an active Collective Bargaining Agreement (CBA) between the league and players\' association. When true, CBA constraints on game counts, rest days, and travel windows must be validated against the fixture calendar.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this event calendar record was first created in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and SOX financial controls compliance.']
  gdpr_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether GDPR data privacy obligations apply to fan and participant data collected during this season, based on the host country and market reach. When true, data processing activities must comply with GDPR Articles 5-7 and fan consent management in Adobe Experience Platform.']
  has_playoff_structure boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this season includes a post-season playoff or knockout tournament phase beyond the regular season. When true, playoff-specific broadcast rights, ticketing tiers, and venue contracts are activated.']
  host_country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the primary host nation for this season (e.g., \'USA\', \'GBR\', \'FRA\'). Determines applicable regulatory frameworks (GDPR, CCPA, local tax), venue safety standards (OSHA, ADA), and broadcast licensing requirements (FCC).']
  international_window_dates varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of date ranges reserved for international competition windows (e.g., FIFA international breaks, Olympic qualifying periods) during which domestic league fixtures are suspended. Critical for fixture scheduling compliance with FIFA and IOC calendars.']
  is_international_season boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this season includes fixtures played in multiple countries or international markets. When true, multi-jurisdiction regulatory compliance (GDPR, local broadcast licensing, visa/work permit requirements) is triggered.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for operational notes, scheduling caveats, or amendment reasons associated with this season calendar record (e.g., \'Schedule amended due to venue unavailability in Week 7\', \'Shortened season per CBA force majeure clause\'). Supports audit trail and change management.']
  number_of_participating_teams varchar [note: 'Total count of teams, clubs, or acts participating in this season. Used for fixture generation, venue capacity planning in Archtics, and broadcast slot allocation. For concert series, represents the number of performing acts.']
  playoff_format_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the playoff bracket or knockout structure (e.g., \'Single-elimination 12-team bracket\', \'Best-of-7 series, 16 teams\'). Supports broadcast production planning and fan engagement content in Adobe Experience Platform.']
  playoff_start_date date [note: 'Scheduled start date of the post-season playoff phase. Null if has_playoff_structure is false. Used to define the playoff broadcast rights window and premium ticketing on-sale dates in Ticketmaster/AXS.']
  publication_date date [note: 'The official date on which the fixture calendar was publicly released by the scheduling authority. Triggers downstream on-sale windows in Ticketmaster/AXS and broadcast scheduling in Dalet Galaxy.']
  season_end_date date [note: 'Official end date of the competitive or programming season, inclusive of any playoff or championship rounds. Defines the effective-until boundary for broadcast rights, venue contracts, and sponsorship activation periods.']
  season_format varchar [note: 'Structural format of the season indicating whether it follows the standard schedule length or a modified format (e.g., shortened due to CBA disputes or COVID, expanded due to league growth). Critical for CBA compliance tracking and broadcast rights valuation.']
  season_phase varchar [note: 'Current active phase of the season lifecycle. Drives phase-specific business rules across ticketing (Ticketmaster/AXS), broadcast (Dalet Galaxy), and venue operations. \'pre_season\' = warm-up fixtures; \'regular_season\' = main competition; \'post_season\' = playoffs/finals; \'off_season\' = between seasons.']
  season_start_date date [note: 'Official start date of the competitive or programming season as published by the governing body. Used as the effective-from boundary for broadcast rights windows, ticketing on-sale periods, and venue booking calendars.']
  season_status varchar [note: 'Operational lifecycle state of the season itself (distinct from calendar publication status). \'planning\' = pre-season setup; \'active\' = season in progress; \'completed\' = all fixtures concluded; \'cancelled\' = season abandoned; \'suspended\' = temporarily halted (e.g., force majeure, labour dispute).']
  season_year varchar [note: 'The primary calendar year associated with this season (e.g., 2025). For multi-year seasons (e.g., 2024-25), this represents the start year. Used for year-over-year reporting and financial period alignment in SAP S/4HANA FI/CO.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the operational system of record from which this season calendar record was ingested (e.g., \'SAP_S4HANA\' for SAP S/4HANA SD module, \'TICKETMASTER\' for Ticketmaster/AXS, \'ARCHTICS\' for Archtics venue seating). Supports data lineage and Silver Layer provenance tracking.']
  source_system_record_reference varchar [note: 'The native primary key or record identifier of this season calendar record in the originating operational system (e.g., SAP S/4HANA season object ID, Archtics season ID). Enables reconciliation and bi-directional traceability between the lakehouse Silver Layer and source systems.']
  sport_discipline varchar [note: 'Specific sport or entertainment discipline for this season (e.g., \'American Football\', \'Basketball\', \'Soccer\', \'Ice Hockey\', \'Tennis\', \'Concert Tour\'). Aligns with FIFA, NFL, NBA, NHL, MLS, ATP governing body classifications.']
  sport_entertainment_type varchar [note: 'High-level classification of the season content type, distinguishing competitive sports leagues from entertainment programming series (e.g., concerts, esports). Drives downstream routing to broadcast, ticketing, and venue operations workflows.']
  sustainability_standard varchar [note: 'Environmental sustainability standard applied to the management of this season\'s events (e.g., \'ISO_20121\' for ISO 20121 Event Sustainability Management Systems). Supports ESG reporting and venue operations compliance.']
  tiebreaker_rules_reference varchar [note: 'Reference code or document identifier for the official tiebreaker rules applicable to this season\'s standings and playoff qualification (e.g., \'NFL-2024-TIEBREAK-v2\', \'FIFA-RANKING-2024\'). Ensures consistent application of competition governance rules.']
  total_scheduled_fixtures varchar [note: 'Total number of individual fixtures (games, matches, events, shows) planned across the entire season. Drives capacity planning for ticketing inventory in Archtics and broadcast slot allocation in Dalet Galaxy.']
  total_scheduled_rounds varchar [note: 'Total number of competitive rounds, match weeks, or programming weeks planned for this season. For NFL this represents weeks; for soccer it represents match rounds; for concert series it represents show dates. Used for broadcast slot planning and revenue forecasting in Workday Adaptive Planning.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this event calendar record was most recently modified. Used to detect calendar amendments and trigger downstream notifications to broadcast, ticketing, and venue operations consumers.']
  var_technology_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether Video Assistant Referee (VAR) or Television Match Official (TMO) technology is deployed for fixtures in this season. Affects broadcast production requirements, OB (Outside Broadcast) unit specifications, and officiating governance.']
  version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the fixture calendar (e.g., \'v1.0\', \'v2.3\'). Incremented each time the calendar is amended post-publication. Enables downstream consumers (broadcast, ticketing, venue) to detect and reconcile schedule changes.']
  wada_compliance_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether WADA anti-doping testing protocols are mandated for this season. When true, athlete testing schedules and PED (Performance Enhancing Drug) compliance reporting obligations are activated for all participating athletes.']

  Note: 'Master entity defining a competitive or programming season within a league or entertainment series. Serves as the SSOT for season identity AND the official published fixture calendar/schedule. Captures season name, year, sport/entertainment type, start/end dates, number of scheduled rounds/weeks, playoff structure flag, season status (planning, active, completed), governing body reference, CBA applicability flag, season format (regular, shortened, expanded), calendar publication date, calendar version, scheduling authority, blackout dates, bye weeks, international window dates, and calendar status (draft, published, amended). Owns the official release of fixture schedules to downstream consumers including broadcast, ticketing, and venue operations.'
}

Table event.tournament {
  tournament_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the tournament record within the Sports Entertainment data platform. Primary key for the tournament master entity.']
  budget_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking this allocation record to the specific budget planning line in Workday Adaptive Planning / SAP CO. Enables budget-level tracking of which tournaments consume each budget line.']
  calendar_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to event.event_calendar. Business justification: tournament is a master entity representing a structured competition format. While it already has a cross-domain season_id (league.season), the event_calendar is the event domain\'s SSOT for the competi']
  capacity_config_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to venue.capacity_config. Business justification: Tournament operations planning requires the specific capacity configuration governing ticket sales, ADA compliance, and safety certification for the host venue. Tournament directors reference this for']
  deal_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sponsorship.deal. Business justification: Tournament-level title/presenting sponsorship deals (e.g., \'Barclays Premier League\', \'Emirates FA Cup\') are a primary revenue stream. Commercial teams must link a tournament to its governing sponsors']
  governing_body_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.governing_body. Business justification: Tournaments are sanctioned and governed by specific governing bodies (FIFA, IOC, NBA, etc.). tournament.governing_body is a plain text denormalization of compliance.governing_body. This FK normalizes ']
  league_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the league or governing body that owns and sanctions this tournament (e.g., NBA, NFL, FIFA, UFC). Links to the league master entity.']
  media_rights_deal_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to broadcast.media_rights_deal. Business justification: Tournaments are governed by media rights deals determining broadcaster exclusivity, PPV status, and territory restrictions. tournament.broadcast_rights_type is a denormalized representation of deal at']
  policy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.policy. Business justification: Tournaments operate under specific compliance policies (eligibility, anti-doping, conduct). Tournament directors reference the governing compliance policy for participant eligibility and operational s']
  property_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sponsorship.property. Business justification: Tournaments are sponsorship properties in sports commercial operations — they appear in property inventories with valuations, reach estimates, and inventory units. Linking tournament to its sponsorshi']
  season_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the competitive season within which this tournament is held. Enables association of tournament results to the broader season calendar.']
  venue_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the primary host venue for the tournament. For multi-venue tournaments, this represents the primary or final-stage venue.']
  actuals_amount decimal [note: 'Actual expenditure or revenue posted against this tournament from this budget line year-to-date. Sourced from detection phase relationship data. Enables tournament-level actuals tracking within the broader budget line.']
  ada_compliance_status varchar [note: 'ADA (Americans with Disabilities Act) accessibility compliance status for the tournament\'s venues and event operations. Required for US-hosted events and venue certification.']
  age_category varchar [note: 'Age classification bracket for tournament eligibility (e.g., Senior, Under-23, Under-18, Masters). Governs participant eligibility per CBA and governing body regulations. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: senior|under_23|under_21|under_18|youth|masters|open — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  announcement_date date [note: 'Date on which the tournament was officially announced to the public and media. Marks the start of the tournament lifecycle for marketing, sponsorship, and fan engagement planning.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the tournament record was first created in the Sports Entertainment data platform. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and SOX financial controls compliance.']
  edition_number varchar [note: 'Sequential edition or iteration number of the tournament (e.g., 23rd FIFA World Cup, 75th NBA Playoffs). Enables historical trend analysis and year-over-year performance comparisons.']
  edition_year varchar [note: 'Calendar year associated with this tournament edition (e.g., 2026 for FIFA World Cup 2026). Used as a human-readable identifier for the edition in reporting and fan-facing content.']
  eligibility_criteria varchar [note: 'Narrative description of participant eligibility requirements including nationality restrictions, ranking thresholds, qualification pathway requirements, and CBA compliance conditions.']
  end_date date [note: 'Official calendar date on which the tournament concludes (final match or closing ceremony). Used for broadcast rights expiry, venue release, and post-event reconciliation.']
  external_code varchar [note: 'Externally recognized unique code or identifier assigned by the governing body or data provider (e.g., FIFA tournament ID, Opta competition code). Used for data exchange and integration with third-party systems.']
  format_type varchar [note: 'Structural competition format that defines how participants advance through the tournament. Drives bracket logic, scheduling, and elimination rules. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: single_elimination|double_elimination|round_robin|group_stage_knockout|multi_stage|league_playoff|swiss — promote to reference product if additional formats are needed]']
  gdpr_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether GDPR (General Data Protection Regulation) data privacy obligations apply to this tournament based on host country jurisdiction or EU participant data processing. Triggers fan data privacy controls in Adobe Experience Platform.']
  gender_category varchar [note: 'Gender classification of the tournament competition (e.g., Men\'s World Cup, Women\'s NBA). Required for scheduling, eligibility, and regulatory compliance with governing body rules.']
  host_country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 three-letter country code of the primary host nation for the tournament (e.g., USA, BRA, FRA). Used for regulatory compliance, broadcast rights geo-restrictions, and tax jurisdiction.']
  host_region varchar [note: 'Geographic region or confederation where the tournament is hosted (e.g., North America, Europe, Asia-Pacific). Used for broadcast rights distribution, regional sponsorship packages, and fan engagement targeting.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text operational notes or remarks about the tournament, including special conditions, governing body directives, or event-specific exceptions not captured in structured fields.']
  official_website_url varchar [note: 'URL of the official tournament website used for fan engagement, media accreditation, and digital content distribution. Integrated with Adobe Experience Platform for fan personalization.']
  planned_amount decimal [note: 'The original planned budget amount allocated to this specific tournament from this budget line for the applicable fiscal period. Sourced from detection phase relationship data. Belongs to the allocation relationship, not the tournament or budget record alone.']
  prize_pool_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total monetary prize pool allocated for distribution to tournament participants and winners, expressed in the tournament\'s base currency. Used for financial planning, sponsor negotiations, and athlete contract compliance.']
  prize_pool_currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the prize pool amount (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Required for multi-currency financial reporting and SAP S/4HANA FI integration.']
  qualification_method varchar [note: 'Primary method by which participants earn entry into the tournament (e.g., direct qualification via league standings, playoff qualifier, wild card selection, invitation by governing body, ranking points accumulation).']
  recurrence_type varchar [note: 'Frequency at which this tournament recurs (e.g., annual for NBA Playoffs, quadrennial for FIFA World Cup, one-time for special events). Supports long-range event planning and broadcast rights negotiation cycles.']
  registration_deadline date [note: 'Final date by which teams or athletes must complete registration and eligibility verification to participate in the tournament. Enforced per governing body regulations.']
  revised_amount decimal [note: 'The most recently approved revised budget amount allocated to this tournament after reforecast cycles. Sourced from detection phase relationship data. Reflects mid-cycle adjustments specific to this tournament-budget pairing.']
  seeding_methodology varchar [note: 'Method used to assign seeds and bracket positions to participants (e.g., ranking-based using WAR or QBR metrics, committee selection, random draw). Governs competitive fairness and bracket integrity.']
  short_name varchar [note: 'Abbreviated or commonly used name for the tournament used in broadcast graphics, digital platforms, and fan-facing displays (e.g., \'World Cup 2026\', \'NBA Playoffs\').']
  social_media_hashtag varchar [note: 'Official social media hashtag designated for the tournament (e.g., #WorldCup2026, #NBAPlayoffs). Used for fan engagement tracking, UGC (User Generated Content) monitoring, and NPS (Net Promoter Score) sentiment analysis.']
  sport_type varchar [note: 'The sport or entertainment discipline associated with this tournament (e.g., \'Football\', \'Basketball\', \'Mixed Martial Arts\', \'Tennis\', \'Concert Series\'). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: football|basketball|baseball|ice_hockey|soccer|tennis|mma|golf|esports|concert — promote to reference product]']
  start_date date [note: 'Official calendar date on which the tournament competition begins (first match or opening ceremony). Used for scheduling, broadcast rights windows, and ticketing activation.']
  sustainability_certified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the tournament has achieved or is pursuing ISO 20121 Event Sustainability Management System certification. Supports ESG reporting and sponsor sustainability commitments.']
  ticket_sales_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether public ticket sales are enabled for this tournament. When false, the tournament may be closed-door, invitation-only, or broadcast-only. Drives Ticketmaster/AXS inventory activation.']
  tiebreaker_rules varchar [note: 'Description of the rules applied to resolve tied standings or scores within the tournament (e.g., head-to-head record, goal difference, penalty shootout, sudden death overtime). Governed by the sanctioning body.']
  total_matches_scheduled varchar [note: 'Total number of matches or games scheduled across all rounds of the tournament (e.g., 64 matches for FIFA World Cup group + knockout stages). Used for venue scheduling, broadcast slot planning, and officiating resource allocation.']
  total_participants varchar [note: 'Maximum number of teams or individual athletes eligible to compete in the tournament (e.g., 32 teams for FIFA World Cup, 16 teams for NBA Playoffs). Defines bracket size and seeding structure.']
  total_rounds varchar [note: 'Total number of competitive rounds or stages in the tournament bracket (e.g., 7 rounds for NBA Playoffs, 6 rounds for FIFA World Cup knockout stage). Drives bracket generation and scheduling logic.']
  tournament_name varchar [note: 'Official full name of the tournament as sanctioned by the governing body (e.g., \'FIFA World Cup 2026\', \'NBA Playoffs 2025\', \'UFC Championship Series Season 3\').']
  tournament_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the tournament from announcement through post-event reconciliation. Drives operational workflows, ticketing availability, and broadcast scheduling.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the tournament record. Used for change data capture, incremental ETL processing, and audit trail compliance.']
  var_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether VAR (Video Assistant Referee) or TMO (Television Match Official) technology is deployed for officiating decisions during this tournament. Relevant for football, rugby, and other applicable sports.']
  wada_compliance_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether WADA (World Anti-Doping Agency) anti-doping testing protocols are mandatory for this tournament. Triggers PED (Performance Enhancing Drug) testing workflows and athlete eligibility verification.']

  Note: 'Master entity representing a structured competition format — knockout brackets, round-robin groups, double elimination, or multi-stage tournaments (e.g., FIFA World Cup, NBA Playoffs, UFC Championship Series). Captures tournament name, format type, number of rounds, seeding methodology, host region, prize pool amount, governing body, eligibility criteria, start/end dates, and tournament status. Distinct from season as tournaments have defined bracket structures and elimination logic.'
}

Table event.bracket {
  bracket_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the tournament bracket record. Primary key for the bracket data product within the event domain.']
  conference_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to league.conference. Business justification: Conference-specific playoff brackets (e.g., AFC/NFC brackets) are a core operational structure in multi-conference leagues. Bracket seeding, advancement rules, and standings reports are all conference']
  deal_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sponsorship.deal. Business justification: Bracket title sponsorship deal traceability: bracket already references sponsor_id but cannot look up contractual terms. A deal_id FK enables bracket sponsorship fulfillment tracking, broadcast integr']
  league_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the governing league or competition body that owns and administers this bracket. Links to league governance rules, CBA provisions, and regulatory reporting requirements.']
  media_rights_deal_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the broadcast rights agreement governing media distribution of this bracket\'s competition. Links to OTT, RSN, and DTC distribution channels for rights management and FCC compliance.']
  playoff_bracket_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to league.playoff_bracket. Business justification: Tournament brackets that implement league playoff structures must reference the authoritative league.playoff_bracket record. This link supports playoff bracket synchronization, seeding validation, and']
  season_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the league season or competition year in which this bracket is active. Enables season-over-season bracket comparison and historical analysis.']
  sponsor_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the primary presenting sponsor associated with this bracket (e.g., \'Presented by [Brand]\'). Links to sponsorship activation and inventory allocation for naming rights and on-screen branding.']
  tournament_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent tournament or competition event that this bracket belongs to. Links bracket structure to the governing event entity.']
  venue_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the primary venue or facility where this bracket\'s competition is hosted. Used for venue operations planning, ADA compliance checks, and OSHA safety assessments.']
  advancement_rule varchar [note: 'Rule governing how participants advance from one round to the next within this bracket. Determines winner progression logic used to fill subsequent bracket slots.']
  age_category varchar [note: 'Age division classification for the bracket (e.g., youth, junior, senior, masters). Ensures compliance with governing body eligibility rules and CBA age restrictions.']
  bracket_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric code uniquely identifying this bracket within the tournament management system. Used in broadcast overlays, fan engagement platforms, and league governance communications.']
  bracket_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable display name for the bracket (e.g., \'Men\'s Singles Main Draw\', \'Western Conference Playoffs Bracket\'). Used in broadcast overlays, fan engagement platforms, and digital content.']
  bracket_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the bracket from initial draft through post-event reconciliation. Drives workflow routing in league governance, broadcast scheduling, and fan engagement platforms. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|announced|seeding_complete|in_progress|completed|cancelled|suspended — promote to reference product]']
  bracket_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the bracket format governing advancement rules. Determines how winners and losers progress through the draw. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: single_elimination|double_elimination|round_robin|swiss|group_stage|repechage — promote to reference product if additional formats are required]']
  broadcast_overlay_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether real-time bracket visualization data is enabled for broadcast overlay graphics. When true, bracket progression data is streamed to broadcast production systems for on-screen display.']
  bye_count varchar [note: 'Number of bye assignments in the first round of the bracket, granted when total entrants do not fill a power-of-two draw. Byes automatically advance the assigned participant to the next round.']
  consolation_bracket boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a consolation or losers bracket exists alongside this main bracket, as in double-elimination formats. When true, loser advancement rules apply and a separate bracket reset may be triggered.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this bracket record was first created in the system. Audit trail field supporting SOX financial controls and data lineage requirements.']
  current_round varchar [note: 'The round number currently in progress within the bracket. Updated in real-time as results are confirmed and participants advance. Drives real-time bracket visualization for broadcast overlays and fan engagement platforms.']
  draw_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the official bracket draw ceremony was or is scheduled to be conducted. Marks the transition from seeding_complete to announced status.']
  draw_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time at which the official bracket draw was executed, including timezone offset. Used for broadcast scheduling and audit trail of bracket creation.']
  end_date date [note: 'Scheduled or actual calendar date on which the final match of this bracket is completed. Used for post-event reconciliation and season structure closure.']
  fan_engagement_visible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this bracket is published and visible on fan engagement platforms (Adobe Experience Platform, DTC apps, OTT services). Controls public-facing bracket display and fan prediction features.']
  gender_category varchar [note: 'Gender classification of the competition bracket as defined by the governing body. Used for scheduling, broadcast rights segmentation, and compliance with IOC and governing body regulations.']
  governing_body_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the international or national governing body whose rules and regulations apply to this bracket. Determines applicable competition rules, anti-doping requirements, and dispute resolution via CAS. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: FIFA|IOC|NFL|NBA|MLB|NHL|MLS|ATP|UFC|WADA — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  last_result_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent match result that triggered a bracket slot update. Used for real-time bracket synchronization with broadcast overlays and fan engagement platforms.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for league operations staff to record special conditions, governance decisions, protest outcomes, or CAS arbitration notes affecting this bracket\'s structure or results.']
  phase varchar [note: 'Specific competition phase or stage this bracket represents within the overall tournament structure (e.g., qualifying, group stage, knockout rounds). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: qualifying|group_stage|round_of_64|round_of_32|round_of_16|quarterfinal|semifinal|third_place|final — promote to reference product]']
  prediction_game_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether fan bracket prediction and pick\'em games are activated for this bracket on fan engagement platforms. Drives fan engagement KPI tracking and NPS measurement.']
  reset_rule varchar [note: 'Rule defining whether and how the bracket resets in double-elimination formats when the losers bracket winner faces the undefeated winners bracket champion. Governs whether a grand final reset match is required.']
  result_source varchar [note: 'Source system or process from which match results are ingested to update bracket slot progression. Includes VAR (Video Assistant Referee) and TMO (Television Match Official) review outcomes for result verification.']
  seeding_locked boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the seeding assignments for this bracket have been finalized and locked, preventing further modifications. Set to true after the official draw ceremony.']
  seeding_method varchar [note: 'Method used to assign seeding positions to participants in the bracket draw. Determines competitive fairness and scheduling of top seeds to avoid early high-profile matchups.']
  slot_fill_method varchar [note: 'Method by which future bracket slots are populated as results are confirmed. \'Automatic\' fills slots immediately upon result confirmation; \'manual\' requires league office intervention; \'pending_result\' holds slot until result is verified.']
  sport_type varchar [note: 'The sport or entertainment discipline for which this bracket is structured (e.g., basketball, soccer, tennis, esports, mixed martial arts). Drives sport-specific advancement and scoring rules. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: promote to reference product for full sport taxonomy]']
  start_date date [note: 'Scheduled calendar date on which competition within this bracket is set to begin. Used for fixture calendar generation and broadcast rights scheduling.']
  third_place_match boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this bracket includes a third-place playoff match between the two semifinal losers. Relevant for broadcast scheduling and ticketing inventory allocation.']
  tiebreaker_rule varchar [note: 'Description of the tiebreaker rule applied when participants are level on points or score at the end of regulation (e.g., \'away goals\', \'penalty shootout\', \'sudden death overtime\', \'head-to-head record\'). Governs conditional scheduling of additional fixtures. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: promote to reference product for full tiebreaker taxonomy]']
  total_entrants varchar [note: 'Number of teams, athletes, or participants entered into this bracket. May differ from total_slots when byes are assigned to fill power-of-two draw sizes.']
  total_rounds varchar [note: 'Total number of rounds in this bracket structure. Defines the depth of the bracket tree and is used to pre-generate slot positions and schedule future fixtures.']
  total_slots varchar [note: 'Total number of matchup slots in the bracket across all rounds. Determines the capacity of the draw and is used for seeding assignment and bye allocation.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this bracket record was most recently modified. Supports change data capture, audit trail, and Silver Layer incremental processing on the Databricks Lakehouse.']
  weight_class varchar [note: 'Weight class designation for combat sports brackets (e.g., UFC lightweight, heavyweight). Null for non-combat sports. Governs participant eligibility and WADA weigh-in compliance requirements.']

  Note: 'Transactional entity capturing the bracket structure and real-time progression of a tournament — rounds, matchup slots, seeding positions, advancement rules, bye assignments, and results-driven slot filling. Tracks which fixture occupies each bracket slot, winner advancement logic, bracket reset rules for double-elimination formats, and conditional scheduling of future fixtures as results are confirmed. Enables real-time bracket visualization for broadcast overlays and fan engagement platforms.'
}

Table event.match_result {
  match_result_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the match result record. Primary key for the match_result data product in the event domain Silver layer.']
  competition_round_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to event.competition_round. Business justification: A match result is recorded within a specific competition round (e.g., Round of 16, Semi-Final). match_result currently stores competition_stage as a denormalized STRING. competition_round owns the aut']
  fixture_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the scheduled fixture (event) for which this result is recorded. Links the outcome back to the pre-game fixture master record.']
  media_rights_deal_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to broadcast.media_rights_deal. Business justification: Match results are the core deliverable of a media rights deal. Rights holders track delivered matches against minimum guaranteed event counts for revenue recognition and deal compliance reporting. A s']
  mvp_athlete_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to athlete.athlete_profile. Business justification: Player-of-the-Match/MVP designation is a core post-match reporting, broadcast, and awards process in sports-entertainment. League statistical systems, broadcast overlays, and fan engagement platforms ']
  playoff_bracket_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to league.playoff_bracket. Business justification: Match results in playoff rounds must be linked to the playoff bracket to drive bracket advancement logic, update seeding, and trigger next-round scheduling. League playoff management systems depend on']
  primary_match_competition_tournament_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the league, cup, or tournament competition governing this fixture. Enables competition-scoped standings and bracket progression.']
  primary_match_franchise_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the home team or home-side participant in the fixture. Used for league standings, head-to-head analytics, and broadcast attribution.']
  primary_match_official_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the primary match official (referee, umpire, or head official) who officiated the match. Used for officiating performance analytics, assignment tracking, and governing body compliance.']
  quaternary_match_winning_team_franchise_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the team that won the match. Null for draws or no-result outcomes. Used for bracket progression in knockout competitions, playoff seeding, and broadcast winner attribution.']
  season_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the competitive season in which this match result occurred. Supports season-level standings aggregation and historical trend analysis.']
  tertiary_match_franchise_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the home team or home-side participant in the fixture. Used for league standings, head-to-head analytics, and broadcast attribution.']
  tertiary_match_winning_team_franchise_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the team that won the match. Null for draws or no-result outcomes. Used for bracket progression in knockout competitions, playoff seeding, and broadcast winner attribution.']
  venue_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the venue where the match was played. Used for venue-level performance analytics and operational reporting.']
  attendance varchar [note: 'Official announced attendance figure (number of spectators present) for the match. Used for venue capacity utilization reporting, revenue reconciliation, and league attendance KPI tracking.']
  away_score_final varchar [note: 'Official final score (goals, points, runs, etc.) for the away team at the conclusion of regulation and any extra time/overtime/shootout. This is the authoritative scoreline used for standings and wagering settlement.']
  away_score_regulation varchar [note: 'Away team score at the end of regulation time only (excluding overtime, extra time, or shootout). Required for sports where regulation draws carry different standings points than overtime losses.']
  broadcast_match_reference varchar [note: 'External identifier assigned to this match result by the broadcast or media rights partner (e.g., RSN, OTT platform, Dalet Galaxy MAM system). Used to link match result data to broadcast highlight packages, content rights windows, and CDN distribution metadata.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'UTC timestamp when this match result record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and SOX financial controls for revenue-impacting result records.']
  doping_control_conducted boolean [note: 'Indicates whether WADA-mandated PED (Performance Enhancing Drug) doping control testing was conducted in connection with this match. Supports WADA compliance reporting and anti-doping program audit trails.']
  home_score_final varchar [note: 'Official final score (goals, points, runs, etc.) for the home team at the conclusion of regulation and any extra time/overtime/shootout. This is the authoritative scoreline used for standings and wagering settlement.']
  home_score_regulation varchar [note: 'Home team score at the end of regulation time only (excluding overtime, extra time, or shootout). Required for sports where regulation draws carry different standings points than overtime losses (e.g., NHL, MLS).']
  kickoff_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise UTC timestamp of the official match start (kickoff, tip-off, first pitch, puck drop, etc.). Distinct from match_date — provides sub-day precision for broadcast scheduling, SLA measurement, and wagering in-play event sequencing.']
  margin_of_victory varchar [note: 'Absolute difference between the winning and losing team\'s final scores (e.g., 3 goals, 14 points). Null for draws. Used for strength-of-schedule analytics, WAR (Wins Above Replacement) calculations, and wagering handicap settlement.']
  match_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the match was played (local venue time). Used for fixture calendar reporting, standings date-range filtering, and broadcast scheduling alignment.']
  match_duration_minutes varchar [note: 'Total actual playing time in minutes from kickoff to final whistle, including stoppage time and overtime but excluding pre-match and half-time intervals. Used for broadcast scheduling, venue operations planning, and SLA compliance.']
  match_outcome varchar [note: 'Categorical result of the match from the perspective of home vs. away: \'home_win\', \'away_win\', \'draw\', or \'no_result\' (abandoned/forfeited). Primary field for league standings points allocation and wagering market settlement.']
  neutral_venue boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the match was played at a neutral venue (neither team\'s home ground). Affects home/away advantage analytics, standings point allocation rules, and ticket revenue attribution.']
  overtime_played boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the match required overtime, extra time, or additional periods beyond regulation to determine a winner. Drives standings point allocation logic (e.g., NHL OTL point) and broadcast scheduling overrun management.']
  period_scores_away varchar [note: 'Pipe-delimited string of away team scores by period, quarter, half, set, or inning (e.g., \'7|21|14|3\' for NFL quarters). Enables granular in-game momentum analytics and broadcast segment attribution.']
  period_scores_home varchar [note: 'Pipe-delimited string of home team scores by period, quarter, half, set, or inning (e.g., \'21|14|7|10\' for NFL quarters, or \'1|0|2\' for NHL periods). Enables granular in-game momentum analytics and broadcast segment attribution.']
  pitch_condition varchar [note: 'Official assessment of the playing surface condition at the time of the match. Used for player welfare analytics, venue maintenance scheduling, and governing body compliance reporting.']
  protest_reference varchar [note: 'Official reference number of any formal protest or appeal lodged against this match result with the governing body or CAS (Court of Arbitration for Sport). Null if no protest filed. Supports legal and compliance tracking.']
  result_confirmed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'UTC timestamp at which the governing body or league operations officially confirmed and locked the match result. Triggers downstream processes: standings updates, wagering settlement, broadcast highlights release, and fantasy sports scoring.']
  result_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for official notes or annotations related to the match result, such as protest grounds, abandonment reasons, score correction explanations, or governing body directives. Supports compliance documentation and operational context.']
  result_reference_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric code assigned to this match result by the governing body or league operations system. Used for official reporting, wagering integrations, and broadcast metadata tagging. Example: \'NFL-2024-WK12-DAL-PHI-RESULT\'.']
  result_source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the originating system or feed from which the match result data was ingested. Supports data lineage, audit trail, and Silver layer quality validation. Values include SportsCode/Hudl performance analytics platform, governing body official data feeds, or manual entry.']
  result_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the match result record. \'official\' = confirmed by governing body; \'under_review\' = VAR/TMO or disciplinary review pending; \'protested\' = formal protest lodged by a participant; \'forfeited\' = awarded due to forfeit; \'abandoned\' = match abandoned before completion; \'postponed_result\' = result deferred pending replay. Drives league standings inclusion and wagering settlement eligibility.']
  shootout_played boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the match was decided by a penalty shootout or shootout format (e.g., FIFA penalty shootout, NHL shootout). Distinct from overtime_played — a match can have overtime without a shootout.']
  shootout_score_away varchar [note: 'Number of successful shootout attempts by the away team during a penalty shootout. Null if shootout_played is false. Used for detailed shootout analytics and historical records.']
  shootout_score_home varchar [note: 'Number of successful shootout attempts by the home team during a penalty shootout. Null if shootout_played is false. Used for detailed shootout analytics and historical records.']
  sport_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the sport discipline for this match result. Determines the applicable scoring schema, period structure, and governing body rules. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: football|basketball|baseball|ice_hockey|soccer|tennis|rugby|cricket|golf|mma — promote to reference product]']
  stoppage_time_minutes varchar [note: 'Total additional stoppage/injury time added by the referee across all periods. Relevant for soccer/football and rugby. Used for broadcast scheduling overrun management and officiating analytics.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'UTC timestamp of the most recent modification to this match result record. Tracks amendments such as score corrections, VAR overturns, or protest resolutions.']
  var_intervention boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a Video Assistant Referee (VAR) or Television Match Official (TMO) intervention occurred during the match that affected the official result (e.g., goal disallowed, red card issued, try awarded). Supports officiating quality analytics and governing body review processes.']
  var_intervention_count varchar [note: 'Total number of VAR (Video Assistant Referee) or TMO (Television Match Official) review incidents during the match. Supports officiating quality analytics and governing body compliance reporting.']
  weather_condition varchar [note: 'Prevailing weather condition at the venue during the match. Used for performance analytics (weather impact on scoring), venue operations reporting, and broadcast production context. \'indoor\' applies to enclosed arenas. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: clear|cloudy|rain|snow|fog|extreme_heat|indoor — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']

  Note: 'Transactional entity capturing the official outcome of a completed sports fixture — final scores, scorelines by period/quarter/half/set, overtime/shootout results, winning team/athlete, margin of victory, result status (official, under review, protested, forfeited), VAR/TMO intervention flag, and result confirmation timestamp. Serves as the SSOT for competitive outcomes used by league standings, broadcast highlights, and wagering integrations. Distinct from fixture (which is the scheduled event) — this is the recorded outcome.'
}

Table event.scoring_event {
  scoring_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each individual in-game scoring moment captured in the system. Primary key for the scoring_event data product.']
  fixture_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent match or game in which this scoring event occurred. Links the scoring moment to its fixture context.']
  highlight_asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to content.asset. Business justification: Scoring events (goals, touchdowns) are directly linked to highlight clips — a core content monetization workflow in sports entertainment. broadcast_clip_reference is a denormalized string; replacing i']
  league_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the league or governing competition body under whose rules this scoring event was recorded (e.g., NFL, NBA, MLB, NHL, MLS, FIFA). Supports multi-league analytics and league-specific rule application.']
  match_result_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent match or game in which this scoring event occurred. Links the scoring moment to its fixture context.']
  media_asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to broadcast.media_asset. Business justification: Highlight clip production workflow: each scoring event must be linked to its broadcast media asset for replay packaging, highlight reels, and clip licensing. Sports broadcasters routinely tag scoring ']
  primary_scoring_athlete_profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the athlete credited with the scoring event (goal scorer, touchdown passer/rusher, basket maker, run batter, etc.).']
  primary_scoring_franchise_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the team that scored this scoring event. Determines which team\'s score is incremented.']
  season_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the league season or competition season in which this scoring event occurred. Supports season-level aggregation and reporting.']
  tertiary_scoring_athlete_profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the athlete credited with the scoring event (goal scorer, touchdown passer/rusher, basket maker, run batter, etc.).']
  venue_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the venue or facility where this scoring event occurred. Supports venue-level scoring analytics, home advantage analysis, and venue operations reporting.']
  away_score_after varchar [note: 'The cumulative score of the away team immediately after this scoring event was recorded. Paired with home_score_after to represent the full score state at the moment of the event.']
  body_part varchar [note: 'The body part or implement used by the athlete to execute the scoring action (e.g., right foot, left foot, head for soccer; bat for baseball; club for golf). Supports detailed performance analytics and broadcast commentary. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: right_foot|left_foot|head|right_hand|left_hand|body|bat|club|other — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  competition_phase varchar [note: 'The phase of the competition or tournament in which this scoring event occurred. Distinguishes regular season scoring from playoff or knockout stage scoring for statistical segmentation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: regular_season|playoffs|knockout|group_stage|final|semifinal|quarterfinal|wildcard|round_of_16|round_of_32 — promote to reference product]']
  coordinate_x decimal(8,4) [note: 'The X-axis coordinate on the standardised pitch or field coordinate system representing the horizontal position from which the scoring action originated. Used for spatial heatmaps and performance analytics in SportsCode/Hudl.']
  coordinate_y decimal(8,4) [note: 'The Y-axis coordinate on the standardised pitch or field coordinate system representing the vertical position from which the scoring action originated. Paired with coordinate_x for full spatial positioning.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time at which this scoring event record was first created and ingested into the data platform. Supports data lineage, audit trails, and SLA compliance monitoring.']
  data_quality_flag varchar [note: 'Indicates the data quality status of this scoring event record. Clean means fully validated; under_review means flagged for investigation; corrected means the record was amended after initial ingestion; disputed means conflicting data from multiple sources.']
  data_source varchar [note: 'The originating data provider or system that supplied this scoring event record (e.g., Opta, Stats Perform, Sportradar, Hudl, manual entry by official scorer, broadcast feed). Supports data lineage, quality auditing, and source reconciliation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: opta|stats_perform|sportradar|hudl|manual|broadcast_feed|official_scorer — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  event_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the scoring event record. Provisional indicates the event is pending official confirmation; confirmed means officially validated; overturned means disallowed after review; amended means corrected post-confirmation; voided means removed from official records.']
  event_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise real-world date and time at which the scoring event occurred, expressed in ISO 8601 format with timezone offset. Used for broadcast overlays, play-by-play timelines, and content production.']
  external_event_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique code or reference number for this scoring event as assigned by the official league data provider or broadcast system (e.g., Opta event ID, Stats Perform ID, Sportradar event ID). Used for cross-system reconciliation.']
  home_score_after varchar [note: 'The cumulative score of the home team immediately after this scoring event was recorded. Captures the score state for broadcast overlays, play-by-play reconstruction, and fan engagement platforms.']
  hudl_event_reference varchar [note: 'The native event identifier assigned by the SportsCode or Hudl performance analytics platform for this scoring event. Used for cross-system reconciliation between the lakehouse silver layer and the performance analytics system of record.']
  is_confirmed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this scoring event has been officially confirmed and validated by the match officials or league data operations team. False for provisional or pending events awaiting VAR review or official confirmation.']
  is_lead_change boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this scoring event resulted in a change of lead between the two competing teams. True when the scoring team overtook the previously leading team. Key metric for broadcast drama and fan engagement analytics.']
  is_own_goal boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this scoring event was an own goal (scored by a player against their own team). Affects attribution logic and statistical reporting for the credited athlete.']
  is_tie boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this scoring event resulted in a tied score between the two competing teams. True when the score differential after the event equals zero.']
  match_clock_seconds varchar [note: 'Elapsed match clock time in seconds at the moment the scoring event occurred within the current period. Enables precise play-by-play positioning and broadcast overlay synchronisation.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional contextual notes or commentary about this scoring event, as recorded by the official scorer, data operations team, or league officials. May include unusual circumstances, corrections rationale, or broadcast annotations.']
  officiating_decision_code varchar [note: 'Official code or reference assigned by the match officiating team or league operations to document any officiating decision associated with this scoring event (e.g., penalty awarded, foul called, offside check). Supports league governance and compliance reporting.']
  period_number varchar [note: 'The period, quarter, half, inning, or round number within the match in which the scoring event occurred (e.g., 1 = first half/quarter/period, 2 = second, etc.). Supports sport-agnostic period-level analytics.']
  period_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the period in which the scoring event occurred, distinguishing regular play from overtime, extra time, penalty shootout, or sudden death. Critical for statistical integrity and broadcast context.']
  pitch_zone varchar [note: 'The designated zone or area of the playing field from which the scoring action was initiated (e.g., penalty area, midfield, three-point arc, red zone, paint area). Supports spatial analytics and performance analysis in SportsCode/Hudl. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: penalty_area|six_yard_box|outside_box|three_point_arc|paint|mid_range|red_zone|end_zone|infield|outfield — promote to reference product]']
  play_pattern varchar [note: 'The tactical play pattern or situation from which the scoring event originated (e.g., open play, set piece, counter attack, fast break, corner, free kick). Supports tactical analysis and content production. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: open_play|set_piece|counter_attack|fast_break|corner|free_kick|penalty|throw_in|other — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  points_value varchar [note: 'The number of points awarded to the scoring team as a result of this scoring event (e.g., 1 for a free throw, 2 for a basket, 3 for a three-pointer, 6 for a touchdown, 1 for a goal in soccer). Core quantitative fact of the scoring event.']
  score_differential_after varchar [note: 'The signed score differential (home minus away) immediately after this scoring event. Positive values indicate home team lead; negative values indicate away team lead; zero indicates a tie. Supports win probability and momentum analytics.']
  scoring_method varchar [note: 'The specific method by which the score was achieved (e.g., penalty kick, free kick, header, slam dunk, three-pointer, home run, touchdown pass, field goal, try, conversion). Sport-specific enumeration managed as a reference product. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: penalty_kick|free_kick|header|open_play|slam_dunk|three_pointer|home_run|touchdown_pass|rushing_touchdown|field_goal|try|conversion|free_throw|layup|own_goal — promote to reference product]']
  sequence_number varchar [note: 'Sequential ordinal number of this scoring event within the match, ordered chronologically. The first score of the match is 1, the second is 2, etc. Enables ordered play-by-play reconstruction and broadcast timeline sequencing.']
  sport_type varchar [note: 'The sport discipline to which this scoring event belongs. Enables cross-sport analytics and correct interpretation of sport-specific scoring rules and methods. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: football|basketball|baseball|ice_hockey|soccer|rugby|tennis|cricket|golf|mma — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time at which this scoring event record was most recently modified in the data platform. Tracks amendments, corrections, and VAR-driven updates to the record.']
  var_review_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this scoring event was subject to a Video Assistant Referee (VAR) or Television Match Official (TMO) review process. True when a review was initiated. Supports officiating analytics and broadcast commentary.']
  var_review_outcome varchar [note: 'The outcome of the VAR or TMO review for this scoring event. Upheld means the original decision stands; overturned means the scoring event was disallowed or modified; inconclusive means no definitive determination was reached. Null or not_reviewed when var_review_flag is false.']

  Note: 'Transactional entity capturing individual in-game scoring moments — goals, touchdowns, baskets, runs, points — with precise match clock time, period/quarter/half, athlete credited, assist attribution, scoring method (penalty, free kick, slam dunk, home run), VAR review flag, and score state after the event. Enables granular play-by-play data for broadcast overlays, content production, and fan engagement platforms. Feeds downstream content and broadcast domains.'
}

Table event.production_schedule {
  production_schedule_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the production schedule record. Primary key for the event-day run-of-show execution plan.']
  broadcast_production_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to broadcast.production. Business justification: Event-to-broadcast production coordination: the venue event production schedule (doors open, halftime, player walkout) must be synchronized with the broadcast production plan. Broadcast directors depe']
  broadcast_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to broadcast.broadcast_schedule. Business justification: Event production schedules must be synchronized with broadcast schedules — broadcast go-live times, pre-show windows, and halftime durations are coordinated between venue operations and broadcast team']
  budget_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.budget. Business justification: Live event production (OB trucks, crew, broadcast infrastructure) carries significant budgeted costs. Production managers track budget vs. actual spend per production schedule. This link enables produ']
  capacity_config_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to venue.capacity_config. Business justification: Production scheduling for halftime stage config, broadcast camera positions, and VIP hospitality setup depends directly on the active capacity configuration. Production managers reference capacity_con']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Production costs (broadcast setup, halftime production, venue setup) are allocated to cost centers. Finance teams require production schedule-to-cost-center linkage for operational cost tracking, prod']
  emergency_response_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to security.emergency_response_plan. Business justification: Production schedule contains a \'weather_contingency_plan\' field and broadcast interruption cues that must reference the active emergency response plan. Broadcast suspension, evacuation announcement cu']
  fixture_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent fixture or event for which this production schedule is the execution plan. Links the run-of-show to the event identity master.']
  performer_athlete_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to athlete.athlete_profile. Business justification: Halftime and pre-show performer scheduling requires linking to the athlete/performer\'s profile for NIL agreement verification, broadcast rights clearance, appearance fee processing, and rider fulfillm']
  policy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.policy. Business justification: Production schedules must comply with broadcast standards policies (FCC/Ofcom), safety policies, and content rating policies. Production managers reference the applicable compliance policy when finali']
  run_of_show_asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to content.asset. Business justification: Production schedules reference a run-of-show document stored as a content asset in the DAM. run_of_show_document_url is a denormalized URL; replacing it with a proper FK to content.asset enables versi']
  type_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to event.event_type. Business justification: production_schedule currently stores event_type as a free-text STRING, which is a denormalized reference to the event_type master table. The event_type entity owns the authoritative classification (ev']
  venue_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the venue where the event is being produced. Drives facility-specific logistics, stage configuration, and clearance timelines.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp at which the production schedule received formal approval from the designated approving authority. Distinct from the schedule lock timestamp.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the senior production official or league operations representative who formally approved the locked production schedule.']
  broadcast_go_live_time timestamp [note: 'Scheduled timestamp at which the broadcast feed goes live to air or OTT (Over-The-Top Streaming) platforms. Coordinated with the OB (Outside Broadcast) truck and network partners. Critical for FCC compliance and RSN (Regional Sports Network) scheduling.']
