  changeover_duration_minutes varchar [note: 'Planned duration in minutes for the changeover of the playing surface or stage between the halftime show and the resumption of competition. Critical for league compliance with CBA timing rules.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp at which this production schedule record was first created in the system. Mandatory audit field for data lineage and compliance tracking.']
  doors_open_time timestamp [note: 'Scheduled timestamp at which venue doors open to the public. Drives fan ingress planning, concession staffing, and security deployment timelines.']
  event_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the live event is scheduled to take place. Used for day-of logistics coordination, staffing scheduling, and broadcast go-live alignment.']
  event_end_time timestamp [note: 'Estimated timestamp for the conclusion of the main event. Used for post-event activity planning, venue clearance scheduling, and broadcast window management.']
  event_start_time timestamp [note: 'Scheduled timestamp for the official start of the main event (e.g., opening whistle, first pitch, curtain up). The principal real-world business event timestamp for this production schedule.']
  halftime_duration_minutes varchar [note: 'Planned duration in minutes of the halftime or intermission break. Governed by league CBA (Collective Bargaining Agreement) and broadcast commercial break requirements.']
  halftime_stage_config varchar [note: 'Stage layout configuration for the halftime or intermission performance. Determines field/court protection requirements, rigging points, and changeover time from competition surface. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: center_stage|end_stage|thrust|in_the_round|field_full|court_full|no_stage — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  halftime_start_time timestamp [note: 'Scheduled timestamp for the start of the halftime or intermission break. Drives performer cue, stage crew deployment, and sponsor activation window execution.']
  is_broadcast_live boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the event is being broadcast live (True) or is a tape-delayed or pre-recorded production (False). Affects broadcast go-live coordination and OB truck scheduling.']
  is_ppv boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this event is distributed as a PPV (Pay-Per-View) broadcast. Triggers additional DRM controls, access entitlement validation, and revenue share reporting obligations.']
  media_accreditation_deadline timestamp [note: 'Deadline timestamp by which media representatives must complete accreditation and credential check-in at the venue. Drives press operations and broadcast partner access management.']
  national_anthem_cue_time timestamp [note: 'Scheduled timestamp for the national anthem performance cue. Broadcast-critical timing element coordinated with the OB truck director and league operations.']
  ob_truck_arrival_time timestamp [note: 'Scheduled timestamp for the OB (Outside Broadcast) truck to arrive at the venue for broadcast setup and signal testing. Drives broadcast production crew scheduling and venue loading dock coordination.']
  player_walkout_time timestamp [note: 'Scheduled timestamp for the athlete or team walkout onto the field, court, or stage. Broadcast-critical cue that triggers pre-match ceremony sequences and sponsor LED activations.']
  post_event_start_time timestamp [note: 'Scheduled timestamp for the commencement of post-event activities including trophy presentations, press conferences, fan meet-and-greet, and sponsor hospitality wrap-up.']
  pre_show_start_time timestamp [note: 'Scheduled timestamp for the commencement of pre-show entertainment programming (e.g., opening acts, fan activation zones, national anthem, player warm-up ceremonies).']
  production_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the production context. \'home\' and \'away\' apply to league fixtures; \'neutral_site\' for finals and showcase events; \'studio\' for non-live productions; \'virtual\' for esports or digital-only events.']
  schedule_lock_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp at which the production schedule was formally locked and frozen for game-day execution. No further edits are permitted after this point without an approved change request.']
  schedule_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name or title for this production schedule (e.g., \'NBA Finals Game 5 – Chase Center Run-of-Show v3\'). Used for identification in planning tools and communications.']
  schedule_reference_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric code uniquely identifying this production schedule across operational systems. Used by event production teams, broadcast partners, and venue operations for cross-system coordination.']
  schedule_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the production schedule. Drives workflow routing and edit permissions. \'locked\' indicates the schedule has been frozen for game-day execution. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|pending_approval|approved|locked|in_progress|completed|cancelled — promote to reference product]']
  schedule_version varchar [note: 'Monotonically incrementing version number of the production schedule. Incremented each time the schedule is revised and re-issued. Version 1 is the initial approved plan.']
  security_briefing_time timestamp [note: 'Scheduled timestamp for the pre-event security briefing with venue security, law enforcement liaisons, and event safety officers. Required for OSHA and ADA compliance documentation.']
  sponsor_activation_window_end timestamp [note: 'Scheduled end timestamp for sponsor activation activities. Marks the close of the contractual activation window for fulfilment reporting and ROI (Return on Investment) measurement.']
  sponsor_activation_window_start timestamp [note: 'Scheduled start timestamp for sponsor activation activities within the venue (e.g., branded fan zones, product sampling, LED board takeovers). Linked to sponsorship deal fulfilment obligations.']
  technical_rider_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text summary of the halftime or pre-show performer\'s technical rider requirements including audio specifications, lighting rigs, pyrotechnics, video wall usage, and power requirements.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp at which this production schedule record was most recently modified. Used for change tracking, version audit, and downstream incremental data pipeline processing.']
  venue_clearance_time timestamp [note: 'Scheduled timestamp by which all fans, non-essential staff, and performers must have vacated the venue. Drives security staffing, transport coordination, and venue handback to facility management.']
  venue_setup_start_time timestamp [note: 'Scheduled timestamp for the commencement of pre-event venue setup activities including stage rigging, AV installation, field/court preparation, and sponsor activation builds.']
  vip_hospitality_start_time timestamp [note: 'Scheduled timestamp for the opening of VIP (Very Important Person) hospitality suites and premium experience areas. Drives catering, host staffing, and sponsor entertainment obligations.']
  weather_contingency_plan varchar [note: 'Designated contingency plan for adverse weather conditions applicable to outdoor or open-air venues. Drives communication protocols with fans, broadcasters, and league operations.']

  Note: 'Operational entity managing the game-day/event-day production run-of-show for a fixture. Covers pre-event setup windows, doors-open coordination, pre-show entertainment, halftime/intermission programming (performer, duration, stage configuration, technical rider), post-event activities, and venue clearance timelines. Captures schedule version, production coordinator, broadcast go-live time, OB truck arrival, schedule lock timestamp, and sponsor activation windows. Serves as the execution plan distinct from fixture identity — owned by event production team.'
}

Table event.participant {

  athlete_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to athlete.athlete_profile. Business justification: Lineup management, broadcast feature flagging, appearance fee processing, and eligibility verification all require knowing which athlete_profile is the event participant. entity_id is polymorphic (ent']
  calendar_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent fixture or live event to which this participant is linked. Establishes the core association between participant and event.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Event participants carry appearance_fee_amount requiring cost center allocation for AP processing and talent cost tracking. Finance teams in sports entertainment allocate appearance fees to cost cente']
  eligibility_certification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.eligibility_certification. Business justification: Event participants (athletes, performers) must have verified eligibility certifications before participation is confirmed. event_participant has eligibility_status and eligibility_verified_date fields']
  eligibility_status_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to athlete.eligibility_status. Business justification: Game-day eligibility verification is a mandatory compliance process: leagues must confirm each athlete\'s authoritative eligibility_status record before allowing participation. Replaces the denormalize']
  fixture_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to event.fixture. Business justification: event_participant.event_id is a raw/legacy column referencing the specific fixture to which an athlete, team, or performer is linked. event_participant is an association entity linking participants to']
  franchise_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to league.franchise. Business justification: When event participants are franchises/teams, a direct franchise_id FK enables franchise-level participation reporting, eligibility verification, and revenue attribution. League operations track franc']
  nil_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to athlete.nil_agreement. Business justification: Per-event NIL compliance verification requires knowing which specific nil_agreement governs an athlete\'s appearance rights, broadcast usage, and in-venue activation for that event. The existing nil_ag']
  policy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.policy. Business justification: Participants are governed by specific compliance policies (athlete conduct, NIL, media access). Compliance teams track which policy version applies to each participant\'s event appearance — essential f']
  replacement_participant_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the event_participant record of the entity that replaced this participant following a withdrawal or disqualification. Enables lineage tracking of substitutions and replacements for broadcast and competition governance.']
  season_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the league season or competition season under which this participation record is governed. Supports season-level reporting and fixture calendar alignment.']
  accreditation_code varchar [note: 'Official accreditation or credential code issued to the participant for venue access and event operations purposes. Sourced from the venue access control system (Ticketmaster/AXS). Required for gate access, broadcast zone entry, and VIP area clearance.']
  actual_end_time timestamp [note: 'Actual date and time when the participant\'s competition or performance concluded. Used for duration calculation, broadcast scheduling, and post-event reconciliation.']
  actual_start_time timestamp [note: 'Actual date and time when the participant\'s competition or performance commenced. Compared against scheduled_start_time for operational punctuality reporting and broadcast SLA compliance.']
  appearance_fee_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Contracted appearance fee or performance guarantee amount payable to the participant for this event, denominated in appearance_fee_currency. Confidential financial data. Used for event cost management, EBITDA reporting, and SAP S/4HANA FI/CO reconciliation.']
  appearance_fee_currency varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the appearance fee or performance guarantee payable to the participant for this event. Confidential financial data. Applicable primarily to entertainment performers and exhibition athletes.']
  broadcast_feature_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this participant has been designated as a featured participant for broadcast production purposes (e.g., pre-match spotlight, in-game feature segment, post-match interview). Consumed by Dalet Galaxy broadcast workflow and content production teams.']
  competition_result varchar [note: 'Official outcome of the event for this participant as determined by the officiating body. Win, loss, or draw for standard fixtures; no_contest for abandoned events; disqualified for rule violations; walkover for uncontested advancement. Populated post-event.']
  contract_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the contractual arrangement under which the participant is engaged for this event. CBA (Collective Bargaining Agreement) player contracts are governed by league CBAs; guest_performer applies to entertainment acts; exhibition applies to non-competitive appearances; developmental applies to academy or minor league participants. Confidential business data.']
  crm_contact_reference varchar [note: 'Salesforce CRM contact or account identifier for the participant or their management entity. Enables fan engagement, sponsor activation, and NIL campaign targeting through Adobe Experience Platform and Salesforce CRM.']
  eligibility_verified_date date [note: 'Date on which the participant\'s eligibility for this event was formally verified by the league compliance or event operations team. Supports audit trail requirements under governing body regulations.']
  entity_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the athlete, team, performer, or competing entity participating in the event. Polymorphic identifier resolved by entity_type.']
  entity_type varchar [note: 'Discriminator indicating whether the participant is an individual athlete, a team, a musical performer, a support act, or a wildcard entry. Drives downstream routing to broadcast, ticketing, and fan engagement domains.']
  entry_method varchar [note: 'Method by which the participant qualified or was selected to participate in the event. Direct qualification indicates earned entry through league standings; wildcard indicates discretionary selection; invitation applies to exhibition and all-star events; playoff_seed applies to post-season competition; draft applies to league draft events; bid applies to hosted events. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: direct_qualification|wildcard|invitation|playoff_seed|draft|bid|other — promote to reference product]']
  home_venue_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the event is being held at the participant\'s designated home venue. True for home fixtures; False for away or neutral venue fixtures. Drives ticketing allocation, fan engagement targeting, and revenue attribution.']
  hudl_profile_code varchar [note: 'Unique identifier linking this participant to their performance analytics profile in SportsCode/Hudl. Enables downstream performance video analysis, WAR (Wins Above Replacement), QBR (Quarterback Rating), and ERA (Earned Run Average) metric computation for this event.']
  is_captain_or_lead boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the participant holds the captain designation (sports) or lead performer designation (entertainment) for this specific event. True when the participant is the designated captain or headlining lead.']
  jersey_number varchar [note: 'Official jersey or kit number assigned to the athlete or team representative for this specific event. Applicable to sports fixtures. Null for entertainment events. Sourced from league registration systems.']
  lineup_confirmed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the submitted lineup has been officially confirmed and accepted by the league or event operations team. True when confirmed; False when pending or rejected.']
  lineup_submission_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the official lineup, roster, or set list was formally submitted by the participant\'s management or coaching staff to the league or event organiser. Critical for compliance with pre-match lineup deadlines mandated by governing bodies.']
  media_rights_clearance_status varchar [note: 'Status of media rights clearance for this participant\'s appearance in the event. Cleared indicates full broadcast and digital distribution rights are confirmed; pending indicates rights are under negotiation; restricted indicates partial rights only; blocked indicates no broadcast rights. Consumed by content and broadcast domains for DRM (Digital Rights Management) enforcement.']
  nationality_country varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code representing the nationality of the participant or the country of registration for a team. Used for international competition eligibility, governing body reporting, and broadcast rights geo-targeting.']
  nil_agreement_active boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the participant has an active NIL (Name, Image and Likeness) agreement in effect at the time of this event. Relevant for collegiate and amateur participants. Drives content rights, merchandise licensing, and sponsorship activation restrictions.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text operational notes recorded by event operations staff regarding this participant\'s involvement in the event. May include special requirements, late changes, officiating observations, or post-event reconciliation comments.']
  participant_role varchar [note: 'Designates the competitive or performance role of the participant within the event. For sports fixtures: home_team or away_team. For concerts and live experiences: headliner, support_act. Wildcard and neutral apply to tournament and exhibition contexts.']
  participation_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the participant\'s involvement in the event. Confirmed indicates full clearance; withdrawn indicates voluntary or involuntary removal; suspended reflects disciplinary action per league CBA or governing body ruling; TBD (To Be Determined) indicates pending confirmation; disqualified reflects post-event ruling; pending_review indicates an active compliance or eligibility review. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: confirmed|withdrawn|suspended|tbd|disqualified|pending_review — promote to reference product]']
  performance_slot_order varchar [note: 'Sequential order in which the participant is scheduled to perform or compete within the event programme. For concerts: set list order. For multi-bout events: bout sequence. Null for single-match sports fixtures.']
  ranking_points_earned decimal(10,2) [note: 'Official ranking or standing points awarded to the participant as a result of their performance in this event. Used to update league tables, world rankings, or tournament standings. Governed by the relevant league or governing body points allocation rules.']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this event participant record was first created in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and GDPR data subject request processing.']
  record_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this event participant record was most recently modified. Supports incremental data processing, change data capture, and audit trail requirements in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  scheduled_start_time timestamp [note: 'Planned date and time when the participant\'s competition bout, match, or performance set is scheduled to begin. Distinct from the overall event start time when multiple participants perform sequentially.']
  score varchar [note: 'Final score, points, or goals recorded for this participant in the event. Applicable to sports fixtures. Null for entertainment events. Sourced from official match data systems.']
  seeding_rank varchar [note: 'Numeric seeding rank assigned to the participant for tournament bracket placement or competition draw purposes. Lower values indicate higher seeding. Null for non-seeded events such as regular season fixtures or concerts.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the operational system of record from which this participant record was ingested into the Silver Layer. Supports data lineage, reconciliation, and master data management across SAP S/4HANA, Ticketmaster/AXS, Salesforce CRM, Dalet Galaxy, Archtics, and SportsCode/Hudl. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: SAP_S4HANA|TICKETMASTER|SALESFORCE_CRM|SUCCESSFACTORS|DALET_GALAXY|ARCHTICS|HUDL|MANUAL — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  travel_origin_country varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code representing the country from which the participant is travelling to the event. Used for logistics planning, visa and work permit compliance, and international governing body reporting.']
  uniform_color_code varchar [note: 'Designated uniform or kit color code for the participant in this event (e.g., HOME, AWAY, THIRD, ALTERNATE). Ensures broadcast production and officiating clarity. Governed by league uniform regulations.']
  withdrawal_reason varchar [note: 'Categorised reason for participant withdrawal from the event. Populated only when participation_status is withdrawn or disqualified. Supports injury reporting, disciplinary tracking, and force majeure documentation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: injury|suspension|personal_reasons|scheduling_conflict|disqualification|force_majeure|other — promote to reference product]']
  withdrawal_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the participant\'s withdrawal or disqualification was officially recorded. Null when participation_status is not withdrawn or disqualified. Used for downstream broadcast, ticketing, and fan notification workflows.']

  Note: 'Association entity linking athletes, teams, performers, or competing entities to a specific fixture. Captures participant role (home team, away team, headliner, support act, wildcard entry), seeding rank, participation status (confirmed, withdrawn, suspended, TBD), lineup submission timestamp, and captain/lead performer designation. Serves as the SSOT for who is participating in each event — consumed by broadcast, ticketing, content, and fan engagement domains.'
}

Table event.officiating_assignment {
  officiating_assignment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each officiating assignment record in the Silver layer lakehouse. Primary key for this transactional entity.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Officiating assignments carry explicit fee amounts (officiating_fee_amount, expense_allowance_amount) that must be allocated to a cost center for AP processing and cost tracking. Finance teams in spor']
  credential_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to security.credential. Business justification: Officials assigned to fixtures must have valid credentials issued for restricted zone access. Officiating_assignment must reference the credential record to enable pre-match credential verification wo']
  eligibility_certification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.eligibility_certification. Business justification: Officials must hold valid eligibility certifications (referee licensing, governing body certification) before assignment confirmation. certification_level and certification_expiry_date are denormalize']
  fixture_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific fixture or event to which the official is assigned. Links this assignment to the event master data.']
  investigation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.investigation. Business justification: Officiating assignments subject to corruption, misconduct, or conflict-of-interest allegations require a formal compliance investigation. The assignment record\'s `disciplinary_incident_flag` and `conf']
  league_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the league or competition governing body under whose jurisdiction this officiating assignment is made.']
  primary_officiating_official_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the match official (referee, linesman, VAR operator, TMO, judge, or scorer) being assigned to the fixture.']
  season_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the competitive season within which this officiating assignment falls, supporting season-level officiating analytics and compliance reporting.']
  venue_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the venue where the assigned fixture will take place, used for logistics planning, travel arrangements, and venue-specific officiating protocols.']
  assignment_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which the officiating assignment was formally issued by the governing body or league officiating department.']
  assignment_method varchar [note: 'The method by which the official was assigned to this fixture — manual selection by officiating coordinator, automated rotation algorithm, rotational schedule, direct appointment by governing body, or emergency replacement.']
  assignment_reference_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric reference code for this officiating assignment, used in league governance communications, official notifications, and compliance audit trails.']
  assignment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the officiating assignment, tracking the workflow from initial proposal through confirmation, acceptance, or withdrawal. Supports governance and scheduling workflows.']
  confirmation_date date [note: 'The date on which the assigned official formally confirmed acceptance of the assignment. Null if not yet confirmed.']
  conflict_of_interest_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a potential conflict of interest has been identified for this official in relation to the assigned fixture (e.g., prior affiliation with a competing team, financial interest). True = conflict identified; False = no conflict.']
  conflict_of_interest_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text description of the nature of the identified conflict of interest, if applicable. Populated only when conflict_of_interest_flag is True. Treated as confidential governance data.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this officiating assignment record was first created in the system, providing the audit trail creation marker.']
  crew_chief_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this official is designated as the crew chief or lead referee for the fixture. True = crew chief; False = supporting official.']
  disciplinary_incident_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a disciplinary incident or formal complaint was raised in connection with this officiating assignment. True = incident recorded; False = no incident. Triggers governance review workflow.']
  expense_allowance_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'The approved expense allowance payable to the official for travel, accommodation, and per diem costs associated with this assignment, per governing body policy.']
  fee_currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the officiating fee amount (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Supports multi-currency operations across global leagues.']
  governing_body_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the sports governing body or league office responsible for this officiating assignment and the applicable officiating standards (e.g., FIFA, NFL, NBA, NHL, MLB, MLS). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: FIFA|NFL|NBA|NHL|MLB|MLS|UFC|ATP|IOC|CAS — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional operational notes related to this officiating assignment, such as special instructions from the governing body, venue-specific protocols, or crew coordination details.']
  notification_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time at which the official was notified of the assignment, supporting SLA compliance tracking for officiating notification windows.']
  official_role varchar [note: 'The specific officiating role assigned to the official for this fixture (e.g., Referee, Assistant Referee, Fourth Official, VAR Operator, TMO, Linesman, Judge, Scorer, Video Review Official). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: referee|assistant_referee|fourth_official|var_operator|tmo|linesman|judge|scorer|video_review_official|head_linesman|back_judge|umpire — promote to reference product]']
  officiating_crew_size varchar [note: 'The total number of officials assigned to the fixture as part of the officiating crew, including all roles (referee, assistants, VAR, TMO, etc.).']
  officiating_fee_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'The gross fee payable to the official for this assignment, as defined by the governing body\'s officiating pay scale or collective bargaining agreement (CBA). Expressed in the league\'s base currency.']
  performance_rating decimal(4,2) [note: 'Numeric performance rating assigned to the official for this fixture by the officiating assessor or governing body evaluator, typically on a standardized scale (e.g., 1.00–10.00). Supports officiating quality management and career development.']
  post_match_report_date date [note: 'The date on which the post-match officiating report was submitted by the official. Null if not yet submitted.']
  post_match_report_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier or document number for the post-match officiating report submitted by the lead official after the fixture. Links to the report stored in the DAM or document management system.']
  post_match_report_submitted_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the post-match officiating report has been submitted by the official following the fixture. True = submitted; False = pending. Supports compliance monitoring.']
  pre_match_briefing_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The scheduled date and time of the pre-match officiating crew briefing, where the lead referee reviews match protocols, VAR/TMO procedures, and competition-specific directives with the crew.']
  replacement_reason varchar [note: 'The reason an official was replaced or withdrew from this assignment. Populated only when assignment_status is \'replaced\' or \'withdrawn\'. Supports officiating continuity and governance reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: injury|illness|conflict_of_interest|scheduling_conflict|disciplinary|withdrawal|other — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  tmo_enabled_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a Television Match Official (TMO) is active for this fixture, applicable in rugby and other sports using TMO review protocols.']
  travel_origin_city varchar [note: 'The city from which the official will travel to the fixture venue, used for logistics planning and travel cost estimation.']
  travel_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the official requires travel arrangements (flights, accommodation) to attend the assigned fixture. Supports logistics and expense management.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this officiating assignment record was last modified, supporting change tracking and audit compliance.']
  var_enabled_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether Video Assistant Referee (VAR) technology is active for this fixture, determining whether a VAR operator role is required in the officiating crew.']
  wada_compliance_verified_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the assigned official\'s WADA compliance status (anti-doping clearance) has been verified prior to the fixture. Applicable for international competitions. True = verified; False = not verified.']

  Note: 'Transactional entity managing the assignment of match officials — referees, linesmen, VAR operators, TMO (Television Match Official), judges, and scorers — to specific fixtures. Captures official role, assignment date, confirmation status, governing body certification level, conflict-of-interest flag, and post-match officiating report reference. Supports league governance, compliance with officiating standards (FIFA, NFL, NBA), and VAR/TMO review workflows.'
}

Table event.event_incident {
  event_incident_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each in-event incident record. Primary key for the event_incident data product in the Silver Layer lakehouse.']
  disciplinary_action_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to league.disciplinary_action. Business justification: Incident-to-disciplinary-ruling traceability is a named regulatory requirement. League integrity officers and governing bodies require a direct link from the originating incident record to the resulti']
  investigation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.investigation. Business justification: Event incidents (crowd violence, player misconduct, doping-related incidents) trigger formal compliance investigations. The `regulatory_report_submitted` flag on event_incident confirms this business ']
  live_feed_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to broadcast.live_feed. Business justification: Incidents occurring during live broadcasts require referencing the live feed for broadcast delay investigation, signal integrity reporting, and regulatory compliance. event_incident.broadcast_delay_mi']
  match_result_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to event.match_result. Business justification: Event incidents (red cards, VAR reviews, crowd disturbances, injuries) are operationally linked to the official match result for disciplinary reporting, regulatory submission, and post-match reconcili']
  media_asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to broadcast.media_asset. Business justification: VAR reviews, red cards, and on-field incidents are directly linked to broadcast media assets (VAR footage, highlight clips). Incident investigation, broadcast compliance reporting, and content rights ']
  official_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to league.official. Business justification: Incidents involving match officials (assaults, threats, interference) require direct official attribution for disciplinary reporting, official safety tracking, and governing body regulatory submission']
  primary_event_athlete_athlete_profile_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the athlete directly involved in the incident (e.g., player receiving a red card, injured athlete). Null for non-athlete incidents such as crowd disturbances or equipment failures. Links to the athlete master data product.']
  primary_event_fixture_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the parent live sports or entertainment event during which this incident occurred. Links to the event master data product.']
  regulatory_filing_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_filing. Business justification: Event incidents requiring regulatory reporting generate specific regulatory filings with governing bodies. The `regulatory_reporting_required` and `regulatory_report_submitted` attributes confirm this']
  seating_section_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to venue.seating_section. Business justification: Incident regulatory reporting, insurance claims, and ADA compliance investigations require precise seating section identification. location_section is a denormalized text field; a proper FK to seating']
  security_incident_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to security.security_incident. Business justification: Event incidents with security dimensions (pitch invasions, crowd disturbances, player altercations) must reference the corresponding security incident record for cross-domain regulatory reporting, ins']
  suspension_record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to athlete.suspension_record. Business justification: Disciplinary workflow requires tracing an on-field incident (violent conduct, doping detection) directly to the resulting suspension record for regulatory filings, league disciplinary hearings, and in']
  venue_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the venue or facility where the incident took place. Supports venue operations reporting and ADA/OSHA compliance tracking.']
  broadcast_delay_minutes varchar [note: 'Duration in minutes that the live broadcast was delayed or interrupted as a result of this incident. Zero if no broadcast delay occurred. Supports RSN and OTT rights holder SLA reporting and FCC compliance.']
  broadcast_impact boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the incident caused a disruption to live broadcast operations (signal loss, unplanned cut-away, OB unit failure, broadcast delay). Triggers notification to broadcast rights holders and Dalet Galaxy media workflow.']
  created_at timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this incident record was first created in the system. Audit trail field for data lineage, Silver Layer ingestion tracking, and compliance record retention.']
  detected_at timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the incident was formally detected and logged by operations staff or automated monitoring systems. May differ from occurred_at when there is a reporting lag. Used to measure detection latency for SLA compliance.']
  fan_impact_count varchar [note: 'Estimated number of fans directly affected by the incident (e.g., evacuated, medically treated, displaced from seating). Used for post-event fan experience reporting, NPS impact analysis, and insurance liability assessment.']
  field_surface_condition varchar [note: 'Assessed condition of the playing surface or field at the time of the incident. Populated for field/surface condition change incidents and weather delays. Supports governing body match-day inspection reporting and player safety compliance. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: playable|unplayable|waterlogged|frozen|damaged|under_repair|acceptable|not_applicable — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  game_delay_duration_minutes varchar [note: 'Total duration in minutes that the event or match was delayed or suspended as a direct result of this incident. Zero if no delay occurred. Critical for broadcast scheduling, insurance claims, and governing body reporting.']
  incident_description varchar [note: 'Free-text narrative description of the incident, capturing contextual details not covered by structured fields. Authored by the reporting operations staff member at time of logging. Used in post-event reports, insurance documentation, and governing body submissions.']
  incident_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the incident record, tracking workflow from initial detection through resolution and post-event closure. Drives operational dashboards and compliance reporting completeness checks.']
  incident_type varchar [note: 'Categorical classification of the incident nature. Covers the full spectrum of in-event operational incidents. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: red_card|ejection|player_injury|crowd_disturbance|equipment_failure|weather_delay|power_outage|medical_emergency|field_condition_change|security_breach|fire_alarm|structural_concern|broadcast_failure|protest|other — promote to reference product]']
  insurance_claim_reference varchar [note: 'External reference number assigned by the insurance carrier for the claim associated with this incident. Populated once a claim is formally opened. Confidential business data used for financial reconciliation in SAP S/4HANA.']
  insurance_claim_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this incident is expected to generate an insurance claim (event cancellation, liability, workers compensation, medical). Triggers insurance documentation workflow and financial provisioning in SAP S/4HANA FI.']
  location_zone varchar [note: 'Designated zone or area within the venue where the incident occurred (e.g., playing_field, north_stand, concourse_level_2, media_tribune, locker_room, parking_lot, broadcast_compound). Supports venue operations analysis and ADA/OSHA compliance mapping.']
  match_clock_time_seconds varchar [note: 'Elapsed match or event clock time in seconds at the moment the incident occurred. Enables precise temporal correlation with broadcast footage, VAR/TMO review, and performance analytics in SportsCode/Hudl. Null for non-competition events (concerts, ceremonies).']
  match_period varchar [note: 'Designated period or segment of the event during which the incident occurred. Supports competition governance reporting and broadcast clip indexing. \'full_event\' used for non-segmented live experiences such as concerts. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: pre_match|1st_half|2nd_half|extra_time_1|extra_time_2|penalty_shootout|intermission|post_match|full_event — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  media_coverage_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the incident received external media coverage (broadcast, digital, print). Triggers communications and PR response workflow. Supports reputation management and sponsor impact assessment.']
  occurred_at timestamp [note: 'Wall-clock timestamp (ISO 8601 with timezone offset) recording the precise real-world moment the incident was first observed or reported. Principal business event timestamp for the incident lifecycle. Distinct from match clock time.']
  ped_related boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the incident involves a suspected or confirmed Performance Enhancing Drug (PED) violation or related medical event. Triggers mandatory WADA reporting workflow and CAS arbitration tracking.']
  precipitation_mm decimal(6,2) [note: 'Recorded precipitation level in millimetres at the time of the incident. Supports weather-delay documentation, field/surface condition assessment, and insurance claim substantiation.']
  reference_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric reference code assigned to the incident at time of occurrence, used for cross-system tracking, insurance documentation, and regulatory reporting. Format: INC-{YYYY}-{NNNNNN}.']
  regulatory_report_submitted boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the required regulatory report has been formally submitted to the relevant governing body or authority. Null when regulatory_reporting_required is False. Supports compliance audit and SLA tracking.']
  regulatory_report_submitted_at timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the regulatory report was formally submitted to the governing body or authority. Null if not yet submitted. Used to verify compliance with mandatory reporting deadlines (e.g., OSHA 8-hour fatality reporting window).']
  regulatory_reporting_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this incident triggers a mandatory regulatory reporting obligation to an external governing body or regulatory authority (OSHA, FIFA, NFL, NBA, WADA, FCC, etc.). Drives automated compliance workflow routing in post-event reconciliation.']
  resolution_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes documenting the resolution actions taken, outcome achieved, and any follow-up actions required. Populated at incident closure. Supports post-event reconciliation, lessons-learned analysis, and insurance claim documentation.']
  resolved_at timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the incident was operationally resolved and normal event operations resumed or the incident was formally closed. Null if incident remains open. Used to calculate response duration and SLA adherence.']
  response_action_taken varchar [note: 'Structured description of the primary operational response action executed in reaction to the incident (e.g., medical_evacuation, play_suspended, crowd_dispersal, equipment_replaced, power_restored, venue_section_cleared, VAR_review_initiated). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: medical_evacuation|play_suspended|crowd_dispersal|equipment_replaced|power_restored|venue_section_cleared|var_review_initiated|match_abandoned|emergency_services_called|shelter_in_place — promote to reference product]']
  response_team_type varchar [note: 'Category of the primary response team deployed to address the incident. Supports post-event resource utilization analysis and emergency preparedness planning. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: medical|security|fire_safety|technical|operations|governing_body|external_emergency_services|broadcast — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  severity_level varchar [note: 'Operational severity rating assigned to the incident at time of occurrence, driving escalation protocols, response resource allocation, and regulatory reporting obligations. Critical = life-safety or event-stopping; High = significant operational impact; Medium = notable but manageable; Low = minor; Informational = logged for record only.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record from which this incident record originated or was ingested into the Silver Layer. Supports data lineage, reconciliation, and Silver Layer provenance tracking. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: venue_ops|ticketmaster|salesforce_crm|sap_s4hana|manual_entry|broadcast_system|security_system — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  subtype varchar [note: 'Secondary classification providing granular detail within the incident type (e.g., within \'weather_delay\': lightning, extreme_heat, heavy_rain, high_wind; within \'player_injury\': concussion, fracture, laceration). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: lightning|extreme_heat|heavy_rain|high_wind|concussion|fracture|laceration|cardiac|crowd_surge|pitch_invasion — promote to reference product]']
  temperature_celsius decimal(5,2) [note: 'Recorded ambient air temperature in degrees Celsius at the venue at the time of the incident. Critical for heat-related medical incidents, weather-delay justification, and OSHA heat illness prevention compliance.']
  updated_at timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this incident record. Supports change data capture (CDC) in the Databricks Silver Layer and audit trail maintenance for compliance reporting.']
  var_tmo_outcome varchar [note: 'Result of the Video Assistant Referee (VAR) or Television Match Official (TMO) review triggered by this incident. Null when var_tmo_review_triggered is False. Supports competition governance reporting and post-match analysis.']
  var_tmo_review_triggered boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the incident triggered a Video Assistant Referee (VAR) or Television Match Official (TMO) review process. Applicable to football, rugby, and other sports with video review protocols. Supports competition governance and SportsCode/Hudl video analysis integration.']
  weather_condition varchar [note: 'Prevailing weather condition at the venue at the time the incident occurred. Mandatory for weather-related incidents (lightning, extreme heat, heavy rain); populated for all outdoor events. \'indoor_na\' for fully enclosed venues. Supports insurance documentation and governing body weather-delay reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: clear|cloudy|rain|heavy_rain|thunderstorm|lightning|snow|fog|extreme_heat|high_wind|hail|indoor_na — 12 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  wind_speed_kmh decimal(6,2) [note: 'Recorded wind speed in kilometres per hour at the venue at the time of the incident. Used for weather-delay justification, structural safety assessments, and insurance documentation.']

  Note: 'Transactional entity recording significant in-event incidents requiring operational response — red cards, ejections, injuries requiring stoppage, crowd disturbances, equipment failures, weather delays (lightning, extreme heat), power outages, medical emergencies, and field/surface condition changes. Captures incident type, match clock time, severity level, response action taken, weather conditions at time of incident, regulatory reporting obligation flag (OSHA, governing body), resolution timestamp, and game-delay duration if applicable. Supports post-event reporting, safety compliance, and insurance documentation.'
}

Table event.venue_assignment {
  venue_assignment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the event-to-venue assignment record. Primary key for this association entity.']
  budget_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.budget. Business justification: Venue assignments carry rental fees, setup costs, and revenue share obligations (rental_fee_amount, revenue_share_pct). Linking to a budget enables venue cost budget vs. actual tracking per event — es']
  calendar_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the fixture or live event being assigned to a venue. Links to the event master record.']
  capacity_config_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to venue.capacity_config. Business justification: When assigning a venue to an event, a specific capacity configuration (fire-code-approved sellable capacity, seating mode, ADA compliance) must be selected. This drives ticketing inventory allocation,']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Venue assignments include rental_fee_amount and revenue_share_pct — costs requiring cost center allocation. Finance teams track venue rental costs per cost center for facilities expense reporting, bud']
  emergency_response_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to security.emergency_response_plan. Business justification: Venue assignment confirmation for an event — especially neutral sites — triggers selection of the applicable emergency response plan. Regulatory requirements for large public gatherings mandate that t']
  fixture_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to event.fixture. Business justification: event_venue_assignment.event_id is a raw/legacy column referencing the specific fixture for which a venue is being assigned. The entity description states it links \'a fixture to its assigned venue.\' T']
  policy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.policy. Business justification: Venue assignments must comply with ADA, safety, and operational policies. The `ada_compliant` and `sustainability_certification` attributes signal policy applicability — venue operations teams referen']
  primary_venue_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the venue facility being assigned to host the event. Links to the venue master record.']
  ada_accessible_seat_count varchar [note: 'Number of ADA-compliant accessible seats allocated for this event at this venue configuration. Used by ticketing domain to configure accessible inventory in Archtics and ensure compliance with ADA minimum accessible seating ratios.']
  ada_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether ADA accessibility compliance has been formally confirmed for this event at this venue, including accessible seating, ramps, restrooms, and concession access. Required for regulatory compliance reporting and venue operations preparation.']
  approved_capacity varchar [note: 'Maximum number of attendees approved for this specific event at this venue, reflecting the capacity configuration type. May differ from the venue\'s total rated capacity due to event-specific restrictions, safety requirements, or reduced-capacity mandates. Used by ticketing domain to cap inventory in Archtics and Ticketmaster/AXS.']
  assignment_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for this venue assignment, used in operational communications, venue operations work orders, and ticketing system configuration (e.g., Archtics seating inventory setup). Format: EVA- followed by 4-12 alphanumeric characters.']
  assignment_effective_date date [note: 'Calendar date from which this venue assignment is operationally effective. Venue operations teams use this date to begin facility preparation scheduling, and ticketing teams use it to open inventory configuration in Archtics.']
  assignment_expiry_date date [note: 'Calendar date after which this venue assignment is no longer valid. Used for provisional or alternate assignments that have a defined validity window. Null for confirmed primary assignments with no expiry.']
  assignment_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text operational notes related to this venue assignment, capturing special conditions, exceptions, or instructions for venue operations, ticketing, and broadcast teams that are not captured in structured fields.']
  assignment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the venue assignment. \'proposed\' indicates initial planning stage; \'confirmed\' indicates venue is locked and ticketing/operations may proceed; \'provisional\' indicates conditional confirmation pending contract or regulatory approval; \'cancelled\' indicates the assignment has been voided; \'superseded\' indicates replaced by a newer assignment record.']
  broadcast_infrastructure_ready boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the venue\'s broadcast infrastructure (OB (Outside Broadcast) unit connections, fiber feeds, camera positions, commentary positions) has been confirmed as ready for this event. Used by broadcast operations and Dalet Galaxy workflow planning.']
  change_history_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this venue assignment record has been modified or superseded since initial creation, signaling that a prior assignment existed for this event. Used for audit trail tracking and downstream notification to venue operations and ticketing teams of venue changes.']
  change_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text description of the reason for a venue assignment change, such as venue unavailability, weather conditions, safety concerns, league mandate, or contractual renegotiation. Populated when change_history_flag is True.']
  confirmation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the venue assignment was formally confirmed, triggering downstream venue operations preparation and ticketing inventory configuration in Archtics. Null if assignment is still in proposed or provisional state.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this venue assignment record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail requirements and data lineage tracking per SOX financial controls.']
  designation_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether this venue is the primary assigned venue, an alternate venue held in reserve, a neutral-site venue (neither team\'s home ground, common in playoff or championship contexts), or a backup venue activated only if the primary is unavailable.']
  event_date date [note: 'Scheduled calendar date of the event at this venue. Denormalized here to support venue operations scheduling and facility resource planning without requiring a join to the event master record.']
  field_setup_type varchar [note: 'Specifies the physical configuration of the playing surface, court, or stage for this event. Determines sightline mapping, seating kill zones, and production logistics. \'standard\' applies to normal league-regulation field/court layout; concert and stage variants apply to live entertainment events. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: standard|concert_stage|in_the_round|end_stage|thrust_stage|custom|floor_only|half_house — promote to reference product]']
  ga_section_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether General Admission (GA) standing sections are enabled for this event at this venue. Affects ticketing inventory configuration, crowd management planning, and OSHA safety compliance for standing areas.']
  gate_open_time timestamp [note: 'Scheduled date and time when venue gates open to the public for this event. Used by venue operations, security staffing, and access control configuration in Ticketmaster/AXS gate scan systems.']
  load_in_date date [note: 'Date on which production equipment, staging, and event infrastructure begins loading into the venue. Critical for venue operations scheduling, HVAC (Heating Ventilation and Air Conditioning) activation, and BMS (Building Management System) configuration.']
  load_out_date date [note: 'Date by which all production equipment and event infrastructure must be removed from the venue. Used for venue turnaround scheduling and back-to-back event planning.']
  media_rights_zone varchar [note: 'Geographic media rights zone classification for this venue assignment, determining which RSN (Regional Sports Network), OTT (Over-The-Top Streaming), or broadcast network holds distribution rights for events at this venue. Used in broadcast rights management and content distribution.']
  neutral_site_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this venue assignment represents a neutral-site event where neither competing team or performer has a home-ground advantage. Common in playoff games, championship events, bowl games, and international fixtures. Affects revenue-sharing, ticketing allocation, and broadcast rights.']
  parking_capacity varchar [note: 'Total number of parking spaces available at or designated for this venue for this event. Used in fan experience planning, transportation logistics, and ancillary revenue forecasting.']
  rental_fee_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Contracted rental fee payable to the venue operator for hosting this event. Used in event P&L (Profit and Loss) analysis and financial planning in Workday Adaptive Planning. Classified as confidential due to commercial sensitivity.']
  rental_fee_currency varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the venue rental fee amount (e.g., USD, GBP, EUR). Required for multi-currency financial reporting and SAP S/4HANA FI integration.']
  revenue_share_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of event gross revenue contractually shared with the venue operator, covering concessions, parking, and ancillary revenue streams. Used in financial planning and EBITDA (Earnings Before Interest Taxes Depreciation and Amortization) modeling.']
  scheduled_start_time timestamp [note: 'Precise scheduled start date and time of the event at this venue, including timezone offset. Used by venue operations for gate opening scheduling, broadcast production (OB unit deployment), and fan communications.']
  seating_manifest_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the seating manifest (kill zones, accessible sections, GA (General Admission) areas, SRO (Standing Room Only) sections) configured in Archtics for this event-venue assignment. Tracks changes to seating layout between events at the same venue.']
  security_level varchar [note: 'Designated security threat level for this event at this venue, driving staffing levels, screening protocols, and law enforcement coordination. Levels align with internal security classification framework and OSHA venue safety requirements.']
  surface_type varchar [note: 'Type of playing surface configured for this event at this venue. Relevant for athlete safety compliance, league regulations (e.g., NFL CBA surface requirements), and broadcast production planning. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: natural_grass|artificial_turf|hardwood|ice|clay|grass|carpet|concrete|synthetic_clay — promote to reference product]']
  sustainability_certification varchar [note: 'ISO 20121 or equivalent sustainability certification status for this venue assignment, documenting environmental management compliance for the event. Supports ESG (Environmental Social Governance) reporting and league sustainability mandates.']
  ticketing_system_venue_code varchar [note: 'Venue identifier code as configured in the ticketing platform (Ticketmaster/AXS or Archtics) for this specific event assignment. Used to map seating inventory, price tiers, and access control zones to the correct venue configuration.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this venue assignment record was last modified. Used for change detection, incremental data pipeline processing, and audit trail compliance.']
  venue_contract_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number of the venue rental or usage contract governing this assignment. Links to the contract management system for financial terms, liability clauses, and cancellation provisions. Classified as confidential due to commercial sensitivity.']
  vip_hospitality_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether VIP (Very Important Person) hospitality suites, premium clubs, and sponsor hospitality entitlements are activated for this event at this venue. Triggers hospitality operations preparation and sponsor entitlement fulfillment workflows.']
  weather_contingency_plan varchar [note: 'Reference identifier or description of the weather contingency plan applicable to this outdoor or partially-covered venue assignment. Includes rain delay protocols, lightning evacuation procedures, and postponement triggers.']

  Note: 'Association entity linking a fixture to its assigned venue, capturing venue confirmation status, primary/alternate venue designation, venue configuration type (full capacity, reduced capacity, neutral site), ADA compliance confirmation, field/court/stage setup type, and assignment change history flag. Enables venue operations domain to prepare facility resources and ticketing domain to configure seating inventory for the specific event.'
}

  conference_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to league.conference. Business justification: Competition rounds are scoped to conferences (e.g., AFC Championship Round).
