  league_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the governing league or competition body that owns this round (e.g., NFL, NBA, FIFA, MLS). Enables cross-league reporting and governance.']
  media_rights_deal_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to broadcast.media_rights_deal. Business justification: Round-level rights compliance: competition rounds are governed by specific media rights deals that determine broadcast windows, blackout rules, and PPV eligibility. Rights compliance teams need round-']
  playoff_bracket_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to league.playoff_bracket. Business justification: Playoff bracket advancement tracking requires linking competition rounds to the specific playoff bracket they belong to. League operations use this for seeding updates, advancement rules enforcement, ']
  price_tier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ticketing.price_tier. Business justification: Ticket pricing varies by competition round — quarterfinals and finals command premium tiers vs. group stages. Pricing teams configure round-specific price tiers for dynamic revenue management. No exis']
  season_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent season to which this competition round belongs. Links the round to its governing season structure for standings and scheduling.']
  tournament_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent tournament to which this competition round belongs. Nullable when the round belongs to a regular season rather than a tournament bracket.']
  venue_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to venue.venue. Business justification: Playoff and tournament round venue assignment is a core scheduling and broadcast operations process. Sports-entertainment operations assign specific venues to competition rounds (e.g., conference fina']
  actual_end_date date [note: 'Actual calendar date on which the last fixture of this round concluded. May differ from scheduled_end_date. Used for post-event reconciliation, standings finalization, and broadcast rights settlement.']
  actual_start_date date [note: 'Actual calendar date on which the first fixture of this round commenced. May differ from scheduled_start_date due to postponements, weather events, or force majeure. Used for post-event reconciliation and compliance reporting.']
  advancement_rule varchar [note: 'Business rule description governing how teams or athletes advance from this round to the next stage (e.g., \'Top 2 teams advance\', \'Winner advances to Semi-Final\', \'Best 4 wild card records qualify\'). Used by bracket progression logic and standings engines.']
  age_category varchar [note: 'Age group classification for the competition round (e.g., Senior, U23, U21, U18, Masters). Governs eligibility rules and is required for youth and age-restricted competitions.']
  broadcast_window_end timestamp [note: 'End timestamp of the broadcast rights window for this competition round. Marks the expiry of live and near-live broadcast entitlements for OTT, RSN, and linear TV distribution.']
  broadcast_window_start timestamp [note: 'Start timestamp of the broadcast rights window for this competition round. Used by Dalet Galaxy and broadcast rights management to schedule OTT, RSN, and linear TV distribution slots.']
  bye_week_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this round is designated as a bye week or rest round in which some or all teams do not compete. Common in NFL scheduling. Affects standings calculation and fixture generation logic.']
  completed_fixture_count varchar [note: 'Number of fixtures within this round that have been fully played and results confirmed. Used to track round completion progress and trigger standings recalculation workflows.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this competition round record was first created in the Sports Entertainment data platform. Used for audit trail, data lineage, and SOX compliance reporting.']
  external_round_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier for this competition round as assigned by an external governing body or source system (e.g., FIFA round ID, NFL schedule system ID, Ticketmaster event group ID). Used for cross-system reconciliation and API integration.']
  fixture_count varchar [note: 'Total number of fixtures (matches, games, bouts) scheduled within this competition round. Used for scheduling validation, broadcast slot planning, and venue operations capacity planning.']
  gender_category varchar [note: 'Gender category of the competition round (e.g., men\'s, women\'s, mixed, open). Required for multi-gender leagues and international competitions governed by FIFA, IOC, and ATP standards.']
  neutral_venue_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether all fixtures in this round are played at a neutral venue rather than home/away venues (e.g., Super Bowl, FIFA World Cup Final, NBA All-Star Game). Affects ticketing allocation and venue operations planning.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text operational notes or administrative remarks about this competition round, such as special scheduling considerations, venue exceptions, regulatory waivers, or broadcast anomalies. For internal use by league operations staff.']
  overtime_rule varchar [note: 'Rule governing how drawn or tied fixtures in this round are resolved beyond regulation time (e.g., extra time followed by penalty shootout in FIFA knockout rounds, overtime period in NHL, shootout in NHL regular season). Affects fixture duration planning and broadcast scheduling.']
  points_for_draw varchar [note: 'Number of competition points awarded to each team for a draw/tie in this round. Typically 1 in soccer/football formats; 0 in elimination formats. Drives standings calculation.']
  points_for_loss varchar [note: 'Number of competition points awarded to a team or athlete for a loss in this round. Typically 0, but some leagues award 1 point for an overtime/shootout loss (e.g., NHL). Drives standings calculation.']
  points_for_win varchar [note: 'Number of competition points awarded to a team or athlete for a win in this round. May vary by stage (e.g., 3 points in group stage, not applicable in knockout). Drives standings calculation.']
  ppv_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether fixtures in this round are distributed as Pay-Per-View (PPV) events rather than included in standard broadcast or subscription packages. Affects revenue recognition and fan access entitlement logic.']
  round_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric business identifier for the competition round used in scheduling systems, broadcast feeds, and fixture APIs (e.g., NFL-2024-WK01, FIFA-WC-QF1, NBA-2024-R1). Unique within a season or tournament.']
  round_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable display name of the competition round as used in broadcast, ticketing, and fan-facing platforms (e.g., \'Week 1\', \'Matchday 3\', \'Quarter-Final\', \'Wild Card Round\', \'Conference Championship\', \'Super Bowl\', \'Group Stage Round 2\').']
  round_number varchar [note: 'Sequential numeric position of this round within its parent season or tournament (e.g., 1 for Week 1, 3 for Matchday 3). Used for ordering, standings calculation, and bracket progression logic.']
  round_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the competition round. Drives fixture scheduling workflows, standings publication, and broadcast planning. Transitions from scheduled → in_progress → completed, or to postponed/cancelled/suspended for disrupted rounds.']
  round_type varchar [note: 'Format of competition within this round — whether fixtures are single-leg, two-legged (home and away), best-of series (e.g., NBA Playoffs best-of-7), round-robin, or elimination format. Governs fixture generation and advancement rules.']
  scheduled_end_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the last fixture of this competition round is scheduled to conclude. Used for season calendar planning, broadcast rights windows, and standings publication timing.']
  scheduled_start_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the first fixture of this competition round is scheduled to begin. Used for fixture calendar generation, broadcast scheduling, and fan-facing event listings.']
  season_ticket_applicable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether season ticket holders\' entitlements apply to fixtures in this round. Some playoff or special event rounds may require separate ticket purchases even for season ticket holders.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the operational system of record from which this competition round record originated (e.g., SAP S/4HANA, Ticketmaster, Salesforce CRM, Dalet Galaxy). Supports data lineage tracking and Silver layer reconciliation in the Databricks Lakehouse. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: SAP_S4HANA|TICKETMASTER|SALESFORCE|SUCCESSFACTORS|DALET|ADOBE_AEP|ARCHTICS|SHOPIFY|WORKDAY|HUDL|MANUAL — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  sponsorship_activation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether dedicated sponsorship activations are planned for this competition round (e.g., title sponsorship of a playoff round, naming rights for a championship week). Triggers Salesforce CRM sponsorship workflow.']
  stage_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the competition stage this round belongs to, distinguishing regular season weeks from playoff brackets, group stages, knockout rounds, and championship finals. Drives standings calculation rules and bracket progression logic. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: regular_season|group_stage|knockout|playoff|final|wildcard|conference_championship|all_star|preseason — promote to reference product]']
  ticket_sale_end_date date [note: 'Date on which ticket sales close for fixtures in this competition round. Used by Ticketmaster/AXS and Archtics to manage inventory cutoff and gate access configuration.']
  ticket_sale_start_date date [note: 'Date on which ticket sales open for fixtures in this competition round. Used by Ticketmaster/AXS and Archtics to configure on-sale windows and dynamic pricing rules.']
  tiebreaker_rule varchar [note: 'Description of the tiebreaker criteria applied when teams or athletes are level on points or wins at the end of this round (e.g., head-to-head record, goal difference, away goals, points differential). Critical for standings and bracket resolution.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this competition round record was last modified in the Sports Entertainment data platform. Used for change tracking, incremental data loads, and audit compliance.']
  var_applicable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether Video Assistant Referee (VAR) technology is in use for fixtures in this competition round. Relevant for FIFA, MLS, and other football competitions. Affects match officiating logistics and broadcast production planning.']
  wada_testing_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether WADA-mandated Performance Enhancing Drug (PED) testing is required for athletes competing in this round. Triggers compliance workflows and athlete notification processes per WADA Anti-Doping Code.']

  Note: 'Reference entity defining the rounds, stages, and phases within a season or tournament — Week 1, Matchday 3, Quarter-Final, Semi-Final, Wild Card Round, Conference Championship, Super Bowl, Group Stage, Knockout Round. Captures round name, round number, stage type, parent season or tournament reference, scheduled start/end dates, and number of fixtures in the round. Enables fixture scheduling, standings calculation, and bracket progression logic.'
}

Table event.broadcast_window {
  broadcast_window_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the broadcast window record. Primary key for the event.broadcast_window data product.']
  broadcast_production_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to broadcast.production. Business justification: Broadcast window-to-production coordination: each event broadcast window must be linked to its broadcast production plan for pre-show/post-show scheduling, crew assignment, and technical setup coordin']
  broadcast_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to broadcast.broadcast_schedule. Business justification: The event-domain broadcast_window (rights scheduling slot) must reference the broadcast-domain broadcast_schedule (operational broadcast record) for schedule reconciliation, rights window activation c']
  channel_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the primary broadcast channel (network or OTT platform) assigned to air this fixture. Links to the broadcast.channel product.']
  fixture_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the fixture (match or live event) that this broadcast window is assigned to. Links the broadcast window to its parent event.']
  media_rights_deal_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the media rights deal under which this fixture\'s broadcast rights are granted.']
  policy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.policy. Business justification: Broadcast windows must comply with broadcast regulatory policies (FCC rules, blackout regulations, DRM policies, geo-restriction requirements). Broadcast compliance officers reference the applicable p']
  rights_window_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the overarching broadcast rights window agreement under which this fixture broadcast slot is governed. Links to the broadcast.broadcast_rights_window product.']
  split_from_broadcast_window_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on broadcast_window (split_from_broadcast_window_id)']
  venue_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to venue.venue. Business justification: Broadcast blackout rules, geo-restrictions, and territory rights are venue-dependent. Broadcast rights teams query broadcast_window→venue directly to apply blackout_applicable rules and territory_code']
  ad_break_count varchar [note: 'Number of scheduled advertising breaks within this broadcast window. Drives ad inventory allocation, CPM (Cost Per Mille) revenue planning, and sponsor activation scheduling.']
  ad_inventory_seconds varchar [note: 'Total available advertising inventory in seconds within this broadcast window. Used for ad sales, CPM revenue forecasting, and sponsor fulfillment tracking.']
  alternate_commentary_language_code varchar [note: 'ISO 639-1/639-2 language code for a secondary or alternate commentary track (e.g., Spanish-language feed on an English-primary broadcast). Supports multi-language broadcast packages.']
  blackout_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether broadcast blackout rules apply to this window in the specified territory. When true, the broadcast.blackout_rule product governs which sub-regions or zip codes are blacked out to protect local ticket sales or competing rights holders.']
  broadcast_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Expected end time of the broadcast window, including post-match coverage, analysis segments, and sign-off. Used for scheduling adjacent programming and managing broadcast slot duration.']
  broadcast_format varchar [note: 'Technical broadcast quality format for this window (SD = Standard Definition, HD = High Definition, 4K = Ultra HD, 8K = Super Hi-Vision, VR = Virtual Reality, AR = Augmented Reality). Drives production equipment requirements and CDN delivery specifications.']
  broadcast_kickoff_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The contractually mandated or broadcaster-driven start time for the fixture as required by the broadcast window. May differ from the venue-local scheduled start time due to broadcast scheduling constraints (e.g., network prime-time requirements).']
  broadcast_rights_region varchar [note: 'The geographic market or territory in which this digital touchpoint is licensed to distribute this fixture. Belongs to the distribution record because the same fixture may have different licensed regions per touchpoint.']
  commentary_language_code varchar [note: 'ISO 639-1/639-2 language code for the primary commentary track delivered in this broadcast window (e.g., \'en\', \'es\', \'fr\', \'pt\'). Determines commentary crew assignment and audio feed routing.']
  confirmation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this broadcast window assignment was formally confirmed by the broadcaster and the rights holder. Marks the transition from draft/provisional to confirmed status.']
  coverage_status varchar [note: 'Operational lifecycle status of this fixture\'s coverage under this deal. Tracks whether the rights coverage is scheduled, confirmed by both parties, currently live, completed post-broadcast, or under dispute.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this broadcast window record was first created in the system. Audit trail field for data lineage and compliance purposes.']
  dalet_schedule_reference varchar [note: 'Source system identifier from Dalet Galaxy Media Asset Management and Broadcast Workflow platform for this broadcast window schedule entry. Enables bi-directional reconciliation between the lakehouse and the operational broadcast scheduling system.']
  digital_streaming_included boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the broadcast window rights package includes simultaneous or catch-up digital/OTT streaming rights in addition to linear broadcast. Drives DTC and OTT platform activation.']
  drm_policy_code varchar [note: 'Code referencing the Digital Rights Management (DRM) policy applied to this broadcast window for digital and OTT distribution. Governs content protection, geo-restriction enforcement, and stream encryption requirements.']
  exclusivity_type varchar [note: 'Defines whether the rights granted under this deal for this specific fixture are exclusive to the licensee within the territory, or shared with other rights holders. May differ per fixture even within the same deal.']
  expected_viewership varchar [note: 'Projected audience size (number of viewers) for this broadcast window in the specified territory. Used for CPM pricing, sponsor valuation, and rights fee benchmarking. Sourced from historical ratings and audience research.']
  geo_restriction_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether geographic blackout or content restriction rules are enforced for this specific fixture-touchpoint combination. Distinct from the touchpoint-level geo_restriction_enabled flag, which is a capability indicator rather than a per-fixture enforcement decision.']
  highlights_rights_included boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the broadcast window rights package includes the right to broadcast post-event highlights clips. Relevant for news programs, digital platforms, and social media distribution rights.']
  is_exclusive boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this broadcast window grants exclusive rights to the primary broadcaster in the specified territory, preventing simultaneous broadcast by competing networks or platforms.']
  is_free_to_air boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this broadcast window is distributed on a free-to-air (FTA) basis, accessible to all viewers without subscription or pay-per-view purchase. Relevant for regulatory compliance in markets with FTA obligations for major sporting events.']
  is_ppv boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this broadcast window is distributed as a Pay-Per-View (PPV) event, requiring individual consumer purchase rather than standard subscription or free-to-air access.']
  kickoff_constraint_reason varchar [note: 'Reason code explaining why the broadcast-driven kickoff time was set, particularly when it differs from the venue-preferred or league-default time. Supports scheduling governance and CBA compliance review. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: broadcaster_prime_time|network_scheduling|rights_agreement|league_mandate|venue_curfew|international_window|other — promote to reference product]']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this broadcast window record was most recently modified. Supports incremental data loading, change detection, and audit trail requirements.']
  local_kickoff_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Territory-specific local start time for the broadcast, accounting for the time zone of the target broadcast market. Distinct from the venue-local kickoff and the broadcaster-mandated kickoff timestamp.']
  local_timezone varchar [note: 'IANA timezone identifier for the target broadcast territory (e.g., \'America/New_York\', \'Europe/London\'). Used to derive local broadcast times for scheduling, EPG (Electronic Programme Guide) listings, and fan-facing communications.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text operational notes for this broadcast window, capturing special production instructions, broadcaster-specific requirements, or scheduling exceptions not captured in structured fields.']
  platform_rights varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of platforms (e.g., linear_tv, ott, mobile, ppv) entitled to carry this specific fixture under this deal. Captures the intersection of deal-level platform rights and fixture-level eligibility (e.g., a PPV-eligible fixture activates ppv rights; a non-PPV fixture does not).']
  post_show_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Scheduled end time for post-match broadcast coverage (interviews, analysis, highlights recap). Defines the full broadcast slot duration for rights and scheduling purposes.']
  pre_show_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Scheduled start time for pre-match broadcast coverage (warm-up shows, analyst panels, player arrivals). Determines when the broadcast feed must be live ahead of kickoff.']
  primary_broadcaster_name varchar [note: 'Name of the primary broadcaster or network holding the rights to air this fixture in the assigned territory (e.g., \'ESPN\', \'Sky Sports\', \'beIN Sports\'). Operational reference for production coordination and rights management.']
  production_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the production methodology for this broadcast window. OB Truck = Outside Broadcast (on-site production vehicle); Studio = studio-based production; Remote = remote production from a central facility; Hybrid = combination; Virtual = fully virtual/cloud production.']
  rights_fee_allocation decimal [note: 'The portion of the total deal value (in USD) allocated to this specific fixture under this media rights deal. Used for per-fixture revenue recognition, reporting, and rights accounting. Belongs to the fixture-deal combination, not to either entity alone.']
  rights_fee_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Contractual rights fee amount payable by the broadcaster for this specific broadcast window assignment. Commercially sensitive financial data used for revenue recognition and rights deal performance tracking.']
  rights_fee_currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the broadcast rights fee amount (e.g., \'USD\', \'GBP\', \'EUR\'). Required for multi-currency financial reporting and SAP S/4HANA FI integration.']
  schedule_conflict_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this broadcast window has a detected scheduling conflict with another fixture or broadcast slot on the same channel or in the same territory. Triggers scheduling review workflow.']
  slot_duration_minutes varchar [note: 'Total allocated broadcast slot duration in minutes, including pre-show, match/event, and post-show coverage. Used for EPG scheduling, ad inventory planning, and broadcaster contractual slot compliance.']
  sublicense_permitted boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the primary broadcaster is contractually permitted to sublicense this broadcast window to third-party networks or platforms within the territory. Governs rights chain management and sublicense agreement creation.']
  territory_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code identifying the specific territory in which this media rights deal covers this fixture. A single fixture may have different deals active in different territories simultaneously.']
  territory_country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code identifying the primary broadcast territory for this window. Governs rights enforcement, blackout rules, and geo-targeted distribution. Use \'WLD\' for worldwide rights.']
  territory_region varchar [note: 'Sub-national or multi-country regional designation for the broadcast territory (e.g., \'Northeast US\', \'Western Europe\', \'APAC\'). Used when rights are granted at a regional rather than national level, particularly for RSN and international distribution.']
  window_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable label for this broadcast window assignment (e.g., \'Sunday Night Football - Week 12\', \'UEFA Champions League Final - Prime Slot\'). Used in scheduling dashboards and production briefs.']
  window_reference_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric code uniquely identifying this broadcast window assignment, used for cross-system reconciliation with Dalet Galaxy and broadcast scheduling systems.']
  window_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the broadcast window assignment. Drives downstream production scheduling, rights enforcement, and fan communication workflows.']
  window_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the broadcast window by distribution modality. Linear = traditional TV; OTT = Over-The-Top streaming; PPV = Pay-Per-View; RSN = Regional Sports Network; International = cross-border rights slot; Delayed = tape-delay broadcast; Highlights = clip/summary rights only. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: linear|ott|ppv|rsn|international|delayed|highlights — promote to reference product]']

  Note: 'Association entity linking fixtures to their assigned broadcast windows and production requirements — capturing primary broadcaster, kickoff time constraints driven by broadcast, and territory-specific start times.'
}

Table event.type {
  type_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for event_type']
  parent_event_type_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on event_type (parent_event_type_id)']
  policy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.policy. Business justification: Event types (PPV, broadcast, ticketed) are governed by specific compliance policies (content rating policy, broadcast standards, PPV regulations). The `content_rating` and `is_pay_per_view` attributes']
  broadcast_network varchar [note: 'Primary broadcast or streaming network partner for events of this type (e.g., USA Network, FOX, Peacock, WWE Network). Null for non-broadcast events.']
  content_rating varchar [note: 'Standard content rating classification for events of this type, following broadcast and entertainment industry standards (G, PG, PG-13, TV-14, TV-MA).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this event type record was first created in the system. Used for audit and data lineage tracking.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when this event type was retired or discontinued. Null for currently active event types.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when this event type became active and available for scheduling. Used for historical tracking and lifecycle management.']
  event_type_category varchar [note: 'High-level classification of the event type into major business categories (wrestling, concert, live event, pay-per-view, television, digital, other).']
  event_type_code varchar [note: 'Short alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the event type (e.g., WWE_RAW, SMACKDOWN, WRESTLEMANIA, CONCERT, LIVE_EVENT). Used as a business key for integration and reporting.']
  event_type_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the event type, including its purpose, format, and distinguishing characteristics.']
  event_type_name varchar [note: 'Full human-readable name of the event type (e.g., Monday Night Raw, SmackDown, WrestleMania, Concert, Live Event).']
  event_type_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the event type. Active types are currently in use; inactive types are temporarily suspended; retired types are no longer used; planned types are under development.']
  format varchar [note: 'Delivery format of the event type: live (in-person only), broadcast (televised), streaming (digital platform), or hybrid (multiple channels).']
  international_distribution boolean [note: 'Indicates whether events of this type are distributed internationally (true) or are domestic-only (false).']
  is_broadcast boolean [note: 'Indicates whether events of this type are broadcast on television or streaming platforms (true) or are live-only (false).']
  is_pay_per_view boolean [note: 'Indicates whether events of this type are premium pay-per-view offerings (true) or standard programming (false).']
  is_ticketed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether events of this type require ticket sales (true) or are non-ticketed (false, e.g., free digital events).']
  league_affiliation varchar [note: 'Name of the professional league or brand associated with this event type (e.g., WWE, NXT, AEW). Null for non-league events like concerts.']
  merchandise_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether events of this type typically include merchandise sales opportunities (true) or not (false).']
  production_complexity varchar [note: 'Relative complexity of production logistics for events of this type: low (minimal setup), medium (standard production), high (advanced staging/effects), premium (flagship events with maximum production value).']
  recording_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether events of this type must be recorded for archive, replay, or content library purposes (true) or are ephemeral live-only experiences (false).']
  requires_venue boolean [note: 'Indicates whether events of this type require a physical venue (true) or can be virtual/digital-only (false).']
  revenue_model varchar [note: 'Primary revenue generation model for events of this type: ticket sales, sponsorship, broadcast rights, merchandise, or hybrid (multiple streams).']
  season_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether events of this type are organized into seasons (true) or are standalone/ad-hoc (false).']
  sort_order varchar [note: 'Numeric value used to control the display order of event types in user interfaces and reports. Lower values appear first.']
  sponsorship_tier varchar [note: 'Standard sponsorship tier classification for events of this type, indicating the level of sponsor engagement and investment opportunity.']
  target_audience varchar [note: 'Primary demographic or fan segment targeted by events of this type (e.g., family-friendly, adult-oriented, premium subscribers, general public).']
  tournament_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether events of this type can be part of tournament brackets or elimination structures (true) or are standalone matches/shows (false).']
  typical_capacity_range varchar [note: 'Expected audience capacity range for events of this type (e.g., 5000-10000, 15000-20000, 50000+). Used for venue selection and revenue forecasting.']
  typical_duration_minutes varchar [note: 'Standard or expected duration of events of this type, measured in minutes. Used for scheduling and venue planning.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this event type record was last modified. Used for audit and change tracking.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for event_type. Referenced by event_type_id.'
}

Table venue.facility {
  facility_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each physical venue asset (stadium, arena, amphitheater, concert hall) managed by Sports Entertainment. Serves as the primary key and SSOT anchor for all downstream event scheduling, ticketing inventory, and broadcast production planning references.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Facilities incur operational costs (HVAC, utilities, general maintenance) tracked to cost centers for facility-level opex reporting. Already has gl_account_id but no cost_center_id. A venue finance ma']
  fixed_asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.fixed_asset. Business justification: The facility building, land, and structural improvements are major capitalized fixed assets. A venue asset accountant tracks building depreciation, capex additions, and impairment at the facility leve']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Venue P&L reporting and financial close require each facility to be mapped to a GL account for revenue/expense posting. Sports finance teams run facility-level income statements; this link enables aut']
  jurisdiction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to gaming.gaming_jurisdiction. Business justification: A venue facility operating a retail sportsbook must be licensed under a specific gaming jurisdiction. Facility-to-jurisdiction mapping drives retail wagering license compliance, determines permitted b']
  league_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to league.league. Business justification: Leagues maintain approved facility lists for game scheduling and compliance (e.g., NFL facility certification, NBA arena standards). A facility.league_id FK supports \'league facility approval\' reporti']
  venue_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to venue.venue. Business justification: facility is the physical asset record and venue is the master reference entity. Every other product in this domain (seating_section, seat, capacity_config, parking_lot, etc.) already carries both faci']
  address_line1 varchar [note: 'Primary street address of the venue used for logistics, emergency services coordination, ADA compliance documentation, and fan-facing wayfinding. Classified as confidential organizational contact data.']
  address_line2 varchar [note: 'Secondary address detail (suite, building, gate reference) for the venue. Used in official correspondence, regulatory filings, and emergency response planning.']
  broadcast_production_level varchar [note: 'Classification of the venue\'s broadcast infrastructure capability tier. Tier 1 = full national/international OB (Outside Broadcast) production with permanent broadcast compound; Tier 2 = RSN (Regional Sports Network) capable; Tier 3 = local/streaming only; Non-broadcast = no permanent broadcast infrastructure.']
  city varchar [note: 'City in which the venue is physically located. Used for market segmentation, regional sports network (RSN) rights territory mapping, and fan travel logistics.']
  concession_stand_count varchar [note: 'Total number of fixed concession and food-and-beverage service points within the venue. Used in event-day staffing planning, POS system configuration, and revenue forecasting for food and beverage operations.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 three-letter country code for the venue\'s physical location (e.g., \'USA\', \'GBR\', \'AUS\'). Governs applicable regulatory frameworks (GDPR, CCPA, FIFA, IOC), broadcast rights territories, and currency defaults.']
  field_dimensions_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text description of the primary playing field or stage dimensions including notable non-standard measurements (e.g., asymmetric outfield dimensions in MLB ballparks, stage depth for concert configurations). Used by event production and broadcast teams for pre-event planning.']
  gross_sqft varchar [note: 'Total gross interior square footage of the venue facility including all concourses, concession areas, back-of-house operations, suites, and playing surface. Used for facility management cost allocation, HVAC sizing, and OSHA compliance reporting.']
  home_tenant_name varchar [note: 'Name of the primary home team, franchise, or resident tenant organization that uses the venue as its principal home base (e.g., \'Los Angeles Rams\', \'New York Knicks\'). Used in event scheduling, broadcast rights attribution, and sponsorship agreement scoping.']
  hvac_system_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the primary HVAC system installed in the venue. Determines energy consumption benchmarks, maintenance scheduling cadence, and climate control capability for event-day comfort and ice surface management (NHL arenas).']
  latitude decimal(9,6) [note: 'Geographic latitude coordinate of the venue in decimal degrees (WGS84 datum). Used for geo-spatial analytics, fan proximity modeling, broadcast satellite uplink positioning, and emergency services dispatch.']
  longitude decimal(9,6) [note: 'Geographic longitude coordinate of the venue in decimal degrees (WGS84 datum). Used in conjunction with latitude for geo-spatial analytics, broadcast OB (Outside Broadcast) unit positioning, and fan travel distance calculations.']
  max_event_capacity_override varchar [note: 'Operationally approved maximum attendance figure that may differ from the certified total capacity due to event-specific configurations (e.g., stage placement reducing seating, COVID-era restrictions, or temporary expansion permits). Used by ticketing systems to set hard inventory ceilings per event.']
  operational_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the venue asset indicating its readiness for event hosting. \'active\' = fully operational; \'mothballed\' = temporarily closed; \'under_renovation\' = capital works in progress; \'decommissioned\' = permanently retired from service.']
  ownership_structure varchar [note: 'Legal and financial relationship between Sports Entertainment and the venue asset. Determines accounting treatment (capital vs. operating lease), maintenance obligations, and naming rights negotiation authority under SAP FI/CO asset accounting.']
  parking_capacity varchar [note: 'Total number of vehicle parking spaces available at or directly affiliated with the venue including surface lots, structured garages, and accessible parking. Used in event-day logistics planning, fan experience design, and revenue management for parking products.']
  playing_surface_type varchar [note: 'Type of playing surface installed in the venue\'s primary competition area. Determines sport eligibility, athlete safety protocols, and maintenance scheduling. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: natural_grass|artificial_turf|hardwood|ice|clay|grass|synthetic_clay — promote to reference product if additional surface types are required]']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code of the venue location. Used for geographic market analysis, fan catchment area modeling, and regulatory jurisdiction mapping.']
  premium_suite_count varchar [note: 'Total number of premium luxury suites and private boxes available in the venue. Drives VIP ticketing inventory, suite license agreement management, and premium hospitality revenue planning in Salesforce CRM and Archtics.']
  primary_sport_category varchar [note: 'The principal sport or entertainment category for which the venue is primarily designed and configured (e.g., \'American Football\', \'Basketball\', \'Ice Hockey\', \'Soccer\', \'Concert/Live Entertainment\', \'Baseball\'). Drives default seating bowl configuration and broadcast production setup.']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the facility master record was first created in the data platform. Supports data lineage, audit trail requirements, and SOX financial controls for asset master data governance.']
  record_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the facility master record. Used for change data capture (CDC) in the Databricks Silver Layer pipeline, data freshness monitoring, and audit compliance.']
  roof_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the venue\'s roof structure indicating weather protection capability. Impacts event scheduling decisions, broadcast lighting requirements, HVAC system design, and contingency planning for weather-affected events.']
  scoreboard_type varchar [note: 'Type of primary scoreboard and video display system installed in the venue. Determines broadcast production integration capability, in-venue fan experience quality, and advertising inventory available on digital displays.']
  seating_bowl_config_code varchar [note: 'Alphanumeric code identifying the specific seating bowl layout configuration loaded in Archtics for this venue (e.g., \'NFL-FULL\', \'CONCERT-360\', \'NBA-STANDARD\'). Maps to the seating manifest used for ticket inventory allocation and pricing zone assignment.']
  state_province varchar [note: 'State or province in which the venue is located. Required for tax jurisdiction determination, OSHA regulatory compliance reporting, and broadcast rights territory assignment.']
  time_zone varchar [note: 'IANA time zone identifier for the venue\'s local time (e.g., \'America/Los_Angeles\', \'Europe/London\'). Critical for event scheduling, broadcast start-time coordination, and ticketing system gate-open logic across global markets.']
  total_capacity varchar [note: 'Maximum total number of spectators the venue can accommodate across all seating categories (GA, reserved, VIP, SRO) as certified by local fire and safety authorities. Primary capacity figure used in ticketing inventory allocation and revenue yield modeling.']
  venue_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the physical venue structure by its primary design and use purpose. Drives seating bowl configuration, capacity planning, and event type eligibility in scheduling and ticketing systems.']
  wifi_capacity_mbps varchar [note: 'Total available Wi-Fi network bandwidth in megabits per second provisioned for the venue\'s public fan-facing network. Supports digital fan engagement platforms, mobile ticketing, OTT streaming, and DTC content delivery during events.']
  year_built varchar [note: 'Four-digit calendar year in which the venue was originally constructed and opened. Used for asset depreciation scheduling in SAP FI/CO, capital planning, and historical context in broadcast storytelling.']
  year_last_renovated varchar [note: 'Four-digit calendar year of the most recent significant capital renovation or refurbishment of the venue. Used in asset lifecycle management, capital expenditure planning, and ADA/OSHA compliance upgrade tracking.']

  Note: 'Master record for every physical venue asset — stadiums, arenas, amphitheaters, and concert halls — owned or operated by Sports Entertainment. Captures venue name, full address, geo-coordinates, venue type (stadium/arena/amphitheater/ballpark), playing surface type, roof type (open/retractable/enclosed), year built, year last renovated, total gross square footage, ownership structure (owned/leased/managed), primary sport or entertainment category, home team or resident tenant, ADA compliance certification status, OSHA safety rating, ISO 20121 sustainability certification, seating bowl configuration code, naming rights holder, time zone, and operational status (active/mothballed/under-renovation/decommissioned). Serves as the SSOT for all venue identity and physical characteristics consumed by event scheduling, ticketing inventory, and broadcast production planning.'
}

Table venue.seating_section {
  seating_section_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each seating section record within the venue seating inventory. Primary key for the seating_section data product sourced from Archtics Venue Seating and Inventory Management.']
  access_zone_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to security.access_zone. Business justification: Each seating section maps to a security access zone for credential enforcement, post assignment, and evacuation routing. Security operations and crowd management plans use this mapping to assign zone-']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Seating sections (premium club, GA, suite-adjacent) generate distinct revenue streams tracked by cost center for P&L reporting. A sports-entertainment finance controller builds section-level revenue a']
  facility_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent venue (stadium, arena, or concert hall) that contains this seating section. Links the section to its facility master record.']
  price_zone_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ticketing.price_zone. Business justification: Section-to-price-zone mapping is foundational to ticketing manifest configuration, on-sale setup, and revenue reporting by zone. Ticketing operations teams assign sections to price zones during event ']
  venue_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent venue (stadium, arena, or concert hall) that contains this seating section. Links the section to its facility master record.']
  accessible_route_available boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a fully ADA-compliant accessible route (ramp, elevator, or level pathway) is available to reach this seating section from the main venue entrance. Required for ADA compliance documentation and fan accessibility communications.']
  ada_seat_count varchar [note: 'Number of seats within this section that are designated as ADA-compliant accessible positions, including wheelchair spaces and companion seats. Required for ADA compliance reporting and venue accessibility audits.']
  alcohol_service_permitted boolean [note: 'Indicates whether alcohol sales and service are permitted within this seating section. Driven by local liquor licensing, venue policy, and proximity to family or all-ages zones. Affects concessions planning and POS configuration.']
  archtics_section_code varchar [note: 'Native section identifier as assigned within the Archtics Venue Seating and Inventory Management system. Used for system-of-record reconciliation, data lineage tracking, and integration with Ticketmaster/AXS ticketing workflows.']
  broadcast_camera_zone boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this seating section falls within a designated broadcast camera or production zone, which may restrict fan movement, signage, or attire during live broadcasts. Relevant for FCC broadcast compliance and OB (Outside Broadcast) production planning.']
  companion_seat_count varchar [note: 'Number of companion seats adjacent to ADA wheelchair positions within this section. Required for ADA compliance to ensure accessible patrons can be accompanied. Distinct from total ADA seat count.']
  concourse_level varchar [note: 'Identifier of the concourse or service corridor level associated with this seating section (e.g., \'Main Concourse\', \'Club Concourse\', \'Upper Concourse\'). Used for concessions assignment, restroom mapping, and fan services logistics.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this seating section record was first created in the Silver Layer data product. Supports data lineage, audit trail, and record lifecycle management per SOX and internal data governance standards.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date from which this seating section configuration became operationally active and available for ticketing. Supports historical capacity tracking and venue reconfiguration audit trails.']
  emergency_exit_route varchar [note: 'Designated emergency evacuation route identifier or description for this seating section, as defined in the venue\'s life safety plan. Required for OSHA compliance, fire marshal approval, and event-day safety briefings.']
  entry_gate_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the primary entry gate or access point assigned to this seating section for fan ingress and egress. Used in access control configuration within Ticketmaster/AXS and for event-day crowd flow management.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which this seating section configuration is scheduled to become inactive or be superseded by a new configuration (e.g., due to renovation, permanent reconfiguration, or decommissioning). Null for indefinitely active sections.']
  floor_level_number varchar [note: 'Numeric floor or deck level within the venue structure where this section is physically located (e.g., 1 = ground/field level, 2 = concourse level, 3 = upper deck). Used for building management, emergency egress planning, and OSHA compliance.']
  hearing_loop_installed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether an assistive listening hearing loop (induction loop) system is installed in this seating section to support patrons with hearing impairments. Required for ADA compliance and venue accessibility certification.']
  hvac_zone_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the HVAC climate control zone assigned to this seating section within the Building Management System (BMS). Used for environmental comfort management, energy efficiency reporting, and OSHA indoor air quality compliance.']
  is_covered boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the seating section is covered by a roof, overhang, or canopy structure, providing weather protection for patrons. Relevant for outdoor venues and open-air stadiums; used in fan experience communications and premium pricing decisions.']
  is_indoor boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the seating section is located within a fully enclosed indoor environment (arena, indoor stadium). Distinct from is_covered which may apply to partially covered outdoor sections. Drives HVAC system assignment and environmental comfort planning.']
  last_inspection_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent structural or safety inspection conducted for this seating section. Required for OSHA compliance documentation and venue operational readiness certification prior to live events.']
  level_name varchar [note: 'Vertical bowl level designation indicating the physical tier of the venue where this section is located (e.g., field, lower bowl, club level, upper bowl, mezzanine, roof deck). Used for pricing, sightline analysis, and fan wayfinding.']
  max_event_capacity varchar [note: 'Maximum permitted occupancy for this section under applicable fire code, local authority, and OSHA safety regulations. May differ from total_capacity due to regulatory constraints, temporary staging, or reduced-capacity configurations for specific event types.']
  obstructed_view boolean [note: 'Indicates whether any seats in this section have a partially or fully obstructed sightline due to structural columns, equipment, or other physical barriers. Required for consumer disclosure compliance and ticket pricing adjustments.']
  premium_amenity_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this section includes premium amenities such as in-seat food and beverage service, dedicated wait staff, private restrooms, or exclusive lounge access. Used for premium product packaging, sponsorship activation, and VIP fan experience design.']
  row_count varchar [note: 'Total number of physical rows within the seating section. Used for capacity planning, seat mapping, and inventory configuration in Archtics. Not applicable (null) for GA or SRO sections.']
  row_label_end varchar [note: 'Label of the last row in this seating section as printed on tickets and venue maps (e.g., \'Z\', \'30\', \'ZZ\'). Used alongside row_label_start to define the full row range for seat map configuration.']
  row_label_start varchar [note: 'Label of the first row in this seating section as printed on tickets and venue maps (e.g., \'A\', \'1\', \'AA\'). Used for seat map rendering and fan wayfinding in Archtics and Ticketmaster/AXS.']
  season_ticket_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether seats in this section are eligible for season ticket packages and multi-game subscription products. Drives season ticket inventory allocation in Archtics and Ticketmaster/AXS.']
  seats_per_row varchar [note: 'Standard number of seats in each row of the section. May vary by row; this field captures the modal or configured default value used for capacity calculations. Null for variable-row or GA sections.']
  section_code varchar [note: 'Alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the seating section within the venue, as defined in Archtics (e.g., \'A101\', \'CLUB-C\', \'SUITE-12\'). Used as the operational lookup key in ticketing and access control systems including Ticketmaster/AXS.']
  section_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable display name of the seating section as presented to fans on tickets, venue maps, and digital platforms (e.g., \'Lower Bowl Section 101\', \'Club Level East\', \'North End Zone\').']
  section_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text operational notes or remarks about this seating section, such as temporary restrictions, special configurations, or event-specific instructions. Used by venue operations staff for event-day logistics and facility management.']
  section_orientation varchar [note: 'Cardinal or relative directional orientation of the seating section within the venue bowl (e.g., north end zone, south sideline, east corner). Used for wayfinding, broadcast camera positioning, and sun/shadow exposure analysis for outdoor venues. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: north|south|east|west|northeast|northwest|southeast|southwest|center — promote to reference product]']
  section_status varchar [note: 'Current operational lifecycle status of the seating section. Active: available for ticketing and event assignment; Inactive: temporarily unavailable; Under Renovation: physically inaccessible due to construction; Decommissioned: permanently removed from inventory; Seasonal: available only during specific seasons or event types.']
  section_type varchar [note: 'Operational classification of the seating section by access and seating arrangement. Reserved: assigned seats; General Admission (GA): open floor/standing; Standing Room Only (SRO): no fixed seats; Suite: premium enclosed box; Accessible: ADA-compliant designated area; Press: media/broadcast credential holders; VIP: premium hospitality. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: reserved|general_admission|sro|suite|accessible|press|vip — promote to reference product]']
  sightline_rating decimal(3,1) [note: 'Numeric quality score (e.g., 1.0–10.0) representing the quality of the view from this section to the primary event surface (field, stage, court, ice). Derived from venue geometry and used in pricing strategy, fan experience scoring, and premium product differentiation.']
  smoking_permitted boolean [note: 'Indicates whether smoking or vaping is permitted within this seating section. Governed by local ordinances, venue policy, and OSHA indoor air quality standards. Used for fan communications and compliance documentation.']
  suite_capacity varchar [note: 'Maximum occupancy of the private suite or box associated with this section, applicable only when section_type is \'suite\'. Includes both seated and standing reception capacity. Used for suite licensing agreements and hospitality event planning.']
  total_capacity varchar [note: 'Total number of seats or standing positions in the section representing the maximum sellable inventory. This is the principal quantitative fact (MEASUREMENT_OR_VALUE) for this MASTER_RESOURCE entity. Sourced from Archtics and used for ticketing inventory management, revenue forecasting, and compliance reporting.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this seating section record in the Silver Layer. Used for change data capture, incremental processing, and audit trail maintenance.']
  wifi_zone_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the wireless network access zone covering this seating section. Used for digital platform connectivity planning, fan engagement app performance monitoring, and Adobe Experience Platform audience activation.']

  Note: 'Defines every named seating section within a facility — bowl sections, club levels, suites, GA standing areas, SRO zones, press boxes, and accessible seating blocks. Attributes include section code, section name, level (lower/club/upper/field), section type (reserved/GA/SRO/suite/accessible), row count, seat count per row, total capacity, ADA-accessible seat count, sightline rating, price zone code, and whether the section is covered. Sourced from Archtics Venue Seating and Inventory Management.'
}

Table venue.seat {
  seat_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each individual seat record within the venue seating inventory. Primary key for the seat data product, serving as the Single Source of Truth (SSOT) for seat-level inventory consumed by the ticketing domain.']
  facility_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the venue (stadium, arena, or concert hall) in which this seat physically resides. Links seat inventory to the venue master record.']
  fixed_asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.fixed_asset. Business justification: Individual seats are capitalized fixed assets (installation cost, useful life, depreciation). Venue finance teams track seat replacement capex and depreciation schedules per seat. A sports-entertainme']
  price_zone_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ticketing.price_zone. Business justification: Seat-to-price-zone assignment drives ticketing manifest builds, dynamic pricing eligibility, and revenue-per-seat reporting. Every seat belongs to exactly one price zone; this FK enforces that relatio']
  seating_section_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the seating section that contains this seat. A section is a named grouping of rows within a venue bowl, stand, or level.']
  venue_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the venue (stadium, arena, or concert hall) in which this seat physically resides. Links seat inventory to the venue master record.']
  accessibility_features varchar [note: 'Free-text description of specific accessibility accommodations available at or near this seat, such as hearing loop proximity, assistive listening device availability, or transfer seat specifications. Supports ADA compliance documentation and fan service.']
  accessible_route_available boolean [note: 'Indicates whether an ADA-compliant accessible route (ramp, elevator, or level pathway) is available to reach this seat from the venue entrance. True = accessible route available; False = not accessible by compliant route. Critical for ADA compliance and fan service.']
  color_code varchar [note: 'Hexadecimal color code of the seat\'s upholstery or shell, used for venue mapping visualizations, brand compliance, and replacement procurement matching.']
  concourse_level varchar [note: 'The concourse or service level associated with this seat, indicating which food, beverage, and restroom facilities are nearest. Used for concessions planning, fan wayfinding, and event-day logistics.']
  condition varchar [note: 'Current physical condition of the seat hardware (frame, cushion, armrests, cup holder). Used for maintenance prioritization, capital expenditure planning, and fan experience quality management.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this seat record was first created in the system, representing the initial provisioning of the seat into the venue inventory master. Supports data lineage, audit trails, and Silver layer ingestion tracking.']
  elevation_m decimal(8,2) [note: 'Vertical elevation of the seat above the playing field or stage floor level, measured in meters. Used for sightline quality scoring, broadcast camera planning, and premium seat valuation.']
  entry_gate varchar [note: 'The recommended or assigned entry gate or turnstile through which a fan holding a ticket for this seat should enter the venue. Used for access control routing, crowd flow management, and fan experience optimization.']
  ga_area_code varchar [note: 'For General Admission (GA) or Standing Room Only (SRO) areas, the code identifying the GA zone to which this inventory record belongs. Null for reserved seating. Used to manage GA capacity and access control.']
  has_cupholder boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this seat is equipped with a built-in cup holder. True = cup holder present; False = no cup holder. Used for fan amenity reporting and concessions upsell targeting.']
  has_power_outlet boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this seat has an accessible power outlet or USB charging port. True = power outlet available; False = no power outlet. Relevant for premium and club-level seat differentiation and fan engagement platform connectivity.']
  hold_reason varchar [note: 'Reason code explaining why a seat is placed in \'hold\' operational status. Values: press (media/press allocation), production (broadcast/stage equipment), vip (Very Important Person hold), sponsor (sponsor contractual hold), operations (venue staff use), safety (OSHA/ADA safety clearance), tbd (To Be Determined). Null when operational_status is not \'hold\'. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: press|production|vip|sponsor|operations|safety|tbd — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  install_date date [note: 'Date on which this seat was physically installed in the venue. Used for asset lifecycle management, warranty tracking, and capital expenditure depreciation schedules.']
  is_ada boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this seat is designated as an ADA-compliant accessible space (wheelchair space or transfer seat) per Americans with Disabilities Act requirements. True = ADA-designated; False = standard seat. Critical for regulatory compliance reporting and accessible inventory management.']
  is_aisle boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this seat is positioned directly adjacent to an aisle, providing easier ingress/egress. True = aisle seat; False = non-aisle seat. Used for premium seat selection, accessibility routing, and emergency egress planning.']
  is_companion boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this seat is designated as a companion seat immediately adjacent to an ADA accessible space, reserved for a companion of a wheelchair user. True = companion seat; False = not a companion seat. Required for ADA compliance inventory tracking.']
  is_kill boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this seat is permanently structurally unavailable and must never be sold or activated. Kill seats arise from structural obstructions, safety requirements, or permanent equipment installations. True = kill seat; False = not a kill seat.']
  is_obstructed_view boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the seat has a partially or fully obstructed sightline to the playing field or stage due to structural columns, equipment, or other physical barriers. True = obstructed; False = unobstructed. Must be disclosed on ticket sales per consumer protection standards.']
  is_premium boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this seat is classified as a premium inventory seat (club, loge, courtside, field-level, or VIP designation) commanding a premium price tier. True = premium; False = standard. Drives revenue management and sponsorship inventory reporting.']
  kill_reason varchar [note: 'Descriptive reason explaining why a seat has been permanently killed and removed from sellable inventory (e.g., structural column obstruction, permanent equipment installation, safety code violation). Null when is_kill is False.']
  last_inspection_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent physical safety and condition inspection of this seat, including structural integrity, fastener checks, and ADA compliance verification. Required for OSHA venue safety compliance and facility maintenance records.']
  manifest_code varchar [note: 'The Archtics manifest identifier that governs this seat\'s configuration, pricing, and availability rules for a given event or season. A venue may have multiple manifests (e.g., hockey vs. basketball configurations in a multi-sport arena).']
  next_inspection_due_date date [note: 'Scheduled date by which the next physical inspection of this seat must be completed to maintain OSHA and venue safety compliance. Drives preventive maintenance scheduling.']
  operational_status varchar [note: 'Current operational lifecycle state of the seat. active = available for ticketing; inactive = temporarily removed from inventory (e.g., renovation); hold = reserved for operational use (press, production, VIP hold); kill = permanently structurally unavailable and never to be sold.']
  row_label varchar [note: 'Alphanumeric label identifying the row within the section in which this seat is located (e.g., \'A\', \'B\', \'12\'). Printed on tickets and used for fan wayfinding and access control.']
  row_sequence varchar [note: 'Numeric ordinal position of the row within the section, used for physical layout ordering and proximity-to-field calculations. Distinct from row_label which may be alphanumeric.']
  season_ticket_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this seat is eligible to be included in a season ticket package. True = eligible for season ticket sales; False = not eligible (e.g., event-only inventory, kill seats, or operational holds). Drives CRM and ticketing inventory allocation.']
  seat_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric code that uniquely identifies the seat within its section, as printed on a ticket or displayed on a seat placard. Corresponds to the Archtics seat identifier used in Ticketmaster/AXS ticketing workflows.']
  seat_number varchar [note: 'The seat number within the row, as physically labeled on the seat and printed on the ticket. May be numeric or alphanumeric depending on venue configuration.']
  seat_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the seat by its physical configuration and service tier. Drives pricing, accessibility compliance, and inventory allocation rules. Values: standard (general bowl seat), accessible (ADA wheelchair space), companion (adjacent to accessible space for a companion), premium (club/loge/VIP seat), stool (bar-height stool in standing/lounge areas), suite (private suite seat).']
  sequence varchar [note: 'Numeric ordinal position of the seat within the row, used for physical layout ordering and adjacent-seat grouping logic in ticketing systems.']
  sightline_quality varchar [note: 'Qualitative rating of the seat\'s sightline to the primary viewing area (field, stage, or court), derived from physical geometry and obstruction analysis. Used for pricing, fan experience design, and ticket disclosure requirements.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record from which this seat record was sourced. Values: archtics (Archtics Venue Seating and Inventory Management), ticketmaster (Ticketmaster platform), axs (AXS ticketing platform), manual (manually entered). Supports data lineage and Silver layer provenance tracking.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this seat record, including status changes, inspection updates, or configuration changes. Used for change data capture (CDC), audit compliance, and Silver layer incremental processing.']
  x_coordinate decimal(10,4) [note: 'Horizontal Cartesian coordinate of the seat\'s physical position within the venue floor plan, measured in meters from a defined venue origin point. Used for venue mapping, sightline analysis, and digital twin applications.']
  y_coordinate decimal(10,4) [note: 'Vertical Cartesian coordinate of the seat\'s physical position within the venue floor plan, measured in meters from a defined venue origin point. Used for venue mapping, sightline analysis, and spatial analytics.']

  Note: 'Atomic seat-level inventory record for every individual seat within a seating section. Captures seat number, row identifier, seat type (standard/accessible/companion/premium/stool), physical coordinates (row-seat position), ADA designation flag, obstructed-view flag, aisle seat flag, kill-seat flag (structurally unavailable), current operational status (active/inactive/hold/kill), and last inspection date. Serves as the SSOT for seat-level inventory consumed by the ticketing domain.'
}

Table venue.capacity_config {
  capacity_config_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each capacity configuration record within the venue domain. Primary key for the capacity_config data product.']
  facility_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the venue (stadium, arena, or concert hall) to which this capacity configuration applies. Links to the venue master record.']
  fiscal_period_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.fiscal_period. Business justification: Capacity configurations have seasonal effective periods aligned to fiscal planning cycles. Finance teams need capacity_config linked to fiscal_period for period-accurate revenue forecasting and budget']
  league_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to league.league. Business justification: Capacity configurations require league approval (capacity_config.league_approved flag exists). A league_id FK identifies which league\'s standards a configuration satisfies — essential for multi-sport ']
  obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.obligation. Business justification: Capacity configurations are constrained by fire code and ADA compliance obligations (max occupancy limits). Linking capacity_config to the governing obligation allows compliance teams to verify that c']
  regulatory_filing_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_filing. Business justification: Each capacity configuration (concert mode, sports mode, reduced capacity) requires fire marshal approval via a regulatory filing. capacity_config has fire_code_approved and league_approved flags but n']
  type_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to event.event_type. Business justification: Capacity configurations are designed for specific event types (concert vs. playoff vs. regular season). event_type_applicability is a denormalized text field; a proper FK to event_type enables venue o']
  venue_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the venue (stadium, arena, or concert hall) to which this capacity configuration applies. Links to the venue master record.']
  accessible_capacity varchar [note: 'Number of ADA-compliant accessible seating positions designated within this configuration, including wheelchair spaces and companion seats. Mandatory for ADA compliance reporting.']
  ada_compliance_verified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this capacity configuration has been formally verified as compliant with ADA (Americans with Disabilities Act) accessible seating requirements, including minimum accessible seat ratios and companion seat placement.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date on which this capacity configuration received formal approval from the relevant governing body or regulatory authority. Required for compliance audit trails.']
  archtics_config_code varchar [note: 'Source system identifier for this capacity configuration as recorded in the Archtics Venue Seating and Inventory Management system. Used for system-of-record reconciliation and data lineage tracing.']
  bowl_orientation varchar [note: 'Describes which sections of the seating bowl are open and in use for this configuration. Drives section-level inventory activation in Archtics and determines sightline-based kill-seat patterns.']
  broadcast_camera_positions varchar [note: 'Number of designated broadcast camera positions activated within this capacity configuration. Impacts kill-seat counts and sightline planning; relevant to OB (Outside Broadcast) and RSN (Regional Sports Network) production requirements.']
  complimentary_hold_count varchar [note: 'Number of seats held as complimentary allocations for athletes, staff, sponsors, and league officials under this configuration. Reduces sellable inventory and is tracked for financial reporting and CBA (Collective Bargaining Agreement) compliance.']
  concession_stand_count varchar [note: 'Number of concession stands and food and beverage service points activated under this capacity configuration. Drives staffing plans, POS (Point of Sale) system activation, and per-cap revenue forecasting.']
  config_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric code uniquely identifying this capacity configuration within the venue management system (e.g., \'FULL-BOWL-SPORTS\', \'END-STAGE-CONCERT\'). Used as the business key in Archtics and Ticketmaster/AXS integrations.']
  config_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative describing the purpose, layout rationale, and operational context of this capacity configuration, including any special setup notes for event-day logistics.']
  config_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name describing the capacity configuration profile (e.g., \'Full Bowl — NFL Game\', \'End-Stage Concert\', \'Half-House Theater\'). Used in operational scheduling and event planning workflows.']
  config_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the capacity configuration. \'active\' indicates the configuration is approved and available for event assignment; \'draft\' indicates it is under review; \'archived\' indicates it is retired from use.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this capacity configuration record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and Silver Layer ingestion tracking.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date after which this capacity configuration is no longer valid for new event assignments. Null indicates an open-ended configuration with no planned expiry. Supports configuration lifecycle management.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date from which this capacity configuration becomes valid and available for assignment to events. Supports temporal capacity management and configuration versioning.']
  field_floor_capacity varchar [note: 'Number of positions allocated on the field, floor, or stage-adjacent area for this configuration. Includes floor-level VIP (Very Important Person) standing, pit areas, and on-field hospitality zones.']
  fire_code_approved boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this capacity configuration has received fire marshal or local authority approval confirming that egress paths, aisle widths, and occupancy limits meet applicable fire safety codes.']
  floor_plan_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the venue floor plan drawing associated with this capacity configuration (e.g., \'v3.2\'). Ensures traceability to the approved architectural drawing used for seating layout.']
  ga_capacity varchar [note: 'Number of General Admission (GA) standing or unreserved floor positions allocated within this configuration. Used for festival-style and floor-level event formats.']
  kill_seat_count varchar [note: 'Number of seats removed from sellable inventory (\'killed\') in this configuration due to obstructed sightlines, staging equipment, production rigging, or operational requirements. Reduces effective sellable capacity from total configured capacity.']
  league_approved boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this capacity configuration has been formally approved by the relevant league or governing body (e.g., NFL, NBA, FIFA) as meeting minimum venue standards for sanctioned competition.']
  max_occupancy_limit varchar [note: 'Regulatory or fire-code maximum occupancy limit for the venue under this configuration as certified by local authorities. May differ from total configured capacity and governs hard attendance caps for safety compliance.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text operational notes and special instructions associated with this capacity configuration, such as temporary structural constraints, sponsor signage requirements, or event-specific safety advisories.']
  operational_hold_count varchar [note: 'Number of seats withheld from sale for operational purposes such as security personnel, event staff, emergency egress, and venue operations under this configuration.']
  parking_spaces_allocated varchar [note: 'Total number of parking spaces designated for patron use under this capacity configuration, including accessible parking. Used in event-day logistics, traffic management, and ancillary revenue planning.']
  press_media_capacity varchar [note: 'Number of credentialed press, broadcast, and media positions allocated within this configuration, including press box, commentary positions, and camera platforms.']
  reduced_capacity_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of total configured capacity at which this configuration operates when a reduced-capacity mode is in effect (e.g., public health restrictions, partial-fill events, or socially distanced seating). Expressed as a decimal percentage (e.g., 50.00 for 50%).']
  reserved_seating_capacity varchar [note: 'Number of individually assigned reserved seats available in this configuration, excluding suites, accessible, press, and field/floor allocations. Core inventory for standard ticket sales.']
  seating_mode varchar [note: 'Physical seating arrangement mode for this configuration. \'fixed\' indicates permanent bowl seating; \'festival\' indicates floor-level General Admission (GA) standing; \'retractable\' indicates use of retractable seating sections; \'portable\' indicates temporary staging.']
  sellable_capacity varchar [note: 'Net number of ticketable positions available for public sale under this configuration, calculated as total configured capacity minus kill seats, operational holds, and complimentary allocations. The operative figure for revenue management and ticket inventory.']
  setup_duration_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Estimated number of hours required to physically configure the venue to match this capacity configuration (e.g., installing portable seating, rigging stage, repositioning retractable sections). Used in event-day logistics and venue scheduling.']
  sro_capacity varchar [note: 'Number of Standing Room Only (SRO) positions available in this configuration, typically in concourse or designated standing areas beyond the primary seating bowl.']
  stage_configuration varchar [note: 'Physical stage layout used in this capacity configuration, which directly determines seating bowl shape and kill-seat patterns. \'end_stage\' is standard for concerts; \'center_stage\' for boxing/UFC; \'none\' for sports without a stage. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: none|end_stage|center_stage|thrust|in_the_round|half_house|traverse — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  suite_capacity varchar [note: 'Total number of seats or positions allocated within premium suites and luxury boxes under this configuration. Drives premium hospitality and sponsorship revenue reporting.']
  teardown_duration_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Estimated number of hours required to dismantle and restore the venue from this capacity configuration back to a neutral state. Critical for back-to-back event scheduling and venue turnaround planning.']
  ticketmaster_manifest_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the seating manifest in the Ticketmaster / AXS ticketing platform that corresponds to this capacity configuration. Enables direct linkage between capacity setup and ticket inventory manifests.']
  total_configured_capacity varchar [note: 'Total number of seats or standing positions available under this configuration. Represents the principal quantitative fact for this resource and is the master capacity figure used in ticketing inventory allocation and revenue management.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this capacity configuration record was most recently modified. Used for change detection, incremental data loads, and audit compliance in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  vip_capacity varchar [note: 'Number of premium VIP (Very Important Person) positions designated in this configuration, covering club-level seats, hospitality lounges, and premium floor access beyond standard suite allocations.']

  Note: 'Event-specific or configuration-specific capacity setup for a facility, capturing how total capacity is allocated across seating modes for a given event type or configuration profile. Attributes include configuration name, event type applicability (concert/sports/mixed), total configured capacity, GA capacity, reserved capacity, suite capacity, accessible capacity, press/media capacity, field/floor capacity, kill-seat count, and configuration effective date range. Enables dynamic capacity management across different event formats (e.g., end-stage concert vs. full-bowl sports).'
}
