Business justification: Parking revenue (event-day, season passes, pre-sale) posts to specific GL accounts for revenue recognition. A venue accountant expects parking_lot.gl_account_id to ensure parking revenue is correctly ']
  obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.obligation. Business justification: Parking lots have ADA obligations (accessible spaces ratio per local code), environmental obligations, and safety obligations. Direct link supports the compliance obligation register for parking opera']
  price_tier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ticketing.price_tier. Business justification: Parking lots are sold as ticketed products (parking passes) with a default price tier governing face value, service fees, and presale eligibility. Parking revenue management and on-sale configuration ']
  sponsor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sponsorship.sponsor. Business justification: Parking lots are standard sponsored assets in sports venues (e.g., \'Acme Official Parking\'). Assigning a sponsor_id to parking_lot enables sponsor activation tracking, signage compliance audits, and r']
  venue_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent venue with which this parking lot is associated. Links the parking facility to its host stadium, arena, or concert hall.']
  access_control_type varchar [note: 'Method used to control vehicle entry and exit at this parking lot: open (no control), ticket_scan (barcode/QR scan via Ticketmaster/AXS), permit_display (visual permit check), license_plate_recognition (automated LPR system), rfid (RFID transponder-based access).']
  accessible_spaces varchar [note: 'Number of parking spaces designated for persons with disabilities in compliance with ADA requirements. Used for ADA compliance reporting and event-day accessibility planning.']
  ada_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this parking lot meets all applicable ADA (Americans with Disabilities Act) accessibility requirements including accessible space count, signage, surface conditions, and van-accessible spaces. Critical for regulatory compliance reporting.']
  address_line1 varchar [note: 'Primary street address of the parking lot facility. Used for wayfinding, third-party operator correspondence, and emergency services coordination.']
  base_event_rate decimal(10,2) [note: 'Standard per-event parking fee charged to general patrons for this lot, expressed in the venue\'s operating currency. Used as the baseline for dynamic pricing calculations and revenue forecasting.']
  bms_zone_code varchar [note: 'Zone identifier within the venue\'s Building Management System (BMS) that monitors environmental and operational systems for this parking facility (e.g., lighting controls, barrier systems, HVAC in garages).']
  capacity_last_verified_date date [note: 'Date on which the total space count and space-type breakdown for this parking lot was last physically verified or audited. Ensures accuracy of capacity data used in event-day logistics and ADA compliance reporting.']
  city varchar [note: 'City in which the parking lot is located. Used for geographic reporting and multi-market venue operations.']
  closure_date date [note: 'Date on which this parking lot was permanently closed or decommissioned. Null if the lot is still operational. Supports asset lifecycle management and historical reporting.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code for the country in which the parking lot is located (e.g., USA, GBR, CAN). Supports multi-market and international venue operations.']
  covered boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the parking lot or garage provides overhead cover/shelter for vehicles. Relevant for premium pricing differentiation and fan experience communications.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this parking lot master record was first created in the system. Supports data lineage, audit trails, and Silver layer ingestion tracking.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code applicable to all monetary amounts on this parking lot record (e.g., USD, GBP, EUR). Supports multi-market venue operations.']
  distance_from_entrance_m varchar [note: 'Measured walking distance in meters from the nearest lot entry point to the venue\'s main public entrance. Used for fan wayfinding, ADA accessibility assessments, and shuttle route planning.']
  ev_charging_spaces varchar [note: 'Number of parking spaces equipped with electric vehicle (EV) charging stations. Supports sustainability reporting, fan amenity communications, and ISO 20121 event sustainability management.']
  last_ada_inspection_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent ADA accessibility compliance inspection conducted for this parking lot. Supports regulatory audit trails and proactive compliance management.']
  last_safety_inspection_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent OSHA safety inspection or internal safety audit conducted for this parking lot. Required for venue safety compliance documentation.']
  latitude decimal(9,6) [note: 'Geographic latitude coordinate of the parking lot centroid in decimal degrees (WGS84). Used for mapping, fan navigation apps, and shuttle route planning.']
  lighting_type varchar [note: 'Type of lighting infrastructure installed in the parking lot. Relevant for OSHA safety compliance, energy management, and ISO 20121 sustainability reporting.']
  longitude decimal(9,6) [note: 'Geographic longitude coordinate of the parking lot centroid in decimal degrees (WGS84). Used for mapping, fan navigation apps, and shuttle route planning.']
  lot_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric short code uniquely identifying the parking lot within the venue (e.g., \'NGA\', \'VIP-A\', \'RSL-03\'). Used on signage, ticketing systems, and access control.']
  lot_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the parking lot as displayed to fans and operations staff (e.g., \'North Garage\', \'VIP Lot A\', \'Remote Shuttle Lot 3\').']
  lot_type varchar [note: 'Categorical classification of the parking facility structure and service tier: surface (open-air flat lot), garage (multi-level structured parking), remote (off-site shuttle-served lot), vip (premium reserved lot), accessible (ADA-designated accessible parking area).']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for operational notes, special instructions, or contextual information about this parking lot (e.g., \'Lot closes 2 hours post-event\', \'Tailgating permitted in designated zones only\'). Used by venue operations staff.']
  open_date date [note: 'Date on which this parking lot was first opened for operational use. Supports asset lifecycle management and depreciation tracking in SAP S/4HANA Fixed Assets.']
  operational_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the parking lot indicating whether it is available for event-day use. Active = open and operational; inactive = temporarily not in use; under_maintenance = undergoing repairs or upgrades; seasonal = available only during specific seasons; closed = permanently decommissioned.']
  oversized_spaces varchar [note: 'Number of spaces designated for oversized vehicles such as buses, RVs, and trailers. Relevant for tailgating event planning and large-vehicle logistics.']
  pre_sale_available boolean [note: 'Indicates whether parking spaces in this lot can be purchased in advance through online or DTC (Direct-To-Consumer) ticketing channels prior to event day.']
  reserved_spaces varchar [note: 'Number of spaces pre-allocated for season ticket holders, sponsors, VIP guests, or other reserved parking programs. Supports Ticketmaster/AXS parking inventory management.']
  season_pass_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this parking lot accepts season parking passes or annual parking permits. Drives eligibility logic in Ticketmaster/AXS access control and CRM entitlement management.']
  security_level varchar [note: 'Classification of the security provision at this parking lot: unstaffed (no personnel or surveillance), staffed (personnel present), cctv_monitored (camera surveillance only), gated (controlled access gate), full_security (staffed, gated, and CCTV monitored). Supports OSHA compliance and fan safety reporting.']
  shuttle_route_code varchar [note: 'Identifier for the shuttle bus route assigned to service this parking lot. Populated only when shuttle_served is True. Used for event-day logistics coordination and fan communications.']
  shuttle_served boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this parking lot is served by a shuttle bus service transporting patrons to and from the venue. Relevant for remote lot operations and fan experience planning.']
  state_province varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-2 state or province code where the parking lot is located (e.g., CA, NY, ON). Used for regulatory jurisdiction determination and geographic segmentation.']
  surface_type varchar [note: 'Physical surface material of the parking lot. Relevant for maintenance scheduling, OSHA safety compliance, and ADA accessibility assessments (e.g., gravel surfaces may not meet ADA standards).']
  total_spaces varchar [note: 'Total number of vehicle parking spaces in the lot, including all space types (standard, accessible, EV, VIP, oversized). Principal capacity metric for event-day logistics and revenue planning.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this parking lot master record was most recently modified. Used for change data capture (CDC), incremental loads in the Databricks Lakehouse Silver layer, and audit compliance.']

  Note: 'Master record for all parking facilities associated with a venue — surface lots, structured garages, VIP lots, accessible parking areas, and remote shuttle lots. Captures lot name, lot code, lot type (surface/garage/remote/VIP/accessible), total spaces, accessible spaces, EV charging spaces, distance from main entrance (meters), managed-by party reference, pricing tier, and operational status. Supports event-day parking logistics, revenue management, and ADA compliance reporting.'
}

  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System-recorded date and time when the maintenance work order record was first created in SAP S/4HANA PM. Serves as the audit trail creation marker and is used for backlog aging and SLA measurement baseline.']

  damage_code varchar [note: 'Standardized SAP PM catalog code describing the type of damage observed on the asset (e.g., corrosion, fracture, leakage, contamination). Used in conjunction with failure code for root cause analysis and insurance claims.']
  estimated_cost decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total planned cost of the maintenance work order including estimated labor, materials, and overhead charges, expressed in the local operating currency. Used for budget planning and cost center pre-commitment.']
  estimated_labor_hours decimal(8,2) [note: 'Planned number of labor hours required to complete the maintenance work order, as estimated during work order creation or planning. Used for resource capacity planning and budget estimation.']
  external_service_cost decimal(15,2) [note: 'Cost of external contractor or vendor services procured via purchase order and charged to this work order. Relevant for specialized maintenance (e.g., elevator servicing, fire suppression systems) requiring licensed third-party contractors.']
  failure_code varchar [note: 'Standardized SAP PM catalog code identifying the root cause or failure mode of the defect that triggered a corrective or emergency work order (e.g., mechanical wear, electrical fault, vandalism). Enables failure mode analysis and reliability-centered maintenance (RCM) reporting.']
  functional_location_code varchar [note: 'SAP PM functional location identifier representing the hierarchical physical location within the venue where the work is performed (e.g., ARENA-01-HVAC-ZONE-B). Enables spatial analysis of maintenance activity across venue infrastructure.']
  is_ada_compliance_related boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this work order is related to Americans with Disabilities Act (ADA) accessibility compliance, such as repairs to accessible seating, ramps, elevators, or accessible restrooms. Flags orders requiring ADA compliance sign-off.']
  is_safety_critical boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this work order involves a safety-critical system or structural element where failure could pose risk to personnel, fans, or athletes. Safety-critical orders require expedited processing and mandatory sign-off per OSHA regulations.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System-recorded date and time of the most recent modification to the work order record in SAP S/4HANA PM. Used for change tracking, data freshness monitoring in the Silver Layer, and audit compliance.']
  notification_number varchar [note: 'SAP PM notification number associated with the defect or malfunction report that triggered this work order. Links the work order back to the originating fault notification for full traceability.']
  parts_cost decimal(15,2) [note: 'Actual cost of spare parts and materials consumed during the maintenance work order, as recorded through SAP MM goods issue against the order. Enables materials cost breakdown separate from labor costs.']
  parts_used_description varchar [note: 'Free-text or structured description of spare parts, materials, and consumables used during the maintenance activity. Includes part names, quantities, and SAP material numbers where applicable. Supports inventory replenishment and parts usage trend analysis.']
  permit_to_work_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the formal permit to work (PTW) issued for this maintenance activity, required for high-risk tasks such as hot work, confined space entry, or electrical isolation. Mandatory for OSHA compliance documentation.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Business priority assigned to the work order indicating urgency of execution. Critical: immediate safety or event-day risk. High: must be resolved within 24 hours. Medium: within 7 days. Low: scheduled maintenance window. Drives technician dispatch sequencing and SLA compliance.']
  requested_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the maintenance work order was formally submitted and entered into the SAP PM system by the requester. Used for SLA measurement and backlog aging analysis.']
  requires_shutdown boolean [note: 'Indicates whether execution of this work order requires a partial or full shutdown of a venue zone, system, or utility (e.g., power isolation, HVAC shutdown). Drives coordination with venue operations and event scheduling teams.']
  scheduled_end_date date [note: 'Planned calendar date by which the maintenance work is expected to be completed. Used for SLA target setting, resource release planning, and event-day readiness confirmation.']
  scheduled_start_date date [note: 'Planned calendar date on which the maintenance work is scheduled to begin. Used for resource planning, technician scheduling, and event-day conflict avoidance.']
  sign_off_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the facility manager formally reviewed and signed off on the completed work order, confirming the work meets quality and safety standards. Required for work order closure in SAP PM and regulatory audit trail.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the originating operational system from which this work order record was ingested into the Silver Layer (e.g., SAP_PM_PROD, SAP_PM_EU). Supports multi-instance SAP landscapes and data lineage traceability in the Databricks lakehouse.']
  work_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the maintenance task to be performed, including the nature of the fault or scheduled activity, scope of work, and any special instructions for the technician. Sourced from SAP PM short and long text fields.']
  work_order_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric identifier assigned by SAP S/4HANA Plant Maintenance (PM) module to uniquely identify this maintenance work order across operational systems. Used for cross-system traceability and field technician reference.']
  work_order_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the maintenance work order within the SAP PM workflow. Draft: created but not yet released. Open: released and awaiting execution. In Progress: technician actively working. On Hold: paused pending parts or approval. Completed: work finished, awaiting sign-off. Closed: fully signed off and costed. Cancelled: voided before execution. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|open|in_progress|on_hold|completed|closed|cancelled — promote to reference product]']
  work_order_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the maintenance activity by its trigger and nature. Preventive: scheduled routine maintenance. Corrective: repair of a known fault. Emergency: unplanned critical failure response. Inspection: condition assessment. Predictive: data-driven proactive maintenance. Capital: improvement or replacement project. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: preventive|corrective|emergency|inspection|predictive|capital — promote to reference product]']

  Note: 'Operational record capturing every planned or reactive maintenance activity performed on facility infrastructure, BMS systems, seating, concourse equipment, or structural elements. Attributes include work order number, work order type (preventive/corrective/emergency/inspection), priority level, asset or system reference, description of work, requested date, scheduled start/end date, actual start/end date, technician assignment, labor hours, parts used, estimated cost, actual cost, completion status, and sign-off by facility manager. Sourced from SAP S/4HANA Plant Maintenance (PM) module.'
}

Linking each inspection to its governing compliance obligation enables obligatio']
  concession_stand_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to venue.concession_stand. Business justification: Safety inspections include health and fire safety inspections of concession stands. concession_stand has health_inspection_score, health_inspection_status, last_health_inspection_date, and next_health']
  facility_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the venue (stadium, arena, or concert hall) at which this safety inspection was conducted.']
  franchise_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to league.franchise. Business justification: Franchise agreements mandate specific venue safety inspection standards at home facilities. Linking safety_inspection to franchise enables compliance reporting against franchise agreement terms, regul']
  governing_body_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.governing_body. Business justification: Safety inspections are conducted by or reported to specific governing bodies (fire marshal, OSHA, local building authority). safety_inspection has a plain-text regulatory_body field that should be nor']
  investigation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.investigation. Business justification: Safety inspection findings with critical outcomes escalate to formal compliance investigations. Linking safety_inspection to investigation supports the escalation workflow — a named compliance escalat']
  parking_lot_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to venue.parking_lot. Business justification: Parking lots are subject to formal safety and ADA compliance inspections. parking_lot has last_safety_inspection_date and last_ada_inspection_date — both driven by safety_inspection records. Adding pa']
  primary_safety_event_fixture_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific live event (game, concert, or match) associated with this inspection when the inspection was triggered by or conducted in preparation for a specific event.']
  prior_inspection_safety_inspection_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the immediately preceding inspection of the same type at this venue, enabling inspection history chaining and trend analysis across inspection cycles.']
  regulatory_filing_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_filing. Business justification: Safety inspections generate mandatory regulatory submissions to fire marshals, OSHA, and local authorities. Linking the inspection to its compliance.regulatory_filing record enables end-to-end traceab']
  report_asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to content.asset. Business justification: Regulatory compliance requires inspection reports to be stored as versioned, managed content assets for audit trails and regulatory submission. safety_inspection has report_document_ref (plain string)']
  screening_checkpoint_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to security.screening_checkpoint. Business justification: Screening checkpoints are physical installations (magnetometers, bag-check equipment, structural lanes) subject to mandatory safety inspections. Regulatory bodies and fire marshals require inspection ']
  venue_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the venue (stadium, arena, or concert hall) at which this safety inspection was conducted.']
  ada_compliance_checked boolean [note: 'Indicates whether ADA (Americans with Disabilities Act) accessibility requirements — including accessible seating, ramps, restrooms, signage, and assistive listening systems — were specifically evaluated during this inspection.']
  areas_inspected varchar [note: 'Descriptive list of the specific venue zones, sections, or systems covered during the inspection (e.g., concourse level A, HVAC — Heating Ventilation and Air Conditioning plant room, emergency exits, accessible seating sections, electrical switchgear rooms, concession stands). Free-text narrative or comma-delimited zone codes.']
  bms_system_checked boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the Building Management System (BMS) — including HVAC, fire suppression, emergency lighting, and access control integrations — was reviewed as part of this inspection.']
  certificate_expiry_date date [note: 'The date on which the compliance certificate issued from this inspection expires and a renewal inspection is required. Drives proactive scheduling of future inspections.']
  certificate_issued boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a formal compliance certificate, clearance letter, or occupancy permit was issued as a result of this inspection. True when a certificate has been formally issued by the regulatory authority.']
  certificate_number varchar [note: 'The unique reference number printed on the compliance certificate or clearance document issued by the regulatory authority following a successful inspection. Null when no certificate was issued.']
  corrective_action_due_date date [note: 'The deadline by which all mandated corrective actions from this inspection must be completed and verified. Set by the regulatory body or agreed with the inspector. Drives compliance escalation workflows.']
  corrective_action_notes varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the corrective actions required, including specific remediation steps, responsible parties, and any interim mitigation measures mandated by the inspector or regulatory authority.']
  corrective_actions_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether one or more corrective actions have been mandated as a result of this inspection. True when any finding requires a formal remediation response tracked in the corrective action management system.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this safety inspection record was first created in the system, establishing the audit trail for data lineage and compliance record-keeping.']
  critical_findings_count varchar [note: 'Number of findings classified as critical or serious — those posing immediate risk to life safety, structural integrity, or regulatory non-compliance requiring urgent corrective action. Subset of total findings_count.']
  findings_count varchar [note: 'Total number of non-conformances, violations, or observations identified during the inspection, including all severity levels. Used for trend analysis and compliance KPI reporting.']
  fire_safety_checked boolean [note: 'Indicates whether fire safety systems — including sprinklers, extinguishers, emergency exits, evacuation routes, and fire suppression equipment — were evaluated during this inspection.']
  follow_up_due_date date [note: 'The deadline by which the follow-up or re-inspection must be completed. Populated only when follow_up_required is True. Used to schedule and track re-inspection compliance.']
  follow_up_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a follow-up or re-inspection has been mandated by the regulatory authority or internal compliance team to verify completion of corrective actions.']
  inspection_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which the physical inspection was conducted at the venue. Used as the primary business event date for regulatory reporting and compliance tracking.']
  inspection_end_time timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time when the inspection walkthrough or audit concluded on-site. Combined with start time to derive inspection duration for operational planning.']
  inspection_method varchar [note: 'Indicates how the inspection was conducted — physical on-site walkthrough, remote/virtual audit via documentation and video, or a hybrid combining both methods.']
  inspection_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric reference code assigned to this inspection, used in regulatory correspondence, certificates, and corrective action tracking. Format: INS-{YYYY}-{NNNNNN}.']
  inspection_scope varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the inspection covered the entire facility or was limited to specific zones, systems, or follow-up items from a prior inspection. Supports risk-based compliance planning.']
  inspection_start_time timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time when the inspection walkthrough or audit commenced on-site. Used for duration tracking and scheduling coordination with venue operations.']
  inspection_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the inspection record, from scheduling through completion or cancellation. Drives workflow routing and compliance dashboard visibility.']
  inspection_trigger varchar [note: 'The reason or event that initiated this inspection. Distinguishes between routine periodic inspections, those triggered by an incident or complaint, pre-event readiness checks, and post-event assessments.']
  inspection_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the inspection by regulatory or operational category. Drives which checklist, regulatory body, and certificate type apply. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: OSHA_WALKTHROUGH|FIRE_MARSHAL|ADA_AUDIT|STRUCTURAL_ENGINEERING|ISO_20121_SUSTAINABILITY|ELECTRICAL|HVAC|FOOD_SAFETY|SECURITY|ENVIRONMENTAL — promote to reference product]']
  inspector_credentials varchar [note: 'Professional certifications, license numbers, or regulatory authority credentials held by the lead inspector (e.g., Certified Safety Professional (CSP), OSHA 30-hour, Certified Access Specialist (CASp), Professional Engineer (PE) license number). Validates inspector authority and competency.']
  inspector_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the lead inspector or auditor who conducted the inspection. May be an external regulatory official or a certified internal safety officer.']
  internal_review_date date [note: 'The date on which the internal venue safety officer or compliance manager completed their review of the inspection findings and acknowledged the corrective action plan.']
  major_findings_count varchar [note: 'Number of findings classified as major — significant non-conformances that require corrective action within a defined timeframe but do not pose immediate life-safety risk. Distinct from critical findings.']
  minor_findings_count varchar [note: 'Number of findings classified as minor — low-risk observations or housekeeping items that should be addressed but do not materially impact safety or compliance status.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional context, observations, or commentary from the inspector or internal compliance team that does not fit into structured fields. May include references to prior incidents, seasonal conditions, or special event considerations.']
  outcome varchar [note: 'The formal pass/fail determination issued at the conclusion of the inspection. CONDITIONAL_PASS indicates the venue passed subject to completion of specified corrective actions within the due date.']
  regulatory_submission_date date [note: 'The date on which the inspection report or corrective action response was formally submitted to the relevant regulatory authority (e.g., OSHA, local fire marshal, ADA compliance office). Supports SOX and regulatory audit trails.']
  risk_rating varchar [note: 'Overall risk classification assigned to the venue based on the aggregate findings of this inspection. Informs prioritization of corrective actions, insurance reporting, and event-day operational readiness decisions.']
  structural_integrity_checked boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a structural engineering assessment of the venue\'s load-bearing elements, seating bowl, roof, and temporary structures (e.g., stages, rigging) was conducted as part of this inspection.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this safety inspection record was most recently modified, supporting data lineage tracking and change audit requirements for regulatory compliance.']

  Note: 'Records all formal safety and compliance inspections conducted at a facility, including OSHA walkthroughs, fire marshal inspections, ADA accessibility audits, structural engineering assessments, and ISO 20121 sustainability audits. Attributes include inspection type, inspection date, inspector name and credentials, regulatory body (OSHA/ADA/fire marshal/local authority), areas inspected, findings count, critical findings count, corrective actions required, corrective action due date, pass/fail outcome, and certificate issued flag. Supports regulatory reporting and compliance tracking.'
}

Table venue.safety_incident {
  safety_incident_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each safety or security incident record in the venue domain. Primary key for the safety_incident data product.']
  athlete_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to athlete.athlete_profile. Business justification: Venue safety incidents involving athletes require athlete identification for OSHA recordable reporting, workers comp claims, insurance processing, and incident investigation. safety_incident.affected_']
  calendar_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the live event (game, concert, match) during which the incident occurred. Null if the incident occurred outside of a scheduled event (e.g., during facility maintenance).']
  emergency_response_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to security.emergency_response_plan. Business justification: Safety incidents that trigger emergency activations must reference the emergency response plan that was activated. Post-incident regulatory reporting to DHS, fire marshal, and OSHA requires linking th']
  event_day_ops_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to venue.event_day_ops. Business justification: A safety incident occurs during a specific event day operation. safety_incident already has event_fixture_id and facility_id, but linking directly to event_day_ops_id allows incident_count on event_da']
  evidence_asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to content.asset. Business justification: Incident investigation, insurance claims, and OSHA regulatory submissions require linking safety incidents to specific video/CCTV evidence assets. safety_incident already has cctv_footage_available fl']
  facility_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the venue (stadium, arena, concert hall) where the incident occurred. Links to the venue master data product.']
  investigation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.investigation. Business justification: Safety incidents frequently trigger formal compliance investigations (OSHA investigation, internal root cause investigation). Linking safety_incident to investigation supports the incident-to-investig']
  obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.obligation. Business justification: Safety incidents trigger specific compliance obligations (OSHA recordable reporting within 24 hours, workers\' comp filings). Linking safety_incident to the triggered obligation enables compliance team']
  primary_safety_event_fixture_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the live event (game, concert, match) during which the incident occurred. Null if the incident occurred outside of a scheduled event (e.g., during facility maintenance).']
  screening_checkpoint_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to security.screening_checkpoint. Business justification: Safety incidents (crush injuries, equipment failures, slip-and-falls) frequently occur at screening checkpoints during fan ingress. OSHA recordable incident reports and regulatory filings require prec']
  seating_section_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to venue.seating_section. Business justification: safety_incident has venue_section (STRING) which is a free-text denormalized reference to the seating section where the incident occurred. Replacing this with a proper FK to seating_section normalizes']
  security_incident_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to security.security_incident. Business justification: Venue safety incidents (crowd crush, structural failure) co-occur with security incidents. Insurance, OSHA, and league regulatory reporting require linking the venue safety record to the corresponding']
  venue_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the venue (stadium, arena, concert hall) where the incident occurred. Links to the venue master data product.']
  affected_party_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the primary category of person(s) affected by the incident. Determines applicable regulatory framework (OSHA for workers, general liability for fans/visitors). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: fan|employee|contractor|athlete|vendor|media|other — promote to reference product]']
  body_part_affected varchar [note: 'Description of the body part(s) injured or affected (e.g., head, left knee, lower back). Required for OSHA 301 incident report and insurance claim documentation.']
  cctv_footage_available boolean [note: 'Indicates whether closed-circuit television (CCTV) or security camera footage of the incident is available and has been preserved. Critical for investigation, legal proceedings, and insurance claims.']
  closure_date date [note: 'Date on which the incident was formally closed following completion of investigation, corrective actions, and all required regulatory filings. Null for open or in-progress incidents.']
  corrective_action_due_date date [note: 'Target completion date for all corrective actions identified in the corrective action plan. Used for tracking remediation progress and ensuring timely closure of safety gaps.']
  corrective_action_plan varchar [note: 'Free-text description of the planned corrective and preventive actions to address the root cause and prevent recurrence (e.g., install non-slip flooring, retrain crowd management staff, replace faulty equipment). Required for OSHA compliance and insurance risk management.']
  corrective_action_status varchar [note: 'Current completion status of the corrective action plan. Enables management oversight of outstanding safety remediation obligations and regulatory compliance tracking.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the safety incident record was first created in the system. Establishes the audit trail for record creation and supports OSHA timeliness compliance verification.']
  crowd_density_level varchar [note: 'Estimated crowd density level in the affected area at the time of the incident: low, moderate, high, sold_out (at venue capacity), or SRO (Standing Room Only). Contextualizes crowd disturbance and crush incidents for risk analysis.']
  days_away_from_work varchar [note: 'Number of calendar days an affected worker was away from work as a result of the incident. Mandatory field for OSHA 300 log (Column H) for work-related injuries and illnesses. Zero for non-worker incidents.']
  days_restricted_work varchar [note: 'Number of calendar days an affected worker was on restricted work activity or job transfer as a result of the incident. Mandatory field for OSHA 300 log (Column I). Zero for non-worker incidents.']
  emergency_services_type varchar [note: 'Type of external emergency services dispatched in response to the incident. Null or \'none\' when is_emergency_services_called is false.']
  estimated_liability_usd decimal(18,2) [note: 'Estimated financial liability or cost exposure associated with the incident in US dollars, including medical costs, legal fees, and potential settlements. Used for insurance reserve setting and financial risk reporting under SOX.']
  immediate_response_actions varchar [note: 'Free-text description of the immediate actions taken by venue staff, security, or medical personnel in response to the incident (e.g., first aid administered, area evacuated, equipment shut down). Documents duty-of-care fulfillment for legal and insurance purposes.']
  incident_datetime timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time the safety or security incident occurred, recorded in ISO 8601 format with timezone offset. This is the principal real-world event timestamp used for OSHA 300 log entries, insurance reporting, and regulatory compliance.']
  incident_description varchar [note: 'Detailed free-text narrative describing what occurred during the incident, including sequence of events, environmental conditions, and contributing factors. Core field for OSHA 300A annual summary and insurance claim documentation.']
  incident_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known, human-readable reference number assigned to the incident at time of reporting (e.g., INC-2024-000123). Used for cross-referencing with OSHA 300 log, insurance claims, and regulatory submissions.']
  incident_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the incident record, from initial reporting through investigation, corrective action, and formal closure. Used for workflow management and compliance tracking.']
  incident_type varchar [note: 'Categorical classification of the nature of the safety or security incident. Drives routing to appropriate response teams and regulatory reporting pathways. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: fan_injury|worker_accident|medical_emergency|structural_failure|fire_alarm|crowd_disturbance|security_breach|hazardous_material|other — promote to reference product]']
  injury_illness_type varchar [note: 'OSHA 300 log classification of the nature of the injury or illness (columns M1–M6 on OSHA Form 300). Required for OSHA 300A annual summary and regulatory reporting.']
  insurance_claim_number varchar [note: 'Reference number assigned by the insurance carrier for the claim associated with this incident. Links the safety incident record to the insurance claims management system for financial tracking and subrogation.']
  is_emergency_services_called boolean [note: 'Indicates whether external emergency services (EMS, fire department, police) were dispatched to the venue in response to this incident. Triggers mandatory insurance notification and may require regulatory reporting.']
  is_fatality boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the incident resulted in one or more fatalities. Triggers mandatory OSHA reporting within 8 hours of employer knowledge under 29 CFR Part 1904.39 and activates crisis management protocols.']
  is_hospitalization_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether any affected person required in-patient hospitalization as a result of the incident. Mandatory OSHA reporting trigger (within 24 hours) for work-related incidents under 29 CFR Part 1904.39.']
  is_osha_recordable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the incident meets OSHA recordability criteria under 29 CFR Part 1904 (i.e., work-related illness or injury requiring medical treatment beyond first aid, restricted work, days away from work, loss of consciousness, or diagnosis by a healthcare professional). Mandatory for OSHA 300 log maintenance.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the safety incident record. Supports audit trail requirements and change tracking for regulatory compliance.']
  location_description varchar [note: 'Free-text narrative describing the precise physical location of the incident within the venue, supplementing the structured zone and section fields (e.g., \'Near stairwell exit at Section 112, Row G\').']
  osha_case_type varchar [note: 'OSHA 300 log case classification indicating the most serious outcome of the incident: death, days away from work, restricted work or job transfer, or other recordable case. Directly maps to OSHA Form 300 columns G through J.']
  persons_affected_count varchar [note: 'Total count of individuals (fans, workers, athletes, staff) directly affected by the incident. Used for severity assessment, insurance claims, and mass casualty protocol activation thresholds.']
  regulatory_notification_datetime timestamp [note: 'Date and time the required regulatory notification was submitted (e.g., OSHA fatality report). Null if regulatory_notification_required is false or notification is pending. Used to verify compliance with mandatory reporting deadlines.']
  regulatory_notification_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the incident requires mandatory notification to a regulatory body (e.g., OSHA fatality/hospitalization reporting within 8/24 hours, FCC, local health authority). Triggers compliance workflow escalation.']
  reported_by_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the venue staff member or official who formally reported the incident. Required for OSHA 301 incident report accountability and internal audit trail.']
  reported_datetime timestamp [note: 'The date and time the incident was formally reported to venue management or safety personnel. May differ from incident_datetime due to delayed discovery or reporting lag. Required for OSHA timeliness compliance.']
  root_cause varchar [note: 'Identified underlying cause of the incident determined through post-incident investigation (e.g., wet floor without signage, inadequate crowd control barriers, equipment malfunction). Essential for corrective action planning and recurrence prevention.']
  root_cause_category varchar [note: 'Structured classification of the root cause to enable trend analysis and systemic risk identification across incidents. Complements the free-text root_cause field. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: human_error|equipment_failure|environmental|procedural_gap|crowd_behavior|structural|third_party|unknown — promote to reference product]']
  severity_level varchar [note: 'Standardized severity classification of the incident: minor (first aid only), moderate (medical treatment beyond first aid), serious (hospitalization or lost workdays), critical (fatality or life-threatening). Determines OSHA recordability and insurance notification thresholds.']
  venue_zone varchar [note: 'High-level zone within the venue where the incident occurred (e.g., concourse, field level, parking lot, backstage, concession stand, loading dock). Used for spatial analysis of incident hotspots.']
  weather_conditions varchar [note: 'Prevailing weather conditions at the time of the incident for outdoor or partially-outdoor venues. Relevant for slip-and-fall liability, crowd management decisions, and structural incident analysis. \'indoor_na\' for fully enclosed venues. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: clear|rain|snow|fog|extreme_heat|high_wind|indoor_na — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  witness_count varchar [note: 'Number of witnesses identified at the time of the incident. Supports investigation completeness assessment and legal discovery preparation.']

  Note: 'Captures every safety or security incident occurring at a venue — fan injuries, worker accidents, medical emergencies, structural failures, fire alarms, crowd disturbances, and security breaches. Attributes include incident date/time, incident type, location within venue (section/zone/lot), severity level (minor/moderate/serious/critical), number of persons affected, emergency services called flag, OSHA recordable flag, incident description, immediate response actions taken, root cause, corrective action plan, and incident closure date. Mandatory for OSHA 300 log compliance and insurance reporting.'
}

Table venue.event_day_ops {
  event_day_ops_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each event-day operational record. Primary key for the event_day_ops data product in the venue domain. Role: TRANSACTION_HEADER.']
  broadcast_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to broadcast.broadcast_schedule. Business justification: Event day operations teams align gate open/close times, security sweeps, and load-in completion against the broadcast schedule air time and pre-show start. This \'broadcast-aligned event day timeline\' ']
  capacity_config_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to venue.capacity_config. Business justification: event_day_ops has a venue_capacity field that duplicates the configured capacity from capacity_config. Adding capacity_config_id FK links the event-day operations record to the specific capacity confi']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Event day operations incur significant costs (security staffing, medical, utilities, concessions support) tracked to cost centers for event-level P&L. A venue operations finance team expects event_day']
  emergency_response_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to security.emergency_response_plan. Business justification: Event day operations checklist includes emergency_system_verified but has no FK to the specific emergency response plan. DHS and fire marshal compliance requires the event day ops record to reference ']
  facility_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the venue master record where this event-day operation is executed. Drives capacity configuration, accessibility features, and facility manager assignments.']
  obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.obligation. Business justification: Event day operations must comply with specific obligations (crowd management regulations, alcohol service laws, fire egress requirements). Linking event_day_ops to the governing obligation supports pr']
  primary_event_fixture_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent event record in the event domain. Links this operational execution record to the scheduled event fixture it supports. This is the venue\'s operational execution layer — distinct from the event domain\'s event record.']
  production_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to event.production_schedule. Business justification: Event day operations coordination requires direct reference to the production schedule for broadcast go-live times, halftime changeover windows, and post-event signoff sequencing. Venue ops managers u']
  staff_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to venue.staff_plan. Business justification: event_day_ops is the operational execution record for a specific event day at a facility. staff_plan is the event-day staffing plan for that same facility and event. Linking event_day_ops to the staff']
  venue_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the venue master record where this event-day operation is executed. Drives capacity configuration, accessibility features, and facility manager assignments.']
  actual_attendance varchar [note: 'Total number of fans and attendees who entered the venue for the event, as recorded by Ticketmaster/AXS access control scan data. Used for capacity utilization reporting, revenue reconciliation, and safety compliance.']
  ada_access_confirmed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether all Americans with Disabilities Act (ADA) accessible entrances, seating areas, restrooms, and concession points have been verified as operational and unobstructed prior to event commencement.']
  bms_alert_count varchar [note: 'Total number of alerts generated by the venue\'s Building Management System (BMS) during the event-day operational window, including HVAC, electrical, plumbing, and structural alerts. Used for facility maintenance prioritization and post-event review.']
  checklist_completion_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of pre-event readiness checklist items marked as completed at the time of facility manager approval. A value of 100.00 indicates all items were verified; values below 100.00 indicate outstanding items requiring conditional approval or rejection.']
  checklist_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the pre-event readiness checklist template applied for this event-day operation. Enables audit traceability and ensures compliance with the most current OSHA and ADA inspection standards.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time at which this event-day operational record was first created in the system, typically at the point of pre-event checklist initiation. Serves as the audit trail creation marker.']
  critical_item_failure_count varchar [note: 'Number of critical checklist items that failed verification during the pre-event readiness assessment. Critical items include life-safety systems, emergency egress, and ADA access points. A non-zero count triggers conditional or rejected readiness status.']
  damage_assessment_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a post-event facility damage assessment has been flagged as required based on incidents, structural observations, or facility manager discretion during event closeout.']
  emergency_system_verified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether emergency communication systems, public address systems, and emergency egress lighting have been tested and verified as operational prior to event commencement.']
  event_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the event is scheduled to take place at the venue. Used as the principal business event date for scheduling, staffing, and operational planning.']
  facility_manager_approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time at which the facility manager formally approved or rejected the pre-event readiness certification. Serves as the official certification timestamp for compliance audit trails.']
  fire_system_verified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the venue\'s fire detection, suppression, and alarm systems have been tested and verified as fully operational prior to event commencement. Mandatory life-safety compliance check.']
  gates_close_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Actual date and time at which venue gates were closed following completion of fan egress post-event. Used for load-out scheduling and venue closeout reconciliation.']
  gates_open_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Actual date and time at which venue gates were opened to allow fan ingress. Used to measure gate-open-to-event-start lead time and to reconcile against Ticketmaster/AXS access control scan data.']
  hvac_verified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the venue\'s Heating, Ventilation, and Air Conditioning (HVAC) system has been inspected and verified as operational prior to event commencement. Required for OSHA compliance and fan comfort standards.']
  incident_count varchar [note: 'Total number of operational, safety, or security incidents recorded during the event-day operational window. Includes medical, security, structural, and crowd management incidents. Non-zero values trigger post-event incident review.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time at which this event-day operational record was most recently modified. Used for data lineage tracking, Silver layer incremental processing, and audit compliance.']
  load_in_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Actual date and time at which all production and equipment load-in activities were completed. Used to calculate load-in duration and assess readiness timeline adherence.']
  load_in_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Actual date and time at which production, equipment, and talent load-in activities commenced at the venue. Critical for event-day logistics scheduling and union labor compliance under applicable Collective Bargaining Agreements (CBA).']
  load_out_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Actual date and time at which all post-event load-out activities were completed and the venue was returned to base operational state. Marks the end of the active event-day operational window.']
  load_out_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Actual date and time at which post-event production and equipment load-out activities commenced. Used for venue turnaround scheduling and labor cost tracking.']
  medical_staff_count varchar [note: 'Total number of medical and emergency medical services (EMS) personnel deployed at the venue during the event. Used for mass-gathering medical compliance and post-event incident review.']
  medical_station_ready boolean [note: 'Indicates whether all designated medical aid stations within the venue are staffed, stocked, and operationally ready prior to gates opening. Required for mass-gathering event compliance.']
  ops_reference_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric reference code assigned to this event-day operational record, used for cross-system identification and communication with venue operations teams. Format: EDO-{YYYY}-{NNNNNN}.']
  ops_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle stage of the event-day operational record, tracking progression from pre-event preparation through in-progress execution to post-event reconciliation and final closure.']
  peak_concourse_occupancy varchar [note: 'Maximum number of fans simultaneously present on the concourse level at any point during the event, as measured by crowd management systems. Used for crowd density analysis and OSHA safety compliance reporting.']
  post_event_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for facility manager observations, anomalies, and recommendations captured during post-event closeout. Feeds into the post-event report and informs future event-day operational planning.']
  post_event_signoff_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time at which the facility manager formally signed off on the post-event closeout, confirming load-out completion, damage assessment disposition, and venue return to base operational state.']
  security_staff_count varchar [note: 'Total number of security personnel deployed at the venue during the event, including contracted and in-house security staff. Used for staffing ratio compliance and post-event security review.']
  security_sweep_completed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a full venue security sweep has been completed prior to gates opening, confirming no unauthorized persons or prohibited items are present in the facility.']
  temperature_celsius decimal(5,2) [note: 'Recorded ambient temperature in degrees Celsius at the venue at gates-open time. Used for HVAC performance benchmarking, fan comfort analysis, and heat/cold-related incident risk assessment.']
  weather_condition varchar [note: 'Prevailing weather condition at the venue during the event, recorded at gates-open time. Used for operational risk assessment, outdoor event safety decisions, and post-event incident correlation analysis.']

  Note: 'Operational readiness, execution, and closeout record for each event held at a venue, capturing the full event-day lifecycle from pre-event certification through post-event reconciliation. Pre-event readiness attributes include checklist completion status, checklist version, HVAC/fire/emergency system verification, security sweep completion, ADA access confirmation, medical station readiness, critical-item failure count, overall readiness status (approved/conditional/rejected), and facility manager approval with timestamp. Execution attributes include gates-open/close times, load-in/load-out windows, actual attendance, peak concourse occupancy, active concession stand count, active parking lot count, security and medical staffing counts, weather conditions, and incident count. Post-event attributes include operational status (pre-event/in-progress/post-event/closed), damage assessment flag, and facility manager sign-off. Distinct from the event domain\'s event record — this is the venue\'s operational execution layer owning the single source of truth for event-day readiness certification and operational performance.'
}

  last_equipment_service_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent preventive maintenance or equipment servicing performed at this concession stand. Supports facility management scheduling and OSHA equipment safety compliance.']
  last_health_inspection_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent health and food safety inspection conducted at this concession stand. Used to track inspection currency and schedule upcoming inspections per regulatory requirements.']
  max_throughput_per_hour varchar [note: 'Engineered maximum number of transactions this stand can process per hour under full staffing and optimal conditions. Used for event-day capacity planning, queue management, and per-capita spend modeling.']
  next_health_inspection_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next mandatory health and food safety inspection at this concession stand. Supports proactive compliance management and operational readiness planning.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for operational notes, special instructions, or contextual remarks about this concession stand (e.g., \'Requires generator power during outdoor events\', \'Halal-certified kitchen\'). Supports event-day logistics coordination.']
  open_date date [note: 'Date on which this concession stand first became operational and available for service within the venue. Supports asset lifecycle tracking and depreciation scheduling.']
  operational_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the concession stand indicating its readiness for event-day service. Active = open for business; inactive = temporarily closed; closed = permanently decommissioned; under_renovation = facility work in progress; seasonal = operates only during specific seasons.']
  operator_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the concession stand is operated directly by the venue (in_house) or by an external contracted concessionaire (third_party). Drives revenue recognition treatment and contract compliance tracking.']
  payment_methods_accepted varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of payment methods accepted at this stand (e.g., \'cash,card,mobile\'). Drives POS terminal configuration and fan communication. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: cash|card|mobile|voucher|contactless|crypto — promote to reference product]']
  pos_system_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the POS system instance or configuration profile assigned to this stand within the POS platform (e.g., Oracle MICROS, Appetize, SpotOn). Used for POS system integration and transaction reconciliation with SAP S/4HANA SD.']
  pos_terminal_count varchar [note: 'Number of active Point of Sale (POS) terminals installed at this concession stand. Used for capacity planning, throughput analysis, and technology asset management.']

  seating_capacity varchar [note: 'Number of guest seats available at or adjacent to this concession stand, applicable for premium club kitchens, bar stations, and sit-down food outlets. Null for walk-up or portable stands with no dedicated seating.']
  self_service_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this concession stand operates as a self-service kiosk or automated unit requiring no staff attendant. Relevant for labor cost modeling and fan experience design.']
  square_footage decimal(10,2) [note: 'Physical floor area of the concession stand in square feet. Used for facility management, lease cost allocation, and OSHA occupancy compliance calculations.']
  staff_count_standard varchar [note: 'Standard number of staff members assigned to operate this concession stand during a typical event. Used for workforce planning, labor cost budgeting, and SAP SuccessFactors scheduling.']
  stand_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric business identifier for the concession stand unit, used in POS system configuration, signage, and operational reporting (e.g., CS-101, BAR-C3). Sourced from SAP S/4HANA SD plant/storage location codes.']

  stand_type varchar [note: 'Operational classification of the concession stand unit indicating its physical configuration and service model: permanent (fixed built-in unit), portable (mobile cart), premium (club/suite kitchen), bar (licensed alcohol bar station), or specialty (themed or branded outlet).']
  storage_area_code varchar [note: 'SAP S/4HANA MM storage location code identifying the back-of-house inventory storage area associated with this concession stand. Enables inventory replenishment tracking and materials management.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this concession stand master record. Supports change data capture (CDC), data lineage, and Silver Layer incremental processing in the Databricks Lakehouse.']

  Note: 'Master record and POS-level transactional detail for individual concession stand units within a facility — food and beverage outlets, portable carts, premium club kitchens, and bar stations. Master attributes include stand name, stand code, stand type (permanent/portable/premium/bar/specialty), concourse location, cuisine/product category (hot food/cold beverage/alcohol/snack/merchandise), operator name (in-house/third-party concessionaire), POS terminal count, seating capacity (if applicable), ADA-accessible service counter flag, health inspection status, and operational status. Supports per-event concession revenue reporting, per-capita spend analysis, top-selling item tracking, and health/safety compliance. Sourced from POS systems integrated with SAP S/4HANA SD.'
}

Managed via Building Management System (BMS) and facility operations.']

[ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: prospect|proposal_sent|negotiation|contract_signed|renewed|lapsed|churned — promote to reference product]']

  primary_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Phone number of the primary business contact for the suite licensee. Used for event-day coordination and account management. Subject to GDPR and CCPA data privacy regulations.']

  renewal_option_deadline date [note: 'Date by which the licensee must exercise their contractual renewal option. Null if no renewal option exists. Critical for lease pipeline management and revenue forecasting.']
  seat_count varchar [note: 'Number of fixed, dedicated seats within the suite. Distinct from total capacity; used for ticketing inventory allocation in Ticketmaster/AXS and Archtics seating management.']
  square_footage decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total interior floor area of the suite measured in square feet. Used for facility management, HVAC load calculations, ADA compliance assessments, and license fee benchmarking.']
  standing_positions varchar [note: 'Number of Standing Room Only (SRO) positions within the suite beyond fixed seating. Contributes to total capacity and is used for event-day access control and OSHA compliance.']
  suite_level varchar [note: 'Physical level or tier within the venue where the suite is located (e.g., \'Club Level\', \'Loge Level\', \'Upper Concourse\'). Used for venue mapping, wayfinding, and premium hospitality segmentation.']
  suite_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable marketing or branded name of the suite (e.g., \'Champions Club Suite\', \'Founders Loge Box\'). Used in CRM, sales collateral, and fan-facing communications.']
  suite_number varchar [note: 'Alphanumeric identifier assigned to the suite within the venue, used for wayfinding, ticketing systems, and operational staff coordination (e.g., \'S-101\', \'PL-22\'). Corresponds to the physical suite designation in Archtics seating inventory.']
  suite_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the premium hospitality unit by physical and commercial configuration. Drives pricing tiers, amenity packages, and license fee structures. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: private_suite|club_box|loge_box|party_suite|press_box|founders_suite — promote to reference product if additional types are added]']
  termination_date date [note: 'Date on which the suite license was early-terminated, if applicable. Null for active or naturally expired agreements. Used for churn analysis, revenue impact assessment, and legal compliance tracking.']
  termination_reason varchar [note: 'Reason code for early termination of the suite license agreement. Used for churn analysis, CRM reporting, and revenue recovery planning.']
  total_capacity varchar [note: 'Maximum total occupancy of the suite including all seated and standing positions. Used for fire safety compliance (OSHA), ADA planning, and event-day capacity management.']
  total_license_fee decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total contractual license fee for the full term of the suite agreement in the venue\'s operating currency. Used for revenue recognition scheduling, P&L analysis, and EBITDA reporting. Sensitive commercial financial data.']

  Note: 'Master record for premium hospitality suites and club boxes within a facility, encompassing physical configuration, amenities, and the full commercial license/lease lifecycle. Physical attributes include suite name, suite number, suite type (private suite/club box/loge box/party suite/press box), level, total capacity, seat count, standing positions, square footage, amenity list (private bar/catering kitchen/AV system/private restroom), and ADA-accessible flag. Commercial license attributes include license number, licensee name, licensee type (corporate/individual), license start/end dates, term (annual/multi-year/per-event), total license fee, payment schedule, included event types and count, catering credit amount, parking passes included, renewal option flag, contract status (active/expired/terminated/pending), and lease pipeline stage. Supports suite revenue management, premium hospitality operations, lease renewal pipeline tracking, and per-suite P&L analysis. Distinct from sponsorship domain partnership contracts — this is a venue-specific hospitality revenue instrument.'

Table venue.accessibility_feature {
  accessibility_feature_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each accessibility feature record in the venue facility catalog.']

Business justification: Each accessibility feature (hearing loop, accessible route, lift) must satisfy specific ADA Title III or local building code obligations. Linking to compliance.obligation enables gap analysis reports ']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: ADA accessibility features have ongoing maintenance and inspection costs tracked to cost centers for ADA compliance budget reporting. Already has fixed_asset_id but no cost_center_id. A venue finance ']
  facility_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the venue/facility where this accessibility feature is installed or available.']
  fixed_asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.fixed_asset. Business justification: ADA accessibility features (elevators, hearing loops, ramps) are individually capitalized assets with dedicated depreciation schedules and ADA compliance CAPEX budgets. Finance teams track installatio']
  seating_section_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to venue.seating_section. Business justification: Accessibility features (wheelchair ramps, companion seating areas, hearing loops) are physically located within or adjacent to specific seating sections. accessibility_feature has venue_zone (STRING) ']
  venue_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the venue/facility where this accessibility feature is installed or available.']
  accessible_route_connected boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this feature is connected to a continuous accessible route as required by ADA Standards for Accessible Design Section 206. Critical for ADA path-of-travel compliance assessments.']
  annual_maintenance_cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'Estimated or actual annual cost to maintain and inspect this accessibility feature, in USD. Used for venue operations budgeting and ADA compliance cost tracking in Workday Adaptive Planning.']
  asset_tag varchar [note: 'Internal fixed asset tag or barcode label affixed to the physical feature unit, used for asset inventory reconciliation with SAP S/4HANA Fixed Asset Management (FI-AA).']
  capacity varchar [note: 'Maximum number of persons or devices this feature can accommodate simultaneously (e.g., elevator capacity in persons, companion seating area capacity in seats, sensory room capacity). Distinct from quantity of units.']
  compliance_standard varchar [note: 'The specific regulatory or code standard this feature is evaluated against (e.g., \'ADA 2010 Standards for Accessible Design\', \'IBC 2021\', \'NFPA 101\', \'Local Municipal Code Section 11B\'). Supports multi-jurisdiction compliance tracking.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current ADA and local building code compliance status of the feature. Drives ADA compliance reporting and remediation tracking. Values: compliant, non_compliant, remediation_in_progress, pending_inspection, decommissioned.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this accessibility feature record was first created in the system. Provides audit trail for data governance and SOX (Sarbanes-Oxley Act) compliance.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date from which this accessibility feature record is considered active and valid in the venue catalog. Supports temporal data management and Silver Layer SCD (Slowly Changing Dimension) processing.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date until which this accessibility feature record is valid. Null indicates the record is currently active. Supports decommissioning workflows and Silver Layer SCD (Slowly Changing Dimension) history management.']
  fan_facing_description varchar [note: 'Consumer-friendly description of this accessibility feature for publication on the venue website, mobile app, and fan engagement platforms (Adobe Experience Platform). Supports fan accessibility experience management and DTC (Direct-To-Consumer) communications.']
  feature_category varchar [note: 'Broad disability category this feature serves: mobility (ramps, elevators), sensory (hearing loops, braille), cognitive (sensory rooms), communication (assistive listening), parking, or seating (companion areas).']
  feature_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique alphanumeric code assigned to this accessibility feature, used for cross-system reference and ADA compliance reporting (e.g., WCR-101, ELV-B2, HLP-MAIN).']
  feature_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the accessibility feature (e.g., \'North Concourse Wheelchair Ramp\', \'Section 112 Hearing Loop\', \'Main Lobby Accessible Restroom Block A\').']
  feature_type varchar [note: 'Categorical classification of the accessibility feature type. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: wheelchair_ramp|elevator|accessible_restroom|hearing_loop|braille_signage|accessible_parking|companion_seating|sensory_room|accessible_entrance|tactile_pathway|assistive_listening_device|lowered_counter|accessible_concession — promote to reference product]']
  inspection_authority varchar [note: 'Name of the authority having jurisdiction (AHJ) or regulatory body that conducted or certified the last inspection (e.g., \'City of Los Angeles Building and Safety\', \'State Fire Marshal\', \'DOJ ADA Inspector\').']
  inspection_certificate_number varchar [note: 'Official certificate or permit number issued by the inspection authority upon successful compliance inspection. Required for ADA audit documentation and regulatory reporting.']
  installation_cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total capital expenditure incurred for the installation of this accessibility feature, in the venue\'s operating currency (USD). Supports fixed asset capitalization in SAP S/4HANA FI-AA and ADA capital investment reporting.']
  installation_date date [note: 'Date the accessibility feature was physically installed or made operational at the venue. Used for asset lifecycle management and warranty tracking.']
  is_ada_certified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this feature has received formal ADA (Americans with Disabilities Act) certification from an accredited inspector or authority having jurisdiction (AHJ). Distinct from compliance_status which tracks ongoing state.']
  is_event_day_available boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this accessibility feature is available and operational during live events. Some features (e.g., sensory rooms) may only be activated on event days. Supports event-day logistics planning.']
  is_published boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this accessibility feature is currently published and visible to fans on public-facing channels (venue website, mobile app, ticketing platforms). Controls fan-facing accessibility information display.']
  last_inspection_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent formal compliance or safety inspection of this accessibility feature. Critical for ADA compliance reporting and regulatory audit trails.']
  last_maintenance_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent preventive or corrective maintenance performed on this feature. Supports BMS (Building Management System) maintenance scheduling and operational readiness tracking.']
  location_description varchar [note: 'Descriptive text identifying the physical location of the feature within the venue (e.g., \'Gate A, Section 101, Level 2 concourse near elevator bank\'). Used for fan wayfinding and maintenance dispatch.']
  maintenance_team varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the internal or contracted maintenance team responsible for upkeep and inspection of this feature (e.g., \'Facilities Ops - Electrical\', \'Third-Party Elevator Services Inc.\'). Supports work order routing.']
  manufacturer varchar [note: 'Name of the manufacturer or supplier of the accessibility feature equipment (e.g., \'Otis Elevator\', \'Ampetronic Hearing Loop Systems\'). Supports warranty management and procurement.']
  model_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturer model number or product designation for the accessibility feature equipment. Used for spare parts procurement, warranty claims, and technical documentation.']
  next_inspection_date date [note: 'Date on which the next scheduled compliance or safety inspection of this feature is due. Drives maintenance scheduling and proactive compliance management.']
  next_maintenance_date date [note: 'Date on which the next scheduled preventive maintenance for this feature is due. Enables proactive BMS (Building Management System) work order generation.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text operational notes or special instructions related to this accessibility feature (e.g., \'Elevator requires staff key for Level 3 access\', \'Hearing loop activated 30 minutes before event start\'). For internal venue operations use.']
  operational_status varchar [note: 'Current operational readiness state of the accessibility feature on event day. Drives real-time fan accessibility experience management and event-day logistics. Values: operational, out_of_service, under_maintenance, decommissioned, seasonal.']
  quantity varchar [note: 'Number of units of this accessibility feature at the specified location (e.g., 4 accessible parking spaces, 2 companion seating pairs, 1 hearing loop system). Principal quantitative fact for capacity and compliance ratio calculations.']
  remediation_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text description of remediation actions required or in progress when compliance_status is non_compliant or remediation_in_progress. Documents corrective action plans for ADA audit purposes.']
  remediation_target_date date [note: 'Target completion date for remediation of a non-compliant accessibility feature. Used for ADA compliance reporting and regulatory deadline tracking.']
  requires_advance_booking boolean [note: 'Indicates whether fans must book or reserve this accessibility feature in advance (e.g., sensory rooms, companion seating, accessible parking). Drives Ticketmaster/AXS ticketing workflow configuration.']
  serial_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturer serial number of the installed accessibility feature unit. Enables individual asset tracking, warranty management, and regulatory inspection records.']
  signage_present boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the required International Symbol of Accessibility (ISA) signage and directional signage is present and compliant at this feature location. Required under ADA Section 216.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this accessibility feature record. Supports Silver Layer incremental processing and change data capture (CDC) in the Databricks Lakehouse.']
  venue_level varchar [note: 'The floor or level within the venue where the feature is located (e.g., \'Level 1\', \'Concourse B\', \'Lower Bowl\', \'Mezzanine\'). Supports spatial analytics and accessibility mapping.']
  venue_zone varchar [note: 'The named zone or section of the venue where the feature is located (e.g., \'North Concourse\', \'Gate C Entry\', \'Section 112\', \'VIP Club Level\'). Aligns with Archtics seating inventory zone taxonomy.']
  warranty_expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which the manufacturer or vendor warranty for this accessibility feature expires. Supports procurement planning and maintenance cost forecasting.']

  Note: 'Catalog of ADA and accessibility features installed or available at a facility — wheelchair ramps, elevator banks, accessible restrooms, hearing loop systems, braille signage, accessible parking spaces, companion seating areas, and sensory rooms. Attributes include feature name, feature type, location description, quantity/count, installation date, last inspection date, compliance standard (ADA/local code), compliance status (compliant/non-compliant/remediation-in-progress), and responsible maintenance team. Supports ADA compliance reporting and fan accessibility experience management.'
}

  staff_plan_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the event-day staffing plan record. Primary key for the venue_staff_plan data product.']
  budget_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.budget. Business justification: Staff plans are built against approved labor budgets. A venue operations finance team expects staff_plan.budget_id for budget vs. actual labor cost variance reporting. Existing estimated_cost_usd/actu']
  capacity_config_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to venue.capacity_config. Business justification: A staff plan is created in response to a specific capacity configuration for an event. The capacity_config determines total sellable capacity, GA capacity, suite capacity, and VIP capacity — all of wh']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Event staffing plans are budgeted against cost centers for labor cost control and variance analysis. Finance teams compare estimated_cost_usd vs. actual_cost_usd by cost center for each event; the den']
  emergency_response_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to security.emergency_response_plan. Business justification: Staff plans must satisfy minimum security staffing requirements mandated by the applicable ERP. Venue operations and security directors validate staff plans against ERP-specified minimums (medical sta']
  facility_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the venue facility for which this staffing plan applies. Ties the plan to the physical venue master record.']
