Business justification: Staff plans must comply with labor law obligations (CBA minimum staffing ratios, OSHA crowd management staffing requirements). staff_plan has is_osha_safety_critical and cba_applicable flags but no FK']
  primary_staff_event_fixture_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific event for which this staffing plan is created. Links the labor demand plan to the scheduled live event.']
  production_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to broadcast.production. Business justification: Staff plans covering broadcast crew roles (camera operators, production assistants, broadcast technicians) are directly scoped to a specific broadcast production. The \'broadcast production staffing\' p']
  production_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to event.production_schedule. Business justification: Venue staffing plans are coordinated directly with the event production schedule — security briefing times, gate open times, and load-in windows on staff_plan must align with production_schedule timin']
  regulatory_filing_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_filing. Business justification: Large event staff plans (crowd management plans, security deployment plans) are submitted to local authorities as regulatory filings. Linking staff_plan to regulatory_filing supports the crowd managem']
  revised_staff_plan_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on staff_plan (revised_staff_plan_id)']
  actual_cost_usd decimal(14,2) [note: 'Actual labor cost in US dollars incurred for this role category after the event, as recorded in the financial system. Compared against estimated_cost_usd for variance analysis.']
  actual_headcount varchar [note: 'Actual number of staff who reported and worked for this role category on event day. Captured post-event for variance analysis against required and confirmed headcount.']
  agency_purchase_order_number varchar [note: 'SAP purchase order number issued to the staffing agency for this role category. Required for accounts payable processing and agency invoice matching.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this staffing plan was formally approved by the designated authority. Marks the transition from submitted to approved status.']
  briefing_location varchar [note: 'Physical location within or near the venue where staff in this role category are required to report for pre-event briefing and credential check-in.']
  briefing_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Scheduled date and time for the pre-event staff briefing for this role category. Must occur before shift_start_timestamp.']
  cba_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the Collective Bargaining Agreement governs the employment terms for staff in this role category. Triggers CBA compliance checks on pay rates, shift durations, and break requirements.']
  confirmed_headcount varchar [note: 'Number of staff positions confirmed and scheduled for this role category as of the latest plan update. Compared against required_headcount to identify fulfillment gaps.']
  contingency_headcount varchar [note: 'Number of additional staff positions held in reserve for this role category to cover no-shows, emergencies, or unexpected demand surges on event day.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this staffing plan record was first created in the system. Audit trail field for data lineage and compliance reporting.']
  credential_requirements varchar [note: 'Description of required certifications, licenses, or credentials that staff in this role category must hold (e.g., CPR certification, security guard license, food handler permit, AV technician certification).']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary amounts in this staffing plan record (e.g., USD, GBP, EUR). Supports multi-currency operations across global venues.']
  deployment_zone varchar [note: 'Venue zone or area where staff in this role category are assigned to operate (e.g., Gate A, Concourse Level 2, Parking Lot C, Field Level). Supports event-day logistics coordination.']
  employment_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the employment arrangement for staff in this role category. Determines payroll processing, CBA applicability, and agency billing procedures.']
  estimated_cost_usd decimal(14,2) [note: 'Total estimated labor cost in US dollars for this role category for the event, calculated from required headcount, shift duration, and pay rate. Used for event budget planning.']
  event_type_category varchar [note: 'Broad classification of the event type driving this staffing plan. Used to apply standard staffing templates and benchmarks appropriate to the event category.']
  fulfillment_source varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the staffing demand for this role category is fulfilled by in-house employees, an external staffing agency, a combination of both, or volunteers.']
  is_ada_role boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this role category is specifically designated to support ADA accessibility services for guests with disabilities (e.g., wheelchair escorts, hearing loop operators).']
  is_osha_safety_critical boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this role category is classified as safety-critical under OSHA standards, requiring mandatory minimum staffing levels and specific credential verification before event approval.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this staffing plan record was most recently modified. Supports change tracking and audit trail requirements.']
  minimum_headcount varchar [note: 'Minimum number of staff for this role category below which the event cannot safely proceed. Used as the go/no-go threshold for operational readiness approval.']
  pay_rate_usd decimal(10,2) [note: 'Planned hourly pay rate in US dollars for staff in this role category. Used for cost estimation and budget reconciliation. Classified as confidential business data.']
  plan_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name identifying this staffing plan, typically including event name, date, and role category (e.g., \'NBA Finals 2025 – Security Plan\').']
  plan_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text operational notes or special instructions for this staffing plan record, such as event-specific requirements, weather contingencies, or VIP security protocols.']
  plan_reference_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric reference number assigned to this staffing plan for cross-system tracking and vendor communication (e.g., VSP-2025-0042).']
  plan_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the staffing plan. Tracks progression from initial draft through approval and execution to completion or cancellation.']
  plan_version varchar [note: 'Incremental version number of the staffing plan. Incremented each time the plan is revised and resubmitted for approval, enabling audit trail of changes.']
  rejection_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text explanation of why this staffing plan was rejected during the approval workflow. Populated only when plan_status is set to a rejected or revision-required state.']
  required_headcount varchar [note: 'Total number of staff positions required for this role category to safely and operationally execute the event. Represents the labor demand target.']
  role_category varchar [note: 'Operational role category for which this staffing plan row specifies headcount requirements. Each plan record covers one role category per event. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: security|medical|usher|concessions|parking|cleaning|av_production|guest_services|other — promote to reference product]']
  shift_duration_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Total planned shift duration in hours for this role category, derived from shift start and end timestamps. Used for cost estimation and CBA compliance checks.']
  shift_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Planned date and time when staff in this role category are scheduled to end their shift, including post-event teardown and debrief time.']
  shift_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Planned date and time when staff in this role category are scheduled to begin their shift, including pre-event setup and briefing time.']
  supervisor_name varchar [note: 'Name of the designated supervisor or team lead responsible for managing staff in this role category on event day. Business reference field, not direct PII in operational context.']
  uniform_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the required uniform or dress code for staff in this role category. Used for procurement planning and event-day identification of staff.']

  Note: 'Event-day staffing plan for a facility specifying required headcount by role (security, medical, ushers, concessions, parking, cleaning, AV/production) for a specific event or event type. Attributes include plan name, event reference, facility reference, role category, required headcount, confirmed headcount, staffing agency reference, shift start/end times, credential requirements, cost estimate, actual cost, and plan approval status. Distinct from workforce domain employee records — this is the venue\'s operational labor demand and fulfillment plan for event execution.'
}

Table venue.venue {
  venue_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for venue']
  company_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.company_code. Business justification: Venues operate under specific legal entities (company codes) for financial consolidation, tax reporting, and SEC filings. A sports-entertainment CFO expects venue.company_code_id for entity-level fina']
  league_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to league.league. Business justification: Venues are formally affiliated with specific leagues for scheduling, broadcast rights, and compliance reporting. venue.league_affiliation_code is a denormalized reference to the league; replacing it w']
  parent_venue_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on venue (parent_venue_id)']
  ada_accessible_seat_count varchar [note: 'Total number of wheelchair-accessible and ADA-compliant seating positions in the venue. Reported to ADA compliance authorities and used in Archtics manifest configuration to ensure minimum accessible inventory per event.']
  ada_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the venue meets all Americans with Disabilities Act (ADA) accessibility requirements as verified by the most recent inspection. Mandatory for public event hosting and federal funding eligibility.']
  address_line1 varchar [note: 'Primary street address of the venue. Used for logistics, emergency services coordination, fan-facing directions, and regulatory filings with OSHA and ADA authorities.']
  address_line2 varchar [note: 'Secondary address detail (suite, building, gate number) for the venue. Supplements address_line1 for precise logistics and emergency response routing.']
  broadcast_production_level varchar [note: 'Classification of the venue\'s broadcast infrastructure capability. Determines eligibility for national network broadcasts, OTT (Over-The-Top Streaming) productions, PPV (Pay-Per-View) events, and RSN (Regional Sports Network) feeds. Aligns with Dalet Galaxy broadcast workflow configurations. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: tier_1_national|tier_2_regional|tier_3_local|ott_enabled|ppv_capable|ob_unit_required — promote to reference product]']
  city varchar [note: 'City in which the venue is located. Used for market segmentation, broadcast blackout rule application (FCC RSN regulations), and fan engagement geo-targeting in Adobe Experience Platform.']
  concession_stand_count varchar [note: 'Total number of active concession stands and food and beverage service points within the venue. Used for event-day staffing plans, revenue forecasting, and health inspection scheduling.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'SAP S/4HANA CO cost center code assigned to the venue for financial controlling, expense allocation, and EBITDA (Earnings Before Interest Taxes Depreciation and Amortization) reporting. Maps venue operational costs to the correct organizational unit.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 three-letter country code for the venue location. Determines applicable regulatory frameworks (GDPR for EU venues, CCPA for US venues), currency defaults, and international broadcast rights windows.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the venue master record was first created in the lakehouse Silver layer. RECORD_AUDIT_CREATED field for the MASTER_RESOURCE role. Supports data lineage, audit trails, and SOX compliance.']
  gross_sqft varchar [note: 'Total gross interior square footage of the venue facility. Used for HVAC load calculations, BMS zone planning, lease valuation, and ISO 20121 sustainability reporting.']
  home_tenant_name varchar [note: 'Name of the primary professional team or organization that uses the venue as its home facility. Drives league scheduling, CBA (Collective Bargaining Agreement) compliance, and revenue-sharing calculations in SAP S/4HANA SD.']
  hvac_system_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the Heating, Ventilation, and Air Conditioning (HVAC) system installed in the venue. Drives BMS (Building Management System) integration, energy consumption reporting for ISO 20121, and OSHA indoor air quality compliance.']
  iso_20121_cert_expiry_date date [note: 'Expiry date of the venue\'s ISO 20121 Event Sustainability Management System certification. Triggers recertification workflows and may affect eligibility for international event bids.']
  iso_20121_certified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the venue holds a current ISO 20121 Event Sustainability Management System certification. Required for hosting major international events (FIFA, IOC) and ESG reporting.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the venue master record. Supports incremental ETL processing, change data capture, and audit trail requirements under SOX and GDPR.']
  latitude decimal(9,6) [note: 'Geographic latitude coordinate of the venue in decimal degrees (WGS 84). Enables geo-spatial analytics, fan proximity targeting in Adobe Experience Platform, and emergency services location broadcasting.']
  longitude decimal(9,6) [note: 'Geographic longitude coordinate of the venue in decimal degrees (WGS 84). Paired with latitude for geo-spatial analytics, broadcast territory mapping, and logistics routing.']
  max_event_capacity_override varchar [note: 'Authority-approved override to the standard total_capacity for specific event types (e.g., festival floor standing, SRO configurations). Must be documented with fire marshal approval. Nullable when no override is in effect.']
  naming_rights_expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which the current naming-rights sponsorship agreement expires. Triggers renewal pipeline activity in Salesforce CRM and venue_name update workflows. Commercially sensitive contract data.']
  naming_rights_holder varchar [note: 'Name of the corporate sponsor that has purchased naming rights for the venue. Commercially sensitive sponsorship data managed in Salesforce CRM. Impacts venue_name display and brand compliance obligations.']
  operational_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the venue indicating whether it is available for event scheduling and operations. LIFECYCLE_STATUS for the MASTER_RESOURCE role. Drives event eligibility, maintenance planning, and capacity availability in Archtics and Ticketmaster.']
  osha_safety_rating varchar [note: 'Current OSHA occupational safety and health compliance rating for the venue as determined by the most recent regulatory inspection. Drives event hosting eligibility and insurance premium calculations.']
  ownership_structure varchar [note: 'Legal ownership and operational control arrangement for the venue. Determines financial reporting treatment in SAP S/4HANA FI/CO, lease accounting obligations under IFRS 16, and naming-rights contract eligibility.']
  parking_capacity varchar [note: 'Total number of vehicle parking spaces available across all venue-managed lots and structures. Used for event-day logistics planning, parking revenue forecasting, and ADA accessible parking compliance.']
  playing_surface_type varchar [note: 'Type of playing surface installed in the primary competition area. Governs league-mandated surface standards (NFL, FIFA, MLB), athlete injury risk assessments, and maintenance scheduling. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: natural_grass|artificial_turf|hardwood|ice|clay|synthetic_clay|dirt_infield|concrete|rubberized_track — promote to reference product]']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code of the venue address. Used for tax rate lookup, fan proximity analytics, and emergency services dispatch coordination.']
  premium_suite_count varchar [note: 'Total number of premium luxury suites available in the venue. Drives suite license revenue forecasting in SAP S/4HANA SD, Salesforce CRM pipeline management, and VIP hospitality capacity planning.']
  primary_sport_category varchar [note: 'The principal sport or entertainment category the venue is designed and configured to host. Informs league governance requirements (NFL, NBA, MLB, NHL, MLS, FIFA), playing surface specifications, and broadcast infrastructure standards. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: football|basketball|baseball|hockey|soccer|tennis|motorsport|mixed_use|concert_entertainment|combat_sports — promote to reference product]']
  roof_type varchar [note: 'Structural roof configuration of the venue. Determines weather contingency planning, HVAC system design, broadcast lighting requirements, and event cancellation insurance triggers.']
  scoreboard_type varchar [note: 'Type and display technology of the primary scoreboard and video board system. Impacts broadcast production quality, sponsor digital signage inventory, and fan engagement experience design.']
  seating_bowl_config_code varchar [note: 'Reference code for the default seating bowl layout configuration as defined in Archtics. Links to the active capacity_config record and drives manifest generation in Ticketmaster.']
  state_province varchar [note: 'State or province in which the venue is located. Drives tax jurisdiction determination in SAP S/4HANA FI, CCPA applicability for California venues, and RSN broadcast territory assignments.']
  time_zone varchar [note: 'IANA time zone identifier for the venue (e.g., America/New_York). Critical for event scheduling, broadcast window calculations, gate-open timestamp normalization, and cross-market reporting in Workday Adaptive Planning.']
  total_capacity varchar [note: 'Maximum total spectator capacity of the venue as approved by local fire and safety authorities. Serves as the MEASUREMENT_OR_VALUE (principal quantitative fact) for the MASTER_RESOURCE role. Basis for sellable capacity calculations, fire code compliance, and insurance underwriting.']
  venue_code varchar [note: 'Short, externally-known alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the venue across operational systems (Archtics, Ticketmaster, SAP). Used as the BUSINESS_IDENTIFIER for cross-system reconciliation and manifest references.']
  venue_name varchar [note: 'Official public-facing name of the venue as marketed to fans and broadcast partners. Reflects current naming-rights branding. Serves as the IDENTITY_LABEL for the venue master resource.']
  venue_type varchar [note: 'Primary structural and functional classification of the venue. Drives capacity configuration rules, broadcast infrastructure requirements, and ADA compliance checklists. CLASSIFICATION_OR_TYPE for the MASTER_RESOURCE role. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: stadium|arena|amphitheater|ballpark|racetrack|multipurpose_complex|concert_hall|training_facility|esports_arena — promote to reference product]']
  wifi_capacity_mbps varchar [note: 'Total available Wi-Fi network bandwidth in megabits per second across the venue. Supports fan digital engagement (Adobe Experience Platform), mobile ticketing (Ticketmaster), POS (Point of Sale) systems, and OTT broadcast uplink requirements.']
  year_built varchar [note: 'Calendar year in which the venue was originally constructed. Used for asset depreciation schedules in SAP S/4HANA FI, structural inspection planning, and historical context in league governance records.']
  year_last_renovated varchar [note: 'Calendar year of the most recent major renovation or capital improvement project. Informs ADA compliance status, BMS system currency, and capital expenditure planning in Workday Adaptive Planning.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for venue. '
}

Table venue.parking_activation {
  parking_activation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for the parking_activation association']
  event_day_ops_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking this parking activation record to the event-day operational record for which this lot is being activated.']
  parking_lot_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking this parking activation record to the specific parking lot being activated for the event.']
  activation_status varchar [note: 'Operational status of this parking lot for the specific event. Indicates whether the lot is open to patrons, closed, on standby, or cancelled for this event. Sourced from detection phase relationship data.']
  active_parking_lot_count varchar [note: 'Number of parking lots or structures that were open and actively managed during the event. Used for parking revenue reconciliation and fan ingress/egress logistics analysis. [Moved from event_day_ops: This aggregate count on event_day_ops is a derived metric that can be computed from the parking_activation association (COUNT of records with activation_status = \'open\' for a given event_day_ops_id). Storing it as a denormalized count on event_day_ops creates a maintenance burden and risks inconsistency with the activation records. It should be removed from event_day_ops and derived via query from this association table.]']
  close_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Actual date and time at which this specific parking lot was closed and cleared following the event. Used for post-event reconciliation and security sweep confirmation. Sourced from detection phase relationship data.']
  open_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Actual date and time at which this specific parking lot was opened to patrons for this event. Distinct from the venue gates_open_timestamp — lots may open earlier for VIP or accessible patrons. Sourced from detection phase relationship data.']
  shuttle_active_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether shuttle service between this parking lot and the venue entrance is active for this event. Relevant for remote and off-site lots. Sourced from detection phase relationship data.']
  spaces_allocated_for_event varchar [note: 'Number of parking spaces designated for use during this specific event. May differ from the lot\'s total capacity (parking_lot.accessible_spaces, total spaces) due to reserved sections, construction, or VIP holds. Sourced from detection phase relationship data.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the Event (activation) between event_day_ops and parking_lot. It captures the operational record of which parking lots are activated for a specific event, including per-lot capacity allocation, open/close timing, shuttle status, and activation state. Each record links one event_day_ops to one parking_lot with attributes that exist only in the context of this activation relationship — data that belongs neither to the event-day operation record nor to the parking lot master record alone.. Existence Justification: In live event venue operations, parking lot activation is a genuinely managed business process: operations managers explicitly open and close specific lots for each event, allocate capacity per lot, and track shuttle status and timing. A single event_day_ops record activates multiple parking lots, and a single parking lot is activated across many different event_day_ops records over its lifetime. The relationship itself — which lots are open, when, with how many spaces allocated — is operationally managed and carries its own data.'
}

Table venue.config_section_allocation {
  config_section_allocation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for the config_section_allocation association']
  capacity_config_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the capacity configuration that governs this section allocation. Identifies which event-format or configuration profile this allocation record belongs to.']
  seating_section_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the seating section being allocated or configured within this capacity profile. Identifies which physical section of the venue this record governs.']
  access_restriction varchar [note: 'Configuration-specific access restriction applied to this seating section under this capacity profile. Examples: \'credential_required\', \'vip_only\', \'staff_only\', \'no_public_access\'. Null if no restriction beyond standard ticketing applies. Belongs to the allocation, not the section or config independently.']
  allocated_capacity varchar [note: 'Number of seats or positions in this seating section that are active and available for sale or assignment under this specific capacity configuration. Differs from the section\'s total capacity when partial kills or restrictions apply.']
  kill_seat_count_for_section varchar [note: 'Number of seats within this specific seating section that are killed (removed from sellable inventory) under this capacity configuration. Section-level kill count, distinct from the config-level aggregate kill_seat_count on capacity_config.']
  price_zone_override varchar [note: 'Config-specific price zone code that overrides the seating section\'s default price_zone_code for this capacity configuration. Used when a concert layout reprices sections differently from the standard sports manifest. Null if the section\'s default price zone applies.']
  section_status_in_config varchar [note: 'Operational status of this seating section within the capacity configuration. \'open\' means fully active; \'partial\' means partially activated with kills; \'killed\' means all seats removed from inventory; \'closed\' means physically inaccessible; \'restricted\' means access-limited. This status belongs to neither the config nor the section alone — it is specific to their combination.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the operational allocation contract between a capacity_config and a seating_section. It captures how each seating section is configured — activated, killed, or restricted — within a specific capacity configuration profile. Each record links one capacity_config to one seating_section and carries attributes that exist only in the context of that pairing: how many seats are allocated, how many are killed, the section\'s operational status within the config, any price zone override, and access restrictions. Directly analogous to the manifest line concept in Archtics and Ticketmaster venue ticketing platforms.. Existence Justification: In professional sports and live entertainment venue operations, a capacity configuration (e.g., \'End-Stage Concert\', \'Full Bowl Sports\', \'Half-House Hockey\') explicitly governs which seating sections are active, how many seats are allocated, which sections are killed, and what price zone overrides apply — for that specific configuration. A single capacity_config spans many seating_sections, and a single seating_section participates in many capacity_configs over its operational lifetime (e.g., Section 101 is open for a sold-out game, partially killed for a smaller event, and entirely closed for a concert stage setup). This is the \'manifest\' concept in Archtics ticketing platforms — a recognized operational entity that venue operations teams actively create, update, and manage per event format.'
}

Table athlete.roster {
  roster_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each roster record. Serves as the primary key for the athlete-team-season affiliation record in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Entity role: MASTER_AGREEMENT — this record represents a binding registration/affiliation between an athlete and a team within a league season.']
  agent_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the certified player agent or agency representing the athlete for this roster record. Used for contract negotiations, trade communications, and CBA-mandated agent certification compliance.']
  announcement_asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to content.asset. Business justification: Roster transactions (trades, waivers, activations, releases) generate announcement content assets for media and fan communications. roster.transaction_type and transaction_date confirm this is a trans']
  contract_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the active player contract record governing this roster slot. Links to the athlete contract master for salary, incentives, and CBA compliance details. Confidential as it references financial agreement data.']
  eligibility_certification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.eligibility_certification. Business justification: Roster activation requires a valid eligibility certification — teams must verify governing body certification before activating a player on game-day rosters. roster has wada_clearance_status and activ']
  eligibility_status_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to athlete.eligibility_status. Business justification: A roster entry\'s eligibility to compete is governed by the authoritative eligibility_status record. The roster table currently stores eligibility_status as a denormalized STRING field, duplicating the']
  league_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the league or competition governing body under which this roster record is maintained. Supports multi-league operations (NFL, NBA, MLB, NHL, MLS, FIFA, etc.).']
  primary_roster_franchise_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the team or franchise master record to which the athlete is currently rostered. Establishes the athlete-team affiliation for the given season.']
  roster_athlete_athlete_profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the athlete master record. Identifies the specific player registered on this roster slot. Used as the primary link to athlete identity, contract, and performance data.']
  roster_athlete_profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the athlete master record. Identifies the specific player registered on this roster slot. Used as the primary link to athlete identity, contract, and performance data.']
  roster_franchise_id varchar [note: 'FK to league.franchise.franchise_id — Roster-to-franchise linkage — must know which franchise a roster belongs to for cap compliance, trade eligibility, and league governance.']
  roster_loaned_from_team_franchise_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the originating team from which the athlete is on loan to the current roster. Applicable in soccer (MLS, FIFA) and hockey (NHL) loan/conditioning assignment scenarios. Null if the athlete is not on loan.']
  season_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the competitive season for which this roster record is valid. Enables point-in-time roster snapshots across multiple seasons.']
  transaction_window_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to league.transaction_window. Business justification: Roster activations, deactivations, and IR designations must occur within valid transaction windows. Linking roster to transaction_window enables transaction window compliance validation for roster mov']
  activation_date date [note: 'The date on which the athlete was officially activated onto this roster slot. Marks the effective start of the athlete-team affiliation for this record. Critical for CBA compliance, salary cap calculations, and eligibility verification.']
  cap_hit_currency varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the salary cap hit amount (e.g., USD, CAD, EUR, GBP). Required for multi-league international operations where cap values may be denominated in different currencies.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this roster record was first created in the system. Provides the audit trail creation marker for data governance, SOX compliance, and lineage tracking in the Silver Layer.']
  deactivation_date date [note: 'The date on which the athlete was removed from this roster slot (via trade, waiver, release, retirement, or season end). Null if the roster record is currently active. Enables point-in-time roster reconstruction for any historical date.']
  depth_chart_rank varchar [note: 'The athlete\'s positional depth chart ranking within the team (1 = starter, 2 = first backup, etc.). Used for game-day roster decisions, broadcast graphics, and performance analytics. Updated by coaching staff via SportsCode/Hudl.']
  draft_pick_number varchar [note: 'The overall pick number at which the athlete was selected in the league draft. Used for historical analysis, WAR (Wins Above Replacement) benchmarking, and broadcast content. Null for undrafted players.']
  draft_round varchar [note: 'The round in which the athlete was selected during the league draft. Used for historical analysis, broadcast content, and talent evaluation benchmarking. Null for undrafted players.']
  draft_year varchar [note: 'The calendar year in which the athlete was originally drafted into the league, if applicable. Used for service time calculations, CBA eligibility tiers, and broadcast storytelling. Null for undrafted free agents or international transfers.']
  effective_from date [note: 'The date from which this roster record version is considered authoritative and binding. Supports Slowly Changing Dimension (SCD) Type 2 history tracking in the Silver Layer, enabling point-in-time roster reconstruction for any historical date.']
  effective_until date [note: 'The date until which this roster record version is considered authoritative. Null indicates the current active version. Supports SCD Type 2 history tracking and point-in-time roster queries for league operations, broadcast, and compliance.']
  international_slot_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this roster slot is designated as an international player slot under league rules (e.g., MLS international roster designation, NHL Entry Level Contract international provisions). Affects roster construction compliance and salary cap treatment.']
  is_captain boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the athlete holds the official team captain designation for the current season. Used in broadcast graphics, fan engagement content, and league ceremonial protocols.']
  jersey_number varchar [note: 'The uniform number assigned to the athlete on this roster for the current season. Used in broadcast graphics, ticketing systems, merchandise, and fan engagement platforms. Must be unique within a team roster for a given season.']
  nationality_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code representing the athlete\'s primary nationality. Used for international roster compliance (e.g., MLS international roster slots, FIFA international transfer rules), visa tracking, and broadcast content.']
  nil_agreement_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the athlete has an active NIL (Name, Image, and Likeness) agreement registered with the league or team. Relevant for collegiate athletes transitioning to professional rosters and for compliance with NIL disclosure rules.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for league operations staff to record supplementary information about this roster record, such as conditional activation clauses, special designations, or transaction context not captured in structured fields.']
  position_code varchar [note: 'Standardized league-defined code for the athlete\'s primary playing position (e.g., QB, WR, C, PG, SG, SP, RP, LW, RW, FWD, MID, DEF, GK). Used in performance analytics, broadcast overlays, and roster compliance checks. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: QB|WR|RB|TE|OL|DL|LB|DB|K|P|PG|SG|SF|PF|C|SP|RP|CP|1B|2B|3B|SS|OF|LW|RW|FWD|MID|DEF|GK — promote to reference product]']
  position_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable full name of the athlete\'s primary playing position (e.g., \'Quarterback\', \'Point Guard\', \'Starting Pitcher\', \'Left Wing\', \'Goalkeeper\'). Used in broadcast graphics, fan-facing content, and reporting.']
  practice_squad_elevation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the athlete has been temporarily elevated from the practice squad to the active roster for a specific game week, per NFL and other league rules allowing short-term elevations without permanently activating the player.']
  roster_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for this roster record, typically assigned by the league operations system. Used in official league communications, broadcast graphics, and regulatory filings. Example: \'NFL-2024-KC-0042\'.']
  roster_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the athlete\'s roster slot. Drives eligibility for game participation, payroll processing, and broadcast credentialing. \'active\' = eligible to play; \'inactive\' = on roster but not eligible for current game; \'injured_reserve\' = on IR per league rules; \'practice_squad\' = developmental squad; \'waived\' = released from roster pending waiver wire.']
  salary_cap_hit decimal(15,2) [note: 'The annual salary cap charge attributed to this athlete\'s roster slot for the current season, expressed in USD. Used for cap compliance reporting, trade analysis, and financial planning. Governed by CBA salary cap rules.']
  service_years decimal(4,2) [note: 'Accumulated years of professional service for the athlete within the league, calculated per CBA rules. Determines eligibility for free agency, arbitration rights, minimum salary tiers, and pension benefits. Expressed as a decimal (e.g., 3.172 years).']
  slot_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the roster slot category as defined by league rules. Determines salary cap implications, game eligibility, and CBA compliance. \'active_roster\' = primary game-day roster; \'practice_squad\' = developmental; \'injured_reserve\' = IR designation; \'reserve\' = non-IR reserve list; \'taxi_squad\' = NHL-specific developmental designation.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the operational system of record from which this roster record was ingested. Supports data lineage tracking in the Silver Layer lakehouse. \'SAP_SF\' = SAP SuccessFactors HCM; \'ARCHTICS\' = Archtics Venue/Roster System; \'LEAGUE_API\' = direct league data feed.']
  transaction_date date [note: 'The official date on which the roster transaction (signing, trade, waiver, draft, transfer) was executed and reported to the league. May differ from activation_date if there is a processing lag.']
  transaction_type varchar [note: 'The type of transaction that created this roster record. Indicates how the athlete came to be on this roster slot. Used for league transaction reporting, broadcast graphics, and historical analysis. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: signed|traded|waived|released|drafted|transferred|loaned|retired — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp of the most recent update to this roster record. Used for change detection in ETL pipelines, audit trails, and data freshness monitoring in the Silver Layer lakehouse.']
  visa_expiry_date date [note: 'The expiration date of the athlete\'s current work visa or immigration authorization. Used to trigger renewal workflows and ensure continuous roster eligibility for international players. Confidential HR/immigration data.']
  visa_type varchar [note: 'Type of work visa or immigration authorization held by the athlete for the jurisdiction in which they are rostered (e.g., P-1A for internationally recognized athletes in the USA, O-1 for extraordinary ability). Required for international roster compliance and HR onboarding. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: P-1A|O-1|TN|H-1B|J-1|work_permit|citizen|permanent_resident — promote to reference product]']
  wada_clearance_status varchar [note: 'Current anti-doping clearance status for the athlete as reported by WADA or the applicable league anti-doping program. \'cleared\' = no active violations; \'pending\' = test results under review; \'suspended\' = serving a PED (Performance Enhancing Drug) suspension; \'exempted\' = holds a Therapeutic Use Exemption (TUE).']
  wada_last_test_date date [note: 'Date of the athlete\'s most recent WADA-compliant anti-doping test. Used to verify testing frequency compliance and support league anti-doping audit trails.']

  Note: 'Master record of all athletes registered to a team or franchise within a league season. Tracks active, inactive, injured reserve (IR), practice squad, and waived status. Serves as the authoritative SSOT for athlete-team affiliation at any point in time, including jersey number, position designation, roster slot type, and activation dates. Critical for league operations, broadcast graphics, and ticketing systems.'
}

Table athlete.athlete_profile {
  athlete_profile_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the athlete profile record. Primary key for the athlete_profile data product and the single source of truth (SSOT) reference key used by all downstream athlete entities.']
  agent_id varchar [note: 'FK to athlete.agent']
  league_id varchar [note: 'FK to league.league']
  team_id varchar [note: 'FK to league.team']
  athlete_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the individual\'s role within the sports organization. Distinguishes active players from coaching staff, officials, prospects, and support personnel for roster management and credentialing.']
  bio varchar [note: 'Official short-form biographical narrative for the athlete, approved for public use in broadcast, digital platforms, and media guides. Managed through the content production workflow.']
  career_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the athlete\'s professional career. Drives roster eligibility, broadcast availability, contract activation, and league reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: active|retired|suspended|injured_reserve|deceased|inactive — promote to reference product]']
  country_of_birth_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code for the country where the athlete was born. Distinct from nationality; used for eligibility determinations, international federation registration, and demographic analytics.']
  data_source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record from which this athlete profile record was sourced or last updated (e.g., \'successfactors\', \'league_registration\'). Used for data lineage, reconciliation, and audit purposes in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  date_of_birth date [note: 'Athlete\'s date of birth in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-dd). Used for age eligibility verification, draft classification, CBA compliance, and league registration.']
  dominant_foot varchar [note: 'The athlete\'s dominant foot for sports where foot preference is analytically significant (e.g., soccer, rugby). Used in performance analytics, tactical planning, and broadcast commentary.']
  dominant_hand varchar [note: 'The athlete\'s dominant hand (throwing, batting, or shooting hand). Critical for performance analytics, matchup strategy, broadcast commentary, and scouting reports.']
  draft_pick_number varchar [note: 'The overall pick number at which the athlete was selected in the league draft. Used for historical analytics, contract benchmarking, and scouting model validation.']
  draft_round varchar [note: 'The round number in which the athlete was selected during the league draft. Used for contract tier classification, scouting evaluation benchmarking, and CBA (Collective Bargaining Agreement) rookie scale assignment.']
  draft_year varchar [note: 'The calendar year in which the athlete was selected in the league draft. Used for career tenure analytics, contract eligibility windows, CBA (Collective Bargaining Agreement) rookie scale calculations, and historical reporting.']
  eligibility_status varchar [note: 'Current league eligibility status of the athlete, reflecting compliance with CBA (Collective Bargaining Agreement), anti-doping (WADA), and disciplinary rules. Distinct from career_status — an athlete may be active but temporarily ineligible.']
  headshot_image_url varchar [note: 'URL reference to the athlete\'s official headshot photograph stored in the Digital Asset Management (DAM) system. Used for broadcast overlays, fan-facing platforms, merchandise, and media kits.']
  height_cm decimal(5,1) [note: 'Athlete\'s official measured height in centimetres as recorded at league registration or combine. Used for scouting analytics, broadcast graphics, merchandise sizing, and physical eligibility assessments.']
  injury_status varchar [note: 'Current injury designation for the athlete as reported to the league. Drives roster availability, broadcast commentary, fantasy sports data feeds, and medical staff workflows. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: healthy|day_to_day|injured_reserve|physically_unable|out_for_season — promote to reference product]']
  jersey_number varchar [note: 'The official jersey/squad number assigned to the athlete on their current roster. Used for broadcast graphics, merchandise production, ticketing displays, and fan engagement content.']
  league_registration_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique identifier assigned by the governing league or federation upon official registration (e.g., NFL Player ID, FIFA Registration Number). Used for inter-system reconciliation, transfer clearances, and regulatory filings.']
  legal_first_name varchar [note: 'Athlete\'s legal given name as recorded on government-issued identification documents. Used for contract execution, league registration, and regulatory compliance filings.']
  legal_last_name varchar [note: 'Athlete\'s legal family/surname as recorded on government-issued identification documents. Used for contract execution, league registration, and regulatory compliance filings.']
  legal_middle_name varchar [note: 'Athlete\'s legal middle name or initial as recorded on government-issued identification. May be required for full legal name matching in contract and compliance contexts.']
  nationality_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code representing the athlete\'s nationality as declared on their passport or official registration. Governs international transfer eligibility, work permit requirements, and national team selection.']
  nil_agreement_active boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the athlete currently has one or more active NIL (Name, Image, and Likeness) agreements in force. Used to trigger compliance checks, sponsorship conflict reviews, and revenue tracking workflows.']
  preferred_name varchar [note: 'The name the athlete prefers to be publicly known by, such as a nickname or shortened name (e.g., \'LeBron\' or \'Messi\'). Used in broadcast graphics, merchandise, and fan-facing content.']
  primary_position varchar [note: 'The athlete\'s primary playing position as designated by the league or team (e.g., \'Quarterback\', \'Center Forward\', \'Pitcher\', \'Point Guard\'). Critical for performance analytics, WAR/ERA/QBR calculations, and broadcast commentary.']
  pro_debut_date date [note: 'The date on which the athlete made their first official professional appearance. Used for career tenure calculations, milestone tracking, broadcast storytelling, and eligibility determinations.']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this athlete profile record was first created in the Databricks Silver Layer data product. Used for data lineage, audit trails, and GDPR data subject request processing.']
  record_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this athlete profile record was most recently modified in the Databricks Silver Layer data product. Used for change data capture, incremental processing, and audit compliance.']
  retirement_date date [note: 'The official date on which the athlete formally retired from professional competition. Nullable for active athletes. Used for career lifecycle reporting, contract termination processing, and alumni engagement programs.']
  social_media_handle_instagram varchar [note: 'Athlete\'s official Instagram social media handle. Used for fan engagement campaigns, NIL (Name, Image, and Likeness) tracking, digital partnership activations, and influencer analytics.']
  social_media_handle_twitter varchar [note: 'Athlete\'s official X (formerly Twitter) social media handle. Used for fan engagement campaigns, NIL (Name, Image, and Likeness) tracking, digital partnership activations, and social media analytics.']
  source_system_athlete_code varchar [note: 'The native identifier for this athlete in the originating operational system of record (e.g., SAP SuccessFactors Employee ID, league registration system player ID). Used for cross-system reconciliation and data lineage tracing.']
  sport_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code identifying the primary sport the athlete competes in (e.g., \'SOCCER\', \'BASKETBALL\', \'BASEBALL\', \'FOOTBALL\', \'HOCKEY\'). Used for cross-sport analytics, content tagging, and league routing.']
  wada_registered_testing_pool boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the athlete is enrolled in the WADA (World Anti-Doping Agency) Registered Testing Pool (RTP), requiring whereabouts reporting and out-of-competition testing. Critical for anti-doping compliance management.']
  weight_kg decimal(5,1) [note: 'Athlete\'s official measured weight in kilograms as recorded at league registration or combine. Used for scouting analytics, broadcast graphics, health monitoring, and physical eligibility assessments.']

  Note: 'Core master identity record for every athlete managed by Sports Entertainment. Stores biographical data (full legal name, preferred name, date of birth, nationality, country of origin), physical attributes (height, weight, dominant hand/foot), headshot image reference, social media handles, and career status (active, retired, suspended, deceased). This is the SSOT for athlete identity — all other athlete entities reference this record. Sourced from SAP SuccessFactors talent module and league registration systems.'
}

Table athlete.contract {
  contract_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the athlete contract record in the lakehouse silver layer. Primary key for the athlete.contract data product.']
  agent_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the certified player agent or agency representing the athlete in contract negotiations.']
  contract_athlete_athlete_profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the athlete (player) who is the primary party to this contract. Links to the athlete master record.']
  contract_athlete_profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the athlete (player) who is the primary party to this contract. Links to the athlete master record.']
  contract_franchise_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the team or franchise that is the employing party on this contract.']
  contract_team_franchise_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the team or franchise that is the employing party on this contract.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Athlete contracts are assigned to cost centers for payroll cost allocation and budget-vs-actual reporting. Sports finance teams track player compensation by cost center (typically per franchise/team).']
  league_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the league governing this contract, determining which Collective Bargaining Agreement (CBA) rules apply.']
  policy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.policy. Business justification: Athlete contracts reference CBA version and compliance requirements. Linking to compliance.policy enables contract compliance audits, CBA version tracking, and regulatory obligation mapping — a named ']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Athlete contracts are assigned to profit centers for team-level P&L reporting. Each franchise\'s player costs must roll up to a profit center for EBITDA reporting and revenue-sharing calculations. Stan']
  regulatory_filing_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_filing. Business justification: CBA-mandated contract registration filings with league offices and governing bodies are a core operational requirement. Contract registration is a distinct filing from cap entries — teams must submit ']
  season_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to league.season. Business justification: Salary cap compliance reporting and CBA audits require contracts to be associated with the specific league season they are active in. Cap hit calculations, performance bonus eligibility, and contract ']
  transaction_window_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to league.transaction_window. Business justification: League compliance requires all contract signings to occur within approved transaction windows. Tracking which transaction window a contract was executed in is a mandatory CBA compliance and league fil']
  aav decimal(18,2) [note: 'The Annual Average Value (AAV) of the contract, calculated as total contract value divided by contract years. Industry-standard metric used for salary cap comparisons, market benchmarking, and broadcast commentary.']
  base_salary_year1 decimal(18,2) [note: 'The base salary amount for the first year of the contract term. Used for payroll processing in SAP SuccessFactors and salary cap hit calculation in year one.']
  cap_hit decimal(18,2) [note: 'The amount of the team\'s salary cap consumed by this contract in the current league year, calculated per CBA rules (base salary + prorated signing bonus + likely incentives). Critical for roster construction and financial planning.']
  contract_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known, league-assigned or organization-assigned unique contract reference number used in official filings, league submissions, and SAP S/4HANA SD sales contract documents.']
  contract_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the contract. Active: currently in force. Expired: term has concluded. Voided: nullified due to breach, failed physical, or mutual agreement. Restructured: terms modified via amendment. Suspended: temporarily inactive due to disciplinary action or league sanction.']
  contract_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the contract by CBA-defined category. Rookie contracts apply to newly drafted players; veteran contracts apply to experienced players; max contracts represent the maximum allowable salary under the CBA; minimum contracts are the league-mandated floor; two-way contracts span both the primary league and a developmental league. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: rookie|veteran|max|minimum|two_way|10_day|exhibit_10|rest_of_season — promote to reference product]']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this contract record was first created in the system, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Supports audit trail, data lineage, and SOX financial controls compliance.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code in which all monetary values on this contract are denominated (e.g., USD for US Dollar, CAD for Canadian Dollar, EUR for Euro). Required for multi-market leagues such as NHL and MLS.']
  dead_cap_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The salary cap charge that remains against the team\'s cap after the athlete is released or traded, representing unearned prorated signing bonus and guaranteed money. Used for financial planning and roster management.']
  end_date date [note: 'The date on which the contract term concludes. Nullable for open-ended or multi-year contracts with option years pending. Maps to SAP S/4HANA SD contract validity end date.']
  free_agency_class varchar [note: 'The athlete\'s free agency classification upon contract expiration. UFA (Unrestricted Free Agent): no team rights retained. RFA (Restricted Free Agent): team retains right of first refusal. ERFA (Exclusive Rights Free Agent): team holds exclusive negotiating rights. Franchise tag and transition tag are team-applied designations preventing free agency.']
  free_agency_open_date date [note: 'The date on which the athlete becomes eligible to negotiate with other teams as a free agent, as defined by the league calendar and CBA provisions.']
  guaranteed_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The portion of the total contract value that is fully guaranteed regardless of injury, performance, or termination. Critical for salary cap dead money calculations and financial liability reporting.']
  negotiation_status varchar [note: 'Current status of free agency or contract renewal negotiations for this athlete. Tracks the negotiation lifecycle from initial contact through signing or expiration of negotiating rights.']
  nil_agreement_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this contract includes or is associated with a Name, Image and Likeness (NIL) agreement granting the team or league rights to use the athlete\'s identity for commercial purposes.']
  no_cut_clause boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the contract contains a no-cut clause preventing the team from releasing the athlete. True = no-cut clause present; False = standard release provisions apply.']
  no_trade_clause boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the contract contains a no-trade clause (NTC) preventing the team from trading the athlete without their consent. True = NTC present; False = no restriction. Critical for roster transaction processing.']
  option_deadline date [note: 'The deadline date by which the option-holding party must notify the other party of their intent to exercise or decline the option year(s). Missing this deadline typically results in automatic option declination.']
  option_type varchar [note: 'Specifies which party holds the right to exercise the option year(s): team option (team decides), player option (athlete decides), mutual option (both parties must agree), or vesting option (automatically vests upon meeting performance thresholds).']
  option_years varchar [note: 'Number of option years appended to the base contract term. Option years may be exercisable by the team, the player, or mutually, as specified in the contract. Zero if no option years exist.']
  performance_bonus_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the contract includes performance-based incentive bonuses (e.g., statistical thresholds, award bonuses, playoff bonuses). True = performance bonuses are included in the contract terms.']
  performance_bonus_max decimal(18,2) [note: 'The maximum total amount the athlete can earn in performance-based incentive bonuses over the life of the contract. Used for salary cap likely/unlikely incentive classification per CBA rules.']
  physical_date date [note: 'The date on which the athlete underwent the pre-contract medical examination. Used for compliance tracking and contract contingency resolution.']
  physical_passed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the athlete successfully passed the team\'s pre-contract medical examination. Contracts are typically contingent on passing the physical; failure may result in contract voiding or restructuring.']
  qualifying_offer_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The one-year salary amount tendered to a Restricted Free Agent (RFA) as a qualifying offer, which preserves the team\'s right of first refusal. Calculated per CBA formula based on prior year salary.']
  right_of_first_refusal boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the team retains the Right of First Refusal (ROFR) to match any offer sheet signed by the athlete with another team. Applicable to Restricted Free Agents (RFA). True = ROFR applies.']
  roster_status varchar [note: 'The athlete\'s current roster designation under this contract, which affects salary cap treatment and payroll processing. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: active_roster|practice_squad|injured_reserve|suspended|exempt|waived|non_football_injury|physically_unable_to_perform|reserve — promote to reference product]']
  sap_contract_doc_number varchar [note: 'The document number assigned to this contract in SAP S/4HANA SD module for financial obligation tracking, payroll integration with SAP SuccessFactors, and accounts payable processing. Serves as the cross-system integration key.']
  signing_bonus decimal(18,2) [note: 'One-time bonus paid to the athlete upon contract execution. For salary cap purposes, signing bonuses are typically prorated across the contract years. Processed via SAP SuccessFactors Payroll and recorded in SAP S/4HANA FI.']
  signing_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which the athlete and team executed (signed) the contract. This is the principal business event timestamp for the contract lifecycle, distinct from the effective start date.']
  signing_deadline date [note: 'The deadline by which the athlete must sign a new contract or tender to remain with the team or enter free agency. Defined by CBA and league calendar (e.g., franchise tag signing deadline).']
  start_date date [note: 'The date on which the contract becomes effective and binding. Corresponds to the first day of the contract term per CBA rules. Maps to SAP S/4HANA SD contract validity start date.']
  total_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'The gross total compensation value of the contract across all years, including base salary, signing bonus, and performance incentives as stated in the contract. Expressed in the contract currency. Used for salary cap accounting and financial obligation reporting in SAP S/4HANA FI/CO.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this contract record was most recently modified, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Tracks restructures, amendments, and status changes for audit and compliance purposes.']
  wada_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the contract includes WADA anti-doping compliance clauses and the athlete has acknowledged their obligations under the World Anti-Doping Agency (WADA) code. True = WADA compliance clause present and acknowledged.']
  years varchar [note: 'Total number of years covered by the contract term, including option years if exercised. Used for AAV calculation, salary cap projection, and roster planning.']

  Note: 'Athlete employment and service contracts governing the full lifecycle from initial signing through free agency. Tracks contract terms (compensation, duration, performance bonuses, trade clauses, option years, CBA-mandated terms), contract type (rookie, veteran, max, minimum, two-way), total value, guaranteed money, signing bonus, annual average value (AAV), and contract status (active, expired, voided, restructured). Includes free agency lifecycle: classification (UFA, RFA, ERFA, franchise tag, transition tag), qualifying offer amounts, tender amounts, right-of-first-refusal flags, free agency open date, signing deadline, and negotiation status. Integrates with SAP S/4HANA SD module for financial obligations and payroll processing via SAP SuccessFactors. Serves as the SSOT for an athlete\'s complete employment relationship from signing through free agency periods and re-signing.'
}

Table athlete.performance_stat {
  performance_stat_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each performance statistic record in the Silver layer lakehouse. Represents one statistical line for one athlete in one game or aggregated period.']
  facility_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to venue.facility. Business justification: Venue-specific athlete performance analytics — surface type, altitude, crowd density, and facility dimensions directly affect stats. Scouts and coaching staff routinely run facility-level performance ']
  fixture_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific game or match event for which these statistics are recorded. Links to the event or fixture product. Null for season-level or career-level aggregated stat records.']
  game_result_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific game or match event for which these statistics are recorded. Links to the event or fixture product. Null for season-level or career-level aggregated stat records.']
  match_result_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to event.match_result. Business justification: Athlete performance statistics must be reconciled against the authoritative match result for box score publishing, fantasy sports data feeds, and league statistical certification. performance_stat has']
  primary_performance_athlete_athlete_profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the athlete whose performance statistics are captured in this record. Links to the athlete master data product in the talent management domain.']
  primary_performance_franchise_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the team the athlete represented during this statistical period. Captures the team context at the time of the stat, which may differ from current roster assignment due to trades or transfers.']
  roster_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to athlete.roster. Business justification: Performance statistics are recorded for an athlete in the context of a specific roster slot (team + season combination). Linking performance_stat to roster via roster_id enables accurate attribution o']
  season_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the league season or competition year to which this statistical record belongs. Enables season-over-season performance trending and standings calculations.']
  tertiary_performance_opponent_team_franchise_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the opposing team faced during this game. Enables head-to-head performance analysis, matchup-based scouting reports, and broadcast narrative generation. Null for season or career aggregate records.']
  aggregation_method_override varchar [note: 'An optional override to the KPI definition\'s default aggregation method, applied specifically when this stat record contributes to this KPI. Allows governance teams to specify non-standard aggregation for edge-case stat-KPI combinations without altering the master KPI definition.']
  assists varchar [note: 'Number of assists credited to the athlete — passes or actions directly leading to a goal or score. Applicable across soccer, hockey, basketball, and other team sports. Key metric for broadcast overlays and fantasy sports scoring.']
  batting_average decimal(5,3) [note: 'Baseball-specific metric representing the ratio of hits to official at-bats (H/AB), expressed as a three-decimal fraction (e.g., 0.315). Null for non-baseball records. Foundational hitting metric for broadcast overlays, fantasy sports, and contract evaluation.']
  blocks varchar [note: 'Number of blocked shots or blocked passes credited to the athlete. Applicable to basketball (NBA shot blocks) and soccer/hockey (blocked shots). Null for non-applicable sport/position combinations.']
  certification_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time at which the statistical record was officially certified by the league or governing body. Formatted as yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX. Null for provisional or pending records. Required for official standings calculations and regulatory reporting.']
  contribution_weight decimal [note: 'The numeric weight (e.g., 0.0 to 1.0 or a raw multiplier) applied to this performance stat record when computing the target KPI value. Belongs to the relationship because the same stat may carry different weights for different KPIs.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time at which this performance statistic record was first ingested into the Silver layer lakehouse. Formatted as yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX. Supports data lineage, audit trails, and SLA monitoring for the SportsCode/Hudl ingestion pipeline.']
  era decimal(5,2) [note: 'Earned Run Average (ERA) — baseball-specific pitching metric representing the average number of earned runs allowed per nine innings pitched. Null for non-baseball records. Primary pitching performance indicator for broadcast overlays, fantasy sports, and contract evaluation.']
  field_goal_percentage decimal(5,3) [note: 'Ratio of successful field goals made to field goals attempted. Applicable to basketball (NBA) and American football (NFL kickers). Null for non-applicable sport/position combinations. Key shooting efficiency metric for broadcast overlays.']
  fouls_committed varchar [note: 'Total number of fouls committed by the athlete in this statistical period. Applicable to basketball (NBA personal fouls), soccer (FIFA/MLS), and hockey (NHL penalties). Tracks disciplinary and gameplay conduct for compliance and broadcast context.']
  games_played varchar [note: 'Number of games in which the athlete appeared and was credited with participation. For game-level records this is always 1; for season or career records this represents the cumulative count. Used in rate-stat calculations and eligibility determinations.']
  games_started varchar [note: 'Number of games in which the athlete was in the starting lineup. Distinguishes starter versus substitute contributions for roster management, contract incentive tracking, and broadcast narrative context.']
  goals varchar [note: 'Number of goals scored by the athlete in this statistical period. Applicable to soccer (MLS/FIFA), ice hockey (NHL), and other goal-scoring sports. Null for sports where goals are not a primary scoring unit.']
  hudl_stat_record_reference varchar [note: 'The native record identifier assigned by the SportsCode/Hudl performance analytics platform for this statistical entry. Used for source system traceability and reconciliation against the operational system of record.']
  innings_pitched decimal(5,1) [note: 'Total innings pitched by a baseball pitcher in this statistical period, expressed in thirds of an inning (e.g., 6.2 = 6 innings and 2 outs). Null for non-pitcher or non-baseball records. Denominator for ERA and other rate-based pitching metrics.']
  is_home_game boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the athlete\'s team was the home team during this game. True = home team; False = away team. Null for season or career aggregate records. Enables home/away performance split analysis for broadcast storytelling and scouting.']
  is_primary_input boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this performance stat record is the primary driver of the KPI value (true) or a secondary/supplementary input (false). Used by reporting systems to prioritise which stat to surface in broadcast overlays and governance dashboards.']
  minutes_played decimal(7,2) [note: 'Total minutes of active participation recorded for the athlete in this statistical period. Used for per-minute rate statistics, workload management, and injury risk analytics. Applicable to basketball, soccer, hockey, and other timed sports.']
  on_base_percentage decimal(5,3) [note: 'Baseball-specific metric measuring how frequently a batter reaches base per plate appearance, including hits, walks, and hit-by-pitch. Null for non-baseball records. Advanced hitting metric used alongside batting average for comprehensive offensive evaluation.']
  passing_yards varchar [note: 'Total passing yards accumulated by the athlete in this statistical period. NFL-specific metric for quarterbacks and passing plays. Null for non-football records or non-passing positions. Key broadcast overlay and fantasy sports metric.']
  plus_minus varchar [note: 'The plus/minus differential representing the net score differential when the athlete was on the ice or court. Applicable to ice hockey (NHL) and basketball (NBA). Positive values indicate the team outscored opponents during the athlete\'s time on the field. Key advanced analytics input.']
  points_scored varchar [note: 'Total points scored by the athlete in this statistical period. Applicable to basketball (NBA), American football (NFL touchdowns/field goals), and other point-based sports. Null for sports where points are not individually attributed.']
  position_played varchar [note: 'The on-field position the athlete occupied during this game or statistical period (e.g., Quarterback, Pitcher, Point Guard, Center Forward, Goalkeeper). Position context is critical for sport-specific metric interpretation and broadcast overlays.']
  qbr decimal(5,1) [note: 'Quarterback Rating (QBR) — NFL-specific composite passing efficiency metric scaled 0–100 measuring a quarterback\'s overall performance including passing, rushing, and situational adjustments. Null for non-quarterback or non-football records. Used for broadcast overlays and fantasy sports feeds.']
  rebounds varchar [note: 'Total number of rebounds (offensive plus defensive) credited to the athlete. Basketball-specific metric (NBA). Null for non-basketball records. Used in broadcast overlays, fantasy sports feeds, and player valuation models.']
  receiving_yards varchar [note: 'Total receiving yards accumulated by the athlete in this statistical period. NFL-specific metric for wide receivers, tight ends, and running backs. Null for non-football records. Key fantasy sports and broadcast overlay metric.']
  red_cards varchar [note: 'Number of red cards (ejections) received by the athlete in this statistical period. Soccer-specific (FIFA/MLS) disciplinary metric. Null for non-soccer records. Triggers automatic suspension and is tracked for league compliance and CBA reporting.']
  rushing_yards varchar [note: 'Total rushing yards accumulated by the athlete in this statistical period. NFL-specific metric for running backs, quarterbacks, and other ball carriers. Null for non-football records. Used in broadcast overlays and fantasy sports scoring.']
  saves varchar [note: 'Number of saves recorded by the athlete. Applicable to goalkeepers in soccer and hockey, and to relief pitchers in baseball (MLB save rule). Null for non-applicable sport/position combinations.']
  slugging_percentage decimal(5,3) [note: 'Baseball-specific advanced hitting metric measuring total bases per at-bat, reflecting extra-base hit power. Null for non-baseball records. Combined with on-base percentage to produce OPS (On-Base Plus Slugging) for comprehensive offensive evaluation.']
  sport_discipline varchar [note: 'The specific sport or discipline for which these statistics apply (e.g., American Football, Baseball, Basketball, Ice Hockey, Soccer, Tennis). Determines which sport-specific metric fields are populated and which are null. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: american_football|baseball|basketball|ice_hockey|soccer|tennis|golf|mma|rugby|cricket — promote to reference product]']
  stat_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which the game was played or the date representing the end of the aggregation period for season/career records. Formatted as yyyy-MM-dd per enterprise data standards.']
  stat_period_end_date date [note: 'The end date of the window over which this performance stat record is considered a valid input to the KPI calculation. Together with stat_period_start_date, defines the contribution scope for this specific mapping.']
  stat_period_start_date date [note: 'The start date of the window over which this performance stat record is considered a valid input to the KPI calculation. Scopes the contribution temporally without altering the stat record or the KPI definition.']
  stat_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the statistical record. Official records are certified by the league; provisional records are pending official certification; revised records have been corrected post-certification; voided records are invalidated due to forfeit, protest, or data error; pending_review records are under investigation.']
  stat_type varchar [note: 'Classifies whether this record represents a single-game statistical line, a season-level aggregate, a career-level aggregate, a playoff period, or a tournament-specific accumulation. Drives how the record is consumed by broadcast overlays, fantasy feeds, and standings engines.']
  steals varchar [note: 'Number of steals credited to the athlete. Primarily a basketball (NBA) defensive metric. Also applicable to baseball (stolen bases). Null for non-applicable sport/position combinations.']
  strikeouts varchar [note: 'Number of strikeouts recorded. For pitchers (MLB), this is the count of batters struck out. For batters, this is the count of times struck out. Null for non-baseball records. Key pitching and batting evaluation metric.']
  tackles varchar [note: 'Number of tackles made by the athlete. Applicable to American football (NFL) defensive players and soccer (FIFA/MLS) players. Null for sports or positions where tackles are not a tracked metric.']
  touchdowns varchar [note: 'Total touchdowns scored or thrown by the athlete in this statistical period. NFL-specific metric covering passing touchdowns, rushing touchdowns, and receiving touchdowns. Null for non-football records.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time at which this performance statistic record was last modified in the Silver layer lakehouse. Formatted as yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX. Tracks revisions, corrections, and re-certifications. Required for change data capture and audit compliance.']
  war decimal(6,2) [note: 'Wins Above Replacement (WAR) — a composite advanced metric estimating the total number of additional wins the athlete contributed to their team compared to a replacement-level player. Sourced from SportsCode/Hudl analytics engine. Used for contract valuation, trade analysis, and broadcast storytelling.']
  yellow_cards varchar [note: 'Number of yellow cards (cautions) received by the athlete in this statistical period. Soccer-specific (FIFA/MLS) disciplinary metric. Null for non-soccer records. Relevant for suspension tracking and league compliance reporting.']

  Note: 'Game-level and season-level performance statistics for athletes across all sports disciplines. Captures sport-specific metrics including WAR (Wins Above Replacement), ERA (Earned Run Average), QBR (Quarterback Rating), goals, assists, rebounds, tackles, saves, batting average, on-base percentage, and advanced analytics inputs. Sourced from SportsCode/Hudl performance analytics platform. Each record represents a statistical line for one athlete in one game or aggregated period, enabling broadcast overlays, fantasy sports feeds, and league standings calculations.'
}

Table athlete.injury_record {
  injury_record_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each athlete injury or medical event record in the system. Primary key for the injury_record data product.']
  announcement_asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to content.asset. Business justification: Injury disclosures require a linked broadcast-approved content asset (injury report video, written statement). injury_record.broadcast_disclosure_approved and broadcast_injury_note confirm content is ']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Injury records carry insurance_reserve_amount and workers_comp_claim_number. Medical costs and insurance reserves must be allocated to a cost center for financial reporting, workers\' comp accounting, ']
  facility_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to venue.facility. Business justification: OSHA venue-level injury reporting and insurance liability tracking require linking athlete injuries to the specific facility where they occurred. Sports operations teams run facility safety scorecards']
  game_result_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to league.game_result. Business justification: Injuries sustained during a specific game must be linked to the official game result for broadcast disclosure workflows, OSHA in-game injury reporting, and insurance claim documentation. The game_resu']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account.
