  content_asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to content.asset. Business justification: Campaign creative management: sports entertainment campaigns are built around a specific content asset (video, image). Replacing the denormalized creative_asset_url string with a proper FK enables cam']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Marketing campaigns in sports entertainment are tracked against cost centers for spend reporting, SOX compliance, and P&L attribution. Campaign has budget_amount and actual_spend_amount requiring cost']
  data_processing_record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.data_processing_record. Business justification: Marketing campaigns process personal data for targeting, analytics, and conversion tracking, requiring ROPA entries under GDPR Article 30. A DPO would expect each campaign\'s personal data processing a']
  deal_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sponsorship.deal. Business justification: Fan marketing campaigns are frequently contracted deliverables within sponsorship deals (e.g., \'5 email campaigns per season\' as a deal term). Linking campaign to deal enables deal fulfillment trackin']
  featured_athlete_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to athlete.athlete_profile. Business justification: Athlete-driven marketing campaigns (jersey retirement, comeback, draft day) require attribution to a specific athlete for campaign performance reporting and ROI analysis. Sports marketing teams routin']
  franchise_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to league.franchise. Business justification: Franchise co-branded campaigns (team-specific fan acquisition, renewal drives) require franchise attribution for revenue sharing, co-op marketing fund reconciliation, and franchise-level marketing per']
  nil_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to athlete.nil_agreement. Business justification: Fan campaigns are frequently the activation vehicle for an athlete\'s NIL agreement. Compliance and finance teams must link campaigns to the governing NIL deal for regulatory disclosure, royalty tracki']
  parent_campaign_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on campaign (parent_campaign_id)']
  policy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.policy. Business justification: Marketing campaigns must comply with specific policies (GDPR consent, CCPA, CAN-SPAM). A compliance officer auditing campaign practices would expect each campaign to reference the governing policy tha']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Campaigns are owned by business units (ticketing, merchandise, broadcast) tracked as profit centers. P&L reporting by business unit requires campaign spend attributed to the owning profit center — sta']
  promotion_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to merchandise.promotion. Business justification: Marketing campaigns activate specific merchandise promotions; campaign performance dashboards require direct linkage to measure promotion redemption rates, revenue lift, and ROI. Sports marketing team']
  season_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to league.season. Business justification: Marketing campaigns in sports are fundamentally season-structured (pre-season, regular season, playoff campaigns). season_id on campaign supports season-level marketing budget reporting, campaign ROI ']
  segment_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the fan segment targeted by this campaign.']
  tournament_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to event.tournament. Business justification: Marketing campaigns are scoped to specific tournaments (World Cup fan acquisition, playoff bracket campaigns). Tournament-level campaign performance reporting and ROI attribution are named business pr']
  venue_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to venue.venue. Business justification: Venue-targeted campaign planning is a core sports marketing process — opening night campaigns, venue re-opening promotions, and venue-specific fan acquisition drives require linking campaigns to a spe']
  actual_conversions varchar [note: 'Actual number of conversions achieved by the campaign.']
  actual_impressions varchar [note: 'Actual number of impressions or views delivered by the campaign.']
  actual_spend_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total amount actually spent on the campaign to date in USD.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval status of the campaign by management or compliance review.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the campaign was approved for execution.']
  budget_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total allocated budget for the campaign in USD. Single-currency operation.']
  campaign_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique business identifier for the campaign, used in marketing materials and tracking URLs.']
  campaign_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative describing the campaign objectives, messaging, and creative approach.']
  campaign_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the marketing campaign.']
  campaign_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the campaign.']
  campaign_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the campaign based on its primary business objective.']
  channel varchar [note: 'Primary marketing channel through which the campaign is delivered to fans.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the campaign record was first created in the system.']
  end_date date [note: 'Date when the campaign concludes. Nullable for ongoing campaigns.']
  is_automated boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the campaign is executed through automated marketing workflows (True) or manual execution (False).']
  landing_page_url varchar [note: 'URL of the landing page where fans are directed when engaging with the campaign.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the campaign record was last modified.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for internal notes, learnings, or observations about the campaign execution.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Business priority level assigned to the campaign for resource allocation and scheduling.']
  start_date date [note: 'Date when the campaign becomes active and begins execution.']
  tags varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of tags or keywords for campaign categorization and search.']
  target_audience varchar [note: 'Description of the intended fan segment or demographic group targeted by this campaign.']
  target_conversions varchar [note: 'Planned number of conversions (ticket purchases, sign-ups, etc.) the campaign aims to achieve.']
  target_impressions varchar [note: 'Planned number of impressions or views the campaign aims to achieve.']
  tracking_code varchar [note: 'Unique tracking or UTM code used to measure campaign performance across analytics platforms.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for campaign. Referenced by acquisition_campaign_id.'
}

Table fan.club_membership {
  club_membership_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for the club_membership association']
  club_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the fan club the fan is enrolled in']
  fan_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the fan profile record of the enrolled member']
  fee_paid_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the fan has paid the current period\'s membership dues for this specific club enrollment. Drives access control for member-only benefits and event ticket allocations.']
  is_fan_club_member boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the fan is an active member of an official fan club. Fan club members receive exclusive content, early ticket access, and community engagement opportunities. [Moved from fan_profile: This boolean flag on fan_profile is a denormalized scalar that cannot represent which clubs a fan belongs to or the status of each membership. With the club_membership association table in place, this flag becomes derivable (EXISTS query on club_membership where membership_status = \'active\') and should be removed from fan_profile to avoid data inconsistency.]']
  join_date date [note: 'The date on which the fan formally enrolled in this fan club. Belongs to the enrollment record, not the fan profile or club, as a fan may have different join dates across multiple clubs.']
  membership_number varchar [note: 'Externally-visible alphanumeric identifier assigned to the fan for this specific club membership. Used on membership cards, CRM communications, and event check-in. Unique per fan-club enrollment.']
  membership_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of this specific fan-club membership enrollment. Distinct from the fan\'s overall account_status — a fan may be active overall but have a lapsed membership in a specific club.']
  membership_tier varchar [note: 'The tier level of the fan\'s membership within this specific club (e.g., bronze, silver, gold, platinum). A fan may hold different tiers in different clubs, so this attribute belongs to the enrollment, not the fan profile.']
  renewal_date date [note: 'The date on which this fan\'s membership in this specific club is due for renewal. Per-enrollment attribute as renewal cycles differ by club.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the Enrollment between fan_profile and club. It captures the operational lifecycle of a fan\'s membership in an official fan club — including join date, membership tier, renewal date, dues payment status, and membership number. Each record links one fan to one club and carries attributes that exist only in the context of that specific fan-club enrollment. Enables CRM segmentation, loyalty benefit delivery, compliance tracking, and fan club governance.. Existence Justification: In sports entertainment, fans actively join multiple official fan clubs (e.g., a fan can be a member of both the LA Lakers Official Fan Club and a regional supporters chapter), and each club has many member fans. This is a genuine operational M:N relationship: the business actively manages fan club memberships as a lifecycle concept — fans enroll, pay dues, renew, and hold tiered membership status within each club. The relationship is not derivable from existing FKs; it requires its own record with join_date, membership_tier, renewal_date, dues payment status, and membership number.'
}

Table ticketing.ticket_inventory {
  ticket_inventory_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate primary key for each ticket inventory unit record within the venue seating manifest. Serves as the SSOT identifier for a single allocatable seat or standing position per event.']
  access_zone_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to security.access_zone. Business justification: Ticket inventory records define which security zone each seat belongs to. Security staffing plans and evacuation planning require knowing how many sold seats map to each security zone. Links inventory']
  capacity_config_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to venue.capacity_config. Business justification: Inventory allocation must reference the active capacity_config to enforce fire-code-approved sellable capacity, kill seat counts, and ADA capacity limits. Manifest approval and league sign-off process']
  franchise_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to league.franchise. Business justification: Franchise-level inventory management, comp ticket pool allocation, and CBA revenue-sharing reports require direct franchise attribution on inventory blocks. Inventory managers pull all inventory by fr']
  league_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to league.league_schedule. Business justification: Inventory on-sale timestamps, PPV flags, and gate revenue split percentages are governed by league_schedule. Ticketing operations enforce on-sale windows directly from league_schedule.ticket_on_sale_d']
  price_tier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ticketing.price_tier. Business justification: Each ticket inventory unit belongs to a specific price tier (GA, VIP, SRO, etc.). ticket_inventory currently has price_zone_id (existing FK) and seat_tier as a STRING, but lacks a direct FK to price_t']
  price_zone_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the event-specific price zone assigned to this inventory unit. Price zones define the base pricing tier and dynamic pricing eligibility for the seat.']
  primary_ticket_event_fixture_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific event for which this inventory unit is allocated. Links the seat record to the event scheduling master.']
  season_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to league.season. Business justification: Season-level inventory planning, capacity forecasting, and annual revenue reporting require direct season attribution on inventory records. Single-game inventory has no season_ticket_package anchor; s']
  seat_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to venue.seat. Business justification: Physical seat manifest validation — kill-seat enforcement, ADA accommodation tracking, and obstructed-view disclosure — requires linking each inventory record to the authoritative venue.seat record. T']
  seating_section_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to venue.seating_section. Business justification: Inventory allocation and manifest validation require linking each ticket inventory record to the canonical seating_section for ADA compliance, sightline disclosure, price zone enforcement, and kill-se']
  venue_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the venue where the event is hosted. Drives seating manifest configuration and ADA compliance mapping.']
  access_gate varchar [note: 'The designated entry gate or access point at the venue through which the holder of this inventory unit is directed. Used for access control configuration in AXS/Ticketmaster gate management and crowd flow planning.']
  ada_accommodation_type varchar [note: 'Specifies the type of ADA accessibility accommodation provided by this inventory unit. Applicable only when is_ada_accessible = true. Supports ADA compliance reporting and accessible inventory allocation.']
  archtics_seat_key varchar [note: 'The native seat-level unique identifier as assigned by the Archtics venue seating system. Used for reconciliation and real-time sync with the source-of-record seating manifest.']
  barcode_format varchar [note: 'The barcode or digital credential format used for access control scanning of this inventory unit. Determines scanner compatibility at venue entry points.']
  comp_reason varchar [note: 'The reason code for complimentary allocation of this inventory unit. Applicable only when inventory_type = COMPLIMENTARY or inventory_status = COMP. Required for SOX financial controls and sponsor/media comp tracking.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this inventory unit record was first created in the Silver layer, typically corresponding to the initial seating manifest load from Archtics for the event.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code applicable to all monetary amounts on this inventory record (e.g., USD, GBP, EUR). Supports multi-currency operations across global markets.']
  digital_delivery_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this inventory unit supports digital ticket delivery (mobile wallet, NFC, QR code). Drives delivery method options presented at checkout and access control configuration at venue gates.']
  dynamic_price_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'The current dynamically adjusted price for this inventory unit, reflecting real-time demand signals, sell-through rates, and competitive pricing algorithms. Updated continuously by the dynamic pricing engine. Null if dynamic pricing is not enabled for this inventory unit.']
  face_value_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'The printed face value price of the ticket for this inventory unit in the event\'s base currency. Represents the base price before dynamic pricing adjustments, fees, or discounts. Used for resale market regulation and revenue reporting.']
  hold_code varchar [note: 'The hold code applied to this inventory unit when status is HELD or RESERVED. Hold codes identify the reason or pool for the hold (e.g., \'SPONSOR\', \'MEDIA\', \'ARTIST\', \'GROUP\', \'SEASON\'). Sourced from Archtics hold code configuration.']
  hold_expiry_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time at which a temporary hold on this inventory unit expires, returning it to AVAILABLE status if not converted to a sale. Applicable when inventory_status = HELD.']
  host_inventory_code varchar [note: 'The inventory unit identifier assigned by the Ticketmaster Host system. Used for cross-system reconciliation between Archtics seating manifest and Ticketmaster ticketing platform.']
  inventory_status varchar [note: 'Current allocation state of the inventory unit: AVAILABLE (open for sale), SOLD (ticket issued), HELD (temporarily locked for a transaction or hold pool), KILLED (removed from sellable inventory), COMP (complimentary allocation), RESERVED (pre-allocated to a specific channel or package). SSOT for available-to-sell calculations.']
  inventory_type varchar [note: 'Classifies the inventory unit into its designated pool: General Admission (GA), Very Important Person (VIP), Standing Room Only (SRO), Press, Complimentary, or Accessible (ADA). Drives pricing, access control, and channel eligibility rules. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: GA|VIP|SRO|PRESS|COMPLIMENTARY|ACCESSIBLE|SUITE|GROUP|SEASON — promote to reference product]']
  is_ada_accessible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this inventory unit is designated as ADA-accessible, meeting Americans with Disabilities Act requirements for wheelchair access, companion seating, or other accessibility accommodations.']
  is_aisle_seat boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this seat is positioned directly adjacent to an aisle. Used for accessibility filtering, premium seat designation, and fan preference matching in CRM-driven personalization.']
  is_dynamic_pricing_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this inventory unit is eligible for dynamic pricing adjustments. When true, the dynamic_price_amount field is actively maintained by the pricing engine.']
  is_obstructed_view boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this seat has a partially or fully obstructed sightline to the stage or playing field. Drives discounted pricing eligibility and mandatory disclosure requirements at point of sale.']
  is_season_ticket_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this inventory unit is eligible to be included in a season ticket package. Drives season ticket renewal workflows and package construction in Archtics.']
  kill_reason varchar [note: 'Reason code explaining why this inventory unit has been killed (removed from sellable inventory). Applicable only when inventory_status = KILLED. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: OBSTRUCTED_VIEW|STRUCTURAL|SAFETY|ADA_COMPANION|EQUIPMENT|SIGHTLINE|BROADCAST|STAGING — promote to reference product]']
  last_status_change_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the inventory_status of this unit was most recently updated. Supports audit trail, real-time inventory monitoring, and SLA tracking for hold-to-sale conversion.']
  manifest_section_type varchar [note: 'The physical section category within the venue manifest as defined in the Archtics seating configuration. Supports venue layout analytics and capacity planning. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: LOWER_BOWL|UPPER_BOWL|FLOOR|SUITE|CLUB|STANDING|PRESS_BOX|FIELD|MEZZANINE — promote to reference product]']
  manifest_version varchar [note: 'The version identifier of the venue seating manifest from which this inventory unit was loaded. Enables tracking of manifest changes (e.g., reconfiguration for different event types such as concert vs. sports) and supports rollback and audit.']
  off_sale_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this inventory unit is removed from public sale (e.g., event day cutoff). After this timestamp, the unit is no longer available through standard sales channels.']
  on_sale_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this inventory unit becomes available for public sale. Supports presale window management, channel release scheduling, and real-time available-to-sell calculations.']
  package_type varchar [note: 'Identifies whether this inventory unit is allocated to a single-event sale, a full season ticket package, a mini-plan, group sales block, flex plan, or suite package. Drives entitlement and renewal logic.']
  resale_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this inventory unit is eligible for listing on the official resale marketplace. Controlled by league rules, event-specific restrictions, and package terms (e.g., complimentary tickets are typically resale-ineligible).']
  resale_max_price decimal(12,2) [note: 'The maximum price ceiling enforced for resale of this inventory unit on the official resale marketplace. Applicable in jurisdictions or leagues with anti-scalping price cap regulations.']
  resale_min_price decimal(12,2) [note: 'The minimum price floor enforced for resale of this inventory unit on the official resale marketplace. Set by league policy or event-specific pricing rules to prevent below-face-value resale.']
  row_label varchar [note: 'The alphanumeric row identifier within the section (e.g., \'A\', \'12\', \'AA\'). Null for General Admission (GA) or Standing Room Only (SRO) inventory types.']
  row_sequence varchar [note: 'Numeric sequence of the row within the section, used for physical ordering and proximity-based seat grouping logic. Supports best-available seat algorithms and contiguous seat selection.']
  sale_channel varchar [note: 'The designated sales channel through which this inventory unit is authorized to be sold. Controls channel-specific routing in Ticketmaster/AXS and supports channel mix analytics. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: BOX_OFFICE|ONLINE|MOBILE|GROUP_SALES|SEASON_PACKAGE|RESALE|API_PARTNER|VIP_HOSPITALITY — promote to reference product]']
  seat_sequence varchar [note: 'Numeric sequence of the seat within the row, used for physical ordering and contiguous seat grouping. Supports best-available algorithms that prioritize adjacent seats for group purchases.']
  seating_capacity_type varchar [note: 'Describes the physical seating configuration of this inventory unit: FIXED (permanent installed seat), PORTABLE (temporary/removable seat), STANDING (no seat, standing position), or LAWN (open lawn area). Affects ADA compliance and capacity calculations.']
  section_label varchar [note: 'The alphanumeric label of the venue section in which this inventory unit is located (e.g., \'101\', \'FLOOR A\', \'PRESS BOX\'). Sourced from the Archtics seating manifest.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record from which this inventory unit record originated: Archtics (venue seating manifest), Ticketmaster Host (TM_HOST), AXS, or MANUAL (manually entered override). Supports data lineage and reconciliation.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this inventory unit record was most recently updated in the Silver layer. Supports incremental data pipeline processing and audit compliance.']

  Note: 'Master record of all available ticket inventory units within the venue seating manifest, sourced from Archtics/Ticketmaster Host. Tracks seat-level availability, hold status, kill status, accessibility designations (ADA), inventory pool assignments (GA, VIP, SRO, press, complimentary), and event-specific price zone mapping. SSOT for what seats exist per event and their current allocation state prior to sale. Supports real-time available-to-sell calculations across all channels.'
}

Business justification: PPV ticket orders are fulfilled through specific platform channels (OTT app, cable PPV, IPTV). Revenue share calculations, platform-specific reporting, and broadcaster settlement require knowing which']
  ppv_asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to content.asset. Business justification: PPV ticket orders must reference the specific content asset being purchased for revenue reconciliation, entitlement validation, and PPV rights reporting. Sports entertainment operators track which con']
  primary_ticket_event_fixture_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the scheduled event (game, concert, match) for which tickets were purchased. Links to the event master record.']
  primary_ticket_fan_fan_profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the fan (purchaser) who placed this ticket order. Links to the fan master record in the CRM system (Salesforce CRM / Adobe Experience Platform).']
  resale_original_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the original primary-market ticket order from which the resale tickets originated. Populated only when resale_flag is True. Enables full resale chain traceability and anti-fraud analysis.']
  season_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to league.season. Business justification: Season-level ticket sales reporting, CBA compliance audits, and annual revenue reconciliation require direct season attribution on orders. Single-game orders lack a season_ticket_package_id; season_id']
  season_ticket_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ticketing.season_ticket_account. Business justification: Season ticket holder orders are placed against a specific season ticket account, which tracks the fan\'s enrollment, seat assignments, payment plan, and loyalty tier. Linking ticket_order to season_tic']
  season_ticket_package_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the season ticket package if this order was placed as part of a season ticket subscription. Null for single-event purchases. Supports season ticket holder analytics and renewal management.']
  venue_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the venue where the event is hosted. Supports venue-level revenue attribution and capacity reporting.']
  access_barcode varchar [note: 'The primary digital access credential (barcode, QR code token, or NFC token reference) associated with this order for venue entry. Stored as a tokenized reference, not the raw barcode value, to prevent unauthorized duplication. Used by access control systems at venue gates.']
  accessible_seating_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this order includes ADA-compliant accessible seating accommodations. True = accessible seating requested and allocated. Required for ADA compliance reporting and venue accessibility management.']
  cancellation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this order was cancelled. Populated only when order_status is \'cancelled\'. Used for cancellation rate analysis, revenue impact assessment, and SLA compliance reporting.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Audit timestamp recording when this order record was first written to the Silver Layer lakehouse. Distinct from purchase_timestamp, which reflects the real-world business event time.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary amounts on this order (e.g., USD, GBP, EUR). Supports multi-currency operations across global markets.']
  delivery_method varchar [note: 'The method by which tickets are delivered to the fan after purchase. Supports digital delivery tracking, will-call management, and access control integration with Ticketmaster/AXS.']
  delivery_status varchar [note: 'Current fulfillment status of ticket delivery to the fan. Tracks whether digital or physical tickets have been successfully delivered, are pending, or encountered a delivery failure.']
  discount_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total discount applied to this order, including promotional codes, season ticket holder discounts, group rate reductions, or sponsor-funded offers. Used for promotional effectiveness analysis and revenue leakage tracking.']
  face_value_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total printed face value of all tickets included in this order, before fees and taxes. Represents the base ticket price as set by the league or venue pricing authority. Used for revenue reporting and regulatory compliance.']
  gdpr_consent_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the fan provided explicit GDPR-compliant data processing consent at the time of this order. True = consent given. Required for EU market compliance and fan data usage governance.']
  loyalty_points_earned varchar [note: 'Number of fan loyalty or rewards points earned through this ticket purchase. Supports fan engagement analytics, loyalty program ROI measurement, and CRM-driven personalization via Adobe Experience Platform.']
  loyalty_points_redeemed varchar [note: 'Number of fan loyalty or rewards points redeemed as part of payment or discount for this order. Used for loyalty liability accounting and fan engagement reporting.']
  order_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or special instructions associated with this order, entered by the fan, box office agent, or group sales coordinator. May include accessibility requests, VIP service notes, or group coordinator instructions.']
  order_number varchar [note: 'Externally-visible, human-readable order reference number assigned by the ticketing platform (Ticketmaster, AXS, box office, or DTC channel). Used for fan-facing communications, customer service lookups, and multi-channel reconciliation.']
  order_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the ticket order. Drives downstream fulfillment, access control, and revenue recognition workflows. \'fulfilled\' indicates tickets have been delivered; \'partially_refunded\' applies when only a subset of tickets in the order were refunded.']
  order_total_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Net total amount charged to the fan for this order, equal to face_value_amount + service_fee_amount + tax_amount minus any discounts. This is the authoritative total for payment processing and revenue recognition.']
  payment_reference varchar [note: 'PCI-DSS compliant tokenized payment reference (e.g., Ticketmaster/AXS payment token or SAP payment document number). NEVER stores raw card numbers or CVV. Used for payment reconciliation, refund processing, and chargeback management.']
  payment_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the payment transaction associated with this order. Distinct from order_status — an order may be confirmed while payment is still in settlement. Critical for financial reconciliation and PCI-DSS audit.']
  ppv_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this order is for a Pay-Per-View (PPV) digital access entitlement rather than a physical venue ticket. True = PPV digital order. Supports broadcast revenue attribution and OTT/DTC channel analytics.']
  pricing_model varchar [note: 'The pricing strategy applied to this order. FIXED (standard face value), DYNAMIC (demand-based variable pricing), SEASON_PACKAGE (season ticket bundle), GROUP_RATE (bulk group discount), or RESALE (secondary market). Supports revenue management analytics.']
  purchase_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The exact date and time (with timezone offset) at which the fan completed the ticket purchase transaction. This is the principal business event timestamp for the order. Used for revenue attribution, demand analysis, and PCI-DSS audit trails.']
  refund_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total amount refunded to the fan for this order. Populated when order_status is \'refunded\' or \'partially_refunded\'. Used for revenue adjustment, financial reconciliation, and fan satisfaction reporting.']
  refund_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized reason code explaining why a refund was issued for this order. Populated when refund_amount > 0. Supports refund trend analysis, event cancellation impact reporting, and fraud detection.']
  resale_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this order was placed through the secondary/resale marketplace rather than the primary ticketing channel. True = resale order. Used for primary vs. secondary market revenue segmentation and league governance reporting.']
  sales_channel_code varchar [note: 'Identifies the commerce channel through which the ticket order was placed. Supports multi-channel revenue attribution and reconciliation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: TICKETMASTER|AXS|BOX_OFFICE|GROUP_SALES|DTC|KIOSK|PARTNER_API — promote to reference product]']
  service_fee_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total service and convenience fees charged on this order by the ticketing platform or box office. Distinct from face value and taxes. Key component for revenue-share reconciliation with Ticketmaster/AXS.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the operational system of record from which this order was ingested. Supports multi-system reconciliation and data lineage tracking in the lakehouse.']
  source_system_order_code varchar [note: 'The native order identifier from the originating ticketing platform (e.g., Ticketmaster order ID, AXS confirmation number, Archtics transaction ID). Enables bi-directional reconciliation between the Silver Layer and the operational system of record.']
  tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total tax amount applied to this order, including applicable sales tax, VAT, or entertainment tax based on the venue jurisdiction. Required for financial reporting and tax remittance.']
  ticket_quantity varchar [note: 'Total number of individual tickets included in this order. Used for capacity management, demand forecasting, and per-ticket revenue calculations.']
  ticket_tier varchar [note: 'Pricing and access tier classification for the tickets in this order. GA (General Admission), VIP (Very Important Person), SRO (Standing Room Only), PREMIUM, SUITE, or ACCESSIBLE. Drives pricing, access control, and fan experience segmentation.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Audit timestamp recording the most recent modification to this order record in the Silver Layer. Tracks status changes, refunds, delivery updates, and any post-purchase amendments.']

  Note: 'Core transactional record capturing a fan\'s purchase of one or more tickets through any sales channel (Ticketmaster, AXS, box office, group sales, DTC, kiosk, partner API). Records order status, sales channel code, purchase timestamp, total face value, fees, taxes, PCI-DSS compliant payment reference, cashier/agent ID (for box office), and delivery method. SSOT for all ticket commerce transactions regardless of channel. Supports multi-channel reconciliation and revenue attribution.'
}

Table ticketing.ticket_order_line {
  ticket_order_line_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for a single ticket order line item within the ticketing system. Represents one ticket unit sold within a parent order.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Individual order lines post to different GL accounts (face value, facility fee, service fee are separate revenue categories). Line-level GL assignment supports detailed revenue recognition by revenue ']
  license_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to content.content_license. Business justification: Order lines for streaming/PPV products must reference the governing content license for revenue recognition, rights compliance audits, and territory restriction enforcement at point of sale. Sports-en']
  platform_channel_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to content.platform_channel. Business justification: Line-level platform channel attribution drives digital revenue reporting, platform commission calculations, and OTT vs. broadcast channel performance analytics. Mixed-basket orders (physical + streami']
  ppv_asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to content.asset. Business justification: PPV order line itemization requires direct asset reference for line-level revenue recognition, per-asset refund processing, and digital rights audit. Parent ticket_order has ppv_asset_id but line-leve']
  price_tier_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the pricing tier (e.g., GA, VIP, SRO, premium) applied to this ticket line. Drives revenue attribution and pricing analytics.']
  price_zone_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ticketing.price_zone. Business justification: ticket_order_line has price_zone as a STRING denormalization but no FK to the authoritative price_zone catalog. Each order line is associated with a specific price zone (section/area of the venue). Ad']
  primary_ticket_event_fixture_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific sports or entertainment event for which this ticket grants admission. Links the ticket line to the event scheduling domain.']
  primary_ticket_fan_fan_profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the fan or patron who holds entitlement to this ticket. Supports fan engagement analytics, CRM integration, and access control.']
  season_ticket_package_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the season ticket package under which this ticket line was issued, if applicable. Links individual game tickets to their parent season ticket agreement for package revenue attribution.']
  ticket_inventory_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific seat inventory record in Archtics representing the physical or virtual seat assigned to this ticket line.']
  ticket_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent ticket order header under which this line item was created. Establishes the header/detail relationship for the ticket commerce transaction.']
  venue_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the venue where the event is held. Supports venue operations reporting and access control entitlement generation.']
  access_status varchar [note: 'The current access control entitlement status for this ticket at the venue. Tracks whether the ticket holder has entered, exited, or been denied entry. Sourced from AXS/Ticketmaster access control scanners.']
  barcode varchar [note: 'The unique barcode or token printed or encoded on the ticket used for access control scanning at venue entry points. May be a traditional barcode, QR code value, or NFC token identifier.']
  cancelled_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this ticket order line was cancelled or voided. Populated only when line_status is \'cancelled\' or \'voided\'. Required for refund processing and revenue reversal tracking.']
  comp_reason_code varchar [note: 'The reason code explaining why a complimentary ticket was issued. Required for comp ticket audit and financial controls. Only populated when is_comp is True. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: media|sponsor|athlete|staff|promotion|accessibility — promote to reference product]']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary amounts on this ticket line (e.g., USD, GBP, EUR). Supports multi-currency operations across global markets.']
  delivered_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the ticket was successfully delivered to the fan via the specified delivery method. Used for fulfillment SLA tracking and fan experience reporting.']
  delivery_method varchar [note: 'The method by which the ticket is delivered to the purchaser. Drives fulfillment workflows and fan experience design. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: mobile|print_at_home|will_call|mail|digital_wallet|kiosk — promote to reference product]']
  delivery_status varchar [note: 'Current fulfillment status of the ticket delivery. Tracks whether the ticket has been successfully delivered to the fan via the specified delivery method.']
  discount_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'The monetary discount applied to this ticket line as a result of a promotion code, loyalty program, or group sales discount. Reduces the effective selling price from the face value.']
  dynamic_price decimal(12,2) [note: 'The actual selling price applied to this ticket after dynamic pricing model adjustments (demand-based, algorithmic, or promotional). May differ from face_value when dynamic pricing is active.']
  external_line_ref varchar [note: 'The externally-known line item identifier assigned by the source ticketing system (Ticketmaster/AXS or Archtics). Used for reconciliation with the operational system of record.']
  face_value decimal(12,2) [note: 'The printed or base face value of the ticket before any service fees, facility fees, or dynamic pricing adjustments. Used for regulatory reporting, resale market compliance, and revenue attribution.']
  facility_fee decimal(10,2) [note: 'The venue facility fee charged per ticket to fund venue operations, maintenance, and capital improvements. Distinct from the service fee and retained by the venue operator.']
  gate_entry_point varchar [note: 'The designated gate or entry point at the venue through which the ticket holder is authorized to enter (e.g., \'Gate A\', \'North Entrance\', \'VIP Entrance\'). Supports venue operations and crowd management.']
  is_ada_accessible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this ticket is for an ADA (Americans with Disabilities Act) accessible seat or accommodation. Required for ADA compliance reporting and venue accessibility management.']
  is_comp boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this ticket was issued as a complimentary (comp) ticket at no charge. Comp tickets require audit tracking for financial controls and league governance compliance.']
  is_resale boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this ticket was sold through the secondary/resale marketplace rather than primary sale. Distinguishes primary and secondary market revenue streams for analytics and league reporting.']
  issued_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this ticket order line was created and the ticket was issued in the ticketing system. Represents the principal business event time for this transaction line.']
  line_number varchar [note: 'Sequential position of this ticket line within the parent order, starting at 1. Used for ordering, display, and reconciliation of multi-ticket orders.']
  line_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of this ticket order line. Drives fulfillment workflows, access control entitlement, and revenue recognition. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: issued|cancelled|refunded|transferred|voided|reissued — promote to reference product]']
  line_total decimal(12,2) [note: 'The total amount charged for this ticket order line, calculated as dynamic_price + service_fee + facility_fee + tax_amount. Represents the net amount collected from the purchaser for this single ticket unit.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'The payment instrument used to purchase this ticket line (e.g., credit card, digital wallet, voucher). Supports PCI-DSS compliance reporting and payment channel analytics. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: credit_card|debit_card|digital_wallet|cash|voucher|comp — promote to reference product]']
  quantity varchar [note: 'The number of ticket units represented by this line item. Typically 1 for individually assigned seats; may be greater than 1 for GA (General Admission) or SRO (Standing Room Only) allocations.']
  resale_original_price decimal(12,2) [note: 'The original face value price at which the ticket was first sold in the primary market, retained for resale margin analysis and league resale policy compliance. Only populated when is_resale is True.']
  sales_channel varchar [note: 'The channel through which this ticket was sold (e.g., online, box office, mobile app, group sales). Supports DTC (Direct-To-Consumer) analytics, channel performance reporting, and fan acquisition cost analysis. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: online|box_office|mobile_app|kiosk|group_sales|resale — promote to reference product]']
  scan_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the ticket barcode was scanned at the venue access control point. Used for attendance analytics, gate throughput reporting, and security compliance.']
  seat_number varchar [note: 'The individual seat number within the assigned row and section. Together with seat_section and seat_row, forms the complete seat assignment for access control and fan experience.']
  seat_row varchar [note: 'The row designation within the assigned section (e.g., \'A\', \'12\', \'AA\'). Combined with seat_section and seat_number to uniquely identify the physical seat.']
  seat_section varchar [note: 'The section designation within the venue where the assigned seat is located (e.g., \'A\', \'101\', \'Lower Bowl\'). Sourced from Archtics seating inventory.']
  service_fee decimal(10,2) [note: 'The service/convenience fee charged by the ticketing platform (Ticketmaster/AXS) on top of the face value. A key revenue component for ticketing operations and partner settlement.']
  tax_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'The total tax amount applied to this ticket line item, including applicable sales tax, amusement tax, or entertainment tax per jurisdiction. Required for financial reporting and tax compliance.']
  ticket_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the ticket unit indicating the commercial nature of the sale. Distinguishes single-event tickets, season ticket packages, group sales, complimentary tickets, resale marketplace tickets, and bundled packages. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: single|season|group|comp|resale|package — promote to reference product]']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this ticket order line record was last modified in the source system. Supports data lineage, audit trail, and incremental data pipeline processing in the Databricks Silver layer.']

  Note: 'Line-item detail within a ticket order representing a single ticket unit sold. Captures seat assignment, price tier, face value, service fee, facility fee, dynamic price applied, barcode/token, delivery method, delivery status, delivery timestamp, and comp flag with reason code. Enables per-seat revenue attribution, access control entitlement generation, fulfillment tracking, and complimentary ticket audit.'
}

Table ticketing.price_tier {
  price_tier_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for a pricing tier record in the ticketing reference catalog. Primary key for the price_tier product.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Price tiers are assigned to cost centers for departmental revenue allocation (e.g., premium seating cost center vs. general admission). The existing cost_center_code is denormalized; a proper FK enabl']
  deal_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sponsorship.deal. Business justification: Sponsor-specific pricing: deals often include custom price tiers for sponsor hospitality allocations (e.g., negotiated rates for sponsor-purchased seats). Linking price_tier to the governing deal enab']
  franchise_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to league.franchise. Business justification: Franchises negotiate their own pricing tier structures within league guidelines. Franchise-level price tier management and revenue reporting requires direct franchise attribution on tiers. A sports-en']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Each price tier maps to a GL account for revenue posting in the ERP (standard SAP/ticketing integration). The existing gl_account_code is denormalized; a proper FK enables automated GL posting configu']
  league_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the league or governing body under whose rules and CBA (Collective Bargaining Agreement) this pricing tier operates. Ensures tier configurations comply with league-mandated pricing policies and fan access requirements.']
  league_season_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to league.season. Business justification: Price tiers are configured per season — face values, service fees, and eligibility rules change each season. Season-specific tier activation and year-over-year pricing comparison is a named revenue ma']
  type_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the event type (e.g., regular season, playoff, concert, special event) for which this pricing tier applies. Pricing tiers may differ by event type — playoff tiers typically carry higher face values than regular season tiers.']
  venue_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the venue for which this pricing tier is configured. Pricing tiers are often venue-specific due to differences in seating manifests, local tax rates, and facility fee structures in Archtics.']
  access_level varchar [note: 'Access control level granted by tickets at this tier, determining which venue zones and areas the ticket holder may enter. Mapped to access control zones in Ticketmaster/AXS and venue access control hardware (scanners, turnstiles).']
  ada_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this pricing tier is designated as ADA (Americans with Disabilities Act) accessible seating, meeting federal accessibility requirements for wheelchair spaces, companion seating, and accessible routes. Required for ADA compliance reporting.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this pricing tier record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail, data lineage, and compliance with SOX financial controls requiring record creation tracking.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the face value and fee amounts defined in this pricing tier (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP, CAD). Supports multi-currency operations across global markets.']
  delivery_method varchar [note: 'Permitted ticket delivery method for this pricing tier: MOBILE (mobile app barcode), PRINT_AT_HOME (PDF download), WILL_CALL (box office pickup), MAIL (physical mail), or DIGITAL_WALLET (Apple/Google Wallet). Determines digital ticket delivery configuration in Ticketmaster/AXS.']
  dynamic_pricing_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this pricing tier participates in the dynamic pricing engine, allowing face value to fluctuate based on demand, time-to-event, and inventory levels. When True, the face_value represents the base floor price. Integrates with Ticketmaster dynamic pricing module.']
  effective_from date [note: 'The date from which this pricing tier configuration is valid and binding. Supports temporal versioning of tier definitions — a tier may be reconfigured for a new season while retaining historical records for prior seasons.']
  effective_until date [note: 'The date until which this pricing tier configuration remains valid. Null indicates the tier is open-ended with no planned expiry. Used for seasonal pricing tier versioning and historical audit.']
  eligibility_rule varchar [note: 'Free-text or structured rule expression defining who is eligible to purchase tickets at this tier (e.g., \'Season ticket holders only\', \'Members with Gold status\', \'Military/First Responder ID required\', \'Age 12 and under\'). Enforced at point of sale in Ticketmaster/AXS.']
  external_tier_code varchar [note: 'The pricing tier identifier as assigned by the external ticketing system of record (Ticketmaster/AXS or Archtics). Used for system integration, reconciliation, and cross-system data lineage. Enables mapping between the internal BIGINT PK and the source system\'s native identifier.']
  face_value decimal(12,2) [note: 'The base printed face value of a ticket at this pricing tier in the operating currency, before any fees, taxes, or dynamic pricing adjustments. This is the regulated face value used for resale compliance and financial reporting. Sourced from Archtics/Ticketmaster pricing configuration.']
  facility_fee decimal(10,2) [note: 'Per-ticket facility/venue fee charged at this tier, retained by the venue operator to fund venue operations and capital improvements. Distinct from the service fee and reported separately in venue revenue accounting.']
  includes_hospitality boolean [note: 'Indicates whether hospitality benefits (food and beverage, lounge access, pre-event reception) are bundled with this pricing tier. Applicable to VIP, suite, and premium club tiers. Drives hospitality operations planning.']
  includes_merchandise boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a merchandise item or voucher (e.g., team jersey, program, gift bag) is bundled with tickets at this pricing tier as part of the package offering.']
  includes_parking boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a parking pass is bundled with tickets at this pricing tier as part of the ticket package (e.g., VIP and suite tiers often include complimentary parking).']
  inventory_class varchar [note: 'Archtics inventory classification that maps this pricing tier to a physical seating inventory class within the venue manifest. Drives seat map allocation and capacity planning in Archtics Venue Seating Inventory system.']
  max_price decimal(12,2) [note: 'The maximum allowable price for this tier when dynamic pricing is active. The dynamic pricing engine will not price tickets above this ceiling value. Null when dynamic_pricing_enabled is False.']
  max_tickets_per_order varchar [note: 'Maximum number of tickets at this tier that a single fan/customer may purchase in one order transaction. Enforces anti-scalping and fair access policies. Configured in Ticketmaster/AXS order rules.']
  member_exclusive boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this pricing tier is exclusively available to registered members or season ticket holders, restricting general public purchase. Integrates with Salesforce CRM membership validation at checkout.']
  min_price decimal(12,2) [note: 'The minimum allowable price for this tier when dynamic pricing is active. The dynamic pricing engine will not price tickets below this floor value. Null when dynamic_pricing_enabled is False.']
  min_tickets_per_order varchar [note: 'Minimum number of tickets at this tier required per order transaction. Primarily applicable to group sales tiers where a minimum group size must be met (e.g., minimum 10 tickets for group pricing).']
  presale_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether tickets at this tier are available during presale windows (e.g., fan club presale, sponsor presale, credit card presale) before general public on-sale. Drives presale campaign configuration in Ticketmaster/AXS.']
  price_tier_description varchar [note: 'Detailed marketing and operational description of the pricing tier, including benefits, inclusions, and eligibility notes. Displayed to fans on the ticketing platform and used by box office staff for customer service.']
  price_tier_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the pricing tier: active (available for sale), inactive (temporarily unavailable), draft (under configuration, not yet published), archived (retired, historical reference only), suspended (paused pending review). Controls availability in the dynamic pricing engine and order processing.']
  resale_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether tickets at this pricing tier are eligible for listing on the secondary/resale marketplace. When False, tickets are non-transferable and resale is prohibited (e.g., certain VIP hospitality packages or promotional tickets).']
  resale_min_price decimal(12,2) [note: 'Minimum price at which a ticket of this tier may be listed on the secondary resale marketplace. Enforces floor pricing to protect face value integrity and prevent below-cost resale. Null when resale_eligible is False.']
  revenue_category varchar [note: 'Financial revenue category classification for this pricing tier used in SAP S/4HANA revenue reporting and Workday Adaptive Planning financial forecasting. Segments ticket revenue streams for EBITDA and ARR (Annual Recurring Revenue) analysis.']
  sale_end_date date [note: 'The date on which tickets at this pricing tier are no longer available for purchase. After this date, the tier is automatically closed in the ticketing system. Null indicates the tier remains on sale until event day.']
  sale_start_date date [note: 'The date on which tickets at this pricing tier become available for general public purchase. Used to control on-sale windows in Ticketmaster/AXS and coordinate marketing campaigns.']
  seating_type varchar [note: 'Physical seating configuration associated with this pricing tier: RESERVED (assigned seat), GENERAL_ADMISSION (first-come seating), STANDING (standing area), SUITE (private suite), BOX (premium box), or ACCESSIBLE (ADA-compliant seating). Determines access control and entry validation logic.']
  service_fee decimal(10,2) [note: 'Fixed service/convenience fee charged per ticket at this tier, in addition to the face value. Represents the ticketing platform processing charge (e.g., Ticketmaster convenience fee). Used in order total calculation and revenue allocation.']
  sort_order varchar [note: 'Numeric display sort order for presenting pricing tiers in the correct sequence on ticketing platforms, box office interfaces, and fan-facing purchase flows. Lower values appear first.']
  tax_rate decimal(6,4) [note: 'Applicable tax rate (as a decimal fraction, e.g., 0.0875 for 8.75%) applied to the face value for this pricing tier, based on the jurisdiction of the venue. Used by the order processing system for tax calculation and SAP S/4HANA tax reporting.']
  tier_category varchar [note: 'High-level classification of the pricing tier aligned with industry-standard seating categories: GA (General Admission), VIP (Very Important Person), SRO (Standing Room Only), PREMIUM, SUITE, ACCESSIBLE (ADA-compliant), CLUB, or FIELD level. Drives dynamic pricing engine segmentation and reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: GA|VIP|SRO|PREMIUM|SUITE|ACCESSIBLE|CLUB|FIELD — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  tier_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the pricing tier across systems (e.g., GA01, VIP_PLAT, SRO_STD, SUITE_A). Used as the business key in Ticketmaster/AXS and Archtics integrations.']
  tier_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable display name for the pricing tier as shown to fans and box office staff (e.g., \'General Admission\', \'VIP Platinum\', \'Standing Room Only\', \'Club Level Premium\').']
  tier_type varchar [note: 'Classifies the commercial packaging type of the tier: SINGLE_EVENT (per-event ticket), SEASON_PACKAGE (full season ticket), GROUP_SALES (bulk group purchase), MINI_PLAN (partial season plan), or FLEX_PLAN (flexible game selection plan). Determines eligibility rules and order processing logic.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this pricing tier record was last modified. Used for change tracking, cache invalidation in the dynamic pricing engine, and audit compliance.']

  Note: 'Reference catalog of pricing tiers available for ticket inventory, including GA (General Admission), VIP, SRO (Standing Room Only), premium club, suite, and accessible seating tiers. Defines base face value, fee structures, and eligibility rules per tier. Used by dynamic pricing engine and order processing.'
}

Table ticketing.season_ticket_package {
  season_ticket_package_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the season ticket package record in the Databricks Silver Layer. Primary key for this master resource entity.']
  calendar_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to event.event_calendar. Business justification: Season ticket packages are scoped to a season defined in event_calendar, which governs blackout dates, playoff structure, and international windows. Season ticket operations teams use event_calendar t']
  deal_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sponsorship.deal. Business justification: Sponsor-funded season ticket programs: sponsors co-brand or subsidize season ticket packages for clients/employees as deal activations. Linking the package to the governing deal enables activation ful']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Season ticket packages generate deferred revenue requiring GL account assignment for pre-recognition revenue accruals and package-level financial reporting. Finance teams need GL account on the packag']
  league_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the league or competition governing body under which this season ticket package is offered (e.g., NFL, NBA, MLB, NHL, MLS). Drives compliance rules, CBA constraints, and broadcast rights applicability.']
  loyalty_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fan.loyalty_program. Business justification: STH loyalty points configuration — season_ticket_package.sth_loyalty_points_awarded must reference the loyalty program under which points are issued to correctly apply earning rules, tier qualificatio']
  platform_channel_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to content.platform_channel. Business justification: Season ticket packages increasingly bundle streaming access on a specific platform channel (e.g., League Pass on a named OTT platform). This FK replaces the denormalized streaming_tier_included text f']
  price_tier_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the pricing tier definition (GA, VIP, SRO, Premium) that governs the face value and dynamic pricing rules applied to this package. Sourced from Ticketmaster/AXS pricing configuration.']
  primary_season_franchise_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the home team or franchise for which this season ticket package is sold. Determines the event schedule, venue, and STH (Season Ticket Holder) benefit entitlements associated with the package.']
  season_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the competitive season (e.g., 2024-25 NBA Season) for which this package is valid. Determines the event schedule, renewal cycle, and pricing validity window.']
  seating_section_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to venue.seating_section. Business justification: Season ticket packages are sold for specific sections. Section-level STH capacity planning, renewal priority management, and ADA accommodation assignment require linking packages to the canonical seat']
  venue_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the primary venue where events included in this season ticket package are held. Used for seat assignment, ADA compliance checks, and venue capacity planning in Archtics.']
  archtics_plan_code varchar [note: 'The native plan identifier assigned to this season ticket package in the Archtics venue seating and inventory management system. Used for cross-system reconciliation between the Silver Layer and the operational Archtics source of record.']
  benefit_tier varchar [note: 'The STH (Season Ticket Holder) benefit tier associated with this package, determining the level of perks, exclusive access, merchandise discounts, and concierge services. Drives benefit fulfillment workflows in Salesforce CRM and Adobe Experience Platform personalization.']
  bundle_discount_pct decimal [note: 'The percentage discount applied to the PPV consumer price for this specific season ticket package and PPV package combination. Varies per bundle pairing and cannot reside on either parent product.']
  bundle_price decimal [note: 'The negotiated price charged for this specific PPV package when accessed through the season ticket bundle. Differs from the standalone consumer_price on ppv_package and belongs to this pairing only.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this season ticket package record was first created in the system, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Provides the audit trail creation anchor for SOX and data governance compliance.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the face value and all monetary fields on this package (e.g., USD, GBP, EUR, CAD). Required for multi-market operations and SAP FI/CO financial reporting.']
  digital_delivery_method varchar [note: 'The method by which digital tickets for events in this package are delivered to the STH (Season Ticket Holder). Drives the ticket fulfillment workflow in Ticketmaster/AXS and determines the access control technology used at venue entry points.']
  dynamic_pricing_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether dynamic pricing rules (demand-based price adjustments) are applied to the individual event tickets within this season ticket package. When True, per-event ticket values may fluctuate from the base face value.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'The date on which this season ticket package expires and entitlements cease. Typically aligns with the last home event of the season. Used for access control expiry in Ticketmaster/AXS and Archtics.']
  effective_from date [note: 'The date from which this bundle inclusion is active and the STH holder is entitled to access the PPV package at the bundle price.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'The date from which this season ticket package is valid and entitlements are active for the holder. Typically aligns with the first home event of the season. Used for access control validation in Ticketmaster/AXS.']
  effective_until date [note: 'The date on which this bundle inclusion expires and the STH holder\'s entitlement to the bundled PPV package ceases.']
  face_value_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'The published face value price of the season ticket package in the operating currency, representing the total cost for the full package before any discounts, taxes, or dynamic pricing adjustments. This is the SSOT for package pricing in SAP S/4HANA SD.']
  includes_merchandise_credit boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a merchandise store credit or discount entitlement is bundled with this season ticket package. Triggers integration with Shopify Plus for credit issuance to the STH account.']
  includes_parking boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a parking pass or parking entitlement is bundled with this season ticket package. Affects venue operations planning and package face value composition.']
  installment_count varchar [note: 'Number of payment installments configured for this package when payment_plan_type is monthly_installment or quarterly_installment. Null for full_upfront plans. Used by SAP FI to generate the accounts receivable schedule.']
  inventory_count varchar [note: 'Total number of this season ticket package available for sale in the current season. Represents the maximum allocatable units in Archtics seating inventory. Drives sold_out status transitions.']
  is_ada_accessible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the seat(s) assigned to this season ticket package meet ADA (Americans with Disabilities Act) accessibility requirements. Required for compliance reporting and fan accommodation management.']
  merchandise_credit_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'The monetary value of merchandise store credit included in this season ticket package when includes_merchandise_credit is True. Denominated in the package currency_code. Null when includes_merchandise_credit is False.']
  package_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric code that uniquely identifies the season ticket package across systems (Archtics, Ticketmaster/AXS, SAP SD). Used as the business key for cross-system reconciliation and fan-facing communications.']
  package_description varchar [note: 'Long-form marketing and operational description of the season ticket package, including included events, seat location narrative, and STH benefit highlights. Used in fan-facing communications, CRM campaigns, and the Ticketmaster/AXS product listing.']
  package_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable marketing name of the season ticket package as displayed to fans and used in CRM communications (e.g., \'Full Season Gold Plan\', \'Half-Season Flex Pack\'). Sourced from Salesforce CRM product catalog.']
  package_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the season ticket package product definition. Active packages are available for sale or renewal; sold_out indicates inventory exhaustion; suspended indicates temporary hold; retired indicates end-of-life for the product definition.']
  package_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the season ticket package by coverage configuration. Determines the number of included events and pricing tier. Full-season covers all home games; half-season covers 50%; mini-plan covers a curated subset; flex-pack allows fan-selected games; group_plan for bulk group sales; premium_suite for luxury suite packages.']
  payment_plan_type varchar [note: 'The payment schedule configuration available for this season ticket package. Determines how the face value is collected from the STH (Season Ticket Holder) — either as a single upfront payment or spread across installment periods. Drives SAP FI accounts receivable scheduling.']
  renewal_deadline_date date [note: 'The date by which an existing STH (Season Ticket Holder) must exercise their renewal right for this package before it is released to the general waitlist. Drives CRM renewal campaign scheduling in Salesforce.']
  renewal_priority_rank varchar [note: 'Numeric rank determining the STH (Season Ticket Holder) renewal priority order for this package tier. Lower values indicate higher priority (e.g., rank 1 = first right of renewal). Critical for managing the annual renewal window and STH retention strategy.']
  resale_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether tickets within this season ticket package may be listed on the official resale marketplace (e.g., Ticketmaster Fan-to-Fan Exchange). Governs resale rights per league CBA (Collective Bargaining Agreement) and team policy.']
  sale_end_date date [note: 'The date on which this season ticket package is no longer available for purchase. After this date, the package status transitions to inactive or sold_out. Controls the off-sale window in Ticketmaster/AXS.']
  sale_start_date date [note: 'The date on which this season ticket package becomes available for new purchase or renewal. Controls the on-sale window in Ticketmaster/AXS and Archtics inventory systems.']
  sap_material_number varchar [note: 'The SAP S/4HANA MM material number assigned to this season ticket package as a sellable product in the ERP system. Enables financial posting, revenue recognition, and SD order management alignment.']
  seat_number varchar [note: 'The specific seat number within the assigned row for this season ticket package. Sourced from Archtics seating inventory. Null for GA (General Admission) or SRO (Standing Room Only) package types.']
  seat_quantity varchar [note: 'Number of seats included in this season ticket package definition. Typically 1 for individual packages; higher values for group plans or suite packages. Drives inventory allocation in Archtics.']
  seat_row varchar [note: 'The row designation within the assigned section for this season ticket package (e.g., \'A\', \'B\', \'12\'). Sourced from Archtics. Null for flex-pack or GA (General Admission) package types where specific seats are not pre-assigned.']
  seat_section varchar [note: 'The venue section designation associated with this season ticket package (e.g., \'Lower Bowl\', \'Club Level\', \'Upper Deck\'). Sourced from Archtics seating inventory and used for seat assignment and pricing tier validation.']
  sold_count varchar [note: 'Number of this season ticket package units that have been sold or confirmed for the current season. Used for inventory tracking, revenue forecasting in Workday Adaptive Planning, and sold_out threshold monitoring.']
  sth_loyalty_points_awarded varchar [note: 'Number of fan loyalty points awarded to the STH (Season Ticket Holder) upon purchase or renewal of this package. Feeds into the fan engagement and NPS (Net Promoter Score) program managed via Adobe Experience Platform.']
  terms_and_conditions_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the legal terms and conditions governing this season ticket package (e.g., \'v2.1\'). Required for GDPR, CCPA, and SOX compliance to ensure the correct contractual terms are associated with each package sold.']
  total_event_count varchar [note: 'Total number of home events (games, matches, or performances) included in this season ticket package. Defines the scope of the package and is used to calculate per-event cost for fan communications and analytics.']
  transferable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the STH (Season Ticket Holder) may transfer individual game tickets within this package to another person via the Ticketmaster/AXS platform. Distinct from resale eligibility — transfer is a non-commercial action.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this season ticket package record was last modified, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Used for change data capture (CDC) in the Databricks Silver Layer pipeline and SOX audit trail.']
  waitlist_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether fans can join a waitlist for this season ticket package when it is sold out. Drives the waitlist management workflow in Salesforce CRM and Archtics inventory management.']

  Note: 'Master record for season ticket packages offered to fans, including full-season, half-season, mini-plan, and flex-pack configurations. Tracks package type, included events, seat assignments, face value, renewal priority rights, and STH (Season Ticket Holder) benefits. SSOT for season ticket product definitions.'
}

Table ticketing.season_ticket_account {
  season_ticket_account_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the season ticket account record in the Databricks Silver Layer. Primary key. Entity role: MASTER_AGREEMENT — represents a long-running contractual relationship between a fan and the organization for a season ticket package.']
  data_processing_record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.data_processing_record. Business justification: Season ticket accounts involve long-term, ongoing personal data processing (multi-year tenure, marketing_opt_in, communication_preference, nps_score). GDPR/CCPA requires a documented processing record']
  fan_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the fan/customer account that holds this season ticket agreement. Links the season ticket account to the fan master record in the CRM system.']
  fiscal_period_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.fiscal_period. Business justification: Season ticket accounts have installment payment schedules tied to fiscal periods. Finance teams track installment revenue recognition and deferred revenue balances by fiscal period during period-close']
  franchise_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to league.franchise. Business justification: STH accounts are franchise-owned assets — franchises track their STH base for renewal campaigns, loyalty programs, and revenue forecasting. Franchise-level STH account reporting and renewal management']
  platform_channel_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to content.platform_channel. Business justification: Season ticket holder accounts include bundled digital streaming access on specific platform channels. STH benefit management and digital delivery operations require knowing which platform channel deli']
  price_tier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ticketing.price_tier. Business justification: season_ticket_account has pricing_tier as a STRING denormalization but no FK to the authoritative price_tier catalog. Each season ticket account is enrolled at a specific pricing tier (e.g., premium, ']
  promotion_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to merchandise.promotion. Business justification: STH exclusive merchandise promotion assignment: season ticket holders are targeted with exclusive merch promotions as a retention tool. CRM and loyalty teams need to link each STH account to its assig']
  season_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the league season or competition season for which this ticket package is valid. Determines the set of home events covered by the account.']
  season_ticket_package_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the season ticket package product definition (e.g., Full Season, Half Season, Mini Plan) that governs pricing, entitlements, and benefits for this account.']
  seating_section_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to venue.seating_section. Business justification: STH accounts are tied to specific sections for renewal priority ranking, seat relocation workflows, and ADA accommodation tracking. Section-level STH capacity reporting and renewal management require ']
  venue_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the venue where the season ticket seats are located. Drives seat map, section, and access control configuration.']
  account_balance decimal(12,2) [note: 'Current outstanding balance on the season ticket account. Positive value indicates amount owed by the fan; negative value indicates a credit. Sourced from SAP S/4HANA AR sub-ledger.']
  account_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric account number assigned to the season ticket holder (STH) by the ticketing system (Archtics/Ticketmaster). Used in all fan-facing communications, invoices, and access control lookups. Satisfies MASTER_AGREEMENT BUSINESS_IDENTIFIER category.']
  account_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the season ticket account. Drives access control, renewal workflows, and CRM segmentation. Satisfies MASTER_AGREEMENT LIFECYCLE_STATUS category.']
  account_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the season ticket account by holder category. Drives pricing eligibility, tax treatment, and benefit entitlements. Satisfies MASTER_AGREEMENT CLASSIFICATION_OR_TYPE category.']
  ada_accommodation boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the season ticket account has an Americans with Disabilities Act (ADA) accessibility accommodation on record, such as wheelchair-accessible seating or companion seat requirements.']
  communication_preference varchar [note: 'Preferred communication channel for season ticket account notifications, renewal reminders, and promotional messages. Drives outbound campaign routing in Salesforce CRM and Adobe Experience Platform.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the season ticket account record was first created in the system. Satisfies MASTER_AGREEMENT RECORD_AUDIT_CREATED category. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary amounts on this account (e.g., USD, GBP, EUR). Supports multi-market operations. Satisfies MONETARY_TRIPLET currency-code component.']
  discount_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total discount applied to the season ticket package face value (e.g., multi-seat discount, loyalty discount, early renewal discount). Satisfies MONETARY_TRIPLET adjustment component.']
  enrollment_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the fan formally enrolled in the season ticket package. Marks the start of the contractual relationship. Satisfies MASTER_AGREEMENT EFFECTIVE_FROM category.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the season ticket account expires or the current season package ends. Null for multi-year rolling agreements. Satisfies MASTER_AGREEMENT EFFECTIVE_UNTIL category.']
  face_value_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total face value of the season ticket package for the current season, representing the gross base amount before any discounts or payment plan adjustments. Expressed in the organization\'s operating currency (USD). Satisfies MONETARY_TRIPLET gross-base-amount component.']
  forward_ticket_opt_in boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the season ticket holder has enabled the ticket forwarding feature, allowing them to digitally transfer individual game tickets to guests via the Ticketmaster/AXS platform.']
  installment_count varchar [note: 'Total number of installment payments scheduled for the season ticket package under the selected payment plan. Applicable when payment_plan_type is \'installment\' or \'auto_pay\'.']
  last_nps_survey_date date [note: 'Date on which the most recent Net Promoter Score (NPS) survey was completed by the season ticket holder. Used to control survey frequency and track sentiment trends over time.']
  marketing_opt_in boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the season ticket holder has provided consent to receive marketing communications from the organization. Required for GDPR and CCPA compliance.']
