  net_package_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Net amount payable for the season ticket package after all discounts are applied (face_value_amount minus discount_amount). Represents the contractual obligation amount. Satisfies MONETARY_TRIPLET net-total component.']
  next_payment_date date [note: 'Date on which the next scheduled payment is due for this season ticket account. Used to trigger payment reminders and auto-payment processing.']
  nps_score varchar [note: 'Most recent Net Promoter Score (NPS) survey response from the season ticket holder, on a scale of 0–10. Used to segment promoters, passives, and detractors for retention and experience improvement programs.']
  parking_included boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the season ticket package includes a parking pass entitlement for home events at the venue.']
  parking_zone varchar [note: 'Designated parking zone or lot code assigned to this season ticket account when parking is included in the package (e.g., \'LOT-A\', \'GARAGE-2\', \'VIP-NORTH\').']
  payment_plan_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the payment plan for this season ticket account. Drives collections workflows, account suspension triggers, and financial reporting.']
  payment_plan_type varchar [note: 'Type of payment arrangement selected by the season ticket holder. Determines billing schedule, installment frequency, and auto-payment eligibility.']
  renewal_deadline_date date [note: 'The date by which the season ticket holder must confirm renewal to retain seat assignments and pricing. Used to trigger automated renewal reminder communications.']
  renewal_status varchar [note: 'Current renewal decision status for the upcoming season. Tracks whether the season ticket holder (STH) has committed to renewing, declined, or is pending a decision. Critical for revenue forecasting and retention campaigns.']
  resale_opt_in boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the season ticket holder has opted into the official resale marketplace program, allowing unused tickets to be listed for resale through the organization\'s authorized platform.']
  row_code varchar [note: 'Row identifier within the assigned section for the season ticket seats (e.g., \'A\', \'B\', \'12\'). Used for access control and seat map display.']
  seat_count varchar [note: 'Total number of seats assigned to this season ticket account. A single account may hold multiple contiguous or non-contiguous seats.']
  seat_numbers varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of seat numbers within the assigned row for this account (e.g., \'5,6,7\'). Stored as a delimited string since seat assignments are managed atomically at the account level in Archtics.']
  section_code varchar [note: 'Alphanumeric code identifying the venue section where the season ticket seats are located (e.g., \'101\', \'CLUB-A\', \'SUITE-12\'). Sourced from Archtics seating inventory.']
  source_system_account_ref varchar [note: 'Native account identifier from the originating operational system (Archtics or Ticketmaster/AXS) used for reconciliation and lineage tracing between the Silver Layer and the system of record.']
  tenure_years varchar [note: 'Number of consecutive seasons the fan has held a season ticket account with the organization. Used to calculate STH loyalty tier eligibility and long-term retention metrics.']
  ticket_delivery_method varchar [note: 'Preferred method for delivering season tickets to the holder. Determines fulfillment channel and associated costs.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the season ticket account record. Used for change data capture (CDC) in the Databricks Silver Layer pipeline and audit trail compliance.']

  Note: 'Transactional record linking a fan account to an active season ticket package. Captures enrollment date, seat assignments, payment plan status, renewal status, account balance, STH loyalty tier, and communication preferences. Represents the active season ticket holder relationship and entitlement.'
}

Table ticketing.group_sale {
  group_sale_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the group ticket sale transaction. Primary key for this record.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the organizational buyer account (corporate, school, community organization) in the CRM system responsible for this group purchase.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Group sales are managed by a dedicated group sales department with its own cost center. Cost center on group_sale enables departmental P&L reporting for the group sales business unit, distinct from in']
  deal_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sponsorship.deal. Business justification: Sponsor group ticket activation: sponsors purchase group ticket blocks as part of deal activations (client entertainment, employee programs). Direct FK enables sponsor group sales reporting, deal fulf']
  fiscal_period_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.fiscal_period. Business justification: Group sales span multiple fiscal periods (deposit in one period, final payment in another). Finance needs fiscal_period on group_sale for deferred revenue tracking and period-close reconciliation of g']
  franchise_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to league.franchise. Business justification: Group sales are managed by franchise sales teams with franchise-specific quotas and commission structures. Franchise-level group sales pipeline reporting and revenue attribution is a core sales operat']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Group sales generate revenue requiring GL account assignment for revenue recognition and pre-invoice accruals. Finance teams need GL account on group_sale for deposit accruals and final payment postin']
  league_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to league.league_schedule. Business justification: Group sales coordinators require league_schedule context (game_type, broadcast_network, ticket_on_sale_date, ppv_flag) for pricing negotiations and fulfillment scheduling. Group sales contracts refere']
  league_season_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to league.season. Business justification: Group sales quotas, pricing windows, and minimum quantities are set per season. Season-level group sales performance tracking and year-over-year comparison is a named sales management process. No seas']
  price_tier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ticketing.price_tier. Business justification: Group sales are priced against a specific price tier (e.g., group discount tier, hospitality tier). group_sale has seating_tier as a STRING denormalization and face_value_price/negotiated_price as raw']
  primary_group_event_fixture_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific event for which the group ticket sale is being processed. Links to the event master record.']
  seating_section_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to venue.seating_section. Business justification: Group sales fulfillment and section capacity management require linking group allocations to the canonical seating_section for capacity availability checks, ADA group accommodation, and section-level ']
  venue_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the venue where the event is hosted and where the group seating block is allocated.']
  approval_date date [note: 'The date on which the pricing authority approval was granted for this group sale. Null if approval was not required or is still pending.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Status of the pricing authority approval workflow for this group sale. Required when negotiated discount exceeds standard group floor thresholds.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the pricing authority manager who approved the group sale discount rate. Null if approval was not required.']
  cancellation_date date [note: 'The date on which the group sale was cancelled, if applicable. Null for active or fulfilled sales. Triggers refund and inventory release workflows.']
  cancellation_reason varchar [note: 'Reason code for the cancellation of the group sale. Used for revenue recovery analysis and policy refinement.']
  catering_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'The monetary value of catering and food and beverage services included in or added to the group sale. Null if catering_included is false.']
  catering_included boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a catering or food and beverage add-on is included as part of the group sale package. Triggers catering coordination workflow with venue operations.']
  coordinator_email varchar [note: 'Primary email address of the group coordinator used for booking confirmations, ticket delivery, and event-day communications.']
  coordinator_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the primary contact person at the purchasing organization who is responsible for coordinating the group experience on the day of the event.']
  coordinator_phone varchar [note: 'Primary phone number of the group coordinator for day-of-event logistics, access control, and urgent communications.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this group sale record was first created in the system. Audit trail field for data lineage and SOX compliance.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary amounts in this group sale record (e.g., USD, GBP, EUR).']
  deposit_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'The initial deposit amount required to confirm and hold the group ticket block. Typically a percentage of the net_amount per group sales policy.']
  deposit_due_date date [note: 'The date by which the deposit payment must be received to maintain the group ticket block reservation. Expiry triggers automatic release of inventory.']
  deposit_received_date date [note: 'The actual date on which the deposit payment was received and confirmed. Null if deposit has not yet been received.']
  discount_rate decimal(5,4) [note: 'The percentage discount applied to the face value price for this group sale, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.15 = 15%). Governed by group pricing authority tiers.']
  face_value_price decimal(12,2) [note: 'The standard published per-ticket face value price before any group discount is applied. Used as the baseline for discount calculation and revenue reporting.']
  final_payment_due_date date [note: 'The date by which the remaining balance (net_amount minus deposit_amount) must be paid in full. Typically set relative to the event date per group sales policy.']
  final_payment_received_date date [note: 'The actual date on which the final balance payment was received and confirmed, completing the payment obligation for the group sale.']
  fulfillment_date date [note: 'The date on which ticket fulfillment was completed and all tickets were successfully delivered to the group coordinator.']
  fulfillment_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the ticket fulfillment process, tracking whether tickets have been issued and delivered to the group coordinator or attendees.']
  gross_amount decimal(14,2) [note: 'Total gross revenue for the group sale before taxes and fees, calculated as negotiated_price multiplied by ticket_quantity. Represents the top-line transaction value.']
  group_name varchar [note: 'The name of the purchasing group or organization (e.g., \'Acme Corp Corporate Outing\', \'Lincoln Elementary School Field Trip\', \'Birthday Party - Smith Family\'). Used for fulfillment, signage, and hospitality coordination.']
  group_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the purchasing group by organizational category, used for pricing authority, package eligibility, and reporting segmentation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: corporate|school|community_org|birthday_party|suite_buyout|military|nonprofit|other — promote to reference product]']
  hospitality_package_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the group experience or hospitality package selected (e.g., pre-game buffet, suite catering, meet-and-greet, field access). References the hospitality package catalog.']
  negotiated_price decimal(12,2) [note: 'The agreed per-ticket price after group discount negotiation. Requires pricing authority approval when below standard group floor price.']
  net_amount decimal(14,2) [note: 'Total net amount due from the organizational buyer including gross amount, taxes, and service fees. This is the final invoice total for the group sale.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for internal notes, special instructions, or coordination details relevant to the group sale (e.g., accessibility requirements, special signage requests, VIP escort needs).']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'The payment instrument used by the organizational buyer to settle the group sale. Governs PCI-DSS compliance requirements and accounts receivable workflow.']
  sale_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which the group sale agreement was formally executed and confirmed by the organizational buyer.']
  sale_number varchar [note: 'Externally-visible, human-readable reference number for the group sale transaction used in customer communications, invoices, and fulfillment workflows. Format: GRP-YYYY-NNNNNN.']
  sale_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the group sale transaction, tracking progression from initial inquiry through fulfillment. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|pending_approval|confirmed|deposit_received|paid|fulfilled|cancelled — promote to reference product]']
  seating_row varchar [note: 'The row designation within the assigned seating section for the group block (e.g., \'A\', \'B\', \'12\'). Null for GA or SRO allocations.']
  service_fee_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total service and facility fees charged on the group sale transaction, separate from the ticket face value.']
  tax_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total applicable taxes assessed on the group sale transaction per applicable jurisdiction tax rules.']
  ticket_delivery_method varchar [note: 'The method by which digital or physical tickets are delivered to the group coordinator or individual attendees. Drives fulfillment workflow in Ticketmaster/AXS.']
  ticket_quantity varchar [note: 'Total number of tickets included in this group sale. Must be 10 or greater per group sale eligibility policy.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this group sale record was last modified. Supports change tracking, audit compliance, and Silver layer incremental processing.']

  Note: 'Transactional record for group ticket sales involving 10+ tickets sold to a single organizational buyer (corporate outings, school field trips, community organizations, birthday parties, suite buyouts). Tracks group name, size, negotiated discount rate, group coordinator contact, deposit schedule, payment milestones, fulfillment status, assigned seating block, catering/hospitality add-ons, and group experience package. Managed as distinct workflow from individual retail sales with separate approval and pricing authority.'
}

Table ticketing.resale {
  resale_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for resale']
  buyer_ticket_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ticketing.ticket_order. Business justification: When a resale transaction completes, a new ticket_order is generated for the buyer. The resale record should reference this new buyer order (buyer_ticket_order_id → ticket_order.ticket_order_id) to cl']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Resale platform fees and revenue share are attributed to the digital ticketing operations cost center for departmental P&L reporting. Cost center assignment on resale enables management reporting for ']
  fiscal_period_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.fiscal_period. Business justification: Resale transactions must be posted to the correct fiscal period for platform fee revenue recognition and revenue share liability tracking. Period-close reconciliation of resale revenue requires fiscal']
  franchise_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to league.franchise. Business justification: Franchises receive a revenue share from official resale transactions and enforce anti-scalping policies. Franchise-level resale revenue share calculation and compliance monitoring requires direct fran']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Resale transactions generate platform fee revenue and revenue share amounts requiring GL account posting. Finance teams post resale platform_fee_amount and revenue_share_amount to specific GL accounts']
  investigation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.investigation. Business justification: Resale fraud investigations (scalping rings, fraudulent listings, price manipulation) require linking investigation records to specific resale transactions. Sports entertainment compliance and legal t']
  league_season_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to league.season. Business justification: League-level seasonal resale volume reporting, revenue share settlement, and anti-scalping compliance audits are named regulatory and financial processes. Season attribution on resale transactions ena']
  obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.obligation. Business justification: Resale transactions must comply with specific regulatory obligations (anti-scalping laws, consumer protection statutes). resale has anti_scalping_rule_code. Linking each resale to its governing obliga']
  original_access_entitlement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ticketing.access_entitlement. Business justification: A resale transaction invalidates the original access entitlement and triggers issuance of a new one. Linking resale to the original access_entitlement via original_access_entitlement_id enables full l']
  original_ticket_order_line_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ticketing.ticket_order_line. Business justification: A resale transaction originates from a specific ticket that was originally purchased as a line item in a ticket order. Linking resale to the original ticket_order_line via original_ticket_order_line_i']
  price_tier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ticketing.price_tier. Business justification: resale has ticket_tier as a STRING denormalization but no FK to the authoritative price_tier catalog. The resale listing price and tier classification should reference price_tier for consistent tier-b']
  regulatory_filing_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_filing. Business justification: Anti-scalping regulatory filings (UK Secondary Ticketing Act, EU consumer protection) require tracing resale transactions to their governing regulatory submission. resale has anti_scalping_compliant a']
  resale_buyer_fan_fan_profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the fan/account record of the individual who purchased or received the resale ticket. Populated upon transaction completion or transfer acceptance.']
  resale_event_fixture_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the event for which the resale ticket is valid. Used for event-level resale volume reporting and anti-scalping rule enforcement.']
  resale_fan_profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the fan/account record of the individual or entity listing the ticket for resale or transfer. Used for seller identity verification and anti-scalping compliance.']
  resale_fixture_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the event for which the resale ticket is valid. Used for event-level resale volume reporting and anti-scalping rule enforcement.']
  resale_original_ticket_ticket_inventory_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the primary market ticket record from which this resale lifecycle originated. Links the secondary market activity back to the original ticketing entitlement.']
  resale_ticket_inventory_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the primary market ticket record from which this resale lifecycle originated. Links the secondary market activity back to the original ticketing entitlement.']
  season_ticket_package_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the season ticket package from which this resale ticket originated, if applicable. Enables season ticket holder (STH) resale volume tracking and package-level revenue share calculations.']
  venue_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the venue where the event is held. Used for venue-level resale volume reporting, ADA compliance validation of resale seat accessibility, and jurisdiction-specific anti-scalping law application.']
  anti_scalping_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the resale transaction complies with applicable anti-scalping rules (e.g., price cap relative to face value, seller type restrictions, event-specific resale windows). True = compliant; False = violation flagged for review.']
  anti_scalping_rule_code varchar [note: 'The specific anti-scalping rule or policy code applied to evaluate this transaction (e.g., \'PRICE_CAP_150PCT\', \'STH_ONLY_RESALE\', \'BLACKOUT_WINDOW\'). Populated when anti_scalping_compliant is False to identify the violated rule.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this resale lifecycle record was first created in the Silver layer data product. Audit trail field for data lineage and GDPR data retention compliance. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary amounts on this record (listing_price, sale_price, original_face_value, platform_fee_amount, seller_proceeds, revenue_share_amount). Supports multi-currency global markets.']
  delivery_method varchar [note: 'The method by which the new entitlement token was delivered to the buyer or transfer recipient: mobile_app (push to Ticketmaster/AXS app), email (email delivery), print_at_home (PDF), will_call (venue box office pickup).']
  dynamic_pricing_applied boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the marketplace applied dynamic pricing algorithms to adjust the listing price during the active listing period. True = dynamic pricing was applied; False = static listing price maintained.']
  gdpr_consent_verified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the seller\'s and buyer\'s GDPR data processing consent has been verified prior to processing the resale transaction. Mandatory for EU-market transactions. True = consent verified; False = consent not confirmed.']
  lifecycle_status varchar [note: 'Current state of the resale record progressing through the secondary market lifecycle: listed (active on marketplace), sold (purchase completed), transferred (peer-to-peer transfer completed), expired (listing lapsed without sale), withdrawn (seller removed listing).']
  listing_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the seller created the resale listing on the marketplace platform. Represents the start of the resale lifecycle. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  listing_expiry_date date [note: 'The date on which the resale listing automatically expires if not sold or withdrawn. Typically set to the event date. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.']
  listing_price decimal(12,2) [note: 'The asking price set by the seller at the time of listing creation, in the transaction currency. Represents the gross amount the buyer is expected to pay before marketplace fees.']
  listing_reference_number varchar [note: 'Externally visible alphanumeric identifier assigned to the resale listing by the marketplace platform (e.g., Ticketmaster Fan-to-Fan, AXS Marketplace). Used by sellers and buyers to reference a specific listing.']
  movement_type varchar [note: 'Classifies the nature of the secondary market activity: resale (commercial sale at a price), gift_transfer (no-cost transfer to another fan), forward_transfer (ticket forwarded to another account), peer_to_peer (direct fan-to-fan exchange outside marketplace).']
  new_entitlement_token varchar [note: 'The new digital access token or barcode issued to the buyer/recipient upon completion of the resale or transfer. Replaces the original ticket token and is used for venue access control via Ticketmaster/AXS or Archtics.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for operational notes related to the resale record, such as manual override reasons, dispute notes, or compliance review comments. Not used for structured data.']
  original_entitlement_token varchar [note: 'The digital access token or barcode of the original ticket that was invalidated upon resale or transfer. Retained for audit trail and anti-fraud verification purposes.']
  original_face_value decimal(12,2) [note: 'The primary market face value of the ticket at the time of original purchase. Used to calculate price uplift, anti-scalping cap compliance, and revenue share calculations.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'The payment instrument used by the buyer to complete the resale purchase (e.g., credit_card, debit_card, digital_wallet, bank_transfer, marketplace_credit). Supports PCI-DSS compliance reporting and payment analytics.']
  payment_reference varchar [note: 'The tokenized or masked payment transaction reference number from the payment processor for the resale purchase. Stored in compliance with PCI-DSS tokenization requirements — no raw card data is stored.']
  platform_fee_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'The fee charged by the marketplace platform (e.g., Ticketmaster, AXS) for facilitating the resale transaction. Deducted from the sale price before calculating seller proceeds.']
  price_uplift_pct decimal(7,4) [note: 'The percentage by which the sale_price exceeds the original_face_value, calculated as ((sale_price - original_face_value) / original_face_value) * 100. Used for anti-scalping compliance monitoring and dynamic pricing analytics.']
  revenue_share_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'The portion of the resale sale price allocated back to the league, team, or rights holder per the applicable revenue share agreement. Calculated based on revenue_share_rate applied to the sale price.']
  revenue_share_rate decimal(5,4) [note: 'The contractual percentage rate (expressed as a decimal, e.g., 0.0500 = 5%) applied to the sale price to calculate the revenue_share_amount owed to the rights holder. Sourced from the applicable league or team revenue share agreement.']
  sale_price decimal(12,2) [note: 'The final price at which the ticket was sold on the secondary market. May differ from listing_price due to dynamic pricing adjustments or negotiated changes. Populated only when lifecycle_status is \'sold\'.']
  seat_number varchar [note: 'The individual seat number within the row for the resale ticket. Sourced from Archtics. Required for access control validation and buyer-facing listing accuracy.']
  seat_row varchar [note: 'The row designation within the section for the resale ticket (e.g., \'A\', \'12\'). Sourced from Archtics. Provides granular seat location for buyer transparency and pricing validation.']
  seat_section varchar [note: 'The venue section designation of the resale ticket (e.g., \'Lower Bowl\', \'101\', \'VIP Club\'). Sourced from Archtics venue seating inventory. Used for pricing tier validation and buyer-facing listing display.']
  seller_proceeds decimal(12,2) [note: 'Net amount remitted to the seller after deducting platform fees and any applicable revenue share from the sale price. Represents the seller\'s actual payout for the transaction.']
  seller_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the seller entity: season_ticket_holder (STH reselling allocated seats), individual_fan (casual reseller), broker (licensed ticket broker), corporate_account (business account holder). Drives anti-scalping rule applicability and revenue share tiers.']
  source_system_listing_code varchar [note: 'The native listing identifier from the originating marketplace system (Ticketmaster, AXS, or other platform). Enables reconciliation between the Silver layer resale record and the operational source system record.']
  transaction_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the resale transaction was executed (purchase completed or transfer accepted). This is the principal business event timestamp for sold and transferred records. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  transfer_restriction_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the original ticket carried a transfer restriction (e.g., non-transferable VIP package, restricted season ticket). True = transfer restrictions applied; False = no restrictions. Used to validate eligibility for resale or transfer.']
  transfer_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when a peer-to-peer ticket transfer (gift or forward) was completed and the new entitlement was issued to the recipient. Distinct from transaction_timestamp which applies to commercial sales. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this resale lifecycle record was last modified in the Silver layer data product. Tracks status transitions, price updates, and entitlement token changes. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']

  Note: 'Unified lifecycle record for all secondary market and peer-to-peer ticket activity. Captures listing creation (price, channel, seller type, listing status), marketplace transaction execution (sale price, platform fees, seller proceeds, buyer reference), and peer-to-peer transfers (gift, forward). Tracks movement type, transfer timestamp, new entitlement token issuance, transfer restrictions, anti-scalping rule compliance, and revenue share calculations. SSOT for all secondary market listings, resale transactions, and fan-to-fan ticket transfers. Replaces separate listing and transaction records with a single lifecycle entity progressing through states: listed → sold/transferred/expired/withdrawn.'
}

Linking to security.access_zone enables validation of entitlement zone against zone credential requirements, capacity limits, and scree']

Business justification: Activation-specific entitlement issuance: sponsor activations (e.g., hospitality suite, VIP experience) generate specific access entitlements. Linking to the activation enables activation-level proof-']
  beneficiary_athlete_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to athlete.athlete_profile. Business justification: Access entitlements issued as player allotment tickets must be traceable to the beneficiary athlete for CBA compliance, credential audits, and revocation workflows (e.g., suspension triggers allotment']
  broadcast_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to broadcast.broadcast_schedule. Business justification: Digital/PPV access entitlements must be validated against the broadcast_schedule window (valid_from/valid_until).   companion_entitlement_id varchar [note: 'Reference to a linked companion entitlement for ADA accessible seating, where a companion seat is paired with an accessible seat. Null if no companion pairing exists. Supports ADA compliance and seating management.']
  credential_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to security.credential. Business justification: VIP/field-access dual-validation: high-tier entitlements (hospitality, field passes, backstage) require a physical security credential issued by accreditation. Ticketing access control systems must re']
  deal_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sponsorship.deal. Business justification: Sponsor hospitality proof-of-performance: access entitlements issued under a sponsorship deal must trace back to that deal for fulfillment verification, audit, and contract compliance reporting. Sport']

Enables redemption fulfillment tracking, points deduction reconcil']
  media_rights_deal_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to broadcast.media_rights_deal. Rights compliance audits require tracing each digital entitl']

Sports entertainment OTT operations require entitlements to']

  price_tier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ticketing.price_tier. Business justification: access_entitlement.price_tier_code (STRING) denormalizes the pricing tier of the entitlement. Replacing with price_tier_id FK normalizes this to the authoritative price_tier catalog, enabling JOIN to ']

  primary_access_ticket_inventory_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent ticket record from which this entitlement was generated. Links the entitlement to the originating ticket purchase or transfer transaction.']
  season_ticket_package_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the season ticket package from which this entitlement was generated, if applicable. Null for single-event purchases. Links season ticket holders to their individual game entitlements.']

R']
  tertiary_access_original_fan_fan_profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the fan who originally purchased this entitlement before any transfers occurred. Null if no transfer has taken place. Used for season ticket holder analytics and NIL (Name Image and Likeness) attribution.']
  ticket_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the originating sales order or transaction from which this entitlement was created. Supports revenue reconciliation and PCI-DSS compliant payment audit trails.']
  ticket_order_line_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ticketing.ticket_order_line. Business justification: Each access entitlement corresponds to a specific ticket order line item — the transactional record of the individual ticket sold. access_entitlement already has order_id → ticket_order (header level)']
  venue_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the venue where the event is held and where this entitlement grants physical access. Used for venue operations and access zone management.']
  admission_type varchar [note: 'Defines the re-entry policy for this entitlement. SINGLE_USE = one-time entry only; MULTI_USE = multiple entries permitted (e.g., festival passes); RE_ENTRY = exit and re-entry allowed within the validity window.']
  age_restriction_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this entitlement is subject to an age restriction requirement (e.g., 18+ or 21+ events). Gate staff must verify age documentation when this flag is true.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this entitlement record was first created in the data platform. Audit trail field for data lineage and compliance reporting.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the face value and any financial amounts on this entitlement (e.g., USD, GBP, EUR). Supports multi-currency operations across global markets.']
  delivery_method varchar [note: 'The channel through which the digital or physical ticket credential was delivered to the fan. Drives fulfilment workflows and fan experience design. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: MOBILE|EMAIL|PRINT_AT_HOME|WILL_CALL|MAIL|NFC_WALLET|KIOSK — promote to reference product]']
  delivery_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the ticket delivery to the fan. PENDING = not yet sent; DELIVERED = successfully delivered to fan; FAILED = delivery attempt failed; RECALLED = credential recalled prior to event.']
  entitlement_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text operational notes associated with this entitlement, such as special access instructions, VIP hospitality details, or box office agent comments. Used by venue operations staff.']
  entitlement_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the access entitlement. VALID = active and unused; USED = scanned and admitted; VOIDED = cancelled or invalidated; TRANSFERRED = ownership transferred to another fan; EXPIRED = validity window passed without use; SUSPENDED = temporarily blocked pending review.']
  entitlement_token varchar [note: 'The unique cryptographic token (barcode, QR code, or NFC credential value) used for physical gate access validation. This is the machine-readable credential presented at the access control point. Classified confidential as it is the operative access credential.']
  entitlement_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the access entitlement by ticket tier or access category. GA (General Admission), VIP (Very Important Person), SRO (Standing Room Only), SEASON (Season Ticket), GROUP (Group Sales), COMP (Complimentary), PRESS (Media/Press credential. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: GA|VIP|SRO|SEASON|GROUP|COMP|PRESS|SUITE|HOSPITALITY — promote to reference product]']
  external_entitlement_ref varchar [note: 'The entitlement or ticket identifier as recorded in the originating source system (Ticketmaster, AXS, or Archtics). Used for cross-system reconciliation and support case resolution.']
  face_value_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The original printed or listed face value of the ticket associated with this entitlement, in the transaction currency. Used for revenue reporting, resale price comparison, and regulatory compliance with ticket scalping laws.']
  gate_code varchar [note: 'The identifier of the physical gate or access control point where this entitlement was scanned and validated. Null if not yet scanned. Used for gate throughput analysis and crowd flow management.']
  is_ada_accessible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the seat or access zone assigned to this entitlement is an ADA (Americans with Disabilities Act) accessible position. Required for compliance reporting and venue accessibility audits.']
  is_resaleable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this entitlement is eligible for listing on the official resale marketplace. Distinct from transferability — resale implies a monetary transaction on the secondary market, subject to league and venue resale policies.']
  is_transferable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this entitlement can be transferred to another fan via the resale marketplace or peer-to-peer transfer. Controlled by league rules, event promoter policy, and CBA restrictions.']
  issued_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this entitlement was originally generated and issued, typically upon ticket purchase confirmation or transfer completion. Represents the principal business event timestamp for this record.']
  loyalty_points_earned varchar [note: 'The number of fan loyalty or rewards points earned upon validation of this entitlement at the gate. Feeds into the fan engagement and CRM loyalty programme managed via Adobe Experience Platform and Salesforce CRM.']
  scan_attempt_count varchar [note: 'The total number of scan attempts recorded against this entitlement at access control points, including both successful and failed attempts. Used for fraud detection and duplicate entry prevention.']
  scan_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this entitlement was scanned and validated at the venue access control point. Null if the entitlement has not yet been used. Used for gate throughput analytics and attendance reporting.']
  seat_number varchar [note: 'The specific seat number within the row assigned to this entitlement. Null for General Admission or Standing Room Only entitlements. Sourced from Archtics seating inventory.']
  seat_row varchar [note: 'The row designation within the assigned section for this entitlement (e.g., \'A\', \'12\', \'GA\'). Null for General Admission or Standing Room Only entitlements.']
  seat_section varchar [note: 'The section designation within the venue assigned to this entitlement (e.g., \'A\', \'101\', \'FLOOR\'). Sourced from Archtics venue seating inventory. Used for access zone routing at gates.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record that originated this entitlement record (e.g., TICKETMASTER, AXS, ARCHTICS, MANUAL for box office, API for third-party integrations). Supports data lineage and reconciliation.']
  token_format varchar [note: 'The physical or digital format of the entitlement credential presented at the gate. Determines the scanning hardware and validation protocol used at access control points. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: BARCODE|QR_CODE|NFC|MOBILE_WALLET|RFID|PAPER — promote to reference product if additional formats are added]']
  transfer_count varchar [note: 'The number of times this entitlement has been transferred between fans. Used to enforce maximum transfer limits set by league policy and to detect potential scalping activity.']
  transferred_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when ownership of this entitlement was transferred to another fan via the resale marketplace or peer-to-peer transfer. Null if no transfer has occurred.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this entitlement record was last modified in the data platform. Tracks status changes, transfers, and voiding events for audit and compliance purposes.']
  valid_from_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time from which this entitlement becomes active and can be used for venue access. Defines the start of the validity window. Gate systems reject scans before this timestamp.']
  valid_until_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time after which this entitlement expires and can no longer be used for venue access. Defines the end of the validity window. Gate systems reject scans after this timestamp.']
  void_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardised reason code explaining why this entitlement was voided. Null if not voided. Used for refund processing, fraud analytics, and compliance reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: REFUND|FRAUD|DUPLICATE|EVENT_CANCELLED|POLICY_VIOLATION|SYSTEM_ERROR|CHARGEBACK — promote to reference product]']
  voided_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this entitlement was voided or cancelled. Null if the entitlement has not been voided. Required for audit trail and refund processing compliance.']

  Note: 'Digital entitlement record granting a specific fan the right to access a venue for a specific event. Generated upon ticket purchase or transfer. Contains barcode/QR token, NFC credential, validity window, seat assignment, access zone, and current entitlement status (valid, used, voided, transferred). SSOT for access control decisions at gate.'
}

Table ticketing.gate_scan {
  gate_scan_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each gate scan event record in the access control system. Primary key for the gate_scan data product in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  access_entitlement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ticketing.access_entitlement. Business justification: A gate scan event validates a specific access entitlement. Adding access_entitlement_id to gate_scan creates the critical operational link between the physical scan event and the digital entitlement r']
  access_zone_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to security.access_zone. Business justification: Gate scan records must reference the structured security access zone for real-time occupancy tracking, crowd management reporting, and security incident correlation. Security operations teams use per-']
  primary_gate_event_fixture_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the live event (game, concert, match) for which this gate scan was performed. Links the scan to the event scheduling and attendance tracking domain.']
  primary_gate_fan_fan_profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the fan/patron account associated with the scanned ticket. Enables real-time attendance tracking, fan engagement analytics, and post-event access audit. Sourced from Salesforce CRM and Adobe Experience Platform fan profiles.']
  primary_gate_ticket_inventory_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific ticket entitlement record that was presented and scanned at the gate. Core link between the scan event and the ticketing inventory.']
  production_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to event.production_schedule. Business justification: Gate scan operations are governed by production_schedule (doors_open_time, gate_open_timestamp, event_start_time). Linking gate_scan to production_schedule enables entry timing compliance reporting an']
  screening_checkpoint_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to security.screening_checkpoint. Business justification: Every fan gate scan occurs at or adjacent to a security screening checkpoint. Linking gate_scan to screening_checkpoint enables throughput analysis per checkpoint, equipment performance reporting, que']
  security_incident_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to security.security_incident. Business justification: Post-incident forensic audit: a gate scan anomaly (duplicate scan, watchlist match, override) that precipitates a security incident must be traceable. Security operations and regulatory post-incident ']
  venue_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the venue where the gate scan occurred. Supports venue operations reporting and multi-venue attendance analytics.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this gate scan record was first captured and written to the Databricks Silver Layer. Represents the audit creation timestamp for data lineage and ingestion tracking. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  denial_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code indicating the specific reason access was denied or flagged. Populated only when scan_result is \'denied\', \'duplicate\', or \'error\'. Used for fraud detection, access control audits, and operational troubleshooting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EXPIRED|INVALID_BARCODE|DUPLICATE_USE|WRONG_EVENT|WRONG_GATE|VOIDED|BLACKLISTED|SYSTEM_ERROR — promote to reference product]']
  device_code varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the scanning hardware device (handheld scanner, fixed turnstile reader) that processed the scan event. Used for device performance monitoring, firmware version tracking, and hardware fault diagnosis.']
  device_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the scanning hardware used to process the gate scan. Distinguishes between fixed turnstile readers, handheld scanners used by staff, mobile app scans, self-service kiosks, and staff tablets. Informs device fleet management and scan performance benchmarking.']
  entitlement_token varchar [note: 'The digital or physical barcode/QR code/NFC token value that was presented and scanned at the gate. Represents the access credential encoded on the ticket. Used for entitlement validation, fraud detection, and duplicate scan identification.']
  event_date date [note: 'Calendar date of the event for which the ticket was scanned. Denormalized from the event record to support efficient date-partitioned querying of scan data and attendance reporting without requiring a join to the event table.']
  firmware_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the firmware running on the scanning device at the time of the scan. Used for device fleet management, compatibility tracking, and correlating scan anomalies with specific firmware releases.']
  gate_identifier varchar [note: 'Alphanumeric code identifying the specific gate or entry point at the venue where the scan occurred (e.g., \'GATE-A\', \'GATE-NW-01\'). Used for ingress flow analysis, bottleneck detection, and venue operations management. Sourced from Archtics venue configuration.']
  is_ada_accessible_entry boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the gate/entry point used for this scan is designated as an Americans with Disabilities Act (ADA) accessible entry point. Supports ADA compliance reporting and accessibility audit requirements.']
  is_mobile_ticket boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the scanned ticket was delivered and presented as a digital mobile ticket (via app or mobile wallet) as opposed to a printed or physical ticket. Tracks digital ticket delivery adoption rates and supports DTC (Direct-To-Consumer) digital strategy analytics.']
  is_reentry_permitted boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the ticket type and event policy allow re-entry after an exit scan. Drives access control logic at the gate and supports policy compliance auditing.']
  minutes_before_event_start varchar [note: 'Calculated number of minutes before the scheduled event start time that the scan occurred. Negative values indicate scans after event start. Supports ingress flow analysis, gate staffing optimization, and fan arrival behavior analytics.']
  network_mode varchar [note: 'Connectivity state of the scanning device at the time of the scan. \'online\' = real-time validation against central entitlement database; \'offline\' = scan processed using locally cached entitlement list; \'degraded\' = partial connectivity with reduced validation capability. Affects scan result reliability and audit requirements.']
  override_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the scan result was manually overridden by an authorized operator (e.g., granting access despite a system denial). Overrides require operator accountability and are flagged for post-event security audit.']
  override_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text or coded reason provided by the operator when manually overriding a scan result. Populated only when override_flag is True. Required for security audit and operator accountability reporting.']
  processing_duration_ms varchar [note: 'Time in milliseconds taken by the access control device to process the scan and return a result. Used for device performance benchmarking, SLA monitoring, and identifying hardware or network latency issues at entry points.']
  scan_direction varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the scan represents an initial entry into the venue, an exit, or a re-entry after leaving. Supports real-time occupancy tracking, re-entry policy enforcement, and post-event ingress/egress flow analysis.']
  scan_reference_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric reference number assigned by the Ticketmaster/AXS access control hardware to uniquely identify this scan transaction. Used for cross-system reconciliation and audit trail.']
  scan_result varchar [note: 'Outcome of the gate scan attempt as returned by the access control system. \'granted\' = valid entitlement, access permitted; \'denied\' = invalid or expired entitlement, access refused; \'duplicate\' = entitlement already used, re-entry attempt flagged; \'void\' = ticket voided prior to scan; \'error\' = device or system error during scan processing.']
  scan_sequence_number varchar [note: 'Sequential count of scan attempts for this specific entitlement token within the event. A value of 1 indicates the first scan; values greater than 1 indicate re-entry or duplicate scan attempts. Critical for duplicate detection and re-entry policy enforcement.']
  scan_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time (with timezone offset) when the gate scan event occurred as recorded by the access control hardware. Principal real-world event time used for real-time attendance tracking, ingress flow analysis, and post-event access audit. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  seat_number varchar [note: 'Individual seat number as encoded on the scanned ticket. Enables precise seat-level attendance confirmation and supports ADA accessibility compliance verification for designated accessible seating.']
  seat_row varchar [note: 'Row designation within the section as encoded on the scanned ticket (e.g., \'A\', \'B\', \'12\'). Supports access validation and post-event attendance mapping at row level.']
  seat_section varchar [note: 'Venue section designation encoded on the scanned ticket (e.g., \'101\', \'LOWER-BOWL\', \'FIELD-LEVEL\'). Used to validate that the patron is entering through the correct gate for their assigned section and for post-event seating utilization analysis.']
  source_event_key varchar [note: 'Natural key or transaction identifier from the originating source system (Ticketmaster/AXS) for this scan record. Enables traceability back to the source system for reconciliation, reprocessing, and audit purposes in the Silver Layer.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the operational source system that generated this gate scan record. Supports data lineage tracking and multi-system reconciliation in the Databricks Silver Layer. Values correspond to the access control systems integrated with the venue.']
  ticket_source_channel varchar [note: 'Distribution channel through which the scanned ticket was originally acquired. Distinguishes primary market sales, resale marketplace, season ticket packages, group sales, complimentary allocations, will-call, and mobile transfers. Supports channel attribution and revenue analytics. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: primary_market|resale_marketplace|season_package|group_sales|comp|will_call|mobile_transfer — promote to reference product]']
  ticket_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the ticket entitlement scanned at the gate. Reflects the pricing tier and access level: General Admission (GA), Very Important Person (VIP), Standing Room Only (SRO), season ticket, group ticket, complimentary, press credential, or staff pass. Sourced from Archtics inventory classification. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: GA|VIP|SRO|season|group|comp|press|staff — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  token_format varchar [note: 'Format of the entitlement token presented at the gate. Indicates whether the ticket was a 1D barcode, 2D barcode, QR code, NFC chip, RFID tag, or mobile wallet pass. Supports digital ticket delivery channel analytics and technology adoption tracking.']
  turnstile_identifier varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the specific turnstile or lane within a gate where the scan was processed. Enables granular throughput analysis and maintenance tracking at the individual turnstile level.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this gate scan record was last modified in the Databricks Silver Layer (e.g., following an override update or data correction). Supports audit trail and change tracking requirements. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of each access control scan event at venue entry points. Captures entitlement token scanned, gate/turnstile identifier, scan timestamp, scan result (granted, denied, duplicate), operator ID, and device ID. Feeds real-time attendance tracking and post-event access audit. Sourced from Ticketmaster/AXS access control hardware.'
}

Table ticketing.ticket_payment {
  ticket_payment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each ticket payment transaction record in the ticketing domain. Primary key for the ticket_payment data product.']
  ar_invoice_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.ar_invoice. Business justification: Ticket payments settle specific AR invoices; this link enables AR clearing and open-item management. Finance teams need to match ticket_payment records to ar_invoice for cash application workflows. No']
  data_processing_record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.data_processing_record. Business justification: PCI-DSS compliance audits require payment transactions (pci_scope_flag exists on ticket_payment) to be traceable to their documented processing activity record. data_processing_record has pci_dss_appl']
  fiscal_period_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.fiscal_period. Business justification: Ticket payments must be reconciled to fiscal periods for cash accounting and bank reconciliation period-close. Finance teams tie payment receipts to specific fiscal periods; this is a standard AR cash']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Ticket payments must be posted to GL accounts (cash receipts, deferred revenue, tax liability) for ERP reconciliation. Sports entertainment finance teams require direct GL account linkage on payment r']
  payment_method_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fan.payment_method. Business justification: PCI compliance audit and payment method fraud scoring require linking payment transactions to the stored payment method record. Enables payment method performance reporting, chargeback analysis by car']
  payment_run_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.payment_run. Business justification: Season ticket installment payments and batch renewal payments are grouped into payment runs for treasury reconciliation. Linking ticket_payment to payment_run enables batch payment processing reconcil']
  primary_ticket_fan_fan_profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the fan/customer who initiated the payment. Links to the fan master record in Salesforce CRM and Adobe Experience Platform for fan relationship management.']
  season_ticket_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ticketing.season_ticket_account. Business justification: Season ticket holders pay in installments against their season_ticket_account. ticket_payment currently only links to ticket_order, but installment payments are account-level transactions not always t']
  ticket_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent ticket order that this payment is associated with. Links the payment to the originating ticket purchase transaction in Ticketmaster/AXS or Archtics.']
  authorization_code varchar [note: 'Alphanumeric code returned by the card network or payment processor confirming that the payment was authorized. Required for dispute resolution, chargeback management, and financial audit trails. Null for failed or pending transactions.']
  authorized_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time when the payment was authorized by the card network or payment processor. This is the principal business event timestamp for the payment lifecycle. Used for fraud detection time-window analysis and PCI-DSS audit trails.']
  captured_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time when the authorized payment was captured (funds actually charged). Distinct from authorization timestamp; the gap between authorization and capture is monitored for compliance with card network rules. Null for non-captured transactions.']
  card_expiry_month varchar [note: 'The expiration month of the payment card (1–12). Stored in compliance with PCI-DSS to support recurring payment validation and card-on-file management for season ticket holders. Null for non-card payment methods.']
  card_expiry_year varchar [note: 'The four-digit expiration year of the payment card. Stored in compliance with PCI-DSS to support recurring payment validation and card-on-file management for season ticket holders. Null for non-card payment methods.']
  card_network varchar [note: 'The card network or scheme associated with the payment instrument (e.g., Visa, Mastercard, American Express, Discover, UnionPay). Used for interchange fee analysis, network-specific reporting, and chargeback routing.']
  chargeback_status varchar [note: 'Current status of any chargeback dispute associated with this payment transaction. Tracks the chargeback lifecycle from initiation through resolution. Used for fraud management, financial loss reporting, and card network compliance. Default is \'none\' for transactions with no chargeback.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this payment record was first created in the system. Serves as the audit trail creation marker for data lineage, PCI-DSS audit log requirements, and Silver layer ingestion tracking.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the payment transaction (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP, CAD). Supports multi-currency operations across global markets and SAP S/4HANA FI foreign currency revaluation.']
  discount_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Total monetary discount applied to the payment, including promotional codes, group sales discounts, season ticket holder discounts, and sponsor-funded promotions. Used for revenue leakage analysis and promotion ROI reporting.']
  exchange_rate decimal(18,6) [note: 'The foreign exchange rate applied at the time of payment when the transaction currency differs from the organization\'s functional currency (USD). Used for multi-currency financial reporting and SAP S/4HANA FI currency revaluation. Null for transactions in the functional currency.']
  failure_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized decline or failure code returned by the payment processor or card network when a payment is not authorized (e.g., insufficient_funds, card_expired, do_not_honor, fraud_suspected). Used for failed payment analysis, retry logic, and fan communication. Null for successful transactions.']
  failure_reason_description varchar [note: 'Human-readable description of the payment failure reason corresponding to the failure_reason_code. Used by customer service teams in Salesforce CRM for fan outreach and dispute resolution. Null for successful transactions.']
  fraud_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric fraud risk score assigned by the payment processor\'s fraud detection engine or an internal fraud scoring model at the time of payment (0.00–100.00, where higher values indicate greater fraud risk). Used for real-time payment decisioning, fraud trend analysis, and PCI-DSS fraud monitoring requirements.']
  gross_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'The total face value of the ticket order before any discounts, promotions, or adjustments are applied. Represents the base revenue figure used in financial reporting and EBITDA calculations in SAP S/4HANA FI.']
  installment_number varchar [note: 'The sequential installment number for recurring or installment-based payments (e.g., 1 of 4 for a season ticket payment plan). Null for one-time payments. Used for installment plan tracking and revenue recognition scheduling.']
  installment_total_count varchar [note: 'The total number of installments in the payment plan for season ticket or group sales installment arrangements (e.g., 4 for a quarterly payment plan). Null for one-time payments. Used alongside installment_number for plan completion tracking.']
  is_3ds_authenticated boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the payment was authenticated using 3D Secure (3DS) protocol (e.g., Visa Secure, Mastercard Identity Check). True if 3DS authentication was completed. Used for fraud liability shift determination and PCI-DSS compliance reporting.']
  is_recurring boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this payment is part of a recurring payment arrangement, such as a season ticket installment plan or subscription-based fan membership. True for recurring charges. Drives card-on-file compliance and CBA-related payment plan management.']
  masked_card_reference varchar [note: 'Masked representation of the payment card number or digital wallet identifier (e.g., last 4 digits of card, masked wallet token). Stored in PCI-DSS compliant masked format for customer service, receipt display, and dispute identification without exposing full PAN.']
  net_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'The final net amount charged to the fan after applying discounts and adding service fees and taxes (gross_amount - discount_amount + service_fee_amount + tax_amount). This is the actual amount captured from the fan\'s payment instrument. SSOT for revenue recognition.']
  payment_channel varchar [note: 'The interface or touchpoint through which the fan submitted the payment (e.g., web browser, mobile app, venue box office, self-service kiosk, call center, third-party resale platform). Distinct from payment method type; used for channel analytics and fraud detection.']
  payment_method_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the payment instrument used by the fan (e.g., credit card, debit card, digital wallet such as Apple Pay/Google Pay, bank transfer, gift card, cash at box office). Drives PCI-DSS scope determination and payment processing routing.']
  payment_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments associated with the payment transaction, entered by customer service agents or system processes (e.g., manual override justification, special handling instructions for VIP fan payments, dispute context). Used in Salesforce CRM case management.']
  payment_reference_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known, human-readable payment reference number assigned by the payment processor or ticketing platform (e.g., Ticketmaster/AXS transaction reference). Used for customer-facing receipts, dispute resolution, and reconciliation with external systems.']
  payment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the payment transaction. Tracks the workflow from authorization through capture, failure, or refund. Critical for revenue recognition, reconciliation, and PCI-DSS compliance reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: authorized|captured|failed|refunded|voided|pending — promote to reference product if additional states are required]']
  pci_scope_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this payment record falls within the PCI-DSS cardholder data environment (CDE) scope. True for card-based payments subject to PCI-DSS controls. Used for PCI-DSS scope boundary management, audit reporting, and data classification governance.']
  processor_transaction_token varchar [note: 'Tokenized reference assigned by the payment processor to uniquely identify this transaction within the processor\'s system. Replaces raw card data per PCI-DSS tokenization requirements. Used for refund initiation, dispute management, and settlement reconciliation.']
  refund_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Total amount refunded to the fan for this payment transaction, applicable when payment_status is \'refunded\'. May be a partial or full refund. Used for revenue reversal accounting in SAP S/4HANA FI and fan satisfaction tracking.']
  refund_reason varchar [note: 'Categorized reason for the refund when payment_status is \'refunded\'. Supports refund trend analysis, event cancellation financial impact reporting, and fraud detection. Null for non-refunded transactions.']
  refunded_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time when the refund was processed and submitted to the payment processor. Null for non-refunded transactions. Used for refund SLA monitoring and fan experience reporting.']
  service_fee_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Ticketing service and convenience fees charged to the fan on top of the ticket face value (e.g., Ticketmaster facility charge, order processing fee). Reported separately for revenue transparency and regulatory compliance.']
  settlement_date date [note: 'The date on which the payment was settled by the acquiring bank and funds were transferred to the organization\'s account. Critical for cash flow reporting, SAP S/4HANA FI bank reconciliation, and revenue recognition timing.']
  settlement_reference varchar [note: 'The batch settlement reference number assigned by the payment processor or acquiring bank when the transaction is included in a settlement batch. Used for bank reconciliation, SAP S/4HANA FI bank statement matching, and financial close processes.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Identifies the originating operational system that generated this payment record (e.g., Ticketmaster, AXS, Archtics box office, resale marketplace). Used for data lineage tracking, system reconciliation, and Silver layer provenance in the Databricks Lakehouse.']
  tax_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Total tax amount applied to the ticket payment, including applicable sales tax, VAT, or entertainment tax based on the venue jurisdiction. Required for tax remittance reporting and SAP S/4HANA FI tax accounting.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this payment record was last modified (e.g., status change from authorized to captured, or refund applied). Supports change data capture, audit trails, and Silver layer incremental processing.']
  wallet_provider varchar [note: 'Name of the digital wallet provider used for payment when payment_method_type is digital_wallet (e.g., Apple Pay, Google Pay, PayPal, Venmo). Null for non-wallet payment methods. Supports DTC fan engagement analytics and wallet adoption reporting.']

  Note: 'PCI-DSS compliant payment transaction record associated with a ticket order. Captures payment method type, masked card/wallet reference, payment processor token, authorization code, amount charged, currency, payment status (authorized, captured, failed, refunded), and settlement reference. SSOT for ticketing payment financial records.'
}

Table ticketing.refund {
  refund_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated surrogate identifier for the ticket refund record. Primary key for the refund data product in the ticketing domain Silver layer.']
  access_entitlement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ticketing.access_entitlement. Business justification: When a refund is processed, the associated access_entitlement must be voided or invalidated. Linking refund → access_entitlement (access_entitlement_id) enables the system to directly identify and voi']
  ar_invoice_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.ar_invoice. Business justification: Refunds reduce AR balances and must be applied against specific AR invoices for AR credit memo processing and open-item clearing. Standard AR workflow in sports entertainment requires refund-to-invoic']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Refund costs are charged back to cost centers for departmental P&L accountability in sports entertainment operations. The existing cost_center_code is denormalized; a proper FK enables cost center-lev']
  fiscal_period_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.fiscal_period. Business justification: Refunds must be posted to the correct fiscal period for accurate P&L reporting and prior-period adjustment tracking. Finance teams reconcile refund liabilities by fiscal period during period-close; th']
  franchise_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to league.franchise. Business justification: Franchise-level refund liability tracking is a named financial reporting process — postponed or cancelled home games trigger refund obligations charged to the franchise. Direct franchise attribution o']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Refunds must be posted to specific GL accounts (revenue reversal, refund liability) for ERP reconciliation. The existing gl_account_code plain attribute is a denormalized reference; replacing it with ']
  investigation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.investigation. Business justification: Compliance-driven refunds (fraud investigations, regulatory sanctions, event cancellations under investigation) require linking refund records to the triggering investigation. refund has is_fraud_rela']
  refund_event_fixture_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the sports or entertainment event associated with the refunded ticket. Enables event-level refund aggregation for cancellation and postponement analysis.']
  refund_fan_fan_profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the fan (customer) account requesting or receiving the refund. Supports CRM-linked fan engagement tracking and PCI-DSS audit trail for cardholder refund activity.']
  refund_fan_profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the fan (customer) account requesting or receiving the refund. Supports CRM-linked fan engagement tracking and PCI-DSS audit trail for cardholder refund activity.']
  refund_fixture_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the sports or entertainment event associated with the refunded ticket. Enables event-level refund aggregation for cancellation and postponement analysis.']
  refund_ticket_inventory_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific ticket being refunded. A single order may contain multiple tickets; this field identifies the individual ticket entitlement subject to the refund.']
  refund_ticket_ticket_inventory_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific ticket being refunded. A single order may contain multiple tickets; this field identifies the individual ticket entitlement subject to the refund.']
  security_incident_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to security.security_incident. Business justification: Refunds are operationally triggered by security incidents — event suspension due to emergency activation, fan ejection leading to ticket refund, or venue evacuation. Linking refund to security_inciden']
  ticket_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the originating ticket order from which this refund was initiated. Links the refund back to the original purchase transaction in Ticketmaster/AXS.']
  ticket_order_line_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ticketing.ticket_order_line. Business justification: A refund is frequently issued at the line-item level (e.g., refunding a single seat within a multi-seat order). refund already has ticket_order_id (existing) for order-level traceability, but lacks a ']
  ticket_payment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ticketing.ticket_payment. Business justification: A refund must trace back to the original payment transaction for PCI-DSS compliance, financial reconciliation, and chargeback management. refund.payment_transaction_ref is a STRING external processor ']
  approval_level varchar [note: 'The authorization tier at which the refund was approved. AUTO indicates system-triggered approval (e.g., event cancellation policy); AGENT, SUPERVISOR, and FINANCE indicate escalating human approval tiers based on refund amount thresholds.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the refund request was approved by an authorized agent or automated rule engine. Null if the refund has not yet been approved or was rejected. Supports approval SLA reporting.']
  completed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the refund was confirmed as successfully received by the fan\'s payment instrument or wallet. Represents the final settled state of the refund lifecycle.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the refund record was first created in the system of record (Ticketmaster/AXS). Audit trail field for data lineage and PCI-DSS compliance logging.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the refund transaction (e.g., USD, GBP, EUR). Supports multi-currency operations across global markets and SAP FI foreign currency revaluation.']
  gross_refund_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'The total face value of the ticket(s) being refunded before any deductions such as service fees or processing charges. Represents the base monetary amount subject to reversal in SAP FI.']
  is_event_initiated boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the refund was triggered by an event-level action (cancellation, postponement, force majeure) rather than a fan-initiated request. Drives bulk refund processing workflows and league governance reporting.']
  is_fraud_related boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the refund was initiated due to a confirmed or suspected fraudulent transaction. When true, triggers PCI-DSS fraud incident reporting and Salesforce CRM fraud case creation.']
  net_refund_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'The actual monetary amount returned to the fan after deducting any non-refundable fees or processing charges from the gross refund amount. This is the amount posted to the fan\'s payment instrument or credit wallet.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Unstructured operational notes added by customer service agents or automated systems during the refund lifecycle. May include escalation context, fan communication history, or special handling instructions. Subject to GDPR data minimisation principles.']
  original_payment_method varchar [note: 'The payment instrument used in the original ticket purchase transaction. Determines eligibility for ORIGINAL_PAYMENT refund routing and informs PCI-DSS cardholder data handling requirements.']
  payment_transaction_ref varchar [note: 'The external payment processor transaction reference number for the refund settlement (e.g., Stripe charge ID, Adyen PSP reference). Used for reconciliation between Ticketmaster/AXS and the payment gateway. Never stores raw card data.']
  processed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the refund was submitted to the payment processor or credit wallet system for execution. Distinct from approved_timestamp; marks the point of financial commitment.']
  reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code classifying the business reason for the refund. EVENT_CANCELLED and EVENT_POSTPONED are league/venue-initiated; FAN_REQUEST is voluntary; FRAUD triggers PCI-DSS fraud reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EVENT_CANCELLED|EVENT_POSTPONED|FAN_REQUEST|FRAUD|DUPLICATE_PURCHASE|VENUE_ISSUE|WEATHER — promote to reference product]']
  reason_description varchar [note: 'Free-text narrative providing additional context for the refund reason beyond the standardized reason code. Captured by customer service agents or automated system messages in Salesforce CRM.']
  refund_method varchar [note: 'The channel or instrument through which the refund value is returned to the fan. ORIGINAL_PAYMENT reverses to the card/account used at purchase; CREDIT_WALLET posts to the fan\'s digital wallet; VOUCHER issues a future-use ticket credit. Governs SAP FI posting type.']
  refund_number varchar [note: 'Externally visible, human-readable refund reference number issued to the fan and used in customer service communications. Formatted as RFD- followed by a 10-digit sequence. Sourced from Ticketmaster/AXS refund workflow.']
  refund_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the refund request within the approval and processing workflow. Drives operational queues in Ticketmaster/AXS and SAP FI posting logic.']
  refund_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the refund covers the full ticket face value (FULL), a portion of the ticket value (PARTIAL), or is issued as a credit/voucher rather than a monetary return (CREDIT). Drives revenue recognition reversal logic in SAP CO.']
  rejection_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text explanation provided when a refund request is rejected. Populated only when refund_status is \'rejected\'. Required for customer service follow-up and regulatory dispute resolution under GDPR Article 12.']
  requested_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the fan or system initiated the refund request. Represents the principal business event time for the refund lifecycle. Used for SLA measurement and PCI-DSS audit trail.']
  resale_refund_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the refund relates to a ticket purchased through the secondary/resale marketplace rather than the primary box office. Resale refunds may follow different policy rules and fee structures.']
  service_fee_refund_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'The portion of the refund attributable to service fees originally charged on the ticket purchase. May be zero if service fees are non-refundable per the event\'s refund policy. Tracked separately for revenue accounting in SAP CO.']
  sla_breach_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the refund processing exceeded the defined SLA target hours. Set to true when the elapsed time between approved_timestamp and processed_timestamp exceeds sla_target_hours. Drives escalation workflows.']
  sla_target_hours varchar [note: 'The contracted or policy-defined maximum number of hours within which the refund must be processed from the point of approval. Used for SLA compliance monitoring and customer service KPI reporting.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The operational system of record from which the refund record originated. Supports data lineage tracking in the Databricks Silver layer and reconciliation across Ticketmaster/AXS, Archtics, and SAP S/4HANA.']
  source_system_refund_code varchar [note: 'The native refund identifier as recorded in the originating operational system (e.g., Ticketmaster refund transaction ID, SAP FI document number). Enables cross-system reconciliation and audit trail back to the system of record.']
  tax_refund_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'The tax component being reversed as part of the refund. Required for VAT/GST compliance reporting in applicable jurisdictions and SAP FI tax reversal postings.']
