  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the refund record was last modified in the system of record. Tracks state transitions through the approval workflow for audit and reconciliation purposes.']
  voucher_code varchar [note: 'The unique alphanumeric voucher code issued to the fan when refund_method is VOUCHER. Redeemable against future ticket purchases in Ticketmaster/AXS. Null for non-voucher refund methods.']
  voucher_expiry_date date [note: 'The date on which the issued refund voucher expires and can no longer be redeemed. Applicable only when refund_method is VOUCHER. Null for monetary refund methods.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of a ticket refund or credit issued to a fan. Captures originating order reference, refund reason (event cancellation, postponement, fan request, fraud), refund amount, refund method (original payment, credit wallet, voucher), processing status, and approval workflow state. Supports PCI-DSS refund audit requirements.'
}

Table ticketing.hold_allocation {
  hold_allocation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each hold allocation record in the ticketing inventory management system. Primary key for the hold_allocation data product.']
  activation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sponsorship.sponsorship_activation. Business justification: Activation-level ticket hold management: ticket holds are created for specific sponsorship activations (e.g., VIP experience, hospitality suite). Linking hold_allocation to the activation enables acti']
  capacity_config_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to venue.capacity_config. Business justification: Hold quantities are constrained by the active capacity_config\'s sellable capacity and kill seat counts. Hold management and manifest reconciliation require knowing which capacity_config governs the ho']
  club_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fan.fan_club. Business justification: Official fan club ticket allocation management — leagues and venues allocate seat holds to registered fan clubs for away fixtures and cup finals. Linking hold_allocation to fan_club enables allocation']
  contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to athlete.contract. Business justification: CBA Contract Comp Ticket Fulfillment: Player contracts specify exact comp ticket quantities, tiers, and game types. hold_allocation.contract_reference is a denormalized text field; a proper FK to athl']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Held inventory (sponsor holds, comp holds, press holds) is charged to cost centers for budget accountability. The existing cost_center_code is denormalized; a proper FK enables cost center-level hold ']
  deal_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sponsorship.deal. Business justification: Sponsorship deal fulfillment: seat holds for sponsors are created per specific deal obligation (e.g., Deal X entitles Sponsor Y to 50 seats per home game). hold_allocation has sponsor_id but no deal_i']
  franchise_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to league.franchise. Business justification: Franchises control comp seat allocations and sponsor holds for their home games. Franchise-level hold inventory management and comp seat reporting is a standard operational process. No franchise_id cu']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Comp holds and sponsor holds carry face value requiring GL account assignment for comp ticket expense accruals and sponsor benefit valuation reporting. Finance teams post comp ticket expense to specif']
  group_sale_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ticketing.group_sale. Business justification: Group sales require inventory holds before the sale is finalized (deposit-based holds). hold_allocation supports group sale holds but has no FK to group_sale. Adding group_sale_id → group_sale.group_s']
  league_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to league.league_schedule. Business justification: Hold release timing and presale window enforcement are governed by league_schedule.ticket_on_sale_date and scheduling_status. Hold allocation managers need the scheduled game record to set presale_win']
  nil_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to athlete.nil_agreement. Business justification: NIL agreements frequently include in-kind ticket/hospitality allotments as compensation. Linking hold allocations directly to the NIL agreement (beyond the generic deal_id) enables NIL compliance trac']
  price_tier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ticketing.price_tier. Business justification: hold_allocation.price_tier (STRING) denormalizes the pricing tier associated with the held inventory block. Replacing with price_tier_id FK normalizes this to the authoritative price_tier catalog, ena']
  primary_hold_event_fixture_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the event for which this hold allocation applies. Links the hold block to the specific sporting or entertainment event in the event master.']
  roster_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to athlete.roster. Business justification: CBA Comp Ticket Hold Allocation: Active roster status directly governs which players receive comp ticket holds each game. Linking hold_allocation to roster enables automated CBA compliance — holds are']
  season_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the league season or competition season associated with this hold allocation. Enables season-level aggregation of hold inventory for season ticket package management and league governance reporting.']
  season_ticket_package_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ticketing.season_ticket_package. Business justification: hold_allocation is used to reserve inventory blocks for season ticket holders (STH holds, renewal holds). Without a FK to season_ticket_package, the hold cannot be structurally associated with the pac']
  seating_section_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to venue.seating_section. Business justification: Sponsor hold fulfillment, comp hold management, and presale hold capacity tracking require linking hold allocations to the canonical seating_section for section-level capacity enforcement and ADA hold']
  segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fan.segment. Business justification: Segment-based presale hold management requires linking ticket holds to the fan segment they are reserved for (e.g., loyalty member presale, VIP segment holds). The existing `eligibility_criteria` plai']
  sponsor_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the sponsorship partner associated with this hold allocation when hold_type is sponsor_comp. Null for non-sponsor holds. Links to the sponsorship master for contractual comp entitlement tracking.']
  ticket_inventory_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ticketing.ticket_inventory. Business justification: hold_allocation places blocks on specific ticket inventory units. Without a FK to ticket_inventory, the hold cannot be structurally linked to the specific seats being held — only loose string fields (']
  venue_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the venue where the event is held. Determines the seating map and inventory pool from which the hold block is drawn.']
  access_code varchar [note: 'Alphanumeric code required by eligible buyers to access a presale window associated with this hold allocation. Confidential as it controls access to restricted inventory. Null for non-presale hold types.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this hold allocation was formally approved by the designated authority. Supports SOX audit trail requirements for complimentary and high-value hold approvals.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the face value and any monetary fields on this hold allocation (e.g., USD, GBP, EUR). Supports multi-currency operations across global markets.']
  eligibility_criteria varchar [note: 'Free-text or structured description of the rules governing who is eligible to access this hold allocation (e.g., \'Season ticket holders only\', \'Fan club members with active membership\', \'Sponsor guest list\'). Supports access control enforcement in Ticketmaster/AXS.']
  face_value decimal(12,2) [note: 'Printed face value per ticket in the held block, expressed in the event\'s operating currency. Used for comp valuation, sponsor entitlement reporting, and revenue recognition under SAP S/4HANA SD.']
  hold_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric code assigned to this hold allocation block, used by Ticketmaster/AXS and Archtics to reference the hold in operational workflows and reporting. Serves as the business-facing identifier for the hold.']
  hold_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this hold allocation was first placed in the ticketing system. Represents the principal business event timestamp — the moment inventory was removed from general availability. Sourced from Ticketmaster/AXS or Archtics system of record.']
  hold_expiry_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time at which this hold allocation automatically expires and unreleased inventory is returned to general on-sale pool. Critical for tiered on-sale sequencing and inventory management SLA enforcement.']
  hold_owner varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the business unit, partner, or individual responsible for managing this hold allocation (e.g., sponsor name, artist management agency, media partner, venue operations team). Operational accountability field used in Archtics hold management workflows.']
  hold_reason varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the business justification for placing this hold allocation (e.g., \'Platinum sponsor contractual comp entitlement Q3\', \'Artist rider requirement - 50 house seats\', \'Media credential access - broadcast partners\'). Supports audit and compliance review.']
  hold_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the hold allocation. Tracks whether the hold is active, has been fully or partially released back to general inventory, has expired past its hold expiry timestamp, or has been cancelled by the hold owner.']
  hold_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the purpose for which the inventory block is held. Drives downstream release sequencing and eligibility rules. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: sponsor_comp|artist_athlete|media|ada|operational|presale|group_sales — promote to reference product]']
  is_ada_hold boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this hold allocation is designated for Americans with Disabilities Act (ADA) accessible seating. ADA holds are subject to specific regulatory requirements for accessible seat inventory management.']
  is_comp boolean [note: 'Indicates whether tickets in this hold allocation are designated as complimentary (zero face value distribution). Comp holds require separate revenue recognition treatment under SAP S/4HANA FI/CO and SOX financial controls.']
  is_dynamic_pricing_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether tickets in this hold allocation are subject to dynamic pricing adjustments when released to general inventory. False for comp, ADA, and fixed-price sponsor holds.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this hold allocation record was last updated in the source system. Used for incremental data pipeline processing and audit trail in the Databricks Silver layer.']
  max_tickets_per_buyer varchar [note: 'Maximum number of tickets a single eligible buyer may purchase from this hold allocation during the presale or comp distribution window. Enforces per-buyer limits to prevent bulk purchasing and ensure equitable access.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text operational notes entered by the hold manager regarding special instructions, distribution logistics, or exceptions for this hold allocation. Supports day-of-event operations and hold management workflows.']
  presale_window_end timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the presale window closes for this hold allocation. Unreleased inventory after this timestamp is subject to release rules defined by hold_expiry_timestamp or manual release.']
  presale_window_start timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the presale window opens for this hold allocation, allowing eligible buyers to purchase before general public on-sale. Applicable when hold_type is presale. Drives tiered on-sale sequencing.']
  public_onsale_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the general public on-sale for the event. Used to calculate the lead time between hold creation and public availability, and to enforce release deadlines for presale and comp holds.']
  quantity_consumed varchar [note: 'Number of tickets from this hold allocation that have been issued, distributed, or sold (e.g., comps distributed to sponsor, presale tickets sold). Tracks actual utilization of the hold block.']
  quantity_held varchar [note: 'Total number of tickets/seats placed on hold under this allocation record. Represents the gross inventory block size at the time the hold was created.']
  quantity_released varchar [note: 'Number of tickets from this hold allocation that have been released back to general on-sale inventory. Enables tracking of partial releases and remaining hold balance.']
  release_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Actual date and time when the hold was released (fully or partially) back to general inventory or to a presale window. Null if the hold has not yet been released. Supports audit trail for inventory lifecycle.']
  release_to_pool varchar [note: 'Designates the inventory pool to which unreleased tickets from this hold will be returned upon expiry or manual release. Controls tiered on-sale sequencing and resale marketplace eligibility.']

  Note: 'Record of ticket inventory blocks placed on hold for specific purposes — sponsor complimentary holds, artist/athlete holds, media holds, ADA holds, operational holds, pre-sale windows (with access codes and eligibility criteria), and comp allocations. Captures hold type, quantity, seat block, hold owner, hold expiry, release status, presale time windows, and access eligibility rules. Manages inventory allocation before public on-sale and enables tiered on-sale sequencing.'
}

Table ticketing.ticket_transfer {
  ticket_transfer_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each peer-to-peer ticket transfer record. Primary key for the ticket_transfer data product.']
  access_entitlement_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the access entitlement record associated with the ticket being transferred. The entitlement is voided for the sender and re-issued to the recipient upon successful transfer.']
  primary_prior_transfer_ticket_transfer_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the immediately preceding transfer record for this ticket, enabling chain-of-custody tracking across multiple transfers. Null for the first transfer of a ticket.']
  primary_ticket_event_fixture_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the event for which the ticket is valid. Used for event-level transfer analytics, capacity management, and access control validation.']
  primary_ticket_fan_profile_id varchar [note: 'Fan account identifier of the ticket holder initiating the transfer (the sender). Used for CRM tracking, season ticket holder (STH) loyalty attribution, and transfer eligibility validation.']
  primary_ticket_inventory_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific ticket inventory record being transferred between fan accounts. Links to the originating ticket entitlement.']
  season_ticket_package_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the season ticket package associated with the transferred ticket, if applicable. Used to enforce STH forward ticket rules, loyalty point attribution, and package-level transfer limits.']
  tertiary_ticket_recipient_fan_fan_profile_id varchar [note: 'Fan account identifier of the fan receiving the transferred ticket. Populated upon successful transfer acceptance. Null if the recipient has not yet accepted or does not have an existing fan account.']
  ticket_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the original ticket order from which the transferred ticket was purchased. Maintains the financial and transactional lineage of the ticket.']
  ticket_order_line_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ticketing.ticket_order_line. Business justification: A ticket transfer moves a specific ticket unit (order line item) from one fan to another. ticket_transfer already has order_id → ticket_order (header level) and ticket_inventory_id (seat level), but t']
  venue_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the venue where the event associated with the transferred ticket will take place. Used for venue-level transfer analytics, access zone validation, and ADA accommodation routing.']
  accepted_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the recipient accepted the ticket transfer. Null if the transfer has not yet been accepted. Triggers entitlement re-issuance to the recipient fan account.']
  cancellation_reason varchar [note: 'Reason code for why the transfer was cancelled, declined, or revoked. Populated only when transfer_status is \'cancelled\', \'declined\', or \'revoked\'. Used for operational reporting and fraud analytics.']
  completed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the transfer reached a terminal state (accepted, declined, cancelled, expired, or revoked). Used for SLA measurement and transfer lifecycle reporting.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this transfer record was first created in the data platform (Silver layer ingestion). Audit field for data lineage and record provenance.']
  expiry_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time after which the transfer invitation is no longer valid for acceptance. If the recipient does not accept before this timestamp, the transfer status transitions to \'expired\' and the entitlement reverts to the sender.']
  fraud_risk_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this transfer was flagged by the fraud detection system as potentially suspicious (e.g., rapid successive transfers, transfers to unknown accounts, high-value event tickets). Triggers manual review workflow.']
  gdpr_consent_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the sender fan account had valid GDPR consent on file at the time of transfer initiation, permitting the sharing of recipient contact data (email/phone) with the platform for transfer delivery. Required for EU market compliance.']
  initiated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the sender initiated the ticket transfer request. This is the principal business event timestamp for the transfer lifecycle. Stored in ISO 8601 format with timezone offset.']
  is_ada_accessible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the transferred ticket is for an Americans with Disabilities Act (ADA) accessible seat. Ensures that ADA accommodations are preserved and communicated to the recipient during the transfer process.']
  is_return_transfer boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this transfer represents a ticket being returned to the original sender after a prior outbound transfer. Supports STH account reconciliation and loyalty point reversal workflows.']
  is_transferable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the ticket was eligible for peer-to-peer transfer at the time the transfer was initiated, based on price tier rules, season ticket package terms, and event-level transfer restrictions.']
  new_entitlement_token varchar [note: 'The new digital access token (barcode, QR code, or NFC token) issued to the recipient fan account upon successful transfer acceptance. Replaces the original entitlement token for gate access validation.']
  original_entitlement_token varchar [note: 'The original digital access token held by the sender prior to transfer. Voided upon successful transfer acceptance to prevent duplicate access. Retained for audit and fraud investigation purposes.']
  recipient_display_name varchar [note: 'Display name or full name of the recipient as entered by the sender or resolved from the recipient fan account. Used for sender confirmation UI and customer service. Classified as restricted PII per GDPR Article 4.']
  recipient_email varchar [note: 'Email address used to deliver the transfer invitation to the recipient. May belong to a fan without an existing account, enabling new fan account creation upon acceptance. Classified as restricted PII per GDPR Article 4 and CCPA.']
  recipient_phone varchar [note: 'Mobile phone number used to deliver the transfer invitation via SMS when transfer_method is \'sms\'. Classified as restricted PII per GDPR Article 4 and CCPA.']
  recipient_terms_accepted_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the recipient explicitly accepted the transfer terms and conditions upon claiming the ticket. Required for entitlement issuance and compliance with consumer protection regulations.']
  seat_number varchar [note: 'Individual seat number within the row for the transferred ticket. Denormalized from ticket inventory for operational convenience and recipient confirmation.']
  seat_row varchar [note: 'Row label of the seat associated with the transferred ticket within the section. Denormalized from ticket inventory for operational convenience.']
  seat_section varchar [note: 'Section label of the seat associated with the transferred ticket, as defined in the venue seating manifest. Denormalized from ticket inventory for operational convenience and access control validation.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the operational system of record from which this transfer record was ingested. Supports multi-platform environments where both Ticketmaster and AXS may be in use across different venues or events.']
  source_system_transfer_code varchar [note: 'Native transfer identifier from the originating operational system (Ticketmaster or AXS). Used for cross-system reconciliation, support case lookup, and data lineage tracing between the Silver layer and the source system of record.']
  terms_accepted_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the sender explicitly accepted the transfer terms and conditions at the time of initiating the transfer. Required for compliance with Ticketmaster/AXS transfer policy and consumer protection regulations.']
  terms_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the transfer terms and conditions accepted by the sender at the time of transfer initiation. Enables legal and compliance teams to audit which version of terms governed each transfer.']
  token_format varchar [note: 'Format of the digital access token used for the transferred ticket. Determines the gate scanning technology required for admission. \'barcode\' — 1D/2D barcode; \'qr_code\' — QR code; \'nfc\' — Near Field Communication tap; \'mobile_id\' — rotating mobile ticket ID.']
  transfer_count varchar [note: 'Cumulative number of times this specific ticket has been transferred between fan accounts, including the current transfer. Used to enforce maximum transfer limits per ticket and detect potential scalping or policy violations.']
  transfer_direction varchar [note: 'Indicates the directional nature of the transfer relative to the originating account. \'outbound\' — sender is giving away the ticket; \'inbound\' — recipient is receiving; \'return\' — ticket returned to original holder after a prior transfer.']
  transfer_method varchar [note: 'Channel or mechanism used to deliver the transfer invitation to the recipient. \'mobile_app\' — in-app transfer via Ticketmaster/AXS mobile; \'email\' — transfer link sent via email; \'sms\' — transfer link sent via SMS; \'platform_link\' — shareable URL generated for transfer.']
  transfer_reference_number varchar [note: 'Externally-visible alphanumeric reference number assigned to the transfer transaction, used for fan-facing communication, customer service lookup, and audit trail. Sourced from Ticketmaster/AXS transfer workflow.']
  transfer_restriction_reason varchar [note: 'Reason code explaining why a transfer was restricted or blocked. \'none\' — no restriction; \'price_tier_restricted\' — price tier does not permit transfer; \'event_restricted\' — event-level transfer lockout; \'package_restricted\' — season ticket package terms prohibit transfer; \'compliance_hold\' — regulatory or compliance hold; \'fraud_hold\' — fraud risk flag applied.']
  transfer_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the ticket transfer. \'pending\' — transfer initiated, awaiting recipient acceptance; \'accepted\' — recipient accepted and entitlement re-issued; \'declined\' — recipient declined; \'cancelled\' — sender cancelled before acceptance; \'expired\' — acceptance window elapsed; \'revoked\' — transfer voided by operator or compliance action.']
  transfer_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the transfer by business context. \'standard\' — ad hoc peer-to-peer transfer; \'season_ticket_forward\' — STH forwarding a game ticket; \'group_forward\' — group sale ticket forwarded to individual attendee; \'comp_forward\' — complimentary ticket forwarded by recipient.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this transfer record was last modified in the data platform. Used for incremental data processing, change detection, and audit trail maintenance.']

  Note: 'Peer-to-peer ticket transfer records between fan accounts — capturing sender, recipient, transfer method (mobile, email), transfer timestamp, and terms acceptance.'
}

Table ticketing.price_zone {
  price_zone_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for price_zone']
  access_zone_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to security.access_zone. Business justification: Price zone to access zone configuration mapping: venue operations teams configure price zones (GA, VIP, Club) in direct correspondence with security access zones. This FK enables access control setup ']
  parent_price_zone_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on price_zone (parent_price_zone_id)']
  venue_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the venue where this price zone is located.']
  accessible_seating boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this zone includes ADA-compliant accessible seating for guests with disabilities.']
  amenities_included varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of amenities included with tickets in this zone (e.g., \'VIP Lounge Access\', \'Complimentary Beverage\', \'Meet and Greet\').']
  base_price decimal(10,2) [note: 'Standard base price for tickets in this zone before dynamic pricing adjustments, fees, or discounts. Denominated in USD.']
  capacity varchar [note: 'Maximum number of tickets/seats available in this price zone. Used for inventory allocation and sell-through analytics.']
  companion_seats_available boolean [note: 'Indicates whether companion seats adjacent to accessible seating are available in this zone.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this price zone record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for pricing (e.g., USD, CAD, GBP).']
  display_order varchar [note: 'Numeric sort order for displaying price zones in customer-facing interfaces and reports. Lower numbers appear first.']
  dynamic_pricing_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether dynamic pricing algorithms are applied to this zone based on demand, event type, and market conditions.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when this price zone configuration expires or is superseded. Null for open-ended zones.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when this price zone configuration becomes active for ticket sales.']
  external_zone_code varchar [note: 'Price zone identifier in the external ticketing system (Ticketmaster, AXS, Archtics). Used for integration and reconciliation.']
  group_sales_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this zone is available for group sales packages (typically 10+ tickets).']
  minimum_group_size varchar [note: 'Minimum number of tickets required for group sales eligibility in this zone. Null if group sales not applicable.']
  mobile_entry_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether mobile/digital ticket delivery and entry is available for this zone.']
  price_zone_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the price zone including location, view characteristics, and special features. Used for customer-facing marketing.']
  price_zone_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the price zone. Controls availability for ticket sales and inventory allocation.']
  print_at_home_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether print-at-home ticket delivery is available for this zone.']
  resale_allowed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether tickets from this zone can be resold on the secondary marketplace.']
  row_range_end varchar [note: 'Ending row identifier for this price zone (e.g., \'Z\', \'50\'). Used for seat mapping and access control.']
  row_range_start varchar [note: 'Starting row identifier for this price zone (e.g., \'A\', \'1\'). Used for seat mapping and access control.']
  season_ticket_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this zone is available for season ticket packages.']
  section_identifier varchar [note: 'Physical section identifier within the venue (e.g., \'101\', \'A\', \'Mezzanine\'). Maps to venue seating chart.']
  ticketing_system_code varchar [note: 'External ticketing platform that manages inventory for this zone.']
  tier varchar [note: 'Pricing tier classification for revenue segmentation and dynamic pricing models.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this price zone record was last modified.']
  view_quality_rating varchar [note: 'Subjective rating of sightline quality from this zone. Influences pricing and customer satisfaction.']
  will_call_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether will-call pickup is available for tickets in this zone.']
  zone_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier code for the price zone used in ticketing systems and venue maps. Externally-known unique code.']
  zone_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the price zone (e.g., \'Floor Section A\', \'Upper Deck\', \'VIP Lounge\').']
  zone_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the price zone by seating/access type. Determines ticketing rules and access control policies.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for price_zone. Referenced by price_zone_id.'
}

Table broadcast.media_rights_deal {
  media_rights_deal_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the media rights deal record in the broadcast domain. Primary key for the media_rights_deal data product.']
  compliance_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.obligation. Business justification: Media rights deals create specific compliance obligations (anti-piracy, content quotas, watershed rules, data privacy requirements per territory). Compliance teams must map each rights deal to its gov']
  franchise_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to league.franchise. Business justification: Franchise-level media rights deals (RSN local broadcast agreements) are a named business construct in sports. Local market rights are negotiated per franchise, and rights compliance reporting requires']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Rights fee revenue from media deals must post to a specific GL account (broadcast rights revenue) for P&L reporting and IFRS 15 compliance. Finance controllers map each deal to its revenue GL account ']
  league_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the sports league or governing body whose content is covered by this media rights deal (e.g., NFL, NBA, FIFA, MLS). Links to the league master data product.']
  licensee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to broadcast.licensee. Business justification: Media rights deals grant broadcast rights to licensees (the parties receiving the license). Every media rights deal must identify the licensee receiving the rights. This is essential for contract mana']
  rights_holder_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to broadcast.rights_holder. Business justification: Media rights deals are negotiated with rights holders who own the content rights being licensed. Every media rights deal must identify the rights holder granting the rights. This is a foundational rel']
  season_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to league.season. Business justification: Season-scoped rights deal management and renewal scheduling require knowing which season a deal is anchored to. Rights deals often have season-specific terms, and rights expiry/renewal workflows are t']
  annual_rights_fee_usd decimal(18,2) [note: 'Annual rights fee payable by the licensee in US Dollars. Used for ARR (Annual Recurring Revenue) tracking, financial planning in Workday Adaptive Planning, and year-over-year revenue comparison.']
  blackout_rules_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether geographic or platform-based blackout rules apply to content distributed under this deal. When true, the CDN and streaming platform must enforce territory-based content restrictions.']
  blackout_territory_codes varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country or market codes where content blackout restrictions apply under this deal. Used by CDN geo-restriction and OTT/IPTV platform access control engines.']
  cdn_provider_name varchar [note: 'Name of the CDN provider responsible for delivering digital content under this deal (e.g., Akamai, Fastly, Cloudflare). Relevant for OTT/IPTV and DTC streaming deals. Supports SLA monitoring and CDN cost allocation.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the deal\'s contractual currency (e.g., USD, GBP, EUR). Required for multi-currency rights deals in international markets.']
  dalet_contract_ref varchar [note: 'External reference identifier from Dalet Galaxy Media Asset Management system linking this deal to the corresponding rights contract object in the broadcast workflow system. Supports data lineage from source system.']
  data_privacy_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the deal includes contractual data privacy compliance obligations (GDPR, CCPA) governing fan data collected through the licensee\'s broadcast and streaming platforms.']
  deal_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this media rights deal record was first created in the system. Audit trail field for data governance and SOX compliance. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  deal_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable descriptive name of the media rights deal (e.g., \'NFL Sunday Ticket RSN Agreement FY2025\'). Used for identification in reporting and dashboards.']
  deal_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric contract reference number assigned by the rights management system (Dalet Galaxy or Salesforce CRM) and used in all correspondence with broadcast partners. Serves as the BUSINESS_IDENTIFIER for this agreement.']
  deal_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the media rights deal. Drives rights enforcement and blackout rule activation. Values: draft, pending_approval, active, suspended, expired, terminated.']
  deal_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the deal by distribution channel or platform type. Values: linear_tv (traditional broadcast), rsn (Regional Sports Network), ott (Over-The-Top Streaming), iptv (Internet Protocol Television), ppv (Pay-Per-View), dtc (Direct-To-Consumer). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: linear_tv|rsn|ott|iptv|ppv|dtc — promote to reference product if additional types emerge]']
  deal_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this media rights deal record was most recently modified. Supports change data capture (CDC) in the Databricks Silver Layer and audit trail requirements. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  deal_value_usd decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total gross contract value of the media rights deal in US Dollars over the full contract term. Represents the headline financial commitment. Used for EBITDA reporting and ARR calculations.']
  digital_streaming_rights_included boolean [note: 'Indicates whether OTT/IPTV digital streaming rights are included in this deal, permitting distribution via internet-based platforms (e.g., DTC apps, OTT services).']
  dispute_resolution_mechanism varchar [note: 'Contractually agreed mechanism for resolving disputes arising from this deal. cas_arbitration refers to the Court of Arbitration for Sport, applicable for international sports rights disputes.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the media rights deal becomes legally binding and rights entitlements are enforceable. Aligns with contract commencement date in Salesforce CRM.']
  exclusivity_type varchar [note: 'Defines whether the rights granted are exclusive to the licensee within the territory, non-exclusive (shared with other licensees), first-window (first broadcast rights before others), or co-exclusive (shared exclusivity with a limited number of partners).']
  execution_date date [note: 'The date on which all parties formally signed and executed the media rights agreement. Distinct from effective_date (when rights begin) and deal_created_timestamp (when the record was created in the system).']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the media rights deal expires and all rights entitlements cease. Nullable for perpetual or open-ended agreements. Critical for rights window enforcement and renewal planning.']
  governing_law_country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the jurisdiction whose laws govern this media rights agreement (e.g., USA, GBR, CHE). Determines applicable regulatory frameworks and dispute resolution venue.']
  highlights_rights_included boolean [note: 'Indicates whether rights to produce and distribute short-form highlight clips are included in this deal. Relevant for social media, news broadcasts, and digital platform distribution.']
  live_rights_included boolean [note: 'Indicates whether live broadcast rights for events are included in this deal. Drives live feed distribution scheduling and OB (Outside Broadcast) resource allocation.']
  max_events_per_season varchar [note: 'Maximum number of events the licensee may broadcast per season under this deal. Caps the licensee\'s broadcast entitlement and protects rights holder\'s ability to distribute remaining events to other partners.']
  min_guaranteed_events varchar [note: 'Contractually guaranteed minimum number of live events the licensee is entitled to broadcast under this deal per contract year. Triggers make-good provisions if not met.']
  payment_schedule_type varchar [note: 'Defines the frequency and structure of rights fee payments from the licensee to the rights holder. Drives accounts receivable scheduling in SAP S/4HANA SD.']
  ppv_rights_included boolean [note: 'Indicates whether Pay-Per-View event packaging rights are included in this deal, allowing the licensee to offer individual event purchases to consumers.']
  renewal_notice_days varchar [note: 'Number of calendar days prior to expiry_date by which either party must provide written notice of intent to renew or terminate. Drives automated renewal alert workflows in Salesforce CRM.']
  renewal_option_type varchar [note: 'Type of renewal option included in the deal. Automatic renewal triggers without action; mutual_agreement requires both parties to agree; right_of_first_refusal gives the licensee priority to match competing offers; none means no renewal option exists.']
  replay_rights_included boolean [note: 'Indicates whether replay/rebroadcast rights are included in this deal, allowing the licensee to air recorded versions of events after the live window closes.']
  revenue_share_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of net broadcast revenue shared back to the rights holder or league under the revenue share structure of this deal. Expressed as a decimal percentage (e.g., 15.00 = 15%). Critical for financial settlement calculations.']
  rights_categories varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of rights categories included in this deal. Typical values: live, replay, highlights, ppv, clip_licensing, archive, digital_streaming, radio. Captures the full scope of content rights granted. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: live|replay|highlights|ppv|clip_licensing|archive|digital_streaming|radio — promote to reference product]']
  rights_window_count varchar [note: 'Total number of distinct rights windows (time-period/platform/territory entitlement combinations) defined under this deal. Supports rights window enforcement and deal complexity classification.']
  rsn_affiliate_code varchar [note: 'Reference to the RSN affiliate entity associated with this deal when the deal type is rsn. Identifies the specific regional network carrying the broadcast rights within a defined geographic footprint.']
  salesforce_opportunity_reference varchar [note: 'External reference identifier from Salesforce CRM linking this media rights deal record to the originating CRM opportunity or contract object. Supports data lineage and system-of-record traceability.']
  sport_name varchar [note: 'Name of the sport covered by this media rights deal (e.g., American Football, Basketball, Soccer, Baseball, Hockey). Supports multi-sport deal classification and rights analytics.']
  sublicensing_permitted boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the licensee is contractually permitted to sublicense the media rights to third parties (e.g., regional affiliates, streaming sub-platforms). Critical for IP (Intellectual Property) protection and rights chain management.']
  termination_for_cause_permitted boolean [note: 'Indicates whether either party may terminate the deal early for cause (e.g., breach of contract, regulatory non-compliance, PED violations affecting league integrity). Relevant for compliance and legal risk management.']
  territory_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code representing the primary geographic territory in which the broadcast rights are granted. Use \'WLD\' for worldwide deals. Drives blackout rule enforcement and CDN geo-restriction.']
  territory_scope varchar [note: 'Categorical scope of the geographic territory covered by this deal. Distinguishes between national, regional (e.g., RSN footprint), local market, worldwide, or multi-territory agreements.']

  Note: 'Master record of media rights agreements with broadcast partners (RSN, OTT, linear TV networks), including granular rights window definitions. Captures rights holder, territory, sport/league, exclusivity type, deal value, contract term, revenue share structure, rights categories (live, replay, highlights, PPV), and per-window platform/territory/time-period entitlements with sublicensing permissions. SSOT for all broadcast rights ownership, licensing terms, and rights window enforcement. Sourced from Dalet Galaxy and Salesforce CRM.'
}

  channel_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the broadcast channel or platform (linear TV, OTT, IPTV, RSN) on which this schedule entry is assigned for distribution. Sourced from the content platform channel registry.']

Business justification: Broadcast operations teams map air schedules to the league\'s official game schedule for rights compliance, production planning, and blackout enforcement. A sports-entertainment domain expert would exp']
  league_season_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to league.season. Business justification: Broadcast scheduling is organized by league season for rights compliance, season-level viewership reporting, and upfront ad sales planning. Every broadcast schedule entry belongs to a specific league ']
  platform_channel_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to content.platform_channel. Business justification: Broadcast scheduling for OTT and digital platforms requires mapping schedule entries to content platform channels to validate platform capabilities (resolution, DRM, ad insertion).   playoff_bracket_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to league.playoff_bracket. Business justification: Playoff broadcasts require scheduling tied to specific bracket rounds and matchups for rights compliance, PPV pricing, and production planning. Broadcast schedulers must know which playoff bracket slo']
  policy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.policy. Business justification: Broadcast schedules must reference the content compliance policy governing rating_classification, watershed rules, and accessibility mandates. Schedulers apply the applicable policy when configuring e']
  primary_broadcast_event_fixture_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the sporting or entertainment event being broadcast in this schedule window. Links the broadcast schedule to the event domain for event-to-channel assignment tracking.']
  regulatory_filing_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_filing. Business justification: Broadcast schedules in regulated markets (FCC, Ofcom) must reference the regulatory filing authorizing each broadcast window. Compliance teams verify filing status before schedule activation; this lin']
  rights_window_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to broadcast.broadcast_rights_window. Business justification: broadcast_schedule has rights_window_id → content.rights_window (cross-domain) but no FK to broadcast.broadcast_rights_window. The in-domain broadcast_rights_window is the authoritative record definin']
  tournament_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to event.tournament. Business justification: Broadcast schedules are organized around tournaments — tournament broadcast packages require schedule records at tournament level for EPG programming, rights window activation, and tournament-wide bro']
  venue_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the venue or facility from which this broadcast originates. Used for OB unit logistics, venue operations coordination, and geographic rights enforcement.']
  actual_end_time timestamp [note: 'Actual timestamp at which the broadcast feed concluded. Used alongside actual_start_time to compute true broadcast duration for rights compliance and billing reconciliation.']
  actual_start_time timestamp [note: 'Actual timestamp at which the broadcast feed went live or playback commenced. Compared against scheduled_start_time for on-time performance reporting and SLA compliance tracking.']
  ad_break_count varchar [note: 'Number of planned commercial advertisement breaks within this broadcast schedule entry. Used for sponsorship inventory management, ad sales planning, and revenue forecasting.']
  ad_break_duration_minutes varchar [note: 'Total planned duration of all commercial advertisement breaks in minutes for this broadcast schedule entry. Used for ad inventory capacity planning and sponsor commitment fulfillment tracking.']
  air_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the broadcast is scheduled to air. Used for day-level scheduling, program guide publication, and rights window validation.']
  audio_description_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether audio description (video description for visually impaired viewers) is required for this broadcast. Driven by FCC accessibility mandates and ADA compliance requirements for broadcast content.']
  blackout_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether broadcast blackout rules apply to this schedule entry. When True, the blackout_rule_id and blackout_region_code must be evaluated before distribution to enforce territorial rights restrictions.']
  broadcast_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the broadcast feed type indicating whether the content is transmitted live, on a tape-delay basis, as a replay, as a highlight package, or as a Pay-Per-View (PPV) event. Drives rights enforcement, DRM policy application, and CDN routing logic.']
  cdn_profile_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the Content Delivery Network (CDN) configuration profile used to distribute this broadcast. Determines routing, caching, and failover behavior for digital platform delivery.']
  closed_caption_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether closed captioning is required for this broadcast schedule entry. Mandatory for FCC compliance under the Twenty-First Century Communications and Video Accessibility Act (CVAA) and ADA accessibility requirements.']
  commentary_language_code varchar [note: 'ISO 639-1/639-2 language code for the commentary/play-by-play audio track, which may differ from the primary broadcast language. Relevant for international feed distribution and multi-language OTT packaging.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this broadcast schedule record was first created in the Dalet Galaxy system and ingested into the Silver Layer. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and SOX financial controls compliance.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the PPV price and any associated financial values on this schedule entry (e.g., \'USD\', \'GBP\', \'EUR\'). Required for multi-currency international broadcast markets.']
  feed_format varchar [note: 'Technical broadcast feed format specifying the video quality standard for this schedule entry (e.g., HD, 4K, SD, HDR, VR). Used for CDN capacity planning, encoder assignment, and viewer experience reporting.']
  international_feed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this broadcast schedule entry uses an international feed (world feed) as opposed to a domestic production feed. Relevant for FIFA, IOC, and other international governing body rights distribution.']
  language_code varchar [note: 'ISO 639-1/639-2 language code for the primary audio track of this broadcast (e.g., \'en\', \'es\', \'fr-CA\'). Supports multi-language distribution and regional audience targeting.']
  league_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the sports league or governing body associated with the event being broadcast (e.g., NFL, NBA, MLB, NHL, MLS, FIFA, UFC, ATP). Used for rights segmentation, league governance reporting, and audience analytics.']
  ob_unit_code varchar [note: 'Identifier for the Outside Broadcast (OB) truck or mobile production unit assigned to this broadcast schedule entry. Used for equipment scheduling, logistics coordination, and production cost allocation.']
  platform_type varchar [note: 'Category of the distribution platform for this broadcast schedule entry. Distinguishes linear TV, Over-The-Top (OTT) streaming, Internet Protocol Television (IPTV), Regional Sports Network (RSN), Direct-To-Consumer (DTC), and Pay-Per-View (PPV) platform delivery modes.']
  ppv_event boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this broadcast schedule entry is a Pay-Per-View (PPV) event requiring individual purchase or access authorization. When True, triggers PPV pricing, access control, and revenue tracking workflows.']
  ppv_price decimal(10,2) [note: 'Retail price charged to consumers for access to this Pay-Per-View (PPV) broadcast event. Applicable only when ppv_event is True. Used for revenue forecasting, pricing strategy analysis, and consumer billing.']
  program_duration_minutes varchar [note: 'Planned duration of the broadcast program in minutes as scheduled in the program guide. Used for slot allocation, ad break planning, and rights window compliance verification.']
  rating_classification varchar [note: 'Content rating classification assigned to this broadcast as per the TV Parental Guidelines system (e.g., TV-G, TV-PG, TV-14, TV-MA). Required for FCC compliance, parental control enforcement, and OTT platform content filtering.']
  reference_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric code assigned by Dalet Galaxy to uniquely identify this broadcast schedule entry across systems. Used for cross-system reconciliation and operational communication with broadcast partners.']
  region_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country or territory code identifying the primary broadcast market for this schedule entry (e.g., \'USA\', \'GBR\', \'CAN\'). Used for rights territory enforcement, blackout rule application, and regional audience analytics.']
  rsn_affiliate_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the Regional Sports Network (RSN) affiliate carrying this broadcast. Applicable for regional distribution entries. Used for affiliate rights tracking, revenue sharing calculations, and regional audience reporting.']
  schedule_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the broadcast schedule entry within the Dalet Galaxy workflow. Drives operational readiness and downstream distribution actions. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|confirmed|on_air|completed|cancelled|postponed — promote to reference product if additional states are required]']
  scheduled_end_time timestamp [note: 'Precise date and timestamp at which the broadcast window is scheduled to conclude, including timezone offset. Used for slot duration calculation, back-to-back scheduling, and CDN resource planning.']
  scheduled_start_time timestamp [note: 'Precise date and timestamp at which the broadcast window is scheduled to begin, including timezone offset. Principal real-world event time for this schedule entry. Stored in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX).']
  simulcast_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this broadcast schedule entry is a simultaneous multi-platform broadcast (simulcast) airing on multiple channels or platforms at the same time. Used for rights management and audience deduplication in ratings reporting.']
  sport_type varchar [note: 'Category of sport or entertainment content being broadcast (e.g., American Football, Basketball, Soccer, Baseball, Hockey, Tennis, MMA, Concert). Used for content classification, audience segmentation, and rights categorization. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: american_football|basketball|soccer|baseball|hockey|tennis|mma|concert|other — promote to reference product]']
  time_zone varchar [note: 'IANA time zone identifier for the primary broadcast market (e.g., \'America/New_York\', \'Europe/London\'). Required for accurate local air time display in program guides and blackout rule enforcement.']
  title varchar [note: 'Human-readable title of the broadcast schedule entry as it appears in the Dalet Galaxy workflow and on-air program guides. Typically includes event name, league, and broadcast type (e.g., \'NFL Sunday Night Football — Live\').']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this broadcast schedule record was last modified in the source system or Silver Layer. Used for change detection, incremental data loading, and audit trail maintenance.']

  Note: 'Operational schedule of all planned and confirmed broadcast windows across linear TV, OTT, IPTV, and RSN platforms. Tracks event-to-channel assignments, air time, time zone, blackout applicability, feed type (live, tape-delay, replay), and production assignments. Core operational backbone for broadcast distribution — every broadcast begins as a schedule entry. Sourced from Dalet Galaxy broadcast workflow.'
}

A sports broadcast finance exper']

The governing_body table has a \'broadcast_regulatory_authority\' flag confirming this relationship. Compliance officers']
  league_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to league.league. Business justification: League-dedicated channels (NFL Network, NBA TV) require a direct channel-to-league link for league-branded channel management and rights compliance reporting. \'league_code\' on channel is a denormalize']
  policy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.policy. Business justification: Channels must operate under content compliance policies (watershed rules, parental controls, accessibility mandates). Channel configuration attributes (parental_rating, closed_caption_supported, conte']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Each broadcast channel generates revenue (subscriptions, ad sales) tracked in a dedicated profit center for segment reporting and EBITDA analysis. Finance uses channel-level profit centers for channel']
  regulatory_filing_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_filing. Business justification: Broadcast channels must hold active regulatory filings (FCC license, Ofcom license, spectrum authorization) to operate legally. Channel compliance management requires reference to the specific regulat']
  rsn_franchise_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to league.franchise. Business justification: RSN channels are franchise-specific (YES Network/Yankees, NESN/Red Sox). RSN channel-to-franchise assignment is a named business construct for local rights management. Role-prefix \'rsn_\' reflects the ']
  ad_insertion_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether dynamic ad insertion (DAI) is enabled for this channel. When True, the channel supports server-side or client-side ad insertion for CPM (Cost Per Mille) based advertising revenue. Critical for sponsorship and advertising revenue planning.']
  audio_format varchar [note: 'Audio encoding format delivered by this channel (e.g., stereo, 5.1 surround, Dolby Atmos). Used for broadcast technical compliance, production scheduling, and premium tier packaging.']

Enforced at the channel level for linear and OTT distribution.']
  broadcast_format varchar [note: 'Primary content delivery format of this channel. Live = real-time event broadcast; Replay = time-shifted full event replay; VOD (Video on Demand) = on-demand library content; Simulcast = simultaneous broadcast across multiple platforms; Highlights = short-form curated content.']
  channel_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric business identifier for the channel, used in rights agreements, scheduling systems, and Dalet Galaxy media asset management workflows. Serves as the natural key across operational systems.']

  channel_status varchar [note: 'Current operational lifecycle status of the channel. Active = currently distributing content; Inactive = temporarily off-air; Suspended = distribution halted due to rights or compliance issue; Pending Launch = configured but not yet live; Decommissioned = permanently retired.']
  channel_type varchar [note: 'Categorical classification of the channel\'s distribution model. Linear = traditional scheduled broadcast; OTT (Over-The-Top) = internet streaming; IPTV (Internet Protocol Television) = managed IP delivery; DTC (Direct-To-Consumer) = owned streaming service; PPV (Pay-Per-View) = event-based purchase; RSN (Regional Sports Network) = regional linear affiliate.']

  country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 three-letter country code representing the primary country of broadcast license or registration for this channel (e.g., \'USA\', \'GBR\', \'CAN\'). Used for regulatory compliance, blackout rule enforcement, and rights territory mapping.']
