  effective_from date [note: 'The calendar date on which this distribution agreement between the channel and vendor becomes effective and the vendor is authorized to begin distribution.']
  effective_until date [note: 'The calendar date on which this distribution agreement expires or is scheduled to terminate. Null indicates an open-ended agreement.']
  encoding_standard varchar [note: 'Video encoding/compression standard used for this channel\'s stream (e.g., H.264/AVC, H.265/HEVC, AV1). Determines device compatibility, CDN efficiency, and streaming quality at given bitrates.']
  epg_code varchar [note: 'External identifier used by Electronic Program Guide (EPG) data providers (e.g., Tribune Media, Gracenote) to reference this channel in scheduling and guide data feeds. Enables automated schedule ingestion and viewer guide population.']
  frame_rate decimal(18,2) [note: 'Video frame rate specification for this channel (e.g., 25fps for PAL markets, 30fps for NTSC markets, 60fps for premium sports HD). Impacts production compatibility, CDN bandwidth, and broadcast technical standards compliance.']
  geographic_coverage varchar [note: 'Textual description or structured list of geographic markets, regions, or territories this channel is licensed to serve (e.g., \'Northeast USA\', \'Western Europe\', \'Global excluding China\'). Distinct from country_code which captures the registration country; this field captures the full distribution footprint for rights enforcement.']
  language_code varchar [note: 'Primary broadcast language of the channel expressed as an ISO 639-1 or ISO 639-2 language code (e.g., \'en\', \'es\', \'fr-CA\'). Used for audience segmentation, rights territory management, and multi-language distribution planning.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this channel record, in ISO 8601 format with timezone offset (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Used for change data capture (CDC), data lineage, and audit compliance.']
  launch_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which this channel was officially launched and made available to audiences. Used for channel lifecycle tracking, anniversary reporting, and rights agreement effective date alignment.']
  max_concurrent_streams varchar [note: 'Maximum number of simultaneous viewer streams permitted on this channel as defined by rights agreements or platform capacity constraints. Used for CDN capacity planning and rights compliance enforcement during peak events.']
  monthly_rights_fee decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monthly fee paid to or received from the platform operator or rights holder for distribution of this channel, expressed in the channel\'s billing currency. Confidential commercial term used for revenue management and EBITDA reporting.']
  multiview_supported boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this channel supports simultaneous multi-angle or multi-game viewing (multiview) for premium subscribers. A differentiating feature for OTT and DTC channels used in fan engagement and premium tier upsell strategies.']
  ob_unit_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this channel requires an OB (Outside Broadcast) unit for live event production. When True, triggers OB unit scheduling and resource allocation workflows in the broadcast operations team.']
  parental_rating varchar [note: 'Content parental advisory rating assigned to this channel per FCC and TV Parental Guidelines standards (e.g., TV-G, TV-PG, TV-14, TV-MA). Used for parental control enforcement, platform compliance, and audience targeting restrictions.']
  platform_type varchar [note: 'The technical platform or delivery mechanism through which this vendor distributes the channel under this agreement. Determines infrastructure requirements and may affect revenue share terms.']
  ppv_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this channel supports PPV (Pay-Per-View) event packaging and transactional purchase by viewers. When True, the channel can be used to distribute PPV events with individual purchase requirements rather than subscription access.']
  resolution_tier varchar [note: 'Video resolution quality tier offered by this channel. SD (Standard Definition), HD (High Definition), 4K (Ultra High Definition), 8K (Super Hi-Vision). Drives CDN (Content Delivery Network) bandwidth planning, rights packaging, and subscriber pricing tiers.']
  revenue_share_pct decimal [note: 'The percentage of distribution revenue allocated to the vendor under this agreement. This is a commercially sensitive term unique to each channel-vendor distribution deal and cannot reside on either the channel or vendor master record.']
  signal_type varchar [note: 'Technical signal delivery mechanism for this channel. Determines infrastructure requirements, regulatory classification, and distribution partner compatibility. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: digital|analog|IP|satellite|cable|terrestrial|fiber|microwave — promote to reference product]']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the operational system of record from which this channel record was originated or last updated (e.g., DALET for Dalet Galaxy MAM, SALESFORCE for Salesforce CRM). Used for data lineage, reconciliation, and Silver layer provenance tracking in the Databricks Lakehouse.']
  stream_url varchar [note: 'Primary ingest or origin stream URL for OTT, IPTV, or DTC channel types. Confidential as it represents a direct access endpoint to live feed infrastructure. Used by broadcast operations and CDN configuration teams.']
  subscriber_count varchar [note: 'Current number of active subscribers or authorized viewers for this channel. For OTT/DTC/IPTV channels, reflects platform subscription count. For linear channels, reflects reported household reach. Used for ARPU (Average Revenue Per User) calculation and rights fee negotiations.']
  time_zone varchar [note: 'IANA time zone identifier for the channel\'s primary broadcast schedule reference (e.g., \'America/New_York\', \'Europe/London\'). Used for EPG scheduling, live event timing, and blackout rule time-window enforcement.']
  uptime_sla_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Contractually committed uptime percentage for this channel as defined in the SLA (Service Level Agreement) with the platform operator or CDN provider (e.g., 99.95). Used for operational performance monitoring and penalty clause management.']

  Note: 'Master catalog of all broadcast channels and distribution outlets managed by Sports Entertainment — including RSN affiliates, OTT streaming channels, IPTV feeds, linear TV networks, and DTC streaming services. Captures channel name, type (linear/OTT/IPTV/DTC), platform operator, geographic coverage, language, resolution tier (SD/HD/4K), and active status.'
}

Table broadcast.blackout_rule {
  blackout_rule_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the blackout rule record in the broadcast rights management system.']
  channel_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the broadcast channel or platform (linear TV, OTT, IPTV, RSN) on which this blackout rule is enforced. Links to the platform channel catalog.']
  franchise_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to league.franchise. Business justification: Franchise territory blackout enforcement is a core named business process in sports broadcasting. Home market blackout rules are franchise-specific (applied when a franchise\'s home game is not sold ou']
  league_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to league.league. Business justification: Blackout rules are league-mandated policies (e.g., NFL blackout policy per CBA). Direct league reference on blackout_rule supports league-level blackout policy management and compliance reporting. \'le']
  license_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to content.content_license. Business justification: Blackout rules are mandated by specific content licenses that restrict distribution in certain territories or windows. Rights enforcement and dispute resolution teams must trace which content license ']
  media_rights_deal_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the media rights agreement that mandates or authorizes this blackout rule. Links the rule to its contractual basis.']
  obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.obligation. Business justification: Blackout rules are derived from compliance obligations (league territorial exclusivity obligations, regulatory blackout mandates). Linking blackout_rule to obligation enables compliance teams to trace']
  platform_channel_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to content.platform_channel. Business justification: Blackout rules must be enforced on specific OTT/digital platform channels, not just linear broadcast channels. Rights enforcement teams need to identify which platform_channel is subject to a blackout']
  primary_blackout_event_fixture_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific event (game, match, concert, PPV event) to which this blackout rule applies. Null if the rule applies to all events under a league or rights window.']
  regulatory_filing_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_filing. Business justification: Sports blackout rules are frequently mandated by regulatory filings (FCC sports blackout regulations, territorial exclusivity filings). The blackout_rule references the regulatory filing that mandates']
  rights_holder_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to broadcast.rights_holder. Business justification: Blackout rules are contractual obligations mandated by rights holders to protect territorial exclusivity and ticket sales. Every blackout rule must identify which rights holder mandated the restrictio']
  rights_window_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to broadcast.broadcast_rights_window. Business justification: blackout_rule has rights_window_id → content.rights_window (cross-domain) but no FK to broadcast.broadcast_rights_window. A blackout rule is derived from and governed by a specific broadcast rights wi']
  tournament_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to event.tournament. Business justification: Blackout rules are applied at tournament level — entire tournaments may be subject to territory-wide blackouts (e.g., host country blackouts during World Cup qualifying). Rights compliance teams enfor']
  applies_to_highlights boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this blackout rule also restricts distribution of highlight clips and short-form content derived from the blacked-out event.']
  applies_to_replay boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this blackout rule extends to replay, VOD (Video on Demand), and time-shifted viewing of the event content, in addition to the live broadcast.']
  approval_date date [note: 'The date on which this blackout rule was formally approved by the designated authority for operational enforcement.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the individual or organizational role that formally approved this blackout rule for enforcement (e.g., \'VP Broadcast Rights\', \'League Office - Media Committee\').']
  blackout_lift_condition varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the condition(s) under which this blackout rule is automatically lifted or deactivated (e.g., \'Blackout lifted when 85% of venue capacity is sold 72 hours prior to event start\').']
  blackout_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the blackout scope: \'local\' (home market territory), \'national\' (entire country), \'international\' (cross-border), \'rsn_exclusivity\' (Regional Sports Network exclusive window), \'ppv\' (Pay-Per-View event blackout), \'ott\' (Over-The-Top streaming platform blackout).']
  blackout_window_hours varchar [note: 'Number of hours before and/or after the event start time during which the blackout is enforced. Defines the temporal scope of the blackout window around the event.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this blackout rule record was first created in the broadcast rights management system.']
  distribution_platform varchar [note: 'The delivery platform type on which the blackout is enforced: \'linear_tv\' (traditional broadcast), \'ott\' (Over-The-Top streaming), \'iptv\' (Internet Protocol Television), \'rsn\' (Regional Sports Network), \'ppv\' (Pay-Per-View), \'dtc\' (Direct-To-Consumer), \'cable\', \'satellite\'. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: linear_tv|ott|iptv|rsn|ppv|dtc|cable|satellite — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  effective_from date [note: 'The calendar date from which this blackout rule becomes binding and enforceable, aligned with the rights agreement or league mandate start date.']
  effective_until date [note: 'The calendar date on which this blackout rule ceases to be enforceable. Null for open-ended rules that remain active until explicitly terminated.']
  enforcement_mechanism varchar [note: 'Technical method used to enforce the blackout rule: \'geo_ip_block\' (IP geolocation blocking), \'drm_token\' (DRM token restriction), \'cdn_restriction\' (CDN-level content blocking), \'access_control\' (platform access control list), \'manual\' (manual operator action), \'api_enforcement\' (API-level enforcement).']
  geo_precision_level varchar [note: 'Granularity of geographic enforcement for this blackout rule: \'country\', \'state\', \'dma\' (Designated Market Area), \'zip_code\', \'city\'. Determines how precisely the blackout territory is defined and enforced.']
  last_reviewed_date date [note: 'The most recent date on which this blackout rule was reviewed for accuracy, compliance alignment, and continued applicability by the rights management team.']
  mandate_source varchar [note: 'The originating source of the blackout mandate: \'league_cba\' (Collective Bargaining Agreement), \'rights_contract\' (media rights deal terms), \'regulatory\' (FCC or government regulation), \'venue_agreement\' (venue exclusivity clause), \'network_policy\' (broadcaster internal policy).']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for broadcast operations staff to capture additional context, exceptions, or operational instructions related to this blackout rule that are not captured in structured fields.']
  notification_lead_hours varchar [note: 'Number of hours in advance that notification must be submitted to the relevant authority before the blackout rule is activated or lifted. Applicable when notification_required is True.']
  notification_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether advance notification to the league office, rights holder, or regulatory body is required before activating or lifting this blackout rule.']
  override_authority varchar [note: 'The organizational role or body authorized to approve an override of this blackout rule: \'league_office\', \'rights_holder\', \'network_ops\' (Network Operations), \'legal\', \'executive\'. Applicable only when override_eligible is True.']
  override_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this blackout rule can be overridden under specific conditions (e.g., emergency broadcast, league waiver, special event designation). True = override is permissible with authorization.']
  penalty_clause varchar [note: 'Description of contractual or regulatory penalties applicable if this blackout rule is violated or improperly enforced, as defined in the rights agreement or league mandate.']
  post_event_blackout_hours varchar [note: 'Number of hours after event conclusion during which the blackout remains enforced. Protects replay and VOD rights windows for RSN and rights holders.']
  pre_event_blackout_hours varchar [note: 'Number of hours prior to event start time during which the blackout is enforced. Used to protect live gate attendance and RSN exclusivity windows.']
  rsn_exclusivity boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this blackout rule is driven by RSN exclusivity rights. True = an RSN holds exclusive broadcast rights in the territory, triggering the blackout on competing platforms.']
  rsn_partner_name varchar [note: 'Name of the RSN partner holding exclusive broadcast rights that trigger this blackout (e.g., \'Bally Sports\', \'MSG Network\', \'ROOT Sports\'). Populated when rsn_exclusivity is True.']
  rule_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric code uniquely identifying this blackout rule, used in league governance documents, RSN contracts, and broadcast operations systems.']
  rule_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the blackout rule, including its business rationale, contractual basis, and operational scope.']
  rule_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name describing the blackout rule (e.g., \'NFL Home Market Local Blackout\', \'RSN Exclusivity Window - Pacific Northwest\').']
  rule_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the blackout rule: \'active\' (currently enforced), \'inactive\' (not enforced), \'pending\' (awaiting activation), \'suspended\' (temporarily halted), \'expired\' (past effective period), \'draft\' (under review).']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the operational system of record from which this blackout rule was sourced (e.g., \'DALET_GALAXY\', \'MANUAL_ENTRY\', \'LEAGUE_FEED\'). Supports data lineage in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  sport_type varchar [note: 'The sport or entertainment category to which this blackout rule applies (e.g., \'American Football\', \'Basketball\', \'Baseball\', \'Hockey\', \'Soccer\', \'MMA\', \'Concert\'). Enables cross-sport analytics and rule management.']
  territory_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code or alpha-2 code identifying the primary geographic territory to which this blackout rule applies (e.g., \'USA\', \'CAN\', \'GBR\').']
  territory_region varchar [note: 'Sub-national region, DMA (Designated Market Area), or RSN coverage zone within the territory where the blackout applies (e.g., \'New York DMA\', \'Pacific Northwest RSN Zone\'). Complements territory_code for granular geographic enforcement.']
  ticket_sales_threshold_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'The minimum percentage of venue capacity that must be sold for the blackout to be lifted (e.g., 85.00 means 85% of tickets must be sold). Applicable when trigger_condition is \'ticket_sales_threshold\' or \'sellout_rule\'.']
  trigger_condition varchar [note: 'The business condition that activates this blackout rule: \'ticket_sales_threshold\' (blackout lifted when ticket sales meet threshold), \'rsn_exclusivity\' (RSN holds exclusive rights), \'league_mandate\' (governing body requirement), \'rights_agreement\' (contractual obligation), \'ppv_purchase\' (PPV event requiring purchase), \'sellout_rule\' (event sold out). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: ticket_sales_threshold|rsn_exclusivity|league_mandate|rights_agreement|ppv_purchase|sellout_rule|capacity_threshold|broadcast_window — promote to reference product]']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp of the most recent modification to this blackout rule record, supporting audit trail and change management requirements.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Incremental version number tracking amendments and revisions to this blackout rule over its lifecycle. Supports audit trail and change management for rights compliance.']

  Note: 'Business rules governing broadcast blackouts by territory, event, channel, and rights agreement. Captures blackout type (local, national, international), triggering conditions (ticket sales threshold, RSN exclusivity, league mandate), effective date range, override eligibility, and enforcement mechanism. Critical for rights compliance and RSN exclusivity protection. Sourced from league governance and media rights deal terms.'
}

Table broadcast.live_feed {
  live_feed_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each live broadcast feed record in the Sports Entertainment broadcast domain. Primary key for the live_feed data product.']
  backup_feed_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the designated backup or failover live feed that will be activated if this primary feed fails. Enables automated failover management and redundancy tracking in broadcast operations.']
  broadcast_channel_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to broadcast.channel. Business justification: A live feed is distributed on a specific broadcast channel. While live_feed has boolean flags (is_ott_enabled, is_iptv_enabled, is_linear_tv_enabled), it lacks a direct FK to the channel master record']
  broadcast_production_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to broadcast.production. Business justification: A live feed is the technical output of a broadcast production. Linking live_feed to production enables crew, equipment, and production metadata to be associated with the feed. This supports post-event']
  broadcast_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to broadcast.broadcast_schedule. Business justification: A live feed is produced for a specific broadcast schedule window. This FK links the operational feed record to its scheduled broadcast slot, enabling operational tracking of which feed serves which sc']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Live feeds incur significant costs (CDN delivery, OB unit operations, satellite uplinks) tracked against cost centers in finance. Finance requires live feed-to-cost-center linkage for production cost ']
  dalet_asset_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the corresponding media asset record in the Dalet Galaxy Media Asset Management (MAM) system. Enables traceability between the operational live feed record and the media asset management workflow for ingest, archiving, and content repurposing.']
  match_result_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to event.match_result. Business justification: Live feeds capture match results in real-time for result overlay graphics, post-match replay rights activation, and broadcast compliance verification. Broadcast operations teams link feed records to c']
  media_rights_deal_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the media rights agreement governing distribution of this live feed across licensed territories and platforms.']
  platform_channel_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to content.platform_channel. Business justification: Live feed OTT distribution requires referencing the content platform channel to configure CDN endpoints, DRM requirements, and geo-restriction rules. Feed engineers need this link to route live feeds ']
  primary_live_event_fixture_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the scheduled sporting or entertainment event for which this live feed is being produced and distributed.']
  regulatory_filing_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_filing. Business justification: Live feeds distributed across territories must be authorized by regulatory filings (spectrum licenses, content distribution authorizations). Operations teams verify regulatory filing status before act']
  rights_window_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the media rights agreement governing distribution of this live feed across licensed territories and platforms.']
  venue_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the source venue from which this live feed originates, used for routing and signal path management.']
  actual_end_time timestamp [note: 'Actual date and time (UTC) when this live feed concluded transmission. Used for post-event reporting, content ingest triggering, and broadcast duration billing. Null if feed is still active.']
  actual_start_time timestamp [note: 'Actual date and time (UTC) when this live feed began transmitting. Compared against scheduled_start_time to measure on-air punctuality and SLA (Service Level Agreement) compliance. Null if feed has not yet started.']
  aspect_ratio varchar [note: 'Width-to-height ratio of the video frame for this live feed. 16:9 is the standard widescreen format for HD and 4K broadcast; 4:3 is legacy SD format. Determines display compatibility across linear TV and OTT (Over-The-Top) platforms.']
  audio_format varchar [note: 'Audio channel configuration and encoding format for the primary audio track of the live feed. Dolby Atmos and 7.1 surround are premium formats for high-end broadcast and streaming platforms.']
  audio_track_count varchar [note: 'Total number of discrete audio tracks embedded in the live feed. Multiple tracks support stereo, 5.1 surround, and separate commentary language channels. Relevant for multi-language broadcast distribution and accessibility compliance.']
  bitrate_kbps varchar [note: 'Target encoding bitrate of the live feed in kilobits per second (Kbps). Determines bandwidth consumption, CDN (Content Delivery Network) capacity requirements, and viewer quality of experience. Typical values range from 1,500 Kbps (SD) to 50,000+ Kbps (4K HDR).']
  cdn_origin_endpoint varchar [note: 'Primary CDN (Content Delivery Network) origin ingest URL or endpoint address from which the live feed is distributed to downstream delivery nodes and OTT (Over-The-Top) platforms. Core operational field for live distribution monitoring.']
  commentary_language varchar [note: 'ISO 639-1 or ISO 639-2 language code for the primary commentary audio track on this live feed (e.g., \'en\' for English, \'es\' for Spanish, \'fr\' for French). Drives regional distribution routing and RSN (Regional Sports Network) assignment.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time (UTC) when this live feed record was first created in the system, typically during pre-event broadcast planning. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and Silver Layer ingestion tracking.']
  distribution_tier varchar [note: 'Geographic scope of distribution authorized for this live feed under the applicable media rights agreement. Global feeds are distributed worldwide; regional feeds are limited to a geographic zone; national feeds are country-specific; local feeds are limited to a designated market area (DMA).']
  encoding_format varchar [note: 'Video codec standard used to compress and encode the live feed signal. H.264 (AVC) and H.265 (HEVC) are the dominant standards for broadcast and OTT (Over-The-Top) streaming delivery. Impacts bandwidth, quality, and platform compatibility.']
  failover_activated boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the backup or redundancy failover mechanism was triggered during this live feed\'s transmission. Used for post-event incident analysis, SLA (Service Level Agreement) reporting, and broadcast reliability KPI (Key Performance Indicator) tracking.']
  failover_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time (UTC) when the failover mechanism was activated for this live feed. Null if failover_activated is false. Used for incident root cause analysis and SLA (Service Level Agreement) breach assessment.']
  feed_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric code uniquely identifying this live feed within broadcast operations and Dalet Galaxy media asset management workflows. Used for signal routing, scheduling, and inter-system reference.']
  feed_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable descriptive name for the live feed, typically including event name, feed type, and language (e.g., \'NFL Championship - World Feed - English\'). Used in operational dashboards and scheduling systems.']
  feed_status varchar [note: 'Current operational lifecycle status of the live feed. Active indicates the feed is live and distributing; standby is ready but not transmitting; failed indicates a signal loss or error condition; backup indicates a redundancy feed has taken over; scheduled indicates pre-event readiness; terminated indicates the feed has ended.']
  feed_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the live feed by its production and distribution purpose. World feed is the primary international signal; clean feed has no graphics; ISO feed is an isolated camera angle; PPV (Pay-Per-View) and OTT (Over-The-Top) feeds are platform-specific variants. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: world_feed|clean_feed|commentary_feed|iso_feed|backup_feed|ppv_feed|ott_feed — promote to reference product]']
  frame_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Number of video frames per second (fps) for this live feed. Common values include 25, 29.97, 50, 59.94, and 60 fps. Affects motion smoothness, storage requirements, and compatibility with broadcast standards across regions (PAL vs NTSC).']
  hdr_format varchar [note: 'High Dynamic Range (HDR) format applied to the live feed for enhanced color and brightness reproduction. HDR10 and HLG (Hybrid Log-Gamma) are the broadcast standards; Dolby Vision is a premium streaming format. None indicates standard dynamic range (SDR).']
  is_iptv_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this live feed is authorized and configured for distribution via IPTV (Internet Protocol Television) networks, including managed IPTV services provided by telecommunications operators.']
  is_linear_tv_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this live feed is authorized and configured for distribution via traditional linear television broadcast channels, including RSN (Regional Sports Network) and national broadcast network affiliates.']
  is_ott_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this live feed is authorized and technically configured for distribution via OTT (Over-The-Top) streaming platforms, including DTC (Direct-To-Consumer) services and third-party streaming partners.']
  is_ppv boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this live feed is designated as a PPV (Pay-Per-View) event feed, requiring consumer purchase or entitlement before access is granted. Drives entitlement checks on DTC (Direct-To-Consumer) and OTT (Over-The-Top) platforms.']
  latency_mode varchar [note: 'Configured latency mode for this live feed delivery. Ultra-low latency (sub-3 seconds) is required for interactive and betting-adjacent use cases; low latency (3-10 seconds) for OTT (Over-The-Top) sports streaming; standard (10-30 seconds) for traditional broadcast; high for archive-quality delivery.']
  ob_unit_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the Outside Broadcast (OB) truck or mobile production unit assigned to capture and produce this live feed at the source venue. Critical for resource scheduling and operational accountability.']
  production_region varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the primary production region where this live feed is originated and managed. Used for regulatory compliance, rights territory management, and broadcast operations routing.']
  redundancy_config varchar [note: 'Redundancy architecture configured for this live feed to ensure continuity in case of primary signal failure. 1+1 means one active and one standby path; N+1 means N active feeds with one shared backup. Critical for broadcast SLA (Service Level Agreement) compliance.']
  replay_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this live feed is authorized and configured to generate replay or catch-up content after the live event concludes. Drives post-event content ingest workflows in Dalet Galaxy and DRM (Digital Rights Management) policy application for replay windows.']
  replay_window_hours varchar [note: 'Number of hours after the live event ends during which replay content derived from this feed is available to authorized viewers. Defined by the media rights agreement and DRM (Digital Rights Management) policy. Null if replay_enabled is false.']
  resolution varchar [note: 'Video resolution tier of the live feed. SD (Standard Definition) is 480p/576p; HD (High Definition) is 720p; FHD (Full HD) is 1080p/1080i; 4K is 2160p; 8K is 4320p. Determines bandwidth requirements, CDN (Content Delivery Network) capacity planning, and platform delivery eligibility.']
  scheduled_start_time timestamp [note: 'Planned date and time (UTC) when this live feed is scheduled to begin transmission. Used for broadcast scheduling, CDN (Content Delivery Network) pre-warming, and OB (Outside Broadcast) unit coordination.']
  signal_type varchar [note: 'Physical or logical transport medium used to carry the live feed signal from the source venue to the broadcast facility or CDN (Content Delivery Network) origin. Determines redundancy planning and SLA (Service Level Agreement) requirements.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time (UTC) when this live feed record was most recently modified. Tracks operational changes such as status transitions, failover events, and configuration updates throughout the feed lifecycle.']

  Note: 'Operational record of each live broadcast feed produced and distributed for a scheduled event. Captures source venue, OB (Outside Broadcast) unit assignment, signal type (satellite, fiber, IP), encoding format (H.264/H.265), resolution (SD/HD/4K/8K), audio tracks, commentary language, feed status (active, standby, failed, backup), CDN origin endpoint, and redundancy configuration. Core operational entity for live broadcast production, distribution monitoring, and failover management.'
}

  streaming_subscription_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each streaming subscription record. Primary key for the streaming_subscription data product within the broadcast domain.']
  broadcast_channel_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to broadcast.channel. Business justification: A streaming subscription grants access to a specific broadcast channel or service. streaming_subscription has platform and service_type (STRING) but no FK to the channel master record. This FK enables']
  data_processing_record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.data_processing_record. Business justification: Streaming subscriptions process fan personal data (viewing behavior, payment info, device data, geo-location) under GDPR/CCPA, requiring documented data processing records. The streaming_subscription ']
  fan_payment_method_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fan.payment_method. Business justification: Streaming subscription recurring billing requires a reference to the fan\'s stored tokenized payment method for charge processing, failed payment retry, and PCI compliance audit. streaming_subscription']
  franchise_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to league.franchise. Business justification: Franchise-specific streaming subscription packages (team OTT offerings, RSN digital subscriptions) are a named business construct. Knowing which franchise a subscription is associated with enables fra']

Business justification: Streaming subscription revenue posts to a designated GL account (subscription revenue) for financial reporting. Revenue accounting teams assign GL accounts to subscription tiers during product setup —']
  loyalty_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fan.loyalty_program. Business justification: Streaming subscriptions are bundled with or earn points under specific loyalty programs (e.g., OTT subscribers auto-enroll in a digital loyalty tier). This link enables loyalty earning attribution per']
  media_rights_deal_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to broadcast.media_rights_deal. Business justification: A streaming subscription grants fan access to DTC/OTT content under a specific media rights deal. The deal governs the rights scope (digital_streaming_rights_included, ppv_rights_included, territory_c']
  platform_channel_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to content.platform_channel. Business justification: A streaming subscription grants entitlement to a specific OTT/digital platform channel. Subscription management and entitlement enforcement systems require this link for access control, churn analysis']
  policy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.policy. Business justification: Streaming subscriptions must comply with data privacy and consumer protection policies (GDPR consent, CCPA opt-out, auto-renewal disclosure laws). Subscription management references the applicable com']
  primary_streaming_fan_fan_profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the fan/subscriber identity record in the fan domain. Links the subscription to the individual consumer. Aligns with Adobe Experience Platform fan profile.']
  season_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to league.season. Business justification: Season-pass streaming subscription management and auto-renewal processing require season scoping. Annual season passes are a primary streaming product in sports; billing cycles, access windows, and re']
  acquisition_channel varchar [note: 'The marketing or distribution channel through which the subscriber was acquired. Used for CAC (Customer Acquisition Cost) attribution, ROI (Return on Investment) analysis, and channel performance reporting in Adobe Experience Platform. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: organic|paid_search|social_media|email|partner|in_app|referral — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  auto_renewal_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the subscription is configured to automatically renew at the end of each billing cycle without requiring explicit subscriber action. True = auto-renew enabled; False = subscription will expire at end_date unless manually renewed.']
  billing_cycle varchar [note: 'The recurring billing frequency for the subscription. Determines how often the subscriber is charged and when renewal events are triggered in the billing system.']
  billing_cycle_day varchar [note: 'The day of the month (1–28) on which the recurring billing charge is processed. Used by the billing engine to schedule charge attempts and generate invoices.']
  blackout_region_code varchar [note: 'ISO country or region code identifying the geographic blackout restriction applied to this subscription. Enforces broadcast rights agreements and RSN (Regional Sports Network) territorial exclusivity. Null if no blackout applies.']
  cancellation_date date [note: 'The date on which the subscriber initiated cancellation of the subscription. Distinct from end_date (which is the access expiry). Null if subscription has not been cancelled. Used for churn analysis and GDPR Article 17 right-to-erasure workflows.']
  cancellation_reason varchar [note: 'Subscriber-provided or system-inferred reason for cancellation. Used for churn root-cause analysis, content investment decisions, and win-back campaign targeting. Null if subscription is not cancelled. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: price|content|technical|competitor|inactivity|payment_failure|other — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  concurrent_stream_limit varchar [note: 'Maximum number of simultaneous streams permitted under this subscription. Enforced by the streaming platform and CDN. Varies by plan tier (e.g., basic = 1, premium = 3, all-access = 5).']
  consent_data_sharing_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the subscriber has consented to their viewing and behavioral data being shared with third-party analytics partners, sponsors, or league data programs. Required for GDPR Article 6 lawful basis and CCPA opt-out compliance.']
  consent_marketing_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the subscriber has provided explicit consent to receive marketing communications related to their subscription and associated sports/entertainment content. Mandatory for GDPR and CCPA compliance in fan engagement workflows.']
  consent_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp at which the subscriber\'s consent preferences (marketing and data sharing) were last captured or updated. Required for GDPR audit trail and proof-of-consent obligations.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp at which this streaming subscription record was first created in the system. Represents the RECORD_AUDIT_CREATED category for this MASTER_AGREEMENT entity. Used for data lineage, audit trails, and subscription cohort analysis.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code in which the subscription is billed (e.g., USD, GBP, EUR). Supports multi-market DTC/OTT operations across global territories.']
  device_code varchar [note: 'Anonymized or hashed identifier of the primary device registered to this subscription. Used for device-binding enforcement under DRM policies and concurrent stream management. May be considered PII in certain jurisdictions.']
  download_enabled_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the subscriber\'s plan tier permits offline content downloads to their device. True = downloads permitted subject to DRM expiry rules; False = streaming only. Governed by content rights windows.']
  drm_profile_code varchar [note: 'Reference to the DRM (Digital Rights Management) policy profile assigned to this subscription tier. Governs content protection, concurrent stream limits, download rights, and device binding rules enforced by the CDN (Content Delivery Network) and streaming platform.']
  end_date date [note: 'The date on which the current subscription term ends. Nullable for open-ended subscriptions. For fixed-term plans this is the contractual expiry; for auto-renewing plans this is the next renewal date minus one day. Represents EFFECTIVE_UNTIL for this MASTER_AGREEMENT entity.']
  geo_country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the subscriber\'s registered billing address. Used for geo-based rights enforcement, tax jurisdiction determination, GDPR/CCPA applicability, and regional content licensing compliance.']
  max_video_quality varchar [note: 'The highest video resolution quality tier permitted under this subscription plan. Governs CDN (Content Delivery Network) stream quality caps and is a key differentiator between subscription tiers.']
  next_billing_date date [note: 'The upcoming date on which the next recurring charge will be attempted for this subscription. Used for billing forecasting, dunning management, and subscriber communications.']
  partner_bundle_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the third-party partner bundle or distribution agreement through which this subscription was provisioned (e.g., IPTV carrier bundle, telco partnership, OTT aggregator). Null for direct DTC subscriptions. Used for partner revenue share reporting.']
  payment_method_type varchar [note: 'The type of payment instrument used for recurring billing of this subscription. Stored as a tokenized reference type only — no raw card data is held in this product in compliance with PCI-DSS.']
  plan_tier varchar [note: 'The commercial tier of the streaming subscription defining the level of content access and features available to the subscriber. Tiers range from basic (limited content) to all-access (full DTC/OTT catalog including PPV events).']
  ppv_access_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this subscription tier includes access to PPV (Pay-Per-View) events as part of the base plan entitlement. True = PPV events included; False = PPV requires separate purchase. Critical for revenue recognition and event packaging decisions.']
  promo_code varchar [note: 'The promotional or discount code applied at the time of subscription sign-up. Used to track campaign effectiveness, partner referral programs, and sponsorship-driven subscriber acquisition.']
  promotional_rate decimal(10,2) [note: 'The discounted charge amount applied during a promotional period (e.g., introductory offer, bundle discount, loyalty reward). When populated, this rate overrides the standard subscription_charge for the promotional duration.']
  promotional_rate_end_date date [note: 'The date on which the promotional pricing expires and the subscriber reverts to the standard subscription_charge. Null if no promotion is active.']
  service_type varchar [note: 'Classifies the streaming service model under which this subscription operates. DTC (Direct-To-Consumer) = owned platform; OTT (Over-The-Top Streaming) = third-party app distribution; IPTV (Internet Protocol Television) = carrier-delivered; RSN (Regional Sports Network) = regional broadcast bundle; PPV (Pay-Per-View) = single-event purchase.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The operational system of record from which this subscription record originated. Used for data lineage tracking, reconciliation, and Silver Layer provenance in the Databricks Lakehouse. Aligns with Adobe Experience Platform as the primary fan engagement and DTC subscription platform.']
  start_date date [note: 'The date on which the subscription became or becomes effective and the subscriber gains access to entitled content. Represents the EFFECTIVE_FROM for this MASTER_AGREEMENT entity.']
  subscription_charge decimal(10,2) [note: 'The gross recurring charge amount billed to the subscriber per billing cycle before any discounts or promotional adjustments. Expressed in the subscription currency.']
  subscription_number varchar [note: 'Externally-visible, human-readable subscription reference number used in customer communications, billing statements, and support interactions. Sourced from the DTC/OTT platform billing system.']
  subscription_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the streaming subscription. Drives entitlement enforcement, billing actions, and fan engagement workflows. Active = currently billable and entitled; Trialing = within a free trial window; Suspended = temporarily paused; Cancelled = subscriber-initiated termination; Expired = end date passed without renewal.']
  suspension_date date [note: 'The date on which the subscription was placed into a suspended state (e.g., due to payment failure, voluntary pause, or compliance hold). Null if subscription has never been suspended.']
  suspension_reason varchar [note: 'The reason the subscription was placed into a suspended state. Used for operational triage, dunning management, and compliance reporting. Null if subscription has never been suspended.']
  trial_end_date date [note: 'The date on which the free trial period ends and the first paid billing cycle commences. Null if trial_flag is False. Critical for churn prediction and trial-to-paid conversion tracking.']
  trial_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the subscription is currently within a free trial period. True = subscriber has not yet been charged; False = paid subscription. Used to distinguish trial cohorts from paying subscribers in ARPU (Average Revenue Per User) and LTV (Lifetime Value) analytics.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp at which this streaming subscription record was last modified. Used for change data capture (CDC) in the Databricks Silver Layer pipeline, audit trails, and detecting stale records.']

  Note: 'Master record of fan streaming subscriptions to DTC and OTT broadcast services. Captures subscriber reference, subscription plan tier (basic, premium, all-access), billing cycle, start/end dates, platform (mobile, web, connected TV), auto-renewal flag, promotional rate, and current status. SSOT for streaming subscription lifecycle within the broadcast domain. Complements fan domain identity records.'
}

  archive_location varchar [note: 'Physical or logical storage path/URI where the media asset master file is archived (e.g., cloud object storage bucket path, SAN mount point, tape library reference). Used by the Dalet Galaxy DAM for asset retrieval and restore operations.']
  archive_tier varchar [note: 'Storage tier classification for the media asset based on access frequency and retention requirements. Hot: frequently accessed active assets; Warm: recent but less active; Cold: infrequently accessed; Deep Archive: long-term preservation. Drives storage cost allocation and retrieval SLA.']
  aspect_ratio varchar [note: 'Video aspect ratio of the media asset. Determines display compatibility across broadcast, OTT, and social media platforms. 16:9 is standard for HD/4K broadcast; 9:16 for vertical social media clips.']
  asset_status varchar [note: 'Current state of the media asset within the Dalet Galaxy broadcast workflow lifecycle. Controls downstream distribution eligibility and archive processing. Values: draft (being created), ingest (being ingested), qc_review (under quality control review), approved (cleared for distribution), archived (moved to long-term storage), deleted (logically removed).']
  asset_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the media asset by its production and editorial purpose within the broadcast workflow. Drives rights clearance rules, distribution eligibility, and archive retention policies. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: live_recording|highlight_reel|replay|promo|interstitial|documentary|clip|raw_feed|press_conference|interview — promote to reference product if additional types are required]']
  audio_channels varchar [note: 'Number of audio channels in the media asset (e.g., 2 for stereo, 6 for 5.1 surround, 8 for 7.1 surround). Required for broadcast playout compatibility and OTT audio track configuration.']
  blackout_rule_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether broadcast blackout rules apply to this media asset. When True, the asset is subject to geographic blackout restrictions per league media rights agreements (e.g., NFL local market blackouts, RSN territorial restrictions). Drives CDN geo-blocking logic.']
  broadcast_date date [note: 'Date on which this media asset was first broadcast or made available to audiences. Used for rights window calculations, replay eligibility, and archive classification.']
  cdn_delivery_url varchar [note: 'Primary CDN-hosted URL for streaming or downloading this media asset. Used by OTT, IPTV, and DTC platforms to serve content to end users. May be a signed or tokenized URL depending on DRM policy.']
  checksum varchar [note: 'SHA-256 cryptographic checksum of the media asset master file. Used to verify file integrity during ingest, transfer, and archive operations. Ensures no corruption or unauthorized modification of broadcast master content.']
  codec varchar [note: 'Video codec used to encode the media asset (e.g., H.264, H.265/HEVC, AVC, ProRes 422). Critical for transcoding decisions, CDN delivery optimization, and OTT/IPTV platform compatibility.']
  content_rating varchar [note: 'Television content rating assigned to the media asset per FCC/TV Parental Guidelines (e.g., TV-G, TV-PG, TV-14, TV-MA). Required for broadcast compliance, parental control enforcement on OTT platforms, and advertising eligibility.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the media asset record was first created in the Dalet Galaxy DAM system and propagated to the Silver Layer lakehouse. Audit trail field for data lineage and compliance reporting.']
  distribution_channel varchar [note: 'Primary distribution channel for which this media asset is packaged or cleared. Drives platform-specific encoding, DRM policy selection, and rights window application. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: linear_tv|ott|iptv|dtc|rsn|ppv|social_media|vod|syndication — promote to reference product if additional channels required]']
  duration_seconds varchar [note: 'Total playback duration of the media asset in seconds. Used for broadcast scheduling, rights window calculations, PPV packaging, and CDN bandwidth planning.']
  file_size_mb decimal(18,2) [note: 'Physical file size of the media asset in megabytes. Used for storage capacity planning, archive cost allocation, and CDN transfer cost estimation.']
  format varchar [note: 'Technical container or wrapper format of the media asset file (e.g., MXF for broadcast master, MP4 for OTT/DTC delivery, HLS for adaptive streaming). Determines compatibility with CDN delivery and playout systems. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: MP4|MXF|MOV|HLS|DASH|ProRes|DNxHD — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  frame_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Video frame rate of the media asset expressed in frames per second (fps), e.g., 23.98, 25.00, 29.97, 50.00, 59.94. Required for broadcast standards compliance and playout scheduling.']
  ingest_source varchar [note: 'Origin source from which the media asset was ingested into the Dalet Galaxy DAM system. OB (Outside Broadcast) truck, studio feed, satellite uplink, file transfer, API ingest, or third-party delivery. Used for provenance tracking and quality control routing.']
  ingest_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the media asset was first ingested into the Dalet Galaxy DAM system. Marks the start of the asset lifecycle and is used for SLA tracking, audit trails, and archive retention calculations.']
  is_live_recording boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this media asset is a live or near-live recording captured during an active broadcast event, as opposed to a pre-produced or post-produced asset. Drives priority ingest workflows and real-time CDN distribution rules.']
  language_code varchar [note: 'Primary spoken/audio language of the media asset expressed as an ISO 639-1 or ISO 639-2 language code (e.g., \'en\', \'es\', \'fr\', \'pt-BR\'). Drives regional distribution rights and localization workflows.']
  league_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the professional league or governing body associated with the media asset (e.g., NFL, NBA, MLB, NHL, MLS, FIFA, UFC, ATP). Drives rights clearance rules, blackout policy application, and RSN distribution eligibility.']
  metadata_tags varchar [note: 'Comma-separated free-text metadata tags applied to the media asset for search, discovery, and content classification purposes within the Dalet Galaxy DAM (e.g., \'playoffs,overtime,game-winner,fourth-quarter\'). Supports AI/ML content recommendation and editorial search.']
  ppv_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this media asset is eligible for Pay-Per-View (PPV) packaging and monetization. When True, the asset may be included in PPV event bundles and premium access tiers.']
  resolution varchar [note: 'Video resolution classification of the media asset. Drives distribution tier eligibility (e.g., 4K for premium PPV, HD for standard broadcast, SD for legacy RSN feeds) and storage cost allocation.']
  rights_expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which the media rights clearance for this asset expires. After this date, the asset must be withheld from distribution pending rights renewal. Critical for automated rights enforcement and compliance with media rights agreements.']
  rights_territory varchar [note: 'Geographic territory or territories in which this media asset is cleared for distribution, expressed as ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country codes or a named territory group (e.g., \'USA\', \'GBR\', \'WORLDWIDE\', \'EMEA\'). Drives blackout rule enforcement and regional RSN assignment.']
  sport_type varchar [note: 'Sport or athletic discipline associated with the media asset (e.g., American Football, Basketball, Soccer, Baseball, Hockey, Tennis, MMA). Used for rights segmentation, RSN assignment, and content discovery. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: american_football|basketball|soccer|baseball|hockey|tennis|mma|golf|motorsport|olympics — promote to reference product]']
  subtitle_available boolean [note: 'Indicates whether closed captions or subtitles are available for this media asset. Required for ADA (Americans with Disabilities Act) compliance for broadcast content and OTT platform accessibility standards.']
  title varchar [note: 'Human-readable title of the media asset as catalogued in the DAM system (e.g., \'NBA Finals Game 7 Full Broadcast\', \'Super Bowl LVIII Halftime Show Highlight Reel\').']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the media asset record was last modified in the source DAM system or Silver Layer. Used for incremental data pipeline processing, audit trails, and change data capture (CDC) workflows.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Incremental version number of the media asset, incremented each time the asset is edited, re-encoded, or updated. Supports version control and ensures downstream systems consume the correct asset revision.']

  Note: 'Master catalog of broadcast media assets managed within the Dalet Galaxy DAM system. Captures asset ID, title, asset type (live recording, highlight reel, replay, promo, interstitial), format, duration, resolution, language, sport/league association, event association, rights clearance status, archive location, and metadata tags. SSOT for broadcast-owned media asset inventory distinct from content domain editorial assets.'
}

Table broadcast.rights_window {
  rights_window_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each granular rights window record within the broadcast domain. Primary key for the broadcast_rights_window data product on the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  broadcast_channel_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to broadcast.channel. Business justification: A broadcast rights window defines rights for a specific channel or distribution outlet. broadcast_rights_window has platform_type (STRING) but no FK to the channel master record. This FK enables chann']
  category_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the content category classification governing what type of content is covered by this rights window (e.g., live match, highlight reel, documentary, PPV event).']
  fiscal_period_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.fiscal_period. Business justification: Rights windows map to fiscal periods for period-end deferred revenue calculations and revenue recognition close. Finance teams must identify which windows fall within each fiscal period to correctly r']
  franchise_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to league.franchise. Business justification: Franchise-level local broadcast rights windows (RSN exclusivity windows, home market windows) are a real named business construct. Local rights windows are franchise-anchored and require a direct fran']
  league_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the sports league or competition governing body whose content is covered by this rights window (e.g., NFL, NBA, FIFA, MLS). Enables league-level rights aggregation and reporting.']
  league_season_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to league.season. Business justification: Rights windows are scoped to specific league seasons for scheduling, rights compliance, and royalty calculation. season_year is a denormalized representation of the season entity. A direct FK to leagu']
  licensee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the broadcaster, OTT platform, or network entity that has been granted the rights for this window (e.g., RSN, DTC streaming service, IPTV provider).']
  media_rights_deal_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent media rights deal from which this window is derived. Links the granular window record back to the overarching rights agreement.']
  policy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.policy. Business justification: Rights windows define territory/exclusivity scope; compliance policies govern permissible content distribution in each territory (data privacy, content standards). Rights window configuration must ref']
  primary_rights_event_fixture_id varchar [note: 'Reference to a specific sporting event or fixture covered by this rights window. Nullable for deal-level windows that cover a season or content category rather than a single event.']
  regulatory_filing_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_filing. Business justification: A broadcast rights window covering a specific territory must be backed by a regulatory filing (e.g., Ofcom authorization, FCC license) in that jurisdiction. Rights window activation is contingent on c']
  rights_holder_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the organisation or party that holds the broadcast rights for this window (e.g., league, federation, production company). Supports rights enforcement and royalty attribution.']
  advertising_rights_included boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the licensee has the right to sell advertising inventory (e.g., CPM-based ad slots) within the broadcast under this rights window. Impacts revenue share calculations and sponsorship conflict management.']
  blackout_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether broadcast blackout rules apply to this rights window. When True, the CDN and DRM layer must enforce geographic or platform-based blackout restrictions as defined in the associated blackout policy.']
  broadcast_window_type varchar [note: 'Classifies the role of this rights window within the overall broadcast schedule. Primary windows cover live or first-run broadcasts; secondary covers repeat broadcasts; catch-up covers time-shifted viewing; archive covers library content; preview covers promotional clips.']
  catchup_window_days varchar [note: 'Number of days after the original broadcast during which the licensee may make the content available for catch-up or time-shifted viewing. Zero or null indicates no catch-up rights are granted.']
  concurrent_stream_limit varchar [note: 'Maximum number of simultaneous streams permitted under this rights window for OTT, IPTV, or DTC platforms. Null indicates no contractual limit. Enforced at the CDN and streaming platform layer to prevent rights overrun.']
  conflict_resolution_status varchar [note: 'Current status of any rights conflict resolution process for this window. Populated only when rights_conflict_flag is True. Tracks progression from detection through resolution or escalation to CAS arbitration.']
  content_type varchar [note: 'Classifies the nature of the content covered by this rights window. Distinguishes live broadcast rights from replay, highlight, documentary, PPV event, and magazine show rights, each carrying different fee structures and DRM policies.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this rights window record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail requirements under SOX financial controls and GDPR data lineage obligations.']
  effective_from date [note: 'The calendar date from which this rights window record is considered valid for business use. Supports slowly changing dimension (SCD) patterns on the Silver Layer and enables point-in-time rights queries.']
  effective_until date [note: 'The calendar date until which this rights window record is considered valid. Nullable for currently active records. Supports SCD Type 2 history management on the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  exclusivity_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the rights granted in this window are exclusive to the licensee within the defined territory and platform. True = exclusive; False = non-exclusive. Exclusive windows command premium rights fees and restrict sublicensing.']
  language_code varchar [note: 'ISO 639-1/639-2 language code specifying the language in which the content may be broadcast under this rights window (e.g., \'en\', \'es\', \'fr\'). Supports multi-language rights management for global markets.']
  max_broadcast_count varchar [note: 'The maximum number of times the content may be broadcast under this rights window. Null indicates unlimited broadcasts within the window period. Enforced by the DRM and broadcast scheduling systems.']
  min_lead_time_hours varchar [note: 'The minimum number of hours before the scheduled broadcast that the licensee must notify the rights holder or activate the content feed. Supports SLA (Service Level Agreement) compliance and OB (Outside Broadcast) logistics.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for capturing additional contractual conditions, special provisions, or operational notes associated with this rights window that are not captured in structured fields (e.g., specific event carve-outs, force majeure clauses).']
  payment_schedule_type varchar [note: 'Defines the payment cadence for the rights fee associated with this window. Drives accounts receivable scheduling in SAP S/4HANA and cash flow forecasting in Workday Adaptive Planning.']
  resolution_tier varchar [note: 'The maximum video resolution permitted for broadcast under this rights window (SD, HD, 4K, 8K). Higher resolution tiers typically command premium rights fees and require specific CDN and DRM capabilities.']
  revenue_share_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'The percentage of broadcast revenue (e.g., subscription, PPV, advertising) that the licensee must remit to the rights holder under this window. Drives financial settlement and EBITDA reporting.']
  rights_category varchar [note: 'Broad classification of the rights type granted in this window. Distinguishes domestic broadcast rights from international, digital, mobile, radio, PPV (Pay-Per-View), and archive rights. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: domestic|international|digital|mobile|radio|ppv|archive — promote to reference product]']
  rights_conflict_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a rights conflict has been detected for this window (e.g., overlapping exclusivity with another window for the same content, platform, and territory). Triggers review workflow in the rights management system.']
  rights_fee_allocation_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'The percentage of the total parent deal rights fee allocated to this specific window. Enables proportional revenue recognition and financial reporting across multiple windows derived from a single deal.']
  rights_fee_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The monetary value of the rights fee allocated to this specific window from the parent rights deal. Represents the portion of the total deal value attributable to this platform-territory-period combination. Used for revenue recognition and financial reporting.']
  rights_fee_currency varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the rights fee amount (e.g., USD, GBP, EUR). Required for multi-currency rights deals across global markets.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the operational system of record from which this rights window record was ingested (e.g., \'DALET_GALAXY\', \'SAP_SD\', \'SALESFORCE_CRM\'). Supports data lineage tracking on the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  sublicensing_permitted boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the licensee is contractually permitted to sublicense the broadcast rights to third parties within this window. Drives downstream rights conflict detection and IP (Intellectual Property) protection enforcement.']
  territory_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 three-letter country code identifying the geographic territory in which this rights window is valid. Drives blackout rule enforcement and geo-restriction logic at the CDN layer.']
  territory_scope varchar [note: 'Indicates the geographic breadth of the rights window beyond a single country code. Supports multi-territory deal structures where a single window covers a defined group of markets.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this rights window record was last modified. Supports change data capture (CDC) pipelines on the Databricks Silver Layer and audit trail requirements under SOX.']
  window_end timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time (exclusive) after which the licensee\'s broadcast rights under this window expire. Nullable for perpetual or open-ended windows. Critical for automated rights expiry enforcement.']
  window_reference_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric business identifier for this rights window, used in contracts, invoices, and rights management systems. Enables cross-system reconciliation with SAP S/4HANA and Dalet Galaxy.']
  window_start timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time (inclusive) from which the licensee is permitted to broadcast content under this rights window. Used for real-time rights enforcement at the CDN and DRM layer.']
  window_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the broadcast rights window. Drives rights enforcement logic: only \'active\' windows permit broadcast. Transitions follow deal negotiation and regulatory approval workflows.']

  Note: 'Granular rights window records derived from media rights deals, defining the specific time periods, platforms, and territories in which a rights holder may broadcast specific content. Captures rights deal reference, content category, platform type, territory, window start/end, exclusivity flag, sublicensing permission, and rights fee allocation. Enables precise rights enforcement at the content-platform-territory level.'
}
