  graphics_package_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the on-screen graphics package (lower thirds, scoreboards, transitions, sponsor overlays) deployed for this production. Critical for brand consistency and sponsor compliance.']
  is_ppv boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this broadcast production is packaged and distributed as a Pay-Per-View (PPV) event, requiring individual purchase by viewers. Drives revenue recognition and DRM policy application.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this broadcast production record in the source system or Silver layer. Supports change tracking, SLA monitoring, and incremental ETL processing.']
  market_designation varchar [note: 'Geographic market or territory for which this production is designated (e.g., \'US-National\', \'RSN-Northeast\', \'UK\', \'LATAM-Spanish\'). Governs blackout rules, rights windows, and distribution platform routing. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: promote to reference product for standardized market codes]']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text operational notes entered by the production team in Dalet Galaxy, capturing special instructions, contingency plans, or event-specific considerations for this broadcast production.']
  ob_unit_identifier varchar [note: 'Identifier of the Outside Broadcast (OB) truck or mobile production unit deployed for remote productions. Null for studio-based productions. Critical for equipment logistics and vendor billing.']
  play_by_play_announcer varchar [note: 'Full name of the primary play-by-play announcer assigned to this production\'s commentary team. Key talent assignment tracked for scheduling, NIL compliance, and audience analytics.']
  postshow_duration_minutes varchar [note: 'Planned duration in minutes of the post-show segment following the main event broadcast. Used for crew scheduling, sponsor slot allocation, and runsheet planning.']
  ppv_price decimal(10,2) [note: 'Retail price charged to consumers for access to this Pay-Per-View (PPV) broadcast production. Null for non-PPV productions. Used in revenue management and financial reporting.']
  preshow_duration_minutes varchar [note: 'Planned duration in minutes of the pre-show segment preceding the main event broadcast. Used for crew scheduling, sponsor slot allocation, and runsheet planning.']
  production_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric code assigned by Dalet Galaxy to uniquely identify this broadcast production instance across systems and workflows. Used in runsheets, crew calls, and equipment manifests.']
  production_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the broadcast production instance within the Dalet Galaxy workflow. \'draft\' = initial planning; \'confirmed\' = crew and equipment locked; \'in_production\' = active pre-show or live execution; \'on_air\' = currently broadcasting; \'completed\' = post-production wrap; \'cancelled\' = production cancelled.']
  production_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the broadcast production method. \'live\' = real-time event coverage; \'studio\' = studio-based production; \'remote_ob\' = Outside Broadcast (OB) unit at venue; \'tape_delay\' = recorded and broadcast later; \'highlight\' = post-event highlight package. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: live|studio|remote_ob|tape_delay|highlight|ppv — promote to reference product if additional types emerge]']
  replay_availability_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when replay or VOD content for this production becomes available to viewers on designated platforms. Null if replay_content_available is false. Governs rights window compliance.']
  replay_content_available boolean [note: 'Indicates whether replay/VOD content will be made available following the live broadcast of this production. Drives post-production workflow initiation and rights window activation in the content domain.']
  rsn_affiliate_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the Regional Sports Network (RSN) affiliate carrying this production for regional linear TV distribution. Null for national or OTT-only productions.']
  scheduled_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Planned date and time when the broadcast production is scheduled to conclude, including post-show segments. Used for rights window management and crew scheduling.']
  scheduled_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Planned date and time (with timezone offset) when the broadcast production is scheduled to begin, including pre-show setup. Principal business event timestamp for scheduling and rights window alignment.']
  sideline_reporter_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the sideline reporter assigned to this production. May be null for studio-only productions. Tracked for talent scheduling and credential management.']
  signal_format varchar [note: 'Technical broadcast signal format used for this production (e.g., HD, 4K, 8K, SD, HDR). Determines equipment requirements, CDN bandwidth allocation, and platform delivery specifications.']
  sponsor_billboard_count varchar [note: 'Number of sponsor billboard/branding slots allocated within this production\'s broadcast runsheet. Used for sponsorship fulfillment tracking and CPM reporting.']
  title varchar [note: 'Human-readable title of the broadcast production (e.g., \'NFL Sunday Night Football – Week 12 – Chiefs vs Eagles\'). Used in scheduling systems, crew communications, and broadcast runsheets.']

  Note: 'Operational record of each broadcast production instance for a scheduled event, including crew and commentary team assignments. Captures production type (live, studio, remote OB), director, technical producer, production company, equipment manifest, venue technical setup, graphics package version, pre-show/post-show segments, production status, and commentary team composition (play-by-play, color analyst, sideline reporter) with language and market designations. Links broadcast schedule to physical production execution. Sourced from Dalet Galaxy broadcast workflow.'
}

Table broadcast.audience_rating {
  audience_rating_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each audience rating measurement record in the broadcast domain. Primary key for the audience_rating data product.']
  athlete_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to athlete.athlete_profile. Business justification: Broadcasters and rights holders segment audience ratings by featured athlete to measure broadcast value, negotiate future rights fees, and set ad rates. Athlete-level ratings analytics (e.g., LeBron e']
  broadcast_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the scheduled broadcast window for which this audience measurement was captured. Links the rating record to the specific broadcast slot.']
  channel_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the broadcast channel or streaming platform on which the content was delivered (e.g., linear TV network, OTT service, RSN feed).']
  data_processing_record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.data_processing_record. Business justification: Audience rating measurement collects viewer demographic and behavioral data (demo_age_band, demo_gender, demo_income_band), triggering GDPR/CCPA data processing obligations. Ratings records must refer']
  deal_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sponsorship.deal. Business justification: Audience ratings validate GRP and impression delivery commitments specified in sponsorship deals. Linking enables deal performance reporting and guarantee reconciliation — a standard post-broadcast sp']
  franchise_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to league.franchise. Business justification: Franchise-level audience ratings (\'Team X games average Y million viewers\') are a standard sports broadcast metric used directly in rights renewal negotiations and franchise valuation. A domain expert']
  live_feed_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to broadcast.live_feed. Business justification: Audience ratings measure viewership for a specific live feed. While audience_rating already links to broadcast_schedule, the direct link to live_feed enables feed-level audience measurement, supportin']
  match_result_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to event.match_result. Business justification: Audience ratings are measured for specific match results — post-match ratings reports, advertiser delivery confirmation, and rights fulfillment analytics all require linking rating records to the matc']
  media_asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to broadcast.media_asset. Business justification: audience_rating has asset_id → content.asset (cross-domain) but no FK to broadcast.media_asset. The in-domain media_asset contains broadcast-specific metadata (resolution, format, rights) needed for a']
  media_rights_deal_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to broadcast.media_rights_deal. Business justification: Audience ratings track guarantee fulfillment against media rights deals. The audience_guarantee_target and guarantee_met fields on audience_rating are deal-level contractual metrics — the deal specifi']
  platform_channel_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to content.platform_channel. Business justification: Audience ratings are measured per delivery platform. Linking audience_rating to content.platform_channel enables platform-level ratings reporting, which is required for audience guarantee validation i']
  primary_audience_asset_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the content asset or media item that was broadcast during the measured window, enabling rights valuation and scheduling optimization.']
  rights_window_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to broadcast.broadcast_rights_window. Business justification: Audience ratings are measured within specific broadcast rights windows. The rights window defines the time period (window_start, window_end), territory (territory_code), and content type (rights_categ']
  season_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to league.season. Business justification: Season-level ratings aggregation is a core input to rights fee renewal negotiations and league revenue sharing reports. Rights holders and licensees require season-scoped ratings data to evaluate deal']
  tournament_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to event.tournament. Business justification: Tournament-level audience ratings are aggregated for rights renewal negotiations, sponsor reporting, and broadcast deal valuation. Rights holders and licensees require tournament-level viewership data']
  ama_viewers varchar [note: 'Average Minute Audience — the average number of viewers watching at any given minute during the broadcast window. The industry-standard linear TV metric used for GRP (Gross Rating Point) calculation and advertiser CPM (Cost Per Mille) pricing.']
  audience_guarantee_target varchar [note: 'Contractually committed minimum audience size (total viewers) for this broadcast window as specified in the media rights or advertising agreement. Used for contractual audience guarantee reconciliation against actual total_viewers. Confidential as it reflects negotiated contract terms.']
  avg_viewing_duration_mins decimal(8,2) [note: 'Average number of minutes each viewer spent watching the broadcast during the measurement window. Used for audience engagement analysis, content scheduling optimization, and streaming platform retention reporting.']
  blackout_applied boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a broadcast blackout rule was in effect for this territory during the measurement window. Blackout rules are contractual restrictions in media rights agreements that limit broadcast in specific markets. Affects audience measurement scope.']
  broadcast_date date [note: 'Calendar date of the broadcast window in yyyy-MM-dd format. Used for day-level scheduling analysis, weekly ratings aggregation, and contractual audience guarantee reconciliation.']
  content_genre varchar [note: 'Genre classification of the broadcast content (e.g., Live Sport, Sports News, Sports Documentary, Concert, Esports, Magazine Show). Used for scheduling optimization and audience segmentation analysis. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Live Sport|Sports News|Sports Documentary|Concert|Esports|Magazine Show|Highlights|Other — promote to reference product]']
  cpm_rate decimal(10,4) [note: 'Cost Per Mille — the advertising rate charged per 1,000 viewers for this broadcast window, in the applicable currency. Used for advertiser rate card justification and media rights deal valuation. Confidential as it reflects negotiated commercial pricing.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this audience rating record was first ingested into the Silver Layer data product. Supports data lineage, audit trail, and SLA (Service Level Agreement) compliance for measurement data delivery.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code applicable to the CPM rate and any monetary values in this record (e.g., USD, GBP, EUR, AUD). Required for multi-territory rights valuation and financial reporting.']
  demo_age_band varchar [note: 'Age band of the primary target demographic for this audience measurement record. Standard age groupings used by Nielsen and BARB for advertiser targeting and rate card segmentation (e.g., Adults 18-49 is the primary US broadcast demographic).']
  demo_gender varchar [note: 'Gender segment of the audience demographic breakdown for this measurement record. Used for targeted advertising rate card differentiation and sponsor demographic guarantee reporting.']
  demo_income_band varchar [note: 'Household income band of the measured audience segment (e.g., Under $50K, $50K-$100K, $100K-$150K, $150K+). Used for premium advertiser targeting and sponsorship package valuation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Under $50K|$50K-$100K|$100K-$150K|$150K-$200K|$200K+ — promote to reference product]']
  demo_viewers_count varchar [note: 'Number of viewers within the specific demographic segment (age band, gender, income band) defined for this record. Enables granular demographic reporting for advertiser guarantee reconciliation.']
  drm_protected boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the broadcast content was delivered with DRM (Digital Rights Management) protection active during the measurement window. Relevant for OTT and IPTV platform compliance and piracy impact assessment on audience figures.']
  frequency decimal(8,3) [note: 'Average number of times a unique viewer was exposed to the broadcast during the measurement period. Used alongside reach to calculate GRP (Gross Rating Point) and assess advertising campaign effectiveness.']
  grp_value decimal(10,3) [note: 'Gross Rating Point value for this broadcast window, representing the sum of ratings across the target audience. Calculated as rating_pct multiplied by frequency. Primary currency for advertising inventory pricing and sponsorship valuation.']
  guarantee_met boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the actual audience (total_viewers) met or exceeded the contractual audience guarantee target for this broadcast window. Drives make-good advertising inventory decisions and rights agreement penalty calculations.']
  live_viewers varchar [note: 'Number of viewers who watched the broadcast in real-time (live) as opposed to time-shifted. Distinguishes live audience from time-shifted for PPV event reporting and live sports rights valuation.']
  market_name varchar [note: 'Name of the designated market area (DMA) or broadcast market within the territory (e.g., New York DMA, London ITV Region, Sydney Metro). Used for sub-national audience reporting and RSN rate card justification.']
  measurement_methodology varchar [note: 'The data collection methodology used by the measurement source. Panel-based (Nielsen People Meter, BARB panel) uses a representative sample; Census measures all streams; Hybrid combines panel and census; Modeled uses statistical estimation; Self-Reported uses platform-provided data.']
  measurement_reference_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique identifier assigned by the measurement provider (e.g., Nielsen panel ID, BARB survey code, OzTAM report reference, Comscore stream ID) for cross-system reconciliation.']
  measurement_source varchar [note: 'The third-party or internal system that provided the audience measurement data. Determines methodology, panel composition, and credibility for advertiser and rights negotiations. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Nielsen|BARB|OzTAM|Comscore|Internal Streaming Analytics|Other — promote to reference product if additional sources are onboarded]']
  measurement_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the audience rating record. Preliminary ratings are released within 24 hours; confirmed ratings are validated by the measurement provider; revised ratings incorporate late-reported data; final ratings are locked for contractual use; disputed ratings are under review.']
  peak_concurrent_viewers varchar [note: 'Maximum number of viewers simultaneously watching the broadcast at any single minute during the window. Key metric for streaming platform capacity planning, CDN (Content Delivery Network) provisioning, and PPV event valuation.']
  rating_pct decimal(6,3) [note: 'Percentage of the total universe of TV households or individuals that watched the broadcast, regardless of whether their TV was on. Distinct from share_pct which is based only on those actively viewing. Used for GRP (Gross Rating Point) calculation.']
  rating_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the audience measurement metric captured in this record. GRP (Gross Rating Point) represents total delivery; CPM (Cost Per Mille) supports advertiser rate card justification; Reach measures unique audience; Frequency measures average exposures; AMA (Average Minute Audience) is the standard linear TV metric; Share is the percentage of viewing universe. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: GRP|CPM|Reach|Frequency|Viewership|AMA|Share — promote to reference product]']
  reach varchar [note: 'Total number of unique individuals who viewed at least one minute of the broadcast during the measured window. Distinct from total_viewers which may count repeat viewing. Used for unduplicated audience reporting to sponsors and rights holders.']
  reporting_period_type varchar [note: 'Defines the time-shifted viewing window included in this measurement record per Nielsen and BARB standards. Live = real-time only; Live+1 includes same-day DVR; Live+3 includes 3-day playback; Live+7 includes 7-day playback; Live+35 is the C35 standard; VOD is on-demand only.']
  share_pct decimal(6,3) [note: 'Percentage of the total viewing universe (all households/individuals with TV/streaming access) that watched this broadcast during the window. Expressed as a decimal percentage (e.g., 12.500 = 12.5%). Core metric for competitive scheduling analysis.']
  sport_type varchar [note: 'The sport or entertainment category of the broadcast content (e.g., NFL Football, NBA Basketball, FIFA Soccer, UFC MMA, Concert). Enables sport-specific audience trend analysis and rights valuation benchmarking. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: NFL Football|NBA Basketball|MLB Baseball|NHL Hockey|MLS Soccer|FIFA Soccer|UFC MMA|Tennis|Concert|Other — promote to reference product]']
  territory_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code identifying the geographic territory in which the audience measurement was conducted. Critical for regional rights valuation, blackout rule enforcement, and RSN (Regional Sports Network) reporting.']
  time_shifted_viewers varchar [note: 'Number of viewers who watched the broadcast on a time-shifted basis (DVR, catch-up, VOD replay) within the measurement window. Reported separately per Nielsen Live+3 and Live+7 standards for contractual audience guarantee reconciliation.']
  total_viewers varchar [note: 'Total number of individual viewers who watched the broadcast during the measured window. The primary headline metric used in media rights negotiations, advertiser rate cards, and contractual audience guarantees.']
  universe_size varchar [note: 'Total size of the viewing universe (total TV households or individuals) used as the denominator for rating_pct and share_pct calculations in the given territory and demographic. Provided by the measurement source.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this audience rating record was last modified, typically when preliminary ratings are revised or confirmed by the measurement provider. Supports audit trail and revised-rating reconciliation workflows.']
  window_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time when the measured broadcast window ended. Combined with window_start_timestamp to define the exact measurement interval for AMA and share calculations.']
  window_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time when the measured broadcast window began, in ISO 8601 format with timezone offset. Used as the principal business event timestamp for scheduling optimization and rights valuation.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of broadcast audience measurement data per scheduled broadcast window. Captures channel, territory, measurement source (Nielsen, BARB, OzTAM, Comscore, internal streaming analytics), rating type (GRP, CPM, reach, frequency, viewership), total viewers, demographic breakdown (age, gender, income), peak concurrent viewers, average minute audience (AMA), share percentage, and time-shifted viewing. Operational data used for rights valuation, advertiser rate card justification, scheduling optimization, and contractual audience guarantee reconciliation.'
}

  website_url varchar [note: 'Official website URL for the rights holder organization.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for rights_holder. Referenced by rights_holder_id.'
}

Table sponsorship.sponsor {
  sponsor_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the sponsoring organization or brand. Primary key for the sponsor master record across all commercial and activation workflows.']
  operator_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to gaming.operator. Business justification: Gaming operators (DraftKings, FanDuel) are major sponsors. Compliance and exclusivity enforcement require knowing if a sponsor is a licensed gaming operator. Sponsorship teams screen operator license ']
  account_tier varchar [note: 'Commercial tier classification of the sponsorship relationship indicating the level of investment, exclusivity, and activation rights. Title sponsors receive the highest prominence; associate sponsors receive baseline rights. Drives benefit entitlement and activation priority.']
  activation_region varchar [note: 'Geographic scope of the sponsor\'s activation rights (e.g., \'Global\', \'North America\', \'USA\', \'GBR\', \'DEU\'). Determines where brand activations, broadcast integrations, and digital placements may be executed. Uses ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 for country-level or named region for multi-country scopes.']
  agency_name varchar [note: 'Name of the sports marketing or media agency representing the sponsor in deal negotiations and activation management (e.g., IMG, Octagon, WME Sports). Relevant when the sponsor engages through an intermediary rather than directly.']
  annual_investment_usd decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total contracted annual cash investment value of the sponsorship in US Dollars. Represents the base financial commitment excluding value-in-kind (VIK) contributions. Used for revenue forecasting, EBITDA (Earnings Before Interest Taxes Depreciation and Amortization) reporting, and tier qualification.']
  brand_name varchar [note: 'Commercial or trade name of the sponsor brand as displayed in activations, signage, broadcast integrations, and fan-facing materials. May differ from the legal entity name (e.g., parent company vs. product brand).']
  broadcast_integration_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this sponsor has contracted broadcast integration rights including virtual signage, sponsored segments, on-screen billboards, or OTT (Over-The-Top Streaming) branded content placements.']
  category_exclusivity_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this sponsor holds exclusive rights within their industry category, preventing competing brands in the same category from entering into sponsorship agreements. Critical for conflict-of-interest checks during new deal prospecting.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'UTC timestamp when the sponsor master record was first created in the system. Supports data lineage, audit trail requirements, and SOX financial control documentation.']
  crm_account_code varchar [note: 'Salesforce CRM Account record identifier linking this sponsor to the authoritative B2B relationship record in Salesforce CRM. Used for bi-directional sync of contact, opportunity, and activity data.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which the sponsorship contract is denominated (e.g., USD, GBP, EUR). Used for multi-currency financial reporting and FX conversion in consolidated revenue statements.']
  data_privacy_consent_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the sponsoring organization\'s primary contact has provided consent for data processing activities under applicable privacy regulations. Required for GDPR-compliant B2B contact data management in EU markets.']
  digital_platform_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this sponsor has contracted digital platform partnership rights including DTC (Direct-To-Consumer) app integrations, social media activations, OTT branded content, or API-driven data partnerships.']
  exclusivity_category varchar [note: 'The specific product or service category for which this sponsor holds exclusivity rights (e.g., \'Official Beer\', \'Official Automotive Partner\', \'Official Financial Services Partner\'). Populated only when category_exclusivity_flag is true. Used to enforce category conflict checks during new deal origination.']
  gdpr_applicable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether GDPR (General Data Protection Regulation) applies to this sponsor relationship based on the sponsor\'s headquarters jurisdiction or the markets in which activations are executed. Triggers enhanced data handling and consent management workflows.']
  headquarters_city varchar [note: 'City of the sponsor\'s global headquarters. Used for geographic market analysis, regional partnership segmentation, and event proximity reporting.']
  headquarters_country varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the sponsor\'s global headquarters. Used for jurisdictional compliance (GDPR, CCPA), tax treaty application, and international partnership reporting.']
  headquarters_state_province varchar [note: 'State or province of the sponsor\'s global headquarters. Used for regional market segmentation, tax jurisdiction determination, and compliance with state-level privacy laws such as CCPA.']
  industry_category varchar [note: 'Primary industry sector of the sponsoring organization using standard classification (e.g., Automotive, Financial Services, Consumer Packaged Goods, Technology, Telecommunications, Apparel). Used for category exclusivity management and competitive conflict checks. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: automotive|financial_services|consumer_packaged_goods|technology|telecommunications|apparel|healthcare|food_beverage|insurance|retail|energy|media — promote to reference product]']
  is_publicly_traded boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the sponsoring organization is a publicly traded company listed on a recognized stock exchange. Drives SEC disclosure obligations, financial due diligence requirements, and SOX compliance considerations.']
  jersey_sponsorship_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this sponsor holds jersey or kit sponsorship rights for one or more teams or athletes. Jersey sponsorships carry premium brand visibility and are governed by league IP and uniform regulations.']
  legal_name varchar [note: 'Full registered legal name of the sponsoring organization as it appears on executed contracts and regulatory filings. Used for invoicing, contract execution, and compliance reporting.']
  naics_code varchar [note: 'Six-digit NAICS code identifying the sponsor\'s primary business activity. Used for industry benchmarking, regulatory reporting, and category exclusivity conflict detection.']
  naming_rights_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this sponsor holds naming rights for one or more venues. Naming rights are among the highest-value sponsorship assets and require specific contractual, signage, and broadcast integration management.']
  nil_partnership_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this sponsor has active NIL (Name, Image, and Likeness) agreements with individual athletes managed through the sports-entertainment ecosystem. NIL deals require separate compliance tracking under applicable state laws and league policies.']
  nps_score varchar [note: 'Most recent NPS (Net Promoter Score) survey result from the sponsoring organization, ranging from -100 to 100. Measures sponsor satisfaction and likelihood to renew or expand the partnership. Collected via annual partner satisfaction surveys.']
  parent_organization_name varchar [note: 'Name of the ultimate parent corporation in the sponsor\'s corporate hierarchy. Supports consolidated revenue reporting, conflict-of-interest checks, and category exclusivity enforcement across subsidiary brands.']
  preferred_language varchar [note: 'BCP 47 language tag representing the sponsor\'s preferred language for contract communications, activation briefs, and reporting (e.g., \'en\', \'fr\', \'de\', \'es\'). Supports multilingual partnership management across global markets.']
  prospecting_source varchar [note: 'Channel or method through which this sponsor was originally identified or acquired. Used for CAC (Customer Acquisition Cost) analysis, sales pipeline attribution, and partnership development strategy reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: inbound|outbound|referral|agency|renewal|conference|other — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  relationship_start_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the first commercial sponsorship relationship with this organization was established. Supports tenure calculations, loyalty tier assessments, and long-term partner recognition programs.']
  relationship_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the sponsor relationship. Drives workflow routing, activation eligibility, and CRM pipeline reporting. Prospect indicates pre-contract; active indicates a live deal; suspended indicates a temporary hold; terminated indicates a closed relationship.']
  renewal_probability decimal(5,2) [note: 'Estimated probability (0.00–100.00) that the sponsor will renew their agreement at the end of the current contract term. Assessed by the account manager and updated in Salesforce CRM. Used for revenue forecasting and pipeline management.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record from which this sponsor master record was originated or last updated. Supports data lineage tracking and master data management reconciliation across Salesforce CRM and SAP S/4HANA.']
  stock_ticker varchar [note: 'Exchange-listed stock ticker symbol for publicly traded sponsoring organizations. Used for financial health monitoring, SEC disclosure cross-referencing, and investor relations reporting.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'UTC timestamp when the sponsor master record was most recently modified. Used for change data capture (CDC), data freshness monitoring, and audit trail compliance.']
  value_in_kind_usd decimal(18,2) [note: 'Estimated USD value of non-cash contributions provided by the sponsor in lieu of or in addition to cash investment (e.g., product supply, media inventory, technology services). Included in total deal value calculations and EBITDA reporting.']
  wada_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this sponsor is subject to WADA (World Anti-Doping Agency) compliance requirements due to NIL athlete partnerships or direct involvement in athlete performance programs. Triggers PED (Performance Enhancing Drug) policy review obligations.']
  website_url varchar [note: 'Official public website URL of the sponsoring organization or brand. Used for digital activation link-throughs, brand verification, and partner directory listings.']

  Note: 'Master record for all sponsoring organizations and brands in the sports-entertainment ecosystem. Captures sponsor identity, corporate hierarchy, industry classification, account tier (title, presenting, official, associate), CRM account linkage from Salesforce, contact details, and relationship status. SSOT for sponsor identity across all commercial and activation workflows.'
}

Table sponsorship.deal {
  deal_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the sponsorship deal record in the Databricks Silver Layer. Primary key for the deal entity.']
  budget_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.budget. Business justification: Sponsorship deals are planned against finance budgets for annual revenue forecasting and variance reporting. Sports finance teams track contracted deal value vs. budgeted sponsorship revenue by season']
  compliance_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.obligation. Business justification: Sponsorship deals create specific compliance obligations (WADA NIL rules, FCC broadcast disclosure, GDPR data sharing). Linking a deal to its primary compliance obligation enables obligation tracking,']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Sponsorship deals incur costs (activation, fulfillment, account management) tracked against cost centers for P&L reporting by business unit. Finance requires cost center assignment on deals for budget']
  fan_segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking this targeting commitment to the fan segment being targeted under the sponsorship deal.']
  franchise_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to league.franchise. Business justification: Franchise-level sponsorship deals (jersey patches, team naming rights, franchise broadcast integrations) are a primary commercial structure in sports. Deal.league_id exists but franchise-specific deal']
  governing_body_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.governing_body. Business justification: Sponsorship deals (naming rights, broadcast integrations, NIL) must comply with the rules of the relevant governing body (NFL, FIFA, FCC). Deal managers need to know which governing body\'s rules apply']
  jurisdiction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to gaming.gaming_jurisdiction. Business justification: Sponsorship deals with betting operators are jurisdiction-scoped — activations are only permitted in jurisdictions where the operator holds a license. Compliance teams validate deal activations agains']
  league_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the league or competition entity associated with this sponsorship deal. Applicable for league-level sponsorships, official partner designations, and league division naming rights.']
  media_rights_deal_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to broadcast.media_rights_deal. Business justification: Sponsorship deals with broadcast integration rights must be validated against the underlying media rights deal covering that territory and platform. Linking enables compliance checking that sponsorshi']
  operator_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to gaming.operator. Business justification: Sponsorship deals with gaming operators require referencing operator license status, responsible gaming certifications, and jurisdiction coverage for regulatory compliance and deal structuring. Legal ']
  policy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.policy. Business justification: Sponsorship deals must comply with internal policies (brand safety, data privacy, anti-doping, anti-corruption). Linking a deal to its governing compliance policy enables policy version tracking, atte']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Sponsorship deal revenue is attributed to specific profit centers (by league, property, or region) for segment reporting and management accounts. Finance requires profit center assignment on deals for']
  season_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to league.season. Business justification: Sponsorship deals are season-scoped contracts — annual partnerships, season-long broadcast integrations, and performance obligations are all tied to a specific league season. Season-level deal reporti']
  sponsor_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the sponsor (brand or corporate entity) that is party to this commercial agreement. Links to the sponsor master record.']
  venue_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the venue or facility asset associated with this deal, particularly for naming rights and venue-based activation deals. Links to the venue master record.']
  activation_deliverables_summary varchar [note: 'High-level summary of the key activation deliverables committed under this deal (e.g., jersey patch, LED signage, broadcast mentions, social media posts, hospitality packages). Supports fulfillment tracking and partner reporting.']
  annual_contract_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Annualized monetary value of the sponsorship deal, derived from TCV divided by contract term years. Used for ARR reporting, budget planning in Workday Adaptive Planning, and year-over-year comparisons.']
  arpu_impact_estimate decimal(10,2) [note: 'Estimated incremental ARPU attributable to the digital sponsorship activation, expressed in deal currency. Used for DTC and OTT partnership ROI modeling and Workday Adaptive Planning forecasts.']
  audience_commitment_size varchar [note: 'The contractually committed number of fan profiles from this segment that the deal is obligated to reach during the targeting flight window. This is a per-segment-per-deal contractual figure that belongs to the targeting relationship, not to the deal total or the segment\'s overall fan_count.']
  audience_delivery_status varchar [note: 'Current fulfillment state of the audience commitment for this segment-deal pairing. Tracks whether the contractual audience reach obligation is being met. Belongs to the targeting relationship — a segment may be fully delivered under one deal while underdelivered under another simultaneously.']
  broadcast_mention_requirement varchar [note: 'Contractual specification for how and how often the sponsor or named asset must be verbally or visually referenced during broadcast coverage (e.g., \'Minimum 3 verbal mentions per broadcast hour\'). Applicable to naming rights and broadcast integration deals.']
  campaign_flight_end_date date [note: 'End date of the active campaign flight period for digital partnership activations. Defines the window during which impression and CTR commitments must be fulfilled.']
  campaign_flight_start_date date [note: 'Start date of the active campaign flight period for digital partnership activations. May differ from the overall deal start date if the digital campaign runs within a sub-window of the contract term.']
  contract_document_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier or URL to the executed contract document stored in the document management system. Enables audit trail and legal review access for SOX and SEC compliance.']
  cpm_rate decimal(10,4) [note: 'Negotiated Cost Per Mille (CPM) rate for digital and broadcast impression-based activations under this deal, expressed in deal currency per 1,000 impressions. Used for ROI benchmarking and media value reporting.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the deal record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail, data lineage, and pipeline age reporting.']
  ctr_target decimal(5,4) [note: 'Contractually committed click-through rate target for digital sponsorship activations, expressed as a decimal proportion (e.g., 0.0250 = 2.50%). Applicable to digital platform partnership deals. Used for performance benchmarking and ROI reporting.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary values on this deal (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Required for multi-territory deals and SAP FI currency translation.']
  data_sharing_consent_scope varchar [note: 'GDPR/CCPA-compliant scope of fan data that may be shared with the sponsor under this deal. Governs data exchange in digital platform partnerships and Adobe Experience Platform personalization activations. Values: none, anonymized_aggregate, pseudonymized, identified_opt_in, full_consent.']
  deal_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable commercial name of the sponsorship deal (e.g., \'Acme Corp Jersey Sponsorship 2025\', \'GlobalBank Arena Naming Rights\'). Used in reporting, presentations, and CRM.']
  deal_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the sponsorship deal by commercial category. Determines which sub-type attributes are applicable and drives activation workflows. Values: jersey_sponsorship, venue_naming_rights, broadcast_integration, digital_platform_partnership, presenting_sponsor, official_partner.']
  digital_partnership_type varchar [note: 'Sub-classification of digital platform partnership deals. Specifies the nature of the digital activation: app title sponsor, OTT streaming sponsor, social media co-branding, DTC campaign integration, API data partnership, or Adobe Experience Platform personalization. Values: app_title_sponsor, ott_streaming_sponsor, social_media_co_branding, dtc_campaign_integration, api_data_partnership, adobe_personalization.']
  digital_property_url varchar [note: 'Web URL of the digital property associated with the deal, such as a renamed stadium website, OTT platform landing page, or DTC campaign microsite. Applicable to naming rights and digital platform partnership deals.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'The date on which this segment targeting commitment expires under the sponsorship deal. May differ from the deal\'s overall end_date if segment targeting is scoped to a specific campaign flight.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'The date on which this segment targeting commitment becomes active under the sponsorship deal. May differ from the deal\'s overall start_date if segment targeting is phased or campaign-flight-specific.']
  end_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the sponsorship deal expires and contractual obligations cease. Nullable for multi-year rolling agreements. Drives renewal pipeline alerts.']
  exclusivity_category varchar [note: 'Industry or product category within which the sponsor holds exclusive rights (e.g., \'Financial Services\', \'Automotive\', \'Telecommunications\'). Prevents conflicting sponsor activations within the same category. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: financial_services|automotive|telecommunications|beverage|apparel|technology|insurance|energy|retail|healthcare — promote to reference product]']
  exclusivity_scope varchar [note: 'Geographic or organizational scope of the sponsor\'s exclusivity rights. Determines where competing sponsors in the same category may not be activated. Values: global, national, regional, local, league, team, event. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: global|national|regional|local|league|team|event — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  executed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time at which the sponsorship deal was formally executed (signed by all parties). Represents the principal business event timestamp for this agreement. Used for revenue recognition and legal effective date tracking.']
  grp_commitment decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total Gross Rating Points (GRP) committed to the sponsor for broadcast and media activations under this deal. GRP = Reach % × Frequency. Used for broadcast integration performance tracking and ROI reporting.']
  hospitality_entitlements varchar [note: 'Description of VIP hospitality entitlements included in the deal (e.g., number of suite tickets, GA tickets, VIP passes, meet-and-greet access per event). Managed through Archtics venue inventory.']
  impression_commitment varchar [note: 'Total number of guaranteed digital or broadcast impressions committed to the sponsor under the deal terms. Used for CPM and GRP performance tracking and ROI reporting.']
  ip_rights_included boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the deal grants the sponsor rights to use Sports Entertainment\'s Intellectual Property (IP), including logos, trademarks, team marks, and league marks in their own marketing and advertising.']
  named_asset varchar [note: 'For venue naming rights deals: the specific asset whose name is being licensed to the sponsor (e.g., stadium, arena, practice facility, trophy, award, league division). Null or \'none\' for non-naming-rights deal types. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: stadium|arena|practice_facility|trophy|award|league_division|none — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  nil_rights_included boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the deal includes Name, Image, and Likeness (NIL) rights for athletes or talent associated with the sponsoring entity\'s activations. Relevant for athlete endorsement components of sponsorship deals.']
  payment_schedule_type varchar [note: 'Defines the contractual payment cadence for the deal\'s total contract value. Drives accounts receivable scheduling in SAP S/4HANA FI and cash flow forecasting in Workday Adaptive Planning.']
  renewal_option_deadline date [note: 'Date by which the sponsor must exercise their contractual renewal option. Null if renewal_option_flag is false. Drives CRM renewal pipeline alerts in Salesforce.']
  renewal_option_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the deal includes a contractual renewal option clause that grants the sponsor the right of first refusal or automatic renewal at defined terms.']
  renewal_option_terms varchar [note: 'Summary description of the contractual renewal option terms, including any escalation clauses, right-of-first-refusal conditions, or pricing adjustments applicable upon renewal.']
  salesforce_opportunity_code varchar [note: 'Reference to the originating opportunity record in Salesforce CRM (Fan and Sponsor Relationship Management). Enables traceability from prospecting through deal execution.']
  signage_specification varchar [note: 'Description of physical or digital signage deliverables committed under the deal, including placement locations, dimensions, and display frequency (e.g., \'LED perimeter board, 4 placements, 30-second rotation\'). Applicable to venue and broadcast deals.']
  stage varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle stage of the sponsorship deal from initial prospecting through renewal. Drives pipeline reporting and revenue recognition scheduling. Values: prospecting, negotiation, executed, active, expired, renewed.']
  start_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the sponsorship deal becomes effective and contractual obligations commence. Used for revenue recognition scheduling and activation planning.']
  targeting_priority varchar [note: 'The priority rank of this fan segment within the deal\'s overall audience targeting strategy. Indicates whether this segment is the primary audience commitment or a supplemental targeting layer. Belongs to the relationship, not to the deal or segment independently.']
  territory varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code representing the primary territory in which the sponsorship deal is active and rights are granted. For multi-territory deals, references the primary market.']
  total_contract_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total committed monetary value of the sponsorship deal across its full term, expressed in the deal currency. Key metric for pipeline valuation, EBITDA forecasting, and ARR reporting.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the deal record was last modified. Used for change data capture (CDC) in the Databricks Silver Layer pipeline and audit compliance.']

  Note: 'Core sponsorship deal record representing a negotiated commercial agreement between Sports Entertainment and a sponsor. Captures deal name, deal type (jersey sponsorship, venue naming rights, broadcast integration, digital platform partnership, presenting sponsor, official partner), total contract value (TCV), deal stage (prospecting, negotiation, executed, active, expired, renewed), start and end dates, exclusivity category and scope, territory. For naming rights deals: named asset (stadium, arena, practice facility, trophy, award, league division), signage specifications, broadcast mention requirements, digital property URL changes, and renewal option details. For digital partnerships: partnership type (app title sponsor, OTT streaming sponsor, social media co-branding, DTC campaign integration, API data partnership, Adobe Experience Platform personalization), digital property, impression commitment, CTR target, ARPU impact estimate, GDPR/CCPA data-sharing consent scope, and campaign flight dates. Includes originating Salesforce opportunity reference and renewal option flags. Central entity in the sponsorship lifecycle from prospecting through renewal, encompassing all deal sub-types.'
}

Table sponsorship.deal_term {
  deal_term_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for a sponsorship deal term record in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Primary key for this entity.']
  deal_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent sponsorship deal or contract to which these terms belong. Links deal terms to the overarching sponsorship agreement.']
  franchise_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to league.franchise. Business justification: Deal terms are often franchise-specific — jersey placement obligations, in-arena signage specs, and performance bonuses apply to a specific franchise. Franchise-level term enforcement, compliance audi']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Deal terms specify cash fees, VIK amounts, and performance bonuses that must post to distinct GL accounts (cash sponsorship revenue vs. VIK revenue vs. bonus income). Revenue classification by GL acco']
  governing_body_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.governing_body. Business justification: Individual deal terms (exclusivity clauses, NIL rights, broadcast integration terms) are governed by specific governing body rules. Linking each term to its governing body enables compliance teams to ']
  jurisdiction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to gaming.gaming_jurisdiction. Business justification: Deal terms for betting operator sponsors are jurisdiction-specific: tax treatment, permitted activation scope, and responsible gaming obligations vary by jurisdiction. Legal teams validate deal terms ']
  league_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to league.league. Business justification: Deal terms define contractual obligations (NIL policy, exclusivity, CBA compliance) governed by a specific league. Compliance teams validate deal terms against league rules directly; league context sh']
  license_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to content.license. Business justification: Sponsorship deal terms granting IP or broadcast clip rights must reference the corresponding content license. Rights management teams reconcile deal_term IP rights flags against content.license record']
  nil_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to athlete.nil_agreement. Business justification: Deal terms with nil_athlete_rights_flag govern specific NIL agreements. Contract management and compliance teams must link deal terms to the exact NIL agreement they authorize, enabling NIL rights aud']
  operator_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to gaming.operator. Business justification: Deal terms with gaming operators include operator-specific clauses (license requirements, responsible gaming mandates). Compliance teams reference the operator record when auditing or enforcing indivi']
  policy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.policy. Business justification: Individual deal terms (NIL rights, exclusivity clauses, broadcast integration) must be governed by specific compliance policies (WADA, FCC, GDPR). Sports entertainment compliance teams map contract te']
  product_category_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to merchandise.product_category. Business justification: Sponsorship deal terms contractually specify which merchandise product categories are covered by exclusivity or licensing rights (e.g., apparel rights vs. hard goods). Linking deal_term to product_cat']
  regulatory_filing_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_filing. Business justification: Specific deal terms generate regulatory filings: NIL athlete rights terms require governing body filings, broadcast integration terms require FCC filings, exclusivity terms may require competition aut']
  revenue_recognition_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.revenue_recognition_schedule. Business justification: Deal terms define the performance obligations that drive ASC 606 revenue recognition. Finance maps each deal term to a recognition schedule to determine how to spread contract revenue across periods. ']
  season_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to league.season. Business justification: Deal terms have season-scoped obligations — escalation rates, performance bonus triggers, and GRP delivery commitments are season-bound. Season-level term tracking is required for revenue recognition ']
  sponsor_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the sponsoring organization or brand entity that is party to this deal term. Supports sponsor lifecycle and CRM integration.']
  activation_deliverable_count varchar [note: 'Total number of distinct brand activation deliverables committed under this deal term (e.g., in-venue signage placements, broadcast integrations, digital content pieces, hospitality events). Drives activation fulfillment tracking.']
  broadcast_integration_type varchar [note: 'Specifies the type of broadcast integration deliverable included in this deal term. Virtual_led = virtual pitch-side LED branding; on_screen_graphic = branded lower-third or overlay; presenter_mention = verbal sponsor mention by broadcaster; sponsored_segment = branded content segment; halftime_show = halftime entertainment sponsorship; replay_sponsor = branded replay sequence. Null if term_type is not broadcast_integration.']
  cash_fee_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The portion of the total contract fee to be paid in cash by the sponsor. Distinguishes cash from Value-in-Kind (VIK) contributions for revenue recognition and EBITDA reporting purposes.']
  cpm_rate decimal(10,4) [note: 'The agreed Cost Per Mille (CPM) rate in the contract currency for digital or broadcast advertising inventory delivered under this deal term. CPM represents the cost per 1,000 impressions. Used for digital platform partnership valuation and ROI reporting.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp recording when this deal term record was first created in the lakehouse Silver Layer. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and SOX compliance requirements.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code in which the total contract fee and all monetary obligations under this deal term are denominated (e.g., USD, GBP, EUR). Required for multi-currency SAP S/4HANA FI integration.']
  dispute_resolution_mechanism varchar [note: 'The contractually agreed mechanism for resolving disputes arising under this deal term. Arbitration typically references CAS (Court of Arbitration for Sport) or ICC; litigation refers to national courts; mediation is non-binding; expert_determination applies to technical valuation disputes.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which this deal term expires or ceases to be binding. Nullable for open-ended or evergreen terms. Drives renewal option evaluation and billing cutoff.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which this deal term becomes legally binding and commercially effective. Used for billing schedule generation in SAP S/4HANA SD.']
  escalation_basis varchar [note: 'The basis on which the escalation rate is calculated. Fixed_rate applies a predetermined percentage; cpi_linked ties escalation to the Consumer Price Index; revenue_linked ties it to the organization\'s revenue growth; negotiated means the rate is renegotiated at each interval.']
  escalation_rate_pct decimal(7,4) [note: 'Annual percentage rate by which the contract fee escalates over the term (e.g., 3.00 = 3% per annum). Supports multi-year deal modeling and SAP S/4HANA SD billing plan escalation configuration. Null if no escalation clause applies.']
  exclusivity_category varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the product or service category in which exclusivity is granted (e.g., \'Official Automotive Partner — passenger vehicles\', \'Official Financial Services Partner — retail banking\'). Null if exclusivity_flag is False.']
  exclusivity_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this deal term grants the sponsor category exclusivity, preventing the rights holder from entering into competing sponsorship agreements within the same product or service category during the term. True = exclusive; False = non-exclusive.']
  force_majeure_clause_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this deal term contains a force majeure clause that suspends or modifies obligations in the event of extraordinary circumstances beyond either party\'s control (e.g., pandemic, natural disaster, government prohibition of events). True = force majeure clause present.']
  governing_law_jurisdiction varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 3166-1 Alpha-3 country code identifying the legal jurisdiction whose laws govern this deal term in the event of a dispute (e.g., USA, GBR, CHE). Determines applicable regulatory frameworks including GDPR, CCPA, and local contract law.']
  grp_delivery_commitment decimal(12,2) [note: 'The contractually committed Gross Rating Point (GRP) delivery target for broadcast or media placements associated with this deal term. GRP measures the total advertising weight delivered to the target audience. Used for ROI tracking and make-good calculations.']
  invoice_milestone_count varchar [note: 'Total number of billing milestones or invoice installments defined under this deal term\'s payment schedule. Used to validate completeness of the SAP S/4HANA SD billing plan and track invoicing progress.']
  ip_rights_granted_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this deal term grants the sponsor rights to use the organization\'s Intellectual Property (IP) — including logos, trademarks, athlete likenesses, and league marks — in their own marketing and advertising. True = IP rights granted; False = no IP rights granted. Relevant to NIL (Name, Image, and Likeness) compliance.']
  jersey_placement_location varchar [note: 'Specifies the physical location on the team kit where the sponsor\'s branding is placed under a jersey sponsorship deal term. Null if term_type is not jersey_sponsorship. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: front_chest|back_upper|sleeve_left|sleeve_right|collar|shorts|training_kit|goalkeeper_kit — promote to reference product]']
  kill_clause_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this deal term contains a kill clause (early termination right) that either party may invoke upon breach of defined thresholds. True = kill clause present; False = no kill clause.']
  kill_clause_threshold varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the specific condition or metric threshold that triggers the kill clause (e.g., \'Sponsor brand NPS falls below 20\', \'Broadcast audience drops below 500,000 for 2 consecutive events\', \'Regulatory sanction imposed on either party\'). Null if kill_clause_flag is False.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp recording when this deal term record was most recently modified in the lakehouse Silver Layer. Supports change data capture, audit trail, and SOX compliance.']
  late_payment_penalty_rate_pct decimal(7,4) [note: 'Annual percentage rate applied to overdue sponsor payments as a contractual late payment penalty (e.g., 1.50 = 1.5% per annum on outstanding balance). Null if no late payment penalty clause exists. Used in SAP S/4HANA FI dunning configuration.']
  naming_rights_asset varchar [note: 'Description of the specific asset (venue, stadium, arena, event, competition, or league) for which naming rights are granted under this deal term (e.g., \'Main Stadium — Exterior and Interior Branding\', \'Annual Championship Series\'). Null if term_type is not naming_rights.']
  nil_athlete_rights_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this deal term specifically includes Name, Image, and Likeness (NIL) rights for one or more individual athletes, allowing the sponsor to use athlete identity in commercial activations. True = NIL rights included; False = no individual athlete NIL rights.']
  payment_schedule_type varchar [note: 'Defines the cadence or trigger basis for sponsor payment obligations under this deal term. Annual, quarterly, and monthly are calendar-based; milestone is event-triggered (e.g., season opener, playoff appearance); upfront is a single lump-sum at signing; deferred is back-loaded. Drives SAP S/4HANA SD billing plan configuration. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: annual|semi_annual|quarterly|monthly|milestone|upfront|deferred — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  performance_bonus_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Additional monetary amount payable by the sponsor upon achievement of defined performance milestones (e.g., team reaching playoffs, broadcast audience exceeding GRP threshold, NPS target met). Null if no performance bonus clause exists.']
  performance_bonus_trigger varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the specific performance condition(s) that must be met to trigger the performance bonus payment (e.g., \'Team qualifies for championship final\', \'Broadcast CPM exceeds $25 for 3 consecutive quarters\', \'GRP delivery exceeds 500 in target demographic\').']
  renewal_notice_days varchar [note: 'Number of calendar days prior to the effective end date by which the sponsor or rights holder must provide written notice to exercise the renewal option. Drives automated renewal alert workflows in Salesforce CRM.']
  renewal_option_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this deal term includes a contractual renewal option that the sponsor or rights holder may exercise before expiry. True = renewal option exists; False = no renewal option.']
  renewal_option_periods varchar [note: 'Number of additional renewal periods the sponsor may exercise under the renewal option clause (e.g., 2 = two additional one-year terms). Null if renewal_option_flag is False.']
  rofr_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the sponsor holds a Right of First Refusal (ROFR) on the renewal of this deal term, giving them priority to match any competing offer before the rights are offered to a third party. True = ROFR granted; False = no ROFR.']
  sap_billing_plan_type varchar [note: 'The SAP S/4HANA SD billing plan type code assigned to this deal term, controlling how and when invoices are generated (e.g., \'01\' for milestone billing, \'02\' for periodic billing). Directly maps to SAP billing plan configuration.']
  sap_contract_number varchar [note: 'The SAP S/4HANA SD sales contract document number associated with this deal term, used for billing plan integration, invoice generation, and revenue recognition. Enables traceability between the lakehouse Silver Layer and the ERP system of record.']
  signed_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which this deal term was formally executed and signed by all parties. Represents the principal business event timestamp for the contractual commitment. Used for SOX revenue recognition cutoff and audit trail.']
  term_reference_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric reference code for this deal term record, used in SAP S/4HANA SD billing and contract correspondence. Format: <ORG_CODE>-DT-<SEQUENCE>.']
  term_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the deal term. Draft indicates terms under negotiation; active indicates binding and in-force; suspended indicates temporarily paused (e.g., force majeure); terminated indicates early exit via kill clause; expired indicates natural end-of-term; pending_renewal indicates renewal option under evaluation.']
  term_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the deal term by its commercial nature. Financial covers cash payment obligations; activation covers brand activation deliverables; naming_rights covers venue or event naming; broadcast_integration covers in-broadcast placements; digital_platform covers OTT/DTC digital integrations; jersey_sponsorship covers kit branding rights. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: financial|activation|naming_rights|broadcast_integration|digital_platform|jersey_sponsorship|merchandise_licensing|hospitality — promote to reference product]']
  total_contract_fee decimal(18,2) [note: 'Gross total monetary value of this deal term over its full effective period, expressed in the contract currency. Represents the face value of the sponsorship obligation before any escalation, bonus, or VIK adjustments. Key input for EBITDA and ARR reporting.']
  vik_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The portion of the total contract fee fulfilled through non-cash Value-in-Kind (VIK) contributions such as goods, services, media inventory, or hospitality provided by the sponsor. Tracked separately from cash for accounting and ROI analysis.']
  vik_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the non-cash Value-in-Kind (VIK) goods or services provided by the sponsor as part of their contractual obligation (e.g., \'Official airline partner — 500 business class flights per season\', \'Official vehicle fleet — 50 SUVs for event logistics\').']

  Note: 'Detailed contractual terms and financial obligations associated with a sponsorship deal. Captures payment schedule type (annual, quarterly, milestone), total fee, cash vs. value-in-kind (VIK) split, escalation clauses, performance bonuses, kill-clause thresholds, renewal options, right-of-first-refusal flags, and governing law jurisdiction. Supports SAP S/4HANA SD billing integration for invoicing milestones.'
}

  fcc_disclosure_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this broadcast activation requires a sponsorship identification disclosure per FCC regulations (47 CFR §317 and §507). Mandatory for paid broadcast integrations.']
  fcc_disclosure_text varchar [note: 'Exact on-air or on-screen disclosure text required by FCC regulations for this broadcast activation (e.g., \'Paid for by [Sponsor Name]\'). Null if FCC disclosure is not required.']
  fulfillment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the activation deliverable tracking what was promised versus what has been delivered.']
  grp_contracted decimal(10,4) [note: 'Gross Rating Points committed in the sponsorship deal for broadcast activations. GRP = Reach % × Frequency, used for broadcast media valuation.']
  grp_delivered decimal(10,4) [note: 'Actual Gross Rating Points delivered for broadcast activations as measured post-airing. Used for sponsor ROI reporting and make-good calculations.']
  impressions_contracted varchar [note: 'Number of audience impressions (CPM basis) committed in the sponsorship deal for this activation. Used for GRP/CPM performance measurement.']
  impressions_delivered varchar [note: 'Actual audience impressions delivered for this activation as measured by third-party or internal analytics. Compared against contracted impressions for ROI reporting.']
  jersey_placement_position varchar [note: 'Specific position on the athlete kit where the sponsor brand appears. Applicable only for jersey/kit channel activations.']
  kit_manufacturer varchar [note: 'Name of the official kit/apparel manufacturer (e.g., Nike, Adidas, Under Armour) relevant for jersey placement activations, as kit manufacturer approval may be required per CBA or league rules.']
  market_category_exclusivity varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the sponsoring brand holds exclusive rights within this betting market category under this deal. Cannot reside on deal (which spans many categories) nor on betting_market (which is a shared catalog definition).']
  naming_rights boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this activation constitutes or is associated with a venue or event naming rights arrangement.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for operational notes, special instructions, make-good arrangements, or exceptions related to this activation deliverable.']
  placement_dimensions varchar [note: 'Physical or pixel dimensions of the activation placement (e.g., \'3m x 1m\', \'728x90px\', \'30s airtime\'). Supports fulfillment verification and creative asset compliance.']
  placement_location varchar [note: 'Specific physical or digital location of the activation (e.g., \'North End Zone LED Board Row 1\', \'Left Sleeve\', \'Pre-Roll Slot — ESPN OTT\', \'Homepage Banner Position 1\').']
  proof_of_performance_url varchar [note: 'URL or DAM (Digital Asset Management) reference link to the proof-of-performance documentation (photos, broadcast clips, screenshots) confirming activation delivery.']
  responsible_team varchar [note: 'Internal business team or department accountable for delivering this activation (e.g., \'Broadcast Partnerships\', \'In-Venue Operations\', \'Digital Marketing\', \'Merchandise & Licensing\').']
  scheduled_end_date date [note: 'Planned date on which the activation deliverable is scheduled to conclude (e.g., last game of season, campaign end date).']
  scheduled_start_date date [note: 'Planned date on which the activation deliverable is scheduled to begin (e.g., first game of season, campaign launch date).']
  sponsor_approval_status varchar [note: 'Status of the sponsor\'s review and approval of the activation creative assets and placement specifications prior to delivery.']
  sponsor_approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the sponsor formally approved the activation creative and placement specifications.']
  sponsorship_activation_category varchar [note: 'High-level classification of the activation type. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: in_venue_signage|jersey_placement|broadcast_billboard|virtual_led_overlay|sponsored_segment|branded_replay|digital_banner|social_media_post|experiential|naming_rights_display|pa_announcement|led_perimeter_board|halftime_show_integration|broadcast_integration — promote to reference product]']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this activation record was most recently modified.']
  virtual_overlay boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this activation uses virtual LED overlay technology (digitally inserted signage visible only in broadcast, not physically present in venue).']
  visibility_tier varchar [note: 'Commercial tier indicating the prominence and audience reach of the placement (Premium = highest visibility/CPM, Standard, Value = lowest).']

  Note: 'Master record for individual brand activation deliverables committed within a sponsorship deal. Captures activation name, activation category (in-venue signage, jersey patch/sleeve/collar placement, broadcast billboard, virtual LED overlay, sponsored segment, branded replay, digital banner, social media post, experiential activation, naming rights display, PA announcement, LED perimeter board, halftime show integration, broadcast integration type with network/OTT platform association, airtime and duration), placement specifications (location, dimensions, visibility tier, kit manufacturer for jersey placements), scheduled and actual delivery dates, fulfillment status, responsible team, broadcast window, FCC/regulatory disclosure requirements, DRM compliance flags, and associated event. Core entity for tracking what was promised vs. delivered across all activation channels including broadcast integrations, jersey placements, and digital activations.'
}

Finance teams require the fulfillment-to-invoice link to enforce billing-on-delivery terms, resolve sponso']

These values are sourced from audience_rating measurement records. Linking provides the measurement record that validates ']
  broadcast_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to broadcast.broadcast_schedule. Business justification: Activation fulfillment proof-of-play requires the authoritative broadcast log. Linking to broadcast_schedule provides the scheduled and actual air timestamps for verification. Replaces denormalized br']
  contest_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to gaming.contest. Business justification: Fulfillment records for contest-sponsored activations (e.g., title-sponsored DFS contests) must reference the specific contest to verify proof-of-performance and confirm contracted deliverables were m']

Business justification: Fulfillment records must be period-stamped for revenue recognition cutoff and accrual accounting at period-close.
