[ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: naming_rights|jersey_sponsorship|broadcast_integration|digital_banner|social_post|in_venue_signage|vip_hospitality|product_placement|stadium_wrap|scoreboard — promote to reference product]']
  audience_demographic_target varchar [note: 'The contracted target demographic segment for this activation (e.g., \'Adults 18-49\', \'Males 25-54\', \'Households with income >$75K\'). Used to assess whether delivered impressions matched the sponsor\'s intended audience profile.']
  audience_reach varchar [note: 'Estimated number of unique individuals reached by this activation fulfillment, distinct from total impressions which may count repeat exposures. Used alongside GRP to calculate frequency and assess campaign effectiveness.']
  brand_safety_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the activation fulfillment met all brand safety requirements as defined in the sponsorship contract (e.g., no adjacency to prohibited content, correct placement context). Critical for broadcast and digital channel fulfillments.']
  broadcast_log_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise timestamp recorded in the broadcast transmission log (Dalet Galaxy) confirming the exact air time of the sponsored content or advertisement. Used for broadcast fulfillment verification and FCC compliance documentation.']
  cpm_contracted decimal(12,4) [note: 'The Cost Per Mille (CPM) rate contractually agreed with the sponsor, representing the cost per 1,000 impressions. Confidential commercial term used to calculate the monetary value of delivered impressions and assess ROI.']
  cpm_realized decimal(12,4) [note: 'The effective Cost Per Mille (CPM) achieved based on actual impressions delivered versus the contracted value. Calculated as (contract_value / impressions_delivered) * 1000. Used in ROI reporting and renewal pricing benchmarking.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this activation fulfillment record was first created in the system. Audit trail field supporting data lineage, SOX compliance, and GDPR data processing records.']
  duration_seconds varchar [note: 'Duration in seconds for which the activation was displayed or broadcast. Applicable to broadcast spots, digital display ads, LED board rotations, and video pre-rolls. Used to verify compliance with contracted exposure time.']
  fulfillment_channel varchar [note: 'The media or physical channel through which the brand activation was delivered to the audience. Broadcast covers linear TV/radio; OTT covers streaming platforms; digital covers web/app; social covers social media platforms; in_venue covers physical stadium/arena placements. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: broadcast|in_venue|digital|social|ott|print|podcast|out_of_home — promote to reference product if additional channels are added]']
  fulfillment_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the brand activation was executed and delivered to the sponsor. Used as the principal business event date for ROI reporting and proof-of-performance documentation.']
  fulfillment_quality_rating decimal(3,1) [note: 'Numeric quality score (1.0 to 5.0) assigned to this activation fulfillment based on placement prominence, brand safety compliance, creative execution fidelity, and audience alignment. Used in sponsor satisfaction reporting and renewal scoring.']
  fulfillment_reference_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric business identifier for this fulfillment record, used in proof-of-performance reports, sponsor invoices, and renewal negotiations. Format: ACT-FUL-YYYYMMDD-XXXX.']
  fulfillment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the activation fulfillment record. \'Partial\' indicates some but not all contracted deliverables were met. \'Shortfall\' indicates a measurable gap against contracted impressions or GRP targets. Drives renewal negotiation and make-good obligations.']
  fulfillment_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time at which the activation was executed, including timezone offset. Used for broadcast log reconciliation, OTT stream timestamping, and in-venue display scheduling verification.']
  fulfillment_value_usd decimal(18,2) [note: 'The monetary value attributed to this specific activation fulfillment in US Dollars, derived from the contracted rate and delivered impressions. Used for revenue recognition, sponsor invoicing, and EBITDA reporting under SAP S/4HANA FI/CO.']
  grp_achieved decimal(10,4) [note: 'Actual Gross Rating Points (GRP) delivered for this activation fulfillment as reported by the audience measurement provider. Compared against grp_contracted to determine fulfillment compliance and trigger make-good obligations.']
  grp_contracted decimal(10,4) [note: 'The Gross Rating Points (GRP) target contractually committed for this activation, representing reach multiplied by frequency against the target demographic. Used as the broadcast and media benchmark for fulfillment assessment.']
  impressions_contracted varchar [note: 'The number of audience impressions contractually obligated for this activation as specified in the sponsorship deal terms. Serves as the benchmark against which delivered impressions are measured for shortfall and make-good calculations.']
  impressions_delivered varchar [note: 'Actual number of audience impressions delivered for this activation fulfillment as measured by the relevant audience measurement system (Nielsen, Comscore, or venue attendance data). Core metric for CPM calculation and ROI reporting.']
  makegood_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a make-good (compensatory activation) is required due to under-delivery against contracted impressions or GRP targets. True triggers a make-good workflow in Salesforce CRM for scheduling a replacement activation.']
  placement_location varchar [note: 'Specific physical or digital location of the activation placement (e.g., \'North End Zone LED Board\', \'Halftime Show Stage Banner\', \'Homepage Hero Banner\', \'Pre-Roll Video Slot 1\'). Provides granular proof-of-placement detail for sponsor reporting.']
  proof_of_play_reference varchar [note: 'Unique identifier or URI pointing to the proof-of-performance artifact (e.g., screenshot filename, broadcast log timestamp key, photo asset ID in DAM, or OTT stream clip reference). Used to substantiate fulfillment claims in sponsor reporting and dispute resolution.']
  proof_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the proof-of-performance artifact submitted for this fulfillment. Determines the verification workflow and the level of evidentiary weight assigned during sponsor audit or dispute resolution.']
  shortfall_impressions varchar [note: 'The gap between contracted impressions and delivered impressions (impressions_contracted minus impressions_delivered). Positive values indicate under-delivery triggering make-good obligations. Zero or negative values indicate full or over-delivery.']
  shortfall_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text explanation of any under-delivery or fulfillment gap, including root cause (e.g., event cancellation, broadcast preemption, technical failure, weather). Used in sponsor communications, make-good planning, and renewal negotiations.']
  sponsor_acknowledged boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the sponsor has formally acknowledged and accepted this fulfillment record as proof-of-performance. True enables revenue recognition and closes the fulfillment obligation in SAP S/4HANA.']
  sponsor_acknowledgement_date date [note: 'Date on which the sponsor formally acknowledged and accepted the fulfillment proof-of-performance. Nullable until acknowledgement is received. Used as the trigger date for revenue recognition and invoice generation.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this activation fulfillment record. Supports audit trail requirements, change tracking, and Silver Layer incremental processing in the Databricks Lakehouse.']
  verification_date date [note: 'Date on which the fulfillment record was formally verified and approved by the responsible party or third-party measurement provider. Marks the transition from \'pending\' to \'fulfilled\' or \'shortfall\' status.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing the actual execution and proof-of-performance for each brand activation. Records fulfillment date, fulfillment channel (broadcast, in-venue, digital, social, OTT), proof-of-play reference (screenshot, broadcast log timestamp, photo), impressions delivered, GRP achieved, CPM realized, fulfillment quality rating, and any shortfall notes. Feeds ROI reporting and renewal negotiations.'
}

Table sponsorship.naming_right {
  naming_right_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the naming rights agreement record in the Sports Entertainment data platform.']

  deal_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent sponsorship deal or master partnership agreement under which this naming rights component is structured. Links to the sponsorship deal master record in Salesforce CRM.']
  facility_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to venue.facility. Business justification: Naming rights agreements are granted for a specific physical facility (e.g., \'Acme Arena\'). naming_right.venue_id identifies the venue but not the specific facility within a multi-facility campus. Con']
  fixed_asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.fixed_asset. Business justification: Naming rights agreements are capitalized as intangible assets on the balance sheet (the right to use the venue name). Finance tracks these as fixed/intangible assets with depreciation schedules. A spo']
  franchise_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to league.franchise. Business justification: Naming rights agreements frequently cover franchise-owned assets — practice facilities, team-branded arenas, and franchise digital properties. Franchise attribution is required for asset valuation, SO']
  governing_body_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.governing_body. Business justification: Naming rights must be approved by the relevant governing body (league, local sports authority). Linking naming_right to governing_body enables compliance teams to track which authority approved the ag']
  jurisdiction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to gaming.jurisdiction. Business justification: Naming rights agreements for venues in gaming-regulated markets must comply with jurisdiction-specific advertising restrictions. Compliance officers verify that gaming brand naming rights are permissi']
  league_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to league.league. Business justification: Naming rights agreements for venues used by league franchises require league approval and compliance with league branding standards. League-level naming rights portfolio management and approval workfl']
  media_rights_deal_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to broadcast.media_rights_deal. Business justification: Naming rights agreements include broadcast_mention_count_min and broadcast_mention_requirement obligations. The governing media rights deal defines which territories and broadcast windows those mentio']
  operator_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to gaming.operator. Business justification: Naming rights held by gaming operators (e.g., \'BetMGM Arena\', \'Caesars Superdome\') require direct operator reference for license validation, regulatory filing, and renewal compliance. This is a standa']
  platform_channel_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to content.platform_channel. Business justification: Digital naming rights (e.g., \'Sponsor X App\', \'Sponsor X Streaming Hub\') apply to specific platform channels. Linking naming_right to platform_channel supports digital naming rights management, broadc']
  property_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sponsorship.property. Business justification: A naming right agreement is fundamentally tied to a specific property (stadium, arena, field, etc.). The naming_right table has named_asset_name and named_asset_type as free-text attributes, but the p']
  regulatory_filing_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_filing. Business justification: Naming rights agreements require regulatory filings with league governing bodies and local authorities for venue name changes. The naming_right has sox_reportable_flag and nil_compliance_flag but no F']
  revenue_recognition_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.revenue_recognition_schedule. Business justification: Naming rights agreements are multi-year contracts requiring ASC 606 treatment — fees must be recognized over the contract term as the naming obligation is satisfied.
