  season_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to league.season. Business justification: Naming rights agreements have season-specific fee escalations, renewal option deadlines, and broadcast mention commitments. Season-level naming rights tracking supports annual revenue recognition, ren']
  sponsor_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the sponsoring organisation (naming rights holder) that holds the brand rights to the named asset. Links to the sponsor master record managed in Salesforce CRM.']
  venue_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the venue or property asset that is the subject of the naming rights agreement. Links to the venue master record in the Venue domain. Nullable when the named asset is a non-venue property (trophy, division, award).']
  activation_budget_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total budget allocated by the sponsor for brand activation activities associated with the naming rights agreement (events, promotions, fan engagement, digital campaigns). Tracked in SAP S/4HANA CO.']
  agreement_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known contract or deal reference number assigned by the commercial/legal team and used in Salesforce CRM and SAP S/4HANA SD module to uniquely identify this naming rights agreement across systems.']
  agreement_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the naming rights agreement. Drives operational workflows including signage activation, broadcast mention compliance, and renewal outreach. Valid values: draft, active, suspended, expired, terminated, pending_renewal.']
  agreement_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the named asset category covered by this agreement. Distinguishes stadium/arena naming rights from trophy, league division, award, practice facility, or digital property naming rights. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: venue_naming_right|trophy_naming_right|division_naming_right|award_naming_right|practice_facility|digital_property — promote to reference product]']
  annual_fee_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Annual fee payable by the naming rights holder to the rights owner, as stipulated in the agreement. Used for annual revenue recognition and Workday Adaptive Planning financial forecasting.']
  broadcast_mention_count_min varchar [note: 'Minimum number of on-air brand mentions of the named asset\'s sponsor name contractually required per broadcast event or season, as specified in the naming rights agreement.']
  broadcast_mention_requirement varchar [note: 'Contractual specification for how and how often the named asset\'s sponsor brand must be verbally or graphically referenced during broadcast coverage, including OTT and RSN distribution. Managed in alignment with Dalet Galaxy broadcast workflow.']
  contract_document_ref varchar [note: 'Reference identifier or URL to the executed naming rights contract document stored in the enterprise document management system or SAP S/4HANA document management. Enables direct access to the legal instrument.']
  cpm_rate decimal(10,4) [note: 'Agreed Cost Per Mille (CPM) rate used to value the media impressions delivered through the naming rights agreement. Supports ROI reporting and sponsor performance analytics.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the naming rights agreement record was first created in the data platform. Supports audit trail and data lineage requirements under SOX and GDPR.']
  digital_url varchar [note: 'The official digital property URL (website or primary digital destination) for the named asset reflecting the sponsor\'s brand, as required by the naming rights agreement.']
  digital_url_change_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the naming rights agreement requires changes to digital property URLs (website domains, social media handles, app store listings) to reflect the sponsor\'s brand name.']
  dispute_resolution_body varchar [note: 'Name of the arbitration or dispute resolution body specified in the naming rights agreement for resolving contractual disputes (e.g., CAS - Court of Arbitration for Sport, ICC International Court of Arbitration).']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the naming rights agreement expires and the sponsor\'s brand name is no longer contractually applied to the named asset. Nullable for open-ended agreements.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the naming rights agreement becomes legally binding and the named asset officially adopts the sponsor\'s brand name. Aligns with the contract commencement date in SAP S/4HANA.']
  exclusivity_category varchar [note: 'Industry or product category in which the naming rights holder is granted exclusivity, preventing competing brands in the same category from obtaining naming rights on the same property (e.g., \'Financial Services\', \'Automotive\', \'Telecommunications\').']
  exclusivity_scope varchar [note: 'Geographic scope of the exclusivity granted to the naming rights holder. Determines the territorial boundaries within which competing brands in the same category are excluded.']
  fee_currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which the naming rights fees are denominated (e.g., USD, GBP, EUR). Required for multi-currency financial consolidation in SAP S/4HANA.']
  fee_escalation_rate decimal(6,4) [note: 'Annual percentage rate at which the naming rights fee escalates over the term of the agreement (e.g., 0.0300 = 3% per annum). Used in Workday Adaptive Planning for multi-year revenue projections.']
  gdpr_compliant_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the data processing activities associated with this naming rights agreement (fan data used in activation campaigns) have been reviewed for GDPR compliance. Relevant for EU market activations.']
  governing_law_country varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the jurisdiction whose laws govern the naming rights agreement. Relevant for dispute resolution and compliance with local regulations.']
  grp_commitment decimal(10,2) [note: 'Contractually committed Gross Rating Point (GRP) delivery for broadcast and media exposure associated with the naming rights agreement. Used to measure and report media value delivered to the sponsor.']
  ip_rights_included_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the naming rights agreement grants the sponsor Intellectual Property (IP) usage rights, such as the right to use league logos, team marks, or event trademarks in their own marketing.']
  named_asset_name varchar [note: 'The official branded name of the asset as it appears publicly following the naming rights agreement (e.g., \'Acme Arena\', \'TechCorp Stadium\'). This is the name used in all broadcast mentions, signage, and digital properties.']
  named_asset_prior_name varchar [note: 'The name of the asset prior to the current naming rights agreement. Used for historical reference, fan communications, and transition period signage management.']
  named_asset_type varchar [note: 'Category of the physical or intangible property subject to the naming rights. Determines applicable signage, broadcast, and digital obligations. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: stadium|arena|practice_facility|trophy|award|league_division|digital_property|broadcast_segment — promote to reference product]']
  nil_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the naming rights agreement has been reviewed and confirmed compliant with applicable Name Image and Likeness (NIL) regulations, particularly relevant when athlete names or likenesses are incorporated into the named asset branding.']
  payment_schedule_type varchar [note: 'Frequency or structure of fee payments from the naming rights holder to the rights owner as defined in the contract. Drives accounts receivable scheduling in SAP S/4HANA.']
  renewal_notice_days varchar [note: 'Number of calendar days prior to the agreement expiry by which the sponsor must exercise their renewal option. Triggers automated renewal outreach workflows in Salesforce CRM.']
  renewal_option_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the naming rights agreement includes a contractual option for the sponsor to renew at the end of the current term. True = renewal option exists; False = no renewal option.']
  renewal_option_terms varchar [note: 'Free-text description of the renewal option conditions, including notice period, fee adjustment methodology, and any right-of-first-refusal provisions as specified in the contract.']
  roi_target_pct decimal(6,4) [note: 'Contractually agreed or internally benchmarked Return on Investment (ROI) target percentage for the naming rights agreement, used to evaluate sponsor value delivery and guide renewal negotiations.']
  signage_count varchar [note: 'Total number of physical and digital signage units contractually required to display the sponsor\'s brand name under the naming rights agreement.']
  signage_spec_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the physical and digital signage obligations under the naming rights agreement, including placement locations, dimensions, illumination requirements, and brand standards compliance.']
  sox_reportable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this naming rights agreement meets the materiality threshold requiring disclosure under SOX financial controls for public company reporting. Drives inclusion in SEC financial reporting.']
  term_years varchar [note: 'Total contractual duration of the naming rights agreement expressed in whole years. Supports long-term revenue planning and renewal pipeline management.']
  termination_clause_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the agreement contains an early termination clause allowing either party to exit the agreement before the effective end date under defined conditions.']
  termination_fee_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Contractual penalty or fee payable upon early termination of the naming rights agreement. Expressed in the contract currency. Applicable only when termination_clause_flag is True.']
  total_fee_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total contractual value of the naming rights agreement over the full term, expressed in the contract currency. Used for financial reporting under SAP S/4HANA FI/CO and SOX compliance disclosures.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the naming rights agreement record was most recently modified in the data platform. Supports change tracking, audit compliance, and Silver layer data freshness monitoring.']

  Note: 'Master record for venue and property naming rights agreements. Captures named asset (stadium, arena, practice facility, trophy, award, league division), naming rights holder (sponsor), naming rights fee, term start and end dates, signage specifications, broadcast mention requirements, digital property URL changes, and renewal option details. Distinct from general deal terms due to the long-term, high-value, and multi-property nature of naming rights.'
}

Table sponsorship.ad_inventory {
  ad_inventory_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each sponsorship and advertising inventory asset record in the master catalog. Primary key for the sponsorship_ad_inventory product.']
  athlete_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to athlete.athlete_profile. Business justification: Athlete-specific inventory units (jersey patch slots, athlete appearance placements, athlete social media inventory) must reference the athlete whose likeness constitutes the asset. Inventory valuatio']
  broadcast_channel_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to broadcast.channel. Business justification: Broadcast-integrated ad inventory (is_broadcast_integrated flag) is valued and managed per broadcast channel. Channel subscriber_count, platform_type, and territory directly drive CPM benchmarking and']
  franchise_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to league.franchise. Business justification: Ad inventory assets are often franchise-specific (in-stadium digital boards, jersey patches, tunnel branding for Team X). Inventory management systems must identify which franchise\'s assets are being ']
  governing_body_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.governing_body. Business justification: Ad inventory (broadcast slots, in-venue signage) is subject to governing body rules on placement, content restrictions, and exclusivity categories. The sponsorship_ad_inventory has ada_compliant and g']
  inventory_unit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sponsorship.inventory_unit. Business justification: sponsorship_ad_inventory is the catalog of available sponsorship and advertising inventory assets. inventory_unit is the master reference table defining the unit types, formats, pricing benchmarks, an']
  jurisdiction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to gaming.gaming_jurisdiction. Business justification: Betting-related ad inventory (odds widgets, sponsored betting segments) is only available in jurisdictions where the operator is licensed. Inventory managers must validate jurisdiction compliance befo']
  league_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the league or competition to which this inventory asset is associated. Governs exclusivity restrictions, CBA (Collective Bargaining Agreement) compliance, and league-level sponsorship category protections.']
  league_season_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to league.season. Business justification: Ad inventory availability is defined per league season. The plain-text \'season_year\' column is a denormalization signal. Structured FK to league.season enables season-level inventory planning, availab']
  operator_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to gaming.operator. Business justification: Ad inventory units reserved exclusively for or restricted to specific gaming operators (category exclusivity) require direct operator reference. Inventory management teams track which licensed operato']
  platform_channel_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to content.platform_channel. Business justification: Ad inventory units belong to specific platform channels (OTT, broadcast, digital). Inventory managers need this FK for availability management, DRM enforcement per channel, and CPM pricing. asset_chan']
  policy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.policy. Business justification: Ad inventory units are subject to compliance policies (FCC advertising rules for broadcast inventory, GDPR for targeted digital inventory, WADA restrictions on prohibited sponsor categories). sponsors']
  property_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the Sports Entertainment property (venue, broadcast channel, OTT platform, mobile app, or league) to which this inventory asset belongs. Links the asset to its owning property for rights management and revenue attribution.']
  venue_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the specific venue where this inventory asset is physically located. Applicable for in-venue asset types such as LED perimeter boards, stadium signage, naming rights units, and VIP hospitality suites. Null for purely digital or broadcast assets.']
  ada_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this inventory asset (particularly in-venue assets such as signage, hospitality suites, and PA systems) meets ADA (Americans with Disabilities Act) accessibility requirements. Mandatory for venue-based assets in US markets. Supports compliance reporting.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'The internal governance approval status of this inventory asset before it may be listed for sale. \'approved\' indicates the asset has passed commercial, legal, and compliance review; \'pending_review\' indicates it is under review; \'rejected\' indicates it failed review; \'draft\' indicates it is being prepared for submission.']
  assessed_value_per_unit decimal(18,4) [note: 'The independently assessed fair market value of a single unit of this inventory asset, expressed in the valuation currency. Represents the SSOT for what each asset unit is worth in the current market. Used as the floor price in sponsor negotiations and for ROI (Return on Investment) reporting.']
  asset_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the inventory asset across all Sports Entertainment properties and Salesforce CRM. Used in sponsor proposals, contracts, and activation briefs as the canonical asset reference.']
  asset_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the inventory asset (e.g., \'Main Pitch LED Perimeter Board East Side\', \'OTT Pre-Roll 30s Slot — League Pass App\', \'Naming Rights Unit — North Stand\'). Used in sales decks, activation plans, and reporting.']
  asset_type varchar [note: 'Category of the inventory asset defining its physical or digital format. Examples include LED perimeter board, broadcast billboard, digital overlay, PA announcement slot, in-app banner, OTT pre-roll, social media post, VIP hospitality suite, naming rights unit, jersey sponsorship patch, stadium signage. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: led_perimeter_board|broadcast_billboard|digital_overlay|pa_announcement|in_app_banner|ott_pre_roll|social_media_post|vip_hospitality_suite|naming_rights|jersey_patch|stadium_signage|scoreboard_display|field_logo|digital_out_of_home|podcast_sponsorship — promote to reference product]']
  audience_demographic_profile varchar [note: 'Description of the primary audience demographic profile associated with this inventory asset (e.g., \'Male 18-34, ABC1, sports enthusiast\', \'Family audience, mixed gender, 25-54\'). Sourced from Adobe Experience Platform fan analytics or Nielsen audience measurement. Used in sponsor targeting and CPM justification.']
  availability_status varchar [note: 'Current availability state of the inventory asset in the sponsorship sales lifecycle. \'available\' indicates the asset is open for sale; \'sold\' indicates it is committed to a sponsor; \'reserved\' indicates a hold pending contract execution; \'held\' indicates a short-term sales hold; \'retired\' indicates the asset is no longer offered; \'pending_approval\' indicates the asset is awaiting internal sign-off before listing.']
  cpm_benchmark decimal(12,4) [note: 'The industry benchmark CPM (Cost Per Mille) rate for this asset type and channel, expressed in the asset\'s valuation currency. Represents the market cost per 1,000 impressions used to benchmark assessed value against industry norms. Sourced from Nielsen, independent agency, or Sports Entertainment commercial intelligence.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this inventory asset record was first created in the sponsorship ad inventory catalog. Provides the audit trail creation anchor for SOX compliance and data lineage tracking.']
  effective_from_date date [note: 'The date from which this inventory asset record is valid and available for sale. Supports temporal versioning of the inventory catalog, ensuring that historical valuations and availability states are preserved for audit and benchmarking purposes.']
  effective_to_date date [note: 'The date after which this inventory asset record is no longer valid or available for sale. Null indicates the record is currently active. Used for temporal versioning and historical audit of the inventory catalog.']
  estimated_impressions varchar [note: 'Estimated number of audience impressions delivered per unit activation of this inventory asset, based on Nielsen media measurement, broadcast ratings, or venue attendance data. Used as the primary input for CPM (Cost Per Mille) valuation and GRP (Gross Rating Point) calculations in sponsor proposals.']
  event_type_eligibility varchar [note: 'Defines the types of events for which this inventory asset is eligible to be activated (e.g., \'regular_season\', \'playoffs\', \'finals\', \'international\', \'concert\', \'all\'). Restricts asset availability to appropriate event contexts and supports event-specific packaging.']
  exclusivity_category varchar [note: 'The commercial exclusivity classification of the inventory asset, defining whether a sponsor receives category exclusivity (e.g., exclusive automotive partner), sub-category exclusivity, or non-exclusive placement. Governs competitive separation rules and sponsor conflict checks. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: category_exclusive|sub_category_exclusive|non_exclusive|presenting_sponsor|title_sponsor|co_sponsor — promote to reference product]']
  gdpr_data_usage_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether activation of this inventory asset involves the collection or processing of fan personal data (True), triggering GDPR (General Data Protection Regulation) or CCPA compliance obligations. Applicable to digital assets such as in-app banners, OTT pre-rolls, and personalized digital overlays that use fan behavioral data for targeting.']
  grp_rating decimal(10,4) [note: 'The GRP (Gross Rating Point) value assigned to this inventory asset, representing the percentage of the target audience reached multiplied by the average frequency of exposure. Used for broadcast and media inventory valuation and sponsor ROI (Return on Investment) reporting. Sourced from Nielsen or independent agency assessment.']
  internal_owner_team varchar [note: 'The internal Sports Entertainment business unit or team responsible for managing and selling this inventory asset (e.g., \'Global Partnerships\', \'Broadcast Sales\', \'Venue Commercial\', \'Digital Partnerships\'). Used for revenue attribution, KPI (Key Performance Indicator) reporting, and workflow routing.']
  is_broadcast_integrated boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this inventory asset is integrated into a broadcast or OB (Outside Broadcast) production feed (True), meaning it appears in the televised or streamed broadcast of an event. Broadcast-integrated assets carry higher CPM values and are subject to FCC broadcast regulation and RSN (Regional Sports Network) rights restrictions.']
  is_digital boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this inventory asset is a digital placement (True) or a physical/static placement (False). Digital assets include OTT pre-rolls, in-app banners, digital overlays, and social media posts. Physical assets include static signage, LED boards, and naming rights plaques. Drives DRM (Digital Rights Management) and DAM (Digital Asset Management) workflow routing.']
  is_naming_rights boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this inventory asset represents a naming rights unit (True), such as a venue naming rights package or a competition naming rights package. Naming rights assets have distinct legal, IP (Intellectual Property), and valuation characteristics and require separate governance approval.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this inventory asset record was most recently modified, including changes to availability status, valuation, capacity, or approval status. Supports change data capture in the Databricks Silver Layer and audit trail requirements.']
  list_price_per_unit decimal(18,4) [note: 'The standard published list price for a single unit of this inventory asset, expressed in the valuation currency. Represents the starting price point in sponsor negotiations before any package discounts or deal-specific adjustments. Distinct from assessed_value_per_unit which reflects independent market valuation.']
  max_units_per_sponsor varchar [note: 'The maximum number of units of this inventory asset that a single sponsor may purchase, enforcing competitive separation and preventing monopolization of high-visibility placements. Null indicates no per-sponsor cap.']
  min_commitment_units varchar [note: 'The minimum number of units a sponsor must purchase to activate this inventory asset. Enforces commercial packaging rules and prevents sub-economic placements. For example, a broadcast billboard may require a minimum of 10 spots per broadcast.']
  placement_location_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the physical or digital placement location of the inventory asset (e.g., \'East perimeter board, pitch-side, between 18-yard box and halfway line\', \'Pre-roll slot on League Pass OTT app home screen\', \'North Stand fascia, upper tier\'). Provides the specificity required for activation briefs and sponsor approval.']
  production_spec_notes varchar [note: 'Technical production specifications and creative requirements for this inventory asset, such as artwork dimensions, file format requirements, broadcast safe zones, LED pixel pitch, audio specifications for PA announcements, or digital ad unit specifications (IAB standards). Used by the activation and production teams.']
  sponsor_category varchar [note: 'The industry or product category of the sponsor occupying or targeted for this inventory asset (e.g., automotive, financial services, beverage, technology, insurance, apparel). Used for competitive exclusivity enforcement and category-based inventory packaging. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: automotive|financial_services|beverage|technology|insurance|apparel|healthcare|telecommunications|retail|energy — promote to reference product]']
  unit_count varchar [note: 'Total number of discrete units available for this inventory asset type at the associated property. For example, the number of LED perimeter board panels, the number of OTT pre-roll slots per broadcast, or the number of PA announcement slots per event. Drives capacity planning and sell-through rate analysis.']
  unit_duration_seconds varchar [note: 'Duration in seconds of a single inventory unit for time-based assets such as broadcast billboards, OTT pre-roll slots, PA announcements, and digital overlays. Null for non-time-based assets such as naming rights or jersey patches. Standard broadcast durations include 15, 30, and 60 seconds.']
  valuation_currency varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which the assessed value per unit and CPM benchmark are denominated (e.g., USD, GBP, EUR). Supports multi-currency operations across global Sports Entertainment markets.']
  valuation_date date [note: 'The date on which the current assessed value per unit was determined. Used to track valuation currency and trigger re-valuation workflows when the valuation exceeds the maximum age threshold defined by Sports Entertainment commercial policy.']
  valuation_methodology varchar [note: 'The methodology used to derive the assessed value per unit for this inventory asset. \'broadcast_equivalency\' values the asset based on equivalent broadcast airtime cost; \'nielsen_media_value\' uses Nielsen-measured audience data; \'independent_agency\' reflects a third-party agency appraisal; \'internal_assessment\' reflects Sports Entertainment commercial team valuation; \'comparable_market\' uses comparable asset transactions.']
  valuation_source varchar [note: 'The name of the organization or system that provided the assessed value (e.g., \'Nielsen Sports\', \'Repucom\', \'Two Circles\', \'Sports Entertainment Internal Commercial Team\', \'Octagon\'). Provides audit traceability for valuation figures used in sponsor proposals and financial reporting.']

  Note: 'Master catalog of available sponsorship and advertising inventory assets across all Sports Entertainment properties, including independently assessed market valuations. Captures asset type (LED perimeter board, broadcast billboard, digital overlay, PA announcement slot, in-app banner, OTT pre-roll, social media post, VIP hospitality suite, naming rights unit), property association (venue, broadcast channel, OTT platform, mobile app), capacity metrics (number of units, duration in seconds, estimated impressions), exclusivity category, current availability status. Includes valuation data: assessed value per unit, valuation methodology (broadcast equivalency, Nielsen media value, independent agency assessment), valuation date, valuation source, and currency. SSOT for what can be sold to sponsors, current market value of each asset, and benchmarking against industry CPM and GRP rates.'
}

Table sponsorship.inventory_allocation {
  inventory_allocation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each inventory allocation record linking a specific ad inventory unit to a sponsorship deal or activation. Primary key for this transactional entity.']
  activation_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific brand activation or campaign deliverable this inventory allocation fulfills within the sponsorship deal.']
  ad_inventory_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific advertising or sponsorship inventory unit being allocated (e.g., LED board slot, broadcast spot, digital banner, naming rights panel, jersey patch position).']
  broadcast_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to broadcast.broadcast_schedule. Business justification: Broadcast-integrated inventory allocations (delivery_start_date, delivery_end_date, channel) must be tied to specific broadcast schedule slots to confirm availability, prevent double-booking, and gene']
  broadcast_window_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the broadcast or media window (e.g., pre-game, halftime, post-game, OTT stream slot) during which this inventory allocation is scheduled for delivery.']
  creative_asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to content.asset. Business justification: Inventory allocations assign specific creative assets to inventory slots. Trafficking and fulfillment teams need this FK to verify DRM compliance and brand safety of the allocated creative before deli']
  deal_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent sponsorship deal or contract under which this inventory allocation is made. Links the allocation to its governing commercial agreement.']
  franchise_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to league.franchise. Business justification: Inventory allocations for franchise-specific assets (jersey patch, home game signage) must reference the franchise. Franchise-level allocation reporting and exclusivity enforcement require this direct']
  inventory_unit_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific advertising or sponsorship inventory unit being allocated (e.g., LED board slot, broadcast spot, digital banner, naming rights panel, jersey patch position).']
  league_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the league or competition governing body under whose jurisdiction this inventory allocation falls (e.g., NFL, NBA, FIFA). Relevant for league-mandated inventory restrictions and governing body approval requirements.']
  league_season_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to league.season. Business justification: Inventory allocations are made for specific league seasons. Season-level allocation reporting, sell-through rate analysis, and unsold inventory management are core commercial operations processes requ']
  operator_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to gaming.operator. Business justification: Inventory allocations to gaming operator sponsors require operator reference for pre-delivery regulatory compliance checks — verifying the operator\'s license is active in the delivery jurisdiction bef']
  original_allocation_inventory_allocation_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the original inventory allocation record that this make-good allocation is compensating for. Populated only when make_good_flag is True. Enables traceability of make-good chains.']
  primary_inventory_event_fixture_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific sporting or entertainment event during which this inventory allocation is to be delivered (e.g., match, game, concert, tournament round).']
  sponsor_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the sponsor or brand partner to whom this inventory is allocated. Serves as the primary party reference for this transaction.']
  venue_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the venue or facility where this physical or digital inventory allocation is to be activated (e.g., stadium LED board, arena naming rights signage).']
  actual_impressions_delivered varchar [note: 'The actual number of audience impressions or exposures recorded upon delivery of this inventory allocation. Compared against impression_target to assess performance and trigger make-good provisions.']
  actual_quantity_delivered varchar [note: 'The actual number of inventory units delivered upon fulfillment of this allocation. Compared against allocated_quantity to determine fulfillment rate and identify make-good obligations.']
  allocated_quantity varchar [note: 'Number of inventory units allocated in this record (e.g., number of broadcast spots, number of digital impressions blocks, number of signage panels). Represents the principal quantitative fact of this allocation transaction.']
  allocated_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'The commercial value assigned to this specific inventory allocation, representing the portion of the sponsorship deal\'s total contract value attributable to this unit. Used for revenue recognition, ROI tracking, and deal fulfillment reporting.']
  allocation_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which this inventory unit was formally allocated to the sponsorship deal or activation. Represents the principal business event date for this transaction.']
  allocation_reference_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for this allocation transaction, used in sponsor-facing communications, fulfillment reports, and cross-system reconciliation (e.g., CRM opportunity line reference, SAP SD delivery order number).']
  allocation_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of this inventory allocation. \'reserved\' indicates a hold pending confirmation; \'confirmed\' indicates a committed booking; \'delivered\' indicates fulfillment is complete; \'cancelled\' indicates the allocation was voided; \'pending_approval\' indicates awaiting internal or sponsor sign-off.']
  allocation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time when this inventory allocation record was created in the system, capturing the exact moment of reservation or booking action.']
  approval_date date [note: 'The date on which this inventory allocation received formal approval from the required approving authority. Supports audit trail and compliance reporting.']
  approval_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this inventory allocation requires explicit approval from a league governing body, broadcast rights holder, or internal compliance team before it can be confirmed and delivered.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the individual or governing body that approved this inventory allocation. Populated when approval_required_flag is True and the allocation has been approved.']
  cancellation_reason varchar [note: 'Reason code explaining why this inventory allocation was cancelled. Used for operational analysis, sponsor relationship management, and capacity planning adjustments.']
  cancelled_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this inventory allocation was cancelled. Populated only when allocation_status is \'cancelled\'. Supports audit trail and capacity reclamation workflows.']
  channel varchar [note: 'The media or activation channel through which this inventory allocation is delivered (e.g., broadcast TV, OTT streaming, in-venue, digital web, social media). Drives measurement methodology and reporting standards. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: broadcast_tv|ott_streaming|in_venue|digital_web|social_media|mobile_app|radio|print — promote to reference product]']
  confirmed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this inventory allocation was formally confirmed by both the sponsorship operations team and the sponsor, transitioning from \'reserved\' to \'confirmed\' status.']
  cpm_rate decimal(10,4) [note: 'The Cost Per Mille (CPM) rate applied to this inventory allocation, representing the cost per one thousand impressions or audience exposures. Standard pricing metric for digital and broadcast advertising inventory in the sports-entertainment industry.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the allocated_value amount (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Supports multi-currency sponsorship deals across global markets.']
  delivered_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when fulfillment of this inventory allocation was completed and verified, transitioning the record to \'delivered\' status. Used for ROI reporting and sponsor proof-of-performance.']
  delivery_end_date date [note: 'The date by which this inventory allocation must be fully delivered or activated. Defines the closing boundary of the fulfillment window and drives SLA compliance tracking.']
  delivery_start_date date [note: 'The date from which this inventory allocation is scheduled to begin delivery or activation. Defines the opening boundary of the fulfillment window.']
  exclusivity_category varchar [note: 'The product or service category for which exclusivity is granted (e.g., \'Automotive\', \'Financial Services\', \'Beverage - Alcohol\', \'Telecommunications\'). Populated only when exclusivity_flag is True. Used to enforce category conflict checks during deal negotiations.']
  exclusivity_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this inventory allocation carries category exclusivity for the sponsor, preventing competing brands in the same product or service category from receiving the same inventory type during the same event or broadcast window.']
  geographic_market varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code representing the primary geographic market for this inventory allocation. Supports multi-market sponsorship deals and regional rights management.']
  grp_target decimal(10,2) [note: 'The target Gross Rating Point (GRP) value committed for this inventory allocation, representing the expected audience reach multiplied by frequency. Used for broadcast and media inventory performance measurement and sponsor ROI reporting.']
  impression_target varchar [note: 'The target number of audience impressions or exposures committed for this inventory allocation. Used for digital and broadcast inventory performance tracking and sponsor proof-of-performance reporting.']
  inventory_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the advertising or sponsorship inventory unit being allocated. Drives fulfillment workflows, measurement methodology (CPM, GRP, impressions), and reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: broadcast_spot|digital_display|venue_signage|jersey_patch|naming_rights|social_media|print|activation_space — promote to reference product]']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this inventory allocation record was last updated. Supports change tracking, audit trail, and incremental data pipeline processing in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  make_good_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this inventory allocation was created as a make-good (compensatory replacement) for a previously under-delivered or cancelled allocation. Make-goods are contractual obligations to sponsors when original delivery commitments are not met.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for operational notes, special instructions, or contextual information related to this inventory allocation (e.g., creative specifications, sponsor preferences, technical delivery requirements).']
  placement_position varchar [note: 'Specific placement position or location descriptor for this inventory allocation (e.g., \'North End LED Row 1\', \'Halftime Break Slot 2\', \'Homepage Banner Top\', \'Left Sleeve Jersey Patch\'). Provides granular fulfillment and proof-of-performance detail.']
  priority_tier varchar [note: 'Commercial priority tier assigned to this allocation, reflecting the sponsor\'s deal level and governing conflict resolution when inventory is oversubscribed. Higher tiers receive preferential placement and first-right-of-refusal on premium inventory.']
  rate_card_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'The published or standard rate card value for this inventory unit prior to any negotiated discount or deal adjustment. Enables calculation of discount depth and benchmarking of deal terms against market rates.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'The unit in which the allocated quantity is expressed (e.g., \'spots\' for broadcast, \'impressions\' for digital, \'panels\' for venue signage, \'seconds\' for broadcast duration, \'placements\' for jersey or naming rights).']

  Note: 'Transactional record linking specific ad inventory units to sponsorship deals and activations. Captures allocation date, allocated quantity, event or broadcast window, exclusivity flag, priority tier, and allocation status (reserved, confirmed, delivered, cancelled). Prevents double-booking of inventory assets and supports capacity planning for future deal negotiations.'
}

Table sponsorship.performance_metric {
  performance_metric_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each sponsorship performance metric record in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Primary key for this transactional measurement entity.']
  activation_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific brand activation or deliverable associated with this performance measurement (e.g., jersey logo placement, broadcast integration, in-venue signage).']
  athlete_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to athlete.athlete_profile. Business justification: Sponsorship ROI reporting requires attributing brand recall, social engagement, and impression metrics to a specific athlete. Existing FK covers athlete_contract; athlete_profile is the master identit']
  audience_rating_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to broadcast.audience_rating. Business justification: Performance metrics track grp_delivered and broadcast_airtime_seconds sourced from audience measurement. Linking to audience_rating provides traceability from contracted KPI to the measurement record ']
  audit_engagement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.audit_engagement. Business justification: Sponsorship performance metrics (GRP delivery, impression fulfillment) are subject to compliance audit engagements to verify accuracy of reported figures for sponsor ROI reporting. Auditors need to tr']
  betting_market_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to gaming.betting_market. Business justification: ROI reports for betting operator sponsors measure engagement at the market level (e.g., handle volume on sponsored markets, CTR on odds overlays). Performance metrics must reference the specific betti']
  broadcast_channel_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to broadcast.channel. Business justification: Sponsorship performance metrics track broadcast_airtime_seconds and impressions by channel. Linking to broadcast.channel enables channel-level ROI reporting, CPM analysis by platform and territory, an']
  contest_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to gaming.contest. Business justification: Sponsorship KPIs are measured against specific gaming contests (e.g., branded DFS contest series). Sponsorship analysts reporting on contest-tied activation performance — impressions, engagement, priz']
  creative_asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to content.asset. Business justification: Sponsorship ROI reporting requires linking performance metrics (impressions, brand recall, CPM realized) to the specific creative asset that ran. Analytics teams measure asset-level effectiveness for ']
  deal_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sponsorship.deal. Business justification: performance_metric captures measured sponsorship performance data against contracted KPIs. While performance_metric already links to activation (which in turn links to deal), many performance metrics ']
  fan_segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fan.segment. Business justification: Sponsorship KPIs (NPS lift, brand recall, reach) are measured against specific fan segments as contracted audience targets. Linking performance_metric to fan.segment enables segment-level sponsor ROI ']
  franchise_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to league.franchise. Business justification: Sponsorship KPIs — jersey patch impressions, in-arena GRP, brand recall scores — are measured at franchise level. Franchise-level performance reporting is a standard sponsor deliverable; without this ']
  league_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the sports league or competition governing body context in which this sponsorship performance metric was measured (e.g., NFL, NBA, MLS, FIFA). Enables league-level aggregation and compliance with league sponsorship governance rules.']
  league_season_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to league.season. Business justification: Sponsorship KPIs (GRP, impressions, brand recall) are measured and reported per league season. Season-level KPI reporting drives renewal decisions and ROI analysis; no structured season FK currently e']
  live_feed_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to broadcast.live_feed. Business justification: Sponsorship performance metrics (broadcast_airtime_seconds, grp_delivered, impressions) are measured against specific live feed broadcasts. The live_feed record provides actual start/end times, feed_s']
  nil_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to athlete.nil_agreement. Business justification: NIL deal ROI measurement requires linking performance metrics directly to the NIL agreement being evaluated. Athlete management, agents, and sponsors use this for NIL deal renewal decisions, performan']
  primary_performance_event_fixture_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific sports or entertainment event during which this performance metric was captured, if applicable (e.g., a specific match, concert, or broadcast window).']
  revenue_recognition_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.revenue_recognition_schedule. Business justification: Performance metrics drive variable consideration adjustments under IFRS 15/ASC 606. When KPIs (impressions, GRP, NPS lift) are met or missed, the revenue recognition schedule must be updated. Direct F']
  sponsor_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the sponsor (brand/company) whose contracted KPI performance is being measured in this record.']
  venue_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the venue where in-venue sponsorship activations were measured. Relevant for naming rights, in-venue signage, and experiential activation metrics.']
  activation_channel varchar [note: 'The specific channel or placement through which the sponsorship was activated and measured. Covers broadcast TV, OTT (Over-The-Top) streaming, in-venue signage, jersey sponsorship, naming rights, digital display, social media, PPV (Pay-Per-View), and other channels. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: broadcast_tv|ott_streaming|in_venue_signage|jersey_sponsorship|naming_rights|digital_display|social_media|print|radio|ppv — promote to reference product]']
  actual_value decimal(18,4) [note: 'The measured actual value delivered for this sponsorship KPI during the measurement period. Sourced from third-party measurement providers (Nielsen, Kantar) or internal analytics systems.']
  brand_recall_score decimal(6,2) [note: 'Unaided or aided brand recall score measured via consumer survey panels (Kantar, Nielsen) during the measurement period. Expressed as a percentage of surveyed audience who recalled the sponsor brand in the context of the sports/entertainment property.']
  broadcast_airtime_seconds varchar [note: 'Total number of seconds of broadcast airtime during which the sponsor\'s brand was visible or mentioned during the measurement period. Tracked via Dalet Galaxy broadcast workflow and third-party monitoring services.']
  contracted_target_value decimal(18,4) [note: 'The agreed-upon target value for this KPI as stipulated in the sponsorship contract. Serves as the benchmark against which actual performance is measured for fulfillment and ROI reporting.']
  cpm_realized decimal(12,4) [note: 'Actual Cost Per Mille (cost per 1,000 impressions) realized for this sponsorship activation during the measurement period. Used to benchmark media efficiency against contracted CPM rates and industry benchmarks.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this performance metric record was first created in the system. Audit trail field for data lineage and compliance with SOX financial controls and GDPR data processing records.']
  data_source varchar [note: 'The originating data source or measurement provider that supplied the actual metric value. Used for data lineage, audit, and credibility assessment in sponsor-facing ROI reports. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: nielsen|kantar|internal_analytics|adobe_experience_platform|social_platform_api|venue_analytics|manual — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  data_source_report_reference varchar [note: 'The external report or file identifier from the measurement data source (e.g., Nielsen report ID, Kantar study reference number) that this metric record was derived from. Enables traceability back to the source measurement report.']
  frequency decimal(8,2) [note: 'Average number of times the target audience was exposed to the sponsorship asset (logo, brand mention, advertisement) during the measurement period. Component of GRP calculation.']
  fulfillment_status varchar [note: 'Assessment of whether the contracted KPI target was met for this measurement period. Fulfilled = actual >= target; Partially Fulfilled = actual between 80-99% of target; Unfulfilled = actual < 80% of target; Over-Delivered = actual > 110% of target.']
  grp_delivered decimal(12,4) [note: 'Total Gross Rating Points delivered for broadcast and media sponsorship activations during the measurement period. GRP = Reach% × Frequency. Core broadcast sponsorship KPI used in media valuation and ROI reporting.']
  impressions varchar [note: 'Total number of times the sponsor\'s brand asset (logo, advertisement, naming rights mention) was served or displayed across all channels (broadcast, digital, in-venue) during the measurement period.']
  in_venue_dwell_time_seconds varchar [note: 'Average number of seconds fans spent in proximity to or engaged with in-venue sponsor activations (branded zones, experiential areas, digital signage) during the measurement period. Captured via venue analytics systems.']
  is_contracted_kpi boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this metric corresponds to a formally contracted KPI obligation in the sponsorship agreement (True) or is a supplementary/bonus metric tracked for additional insight (False). Drives fulfillment compliance reporting.']
  make_good_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a make-good (compensatory delivery of additional sponsorship inventory) is required due to under-delivery against the contracted KPI target. Triggers make-good workflow in sponsorship fulfillment operations.']
  measurement_methodology varchar [note: 'The methodology used to capture and validate this performance metric. Determines the reliability and comparability of the measurement. Examples: panel survey (Nielsen/Kantar), set-top box data, digital pixel tracking, AI image recognition for logo exposure, manual audit. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: panel_survey|set_top_box|digital_pixel|manual_audit|ai_image_recognition|third_party_monitor|internal_analytics — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  measurement_period_end date [note: 'End date of the measurement window during which the sponsorship KPI data was collected. Together with measurement_period_start defines the reporting interval.']
  measurement_period_start date [note: 'Start date of the measurement window during which the sponsorship KPI data was collected. Aligns with contracted reporting periods (weekly, monthly, quarterly, seasonal).']
  measurement_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of this performance metric record. Tracks the workflow from initial capture through sponsor verification and final acceptance used in renewal negotiations.']
  measurement_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time when this performance metric observation was recorded or ingested from the measurement data source (Nielsen, Kantar, internal analytics platform). Represents the principal real-world event time.']
  metric_category varchar [note: 'High-level category grouping the metric type for reporting and ROI analysis. Enables aggregation across activation channels (broadcast, digital, in-venue, brand health, social media, print).']
  metric_reference_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for this performance metric record, used in ROI reports, renewal negotiations, and sponsor-facing dashboards. Format: PM-YYYYMMDD-XXXX.']
  metric_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the sponsorship KPI being measured. Covers broadcast, digital, in-venue, and brand health metrics. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: grp_delivered|cpm_realized|impressions|reach|frequency|brand_recall_score|nps_lift|social_engagement_rate|broadcast_airtime_seconds|in_venue_dwell_time|jersey_logo_exposure|naming_rights_mention|digital_banner_clicks|share_of_voice — promote to reference product]']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for capturing contextual notes, measurement caveats, methodology exceptions, or sponsor feedback related to this performance metric record. Used by sponsorship account managers during review and renewal negotiations.']
  nps_lift decimal(6,2) [note: 'Change in Net Promoter Score for the sponsor brand attributable to the sponsorship activation, measured via fan/consumer surveys. Positive values indicate brand sentiment improvement driven by the partnership.']
  reach_000s decimal(14,2) [note: 'Total unique audience reached by the sponsorship activation during the measurement period, expressed in thousands. Sourced from Nielsen or Kantar audience measurement panels.']
  reporting_period_type varchar [note: 'The cadence or granularity of the measurement reporting period. Aligns with contractual reporting obligations to sponsors (weekly, monthly, quarterly, seasonal, annual, or tied to a specific event).']
  social_engagement_rate decimal(8,4) [note: 'Percentage engagement rate for sponsor-related social media content during the measurement period ((likes + comments + shares + saves) / total impressions × 100). Sourced from Adobe Experience Platform social analytics.']
  sponsor_verified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the sponsor has formally reviewed and accepted this performance metric measurement as accurate. Required for final ROI reporting and renewal negotiation documentation.']
  sponsor_verified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the sponsor formally verified and accepted this performance metric measurement. Null if sponsor_verified is False.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'The unit in which the metric value is expressed. Examples: seconds (broadcast airtime), impressions (digital/broadcast), GRP points (Gross Rating Points), CPM USD (Cost Per Mille in USD), score (brand recall/NPS), percentage (engagement rate). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: seconds|impressions|grp_points|cpm_usd|score|percentage|mentions|clicks|views|reach_000s — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this performance metric record was most recently modified. Supports audit trail requirements, Silver Layer incremental processing, and SOX financial reporting controls.']
  variance_pct decimal(8,4) [note: 'Percentage variance of actual performance against the contracted target value ((actual - target) / target * 100). Used in sponsor ROI dashboards and renewal negotiation scorecards.']
  variance_value decimal(18,4) [note: 'Absolute difference between actual_value and contracted_target_value (actual minus target). Positive values indicate over-delivery; negative values indicate under-delivery against contracted KPI obligations.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing measured sponsorship performance data against contracted KPIs. Records metric type (GRP delivered, CPM realized, impressions, reach, frequency, brand recall score, NPS lift, social media engagement rate, broadcast airtime seconds, in-venue dwell time), measurement period, actual value, contracted target value, variance, measurement methodology, and data source (Nielsen, Kantar, internal analytics). Feeds ROI reporting and renewal negotiations.'
}

Table sponsorship.payment_schedule {
  payment_schedule_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the payment schedule milestone record within the sponsorship domain. Primary key for this entity.']
  ar_invoice_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.ar_invoice. Business justification: Each payment milestone triggers an AR invoice in the finance system. Finance uses this link for cash application, dunning, and revenue reporting. The denormalized `invoice_number` text field is replac']
  company_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.company_code. Business justification: Payment schedules must post to the correct legal entity\'s books for intercompany billing, tax compliance, and SOX reporting. The existing sap_company_code plain attribute is a denormalized code; repla']
  deal_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent sponsorship deal contract that this payment schedule milestone belongs to. Links the milestone to its governing commercial agreement.']
  deal_term_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sponsorship.deal_term. Business justification: Payment schedule milestones are governed by specific deal terms — deal_term defines payment_schedule_type, invoice_milestone_count, late_payment_penalty_rate_pct, and escalation terms that directly dr']
  fiscal_period_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.fiscal_period. Business justification: Payment schedule milestones must align to fiscal periods for accrual accounting, cash flow forecasting, and period-close revenue recognition. Finance teams run period-level sponsorship cash receipt fo']
  franchise_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to league.franchise. Business justification: Payment milestones for franchise-specific sponsorship deals must be attributed to the franchise for AR management, franchise-level revenue allocation, and revenue-sharing calculations. Without this FK']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Payment milestones post to specific GL accounts (deferred revenue, sponsorship revenue receivable). Finance requires GL account assignment for accurate financial posting and period-end close. The deno']
  league_season_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to league.season. Business justification: Payment milestones are triggered by season events (season opener, playoff qualification, championship). Revenue recognition aligned to league season is a standard finance and audit requirement; struct']
  nil_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to athlete.nil_agreement. Business justification: NIL deal payment tracking requires linking payment schedules to the specific NIL agreement being paid against. Finance and athlete management teams use this for NIL income reporting, agent commission ']
  operator_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to gaming.operator. Business justification: Payment schedules for gaming operator sponsors require operator-level tracking for tax withholding compliance, regulatory remittance validation, and license status verification before payment release.']
  regulatory_filing_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_filing. Business justification: Payment schedules involving withholding tax and cross-border payments require regulatory filings (tax authority filings, foreign payment disclosures). payment_schedule has withholding_tax_amount as a ']
  revenue_recognition_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.revenue_recognition_schedule. Business justification: ASC 606 / IFRS 15 requires sponsorship revenue to be recognized as performance obligations are satisfied, not when cash is received. Finance links each payment milestone to a recognition schedule to c']
  sponsor_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the sponsor (brand or corporate entity) responsible for this payment obligation. Supports accounts receivable aging and sponsor-level cash flow reporting.']
  adjustment_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary adjustment applied to the scheduled amount for this milestone, including contractual discounts, performance-based deductions, early payment incentives, or penalty surcharges. Negative values represent reductions; positive values represent surcharges.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'SAP S/4HANA Controlling (CO) cost center code associated with the business unit responsible for fulfilling the sponsorship obligations tied to this payment milestone. Supports internal profitability analysis and EBITDA reporting.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this payment schedule milestone record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and SOX financial controls compliance.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the payment milestone amounts (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP, AUD). Supports multi-currency sponsorship deals across global markets.']
  days_overdue varchar [note: 'Number of calendar days this payment milestone is past its due_date without confirmed receipt. Calculated as current date minus due_date when payment_status is \'overdue\'. Used for accounts receivable aging buckets and collections escalation triggers.']
  dispute_raised_date date [note: 'Date on which the sponsor formally raised a dispute for this payment milestone. Null if no dispute has been raised. Used to track dispute resolution SLA compliance and aging.']
  dispute_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text description of the reason provided by the sponsor for disputing this payment milestone. Populated when payment_status is \'disputed\'. Supports dispute resolution tracking and contract management.']
  due_date date [note: 'Contractually agreed date by which the sponsor must remit this payment milestone. Used for accounts receivable aging, overdue detection, and cash flow forecasting in Workday Adaptive Planning.']
  dunning_date date [note: 'Date on which the most recent dunning (collections) notice was issued to the sponsor for this overdue payment milestone. Null if no dunning has been initiated. Used to track collections activity and enforce escalation timelines.']
  dunning_level varchar [note: 'Current dunning (collections escalation) level for this overdue payment milestone, as managed in SAP S/4HANA FI dunning procedures. Level 0 = no dunning initiated; higher levels indicate escalating collection notices sent to the sponsor.']
  early_payment_discount_date date [note: 'Last date by which the sponsor must remit payment to qualify for the early payment discount on this milestone. Null if no early payment discount applies.']
  early_payment_discount_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage discount offered to the sponsor for early payment of this milestone, as defined in the payment terms (e.g., 2.00 for a 2% early payment discount). Null if no early payment discount applies.']
  forecast_period varchar [note: 'Fiscal quarter in which this payment milestone is expected to be received for cash flow forecasting purposes, formatted as YYYY-Q{N} (e.g., 2025-Q2). Used in Workday Adaptive Planning for sponsorship revenue forecasting and EBITDA projections.']
  invoice_date date [note: 'Date on which the invoice was formally issued to the sponsor for this payment milestone. Establishes the billing cycle start for payment terms calculation and revenue recognition timing.']
  late_fee_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Contractual late payment penalty fee assessed against the sponsor for overdue payment of this milestone, as defined in the sponsorship deal terms. Expressed in the deal currency. Zero or null if no late fee applies or has been assessed.']
  milestone_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive label for this payment milestone as defined in the sponsorship contract (e.g., \'Q1 Installment\', \'Season Opener Activation Fee\', \'Signing Bonus\', \'Annual Naming Rights Fee\'). Used in sponsor communications and invoice descriptions.']
  milestone_sequence varchar [note: 'Ordinal position of this payment milestone within the overall deal payment schedule (e.g., 1 = first installment, 2 = second installment). Supports chronological ordering and installment tracking.']
  net_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Final net amount due after applying all adjustments to the scheduled amount (scheduled_amount + adjustment_amount). This is the amount reflected on the issued invoice and tracked in SAP S/4HANA accounts receivable.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional context, internal commentary, or special instructions related to this payment milestone (e.g., \'Sponsor requested 30-day extension per email dated 2025-01-15\', \'VIK to be fulfilled via stadium signage Q2\'). For internal use by the sponsorship and finance teams.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'Instrument or mechanism used by the sponsor to remit this payment (e.g., wire_transfer, ACH, check, credit_card, direct_debit, offset). Distinct from payment_type which captures the consideration form. Used for treasury reconciliation and PCI-DSS compliance tracking.']
  payment_reference varchar [note: 'Sponsor-provided remittance reference or bank transaction reference number accompanying the payment. Used for automated cash application and matching in SAP S/4HANA accounts receivable.']
  payment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of this payment milestone. \'scheduled\' = not yet invoiced; \'invoiced\' = invoice issued awaiting receipt; \'received\' = funds confirmed; \'overdue\' = past due date without receipt; \'cancelled\' = milestone voided; \'disputed\' = sponsor has raised a formal dispute.']
  payment_terms_code varchar [note: 'SAP S/4HANA payment terms code governing the due date calculation and early payment discount conditions for this milestone (e.g., \'NET30\', \'NET60\', \'2/10NET30\'). Derived from the sponsorship deal contract terms.']
  payment_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the payment consideration form. \'cash\' = monetary wire/ACH/check; \'VIK\' = Value-In-Kind (goods or services in lieu of cash); \'barter\' = reciprocal exchange of services; \'offset\' = applied against a credit balance; \'credit_note\' = formal credit adjustment. Drives revenue recognition treatment under ASC 606.']
  profit_center_code varchar [note: 'SAP S/4HANA Controlling (CO) profit center code for revenue attribution of this payment milestone. Enables segment-level P&L reporting by league, venue, or business unit for management accounting and ROI analysis.']
  received_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Actual amount received from the sponsor for this milestone. May differ from net_amount in cases of partial payment, short payment, or currency conversion variance. Null until payment is received.']
  received_date date [note: 'Actual date on which the sponsor\'s payment was confirmed as received and cleared in the bank or SAP FI clearing account. Null when payment has not yet been received. Used for days-sales-outstanding (DSO) calculation and cash flow actuals.']
  revenue_recognition_date date [note: 'Date on which the revenue associated with this payment milestone is recognized in the financial statements under ASC 606 / IFRS 15. May differ from the received_date for multi-period or performance-obligation-based sponsorship deals.']
  sap_fi_document_number varchar [note: 'SAP S/4HANA Financial Accounting document number associated with the accounts receivable posting for this payment milestone. Serves as the primary cross-reference between the sponsorship data product and the general ledger for audit and reconciliation purposes.']
  schedule_number varchar [note: 'Externally-visible business identifier for this payment schedule milestone, formatted as PS-{YYYY}-{NNNNNN}. Used in sponsor-facing invoices, SAP FI correspondence, and accounts receivable reconciliation.']
  scheduled_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Gross contractual amount due for this payment milestone as specified in the sponsorship deal. Represents the base obligation before any adjustments, discounts, or withholding taxes. Expressed in the deal currency.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this payment schedule milestone record was most recently modified. Tracks the latest change for audit trail, data lineage, and change management purposes.']
  vik_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the goods or services provided as Value-In-Kind (VIK) consideration for this milestone when payment_type is \'VIK\' or \'barter\'. Captures the nature, quantity, and estimated fair market value of the non-cash consideration for revenue recognition purposes.']
  withholding_tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Amount of withholding tax deducted from this payment milestone by the sponsor, applicable for cross-border sponsorship deals subject to bilateral tax treaties. Impacts net cash received and requires separate GL posting in SAP S/4HANA.']

  Note: 'Master record defining the contractual payment milestones and installment schedule for a sponsorship deal. Captures milestone name, due date, payment amount, payment type (cash, VIK, barter), invoice reference, payment status (scheduled, invoiced, received, overdue), and SAP S/4HANA FI document number. Supports accounts receivable tracking and cash flow forecasting for the finance domain.'
}

Table sponsorship.property {

  inventory_units_sold varchar [note: 'Number of sponsorship inventory units currently sold or committed.']

  season_end_date date [note: 'End date of the season or active period for this property.']
  season_start_date date [note: 'Start date of the season or active period for this property.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User or system identifier that created this property record.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for property. Referenced by property_id.'
}

Table sponsorship.inventory_unit {
  inventory_unit_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for inventory_unit']
  franchise_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to league.franchise. Business justification: Inventory units (jersey patches, in-arena LED signage, team digital overlays) are franchise-specific assets. Franchise-level inventory valuation, availability management, and rate card pricing require']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Inventory units (broadcast spots, signage placements, digital impressions) have base_rate_amount and revenue that must post to specific GL accounts by inventory type for revenue classification. Financ']
  parent_inventory_unit_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on inventory_unit (parent_inventory_unit_id)']
  platform_channel_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to content.platform_channel. Business justification: Inventory units (digital ad slots, OTT pre-rolls, social placements) belong to specific platform channels. The existing `platform` text field is denormalized. Linking to platform_channel enables accur']
  policy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.policy. Business justification: Inventory unit definitions (broadcast slots, physical signage positions) are governed by compliance policies (FCC rules for broadcast units, ADA requirements for physical signage). Inventory unit setu']
  season_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to league.season. Business justification: Inventory units have season-specific availability windows, CPM benchmarks, and impression estimates. Season-scoped inventory planning, pre-season rate card publication, and season-end inventory utiliz']
  type_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the type of event where this inventory unit is activated (e.g., weekly show, pay-per-view, live event).']
  venue_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the venue where this inventory unit is located for venue-based sponsorship assets.']
  base_rate_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Standard list price for this inventory unit before discounts or package pricing.']
  cost_per_thousand_cpm decimal(10,2) [note: 'Cost per thousand impressions metric used for media buying comparisons and sponsor ROI analysis.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this inventory unit record was first created in the system.']
  demographic_target varchar [note: 'Primary demographic audience segment this inventory unit reaches (e.g., \'Adults 18-49\', \'Males 25-54\').']
  duration_seconds varchar [note: 'Length of exposure time in seconds for time-based inventory units (broadcast spots, digital video). Null for static placements.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when this inventory unit is retired or no longer available for sale. Null for ongoing availability.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when this inventory unit becomes available for sale and activation.']
  exclusivity_category varchar [note: 'Industry category for which exclusivity is granted (e.g., \'Automotive\', \'Financial Services\', \'Telecommunications\').']
  exclusivity_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this inventory unit offers category exclusivity to the sponsor (true) or allows multiple sponsors (false).']
  format varchar [note: 'Technical format specification for the inventory unit (e.g., \'30-second video spot\', \'728x90 banner\', \'10x10 ft signage\').']
  frequency_cap varchar [note: 'Maximum number of times this inventory unit can be activated per event or time period to prevent oversaturation.']
  geographic_coverage varchar [note: 'Geographic reach scope of the inventory unit for audience measurement and sponsor targeting.']
  impressions_per_event varchar [note: 'Estimated number of impressions delivered per event occurrence for this inventory unit.']
  inventory_unit_category varchar [note: 'High-level category grouping for the inventory unit used for portfolio management and reporting.']
  inventory_unit_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the inventory unit including placement, dimensions, duration, and visibility specifications.']
  inventory_unit_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the inventory unit indicating availability for sale.']
  lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Number of days advance notice required for sponsor creative submission and activation of this inventory unit.']
  maximum_availability_quantity varchar [note: 'Maximum number of units available for sale per time period to maintain inventory scarcity and value.']
  measurement_method varchar [note: 'Methodology used to measure and report performance of this inventory unit (e.g., \'Nielsen Ratings\', \'Google Analytics\', \'Manual Count\').']
  minimum_commitment_quantity varchar [note: 'Minimum number of units or activations required for purchase of this inventory unit.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this inventory unit record was last updated.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes, restrictions, or special instructions for this inventory unit relevant to sales and fulfillment teams.']
  package_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this inventory unit can be included in bundled sponsorship packages (true) or must be sold standalone (false).']
  placement_location varchar [note: 'Physical or digital location where the sponsorship asset appears (e.g., \'Arena North Entrance\', \'Broadcast Overlay\', \'Mobile App Home Screen\').']
  production_requirements varchar [note: 'Technical specifications and production requirements for sponsor creative assets (file formats, dimensions, lead times).']
  rate_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the base rate amount.']
  rate_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the base rate pricing (e.g., per event, per season, per thousand impressions).']
  reach_estimate varchar [note: 'Estimated unique audience reach for this inventory unit across all distribution channels.']
  seasonality_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this inventory unit is available year-round (false) or only during specific seasons or events (true).']
  unit_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier code for the inventory unit used in sponsor proposals and contracts.']
  unit_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the sponsorship inventory unit (e.g., \'Ring Apron Logo\', \'Broadcast Lower Third\', \'Jersey Patch\').']
  unit_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the inventory unit by media channel or activation type.']
  visibility_rating varchar [note: 'Qualitative rating of the inventory unit\'s prominence and visibility to audiences.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for inventory_unit. Referenced by inventory_unit_id.'
}

The existing athlete_association is a plain-text denormalization. A proper FK enables athlete-level royalty calculation, licen']
