Compliance teams enforce brand usage policies at SKU level during product approval. sku_catalog.brand_compliance_app']
  franchise_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to league.franchise. Business justification: Franchise-level product catalog filtering, royalty attribution, and brand compliance audits require linking SKUs to the specific franchise whose IP is licensed. team_code is a denormalized franchise i']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: SKUs are assigned to GL accounts for automated revenue and COGS posting during order processing. Standard retail accounting practice; sports merchandise finance requires SKU-level GL assignment for ac']
  league_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to league.league. Business justification: League-level product catalog management, brand compliance reporting, and royalty attribution require linking SKUs to the owning league. league_code is a denormalized league identifier replaced by this']
  league_season_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to league.season. Business justification: Season-specific product launches (championship merchandise, anniversary editions) and end-of-season clearance planning require linking SKUs to the applicable league season. Merchandise planners and li']
  licensing_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to merchandise.licensing_agreement. Business justification: sku_catalog has license_required (BOOLEAN) and license_type (STRING) fields indicating that many SKUs are governed by a specific licensing agreement (e.g., an athlete NIL deal, a franchise IP license,']
  loyalty_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fan.loyalty_program. Business justification: Loyalty-exclusive merchandise (points-only redemption items, member-only products) must be linked to the specific loyalty program that grants access. Sports loyalty programs frequently offer exclusive']
  product_category_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to merchandise.product_category. Business justification: Every SKU belongs to a product category. Currently tracked as STRING attribute \'product_category\'. Normalizing to FK allows joining to product_category master table for category hierarchy, attributes,']
  sponsorship_deal_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sponsorship.deal. Business justification: Co-branded sponsor merchandise SKUs (e.g., sponsor logo apparel, limited-edition activation products) must reference the governing sponsorship deal for IP rights compliance, brand approval workflows, ']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to merchandise.supplier. Business justification: A SKU in the merchandise catalog is manufactured or supplied by a specific supplier. This 1:N relationship (many SKUs per supplier) is essential for procurement, quality tracking, and brand compliance']
  target_segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fan.segment. Business justification: SKUs are targeted at specific fan segments for personalized catalog display and availability (e.g., youth jerseys shown only to youth segment, VIP exclusives to VIP segment). Sports retailers use segm']
  tournament_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to event.tournament. Business justification: Tournament-specific merchandise lines (e.g., World Cup kits, playoff commemorative gear) are created per tournament for licensing compliance and royalty tracking. Merchandising teams launch and manage']
  type_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to event.event_type. Business justification: event_type carries a \'merchandise_applicable\' flag, indicating the model explicitly tracks which event types support merchandise. SKU catalog items are designed for specific event type contexts (playo']
  age_restriction varchar [note: 'Age restriction classification for the SKU. Applies to products such as alcohol-branded merchandise or items with content restrictions. Drives age-gate enforcement on e-commerce and POS channels.']
  barcode varchar [note: 'Universal Product Code (UPC) or European Article Number (EAN) barcode assigned to the SKU for POS scanning, inventory management, and retail distribution. Aligns with GS1 barcode standards.']
  brand_compliance_approved boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the SKU has received formal brand compliance approval from the relevant league or team IP owner. Must be True before the SKU can be activated for sale. Supports IP protection and brand integrity workflows.']
  brand_name varchar [note: 'The licensed or proprietary brand associated with the SKU (e.g., Nike, Fanatics, Mitchell & Ness, New Era, \'47 Brand). Critical for brand compliance tracking, royalty calculation, and IP protection workflows.']
  color_hex_code varchar [note: 'Hexadecimal color code representing the primary color of the SKU for digital display consistency across e-commerce platforms, digital asset management (DAM), and brand compliance validation.']
  color_name varchar [note: 'Primary color name or color combination of the SKU variant as displayed to consumers (e.g., \'Lakers Gold\', \'Midnight Navy\', \'Heather Grey\'). Aligns with Shopify Plus variant option for color and supports visual merchandising.']
  compliance_approved_date date [note: 'Date on which brand compliance approval was granted by the league or team IP owner for this SKU. Used for audit trails, licensing compliance reporting, and SOX financial controls.']
  cost_price decimal(10,2) [note: 'Standard cost of goods for this SKU representing the landed cost per unit including manufacturing, materials, and inbound freight. Used for gross margin calculation, inventory valuation, and EBITDA reporting.']
  country_of_origin varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code indicating where the product was manufactured (e.g., USA, CHN, BGD, VNM). Required for customs declarations, import duty calculation, and consumer labeling compliance.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the SKU record was first created in the merchandise catalog system. Audit trail field supporting data lineage, SOX compliance, and Silver Layer ingestion tracking.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the MSRP and cost price fields (e.g., USD, GBP, EUR, CAD). Supports multi-currency pricing for international merchandise operations.']
  discontinuation_date date [note: 'Date on which the SKU was or is scheduled to be discontinued and removed from active sales channels. Null for currently active SKUs. Supports end-of-life inventory planning and catalog archival.']
  edition_size varchar [note: 'Total number of units in the limited edition production run. Applicable only when is_limited_edition is True. Used for scarcity marketing, collector value assessment, and inventory allocation.']
  gender_target varchar [note: 'Target gender or age demographic for the SKU used for product classification, catalog filtering, and merchandising segmentation across sales channels.']
  harmonized_tariff_code varchar [note: 'Harmonized System (HS) tariff classification code used for international trade, customs clearance, and import duty determination. Required for cross-border merchandise distribution and third-party retail export compliance.']
  is_autographed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the SKU is an autographed memorabilia item. True triggers certificate of authenticity (COA) requirements, premium pricing tiers, and specialized inventory handling protocols.']
  is_game_used boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the SKU is a game-used item (equipment or apparel actually used during an official game or event). True requires provenance documentation, chain-of-custody tracking, and premium authentication certification.']
  is_limited_edition boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the SKU is a limited edition release with a defined production run cap. True triggers limited edition pricing strategy, scarcity marketing, and edition size tracking.']
  launch_date date [note: 'Planned or actual date on which the SKU was first made available for sale across authorized channels. Used for product lifecycle management, seasonal planning, and marketing campaign alignment.']
  license_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this SKU requires an active licensing agreement to manufacture, distribute, or sell. True for all officially licensed league, team, or athlete-branded merchandise. Drives compliance checks in the licensing workflow.']
  lifecycle_status varchar [note: 'Current state of the SKU within its product lifecycle. \'active\' = available for sale across channels; \'discontinued\' = no longer produced or sold; \'draft\' = pending approval before activation; \'seasonal\' = available only during defined seasonal windows; \'archived\' = retained for historical reference only; \'blocked\' = temporarily suspended from sale (e.g., IP dispute, quality hold).']
  material_composition varchar [note: 'Fabric or material composition of the product (e.g., \'100% Polyester\', \'60% Cotton 40% Polyester\', \'Genuine Leather\'). Required for product labeling compliance, customs declarations, and consumer transparency regulations.']
  msrp decimal(10,2) [note: 'Manufacturer\'s Suggested Retail Price in the base currency (USD) for this SKU. Serves as the reference price point for all sales channels including Shopify Plus e-commerce, venue POS, and third-party retail. Does not represent actual transaction price.']
  product_description varchar [note: 'Detailed marketing and operational description of the product including materials, features, fit, and intended use. Used for e-commerce listings, catalog publishing, and digital asset management.']
  product_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable commercial name of the merchandise product as displayed on sales channels, e-commerce storefronts, and POS systems (e.g., \'Los Angeles Lakers City Edition Jersey – LeBron James #23\').']
  product_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the SKU represents a physical tangible good, a digital product (e.g., digital collectible, downloadable content), or a bundle of multiple items. Drives fulfillment routing and revenue recognition treatment.']
  requires_authentication boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this SKU requires a certificate of authenticity (COA) or third-party authentication (e.g., PSA/DNA, JSA) before sale. Applies to autographed memorabilia, game-used items, and high-value collectibles.']
  royalty_rate decimal(5,4) [note: 'Contractual royalty rate expressed as a decimal fraction of net sales revenue payable to the licensor (league, team, or athlete) for each unit sold. For example, 0.1500 = 15% royalty. Used in royalty tracking and financial reporting per licensing agreements.']
  sales_channel_availability varchar [note: 'Pipe-delimited list of sales channels where this SKU is authorized for sale (e.g., \'shopify_online|venue_pos|third_party_retail|dtc_app\'). Drives omnichannel availability logic and channel-specific pricing rules. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: shopify_online|venue_pos|third_party_retail|dtc_app|wholesale|international — promote to reference product]']
  sap_material_number varchar [note: 'SAP S/4HANA Materials Management (MM) material number (MATNR field) for this SKU. Enables reconciliation between the merchandise catalog and SAP procurement, inventory valuation, and financial reporting modules.']
  shopify_variant_code varchar [note: 'Native variant identifier from the Shopify Plus e-commerce platform used for system-of-record reconciliation, API integration, and omnichannel order management. Enables traceability between the Silver Layer catalog and the Shopify Plus source system.']
  size_code varchar [note: 'Standardized size designation for the SKU variant (e.g., XS, S, M, L, XL, XXL, 3XL, 7, 7.5, 32x30). Null for non-sized items such as collectibles or accessories. Aligns with Shopify Plus variant option for size.']
  sku_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric code that uniquely identifies a specific product variant (team, style, size, color combination) across all sales channels including Shopify Plus, POS, and third-party retail. Serves as the SSOT business identifier for omnichannel product lookup.']
  taxable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this SKU is subject to sales tax. Drives tax calculation at POS and e-commerce checkout. Certain memorabilia or resale items may have different tax treatment depending on jurisdiction.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the SKU record in the merchandise catalog. Supports change data capture (CDC), data lineage tracking, and audit compliance in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  weight_grams decimal(8,2) [note: 'Physical weight of the SKU in grams used for shipping rate calculation, customs documentation, and carrier SLA compliance across e-commerce fulfillment and third-party retail distribution.']

  Note: 'Master product catalog for all official merchandise SKUs including team apparel, memorabilia, accessories, and licensed goods. Serves as the SSOT for product identity, descriptions, brand classifications, league/team associations, and product lifecycle status across all sales channels (Shopify Plus, POS, third-party retail). Tracks SKU codes, product names, categories, sizes, colors, materials, MSRP, and licensing requirements.'
}

Table merchandise.inventory_position {
  inventory_position_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each inventory position record representing a SKU-location snapshot in the merchandise domain.']
  activation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sponsorship.sponsorship_activation. Business justification: Sponsor activations at events require pre-allocated merchandise inventory (giveaways, co-branded items). Linking inventory_position to sponsorship_activation enables activation fulfillment tracking an']
  brand_compliance_policy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.policy. Business justification: inventory_position.brand_compliance_status is a policy-driven field. Compliance audits of inventory brand compliance require traceability to the governing policy. Sports merchandise compliance teams a']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Inventory is valued and allocated to cost centers for balance sheet reporting and internal P&L. Sports merchandise finance teams require cost-center-level inventory valuation for period-end close and ']
  event_day_ops_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to venue.event_day_ops. Business justification: Event-day inventory snapshots (captured via snapshot_timestamp and is_event_allocated fields) must be tied to the specific event day ops record for shrinkage analysis, end-of-event stock reconciliatio']
  fiscal_period_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.fiscal_period. Business justification: Period-end inventory valuation and COGS calculation require inventory snapshots tied to fiscal periods. Finance teams run inventory close reports by fiscal period; this FK enables that linkage directl']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Inventory positions are posted to inventory asset GL accounts for balance sheet valuation. Linking inventory_position to gl_account supports automated inventory accounting, shrinkage posting, and peri']
  licensing_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the IP licensing agreement governing the merchandise SKU at this location. Required for royalty calculation, brand compliance audits, and IP protection reporting.']
  primary_inventory_event_fixture_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific event (game, concert, championship) for which this inventory position has been allocated. Populated only when is_event_allocated is true. Supports event-day stocking and post-event reconciliation.']
  primary_inventory_retail_location_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the storage location (venue store, warehouse fulfillment center, or third-party retail consignment site) where this inventory position is held.']
  primary_inventory_sku_catalog_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the merchandise SKU catalog entry for which this inventory position is tracked. Links to the product master.']
  reserved_segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fan.segment. Business justification: Inventory is pre-allocated for specific fan segments during VIP pre-sales, member-exclusive drops, and season ticket holder windows. This FK enables inventory reservation management by segment, a stan']
  season_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to league.season. Business justification: Season-scoped inventory planning and end-of-season valuation are named business processes in sports merchandise operations. Inventory positions must be attributed to a league season for seasonal stock']
  security_incident_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to security.security_incident. Business justification: Loss prevention reconciliation: inventory shrinkage (shrinkage_qty) at retail locations must be linked to the triggering security incident (theft, vandalism) for insurance claim filing, loss preventio']
  tertiary_inventory_transfer_destination_location_retail_location_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the destination storage location for the most recent or active stock transfer affecting this inventory position. Used for event-day venue stocking and warehouse rebalancing.']
  tournament_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to event.tournament. Business justification: Tournament-level inventory pre-positioning and allocation (e.g., Super Bowl merchandise stock at host venue) is a distinct operational need from fixture-level allocation. Inventory managers report on ']
  available_to_sell_qty varchar [note: 'Net units of the SKU at this location that can be sold or allocated to new orders. Calculated as on_hand_qty minus reserved_qty minus blocked_qty. Drives omnichannel availability display and reorder triggers.']
  blocked_qty varchar [note: 'Units of the SKU held at this location that are blocked from sale due to quality inspection, compliance hold, damage assessment, or licensing dispute. Tracked separately from reserved stock.']
  brand_compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current brand compliance status of the SKU inventory at this location. Non-compliant or under-review stock may be blocked from sale pending IP or licensing verification.']
  count_variance_qty varchar [note: 'Difference between last_physical_count_qty and system on_hand_qty at the time of the count. Positive values indicate system over-count; negative values indicate shrinkage or unrecorded losses.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this inventory position record was first created in the lakehouse silver layer. Used for data lineage, audit trail, and SLA compliance monitoring.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the unit_cost and inventory_value fields (e.g., USD, GBP, EUR). Supports multi-market merchandise operations across global venues.']
  in_transit_qty varchar [note: 'Units of the SKU currently in transit to this location from another storage location (warehouse, venue, or third-party). Included in inventory planning but not yet physically received.']
  inventory_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total financial value of on-hand stock for this SKU at this location (on_hand_qty × unit_cost). Used for balance sheet inventory reporting and EBITDA analysis. Confidential financial data.']
  is_consignment boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the inventory at this location is held on consignment with a third-party retailer. Consignment stock requires separate royalty tracking and licensing compliance under IP agreements.']
  is_event_allocated boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this inventory position has been specifically allocated for an upcoming event (e.g., game day, concert, championship). Event-allocated stock is excluded from general omnichannel availability.']
  last_movement_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent stock movement (sale, transfer, adjustment, receipt) that changed the on-hand quantity for this SKU-location combination. Critical for real-time inventory sync validation.']
  last_movement_type varchar [note: 'Type of the most recent stock movement that updated this inventory position. Used for audit trail and inventory reconciliation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: sale|return|transfer_in|transfer_out|adjustment|receipt|write_off|event_allocation — promote to reference product]']
  last_physical_count_date date [note: 'Date on which the most recent physical inventory count was conducted for this SKU at this location. Used to assess count freshness and schedule cycle counts per SOX and internal audit requirements.']
  last_physical_count_qty varchar [note: 'Actual unit count recorded during the most recent physical inventory count for this SKU at this location. Compared against system on-hand quantity to identify shrinkage or discrepancies.']
  location_code varchar [note: 'Alphanumeric code identifying the physical storage location (e.g., venue store bin, warehouse zone, consignment shelf). Used for warehouse management and event-day stocking operations.']
  location_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the storage location where inventory is held. Drives omnichannel fulfillment routing, event-day stocking logic, and consignment royalty tracking. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: venue_store|warehouse|consignment|pop_up|online_fulfillment|third_party_retail — promote to reference product]']
  max_stock_qty varchar [note: 'Upper capacity limit for this SKU at this storage location. Prevents over-stocking and supports space planning for venue stores and warehouse zones.']
  on_hand_qty varchar [note: 'Total physical units of the SKU currently present at the storage location, including reserved and blocked stock. Sourced from Shopify Plus inventory sync or warehouse management system.']
  pos_location_code varchar [note: 'Identifier for the POS terminal or venue store location as registered in the POS system. Used to reconcile POS transaction-driven inventory deductions with Shopify Plus and SAP inventory records.']
  position_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the inventory position record. \'blocked\' indicates stock held for quality inspection or compliance review; \'pending_audit\' indicates a physical count discrepancy under investigation.']
  reorder_point_qty varchar [note: 'Stock level threshold at which a replenishment order should be initiated for this SKU at this location. Accounts for lead time and safety stock. Used by merchandise operations for automated reorder triggers.']
  reorder_qty varchar [note: 'Standard replenishment order quantity for this SKU at this location when the reorder point is reached. Reflects economic order quantity or vendor minimum order quantity.']
  reserved_qty varchar [note: 'Units of the SKU at this location that are committed to open orders, event-day allocations, or transfer requests and are not available for new sales. Sourced from Shopify Plus committed inventory.']
  safety_stock_qty varchar [note: 'Minimum buffer stock level for the SKU at this location below which a replenishment alert is triggered. Set by merchandise planners to prevent stockouts during peak event periods.']
  shopify_inventory_item_code varchar [note: 'External identifier assigned by Shopify Plus to the inventory item for this SKU. Used for real-time inventory sync between Shopify Plus and the lakehouse silver layer.']
  shopify_location_code varchar [note: 'External identifier assigned by Shopify Plus to the fulfillment location. Used to reconcile Shopify inventory levels with SAP and POS system records across omnichannel operations.']
  shrinkage_qty varchar [note: 'Cumulative units of the SKU lost at this location due to theft, damage, or unrecorded consumption since the last physical count. Used for loss prevention reporting and inventory reconciliation.']
  sku_code varchar [note: 'Human-readable alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the merchandise SKU, sourced from Shopify Plus or SAP material master. Used for omnichannel inventory reconciliation and POS scanning.']
  snapshot_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time at which this inventory position record was captured or last refreshed from the source system (Shopify Plus, POS, or warehouse management). Represents the as-of time for all quantity fields.']
  transfer_completion_date date [note: 'Date on which the most recent stock transfer to or from this inventory position was completed and receiving confirmation was recorded.']
  transfer_qty varchar [note: 'Number of units involved in the most recent or active stock transfer for this SKU-location. Captured to support transfer reconciliation and event-day stocking verification.']
  transfer_reason varchar [note: 'Business reason for the most recent stock transfer affecting this inventory position. Supports event-day logistics planning and omnichannel inventory optimization reporting.']
  transfer_status varchar [note: 'Current workflow status of the most recent stock transfer associated with this inventory position. Drives in-transit quantity calculations and fulfillment center receiving workflows.']
  unit_cost decimal(18,4) [note: 'Standard or moving average cost per unit of the SKU at this location used for inventory valuation. Sourced from SAP S/4HANA material ledger. Confidential as it reflects supplier pricing and margin data.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this inventory position record was last modified in the lakehouse silver layer. Supports incremental data processing, change data capture, and audit trail requirements.']
  valuation_method varchar [note: 'Accounting method used to value inventory for this SKU at this location. Determines how unit_cost is calculated and reported on financial statements. Must align with SAP material ledger configuration.']

  Note: 'Real-time inventory management record tracking on-hand stock quantities, reserved quantities, available-to-sell units, and stock movement history for each SKU across all storage locations — venue stores, warehouse fulfillment centers, and third-party retail consignment. Captures current position (reorder points, safety stock levels, inventory valuation per location) and transfer activity (origin, destination, quantities moved, transfer reason such as rebalancing or event-day stocking, transfer status, completion date). Sourced from Shopify Plus inventory sync, POS systems, and warehouse management. Supports omnichannel inventory optimization, event-day venue stocking, and inventory reconciliation.'
}

Table merchandise.retail_location {
  retail_location_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each retail point-of-presence record in the merchandise sales channel master. Primary key for the retail_location data product.']

Security staffing plans, incident response, and evacuation procedures require knowing which zone each re']
  company_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.company_code. Business justification: Retail locations belong to specific legal entities (company codes) for statutory financial reporting, tax registration, and intercompany transactions. Multi-entity sports organizations require this fo']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Each retail location maps to a cost center for location-level P&L reporting, budgeting, and expense allocation. Sports organizations track profitability by store/venue location. cost_center_code is a ']
  facility_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to venue.facility. Business justification: Retail stores and kiosks inside a venue are physically housed in a specific facility building. Facility-level ADA compliance, OSHA ratings, BMS zones, and event-day operational readiness checks all ap']
  franchise_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to league.franchise. Business justification: Franchise-operated team stores require franchise attribution for revenue sharing, royalty reporting, and brand compliance audits. A sports merchandise domain expert would expect every team store to re']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Retail locations are profit centers in sports merchandise operations, enabling revenue and margin reporting by location. Required for segment reporting and management P&L. profit_center_code is a deno']
  regulatory_filing_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_filing. Business justification: Retail locations require regulatory filings: business licenses, sales tax registrations, ADA compliance filings, and gaming/lottery permits for merchandise contests. retail_location.ada_compliant and ']
  venue_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent venue or facility with which this retail location is physically associated (e.g., stadium, arena, event center). Null for standalone e-commerce or third-party locations not tied to a specific venue.']
  ada_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the physical retail location meets Americans with Disabilities Act (ADA) accessibility requirements including accessible entrances, checkout counters, and pathways. Required for venue compliance reporting and fan experience standards.']
  address_line_1 varchar [note: 'Primary street address of the physical retail location. Required for venue stores, kiosks, warehouse outlets, and pop-up shops. Null for pure e-commerce storefronts.']
  address_line_2 varchar [note: 'Secondary address detail for the physical retail location (suite, unit, concourse level, gate reference). Supplements address_line_1 for precise wayfinding within large venue complexes.']
  channel_segment varchar [note: 'Sales channel segment classification for the retail location. Distinguishes Direct-To-Consumer (DTC) owned channels from wholesale, marketplace, venue retail, and concession operations. Drives revenue attribution, royalty calculation, and licensing compliance reporting.']
  city varchar [note: 'City in which the retail location is situated. Used for geographic sales reporting, tax jurisdiction determination, and regional merchandise allocation.']
  close_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the retail location permanently closed or was decommissioned. Null for currently active locations. Used for lifecycle reporting, lease termination tracking, and historical channel analysis.']
  contact_email varchar [note: 'Primary contact email address for the retail location. Used for operational notifications, inventory alerts, and inter-department communications. Organizational contact data classified as confidential.']
  contact_phone varchar [note: 'Primary contact telephone number for the retail location. Used for operational communications, vendor deliveries, and fan-facing store locator directory.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 three-letter country code for the retail location (e.g., USA, GBR, CAN). Drives currency defaults, tax rules, GDPR/CCPA applicability, and international merchandise licensing compliance.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this retail location record was first created in the system. Serves as the RECORD_AUDIT_CREATED field for the MASTER_RESOURCE role. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  data_source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record from which this retail location record was sourced or last updated (e.g., Shopify Plus, SAP S/4HANA SD, Archtics). Supports data lineage, master data governance, and Silver layer reconciliation.']
  default_currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code used as the default transaction currency for this retail location (e.g., USD, GBP, EUR). Drives POS configuration, Shopify Plus storefront currency, and SAP SD pricing procedures.']
  event_day_hours varchar [note: 'Extended or modified operating hours applied on event days (game days, concerts, PPV events). Overrides standard weekday/weekend hours when an event is scheduled at the associated venue. Format: HH:MM-HH:MM.']
  floor_area_sqft decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total usable retail floor area of the location in square feet. Used for merchandise density planning, inventory capacity modeling, and lease/facility cost allocation. Serves as the MEASUREMENT_OR_VALUE for the MASTER_RESOURCE role.']
  inventory_location_code varchar [note: 'SAP S/4HANA MM storage location or warehouse code linked to this retail location for inventory management, stock replenishment, and goods movement tracking. Enables real-time inventory visibility across omnichannel merchandise operations.']
  is_flagship boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this retail location is designated as the flagship store for the associated venue or brand. Flagship stores receive priority inventory allocation, exclusive product launches, and premium visual merchandising standards.']
  is_online boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this retail location is a virtual/online storefront (e.g., Shopify Plus e-commerce) rather than a physical location. Drives omnichannel routing logic, inventory fulfillment rules, and digital analytics tagging.']
  last_audit_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent compliance or operational audit conducted at this retail location. Covers PCI-DSS POS terminal audits, ADA accessibility inspections, and merchandise licensing compliance reviews.']
  latitude decimal(9,6) [note: 'Geographic latitude coordinate of the retail location in decimal degrees (WGS 84). Enables proximity-based fan engagement, geofencing campaigns via Adobe Experience Platform, and venue map integrations.']
  license_territory varchar [note: 'Geographic territory designation under which this retail location is authorized to sell licensed merchandise (e.g., \'NFL_NFC_WEST\', \'FIFA_EUROPE\', \'NBA_EASTERN_CONFERENCE\'). Enforces IP licensing boundaries and royalty calculation scope per league governing body agreements.']
  location_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric business identifier for the retail location used across Shopify Plus, POS terminals, and SAP SD sales channel configuration. Serves as the BUSINESS_IDENTIFIER (MASTER_RESOURCE role) for cross-system reconciliation.']
  location_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable display name of the retail point-of-presence (e.g., \'Allegiant Stadium Flagship Store\', \'Gate 4 Kiosk\', \'Official Online Store\'). Serves as the IDENTITY_LABEL for the MASTER_RESOURCE role.']
  location_status varchar [note: 'Current operational lifecycle state of the retail location. Controls whether the location is available for sales transactions, inventory replenishment, and POS terminal assignment. Serves as the LIFECYCLE_STATUS for the MASTER_RESOURCE role.']
  location_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the retail point-of-presence by channel format. Drives operational rules, POS configuration, and omnichannel reporting segmentation. Serves as the CLASSIFICATION_OR_TYPE for the MASTER_RESOURCE role. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: venue_store|kiosk|e_commerce|pop_up|third_party|warehouse_outlet — promote to reference product if additional types emerge]']
  longitude decimal(9,6) [note: 'Geographic longitude coordinate of the retail location in decimal degrees (WGS 84). Used alongside latitude for geospatial analytics, fan proximity targeting, and digital wayfinding.']
  manager_name varchar [note: 'Name of the store manager or location lead responsible for day-to-day operations of this retail location. Used for operational escalation, staffing accountability, and SAP SuccessFactors workforce assignment reference.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this retail location record. Used for change data capture (CDC), data lineage tracking, and Silver layer incremental load processing in the Databricks Lakehouse.']
  open_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the retail location first opened for business or was activated in the system. Used for tenure analysis, lease management, and historical sales performance benchmarking.']
  operating_hours_weekday varchar [note: 'Standard weekday operating hours for the retail location in HH:MM-HH:MM 24-hour format (e.g., \'09:00-21:00\'). Used for fan-facing store locator, staff scheduling, and omnichannel availability configuration.']
  operating_hours_weekend varchar [note: 'Standard weekend operating hours for the retail location in HH:MM-HH:MM 24-hour format (e.g., \'10:00-18:00\'). Distinct from weekday hours to support event-day extended trading and fan engagement scheduling.']
  payment_methods_accepted varchar [note: 'Comma-delimited list of payment methods accepted at this retail location (e.g., \'credit_card,debit_card,cash,mobile_pay,gift_card\'). Drives POS configuration, fan-facing signage, and PCI-DSS scope assessment. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: credit_card|debit_card|cash|mobile_pay|gift_card|crypto — promote to reference product]']
  pos_terminal_count varchar [note: 'Number of active Point of Sale (POS) terminals assigned to this retail location. Used for capacity planning, PCI-DSS terminal compliance audits, and peak event staffing models.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code of the retail location. Used for tax jurisdiction mapping, shipping zone configuration, and geographic market analysis.']
  sales_org_code varchar [note: 'SAP S/4HANA SD sales organization code to which this retail location belongs. Determines pricing procedures, output determination, and revenue recognition rules applicable to merchandise transactions at this location.']
  shopify_location_code varchar [note: 'External identifier assigned by Shopify Plus to this retail location within the e-commerce platform. Used for inventory sync, order routing, and fulfillment assignment via the Shopify Locations API.']
  state_province varchar [note: 'State or province of the retail location. Used for sales tax calculation, regulatory compliance reporting, and regional performance analytics.']
  timezone varchar [note: 'IANA timezone identifier for the retail location (e.g., \'America/New_York\', \'Europe/London\'). Required for accurate operating hours interpretation, event-day scheduling, and cross-market reporting normalization.']

  Note: 'Master record for all physical and virtual retail points-of-presence selling official merchandise — venue flagship stores, arena kiosks, warehouse outlets, pop-up event shops, and online storefronts (Shopify Plus). Tracks location name, type (venue store, kiosk, e-commerce, pop-up, third-party), address, operating hours, POS terminal assignments, associated venue, default currency, supported payment methods, and active status. Serves as the SSOT for retail channel geography and storefront configuration across all merchandise sales channels.'
}

Table merchandise.merch_order {
  merch_order_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for a merchandise order record in the Sports Entertainment lakehouse silver layer. Primary key for the merch_order data product.']
  activation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sponsorship.sponsorship_activation. Business justification: Merchandise orders generated during sponsor activations (scan-to-buy, sponsor-funded fan giveaways) must be attributed to the specific activation for activation ROI measurement and sponsor proof-of-pe']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Merchandise orders are allocated to cost centers for channel-level P&L reporting (e.g., event-day vs. online vs. wholesale). Required for management reporting and budget variance analysis in sports me']
  event_day_ops_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to venue.event_day_ops. Business justification: Event-day merchandise sales reporting requires joining orders to the event day operations record to correlate sales volume with actual attendance, gate times, and operational status. Sports entertainm']
  fan_payment_method_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fan.payment_method. Business justification: Merchandise orders must reference the fan\'s stored payment method for PCI audit trails, fraud detection, and auto-renewal/subscription merchandise billing. Sports entertainment operators require this ']
  league_season_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to league.season. Business justification: Season attribution of merchandise orders is required for league revenue sharing calculations, royalty period reporting, and season-over-season sales analytics. Sports merchandise operations always tra']
  licensing_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Intellectual Property (IP) licensing agreement under which the merchandise in this order was produced and sold. Required for royalty tracking, IP protection compliance, and licensing revenue reporting to league governing bodies.']
  membership_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fan.membership. Business justification: Season-ticket and VIP membership renewals trigger merchandise fulfilment orders (welcome kits, exclusive gear). Linking merch_order to the originating membership enables membership-benefit fulfilment ']
  price_list_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to merchandise.price_list. Business justification: merch_order captures the gross_amount, discount_amount, and net_total but has no reference to which price_list configuration governed the transaction. Adding price_list_id creates an order-level audit']
  primary_merch_event_fixture_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the sports or entertainment event associated with this merchandise order. Enables revenue attribution to specific events for sponsorship and performance reporting.']
  primary_merch_fan_fan_profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the fan or customer who placed this merchandise order. Links to the fan master record in the Fan domain. Null for anonymous or guest checkout transactions.']
  primary_merch_franchise_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the sports team whose licensed merchandise was primarily purchased in this order. Enables team-level merchandise revenue attribution, licensing royalty calculation, and brand performance reporting across the league.']
  promotion_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to merchandise.promotion. Business justification: merch_order.promo_code (STRING) is a denormalized reference to a promotion applied at the order level. promotion is the authoritative master. Adding promotion_id FK normalizes the order-level promotio']
  retail_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to merchandise.retail_location. Business justification: merch_order covers POS transactions at venue stores and online channels. While venue_id links to the venue, the specific retail_location (store within a venue, or online storefront) is the transaction']
  ticket_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ticketing.ticket_order. Business justification: Bundled ticket-merchandise package sales (e.g., VIP ticket + jersey combos) are a core revenue model in sports entertainment. Linking merch_order to the originating ticket_order enables bundle revenue']
  tournament_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to event.tournament. Business justification: Tournament-context merchandise orders (multi-day events, online sales during tournament windows) require tournament attribution for revenue reporting, sponsor activation tracking, and royalty calculat']
  venue_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the venue where the order was placed for in-venue Point of Sale (POS) transactions (arena stores, kiosks, pop-ups). Null for e-commerce and wholesale orders.']
  channel_subtype varchar [note: 'Granular classification of the sales channel within the primary channel type. Enables detailed omnichannel analytics distinguishing mobile app vs. online store within e-commerce, or kiosk vs. arena store within venue POS. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: online_store|mobile_app|arena_store|kiosk|pop_up|third_party_retailer — promote to reference product]']
  channel_type varchar [note: 'The omnichannel sales channel through which the merchandise order was placed. \'ecommerce\' = Shopify Plus Direct-To-Consumer (DTC) online store; \'venue_pos\' = in-venue Point of Sale (POS) terminal at arena stores, kiosks, or pop-up locations; \'wholesale\' = Business-to-Business (B2B) wholesale account order. Drives channel-specific revenue attribution and reporting.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System audit timestamp recording when this merchandise order record was first created in the lakehouse silver layer. Distinct from order_date which captures the business event time. Used for data lineage, SLA monitoring, and ETL audit trails.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary amounts on this order (e.g., USD, GBP, EUR, CAD). Supports multi-currency operations across global markets. All amounts stored in the transaction currency; FX conversion handled in the Finance domain.']
  device_type varchar [note: 'Type of device used to place the order. Enables omnichannel device-mix analytics and informs mobile-first merchandising strategy. \'pos_terminal\' applies to in-venue transactions. Used by Adobe Experience Platform for fan journey personalization.']
  discount_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Total monetary discount applied at the order header level, including promotional codes, loyalty discounts, and event-day specials. Excludes line-level discounts captured on individual order lines. Used for promotional effectiveness analysis and revenue attribution.']
  fulfillment_status varchar [note: 'Aggregate fulfillment state of the order header, reflecting whether all line items have been shipped or handed over. \'partial\' indicates some lines fulfilled and others pending. Drives warehouse operations, customer notifications, and SLA (Service Level Agreement) compliance tracking.']
  gift_order_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the merchandise order was designated as a gift by the purchaser. When true, triggers gift wrapping, suppresses price disclosure on packing slip, and enables gift receipt generation. Relevant for holiday and event-driven merchandise campaigns.']
  gross_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Total pre-discount, pre-tax merchandise value of all line items in the order. Represents the sum of unit prices multiplied by quantities before any adjustments. Used as the base for revenue reporting and discount analysis.']
  ip_address varchar [note: 'IP address of the device used to place the e-commerce order via Shopify Plus. Used for fraud detection, geographic analytics, and GDPR/CCPA compliance audit trails. Null for in-venue POS transactions. Classified as confidential per GDPR Article 4 as it may constitute personal data.']
  loyalty_points_earned varchar [note: 'Number of fan loyalty program points awarded to the customer for this merchandise purchase. Used for loyalty program accrual accounting, fan engagement reporting, and Customer Acquisition Cost (CAC) / Lifetime Value (LTV) analytics.']
  loyalty_points_redeemed varchar [note: 'Number of fan loyalty program points redeemed against this order as a payment offset. Used for loyalty program liability accounting, fan engagement analytics, and Net Promoter Score (NPS) correlation analysis.']
  net_total decimal(18,4) [note: 'Final amount charged to the customer: gross_amount minus discount_amount plus tax_amount plus shipping_amount. Represents the actual revenue collected for this order. Used as the primary revenue figure for financial reporting and reconciliation against payment records.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or special instructions associated with the order, entered by the customer at checkout or by staff during order processing. May include gift messages, personalization requests, or customer service annotations. Not used for structured data.']
  order_date timestamp [note: 'The principal real-world timestamp when the merchandise order was placed by the customer or captured at the Point of Sale (POS) terminal. Represents the business event time, distinct from system audit timestamps. Stored in ISO 8601 format with timezone offset.']
  order_number varchar [note: 'Externally visible, human-readable order reference number surfaced to fans, retail staff, and wholesale partners. Sourced from Shopify Plus for e-commerce orders or the POS system for in-venue transactions. Used for customer service lookups and order tracking.']
  order_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the merchandise order workflow. Drives fulfillment operations, customer communications, and revenue recognition. \'pending\' = awaiting payment confirmation; \'confirmed\' = payment captured; \'processing\' = being picked/packed; \'fulfilled\' = shipped or handed over; \'cancelled\' = voided before fulfillment; \'refunded\' = post-fulfillment reversal.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'The payment instrument type used to settle the merchandise order. Drives PCI DSS compliance scope determination and tender-type reporting. \'credit_card\' and \'debit_card\' are in-scope for PCI DSS. \'digital_wallet\' includes Apple Pay, Google Pay. \'invoice\' applies to wholesale B2B orders.']
  payment_reference varchar [note: 'Payment gateway transaction reference or authorization code for the order. For PCI DSS compliance, this stores only the tokenized or masked payment reference — never raw card numbers, CVV, or full PAN. Used for payment reconciliation and dispute resolution.']
  payment_status varchar [note: 'Current state of the payment transaction associated with this order. Distinct from order_status which tracks fulfillment lifecycle. Used for financial reconciliation, accounts receivable management, and PCI DSS audit trails.']
  pos_terminal_code varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the Point of Sale (POS) terminal device that processed the in-venue transaction. Populated only for venue_pos channel orders. Used for reconciliation, audit trails, and PCI DSS compliance reporting.']
  return_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the order is eligible for return or exchange under the current merchandise return policy. May be false for event-day clearance items, personalized/customized merchandise, or items past the return window. Drives customer service and reverse logistics workflows.']
  return_window_expiry date [note: 'The last date on which a return or exchange can be initiated for this order, calculated from the order date based on the applicable return policy. Used by customer service to validate return eligibility and by operations to manage reverse logistics SLA (Service Level Agreement) compliance.']
  shipping_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Shipping and handling charges applied to the order. Populated for e-commerce orders with physical delivery. Zero or null for in-venue POS transactions where goods are handed over at point of purchase.']
  shipping_country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the shipping destination for e-commerce orders (e.g., USA, GBR, CAN). Used for international shipping compliance, tax jurisdiction determination, and geographic sales reporting. Null for in-venue POS transactions.']
  shipping_method varchar [note: 'The delivery method selected for the order (e.g., Standard Ground, Express 2-Day, Overnight, In-Store Pickup, Will Call). Populated for e-commerce orders. Null for in-venue POS transactions. Used for logistics planning and shipping cost analysis. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: standard_ground|express_2day|overnight|in_store_pickup|will_call|international — promote to reference product]']
  shipping_postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code of the shipping destination for e-commerce orders. Used for geographic sales analysis, regional fan merchandise demand mapping, and shipping carrier zone determination. Classified as confidential address data per GDPR and CCPA.']
  source_order_reference varchar [note: 'The native order identifier from the originating operational system (e.g., Shopify Plus order ID, POS transaction ID, wholesale portal order number). Enables traceability back to the system of record for reconciliation, dispute resolution, and audit purposes.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The operational system of record that originated this merchandise order record. Enables data lineage tracking and system-of-record attribution in the lakehouse silver layer. \'shopify_plus\' = Shopify Plus e-commerce platform; \'venue_pos\' = in-venue POS system; \'wholesale_portal\' = B2B wholesale ordering portal; \'manual\' = manually entered order.']
  tax_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Total tax amount charged on the merchandise order, including applicable sales tax, VAT, or GST based on the jurisdiction of the transaction. Required for financial reporting, tax remittance, and SAP S/4HANA FI tax reconciliation.']
  tax_exempt_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the order is exempt from sales tax, typically applicable to wholesale B2B orders with a valid tax exemption certificate or reseller permit. Drives tax calculation logic and financial reporting for SAP S/4HANA FI tax reconciliation.']
  tax_exemption_ref varchar [note: 'Tax exemption certificate number or reseller permit reference for tax-exempt orders. Populated only when tax_exempt_flag is true. Required for tax audit documentation and compliance with applicable sales tax regulations.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System audit timestamp recording when this merchandise order record was last modified in the lakehouse silver layer. Used for incremental data processing, change data capture, and audit trail compliance.']

  Note: 'Unified transactional record of any merchandise purchase across all channels — Shopify Plus e-commerce, venue POS (arena stores, kiosks, pop-ups), and third-party wholesale. Captures order header (date, channel type, status, total amount, currency, tax, shipping method, fulfillment status) and line-level detail (SKU, quantity, unit price, line discount, line total, line fulfillment status, return eligibility). Channel-specific metadata includes POS terminal ID, cashier ID, payment tender type for in-venue sales; cart session and shipping address for e-commerce; and wholesale account reference for B2B orders. Supports unified omnichannel sales reporting, revenue attribution at SKU level, and PCI-DSS compliant payment data handling. Single source of truth for \'a sale happened\' regardless of channel.'
}

Table merchandise.order_line {
  order_line_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each individual order line item within the merchandise domain. Primary key for this entity.']
  athlete_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to athlete.athlete_profile. Business justification: Per-line royalty amounts are already tracked on order_line (royalty_rate, royalty_amount). Without athlete_profile_id, per-athlete royalty reporting requires multi-hop joins through sku_catalog. A dir']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Order line revenue and COGS must be posted to specific GL accounts for revenue recognition and financial close. Line-level GL assignment supports product-category-level financial reporting required by']
  licensing_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the active licensing agreement governing the intellectual property (IP) rights for the merchandise on this line. Required for royalty tracking, IP compliance, and licensor reporting.']
  merch_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent merchandise order header to which this line item belongs. Establishes the header-detail relationship for order processing and revenue attribution.']
  price_list_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to merchandise.price_list. Business justification: order_line captures the unit_price at execution time but has no reference to which price_list configuration was applied. Adding price_list_id creates an audit trail linking each line item to the gover']
  primary_order_event_fixture_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the sports or entertainment event associated with this merchandise purchase (e.g., a game-day jersey purchase linked to a specific fixture). Enables event-level merchandise revenue attribution and post-event sales analysis.']
  primary_order_franchise_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the sports team or franchise whose branding, intellectual property (IP), or licensing rights are associated with the merchandise SKU on this line. Enables team-level revenue attribution and royalty calculation.']
  primary_order_sku_catalog_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific Stock Keeping Unit (SKU) purchased on this line. Enables SKU-level sales performance tracking, inventory reconciliation, and product-level revenue attribution across channels.']
  product_category_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to merchandise.product_category. Business justification: Order lines capture product category for sales analytics and reporting (category-level revenue, bestsellers by category). Currently tracked as STRING attribute \'product_category\'. Normalizing to FK al']
  promotion_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to merchandise.promotion. Business justification: order_line.promotion_code (STRING) is a denormalized reference to a promotion. promotion is the authoritative master for discount campaigns. Adding promotion_id FK normalizes this reference and enable']
  venue_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the venue where this line item was purchased (applicable for venue Point of Sale (POS) transactions). Supports venue-level sales performance reporting and inventory management.']
  brand_compliance_verified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the merchandise SKU on this line has been verified as compliant with the applicable league or team brand standards and licensing requirements prior to sale. Supports IP protection and brand governance.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this order line record was first created in the source system, in ISO 8601 format with timezone offset. Used for order processing SLA measurement and audit trail.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which all monetary amounts on this line item are expressed (e.g., USD, GBP, EUR). Supports multi-currency operations across global markets.']
  fulfillment_location_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the fulfillment origin for this line item, distinguishing between central warehouse, venue retail store, third-party logistics (3PL) provider, or direct drop-ship from supplier. Supports omnichannel fulfillment analytics.']
  fulfillment_status varchar [note: 'Current fulfillment state of this line item within the order lifecycle. Drives warehouse operations, customer notifications, and order completion tracking.']
  is_gift boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this line item was purchased as a gift, triggering gift wrapping, gift receipt generation, and suppression of price information on packing slips.']
  is_personalized boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this line item includes a personalization or customization (e.g., player name/number printing on a jersey). Personalized items typically have different return eligibility and production lead times.']
  is_return_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this line item is eligible for return based on product type, licensing restrictions, event-specific merchandise policies, or return window expiry. Drives customer-facing return eligibility checks.']
  line_discount_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Total monetary discount applied to this specific line item, including promotional codes, loyalty discounts, and manual overrides. Expressed in the order currency.']
  line_discount_pct decimal(7,4) [note: 'Discount rate applied to this line item expressed as a percentage of the unit price multiplied by quantity. Supports promotional effectiveness analysis and margin impact reporting.']
  line_number varchar [note: 'Sequential position of this line item within the parent order, starting at 1. Used for ordering, display, and reconciliation of multi-line orders.']
  line_subtotal decimal(18,4) [note: 'Pre-tax line total calculated as (unit_price × quantity_ordered) minus line_discount_amount. Represents the net merchandise value of this line before tax. Used for revenue attribution and financial reporting.']
  line_tax_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Total tax amount charged on this line item, aggregating all applicable tax lines (e.g., state, local, VAT). Required for tax remittance reporting and financial reconciliation.']
  line_total decimal(18,4) [note: 'Final total for this line item inclusive of tax and after all discounts (line_subtotal + line_tax_amount). Represents the amount the customer is charged for this specific line. Used for revenue reporting and financial reconciliation.']
  personalization_detail varchar [note: 'Free-text description of the personalization applied to this line item (e.g., \'Name: JOHNSON, Number: 23\'). Populated only when is_personalized is true. Drives production instructions for customization fulfillment.']
  product_name varchar [note: 'Display name of the merchandise product at the time of purchase (e.g., \'Home Jersey 2024 – Size L\'). Captured as a point-in-time snapshot to preserve historical accuracy independent of catalog changes.']
  quantity_fulfilled varchar [note: 'Number of units of this line item that have been physically picked, packed, and shipped or handed over to the customer. Enables partial fulfillment tracking and backorder identification.']
  quantity_ordered varchar [note: 'Number of units of the SKU ordered on this line item. Used for inventory reservation, fulfillment picking, and sales volume reporting.']
  quantity_returned varchar [note: 'Number of units of this line item that have been returned by the customer. Supports return rate analysis, refund processing, and net revenue calculation at the SKU level.']
  requires_shipping boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this line item requires physical shipment to the customer, as opposed to in-venue pickup or digital delivery. Drives shipping cost allocation and logistics workflow routing.']
  return_status varchar [note: 'Current status of any return or refund request associated with this line item. Supports returns management, refund authorization workflows, and customer service operations.']
  return_window_expiry_date date [note: 'The last date on which a return for this line item can be initiated, calculated from the fulfillment date based on the applicable return policy. Used to enforce return eligibility and automate policy expiry.']
  royalty_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Calculated royalty obligation for this line item (line_subtotal × royalty_rate). Supports licensor payment processing, royalty accrual accounting, and IP rights holder reporting.']
  royalty_rate decimal(7,4) [note: 'Contractual royalty percentage applicable to this line item\'s net sales value, sourced from the associated licensing agreement. Used to calculate royalty obligations payable to licensors (leagues, athletes, IP holders).']
  sales_channel varchar [note: 'The omnichannel sales channel through which this line item was transacted. Supports Direct-To-Consumer (DTC) vs. wholesale vs. venue Point of Sale (POS) revenue attribution and channel performance analytics.']
  sku_code varchar [note: 'Human-readable alphanumeric Stock Keeping Unit (SKU) code identifying the specific product variant (e.g., team, size, color) at the time of purchase. Denormalized for auditability and reporting without requiring a join to the product catalog.']
  source_system_line_ref varchar [note: 'The native line item identifier from the originating operational system (e.g., Shopify Plus line item ID or SAP S/4HANA SD item number). Enables traceability and reconciliation between the lakehouse silver layer and the system of record.']
  tax_exempt boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this line item is exempt from sales tax, applicable for wholesale transactions, reseller purchases, or tax-exempt organizations. Supports tax compliance and audit reporting.']
  unit_cost decimal(18,4) [note: 'Cost of goods sold (COGS) per unit for this SKU at the time of order, sourced from the product catalog or inventory valuation. Used for gross margin analysis and profitability reporting at the line level.']
  unit_price decimal(18,4) [note: 'Listed selling price per single unit of the SKU at the time of order placement, before any line-level discounts or promotions are applied. Expressed in the order currency.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this order line record was last modified in the source system, in ISO 8601 format with timezone offset. Supports change data capture, audit trails, and incremental lakehouse ingestion.']
  variant_description varchar [note: 'Descriptive label for the specific product variant attributes such as size, color, and team affiliation (e.g., \'Red / XL / Chicago Bulls\'). Supports customer-facing order confirmations and returns processing.']
  warehouse_code varchar [note: 'Reference to the fulfillment warehouse or distribution center assigned to fulfill this line item. Used for inventory allocation, pick-pack-ship operations, and logistics cost attribution.']

  Note: 'Individual line item within a merchandise order representing a single SKU quantity purchased. Captures SKU reference, quantity ordered, unit price, line-level discount, line total, fulfillment status, and return eligibility. Supports revenue attribution at the product level and enables SKU-level sales performance tracking across channels.'
}

Table merchandise.licensing_agreement {
  licensing_agreement_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the IP licensing agreement record within the merchandise domain.']
  agent_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to athlete.agent. Business justification: Athlete merchandise licensing agreements are negotiated by the athlete\'s agent, who holds representation authority and commission entitlement. Sports-entertainment legal and finance teams track the ne']
  company_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.company_code. Business justification: Licensing agreements are executed by specific legal entities in multi-entity sports organizations. Company code assignment is required for entity-level royalty reporting, intercompany elimination, and']
  compliance_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.obligation. Business justification: Licensing agreements are created to fulfill specific compliance obligations (league IP protection, anti-counterfeiting mandates). Linking to obligation enables compliance teams to track which obligati']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Licensing agreement costs (royalties, advances, minimum guarantees) are allocated to cost centers for brand/league-level P&L reporting. Sports organizations track licensing costs by business unit via ']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Licensing agreements drive royalty expense accruals posted to specific GL accounts. Finance teams require GL-level traceability from licensing agreement to journal entry for royalty accrual, minimum g']
  governing_body_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.governing_body. Business justification: Licensing agreements are governed by specific sports governing bodies (FIFA, NFL, IOC) that set mandatory licensing rules, royalty rates, and brand standards. Sports merchandise licensing experts expe']
  league_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to league.league. Business justification: League-level IP licensing (e.g., NFL Properties as licensor) requires direct league attribution on licensing agreements. This is a named business process — leagues grant master licenses to manufacture']
  league_season_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to league.season. Business justification: Licensing agreements are frequently scoped to a specific league season (e.g., \'2024-25 season only\'). Season-scoped royalty settlement, minimum guarantee tracking, and compliance audits depend on this']
  license_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to content.content_license. Business justification: Athlete and team IP licensing agreements for physical merchandise are frequently paired with a content license governing digital use of the same IP (e.g., athlete likeness on jersey AND in digital mar']
  policy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.policy. Business justification: Licensing agreements are governed by specific compliance policies (brand standards policy, anti-counterfeiting policy, IP protection policy). Linking to policy enables policy-level governance tracking']
  primary_licensing_athlete_athlete_profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the athlete whose NIL (Name Image and Likeness) rights are licensed under this agreement. Populated only when is_nil_agreement is True. Links to the athlete profile in the athlete domain.']
  primary_licensing_franchise_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the party (league, team, athlete, or rights-holding entity) granting the IP license.']
  product_category_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to merchandise.product_category. Business justification: licensing_agreement has a product_category_scope (STRING) field that describes which product categories are covered by the license. Adding a product_category_id FK normalizes this into a proper refere']
  regulatory_filing_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_filing. Business justification: Sports merchandise licensing agreements require periodic regulatory filings (IP registration, territory filings, royalty reporting to governing bodies). Linking licensing_agreement to regulatory_filin']
  revenue_recognition_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.revenue_recognition_schedule. Business justification: Licensing agreements with advance payments and minimum guarantees require ASC 606 revenue recognition schedules. Sports merchandise licensing advances must be deferred and recognized over the agreemen']
  sponsorship_deal_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sponsorship.deal. Business justification: Merchandise licensing agreements for sponsor co-branded products are operationalized from sponsorship deals where ip_rights_included=true. Linking enables IP rights audit trails, deal compliance repor']
  advance_payment decimal(18,2) [note: 'Upfront payment made by the licensee to the licensor at agreement execution, typically applied against future royalty obligations (recoupable advance).']
  agreement_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique reference number assigned to this licensing agreement, used in correspondence with licensees and in SAP S/4HANA SD contract management.']
  agreement_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the licensing agreement. Draft indicates under negotiation; active indicates fully executed and in force; suspended indicates temporarily halted due to compliance or legal hold; expired indicates past end date without renewal; terminated indicates early cancellation.']
  agreement_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the licensing agreement by the nature of the IP being licensed. NIL (Name Image and Likeness) covers athlete-branded merchandise; team_brand and league_brand cover organizational IP; co_brand covers joint brand partnerships; event_specific covers single-event IP grants.']
  anti_counterfeiting_clause boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the agreement includes specific anti-counterfeiting obligations requiring the licensee to actively monitor and report unauthorized use of licensed IP.']
  audit_requirement varchar [note: 'Specifies the contractually mandated frequency of compliance audits the licensor may conduct on the licensee\'s books and production records to verify royalty accuracy and brand compliance.']
  brand_compliance_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the licensee is required to submit product samples and artwork for brand compliance review and approval before production. Supports IP (Intellectual Property) protection and brand integrity.']
  contract_document_ref varchar [note: 'Reference identifier or URL to the executed contract document stored in the DAM (Digital Asset Management) system or document management repository.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this licensing agreement record was first created in the system, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Supports audit trail and data lineage.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code applicable to all monetary values in this agreement (royalty rate base, minimum guaranteed royalty, advance payment).']
  dispute_resolution varchar [note: 'The contractually agreed mechanism for resolving disputes arising under this licensing agreement.']
  distribution_channel varchar [note: 'The sales and distribution channels through which the licensee is authorized to sell licensed merchandise. DTC (Direct-To-Consumer) includes brand-owned online and physical stores. Venue covers in-stadium and arena retail.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'The date on which the licensing agreement expires and the licensee\'s authorization to produce and sell licensed merchandise ceases. Nullable for open-ended agreements.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'The date on which the licensing agreement becomes legally binding and the licensee is authorized to produce and sell licensed merchandise.']
  exclusivity_scope varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the specific dimensions of exclusivity granted (e.g., exclusive by product category, by channel, by territory, or by distribution tier). Only populated when is_exclusive is True.']
  execution_date date [note: 'The date on which all parties formally signed and executed the licensing agreement.']
  governing_law varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the jurisdiction whose laws govern the interpretation and enforcement of this licensing agreement.']
  insurance_required_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Minimum liability insurance coverage amount the licensee is contractually required to maintain and evidence throughout the agreement term.']
  is_exclusive boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this licensing agreement grants exclusive rights to the licensee within the defined territory and product category scope. True = exclusive; False = non-exclusive.']
  is_nil_agreement boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this licensing agreement covers NIL (Name Image and Likeness) rights for an individual athlete, enabling athlete-branded merchandise production and sale.']
  last_audit_date date [note: 'The date on which the most recent compliance audit of the licensee was completed under this agreement.']
  licensed_ip_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the specific intellectual property rights granted under this agreement, including team names, logos, player NIL (Name Image and Likeness) rights, trademarks, and other brand assets authorized for use.']
  minimum_guaranteed_royalty decimal(18,2) [note: 'The minimum royalty amount the licensee is contractually obligated to pay the licensor over the agreement term, regardless of actual sales volume. Protects licensor revenue floor.']
  next_audit_date date [note: 'The scheduled date for the next compliance audit of the licensee under this agreement, based on the audit_requirement frequency.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for capturing additional contractual terms, special conditions, amendments, or operational notes relevant to this licensing agreement that are not captured in structured fields.']
  renewal_notice_days varchar [note: 'Number of calendar days prior to the effective_end_date by which the licensee must provide written notice of intent to exercise the renewal option.']
  renewal_option boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the licensing agreement contains a contractual option for the licensee to renew the agreement at or before expiration.']
  royalty_rate_pct decimal(7,4) [note: 'The contractually agreed royalty rate expressed as a percentage of net sales that the licensee must remit to the licensor for each unit of licensed merchandise sold.']
  royalty_reporting_frequency varchar [note: 'The contractually required frequency at which the licensee must submit royalty statements and remit royalty payments to the licensor.']
  sell_off_period_days varchar [note: 'Number of calendar days after agreement expiration or termination during which the licensee is permitted to sell existing inventory of licensed merchandise before all sales must cease.']
  sublicensing_permitted boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the licensee is contractually permitted to sublicense the IP rights to third-party manufacturers or distributors.']
  termination_date date [note: 'The actual date on which the agreement was terminated early, if applicable. Null if the agreement expired naturally or is still active.']
  termination_notice_days varchar [note: 'Number of calendar days of advance written notice required by either party to terminate the agreement for convenience, as specified in the contract.']
  termination_reason varchar [note: 'Reason code for early termination of the licensing agreement. Populated only when termination_date is set.']
  territory_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code defining the primary geographic territory in which the licensee is authorized to produce and sell licensed merchandise. Use \'WLD\' for worldwide agreements.']
  territory_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the authorized sales and distribution territory when the territory spans multiple countries or requires specific regional clarification beyond the primary territory code.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this licensing agreement record was most recently modified, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Supports audit trail and change tracking.']

  Note: 'Master record for IP licensing agreements with manufacturers, brand partners, and third-party retailers authorizing production and sale of officially licensed merchandise. Captures licensor, licensee, licensed IP (team name, logo, player NIL rights), product categories covered, royalty rate, minimum guaranteed royalty, territory, agreement start/end dates, exclusivity terms, and compliance audit requirements. Supports NIL (Name Image and Likeness) tracking for athlete-branded merchandise.'
}

Table merchandise.royalty_payment {
  royalty_payment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each royalty payment record in the merchandise licensing domain. Primary key for the royalty_payment data product.']
  agent_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to athlete.agent. Business justification: Agent commission calculation on merchandise royalties is a named business process in sports-entertainment: agents receive a contractual percentage of athlete merchandise royalty payments. Finance team']
  ap_invoice_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.ap_invoice. Business justification: Royalty payments to licensors generate AP invoices in the procure-to-pay cycle. Linking royalty_payment to ap_invoice enables three-way reconciliation (agreement → payment report → AP invoice) require']
  audit_engagement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.audit_engagement.
