Business justification: Royalty payments are subject to formal compliance audits (royalty audits verifying accuracy of reported sales). Linking royalty_payment to audit_engagement enables tracking which payment periods have ']
  company_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.company_code. Business justification: Royalty payments are legal-entity obligations; multi-entity sports organizations must attribute each royalty payment to the correct company code for statutory reporting, intercompany elimination, and ']
  compliance_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.obligation. Business justification: Royalty payments fulfill specific compliance obligations: CBA royalty reporting obligations, licensing authority reporting requirements. royalty_payment.cba_compliance_flag and report_submission_statu']
  contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to athlete.athlete_contract. Business justification: Royalty reconciliation and contract compliance reporting requires linking royalty payments to the athlete_contract governing IP licensing terms and royalty rates. Finance and legal teams audit that ro']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Royalty payments are allocated to cost centers for internal P&L reporting by brand/league/team. Sports finance requires cost-center-level royalty expense tracking for budgeting and variance analysis. ']
  fiscal_period_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.fiscal_period. Business justification: Royalty payments must be reported per fiscal period for financial close, CBA compliance, and licensing audit requirements. Sports licensing agreements mandate period-level royalty reporting; this FK t']
  franchise_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to league.franchise. Business justification: Royalty payments flow to franchise IP holders. Franchise-level royalty reporting, financial settlement, and revenue sharing calculations require linking each payment to the franchise whose IP generate']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Royalty payments must be posted to specific GL accounts for royalty expense recognition, accrual, and SOX-compliant financial close. Sports finance teams require GL-level traceability for every royalt']
  investigation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.investigation. Business justification: Royalty payments are subject to compliance investigations: royalty underpayment audits, dispute investigations, and CBA compliance reviews. royalty_payment.audit_flag and dispute_reason indicate inves']
  league_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to league.league. Business justification: Leagues receive royalty payments as IP owners (e.g., league-level licensed product programs). Direct league_id FK is required for league-level royalty financial reporting, compliance filings, and reve']
  league_season_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to league.season. Business justification: Royalty payments are settled by league season — season-level royalty reporting, minimum guarantee reconciliation, and financial audits all require linking payments to the applicable season. Finance an']
  licensing_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the underlying licensing agreement that governs the royalty obligation, royalty rate, and reporting terms for this payment.']
  nil_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific NIL (Name Image and Likeness) agreement governing athlete IP royalties, when the licensor_type is \'athlete\'. Null for league, team, or player association royalties.']
  product_category_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to merchandise.product_category. Business justification: Royalty payments are calculated and reported by product category (licensing agreements often specify royalty rates per category). Currently tracked as STRING attribute \'product_category\'. Normalizing ']
  regulatory_filing_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_filing. Business justification: Royalty payments in sports merchandise require regulatory filings (royalty reporting to governing bodies, tax authority filings). Linking royalty_payment to regulatory_filing enables compliance teams ']
  tournament_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to event.tournament. Business justification: Royalty payments are calculated and reported per tournament under tournament-scoped licensing agreements (e.g., FIFA World Cup licensing royalties). Direct tournament attribution on royalty_payment en']
  adjustment_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Net value of any contractual adjustments applied to the royalty due, including audit-driven corrections, prior period true-ups, disputed amount resolutions, or licensor-agreed modifications. Positive values increase the obligation; negative values reduce it.']
  advance_offset_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Amount of previously paid royalty advance applied as an offset against the current period royalty due. Reduces the net royalty payable when an advance has been recouped against earned royalties.']
  audit_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this royalty payment record is currently subject to a licensor-initiated audit under contractual audit rights provisions. True = under audit; False = not under audit.']
  brand_compliance_status varchar [note: 'Status of brand compliance verification for the licensed merchandise covered by this royalty period. Confirms that products sold meet licensor brand guidelines and IP usage standards before royalty report submission.']
  cba_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this royalty payment satisfies all applicable CBA (Collective Bargaining Agreement) reporting and payment obligations for the period. True = compliant; False = non-compliant or pending review.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this royalty payment record was first created in the system, used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts in this royalty payment record (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Supports multi-territory licensing operations across global markets.']
  dispute_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text description of the reason for a royalty dispute raised by the licensor or licensee. Populated when payment_status or report_submission_status is \'disputed\'. Supports audit rights documentation and CBA dispute resolution processes.']
  dispute_resolution_date date [note: 'Date on which a royalty dispute was formally resolved between the licensee and licensor. Null if no dispute exists or if dispute is still open. Used to track dispute lifecycle and SLA compliance.']
  due_date date [note: 'Contractual deadline by which the royalty payment must be disbursed to the licensor for the reporting period. Used to monitor payment timeliness and trigger late-payment penalty calculations.']
  gross_sales_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total gross sales revenue from licensed merchandise for the reporting period, before any deductions, returns, or allowances. Serves as the royalty base for rate application. Sourced from Shopify Plus e-commerce and POS transaction data.']
  ip_rights_type varchar [note: 'Type of intellectual property rights being licensed and generating the royalty obligation. Distinguishes trademark (team/league marks), copyright (creative works), NIL (Name Image and Likeness for athletes), likeness rights, logo rights, or patent rights.']
  licensor_acceptance_date date [note: 'Date on which the licensor formally accepted the royalty report and payment amount for the reporting period. Null if not yet accepted or if disputed.']
  licensor_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the IP rights holder receiving the royalty payment. Distinguishes between league-level IP (e.g., NFL, NBA, FIFA), team IP, individual athlete NIL (Name Image and Likeness) rights, player association collective rights, or event-specific IP.']
  minimum_guarantee_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Contractual minimum royalty amount guaranteed to the licensor for the reporting period, regardless of actual sales performance. If royalty_due_amount is less than this value, the minimum guarantee applies.']
  net_royalty_paid_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Final net amount actually disbursed to the licensor after applying minimum guarantees, advance offsets, and adjustments. This is the monetary triplet net total for the royalty payment transaction.']
  net_sales_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Net sales amount after deducting returns and allowances from gross sales. Represents the contractually defined royalty base upon which the royalty rate is applied to calculate royalty due.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional context, licensor correspondence references, special payment instructions, or operational notes relevant to this royalty payment record. Used by licensing operations team for case management.']
  payment_date date [note: 'Principal business event date on which the royalty disbursement was executed and funds were transferred to the licensor. Corresponds to the SAP S/4HANA FI payment posting date.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'Financial instrument or mechanism used to disburse the royalty payment to the licensor. Wire transfer and ACH are standard for international and domestic disbursements respectively; offset applies when netting against amounts owed by the licensor.']
  payment_reference_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for this royalty payment, used in licensor correspondence, audit submissions, and SAP S/4HANA FI payment documents. Format: RPY-YYYY-NNNNNN.']
  payment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the royalty payment record. Draft: under preparation; Calculated: royalty amount computed; Approved: internally authorized; Paid: disbursement completed; Disputed: licensor has raised a challenge; Voided: cancelled.']
  report_submission_date date [note: 'Date on which the royalty report for the period was formally submitted to the licensor. Used to verify compliance with contractual reporting deadlines and to support licensor audit rights.']
  report_submission_status varchar [note: 'Status of the royalty report submitted to the licensor for the reporting period. Tracks whether the report is in draft, has been formally submitted, accepted by the licensor, disputed, or is overdue per contractual deadlines.']
  reporting_period_end_date date [note: 'Last day of the sales reporting period covered by this royalty calculation. Together with reporting_period_start_date defines the window of licensed merchandise sales subject to royalty.']
  reporting_period_start_date date [note: 'First day of the sales reporting period covered by this royalty calculation, typically aligned to a calendar quarter, fiscal period, or CBA-defined reporting cycle.']
  returns_allowances_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total value of merchandise returns and trade allowances deducted from gross sales to arrive at net royalty base. Includes customer returns, damaged goods credits, and promotional allowances per licensing agreement terms.']
  royalty_due_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Calculated royalty amount due to the licensor, derived by applying the royalty rate to net sales. Represents the gross royalty obligation before adjustments, minimum guarantees, or advance offsets.']
  royalty_rate decimal(8,6) [note: 'Contractual royalty rate expressed as a decimal fraction (e.g., 0.120000 = 12%) applied to net sales to calculate the royalty due. Rate is sourced from the governing license agreement and may vary by product category, territory, or channel.']
  sales_channel varchar [note: 'Distribution channel through which the licensed merchandise sales were generated for this royalty period. Venue retail covers in-stadium POS; ecommerce covers Shopify Plus DTC (Direct-To-Consumer) online; third_party covers authorized external retailers; wholesale covers bulk distribution.']
  territory_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code identifying the geographic territory in which the licensed merchandise sales occurred. Supports territory-specific royalty rate application and multi-jurisdiction compliance reporting.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this royalty payment record, supporting audit trail, reconciliation, and change tracking requirements.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of royalty calculations, periodic reporting submissions, and disbursements to IP rights holders (leagues, teams, athletes, player associations) based on licensed merchandise sales. Captures reporting period, gross sales by category/territory/channel, applicable royalty rate, calculated royalty due, adjustments, deductions, net royalty paid, payment date, payment status, report submission date, report status (draft, submitted, accepted, disputed), and licensor acceptance. Supports CBA and NIL compliance reporting, licensor audit rights, financial reconciliation of IP revenue sharing, and licensing agreement reporting obligations.'
}

Table merchandise.fulfillment_shipment {
  fulfillment_shipment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each outbound fulfillment shipment record in the merchandise domain. Primary key for the fulfillment_shipment data product.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Shipping and fulfillment costs are allocated to cost centers for merchandise P&L reporting. Sports merchandise operations track fulfillment expense by cost center (e.g., e-commerce vs. venue) to suppo']
  fixture_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to event.fixture. Business justification: Event-day fulfillment shipments (in-venue pickup, same-day delivery for game-day orders) require fixture attribution for post-event logistics reconciliation, SLA breach reporting, and fan experience a']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Shipping expense must post to a specific GL account for financial reporting. Sports merchandise finance teams require direct GL account assignment on shipments to support freight cost accruals, carrie']
  merch_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent merchandise order that this shipment fulfills. Supports traceability from shipment back to the originating customer order.']
  order_line_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the order line item being fulfilled in this shipment']
  pickup_facility_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to venue.facility. Business justification: Will-call and venue-pickup merchandise fulfillment requires recording the specific facility where fans collect orders on event day. Venue operations staff use this to manage will-call queues and recon']
  primary_fulfillment_fan_fan_profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the fan or end customer who is the recipient of this shipment. Supports fan delivery experience management and CRM integration via Salesforce CRM.']
  regulatory_filing_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_filing. Business justification: International merchandise shipments require customs/export regulatory filings. fulfillment_shipment.customs_declaration_number and declared_customs_value are filing-driven fields. Sports merchandise o']
  retail_location_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the origin warehouse or venue store location from which this shipment was dispatched. Supports omnichannel fulfillment routing and inventory source tracking.']
  actual_delivery_date date [note: 'The confirmed date on which the parcel was delivered to the fan destination as reported by the carrier. Compared against estimated_delivery_date to determine on-time delivery performance.']
  carrier_service_level varchar [note: 'Specific service tier selected for this shipment (e.g., Ground, 2-Day, Overnight, International Priority). Determines transit time commitment and cost. Used for SLA compliance analysis.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this fulfillment shipment record was first created in the system. Serves as the audit creation timestamp for data lineage and Silver layer ingestion tracking.']
  customs_declaration_number varchar [note: 'Official customs declaration or harmonized tariff reference number assigned for international shipments. Required for cross-border compliance and import/export regulatory reporting.']
  declared_customs_value decimal(10,2) [note: 'The declared monetary value of the shipment contents for customs purposes on international orders. Used to determine applicable duties, taxes, and import thresholds.']
  delivered_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time of confirmed delivery at the fan destination as reported by the carrier scan event. Provides granular delivery performance data beyond the date-level actual_delivery_date.']
  estimated_delivery_date date [note: 'The carrier-committed or system-calculated date by which the parcel is expected to arrive at the fan destination. Used for SLA compliance monitoring and fan communication.']
  failure_reason varchar [note: 'Reason code for a failed or undeliverable shipment as reported by the carrier. Used for exception management, fan re-engagement, and carrier performance analysis. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: address_not_found|recipient_unavailable|refused_delivery|damaged_in_transit|lost_in_transit|customs_hold|other — promote to reference product]']
  fulfillment_channel varchar [note: 'The sales and fulfillment channel through which this shipment originated. Distinguishes DTC (Direct-To-Consumer) e-commerce orders from venue store POS transactions, third-party retail, or wholesale distribution.']
  fulfillment_status varchar [note: 'Lifecycle state of this specific order line within this specific shipment. Distinct from order_line.fulfillment_status (overall line status) — a line may be DELIVERED in shipment 1 and RETURNED in shipment 2.']
  fulfillment_type varchar [note: 'Indicates the operational model used to fulfill this shipment: warehouse pick-and-pack, venue store dispatch, drop-ship from supplier, or third-party logistics (3PL) provider.']
  height_cm decimal(8,2) [note: 'Height dimension of the shipment package in centimetres. Used in dimensional weight calculation for carrier billing and packaging optimization.']
  is_international boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this shipment crosses an international border (True). Triggers customs documentation requirements, duties/taxes assessment, and compliance with cross-border trade regulations.']
  is_partial_fulfillment boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this shipment represents a partial fulfillment of the associated order (True) or a complete fulfillment (False). Relevant when orders contain multiple SKUs fulfilled in separate shipments.']
  label_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time when the shipping label was generated in the fulfillment system. Marks the start of the shipment lifecycle and is used for warehouse throughput analysis.']
  length_cm decimal(8,2) [note: 'Length dimension of the shipment package in centimetres. Combined with width and height for dimensional weight (DIM weight) calculation used in carrier billing.']
  line_sequence decimal [note: 'Sequential position of this order line within the shipment packing manifest. Used by warehouse pick/pack operations to order items within a shipment for efficient picking. Belongs to the shipment-line intersection, not to the shipment or order line alone.']
  package_count varchar [note: 'Number of individual packages or parcels included in this shipment. Supports multi-box shipment tracking and carrier billing reconciliation.']
  pick_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time when this specific order line item was physically picked from inventory for inclusion in this shipment. Belongs to the pick event at the intersection of shipment and line — not to the shipment (which has a ship_date) nor to the order line (which has no pick timing).']
  quantity_shipped decimal [note: 'Number of units of this order line packed into this specific shipment. Critical for partial fulfillment scenarios where an order line is split across multiple shipments — belongs to the shipment-line intersection, not to either entity alone.']
  recipient_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the shipment recipient used for carrier tracking notifications and delivery confirmation communications. Classified as PII per GDPR and CCPA.']
  recipient_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the individual designated to receive the shipment as captured at order time. Required for carrier label generation and delivery confirmation. Classified as PII per GDPR and CCPA.']
  recipient_phone varchar [note: 'Phone number of the shipment recipient used for carrier delivery notifications and failed delivery contact. Classified as PII per GDPR and CCPA.']
  requires_signature boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a recipient signature is required upon delivery (True). Typically applied to high-value merchandise, limited-edition memorabilia, or VIP fan orders.']
  return_tracking_number varchar [note: 'Carrier tracking number assigned for the return shipment if the fan initiates a return or the delivery fails. Supports reverse logistics tracking and merchandise returns processing.']
  ship_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which the shipment was physically handed off to the carrier. Used as the baseline for SLA delivery window calculations and carrier performance reporting.']
  shipment_number varchar [note: 'Externally visible business identifier for this shipment, used in customer-facing communications, packing slips, and carrier handoff documentation. Distinct from the internal surrogate key.']
  shipment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the shipment as reported by the carrier or fulfillment system. Drives fan-facing delivery notifications and DTC SLA tracking. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: pending|label_created|in_transit|out_for_delivery|delivered|failed|returned|cancelled — promote to reference product]']
  shipped_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time when the shipment was confirmed as dispatched and handed to the carrier. Used for SLA measurement and fulfillment lead time analytics.']
  shipping_address_line1 varchar [note: 'Primary street address line of the fan delivery destination. Required for carrier label generation. Classified as PII per GDPR and CCPA.']
  shipping_address_line2 varchar [note: 'Secondary address line (apartment, suite, unit number) for the fan delivery destination. Classified as PII per GDPR and CCPA.']
  shipping_city varchar [note: 'City of the fan delivery destination. Used for geographic delivery analytics and carrier zone determination. Classified as PII per GDPR and CCPA.']
  shipping_cost decimal(10,2) [note: 'Actual cost charged by the carrier for transporting this shipment, in the operating currency. Used for fulfillment cost analysis, margin reporting, and carrier contract performance evaluation.']
  shipping_cost_charged_to_fan decimal(10,2) [note: 'The shipping fee amount collected from the fan at checkout. May differ from the actual carrier cost due to flat-rate shipping promotions, free shipping thresholds, or subsidized DTC shipping programs.']
  shipping_cost_currency varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the shipping cost amount (e.g., USD, GBP, EUR). Required for multi-currency international fulfillment operations.']
  shipping_country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 three-letter country code of the fan delivery destination (e.g., USA, GBR, CAN). Used for international shipping compliance, customs documentation, and cross-border analytics.']
  shipping_postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code of the fan delivery destination. Used for carrier zone assignment, delivery time estimation, and geographic analytics. Classified as PII per GDPR and CCPA.']
  shipping_state_province varchar [note: 'State or province of the fan delivery destination. Used for carrier zone pricing, tax jurisdiction determination, and regional fulfillment analytics.']
  shopify_fulfillment_code varchar [note: 'External fulfillment identifier assigned by Shopify Plus at the time the fulfillment record is created. Used to reconcile shipment records with the Shopify Plus e-commerce platform and DTC fulfillment workflows.']
  sla_breach boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this shipment breached the committed delivery SLA (True), meaning actual_delivery_date exceeded estimated_delivery_date. Used for DTC fulfillment SLA KPI reporting and carrier performance management.']
  tracking_number varchar [note: 'Carrier-assigned tracking number provided at the time of shipment handoff. Used by fans and operations teams to monitor parcel transit status via the carrier portal. Supports DTC SLA tracking.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this fulfillment shipment record was last modified. Used for change data capture, incremental loads, and audit trail compliance.']
  weight_kg decimal(8,3) [note: 'Actual weight of the packaged shipment in kilograms as measured at the time of dispatch. Used for carrier billing reconciliation, dimensional weight calculations, and logistics cost modeling.']
  width_cm decimal(8,2) [note: 'Width dimension of the shipment package in centimetres. Used in dimensional weight calculation for carrier billing and packaging optimization.']

  Note: 'Outbound shipment record for merchandise orders fulfilled from warehouse or venue store to end customer. Captures carrier name, tracking number, ship date, estimated delivery date, actual delivery date, shipment status, shipping cost, package dimensions/weight, and origin location. Supports DTC e-commerce fulfillment SLA tracking and fan delivery experience management via Shopify Plus fulfillment workflows.'
}

Table merchandise.return_request {
  return_request_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the merchandise return or exchange request record in the Silver Layer lakehouse.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Return processing costs and refund amounts are allocated to cost centers for merchandise financial reporting. Sports merchandise operations track return costs by cost center to support shrinkage analy']
  fixture_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to event.fixture. Business justification: Returns of event-day merchandise purchases require fixture attribution for post-event revenue reversal reporting, fan experience tracking, and IP compliance audits. return_request has venue_id but no ']
  fulfillment_shipment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to merchandise.fulfillment_shipment. Business justification: return_request represents a customer return of merchandise that was originally shipped via a fulfillment_shipment. Linking return_request to the originating fulfillment_shipment enables carrier perfor']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Return refunds and restocking fees are posted to specific GL accounts (sales returns, contra-revenue, restocking income). Sports merchandise finance requires GL-level traceability for returns to suppo']
  licensing_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the licensing agreement governing the returned merchandise item. Used for royalty reversal calculations and IP compliance validation upon return processing.']
  order_line_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to merchandise.order_line. Business justification: return_request already links to merch_order via original_order_id, but a return is typically for a specific line item (specific SKU, quantity, price). Adding order_line_id allows precise traceability ']
  policy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.policy. Business justification: Return requests are governed by consumer protection compliance policies and IP return policies for licensed merchandise. return_request.ip_compliance_flag, policy_exception, and policy_exception_reaso']
  primary_return_customer_fan_profile_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the fan or customer who submitted the return request. Used for CRM tracking, return history analysis, and loyalty program impact assessment.']
  primary_return_franchise_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the sports team whose licensed merchandise is being returned. Used for royalty reversal tracking, licensing compliance, and team-level return rate analytics.']
  primary_return_original_order_merch_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the original merchandise sales order from which the items being returned were purchased. Links the return to the originating transaction for refund calculation and inventory reconciliation.']
  primary_return_sku_catalog_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific merchandise SKU being returned. Links to the product master catalog for item identification, inventory restock eligibility, and licensing compliance validation.']
  retail_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to merchandise.retail_location. Business justification: return_request captures in-store and online returns. For in-store returns, the specific retail_location (venue store, flagship store) where the return is processed is operationally important for inven']
  tertiary_return_merch_order_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the original Point of Sale transaction from which the returned item was purchased. Populated for POS-channel returns; used for in-store reconciliation and receipt validation.']
  venue_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the venue or retail location where an in-store return is being processed. Populated for POS-channel returns at venue stores or retail outlets.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the return request was formally approved by the authorized agent or system. Distinct from request_timestamp; used for SLA and workflow performance analytics.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the customer service agent or manager who approved the return request. Required for audit trail and policy exception accountability.']
  carrier_name varchar [note: 'Name of the shipping carrier used for mail-in returns (e.g., UPS, FedEx, USPS, DHL). Populated for mail-in return method; used for reverse logistics tracking.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the return request record was first captured in the data platform. Distinct from request_timestamp which reflects the customer-facing event time.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary amounts on this return request (e.g., USD, GBP, EUR). Supports multi-currency operations across global markets.']
  customer_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the customer associated with the return request, captured at the time of submission for communication and notification purposes. Subject to GDPR and CCPA data privacy regulations.']
  ip_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the returned item has been flagged for potential IP, counterfeit, or brand compliance concerns. Triggers escalation to the licensing and IP protection team for investigation.']
  item_condition varchar [note: 'Physical condition of the merchandise item as assessed upon receipt of the return. Determines restock eligibility, supplier chargeback applicability, and IP/brand compliance escalation.']
  net_refund_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Final refund amount payable to the customer after deducting restocking fees and any other adjustments from the gross refund amount. Used for financial settlement and AR reconciliation.']
  policy_exception boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this return request was approved as an exception to the standard merchandise returns policy (e.g., outside return window, non-returnable item category). Requires manager authorization.']
  policy_exception_reason varchar [note: 'Narrative explanation for why a policy exception was granted on this return request. Populated only when policy_exception is True. Required for audit and compliance review.']
  pos_transaction_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier of the original Point of Sale transaction from which the returned item was purchased. Populated for POS-channel returns; used for in-store reconciliation and receipt validation.']
  product_name varchar [note: 'Name of the merchandise product being returned at the time of the original order (e.g., \'Home Jersey – Size L – Team Name\'). Captured as a snapshot to preserve historical accuracy independent of catalog changes.']
  quantity_returned varchar [note: 'Number of units of the specified SKU included in this return request. Used for inventory restock calculations and refund amount determination.']
  received_date date [note: 'Date on which the returned merchandise was physically received at the returns processing center or venue store. Triggers quality inspection and refund/exchange processing workflows.']
  refund_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Gross monetary amount approved for refund to the customer for this return request, before any restocking fees or deductions. Expressed in the transaction currency.']
  refund_method varchar [note: 'Payment instrument or channel through which the approved refund will be issued to the customer. Drives downstream payment processing and PCI-DSS compliance workflows.']
  request_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the customer submitted the return or exchange request. Used for return window eligibility validation against the merchandise returns policy.']
  request_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the return request workflow. Drives omnichannel returns policy enforcement and reverse logistics tracking. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: pending|approved|rejected|in_transit|received|resolved|cancelled — promote to reference product]']
  request_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time (with timezone offset) when the return request was submitted by the customer. Serves as the principal business event timestamp for SLA measurement and omnichannel audit trails.']
  resolution_date date [note: 'Date on which the return request was fully resolved (refund issued, exchange dispatched, or store credit applied). Used for SLA compliance measurement and customer satisfaction reporting.']
  resolution_type varchar [note: 'Customer-requested or business-approved resolution outcome for the return request. Drives downstream refund processing, exchange order creation, or store credit issuance workflows.']
  restock_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the returned merchandise item is eligible to be restocked and resold. Determined by item condition assessment and licensing compliance check.']
  restocking_fee decimal(10,2) [note: 'Fee deducted from the refund amount to cover handling and restocking costs, applied per the merchandise returns policy. May be zero for defective or mis-shipped items.']
  return_channel varchar [note: 'The omnichannel sales channel through which the original purchase was made and/or the return is being processed. Supports channel-specific returns policy enforcement and reverse logistics routing.']
  return_method varchar [note: 'Physical method by which the customer is returning the merchandise item(s). Determines reverse logistics workflow, carrier label generation, and processing location assignment.']
  return_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized reason code selected by the customer or agent explaining why the merchandise is being returned. Used for quality analytics, supplier chargebacks, and brand compliance reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: defective|wrong_item|wrong_size|not_as_described|damaged_in_transit|changed_mind|duplicate_order|counterfeit_suspected — promote to reference product]']
  return_reason_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text narrative provided by the customer or customer service agent elaborating on the reason for return. Supplements the standardized return_reason_code with contextual detail for quality review.']
  return_window_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the return request was submitted within the allowable return window defined by the merchandise returns policy. Drives automated approval or escalation routing.']
  rma_number varchar [note: 'Externally-visible Return Merchandise Authorization number issued to the customer as proof of approved return. Used across omnichannel touchpoints (Shopify Plus, POS, venue stores) to track the return through reverse logistics.']
  shopify_return_code varchar [note: 'Native return identifier assigned by Shopify Plus for e-commerce channel returns. Used for system-of-record reconciliation between the lakehouse Silver Layer and the Shopify Plus operational platform.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Operational system of record from which this return request record originated. Supports data lineage tracking and reconciliation between Shopify Plus e-commerce, SAP S/4HANA SD, and POS systems.']
  tracking_number varchar [note: 'Carrier-issued tracking number for the inbound return shipment. Enables reverse logistics visibility and confirmation of receipt at the returns processing center.']
  unit_price decimal(12,2) [note: 'Original sale price per unit of the merchandise item at the time of purchase. Used as the basis for refund amount calculation and financial reconciliation.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the return request record, capturing status transitions, resolution updates, or refund processing events.']

  Note: 'Customer merchandise return and exchange request record capturing the reason for return, original order reference, items being returned, return authorization number, return method (mail-in, in-store), refund or exchange preference, refund amount, and resolution status. Supports omnichannel returns policy enforcement and reverse logistics tracking for both Shopify Plus and POS channels.'
}

Table merchandise.price_list {
  price_list_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each price list record in the merchandise pricing and promotions catalog.']
  competition_round_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to event.competition_round. Business justification: Playoff and championship round merchandise pricing is a distinct business process — pricing managers configure round-specific price lists (e.g., Conference Finals pricing tier) separate from regular-s']
  fiscal_period_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.fiscal_period. Business justification: Price lists in sports merchandise are governed by fiscal periods for seasonal pricing, revenue recognition compliance, and financial audit trails. Finance requires price list validity to align with fi']
  franchise_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to league.franchise. Business justification: Franchise-specific price lists (team store pricing, franchise-exclusive wholesale pricing) are a standard merchandise operations practice. Linking price lists to franchises enables franchise-level pri']
  league_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the league or competition governing body whose licensed merchandise is covered by this price list (e.g., NFL, NBA, MLS). Supports league-specific pricing compliance.']
  league_season_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to league.season. Business justification: Season-specific pricing strategies (playoff pricing, championship pricing, season opener promotions) require linking price lists to the applicable league season. Merchandise pricing managers and leagu']
  licensing_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the IP licensing agreement that governs royalty obligations and brand compliance constraints for merchandise priced under this list. Ensures pricing does not violate MAP or royalty floor requirements.']
  loyalty_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fan.loyalty_program. Business justification: Loyalty member price lists (is_loyalty_redemption=true, loyalty_points_required populated) must reference the specific loyalty program they belong to. Without this FK, the pricing engine cannot valida']
  primary_price_event_fixture_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific sports or entertainment event (e.g., championship game, concert, playoff) that triggers event-day or championship launch pricing. Null for non-event-specific price lists.']
  primary_price_sku_catalog_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific merchandise SKU to which this price list entry applies. Null when the price list applies to an entire product category or all SKUs within a campaign scope.']
  product_category_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the merchandise product category (e.g., apparel, headwear, memorabilia, accessories) to which this price list applies when not scoped to a specific SKU.']
  promotion_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to merchandise.promotion. Business justification: price_list.promo_code (STRING) is a denormalized reference to a promotion. A price_list entry can be tied to a specific promotional campaign (e.g., a limited-time event-day price list linked to a prom']
  retail_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to merchandise.retail_location. Business justification: price_list already has venue_id → venue (cross-domain) for event-day pricing. For store-specific pricing (e.g., flagship store premium pricing, airport store pricing), a retail_location_id FK enables ']
  target_segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fan.segment. Business justification: Segment-targeted price lists (e.g., season ticket holder pricing, student pricing) require a FK to the fan segment to enforce eligibility at POS and e-commerce checkout. Sports retailers use segment-s']
  tournament_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to event.tournament. Business justification: Tournament-specific merchandise price lists (e.g., World Cup official merchandise catalog, championship tournament pricing) are a standard sports-entertainment business process. Tournament pricing is ']
  venue_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific venue where this price list applies for in-stadium retail or POS transactions. Null for online or multi-venue price lists.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Workflow approval state of this price list before it can be activated. Ensures pricing changes are reviewed and authorized per internal controls and SOX financial governance requirements.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this price list was formally approved for activation. Part of the SOX-compliant audit trail for pricing changes.']
  base_price decimal(18,4) [note: 'The standard unit price (MSRP or list price) for merchandise SKUs under this price list, expressed in the designated currency. This is the principal quantitative pricing fact before any discounts or promotions are applied.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this price list record was first created in the system. Part of the standard audit trail for data lineage and compliance.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which all price points on this list are denominated (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP, CAD). Supports multi-currency global merchandise operations.']
  discount_type varchar [note: 'The mechanism by which the promotional discount is applied: percentage (% off base price), fixed_amount (flat dollar reduction), bogo (Buy One Get One), free_shipping (shipping waiver), bundle (multi-SKU deal price), loyalty_points (fan loyalty reward redemption). Null for non-promotional price lists.']
  discount_value decimal(10,4) [note: 'The magnitude of the discount: a percentage (0–100) for percentage-type discounts, or a fixed monetary amount for fixed_amount-type discounts. Interpreted in conjunction with discount_type.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which this price list ceases to be applicable. Null indicates an open-ended price list with no scheduled expiry. Aligns with SAP SD condition validity end date (DATBI).']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'The calendar date from which this price list becomes applicable to merchandise SKU transactions. Aligns with SAP SD condition validity start date.']
  geographic_scope varchar [note: 'Pipe-delimited list of ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country codes defining the geographic markets where this price list is valid (e.g., \'USA|CAN|GBR\'). Supports global merchandise operations and regional pricing compliance.']
  is_combinable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this price list or promotional discount may be combined with other active promotions in the same transaction. False enforces exclusivity (e.g., championship launch pricing cannot stack with loyalty discounts).']
  is_loyalty_redemption boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this price list entry is redeemable via fan loyalty reward points rather than direct monetary payment. Supports fan loyalty reward redemption tracking.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this price list record was most recently updated. Used for incremental data pipeline processing and audit trail maintenance.']
  list_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the price list: draft (under configuration), active (live and applicable), scheduled (approved but not yet effective), expired (past effective_end_date), suspended (temporarily disabled), archived (retired from use).']
  loyalty_points_required varchar [note: 'Number of fan loyalty reward points required to redeem this price list offer. Applicable only when is_loyalty_redemption is True. Null for non-loyalty price lists.']
  min_order_quantity varchar [note: 'Minimum number of SKU units that must be included in a transaction for this price list to be applicable. Used for wholesale bulk pricing and bundle deal qualification.']
  min_order_value decimal(18,4) [note: 'Minimum transaction value (in the price list currency) required to qualify for this price list or promotional discount. Used for free shipping thresholds and tiered promotional offers.']
  minimum_advertised_price decimal(18,4) [note: 'The lowest price at which merchandise may be advertised per licensing and brand compliance agreements. Enforced across all channels to protect brand integrity and licensor relationships.']
  msrp decimal(18,4) [note: 'The Manufacturer\'s Suggested Retail Price (MSRP) as recommended by the league licensor or brand owner. Used as the reference ceiling price for compliance with licensing agreements and MAP (Minimum Advertised Price) policies.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for business commentary, pricing rationale, or special instructions associated with this price list (e.g., \'Championship edition — limited to 72 hours post-game\', \'VIP tier only — do not publish publicly\').']
  per_customer_usage_limit varchar [note: 'Maximum number of times a single customer may redeem this promotional price list or promo code. Prevents abuse of fan loyalty and event-day promotions.']
  price_list_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric code uniquely identifying this price list across systems (Shopify Plus, SAP SD, POS). Used as the business key for cross-system reconciliation.']
  price_list_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the price list (e.g., \'Championship Launch MSRP 2025\', \'VIP Member Wholesale Spring\', \'Event-Day GA Promo\'). Used in Shopify Plus and POS displays.']
  price_list_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the price list by its commercial purpose: base (standard MSRP), wholesale (B2B/retailer), vip_member (fan loyalty tier), promotional (campaign-driven discount), event_day (championship or game-day launch), clearance (end-of-season markdown). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: base|wholesale|vip_member|promotional|event_day|clearance — promote to reference product if additional types emerge]']
  promo_code_redemption_count varchar [note: 'Running count of the number of times this promotional code has been successfully redeemed. Used for campaign performance tracking and budget burn monitoring.']
  promo_code_usage_limit varchar [note: 'Maximum total number of times this promotional code may be redeemed across all customers. Null indicates unlimited usage. Used to control promotional budget exposure.']
  royalty_rate_pct decimal(7,4) [note: 'The royalty percentage owed to the licensor (league, team, or IP owner) on each unit sold at this price point. Used for royalty accrual calculations and licensor reporting.']
  sales_channel varchar [note: 'The distribution channel to which this price list applies: online (Shopify Plus e-commerce), venue_store (in-stadium retail), pos (Point of Sale terminal), third_party_retail (licensed external retailers), dtc_app (Direct-To-Consumer mobile app), wholesale (B2B bulk buyers). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: promote to reference product if channels expand]']
  sap_condition_record_code varchar [note: 'The condition record number (KNUMH) from SAP S/4HANA SD pricing configuration corresponding to this price list entry. Enables traceability back to the ERP pricing master.']
  shopify_price_list_code varchar [note: 'The native price list identifier assigned by Shopify Plus for this record. Used for cross-system reconciliation between the lakehouse silver layer and the Shopify Plus e-commerce platform.']
  tax_class varchar [note: 'Tax classification applicable to merchandise sold under this price list, determining the VAT/sales tax treatment at point of sale. Aligns with SAP SD tax condition types.']
  wholesale_price decimal(18,4) [note: 'The unit price offered to licensed wholesale partners and third-party retailers for bulk merchandise orders. Null when price list type is not wholesale or B2B.']

  Note: 'Unified pricing and promotions configuration defining all price points and discount offers for merchandise SKUs across channels and customer segments. Captures base pricing (MSRP, wholesale, VIP member tiers), effective date ranges, channel applicability, and currency. Also owns promotional campaigns — percentage discounts, BOGO offers, free shipping, bundle deals, promo codes, usage limits, redemption counts, and event-day or championship launch pricing events. Supports dynamic pricing, fan loyalty reward redemptions, and promotional campaign performance tracking.'
}

  promotion_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the promotional campaign record within the merchandise domain.']
  athlete_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to athlete.athlete_profile.   broadcast_channel_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to broadcast.channel. Business justification: Channel-exclusive merchandise promotions (e.g., \'ESPN subscribers get 20% off\') are a standard sports entertainment fan engagement and broadcast partnership business process. The existing channel_rest']
  broadcast_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to broadcast.broadcast_schedule. Business justification: Broadcast-timed merchandise promotions are a named sports entertainment business process: game-day or nationally-televised-event promotions are scheduled against specific broadcast slots. Merch manage']
  budget_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.budget. Business justification: Promotions have budget allocations tracked in the finance budget system. Linking promotion to budget enables promotional spend tracking, budget consumption monitoring, and variance analysis — standard']
  collection_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to content.collection. Business justification: Merchandise promotional campaigns (e.g., playoff jersey drops, limited edition launches) are anchored to curated content collections for fan engagement. Campaign performance reporting requires linking']
  competition_round_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to event.competition_round. Business justification: Round-based promotional campaigns (e.g., \'Playoff Round 2 Bundle\', \'Championship Round Fan Pack\') are a named business process in sports merchandise. Promotions scoped to a competition round require t']
  compliance_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.obligation. Business justification: Promotions must comply with specific regulatory obligations (FTC disclosure requirements, state sweepstakes laws, league promotional guidelines). Linking promotion to obligation enables tracking which']
  contest_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to gaming.contest. Business justification: Contest-linked merchandise promotions: DFS/gaming contests award merchandise prizes or trigger merchandise discounts (e.g., \'enter contest, win free jersey\'). Marketing operations and prize fulfillmen']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Promotion costs are allocated to cost centers for marketing spend tracking and P&L reporting. cost_center_code is a denormalized plain attribute on promotion; a proper FK enables budget vs. actual pro']

Business justification: Merchandise promotion execution requires associating each promotion with its approved creative asset (banner, video, social image) from the DAM for digital storefront display and campaign activation. ']
  deal_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sponsorship.deal. Business justification: Sponsor-funded merchandise promotions are governed by a specific sponsorship deal that defines the activation budget, brand usage rights, and deliverables. Account managers need this link to reconcile']
  fan_segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fan.segment. Business justification: Merchandise promotions are targeted at specific fan segments (e.g., season-ticket holders, loyalty gold tier). Replacing the denormalized target_audience_segment string with a proper FK to fan.segment']

Business justification: Promotion spend must be tracked and reported per fiscal period for budget variance analysis and financial close. Sports entertainment finance teams require promotion costs allocated to fiscal periods ']
  fixture_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to event.fixture. Business justification: Fixture-specific promotions (e.g., \'buy merchandise at tonight\'s game, get 20% off\') are standard sports retail practice. promotion already links to competition_round and tournament but not individual']
  franchise_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to league.franchise. Business justification: Franchise-specific promotions (team store campaigns, franchise fan loyalty offers) are a core sports merchandise marketing activity. Linking promotions to franchises enables franchise-level campaign p']
