Business justification: Promotion discounts and costs are posted to specific GL accounts (promotional expense, contra-revenue). Sports finance requires GL-level traceability for promotional spend to support financial close a']
  league_season_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to league.season. Business justification: Season-long promotions (e.g., \'buy any jersey all season, get 20% off\') and season-end clearance campaigns require linking promotions to the applicable league season. Marketing and merchandise teams p']
  loyalty_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fan.loyalty_program. Business justification: Merchandise promotions are frequently scoped to a specific loyalty program (e.g., \'double points weekend\' applies only to the Premier Rewards program). The existing requires_loyalty_membership flag ne']
  nil_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to athlete.nil_agreement. Business justification: NIL compliance teams must verify that athlete-featured merchandise promotions are covered by an active, approved NIL agreement before launch. Direct FK from promotion to nil_agreement supports NIL com']
  promotion_associated_league_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the professional league or competition (e.g., NFL, NBA, MLS) whose licensed merchandise is covered by this promotion. Supports league-specific licensing compliance and royalty tracking.']
  promotion_league_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the professional league or competition (e.g., NFL, NBA, MLS) whose licensed merchandise is covered by this promotion. Supports league-specific licensing compliance and royalty tracking.']
  regulatory_filing_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_filing. Business justification: Sports merchandise promotions (sweepstakes, contests, giveaways) require regulatory filings with state/federal authorities. Linking promotion to regulatory_filing enables compliance teams to track whi']
  season_ticket_package_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ticketing.season_ticket_package. Business justification: Season ticket holder merchandise benefit programs (exclusive discounts, early access to licensed gear) are a standard STH retention tool. Linking promotion to season_ticket_package enables STH benefit']
  sponsor_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the sponsoring partner who is co-funding or co-branding this promotional campaign. Null for internally-funded promotions. Supports sponsor ROI reporting and partnership management in Salesforce CRM.']
  tournament_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to event.tournament. Business justification: Tournament-scoped promotional campaigns (e.g., \'Championship Finals Sale\', \'Tournament Qualifier Bundle\') are a named business process. Promotions tied to a tournament — rather than a single fixture —']
  venue_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to venue.venue. Business justification: Venue-exclusive merchandise promotions (stadium anniversary deals, venue-specific fan engagement offers) are a standard sports entertainment commercial practice. Merchandise promotion managers scope p']
  applicable_scope varchar [note: 'Defines the breadth of merchandise to which the promotion applies: all_products (entire catalog), specific_skus (individual SKU-level targeting), specific_categories (e.g., apparel, headwear), specific_collections (e.g., championship collection), or entire_order (applied to the full cart value).']
  approved_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the promotion received final business and brand compliance approval. Distinct from the promotion start_date. Required for SOX audit trails and promotional governance.']
  bogo_buy_quantity varchar [note: 'For Buy One Get One (BOGO) promotion types, the number of items the customer must purchase to qualify for the free or discounted item(s). Null for non-BOGO promotion types.']
  bogo_get_quantity varchar [note: 'For Buy One Get One (BOGO) promotion types, the number of items the customer receives free or at a discounted price after meeting the bogo_buy_quantity threshold. Null for non-BOGO promotion types.']
  brand_compliance_approved boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the promotional creative, messaging, and discount terms have been reviewed and approved by the brand compliance team to ensure alignment with league IP guidelines and sponsor brand standards. True = approved; False = pending or rejected.']
  budget_amount decimal(14,2) [note: 'Total financial budget allocated to fund this promotional campaign, expressed in the currency defined by currency_code. Includes cost of discounts, free shipping subsidies, and any co-funded sponsor contributions. Used for financial planning in Workday Adaptive Planning and EBITDA impact analysis.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this promotion record was first created in the system, in ISO 8601 format with timezone offset. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and SOX financial controls for promotional spend governance.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code applicable to fixed_amount discount values (e.g., USD, GBP, EUR). Required for international merchandise operations and multi-currency e-commerce storefronts.']
  discount_value decimal(10,4) [note: 'The principal quantitative discount amount applied by this promotion. Interpretation depends on promotion_type: for percentage_discount this is the percentage (e.g., 20.0000 = 20%), for fixed_amount this is the currency amount (e.g., 10.0000 = $10 off). Null for promotion types where a fixed value does not apply (e.g., free_shipping, bogo).']
  discount_value_type varchar [note: 'Specifies the unit of measure for the discount_value field: percentage (proportion of item price), fixed_amount (absolute currency deduction), or free (item/shipping provided at no cost). Enables correct calculation logic in Shopify Plus and POS systems.']
  end_date timestamp [note: 'Date and time (inclusive) when the promotion expires and is no longer redeemable. Null indicates an open-ended promotion with no scheduled expiry. Critical for championship merchandise launches and limited-time event-day offers.']
  event_trigger varchar [note: 'Business event that triggers or is associated with this promotion. Captures the sports-entertainment context: none (standard promotion), game_day (active on event day), championship_win (post-championship merchandise launch), playoff_entry, season_opener, concert_event, or anniversary. Enables event-driven promotion activation workflows. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: none|game_day|championship_win|playoff_entry|season_opener|concert_event|anniversary — promote to reference product if additional triggers emerge]']
  free_shipping_threshold decimal(10,2) [note: 'Minimum order subtotal (in promotion currency) required to qualify for free shipping when promotion_type is \'free_shipping\'. Null for non-shipping promotions or promotions with no spend threshold for free shipping.']
  geographic_restriction varchar [note: 'Comma-delimited list of ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country codes where this promotion is valid. Null indicates no geographic restriction (global). Required for international licensing compliance and GDPR/CCPA-compliant targeted promotions in EU and California markets.']
  ip_license_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the merchandise covered by this promotion requires a valid Intellectual Property (IP) licensing agreement (e.g., team logo, league trademark). True = IP license verification required before promotion activation; False = no IP licensing constraint. Supports brand compliance and IP protection obligations.']
  is_auto_apply boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the promotion is automatically applied to qualifying carts without requiring the customer to enter a promo code. True = automatic discount; False = requires promo_code entry. Supports seamless event-day fan experience promotions.']
  is_combinable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this promotion can be stacked or combined with other active promotions on the same order. True = combinable with other discounts; False = exclusive, cannot be combined. Prevents unintended margin erosion on championship merchandise launches.']
  loyalty_points_cost varchar [note: 'Number of fan loyalty points required to redeem this promotion when requires_loyalty_membership is True and promotion_type is \'loyalty_reward\'. Null for non-loyalty promotions. Managed through the fan loyalty program in Adobe Experience Platform and Salesforce CRM.']
  max_discount_cap_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Maximum absolute discount amount (in promotion currency) that can be applied per transaction, regardless of the calculated percentage discount. Prevents excessive margin erosion on high-value merchandise orders. Null indicates no cap.']
  min_purchase_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Minimum cart or order subtotal (in the promotion currency) that must be met before the promotion can be applied. Null indicates no minimum spend threshold. Used to drive higher basket values on event-day and championship merchandise campaigns.']
  min_quantity varchar [note: 'Minimum number of qualifying items that must be in the cart before the promotion activates. Primarily used for bundle and BOGO promotion types. Null indicates no quantity threshold.']
  promo_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric code that fans, customers, or loyalty members enter at checkout to redeem the promotion (e.g., CHAMP2024, BOGO_JERSEY). Serves as the business-facing identifier for the promotion across Shopify Plus, POS, and partner channels.']
  promotion_description varchar [note: 'Free-text marketing description of the promotion as communicated to fans and retail staff. Includes terms and conditions summary, eligible merchandise categories, and any event-specific context (e.g., \'Celebrate the championship with 20% off all official team jerseys this weekend only\').']
  promotion_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable marketing name for the promotional campaign as displayed to fans and retail staff (e.g., \'Championship Day 20% Off\', \'Buy One Get One Jersey\', \'Fan Loyalty Free Shipping\').']
  promotion_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the promotional campaign: draft (being configured), scheduled (approved but not yet live), active (currently redeemable), paused (temporarily suspended), expired (past end date), or cancelled (terminated before expiry).']
  promotion_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the promotional mechanism applied: percentage_discount (e.g., 20% off), fixed_amount (e.g., $10 off), bogo (Buy One Get One), free_shipping, bundle (multi-item deal), or loyalty_reward (fan loyalty point redemption). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: percentage_discount|fixed_amount|bogo|free_shipping|bundle|loyalty_reward — promote to reference product if additional types emerge]']
  redemption_count varchar [note: 'Running total of the number of times this promotion has been successfully redeemed across all channels since activation. Updated in near-real-time from Shopify Plus and POS transaction feeds. Used to monitor usage against usage_limit_total.']
  requires_loyalty_membership boolean [note: 'Indicates whether redemption of this promotion requires the customer to be an active member of the fan loyalty program. True = loyalty members only; False = available to all customers. Supports fan loyalty reward redemption campaigns managed via Adobe Experience Platform.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Operational system of record from which this promotion record originated: shopify_plus (DTC e-commerce), sap_sd (SAP S/4HANA Sales and Distribution), salesforce_crm (CRM-driven campaign), pos_system (venue Point of Sale), or manual (manually entered). Supports data lineage and Silver Layer reconciliation in the Databricks Lakehouse.']
  start_date timestamp [note: 'Date and time (inclusive) when the promotion becomes active and redeemable by customers. Supports event-day promotions that activate at a precise game or concert start time.']
  terms_and_conditions varchar [note: 'Full legal terms and conditions text governing the promotion, including exclusions, geographic restrictions, and expiry terms. Required for consumer protection compliance and GDPR/CCPA transparency obligations when promotions are targeted to EU or California-based fans.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this promotion record, in ISO 8601 format with timezone offset. Tracks changes to discount values, dates, usage limits, or status throughout the promotion lifecycle.']
  usage_limit_per_customer varchar [note: 'Maximum number of times a single customer or fan account may redeem this promotion. Null indicates no per-customer restriction. Prevents abuse of fan loyalty reward promotions.']
  usage_limit_total varchar [note: 'Maximum total number of times this promotion can be redeemed across all customers and channels combined. Null indicates unlimited redemptions. Critical for scarcity-driven championship and limited-edition merchandise promotions.']

  Note: 'Promotional campaign record defining discount offers, bundle deals, and limited-time pricing events for merchandise. Captures promotion name, type (percentage discount, BOGO, free shipping, bundle), applicable SKUs or categories, discount value, promo code, start/end dates, channel restrictions, usage limits, and redemption count. Supports event-day promotions, championship merchandise launches, and fan loyalty reward redemptions.'
}

Table merchandise.supplier {
  supplier_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the merchandise supplier record in the Sports Entertainment merchandise procurement master.']
  company_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.company_code. Business justification: Supplier vendor master records are assigned to company codes in SAP-style finance systems for AP processing, tax reporting, and intercompany transactions. Sports merchandise suppliers must be linked t']
  league_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to league.league. Business justification: Leagues maintain approved manufacturer/supplier programs (e.g., NFL\'s licensed product manufacturer list). Suppliers must be certified per league for brand protection and anti-counterfeiting complianc']
  licensing_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to merchandise.licensing_agreement. Business justification: supplier stores license_number (STRING) and license_expiry_date (DATE) as denormalized fields for licensed producers. licensing_agreement is the authoritative master for IP licensing terms. Adding lic']
  policy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.policy. Business justification: Suppliers must comply with specific compliance policies: ethical sourcing, anti-bribery, data security, and licensed production standards. supplier.ethical_sourcing_certified and iso_certification_cod']
  sponsor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sponsorship.sponsor. Business justification: In sports entertainment, a sponsor frequently is also the merchandise supplier (e.g., Nike as kit sponsor and apparel supplier). Linking supplier to sponsor enables consolidated partner management, co']
  address_line1 varchar [note: 'Primary street address of the supplier\'s registered business location. Used for shipment coordination, legal correspondence, and audit trail.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date on which the supplier was formally approved for procurement use by the authorized procurement officer. Required for SOX audit trail and supplier onboarding compliance.']
  bank_account_ref varchar [note: 'Masked or tokenized reference to the supplier\'s bank account used for electronic payment processing. Full account details are stored in the SAP S/4HANA FI payment configuration. PCI-DSS and SOX controls apply.']
  city varchar [note: 'City of the supplier\'s registered business address. Used for logistics routing and geographic sourcing analysis.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code representing the supplier\'s country of origin or primary place of business. Used for import/export compliance, tariff classification, and ethical sourcing reporting.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the supplier master record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail, data lineage, and compliance with SOX internal controls.']
  defect_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage (0.00–100.00) of units received from this supplier that failed quality inspection. Used to monitor merchandise quality compliance and trigger corrective action plans.']
  ethical_sourcing_cert_name varchar [note: 'Name of the ethical sourcing certification held by the supplier (e.g., Fair Trade, SA8000, WRAP, B Corp). Supports ESG due diligence and brand compliance reporting.']
  ethical_sourcing_certified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the supplier holds a recognized ethical sourcing certification (e.g., Fair Trade, SA8000, WRAP). Required for brand compliance and ESG reporting.']
  incoterms_code varchar [note: 'International Commercial Terms (Incoterms 2020) code defining the division of shipping costs, risk, and responsibilities between the supplier and Sports Entertainment. Critical for merchandise import logistics and customs compliance. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EXW|FCA|CPT|CIP|DAP|DPU|DDP|FAS|FOB|CFR|CIF — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  is_licensed_producer boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the supplier holds an active official merchandise license from the relevant sports league or governing body (e.g., NFL, NBA, FIFA) to produce and distribute branded goods. Mandatory for IP protection and brand compliance.']
  iso_cert_expiry_date date [note: 'Expiry date of the supplier\'s ISO certification. Procurement teams use this to schedule re-certification audits and ensure continuous compliance.']
  iso_certification_code varchar [note: 'ISO standard certification code held by the supplier (e.g., ISO 9001 for quality management, ISO 14001 for environmental management). Used in supplier qualification and audit processes.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the supplier master record. Used for change tracking, data freshness monitoring, and audit compliance.']
  lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Standard number of calendar days from purchase order placement to goods receipt for this supplier. Used for merchandise replenishment planning, event inventory readiness, and Shopify Plus fulfillment SLA management.']
  legal_name varchar [note: 'Full registered legal name of the supplier entity as recorded in official business registration documents. Used for contract execution, licensing agreements, and royalty payments.']
  minimum_order_quantity varchar [note: 'Minimum number of units the supplier requires per purchase order line. Used in procurement planning to ensure order quantities meet supplier thresholds and avoid penalties.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for procurement team annotations regarding the supplier, including special handling instructions, relationship history, or compliance observations not captured in structured fields.']
  on_time_delivery_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage (0.00–100.00) of purchase orders delivered by the supplier on or before the confirmed delivery date. Key performance indicator for supplier reliability and event merchandise readiness.']
  payment_currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which the supplier is paid (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Used for multi-currency procurement and financial settlement.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code of the supplier\'s registered business address. Used for logistics, tax jurisdiction determination, and geographic analysis.']
  preferred_supplier_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this supplier has been designated as a preferred vendor for merchandise procurement, qualifying them for priority purchase order allocation and strategic partnership benefits.']
  primary_contact_email varchar [note: 'Business email address of the primary contact at the supplier. Used for purchase order transmission, quality issue escalation, and licensing communications.']
  primary_contact_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the primary business contact at the supplier organization responsible for procurement and account management communications.']
  primary_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Business phone number of the primary contact at the supplier. Used for urgent procurement communications and quality escalations.']
  quality_rating_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric quality performance score (0.00–100.00) assigned to the supplier based on defect rates, return rates, and compliance with merchandise quality standards. Used in supplier evaluation and sourcing decisions.']
  region varchar [note: 'Geographic region where the supplier primarily operates (e.g., North America, Europe, Asia-Pacific). Used for supply chain risk segmentation and regional sourcing strategy.']
  royalty_rate decimal(5,4) [note: 'Contractual royalty rate (expressed as a decimal fraction, e.g., 0.1200 = 12%) payable by the supplier on net sales of licensed merchandise. Used for royalty accrual, tracking, and IP revenue reporting.']
  sap_vendor_account_number varchar [note: '10-digit vendor account number (LIFNR) assigned in SAP S/4HANA MM/FI for this supplier. Used as the cross-system reconciliation key between the merchandise supplier master and the ERP financial and procurement modules.']
  supplier_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric code assigned to the supplier for use in purchase orders, invoices, and procurement communications. Aligns with SAP S/4HANA vendor account number (LIFNR) in the MM module.']
  supplier_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the supplier in the merchandise procurement master. Active suppliers are eligible for purchase orders; suspended suppliers are under review; blacklisted suppliers are permanently excluded from procurement.']
  supplier_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the supplier\'s operational role in the merchandise supply chain. Manufacturer produces goods; distributor warehouses and ships; print-on-demand fulfills custom orders; licensee holds IP rights to produce; wholesaler sells in bulk; dropshipper ships directly to consumer. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: manufacturer|distributor|print_on_demand|licensee|wholesaler|dropshipper — promote to reference product]']
  tax_number varchar [note: 'Government-issued tax identification number (e.g., EIN in the USA, VAT number in the EU) for the supplier entity. Required for financial reporting, 1099/W-9 compliance, and royalty payment processing.']
  trade_name varchar [note: 'Operating or brand name of the supplier, which may differ from the legal name. Used in day-to-day procurement communications and vendor-facing materials.']
  website_url varchar [note: 'Official website URL of the supplier. Used for brand compliance verification, product catalog access, and due diligence.']

  Note: 'Master record for merchandise suppliers, manufacturers, and contract producers who supply official licensed goods. Captures supplier name, type (manufacturer, distributor, print-on-demand), country of origin, contact details, payment terms, lead time, quality rating, certifications (ethical sourcing, ISO), and active status. Distinct from the partner domain\'s broader vendor registry — this is the merchandise-specific supplier master for procurement and quality management.'
}

Table merchandise.product_category {
  product_category_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for product_category']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Product categories drive automatic GL account determination for revenue and COGS posting in sports merchandise. Finance requires category-level GL mapping to ensure correct revenue stream classificati']
  parent_category_product_category_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent product category for hierarchical categorization. Null for top-level categories.']
  policy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.policy. Business justification: Product categories have specific compliance policies governing IP protection, age restrictions, and licensing requirements. product_category.requires_ip_protection, minimum_age_requirement, and is_lic']
  brand_compliance_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether products in this category must undergo brand compliance review before being offered for sale.']
  category_code varchar [note: 'Short alphanumeric code used as the business identifier for the product category (e.g., APRL, MEMB, ACCS).']
  category_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the product category, including the types of products it encompasses and its purpose within the merchandise catalog.']
  category_level varchar [note: 'Numeric level in the category hierarchy (e.g., 1 for top-level, 2 for sub-category, 3 for sub-sub-category).']
  category_name varchar [note: 'Full descriptive name of the product category (e.g., Apparel, Memorabilia, Accessories).']
  category_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the product category by its business purpose (e.g., merchandise, licensed, branded, generic, promotional, seasonal).']
  created_by_user varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the user who created this product category record.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this product category record was first created in the system.']
  display_order varchar [note: 'Numeric sequence used to control the display order of categories in catalogs, websites, and point-of-sale systems.']
  ecommerce_platform_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the e-commerce platform used for online sales of this category (e.g., SHOPIFY_PLUS).']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when this product category is no longer active. Null for categories with no planned end date.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when this product category becomes active and available for use in the merchandise catalog.']
  icon_url varchar [note: 'URL path to the icon representing this product category in navigation menus and mobile applications.']
  image_url varchar [note: 'URL path to the primary image representing this product category in digital catalogs and e-commerce platforms.']
  inventory_tracking_method varchar [note: 'Method used to track inventory for products in this category (e.g., SKU-level, batch, serial number, or no tracking).']
  is_featured boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this category is featured prominently in marketing campaigns and storefront displays.']
  is_licensable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether products in this category are subject to licensing agreements and royalty tracking.']
  last_modified_by_user varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the user who last modified this product category record.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this product category record was last updated.']
  merchandise_class varchar [note: 'High-level merchandise classification for products in this category (e.g., apparel, headwear, collectibles).']
  meta_description varchar [note: 'Meta description text used for search engine results and social media sharing for this category.']
  minimum_age_requirement varchar [note: 'Minimum age required to purchase products in this category, if applicable (e.g., for age-restricted merchandise).']
  product_category_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the product category indicating whether it is actively used in the catalog.']
  requires_ip_protection boolean [note: 'Indicates whether products in this category require intellectual property protection and brand compliance monitoring.']
  return_policy_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the return policy applicable to products in this category.']
  royalty_rate_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Default royalty rate percentage applied to products in this category for licensing revenue calculations.']
  sales_channel_eligibility varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of sales channels where this category is eligible for sale (e.g., venue_store, online, third_party_retail, pos).']
  season_code varchar [note: 'Code representing the season or event associated with this category (e.g., FALL2024, WRESTLEMANIA40, HOLIDAY2024).']
  seo_keywords varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of keywords used for search engine optimization and product discovery for this category.']
  target_demographic varchar [note: 'Primary demographic segment targeted by products in this category.']
  tax_category_code varchar [note: 'Tax classification code used for sales tax calculation and compliance reporting for products in this category.']
  warranty_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether products in this category are eligible for warranty coverage.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for product_category. Referenced by product_category_id.'
}

Table merchandise.supplier_category_authorization {
  supplier_category_authorization_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for the supplier_category_authorization association']
  product_category_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the product category the supplier is authorized to produce']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the merchandise supplier authorized to produce in this category']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date on which this supplier\'s authorization to produce in this product category expires or was revoked. Null indicates an open-ended active authorization.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date on which this supplier\'s authorization to produce in this product category became active. Used to enforce sourcing windows and audit authorization history.']
  lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Category-specific lead time in calendar days from purchase order placement to goods receipt for this supplier-category combination. May differ from the supplier\'s default lead_time_days when category complexity or tooling requirements vary.']
  minimum_order_quantity varchar [note: 'Minimum number of units required per purchase order for this supplier-category combination. May differ from the supplier\'s global MOQ due to category-specific tooling or setup costs.']
  preferred_supplier_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this supplier is the preferred source for this specific product category. Preferred status is category-scoped — a supplier may be preferred for Jerseys but not for Accessories.']
  product_categories varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of merchandise product categories supplied by this vendor (e.g., apparel, headwear, memorabilia, accessories, footwear). Used for category management and sourcing strategy. [Moved from supplier: This denormalized comma-separated STRING field on supplier is a workaround for the unmodeled M:N relationship. It must be removed and replaced by the supplier_category_authorization association table, which provides referential integrity, queryability, and per-authorization commercial terms.]']
  unit_cost decimal [note: 'Negotiated unit cost for goods produced by this supplier in this product category. Varies per supplier-category combination and cannot reside on either entity alone.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the formal Contract between a merchandise supplier and a product category, capturing the authorization for that supplier to produce goods in that category under defined commercial terms. Each record documents one supplier\'s approval to manufacture or supply a specific product category, including pricing, lead time, order minimums, preferred status, and the validity window of the authorization. This is the operational Approved Supplier List at the category level, distinct from the licensing_agreement (which governs IP rights with the licensor) and from the sku_catalog (which represents resulting products).. Existence Justification: In licensed merchandise operations, a supplier is formally approved to produce specific product categories under defined commercial terms — this is the \'Approved Supplier Category Agreement\' or \'Supplier Category Authorization\'. A single supplier can be authorized to produce multiple product categories (e.g., Apparel AND Accessories), and a single product category can be produced by multiple approved suppliers. This is an actively managed operational record: procurement teams create, update, and expire these authorizations as part of sourcing and brand compliance workflows.'
}

Cleared = fully rights-cleared for distribution; Embargoed = time-restricted; Expired = rights window has lapsed.']

  file_size_bytes varchar [note: 'Size of the primary media file in bytes as stored in Dalet Galaxy DAM. Used for storage capacity planning, CDN bandwidth estimation, and ingest SLA monitoring.']
  frame_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Video frame rate in frames per second (fps) for video assets (e.g., 23.98, 25.00, 29.97, 50.00, 59.94). Critical for broadcast compliance, transcoding workflow selection, and OTT adaptive bitrate streaming configuration.']
  ingest_source varchar [note: 'Origin channel through which the asset was ingested into Dalet Galaxy DAM. Supports provenance tracking, quality control routing, and UGC (User-Generated Content) compliance workflows. OB refers to Outside Broadcast feed.']
  ingest_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the asset was first ingested into the Dalet Galaxy DAM system. Used for SLA tracking, content freshness reporting, and audit trail compliance.']
  is_live_clip boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this asset was captured or clipped from a live broadcast event (e.g., live match highlight, live concert clip). Drives near-real-time CDN distribution workflows and live rights clearance checks.']
  is_ppv_restricted boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this asset is associated with a Pay-Per-View (PPV) event and subject to PPV access restrictions. When true, distribution is gated behind PPV entitlement checks at the OTT and digital platform layer.']
  is_ugc boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this asset was submitted as User-Generated Content (UGC) by a fan or external contributor rather than produced by the organisation\'s internal content team. UGC assets require additional rights clearance and moderation workflows.']
  language_code varchar [note: 'Primary language of the asset content expressed as an IETF BCP 47 language tag (e.g., en, en-US, es, fr, pt-BR). Used for localisation routing, subtitle matching, and regional distribution targeting.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the asset record was most recently updated in the DAM. Supports change detection, incremental ETL processing, and editorial audit trails.']
  lifecycle_status varchar [note: 'Current state of the asset within the content production and distribution lifecycle managed in Dalet Galaxy. Controls editorial workflow progression, rights enforcement, and CDN availability. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|ingest|review|approved|published|archived|deleted|quarantined — promote to reference product if additional states are required]']
  published_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the asset was first published and made available for distribution via CDN to OTT, digital, or social platforms. Null if the asset has not yet been published.']
  resolution varchar [note: 'Video or image resolution classification of the asset (SD=Standard Definition, HD=High Definition 720p, FHD=Full HD 1080p, 4K=Ultra HD, 8K=Super Hi-Vision). Determines CDN delivery tier, OTT streaming profile, and broadcast transmission specification.']
  rights_owner varchar [note: 'Name of the entity that holds the intellectual property (IP) rights for this asset (e.g., league name, broadcaster, production company, athlete NIL rights holder). Determines licensing obligations and revenue share arrangements.']
  rights_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the media rights granted for this asset. Determines which distribution channels are permitted. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: NFL|NBA|MLB|NHL|MLS|UFC|FIFA|ATP|concert|esports — promote to reference product]']
  storage_path varchar [note: 'Internal storage path or object storage URI (e.g., S3 bucket path, NAS mount path) where the master media file is archived in the DAM storage tier. Used for file retrieval, backup verification, and disaster recovery operations.']
  subtype varchar [note: 'Secondary classification providing granular categorisation within the asset type (e.g., highlight_clip, press_photo, podcast_episode, match_report, story_reel, fan_submission). Supports editorial workflow segmentation and metadata-driven distribution rules. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: highlight_clip|full_match|press_photo|podcast_episode|match_report|story_reel|fan_submission|interview|promo_spot|behind_scenes — promote to reference product]']
  tags_keywords varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of editorial metadata tags and keywords associated with the asset (e.g., athlete names, team names, event names, competition rounds). Supports full-text search, AI/ML content recommendation, and Adobe Experience Platform personalisation.']
  thumbnail_url varchar [note: 'URL of the representative thumbnail image for the asset, used in DAM browse interfaces, OTT content catalogues, and digital platform content cards. Auto-generated or manually selected by the editorial team.']
  title varchar [note: 'Human-readable title or display name of the digital content asset as catalogued in Dalet Galaxy. Used for search, discovery, and editorial labelling across OTT, broadcast, and digital platforms.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Sequential version number of the asset, incremented each time the asset is revised, re-edited, or re-ingested. Supports version control, editorial rollback, and audit trail requirements within the Dalet Galaxy DAM versioning framework.']

  Note: 'Core master entity representing every digital content asset managed in Dalet Galaxy DAM — video highlights, photography, audio clips, editorial articles, social media assets, and UGC. SSOT for all content asset identity, format metadata, file specifications, ingest status, lifecycle state, and tag associations across OTT, broadcast, and digital platforms. Serves as the central node connecting rights, production, distribution, and analytics workflows.'
}

May vary by role, production type, or CBA agreement. Belongs to the assignment, not to the employee master record (which holds base salary) or the production order (which does not hold per-person rates).']
  brief_description varchar [note: 'Short editorial brief or production description summarising the creative intent, story angle, or technical specification of the content to be produced. Provides context for assigned editors, producers, and journalists on the scope and purpose of the work order.']
  cdn_delivery_url varchar [note: 'The CDN (Content Delivery Network) endpoint URL where the produced content asset has been published and is accessible for distribution. Populated upon successful delivery. Used by OTT platforms, digital editorial systems, and social media publishing tools to retrieve the asset.']
  content_domain_code varchar [note: 'Reference to the content domain or channel category this production order belongs to (e.g., OTT platform, social media, broadcast, digital editorial). Determines distribution pathway and rights clearance requirements.']
  cost_currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the production cost amount (e.g., USD, GBP, EUR). Required for multi-currency financial reporting and SAP S/4HANA FI integration.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System audit timestamp recording when the production order record was first created in the Dalet Galaxy DAM and ingested into the Silver Layer lakehouse. Distinct from the requested_date business event timestamp. Used for data lineage and audit trail.']
  deadline timestamp [note: 'Hard deadline by which the production order must be completed and delivered. Drives SLA monitoring, priority escalation, and resource scheduling. For broadcast segments, this is typically tied to the broadcast transmission schedule. For social cuts, it is tied to the event window.']
  duration_seconds varchar [note: 'Target runtime duration in seconds for video, audio, or broadcast segment production orders. Null for editorial/text-based content types. Used for scheduling, CDN storage estimation, and broadcast slot planning.']
  editorial_approval_status varchar [note: 'Status of the editorial review and approval workflow for this production order. Tracks whether the content has been submitted for editorial review, approved for publication, rejected, or sent back for revision. Distinct from the overall order status — an order may be in_production while editorial approval is pending.']
  editorial_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes from the commissioning editor or editorial reviewer providing feedback, revision instructions, or approval commentary. Captured during the editorial approval workflow in Dalet Galaxy. Supports iterative content refinement and audit trail for editorial decisions.']
  end_date date [note: 'The date on which this employee\'s assignment to the production order concludes. May differ from the production order deadline if the employee\'s contribution is scoped to a subset of the production timeline.']
  hours_allocated decimal [note: 'The planned number of hours this employee is expected to contribute to this production order in their assigned role. Used for capacity planning, workload balancing, and production cost estimation. This value is specific to the employee-order-role combination and cannot reside on either entity alone.']
  is_sponsored_content boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this production order is for sponsored or branded content produced under a commercial partnership agreement. When true, FCC-mandated sponsored content disclosure labels must be applied to the output asset before distribution.']
  language_code varchar [note: 'ISO 639-1 language code (optionally with ISO 3166-1 region suffix) for the primary language of the produced content (e.g., en, en-US, es-MX, fr-FR, pt-BR). Supports multi-language content production for global markets and OTT localisation workflows.']
  nil_clearance_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether Name, Image, and Likeness (NIL) rights clearance is required for this production order due to featured athlete content. When true, the order cannot be delivered without confirmed NIL clearance from the athlete or their representative.']
  order_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known, human-readable business identifier for the production order as assigned by Dalet Galaxy. Used by editorial and production teams to reference work orders in communications, scheduling boards, and delivery manifests.']
  order_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the production order within the Dalet Galaxy workflow. Tracks progression from initial draft through assignment, active production, editorial review, approval, and final delivery or cancellation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|assigned|in_production|pending_review|approved|delivered|cancelled — promote to reference product]']
  order_type varchar [note: 'Classifies the nature of the production work order. Distinguishes between technical production types (video highlight packages, social cuts, broadcast segments, audio packages) and editorial commissioning types (editorial features, commissioned editorial/articles). Drives workflow routing in Dalet Galaxy. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: video_highlight|editorial_feature|social_cut|broadcast_segment|audio_package|commissioned_editorial — promote to reference product]']
  output_format varchar [note: 'Technical output format specification for the produced content asset. For video: codec, resolution, and container format (e.g., H.264 1080p MP4, ProRes 4K MOV). For audio: bitrate and format (e.g., AAC 320kbps). For editorial: file format (e.g., HTML, DOCX). Drives technical delivery requirements in Dalet Galaxy.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Editorial and operational priority level assigned to the production order. Critical priority is reserved for breaking news, live event highlights, and time-sensitive broadcast segments. Drives queue ordering in Dalet Galaxy production workflows.']
  production_cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'Estimated or actual cost incurred to produce the content order, in the operating currency. Includes editor/producer time, freelancer fees, licensing costs, and technical production expenses. Used for content ROI analysis, budget tracking, and EBITDA reporting. Classified as confidential business financial data.']
  production_stage varchar [note: 'Current stage within the content production workflow pipeline. Tracks granular progress within the broader order status. Briefing covers initial creative direction; pre-production covers asset gathering and scripting; production covers active editing/writing; post-production covers finishing and QC; review covers editorial approval; delivery covers CDN publishing.']
  requested_date timestamp [note: 'The principal business event timestamp recording when the production order was formally raised and submitted in Dalet Galaxy. Represents the real-world moment the content production request was initiated, distinct from system audit timestamps.']
  rights_clearance_status varchar [note: 'Current status of Digital Rights Management (DRM) clearance for the content being produced. Indicates whether all necessary rights (music, athlete likeness, broadcast footage, third-party IP) have been cleared for the intended distribution channel. Blocked orders cannot proceed to delivery without rights resolution.']
  role_on_production varchar [note: 'The specific crew role this employee fulfills on this production order. Determines responsibilities, billing rate applicability, and scheduling requirements. Belongs to the assignment, not to the employee (who may serve different roles on different orders) or the production order (which has multiple roles filled by different people).']
  scheduled_publish_date date [note: 'The planned calendar date on which the produced content asset is scheduled for publication or broadcast. Used for editorial calendar planning and CDN pre-positioning. May differ from the production deadline if content is produced in advance.']
  start_date date [note: 'The date on which this employee\'s assignment to the production order begins. Used for crew scheduling, capacity planning, and determining active assignments. Belongs to the assignment record, not to the employee or the order independently.']
  story_angle varchar [note: 'The editorial perspective, narrative framing, or journalistic angle for the content piece. Applicable primarily to editorial features, commissioned articles, and interview content. Guides the journalist or writer on the intended narrative direction and audience focus.']
  territory_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code indicating the primary geographic territory for which this content is being produced and cleared for distribution. Drives territorial rights enforcement, geo-blocking rules on CDN, and regional broadcast compliance.']
  title varchar [note: 'Human-readable title or working headline of the content piece being produced. For video highlights this is the clip title; for editorial features it is the article headline or working title; for social cuts it is the post caption concept. Used in editorial planning boards and content calendars.']
  ugc_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this production order involves User-Generated Content (UGC) as source material. UGC-sourced orders require additional rights clearance and moderation workflows before publication. Impacts DRM clearance and editorial approval processes.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System audit timestamp recording when the production order record was most recently modified in the source system or Silver Layer. Used for incremental data pipeline processing, change detection, and audit trail compliance.']
  word_count_target varchar [note: 'Target word count for editorial content types (articles, features, interviews, commissioned editorial). Null for non-editorial production types such as video highlights or audio packages. Used for editorial planning and writer briefing.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing content production work orders within Dalet Galaxy — covering video highlight packages, editorial features (story angle, journalist assignment, word count targets), social cuts, broadcast segments, and commissioned editorial content. Tracks assignment, production stage, priority, deadline, assigned editor/producer, output specifications, delivery status, and editorial approval workflow. Supports both technical production (video editing, audio mixing) and editorial commissioning (articles, features, interviews) as order types.'
}

Table content.ingest_job {
  ingest_job_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each content ingest job record in the Digital Asset Management (DAM) system. Primary key for the ingest_job data product.']
  content_asset_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the DAM content asset record created or updated as a result of this ingest job. Establishes traceability from raw ingest event to registered DAM asset identity.']
  data_processing_record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.data_processing_record. Business justification: Content ingest jobs systematically process media that may contain personal data (athlete biometrics, fan footage from venues). GDPR Article 30 requires data processing records for systematic ingestion']
  franchise_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to league.franchise. Business justification: Content ingest jobs for franchise-produced footage (team media feeds, club-owned content) require franchise attribution for rights ownership tracking and distribution clearance. Sports content operati']
  game_result_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to league.game_result. Business justification: Post-game broadcast ingest workflows reference the game result for automated metadata tagging (score, teams, OT status), QC validation, and rights clearance. Ingest operations teams use game_result to']
  live_feed_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to broadcast.live_feed. Business justification: Content ingest jobs sourced from live broadcasts must reference the originating live feed for MAM/DAM integration. Content operations teams track which live feed produced each ingest job for quality c']
  media_rights_deal_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to broadcast.media_rights_deal. Business justification: Content ingest jobs must validate rights clearance against the governing media rights deal before ingesting and distributing content. Rights compliance during ingest is a regulatory requirement in spo']
  playoff_bracket_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to league.playoff_bracket. Business justification: Playoff broadcast ingestion operates under bracket-specific rights clearance rules and distribution restrictions. Ingest jobs for playoff content require bracket context to apply correct DRM schemes, ']
  primary_ingest_event_fixture_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the sports or entertainment event associated with the ingested content (e.g., match, concert, press conference). Links ingest jobs to the event scheduling and production context.']
  production_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to content.production_order. Business justification: Ingest jobs are often initiated as part of a production order workflow. Linking ingest_job to production_order enables tracking which production work order triggered the ingest, supporting production ']
  season_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to league.season. Business justification: Ingest jobs process season-specific broadcast feeds and archive content under season-scoped rights agreements. Season-level ingest tracking is required for content archiving compliance, rights window ']
  tournament_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to event.tournament. Business justification: Broadcast content ingestion workflows are organized at tournament level for bulk ingest operations (e.g., ingesting all match footage for a tournament). Operations teams track ingest jobs per tourname']
  venue_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the venue or facility where the content was captured. Supports geographic and operational reporting on ingest activity by venue.']
  broadcast_rights_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the ingested content carries cleared broadcast distribution rights for linear television and RSN (Regional Sports Network) channels. True = broadcast rights confirmed; False = broadcast rights not cleared or not applicable.']
  cdn_distribution_status varchar [note: 'Status of the CDN (Content Delivery Network) distribution push for this ingested asset following successful DAM registration. Tracks whether the asset has been propagated to CDN edge nodes for OTT and digital platform delivery.']
  checksum_algorithm varchar [note: 'The hashing algorithm used to generate the file integrity checksum for this ingest job. Used to verify that files were transferred without corruption from source to DAM storage.']
  checksum_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'The computed checksum hash value for the primary file or manifest package of this ingest job. Validated against the source-declared checksum to confirm file integrity upon arrival in the DAM.']
  completed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp recording when the ingest job reached a terminal state (completed, failed, or cancelled). Used alongside ingest_timestamp to calculate ingest_duration_seconds and for SLA compliance reporting.']
  content_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which the content was originally captured or created, as declared by the source system or embedded metadata. May differ from ingest_timestamp if content is ingested after the fact (e.g., archival uploads).']
  content_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the media type being ingested. Covers video highlights, photography, audio recordings, editorial content, social media assets, and User-Generated Content (UGC). Drives downstream DAM workflow routing and metadata schema selection.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Audit timestamp recording when this ingest job record was first created in the DAM system. Used for data lineage, audit trails, and Silver layer traceability.']
  destination_storage_path varchar [note: 'The target storage path or URI in the DAM or cloud storage layer where the ingested content is written upon successful completion. Supports traceability from raw capture to registered DAM asset location.']
  drm_clearance_status varchar [note: 'Rights clearance status for the ingested content under the Digital Rights Management (DRM) framework. Determines whether the asset is cleared for distribution across OTT, broadcast, social, and digital channels.']
  duration_seconds decimal(12,3) [note: 'Duration of the ingested video or audio content in seconds. Used for rights clearance calculations, broadcast scheduling, and content catalogue metadata.']
  error_code varchar [note: 'Standardized error code returned by the DAM ingest pipeline when the job encounters a failure condition. Populated only for failed or on-hold jobs. Used for automated alerting, root cause analysis, and SLA reporting.']
  file_count varchar [note: 'Total number of individual files included in this ingest job as declared in the file manifest. Used to validate completeness of ingest against expected file count.']
  file_manifest_path varchar [note: 'Storage path or URI to the file manifest document listing all individual files included in this ingest job. The manifest captures filenames, sizes, checksums, and sequence information for multi-file ingest packages.']
  frame_rate decimal(6,3) [note: 'Frames per second of the ingested video content. Standard values include 23.976, 25, 29.97, 50, 59.94, and 60 fps. Critical for broadcast compliance and transcoding pipeline configuration.']
  ingest_channel varchar [note: 'The operational channel through which this ingest job was initiated. Distinguishes between manual operator-triggered ingests, fully automated pipeline ingests, scheduled batch ingests, and API-driven ingests from external systems.']
  ingest_duration_seconds decimal(12,3) [note: 'Elapsed time in seconds from ingest job initiation to completion or failure. Used for SLA (Service Level Agreement) compliance monitoring, pipeline performance benchmarking, and capacity planning.']
  ingest_priority varchar [note: 'Business priority level assigned to this ingest job, governing queue position and resource allocation in the DAM ingest pipeline. Critical priority is used for live event highlights requiring immediate OTT/social distribution.']
  ingest_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The principal real-world event timestamp recording when the content ingest process was initiated and the raw asset capture began entering the DAM pipeline. Distinct from record audit timestamps.']
  is_ugc boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the ingested content is User-Generated Content (UGC) submitted by fans or external contributors rather than produced by the organization\'s own production teams. UGC assets require additional rights clearance and moderation workflows.']
  job_reference_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for the ingest job, used by production teams and operations staff to reference and track a specific ingest event across systems. Follows the format INJ-{YEAR}-{SEQUENCE}.']
  job_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the ingest job within the DAM workflow. Tracks progression from initial queuing through active processing to terminal states (completed, failed, cancelled). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: queued|in_progress|completed|failed|cancelled|on_hold — promote to reference product]']
  metadata_extraction_status varchar [note: 'Status of the automated metadata extraction process that reads technical and descriptive metadata from the ingested files (e.g., EXIF, XMP, embedded timecode, audio levels). Drives DAM catalogue completeness.']
  metadata_profile varchar [note: 'The metadata schema profile applied during extraction and tagging for this ingest job (e.g., sports_event_video, ugc_social, broadcast_highlight, editorial_photo). Determines which metadata fields are populated in the DAM asset record.']
  moderation_status varchar [note: 'Editorial moderation status for ingested content, particularly applicable to UGC (User-Generated Content) and social media assets. Indicates whether the content has been reviewed and approved for DAM registration and distribution.']
  ott_rights_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the ingested content carries cleared OTT (Over-The-Top Streaming) distribution rights for DTC (Direct-To-Consumer) streaming platforms. True = OTT rights confirmed; False = OTT rights not cleared.']
  primary_format varchar [note: 'The dominant file format or codec of the ingested content (e.g., MXF, MP4, MOV, RAW, JPEG, WAV, MP3). Determines transcoding requirements and DAM storage profile. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: MXF|MP4|MOV|RAW|JPEG|TIFF|WAV|MP3|AIFF|PDF — promote to reference product]']
  qc_failure_reason varchar [note: 'Descriptive reason code or message explaining why the quality check failed. Populated only when qc_status is \'failed\'. Supports remediation workflows and root cause analysis.']
  qc_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric quality score assigned by the automated QC engine during ingest validation. Expressed as a percentage (0.00–100.00). Scores below threshold trigger QC failure and manual review routing.']
  qc_status varchar [note: 'Result of the automated and/or manual quality check performed on the ingested content. Indicates whether the asset meets technical and editorial standards required for DAM registration and downstream distribution.']
  resolution varchar [note: 'Video resolution classification of the ingested content. Applicable to video and photo assets. Drives transcoding profiles, storage tier assignment, and OTT/broadcast distribution suitability.']
  retry_count varchar [note: 'Number of times this ingest job has been retried following a failure or interruption. Tracks pipeline resilience and identifies problematic source feeds or assets requiring manual intervention.']
  rights_territory varchar [note: 'Geographic territory or territories for which rights have been cleared for this ingested content. Expressed as ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country codes or territory group codes (e.g., USA, GBR, WORLDWIDE). Governs CDN distribution routing.']
  source_device_identifier varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the physical device or system that captured or submitted the content (e.g., camera serial number, OB truck unit ID, social feed connector ID). Supports chain-of-custody traceability and device-level quality diagnostics.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Reference to the originating system or device that submitted the content for ingest (e.g., Outside Broadcast truck, camera card reader, social media feed connector, UGC upload portal). Links to the content source system registry.']
  source_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the physical or digital origin of the content being ingested. Distinguishes between Outside Broadcast (OB) truck feeds, camera card transfers, social media feeds, User-Generated Content (UGC) uploads, broadcast feeds, and API push integrations. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: ob_truck|camera_card|social_feed|ugc_upload|broadcast_feed|api_push|satellite_feed|ftp_transfer — promote to reference product]']
  storage_tier varchar [note: 'Storage tier classification assigned to the ingested content based on access frequency and retention policy. Hot tier for active production assets, warm for recent archive, cold for long-term retention, archive for compliance-only storage.']
  total_file_size_mb decimal(18,3) [note: 'Aggregate size in megabytes of all files included in this ingest job as declared in the manifest. Used for storage capacity planning, CDN bandwidth tracking, and ingest throughput reporting.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Audit timestamp recording when this ingest job record was last modified in the DAM system. Supports change tracking and incremental Silver layer processing.']

  Note: 'Transactional record for each content ingest event into the DAM — capturing source system (OB truck, camera card, social feed, UGC upload), ingest timestamp, file manifest, quality check status, metadata extraction results, and destination storage path. Supports traceability from raw capture to DAM registration.'
}
