  distribution_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text operational notes recorded by content operations staff regarding this publish event. May include special handling instructions, rights clearance notes, or platform-specific configuration details.']
  distribution_reference_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for this publish event, sourced from Dalet Galaxy distribution workflow. Used for cross-system reconciliation and audit trail tracking across CDN, OTT, and broadcast systems.']
  distribution_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the content publication event. Tracks the workflow from initial queue through active distribution to final published or failed terminal states. Supports operational monitoring and SLA compliance reporting.']
  drm_license_url varchar [note: 'The URL endpoint of the DRM license server used to validate and serve decryption keys for this content distribution. Required for OTT and DTC platform playback authorization and rights enforcement audit.']
  drm_scheme varchar [note: 'The Digital Rights Management (DRM) packaging technology applied to the content asset during this distribution event. Governs encryption and access control for the published content on the target platform.']
  encoding_profile varchar [note: 'The video/audio encoding specification applied to the content asset for this distribution event (e.g., \'H264_1080p_5Mbps\', \'HEVC_4K_HDR\', \'AAC_128kbps\'). Supports quality assurance, platform compatibility validation, and CDN bandwidth planning.']
  failure_reason varchar [note: 'Descriptive reason code or message explaining why a publish event failed or required retry. Populated only when distribution_status is \'failed\' or \'retrying\'. Supports root cause analysis and SLA reporting.']
  geo_restriction_applied boolean [note: 'Indicates whether geographic access restrictions are enforced for this content distribution event. When true, the content is restricted to specific territories based on broadcast rights agreements and regulatory requirements.']
  geo_restriction_mode varchar [note: 'Defines whether the territory codes in geo_restriction_territory_codes represent an allowlist (content permitted only in listed territories) or a blocklist (content blocked in listed territories). Governs territorial rights enforcement logic.']
  geo_restriction_territory_codes varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country codes defining the territories where this content is permitted or blocked. Supports broadcast rights territorial enforcement and GDPR/CCPA regional compliance. Example: \'USA,CAN,GBR\'.']
  is_live_content boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this publish event distributes live or near-live content (e.g., live match stream, live press conference) as opposed to pre-produced or archived content. Drives latency SLA requirements and CDN live-streaming configuration.']
  is_ppv boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this content distribution event is a Pay-Per-View (PPV) monetized event requiring consumer purchase or entitlement validation prior to access. Drives paywall enforcement and revenue attribution.']
  moderation_status varchar [note: 'The content moderation review status for this publish event, particularly relevant for UGC and social media assets. Ensures only approved content is distributed and supports compliance with platform content policies.']
  publish_initiated_by varchar [note: 'Indicates how the publish event was triggered — manually by a content operator, automatically by a workflow rule, via an API (Application Programming Interface) call from an external system, or by a scheduled distribution job. Supports operational audit and workflow optimization.']
  publish_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The principal real-world event timestamp recording when the content asset was successfully published and made available on the target platform. Distinct from scheduled or requested times. Conforms to yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX format.']
  retry_count varchar [note: 'Number of times this publish event has been retried following a failure. Supports SLA breach analysis, CDN reliability monitoring, and identification of systemic distribution issues.']
  scheduled_publish_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The planned date and time at which the content asset was scheduled for publication on the target platform. Used for embargo management, coordinated release windows, and SLA variance analysis against actual publish timestamp.']
  takedown_reason varchar [note: 'Reason code explaining why a content asset was taken down from the target platform. Supports IP rights enforcement audit, regulatory compliance reporting, and editorial governance review.']
  takedown_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp recording when a published content asset was withdrawn or taken down from the target platform. Populated when distribution_status transitions to \'withdrawn\'. Supports rights expiry enforcement and DMCA/IP compliance audit.']
  ugc_source_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the content asset being distributed originated as User-Generated Content (UGC). UGC assets require additional rights clearance workflows and moderation approval before distribution.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Audit timestamp recording when this publish event record was last modified. Tracks status transitions, retry attempts, and metadata corrections for operational and compliance audit purposes.']
  workflow_job_reference varchar [note: 'The Dalet Galaxy distribution workflow job identifier associated with this publish event. Enables cross-system traceability between the Silver layer record and the source DAM workflow execution for debugging and audit.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of each content publication or distribution event — capturing asset, target platform (OTT, social, CDN, broadcast), publish timestamp, distribution status, CDN endpoint, geo-restriction applied, and DRM packaging applied. Provides the audit trail for all content distribution activity.'
}

Table content.metadata_tag {
  metadata_tag_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each controlled vocabulary metadata tag record in the Digital Asset Management (DAM) system. Primary key for the metadata_tag master record. [_canonical_skip_reason: MASTER_RESOURCE role — this is a controlled vocabulary tag asset managed in Dalet Galaxy DAM with its own identity and lifecycle.]']
  league_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to league.league. Business justification: metadata_tag carries league_code as a plain denormalized attribute. Normalizing to league_id enables league-specific tag governance, rights-sensitive tagging policies, and league-branded taxonomy mana']
  policy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.policy. Business justification: Metadata tags with compliance_flag=true or is_rights_sensitive=true are mandated by specific compliance policies (rights sensitivity policy, NIL tagging policy). Linking tags to their governing policy']
  alias_terms varchar [note: 'Pipe-delimited list of alternative names, synonyms, or common misspellings associated with this tag (e.g., \'Football|Footy|Association Football\' for the Soccer tag). Used to enhance search recall, support natural language queries, and map legacy tag names to current controlled vocabulary terms during DAM migration.']
  auto_tagging_confidence_threshold decimal(5,4) [note: 'Minimum confidence score (0.0000 to 1.0000) required from the auto-tagging AI/ML model before this tag is automatically applied to a content asset without human review. Tags with lower model confidence scores are routed to editorial review queues. Null when auto_tagging_eligible is False.']
  auto_tagging_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this tag may be applied automatically by AI/ML-powered auto-tagging engines integrated with Dalet Galaxy DAM (e.g., computer vision for athlete recognition, speech-to-text for topic detection). When False, the tag requires manual editorial application only.']
  cdn_cache_ttl_seconds varchar [note: 'Recommended cache Time-To-Live (TTL) in seconds for content assets bearing this tag when served via CDN. Tags associated with live or near-live event content (e.g., breaking news, live match highlights) have shorter TTLs to ensure freshness. Tags for evergreen content have longer TTLs to optimize CDN performance.']
  compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this tag is associated with regulatory or compliance-sensitive content categories (e.g., PED/doping-related content, gambling-adjacent content, athlete NIL content, GDPR-sensitive fan data content). When True, additional compliance workflows are triggered in the DAM and distribution systems.']
  content_type_affinity varchar [note: 'Indicates which content asset types this tag is applicable to within the DAM. Restricts tag availability in editorial workflows to relevant asset types (e.g., a \'mood\' tag may apply to \'video\' and \'photo\' but not \'editorial\'). \'all\' indicates the tag is applicable across all content types. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: video|photo|audio|editorial|social|ugc|all — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this metadata tag record was first created in the DAM controlled vocabulary registry. Supports audit trail requirements, taxonomy version history, and data lineage tracking in the Silver Layer lakehouse.']
  dam_category_path varchar [note: 'Full category path within Dalet Galaxy DAM where this tag is classified (e.g., \'/Sports/Soccer/Leagues/MLS\'). Used for DAM navigation, bulk asset operations, and workflow routing within the media asset management and broadcast workflow system.']
  display_priority varchar [note: 'Numeric ordering weight that controls the display sequence of this tag within its taxonomy type in search facets, editorial tag pickers, and fan-facing discovery interfaces. Lower values indicate higher priority display position. Used by DAM editors and OTT platform UX teams to surface the most relevant tags first.']
  effective_from_date date [note: 'Date from which this metadata tag becomes valid and available for application to content assets. Supports seasonal taxonomy management (e.g., a tag for a new league season becomes effective at season start) and planned tag activations aligned with event schedules.']
  effective_until_date date [note: 'Date after which this metadata tag is no longer valid for application to new content assets. Null indicates the tag has no planned expiry. Used for time-bound tags such as event-specific tags (e.g., a Super Bowl LIX tag expires after the event window) and seasonal competition tags.']
  geo_restriction_scope varchar [note: 'Pipe-delimited list of ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country codes where content tagged with this tag has distribution restrictions (e.g., \'USA|CAN\' for North American blackout). Used by CDN and OTT platforms to enforce territorial rights. Null indicates no geographic restriction. Aligns with RSN blackout and international broadcast rights agreements.']
  hierarchy_level varchar [note: 'Numeric depth of the tag within the taxonomy hierarchy tree, starting at 1 for root-level tags (e.g., Sport=1, League=2, Team=3, Athlete=4). Used for hierarchical search faceting, breadcrumb navigation, and roll-up reporting in analytics platforms.']
  hierarchy_path varchar [note: 'Full delimited path from root to this tag in the taxonomy tree (e.g., \'sport/soccer/league/mls/team/la_galaxy\'). Enables efficient subtree queries, breadcrumb rendering, and hierarchical filtering in DAM and OTT discovery interfaces without recursive joins.']
  ip_owner varchar [note: 'Name of the entity that holds Intellectual Property (IP) rights over content associated with this tag (e.g., \'NFL Properties LLC\', \'FIFA\', \'NBA Properties Inc.\'). Used for rights clearance workflows, licensing fee calculations, and IP compliance reporting. Critical for league-branded and athlete-branded content tags.']
  is_personalization_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this tag may be used as a personalization signal in fan preference profiles and recommendation engines within Adobe Experience Platform. When True, the tag contributes to fan interest graphs, content affinity scoring, and targeted content delivery for DTC and OTT platforms.']
  is_rights_sensitive boolean [note: 'Indicates whether content assets tagged with this tag are subject to Digital Rights Management (DRM) restrictions, broadcast rights windows, or territorial licensing constraints. When True, downstream distribution systems must perform rights clearance checks before serving content. Critical for OTT and CDN distribution compliance.']
  is_searchable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this tag is exposed as a searchable facet in DAM search interfaces, OTT content discovery, and digital platform search indexes. When False, the tag is used for internal classification and workflow routing only and is not surfaced to end users or search APIs.']
  is_ugc_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this tag may be applied to User-Generated Content (UGC) assets submitted by fans or third parties. When True, the tag is available in fan-facing content submission workflows and UGC moderation queues. When False, the tag is restricted to professionally produced content only.']
  language_code varchar [note: 'ISO 639-1 language code (optionally with ISO 3166-1 region subtag) indicating the language of the tag_name and tag_description fields (e.g., \'en\', \'es\', \'fr\', \'pt-BR\'). Supports multilingual taxonomy management for global markets and international broadcast distribution.']
  last_modified_by varchar [note: 'Username or system identifier of the user or automated process that last modified this metadata tag record. Supports editorial accountability and taxonomy governance audits within Dalet Galaxy DAM and the Silver Layer data product.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this metadata tag record was most recently updated in the DAM controlled vocabulary registry. Used for incremental ETL processing, change data capture, and taxonomy version management in the Silver Layer lakehouse.']
  nil_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this tag is associated with athlete Name, Image, and Likeness (NIL) rights considerations. When True, content assets bearing this tag must be checked against athlete NIL agreements before commercial use, licensing, or sponsorship activation. Particularly relevant for athlete-specific tags.']
  ott_platform_eligibility varchar [note: 'Pipe-delimited list of OTT and DTC platform identifiers where content tagged with this tag is eligible for distribution (e.g., \'nfl_plus|espn_plus|peacock\'). Null indicates no platform-specific restriction. Used by CDN distribution rules and OTT content ingestion pipelines to enforce platform-level rights.']
  review_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether application of this tag to a content asset requires editorial review and approval before the tag association is published. Used for sensitive tags (e.g., athlete injury, disciplinary action, PED-related content) where incorrect tagging carries reputational or legal risk.']
  rights_restriction_type varchar [note: 'Category of rights restriction applicable to content assets bearing this tag. Territorial restrictions limit distribution by geography; temporal restrictions apply broadcast windows; platform restrictions limit to specific OTT or DTC channels; league blackout enforces RSN blackout rules; PPV exclusive restricts to Pay-Per-View events. Null or \'none\' when is_rights_sensitive is False.']
  social_channel_eligibility varchar [note: 'Pipe-delimited list of social media channel identifiers where content tagged with this tag is approved for distribution (e.g., \'instagram|twitter|tiktok|youtube\'). Null indicates no social channel restriction. Used by social publishing workflows to enforce league and rights holder social media policies.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Operational system of record from which this metadata tag was originated or last synchronized. Dalet Galaxy is the primary DAM source; Adobe Experience Platform may contribute audience-derived tags; Salesforce CRM may contribute sponsor or fan segment tags. Used for data lineage tracking and conflict resolution during ETL.']
  source_tag_reference varchar [note: 'Native identifier of this tag in the originating source system (e.g., Dalet Galaxy internal tag ID, Adobe Experience Platform segment ID). Enables bidirectional synchronization, change detection, and audit tracing between the Silver Layer lakehouse and operational systems.']
  sport_code varchar [note: 'Standardized sport classification code associated with this tag (e.g., \'SOC\' for Soccer, \'BBL\' for Basketball, \'AMF\' for American Football). Aligns with governing body sport codes from FIFA, NFL, NBA, MLB, NHL, MLS. Null for non-sport-specific tags such as topic or mood tags.']
  tag_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known, system-unique alphanumeric code identifying the tag within the DAM and digital platforms (e.g., \'SPORT_SOCCER\', \'LEAGUE_NFL\', \'ATHLETE_LEBRON\'). Used as the stable business key for API lookups, CDN metadata headers, and cross-system integrations. Sourced from Dalet Galaxy tag registry.']
  tag_description varchar [note: 'Editorial description of the tag\'s meaning, scope, and intended usage within the controlled vocabulary. Guides content editors and automated tagging systems on correct application. Includes disambiguation notes for tags with similar names (e.g., distinguishing \'MLS\' as Major League Soccer vs. other uses).']
  tag_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable display label for the metadata tag as shown in the DAM user interface, search facets, and editorial tools (e.g., \'Soccer\', \'National Football League\', \'LeBron James\'). Used for content discovery, faceted search, and personalization surfaces.']
  tag_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the metadata tag within the controlled vocabulary. Active tags are available for application to content assets. Deprecated tags are retained for historical asset associations but blocked from new tagging. Draft tags are under editorial review before activation.']
  tag_type varchar [note: 'Primary classification category of the tag within the controlled vocabulary taxonomy. Determines which facet the tag belongs to for search, discovery, and rights filtering. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: sport|league|team|athlete|event|topic|mood|content_type|venue|sponsor|geography|season|competition — promote to reference product]']
  taxonomy_domain varchar [note: 'High-level domain grouping within the controlled vocabulary that the tag belongs to. Supports multi-domain tagging strategies across sport classification, editorial categorization, rights management, commercial/sponsorship, audience segmentation, and production metadata.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Monotonically incrementing version counter for this metadata tag record, incremented on each update. Supports optimistic concurrency control in DAM workflows, change history tracking, and Silver Layer SCD (Slowly Changing Dimension) management for taxonomy evolution over time.']

  Note: 'Master record for controlled vocabulary metadata tags applied to content assets — including sport, league, team, athlete, event, topic, mood, and content type taxonomies. Supports structured tagging for search, discovery, personalization, and rights filtering across the DAM and digital platforms.'
}

Table content.collection {
  collection_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the content collection record in the Sports Entertainment digital asset management platform (Dalet Galaxy DAM). Primary key for the collection entity.']
  athlete_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to athlete.athlete_profile. Business justification: Athlete-curated content collections: sports-entertainment platforms curate collections around specific athletes (career highlight reels, \'Best of\' packages). The collection must reference the featured']
  brand_sponsor_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the sponsoring brand or commercial partner associated with this collection for branded content packages. Supports sponsorship attribution, revenue reporting, and DRM rights enforcement.']
  category_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to content.content_category. Business justification: collection.sport_category is a free-text STRING that duplicates the controlled vocabulary in content_category. Adding content_category_id FK normalizes this to the authoritative content_category refer']
  collection_event_fixture_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the primary sports or entertainment event (game, match, concert, PPV event) that this collection is associated with. Nullable for evergreen or multi-event collections.']
  collection_fixture_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the primary sports or entertainment event (game, match, concert, PPV event) that this collection is associated with. Nullable for evergreen or multi-event collections.']
  competition_round_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to event.competition_round. Business justification: OTT and broadcast teams curate round-specific content collections (e.g., \'Quarterfinal Highlights Pack\', \'Round 4 Preview\') for distribution. collection has fixture_id and tournament_id but no round-l']

  franchise_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to league.franchise. Business justification: Franchise-branded content collections (team highlight reels, franchise season recaps) are a primary content product for team OTT channels and franchise media hubs. Franchise attribution on collections']
  league_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to league.league. Business justification: Content collections are curated per league (e.g., \'NFL Season Highlights\') for OTT packaging and rights window enforcement. A sports-entertainment domain expert expects collections to be league-scoped']
  league_season_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to league.season. Business justification: Collections are explicitly organized by league season (e.g., \'2024-25 NBA Regular Season Highlights\') for OTT packaging, broadcast rights windows, and archive management. collection.league_season is a']
  playoff_bracket_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to league.playoff_bracket. Business justification: Playoff bracket content collections (bracket challenge hubs, round-by-round highlight collections, playoff content packages) are curated against specific brackets for OTT and fan engagement platforms.']
  primary_metadata_tag_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to content.metadata_tag. Business justification: A collection is classified under a primary taxonomy tag (e.g., \'Premier League Highlights\', \'Tennis\', \'Match Day\'). This FK normalizes the collection\'s primary content classification and gives metadat']
  rights_window_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to broadcast.broadcast_rights_window. Business justification: Content collections distributed via broadcast have rights_window_start/rights_window_end as denormalized plain attributes. Linking to broadcast_rights_window normalizes this and enables rights teams t']
  sponsorship_deal_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sponsorship.deal. Business justification: Branded content collections (e.g., \'Sponsor X Season Highlights\') are created to fulfill specific deal deliverables, not just sponsor relationships. Linking collection to deal enables deal-level conte']
  target_fan_segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fan.segment. Business justification: Segment-targeted content curation: sports entertainment platforms curate content collections for specific fan segments (e.g., \'VIP members exclusive\', \'lapsed fans re-engagement\'). Linking collection ']
  tournament_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to event.tournament. Business justification: Content collections are curated at tournament level (e.g., \'Champions League 2024 Best Goals\'). OTT and DAM teams build tournament collections for fan engagement and subscription platforms. league_sea']
  archived_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time at which this collection was moved to archived status, removing it from active distribution channels. Supports rights window enforcement and content lifecycle management.']
  cdn_manifest_url varchar [note: 'The CDN-hosted manifest URL (e.g., HLS/DASH playlist) for streaming delivery of this collection to OTT and digital platforms. Populated upon publication approval.']
  collection_description varchar [note: 'Long-form editorial description of the collection\'s content, context, and purpose. Displayed on OTT platforms, digital storefronts, and fan engagement interfaces to drive discovery and engagement.']
  collection_type varchar [note: 'Categorical classification of the collection defining its structural and commercial purpose. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: highlight_reel|playlist|photo_gallery|branded_package|ppv_bundle|editorial_series|audio_album|ugc_compilation — promote to reference product]']
  content_rating varchar [note: 'Audience content rating classification applied to the collection per broadcast and streaming standards (e.g., TV-PG, TV-14). Governs age-gating and parental control enforcement on OTT and digital platforms. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: G|PG|PG-13|R|NR|TV-G|TV-PG|TV-14|TV-MA — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time at which this collection record was first created in the DAM system. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and content lifecycle reporting.']
  curation_method varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the collection was assembled by human editorial staff, an algorithmic recommendation engine, a hybrid of both, or automated business rules. Drives content governance and quality review workflows.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the PPV price (e.g., \'USD\', \'EUR\', \'GBP\'). Supports multi-currency pricing for global OTT market distribution.']
  dam_collection_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique business identifier for the collection as assigned by the Dalet Galaxy DAM system. Used for cross-system reference, API lookups, and CDN manifest identification.']
  dam_version_number varchar [note: 'Incremental version counter for this collection record as tracked in the Dalet Galaxy DAM system. Supports optimistic concurrency control, change history auditing, and rollback capabilities.']
  is_featured boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this collection is designated as a featured or promoted collection on OTT homepages, digital storefronts, or fan engagement platform carousels. Drives editorial merchandising and fan experience design.']
  is_ppv boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this collection is designated as a Pay-Per-View (PPV) content bundle requiring individual purchase or entitlement before access is granted on OTT platforms.']
  is_ugc boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this collection contains or is composed of User-Generated Content (UGC) submitted by fans. Triggers additional rights clearance, moderation, and GDPR/CCPA consent validation workflows.']
  language_code varchar [note: 'ISO 639-1/639-2 language code for the primary language of the collection\'s content (e.g., \'en\', \'es\', \'fr-CA\'). Drives localization, subtitle availability, and regional platform routing.']
  moderation_status varchar [note: 'Current moderation review state for collections containing UGC or community-sourced content. Ensures brand safety and regulatory compliance before publication on fan-facing platforms.']
  ppv_price decimal(10,2) [note: 'Retail price in USD for purchasing access to this PPV collection bundle. Applicable only when is_ppv = true. Used for revenue management, fan entitlement validation, and financial reporting.']
  primary_content_format varchar [note: 'Dominant media format of assets within the collection. Determines rendering templates, CDN delivery profiles, and platform compatibility requirements.']
  publish_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the collection within the content production and distribution workflow. Controls visibility on OTT and digital platforms. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|pending_review|approved|published|scheduled|archived|withdrawn|embargoed — promote to reference product]']
  published_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The actual date and time at which this collection was first made publicly available on OTT or digital platforms. Distinct from scheduled_publish_timestamp; records the real-world publication event.']
  rights_territory_codes varchar [note: 'Pipe-delimited list of ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country codes defining the geographic territories where this collection is licensed for distribution (e.g., \'USA|CAN|GBR\'). Drives geo-blocking and CDN routing logic.']
  rsn_distribution_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this collection is cleared for distribution via Regional Sports Networks (RSN). Governs blackout rules and territorial broadcast rights compliance.']
  scheduled_publish_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The planned date and time at which this collection is scheduled to be automatically published to OTT and digital platforms. Supports embargo management and coordinated multi-platform release strategies.']
  seo_keywords varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of SEO keywords and metadata tags associated with this collection for search discoverability on digital platforms, OTT storefronts, and web search engines.']
  short_description varchar [note: 'Concise summary (typically 160 characters or fewer) of the collection used for social media previews, push notifications, and mobile platform display cards.']
  slug varchar [note: 'URL-safe, lowercase hyphenated identifier used for deep-linking and SEO-friendly routing on digital platforms and CDN-delivered pages.']
  social_channel_codes varchar [note: 'Pipe-delimited list of social media channel identifiers where this collection is approved for distribution (e.g., \'YOUTUBE|INSTAGRAM|TIKTOK|TWITTER\'). Supports social content rights and distribution tracking.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record from which this collection record originated (e.g., Dalet Galaxy DAM, Adobe Experience Platform, manual entry). Supports data lineage and Silver Layer provenance tracking.']
  thumbnail_asset_code varchar [note: 'DAM asset code referencing the primary thumbnail or cover image used to represent this collection on OTT platforms, digital storefronts, and social media channels.']
  title varchar [note: 'Human-readable display title of the collection as it appears on OTT platforms, digital channels, and fan-facing interfaces (e.g., \'NBA Finals 2024 Highlight Reel\', \'Champions League Photo Gallery\').']
  total_duration_seconds varchar [note: 'Aggregate playback duration of all video and audio assets within the collection, expressed in seconds. Used for OTT platform display, broadcast scheduling, and PPV event bundle packaging.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time at which this collection record was most recently modified. Supports incremental data pipeline processing, change detection, and audit trail requirements.']

  Note: 'Master entity representing curated groupings of content assets — highlight reels, editorial playlists, photo galleries, branded content packages, and PPV event content bundles. Tracks collection type, member assets with sequence ordering, curation method (editorial vs. algorithmic), publish status, and associated rights window. Supports ordered playlists, highlight packages, and curated galleries for OTT and digital platform delivery.'
}

Table content.license {
  license_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each outbound content license or syndication agreement record in the Sports Entertainment content commercialization master.']
  asset_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific digital content asset (video highlight, photograph, audio clip, editorial) being licensed, as managed in Dalet Galaxy DAM. Null for collection-level or bulk syndication licenses.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Content licensing administration (rights acquisition, legal, clearance) incurs costs allocated to the licensing department cost center. Sports entertainment finance tracks licensing overhead by cost c']
  fixed_asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.fixed_asset. Business justification: Long-term broadcast rights licenses (e.g., multi-year league rights deals) are capitalized as intangible fixed assets in SAP Asset Accounting. Finance teams in sports entertainment routinely capitaliz']
  franchise_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to league.franchise. Business justification: Content licenses can be franchise-specific (team channel rights, franchise content packages, local broadcast deals). Franchise-scoped license management is required for territorial rights compliance, ']
  gaming_operator_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to gaming.operator. Business justification: Gaming operators license sports highlight clips and live content for display on their sportsbook platforms. Rights management teams issue content licenses specifically to gaming operators; this FK tra']
  league_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the league or competition governing body whose content is being licensed (e.g., NFL, NBA, FIFA Champions League). Populated when content_scope is league, event, or season.']
  media_rights_deal_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to broadcast.media_rights_deal. Business justification: Content licenses are granted under the authority of a media rights deal (1:N). Rights compliance and revenue recognition teams must reconcile content licenses against their governing media rights deal']
  platform_channel_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to content.platform_channel. Business justification: Content licenses are often specific to a distribution platform channel. Linking content_license to platform_channel enables channel-specific license enforcement and reporting.']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Content license revenue (syndication fees, royalties, sublicensing) is attributed to profit centers for business unit P&L reporting. Sports entertainment finance tracks licensing revenue streams by pr']
  regulatory_filing_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_filing. Business justification: Content licenses for broadcast distribution in regulated markets (FCC, Ofcom, ARPU) must reference the regulatory filing that authorizes the license terms. Sports broadcast rights compliance requires ']
  revenue_recognition_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.revenue_recognition_schedule. Business justification: Content licenses create performance obligations under ASC 606. Finance creates revenue recognition schedules for multi-period license fees. This link drives the content licensing revenue recognition w']
  season_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to league.season. Business justification: Content licenses are granted for specific seasons (season-long broadcast rights, season-specific OTT streaming licenses). Season-scoped license tracking is required for rights expiry management, renew']
  sponsorship_deal_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sponsorship.deal. Business justification: Content licenses issued as part of sponsorship deal IP rights packages must reference the originating sponsorship deal. Rights teams reconcile content license terms against the sponsorship deal\'s ip_r']
  tournament_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to event.tournament. Business justification: Content licenses in sports entertainment are frequently scoped to specific tournaments (e.g., a broadcaster licenses all content from a specific championship). Rights managers need tournament-scoped l']
  attribution_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the licensee must display a credit or attribution line (e.g., \'Courtesy of Sports Entertainment / League Name\') when publishing or broadcasting the licensed content. True = attribution mandatory.']
  attribution_text varchar [note: 'Exact credit line or attribution text the licensee must display when using the licensed content (e.g., \'Photo: Sports Entertainment / John Smith\'). Populated when attribution_required is True.']
  auto_renewal_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the license agreement automatically renews at the end of the effective period without requiring a new negotiation cycle. True = auto-renews; False = requires explicit renewal action.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current regulatory and contractual compliance state of the license: compliant (all obligations met), under_review (audit in progress), breach_identified (licensee violation detected), remediated (breach resolved), terminated_for_cause (agreement ended due to material breach). Supports GDPR, CCPA, and IP compliance monitoring.']
  content_scope varchar [note: 'Breadth of content covered by the license: single_asset (one specific file), collection (curated package), sport (all content for a sport), league (all content for a league), event (all content for a specific event), season (all content for a competition season). Determines which asset_id or collection_id fields are populated.']
  contract_document_ref varchar [note: 'Reference identifier or URL to the executed license agreement document stored in the Digital Asset Management (DAM) system or document management repository (e.g., Dalet Galaxy document store, SharePoint). Enables direct access to the legal instrument.']
  cpm_rate decimal(10,4) [note: 'Monetary rate charged per one thousand impressions (Cost Per Mille) when fee_structure is cpm_based. Used for digital display and OTT advertising-supported license arrangements. Expressed in currency_code.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the license record was first created in the system, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Supports audit trail, data lineage, and SOX compliance reporting.']
  crm_opportunity_reference varchar [note: 'Salesforce CRM Opportunity record identifier associated with the commercial negotiation that resulted in this license. Enables traceability from CRM pipeline to executed license and supports revenue attribution reporting.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which all monetary amounts (flat_fee_amount, royalty_rate, cpm_rate) are denominated (e.g., USD, GBP, EUR). Required for multi-currency international licensing deals.']
  dalet_rights_reference varchar [note: 'Dalet Galaxy Digital Asset Management (DAM) system rights record identifier linked to this license. Provides traceability to the source-of-truth rights metadata and DRM policy configuration in the DAM workflow.']
  deal_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the commercial arrangement: one_off (single asset transaction), syndication (ongoing bulk content feed to media partner), revenue_share (partner monetizes and shares proceeds), broadcast_feed (live or near-live feed to broadcast partner), social_platform (platform partnership for social channel distribution), sublicense (rights passed to a third party). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: one_off|syndication|revenue_share|broadcast_feed|social_platform|sublicense|ppv_window|dtc_exclusive — promote to reference product]']
  delivery_format varchar [note: 'Technical mechanism by which licensed content is delivered to the licensee: api_feed (Application Programming Interface automated feed), ftp_transfer (file transfer protocol batch), cdn_push (Content Delivery Network push via CDN), manual_download (portal-based manual retrieval), broadcast_signal (OB/satellite signal), email_package (packaged file via email). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: api_feed|ftp_transfer|cdn_push|manual_download|broadcast_signal|email_package|iptv_stream — promote to reference product]']
  drm_restrictions varchar [note: 'Free-text description of specific Digital Rights Management technical restrictions applied to the licensed content (e.g., watermarking required, geo-blocking enforced, no download permitted, time-limited playback window). Sourced from Dalet Galaxy DAM rights metadata.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the license agreement expires and content distribution rights are revoked. Null for perpetual or open-ended agreements. Triggers renewal workflow when approaching.']
  effective_from_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the vendor\'s rights under this license agreement become active and the vendor may begin using the asset. Used for distribution window enforcement and rights scheduling.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the license agreement becomes legally binding and content distribution rights are activated. Aligns with contract execution date or agreed commencement date.']
  effective_until_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the vendor\'s rights under this license agreement expire. After this date the vendor must cease use of the asset. Triggers automated rights-expiry workflows in the DAM and DRM systems.']
  exclusivity_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this license grants the vendor exclusive rights to the asset within the defined territory_scope. When true, no other vendor may hold a concurrent license for the same asset in the same territory. Critical for rights conflict detection.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which the asset\'s distribution rights or licence window expires and the asset must be withdrawn from active distribution channels. Critical for DRM (Digital Rights Management) enforcement and rights clearance compliance. [Moved from asset: expiry_date on the asset master conflates the asset\'s overall distribution window with the expiry of individual vendor licenses. A single asset may have licenses expiring on different dates for different vendors. The per-license expiry belongs on content_license.effective_until_date; the asset-level expiry_date should represent the latest possible distribution window across all licenses, not a specific vendor\'s term.]']
  fee_amount decimal [note: 'Agreed monetary consideration paid by the vendor to Sports Entertainment for the rights granted under this license. Expressed in the currency defined by license_fee_currency. Belongs to the license agreement, not to the asset or vendor master.']
  fee_currency varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which the license_fee_amount is denominated (e.g., USD, GBP, EUR). Required to support multi-currency rights deals with international broadcasters.']
  fee_structure varchar [note: 'Commercial model governing how the licensee compensates Sports Entertainment: flat_fee (fixed one-time or periodic payment), royalty (per-use or per-unit fee), revenue_share (percentage of licensee revenue), cpm_based (Cost Per Mille impression-based), subscription (periodic access fee), free (no monetary consideration, e.g., promotional or editorial). Drives invoicing logic in SAP S/4HANA SD.']
  feed_frequency varchar [note: 'Cadence at which content is delivered to the licensee under ongoing syndication or broadcast feed arrangements: real_time, hourly, daily, weekly, on_demand (licensee-initiated pull), event_triggered (delivery upon specific event occurrence). Null for one-off licenses.']
  flat_fee_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Fixed monetary amount payable by the licensee for the license term when fee_structure is flat_fee or subscription. Expressed in the currency defined by currency_code. Null for royalty or revenue_share structures.']
  last_compliance_review_date date [note: 'Date on which the most recent compliance audit or review of the license was conducted. Used to schedule periodic compliance checks and track regulatory adherence timelines.']
  license_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable descriptive name of the license agreement (e.g., \'ESPN NFL Highlight Syndication 2025\', \'UEFA Champions League Photo License — Getty Images\'). Used in reporting and partner communications.']
  license_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric reference code for the license agreement, used in partner correspondence, invoicing, and legal documentation. Format: LIC-{DOMAIN}-{YEAR}-{SEQUENCE}.']
  license_scope varchar [note: 'Defines the permitted scope of use for this specific IP asset within the license agreement (e.g., broadcast_only, merchandise, digital_streaming). Scope may differ per IP asset within the same license.']
  license_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the license agreement: draft (being negotiated), active (in force), suspended (temporarily halted due to breach or dispute), expired (past end date without renewal), terminated (cancelled before end date), pending_renewal (auto-renewal under review).']
  max_usage_count varchar [note: 'Maximum number of times the licensee is permitted to use, broadcast, or distribute the licensed content within the license term. Null indicates unlimited usage. Enables usage cap enforcement and royalty calculation.']
  payment_frequency varchar [note: 'Cadence at which the licensee is invoiced or remits payment: one_time (single payment), monthly, quarterly, semi_annual, annual. Drives billing schedule generation in SAP S/4HANA SD.']
  renewal_notice_days varchar [note: 'Number of calendar days prior to effective_end_date by which either party must provide written notice of intent not to renew. Drives automated renewal notification workflows.']
  renewal_term_months varchar [note: 'Duration in months of each automatic renewal cycle. Applicable only when auto_renewal_flag is True. Used to project future effective_end_date upon renewal.']
  revenue_share_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of licensee-generated revenue remitted to Sports Entertainment under a revenue_share fee structure (e.g., 30.00 = 30%). Used in ARPU and ARR revenue forecasting and partner settlement calculations.']
  rights_territory varchar [note: 'Geographic territory or territories in which the asset is licensed for distribution, expressed as ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country codes or a named territory group (e.g., USA, GBR, WORLDWIDE, EU_EXCL_UK). Enforced at CDN distribution layer for geo-blocking compliance. [Moved from asset: rights_territory on the asset master represents a default or aggregate territory, but the actual licensed territory is specific to each vendor-asset license agreement. When the same asset is licensed to multiple vendors across different territories, a single rights_territory field on the asset cannot represent all concurrent license scopes. The per-license territory belongs on content_license.territory_scope.]']
  royalty_rate decimal(8,4) [note: 'Per-use or per-unit royalty rate expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.0500 = 5%). Applicable when fee_structure is royalty. Used to calculate royalty invoices based on reported usage volumes.']
  signed_date date [note: 'Date on which all parties executed the license agreement. May differ from effective_start_date when agreements are backdated or have a future commencement.']
  sport_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code identifying the sport associated with the licensed content (e.g., NFL, NBA, MLB, NHL, MLS, FIFA, UFC, ATP). Populated when content_scope is sport, league, event, or season. Enables sport-level rights analytics.']
  sublicense_permitted boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the licensee is contractually permitted to sublicense the content to third parties. True = sublicensing allowed (subject to conditions); False = sublicensing prohibited. Critical for IP chain-of-title integrity.']
  territory_exclusivity varchar [note: 'Defines whether the licensee holds exclusive rights in the specified territory (exclusive), shares rights with other licensees (non_exclusive), or holds first-window broadcast priority before other licensees (first_window). Directly impacts RSN and OTT deal structuring.']
  ugc_rights_included boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the license grants the licensee rights to use or incorporate User-Generated Content (UGC) associated with the licensed assets (e.g., fan footage, social clips). True = UGC rights included; False = excluded.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the license record, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Used for change tracking, incremental data pipeline processing, and audit compliance.']
  usage_rights_granted varchar [note: 'Classification of the media usage rights conveyed to the vendor under this license agreement. Determines which distribution channels and use cases the vendor is permitted to exercise.']
  usage_type varchar [note: 'Permitted exploitation category for the licensed content: broadcast (linear TV/radio), streaming_ott (Over-The-Top Streaming platforms), social_media (social channel posting), editorial (news/journalism), commercial (advertising/promotional), print (physical/digital publication), digital_display (website/app display). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: broadcast|streaming_ott|social_media|editorial|commercial|print|digital_display|ppv|dtc|iptv — promote to reference product]']
  watermark_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the licensee is contractually required to apply a visible or forensic watermark to distributed content for IP tracking and anti-piracy enforcement. True = watermark mandatory; False = not required.']

  Note: 'Master record for all outbound content commercialization agreements — covering individual asset licenses, bulk syndication deals (ongoing content feeds to media partners), third-party media outlet arrangements, social platform partnerships, broadcast partner feeds, and commercial licensees. Captures licensee/partner identity, licensed scope (asset, collection, sport, league), deal type (one-off license, ongoing syndication, revenue-share partnership), territory, usage type, fee structure (flat fee, royalty, revenue share), delivery format and schedule, content feed frequency, start/end dates, auto-renewal terms, and compliance status. SSOT for all outbound content monetization distinct from inbound rights_clearance.'
}

  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current state of the editorial approval workflow for this brief. Tracks progression through editorial review, legal clearance, and final sign-off stages before publication.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the editorial brief and associated content received final editorial approval. Null if not yet approved. Used for SLA (Service Level Agreement) compliance measurement.']

  brief_reference_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric code used to identify and reference the editorial brief across production systems, editorial calendars, and commissioning workflows. Format: EB-{SPORT}-{YEAR}-{SEQUENCE}.']
  brief_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the editorial brief within the content production pipeline, from initial commissioning through publication or cancellation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|commissioned|in_production|under_review|approved|published|cancelled — promote to reference product]']
  brief_title varchar [note: 'Human-readable working title of the editorial brief, describing the story or content piece being commissioned. Used in editorial calendars and production dashboards.']
  cancellation_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text explanation of why the editorial brief was cancelled, if applicable. Null for active or completed briefs. Used for editorial pipeline analysis and resource recovery tracking.']
  commissioned_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the editorial brief was formally commissioned and entered the production pipeline. Represents the principal business event timestamp for this transaction.']
  content_priority varchar [note: 'Editorial priority level assigned to this brief, determining resource allocation, production queue position, and escalation thresholds. Breaking news content receives highest priority.']
  content_sensitivity_level varchar [note: 'Classification of the editorial content\'s sensitivity level, determining access controls, legal review requirements, and distribution restrictions. Sensitive content (e.g., PED allegations, CBA disputes) requires legal sign-off.']
  content_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the content format being produced under this brief. Determines production workflow, asset requirements, and distribution channel eligibility. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: article|video_highlight|photo_gallery|podcast|social_post|long_form|match_report|infographic|live_blog — promote to reference product]']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the editorial brief record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  drm_rights_status varchar [note: 'Status of digital rights clearance for the content covered by this editorial brief. Governs whether the content can be distributed across target platforms. DRM (Digital Rights Management) clearance is mandatory before publication.']
  duration_target_seconds varchar [note: 'Target runtime duration in seconds for audio-visual content (video highlights, podcasts, broadcast segments). Null for text-based content types. Drives production scheduling and CDN (Content Delivery Network) capacity planning.']
  embargo_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time before which the content must not be published or distributed. Used for coordinated release of exclusive stories, pre-match previews, and league-mandated embargo windows.']
  exclusivity_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the editorial content is designated as exclusive to the commissioning platform or outlet. Exclusive content carries higher production priority and stricter embargo controls.']
  language_code varchar [note: 'IETF BCP 47 language tag specifying the primary language in which the content is to be produced (e.g., en-US, es-ES, fr-FR). Drives localisation workflows and multi-market distribution.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the editorial brief record was most recently updated. Used for change tracking, cache invalidation, and audit compliance.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional editorial guidance, special instructions, contextual background, or production notes provided by the commissioning editor to the assigned journalist or producer.']
  publication_deadline timestamp [note: 'Target date and time by which the content must be published or broadcast. May differ from submission deadline to allow for editorial review, legal clearance, and CDN distribution lead time.']
  published_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the content produced under this brief was formally published or broadcast. Null if not yet published. Used for SLA (Service Level Agreement) compliance and editorial performance reporting.']
  revision_count varchar [note: 'Number of revision cycles the editorial brief has undergone since initial commissioning. Tracks editorial iteration and is used to identify briefs requiring excessive rework for process improvement.']
  rights_territory varchar [note: 'Geographic territory or market for which content distribution rights have been cleared. Expressed as ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country codes or regional groupings. Drives geo-restricted CDN (Content Delivery Network) distribution rules.']
  seo_keywords varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of target keywords and search terms to be incorporated into the content for digital discoverability. Supports DTC (Direct-To-Consumer) audience acquisition strategy.']
  social_channel_target varchar [note: 'Specific social media platform(s) targeted for distribution of content produced under this brief (e.g., Instagram, X/Twitter, TikTok, YouTube). Drives format and duration specifications for social-first content.']
  sport_discipline varchar [note: 'The sport or athletic discipline that is the primary subject of the editorial brief (e.g., football, basketball, tennis, mixed martial arts). Aligns with league and competition governance taxonomy.']
  story_angle varchar [note: 'Narrative perspective, editorial angle, or thematic focus of the content piece. Describes the unique hook or framing that differentiates this story from standard match or event coverage.']
  submission_deadline timestamp [note: 'Date and time by which the assigned journalist or producer must submit the completed content for editorial review. Critical for time-sensitive sports news and live event coverage.']
  ugc_sourced_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this editorial brief incorporates or is based on UGC (User Generated Content) sourced from fan platforms or social channels. Triggers additional rights clearance and GDPR compliance workflows.']
  word_count_target varchar [note: 'Target word count for written editorial content (articles, long-form, match reports). Null for non-text content types such as video or photo galleries. Used for editorial planning and writer briefing.']

  Note: 'Master record for editorial content briefs — capturing story angle, assigned journalist or producer, target platform, sport/league/event context, word count or duration target, deadline, approval workflow status, and associated production order. Governs the editorial content production pipeline from commissioning to publication.'
}

The channel has content_rating_system and drm_requi']
  category_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to content.content_category. Business justification: platform_channel.content_category is a free-text STRING column that should reference the authoritative content_category master. Adding content_category_id FK enforces referential integrity and ensures']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Platform channels (OTT, broadcast, social) have ongoing operating costs (CDN, streaming infrastructure, staffing) allocated to departmental cost centers. Sports entertainment finance tracks channel op']
  dalet_channel_id varchar [note: 'The native channel identifier assigned to this distribution channel within the Dalet Galaxy Media Asset Management (MAM) and broadcast workflow system.   league_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to league.league. Business justification: Platform channels are licensed to distribute specific league content (league-branded OTT apps, RSN league packages, league pass channels). League-channel distribution agreements are a core business re']

Business justification: A platform channel (OTT, IPTV) operates under a governing media rights deal that defines distributable content, territory scope, and exclusivity terms. Platform channel setup, rights compliance, and s']
  operator_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to gaming.operator. Business justification: Sportsbook-branded streaming channels (e.g., FanDuel TV, ESPN Bet integrations) are operated by or affiliated with a licensed gaming operator. Content distribution teams must track which gaming operat']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Platform channels are distinct revenue-generating business units tracked as profit centers (e.g., OTT platform P&L, broadcast channel P&L). Sports entertainment finance requires channel-level profitab']
  regulatory_filing_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_filing. Business justification: Platform channels operating as broadcast or OTT services in regulated markets hold regulatory licenses (FCC broadcast license, Ofcom license). Compliance teams must link each channel to its operating ']
  ad_insertion_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether dynamic ad insertion (DAI) or server-side ad insertion (SSAI) is enabled for content delivered on this platform channel. Classified as confidential as it represents proprietary infrastructure routing information. Used by Dalet Galaxy automated publish workflows to push packaged content to the correct CDN origin.']

  channel_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable display name of the distribution platform channel (e.g., \'ESPN+ OTT United States\', \'Bally Sports Midwest RSN\', \'UFC PPV Global Portal\'). Used in reporting, content routing dashboards, and rights management interfaces.']
  channel_status varchar [note: 'Current operational lifecycle status of the platform channel. Active channels are eligible to receive publish events and content routing. Suspended channels are temporarily blocked from receiving new content. Decommissioned channels are retired from distribution operations.']
  channel_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the distribution channel by delivery technology and business model. Drives rights window routing and DRM policy selection. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: OTT|RSN|PPV|IPTV|DTC|social_media|linear_broadcast — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  channel_url varchar [note: 'The public-facing URL of the platform channel accessible to end consumers (e.g., the OTT app store URL, the social media channel page URL, the DTC streaming portal URL). Used for fan engagement linking, marketing campaigns, and digital platform analytics in Adobe Experience Platform.']
  closed_captioning_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether closed captioning (CC) is mandatory for all content published to this platform channel.   concurrent_stream_limit varchar [note: 'Maximum number of simultaneous content streams permitted on this platform channel at any given time, as defined in the operator SLA or broadcast rights agreement. Used for capacity planning, CDN bandwidth provisioning, and SLA compliance monitoring.']
  content_rating_system varchar [note: 'The content rating classification system applicable to this platform channel\'s territory and regulatory environment (e.g., MPAA for US theatrical, TV Parental Guidelines for US broadcast, BBFC for UK, FSK for Germany). Determines which content rating metadata must be included in publish packages and enforced at the channel level.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this platform channel record was first created in the content domain master data registry. Follows ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Used for audit trail, data lineage, and SOX compliance reporting.']
  decommission_date date [note: 'The date on which this platform channel was or is scheduled to be decommissioned and removed from active content distribution. Nullable for active channels. Used to enforce rights window expiry and cease automated publish routing to retired endpoints.']
  drm_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether Digital Rights Management (DRM) encryption is mandatory for all content published to this platform channel. True = DRM packaging required before delivery; False = unencrypted delivery permitted (e.g., free-to-air social channels). Used as a gate in the Dalet Galaxy publish workflow.']
  drm_system varchar [note: 'The Digital Rights Management (DRM) technology required by this platform channel for content protection and access control. Widevine is used for Android/Chrome; FairPlay for Apple devices; PlayReady for Microsoft/Smart TVs; multi_drm indicates the channel supports multiple DRM systems simultaneously. Drives DRM packaging decisions in Dalet Galaxy and CDN delivery configurations.']
  geo_restriction_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether geographic (geo-blocking) restrictions are enforced for content delivered to this platform channel. True = CDN geo-restriction rules are applied based on territory_code; False = global delivery without geo-blocking. Critical for broadcast rights compliance and territorial exclusivity enforcement.']
  hdr_supported boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this platform channel supports High Dynamic Range (HDR) video delivery. True = HDR content (HDR10, Dolby Vision, HLG) can be delivered to this channel; False = SDR only. Drives HDR transcoding and packaging decisions in the content production workflow.']
  language_code varchar [note: 'ISO 639-1 or ISO 639-2 language code representing the primary language of content delivered on this platform channel (e.g., \'en\', \'es\', \'fr\', \'pt-BR\'). Used for content localization routing, subtitle/dubbing requirements, and regional audience targeting in Adobe Experience Platform.']
  launch_date date [note: 'The date on which this platform channel became operational and began accepting content distribution. Used to calculate channel tenure, validate rights window effective dates, and support historical analytics on channel performance.']
  live_streaming_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this platform channel is configured and authorized to receive and distribute live streaming content (e.g., live game broadcasts, live PPV events). True = live streaming supported; False = VOD/on-demand only. Critical for live event publish routing decisions.']
  max_resolution varchar [note: 'Maximum video resolution supported by this platform channel for content delivery. SD = Standard Definition (480p); HD = High Definition (720p); FHD = Full High Definition (1080p); UHD_4K = Ultra High Definition 4K (2160p); UHD_8K = Ultra High Definition 8K (4320p). Used to determine transcoding profiles in Dalet Galaxy and CDN packaging.']
  metadata_standard varchar [note: 'The metadata schema standard required by this platform channel for content package delivery (e.g., EIDR for entertainment identifier registry, EBUCore for broadcast, Dublin Core for general digital assets, IPTC for news/sports photography). Drives metadata mapping and transformation in Dalet Galaxy export workflows.']
  ppv_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this platform channel is configured to support Pay-Per-View (PPV) transactional content purchases. True = PPV events can be routed to this channel with associated purchase gating; False = subscription or free-to-air only. Drives PPV event publish routing and revenue management integration.']
  publish_lead_time_minutes varchar [note: 'Minimum number of minutes required before a scheduled broadcast or rights window start time that content must be submitted to this platform channel for successful ingestion and processing. Used by Dalet Galaxy automated scheduling to trigger publish events with sufficient lead time.']
  rights_window_default_days varchar [note: 'Default number of days that content rights are valid for distribution on this platform channel, used as a baseline when no specific rights agreement overrides are present. Supports automated rights window calculation in the Dalet Galaxy DRM and rights clearance workflow.']
  sla_uptime_target_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'The contractually agreed Service Level Agreement (SLA) uptime target percentage for this platform channel, expressed as a percentage (e.g., 99.90 = 99.9% uptime). Used for CDN performance monitoring, operator SLA compliance reporting, and penalty clause evaluation.']
  social_handle varchar [note: 'The official social media handle or username for this platform channel (e.g., @NBASports, @PremierLeague). Applicable for social_media channel types. Used for content routing, fan engagement tracking, and UGC attribution in Adobe Experience Platform and social analytics.']
  subscription_model varchar [note: 'Business model governing how consumers access content on this platform channel. free = no cost access; subscription = recurring subscription fee (ARR/ARPU driven); ppv = Pay-Per-View (PPV) transactional; freemium = free tier with premium upgrade; ad_supported = free with advertising (CPM-based revenue). Informs rights window configuration and revenue attribution.']
  supported_content_formats varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of content asset formats accepted by this platform channel (e.g., \'MP4,HLS,DASH,MXF,HEVC\'). Defines the technical delivery specifications required when routing publish events from Dalet Galaxy to this channel endpoint. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: MP4|HLS|DASH|MXF|HEVC|H264|H265|ProRes|AAC|MP3|JPEG|PNG|WebM — promote to reference product]']
  ugc_accepted boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this platform channel accepts and distributes User-Generated Content (UGC) from fans and community contributors. True = UGC assets can be routed to this channel after rights clearance; False = professionally produced content only. Relevant for social media channels and fan engagement platforms managed via Adobe Experience Platform.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this platform channel record was most recently modified. Follows ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Used for change data capture (CDC), data lineage tracking, and audit compliance in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  vod_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this platform channel supports Video On Demand (VOD) content delivery. True = VOD content (highlights, replays, documentaries) can be published to this channel; False = live-only channel. Used in content routing logic to determine eligible publish targets.']
  watermarking_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether forensic or visible watermarking is required for content delivered to this platform channel to enable piracy detection and source identification. True = watermarking must be applied during packaging in Dalet Galaxy; False = no watermarking required. Supports IP protection and anti-piracy compliance.']

  Note: 'Reference master for all distribution platform channels — OTT apps, RSN broadcast slots, social media channels, DTC streaming, IPTV, and PPV portals. Captures platform name, channel type, operator, territory, supported content formats, and DRM requirements. Used to route publish events and rights windows to the correct distribution endpoint.'
}

Table content.category {
  category_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for content_category']
  parent_category_id varchar [note: 'Reference to parent content category for hierarchical taxonomy structure. Null for top-level categories. Enables multi-level content classification trees.']
  parent_content_category_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on content_category (parent_content_category_id)']
  policy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.policy. Business justification: Content categories (e.g., Gambling-Adjacent, Adult, Athlete Personal) are governed by specific compliance policies determining age rating requirements, geo-restrictions, and approval workflows. The co']
  age_rating_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether content in this category must be assigned an age rating classification (e.g., G, PG, PG-13, R) for audience suitability and parental guidance.']
  allowed_file_formats varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of permitted file format extensions for content assets in this category (e.g., mp4, mov, jpg, png, mp3). Enforced during content ingestion workflows.']
  analytics_tracking_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether detailed user engagement analytics and viewing metrics are collected for content in this category for performance analysis and content optimization.']
  archive_priority varchar [note: 'Priority classification for archival processing when content reaches end of active lifecycle. Immediate priority content is archived within 24 hours; deferred may wait up to 90 days.']
  category_code varchar [note: 'Short alphanumeric code representing the content category for system integration and API usage. Used as business identifier across digital asset management systems.']
  category_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the content category explaining its purpose, scope, and usage guidelines for content producers and editors.']
  category_level varchar [note: 'Numeric level in the category hierarchy tree where 1 represents top-level categories and higher numbers represent deeper nesting. Used for taxonomy navigation and filtering.']
  category_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the content category displayed in user interfaces and content management systems.']
  category_type varchar [note: 'Primary media type classification for this category indicating the dominant content format (video highlights, photography, audio clips, documents, social media assets, or user-generated content).']
  cdn_distribution_tier varchar [note: 'Distribution priority tier for content delivery network (CDN) caching and edge delivery strategy. Premium tier receives highest bandwidth allocation and global edge presence.']
  content_category_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the content category indicating whether it is available for content tagging and classification workflows.']
  created_by_user varchar [note: 'Username or user identifier of the person who created this content category record. Used for accountability and audit purposes.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this content category record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  default_retention_days varchar [note: 'Standard number of days that content assets in this category are retained in active storage before archival or deletion per content lifecycle management policy.']
  display_order varchar [note: 'Numeric value controlling the presentation sequence of categories in user interfaces, content management systems, and API responses. Lower values appear first.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when this content category was retired or deprecated and is no longer available for new content tagging. Null for currently active categories.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when this content category became active and available for content classification and tagging workflows. Used for temporal validity tracking.']
  geo_restriction_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether content in this category is subject to geographic distribution restrictions based on licensing agreements and territorial rights.']
  is_rights_sensitive boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether content in this category requires special digital rights management (DRM) clearance and licensing review before distribution.']
  is_searchable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this category is exposed in public-facing search interfaces and content discovery features on digital platforms and mobile applications.']
  is_user_selectable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether end users can select this category when uploading user-generated content (UGC) or filtering content in fan engagement platforms.']
  max_file_size_mb decimal(10,2) [note: 'Maximum allowed file size in megabytes for content assets in this category. Used for upload validation and storage capacity planning.']
  metadata_schema_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the metadata schema template applied to content assets in this category within Dalet Galaxy DAM system. Format: major.minor.patch (e.g., 2.1.0).']
  modified_by_user varchar [note: 'Username or user identifier of the person who last modified this content category record. Used for change accountability and audit trail.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this content category record was last modified. Updated whenever any attribute value changes. Used for change tracking and synchronization.']
  monetization_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether content in this category is eligible for monetization through advertising, sponsorships, pay-per-view, or subscription revenue models.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-form text field for additional notes, usage guidelines, or special instructions related to this content category for content producers and editors.']
  requires_approval boolean [note: 'Indicates whether content tagged with this category must undergo editorial or compliance approval workflow before publication to digital platforms or social channels.']
  seo_priority varchar [note: 'Priority level for search engine optimization efforts and metadata enrichment for content in this category to maximize organic discovery and traffic.']
  social_sharing_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether content in this category can be shared by users on social media platforms through embedded sharing widgets and social integration features.']
  target_platforms varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of intended distribution platforms for this content category (e.g., OTT, mobile app, social media, broadcast, website). Guides content production and format specifications.']
  thumbnail_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether automatic thumbnail image generation is required for content assets in this category for preview and navigation purposes in content management interfaces.']
  watermark_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether digital watermarking must be applied to content assets in this category for brand protection and unauthorized distribution prevention.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for content_category. Referenced by content_category_id.'
}

Table content.collection_item {
  collection_item_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for the collection_item association']
  asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the digital content asset that is a member of the collection.']
  collection_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the parent content collection that contains this asset membership record.']
  added_by_user varchar [note: 'Identifier or username of the editorial staff member or system process that added this asset to the collection. Captures the operational provenance of the membership record.']
  added_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this asset was added to the collection by an editorial user or automated curation process. Belongs to the membership event, not to the asset or collection independently.']
  is_featured_in_collection boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this asset is designated as a featured or promoted item within this specific collection. A single asset may be featured in one collection but not another, making this a relationship-level attribute.']
  position_in_collection varchar [note: 'Ordinal position of the asset within the collection sequence. Drives playback order for playlists and display order for galleries. Belongs to the membership relationship, not to the asset or collection independently.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the membership relationship between a content collection and a digital asset in the Dalet Galaxy DAM. It captures the operational fact that a specific asset has been curated into a specific collection, including its position in the sequence, when it was added, who added it, and whether it is featured within that collection. Each record links one collection to one asset with attributes that exist only in the context of this membership relationship — not on the collection or asset alone.. Existence Justification: In DAM (Digital Asset Management) operations for sports entertainment, a content collection is explicitly defined as a curated grouping of multiple assets (highlight reels, playlists, galleries), and any single asset (a video clip, photo, article) can appear in multiple collections simultaneously — e.g., a LeBron James dunk clip may appear in a \'Top 10 Plays\' collection, a \'Lakers Season Highlights\' collection, and a \'PPV Event Bundle\'. This is a foundational operational relationship in Dalet Galaxy DAM, where collection membership is actively managed by editorial staff with ordering, featured flags, and timestamps.
