  trade_transaction_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for trade_transaction']
  contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to athlete.athlete_contract. Business justification: When an athlete is traded, their contract obligations transfer to the acquiring franchise. Linking trade_transaction to the specific athlete_contract being transferred is essential for cap accounting ']
  investigation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.investigation. Business justification: Trade transactions can trigger integrity investigations (tampering, salary cap circumvention, undisclosed side agreements). Direct FK from trade_transaction to investigation enables transaction-level ']
  journal_entry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.journal_entry. Business justification: Trade transactions involving cash consideration (cash_consideration_amount field) require journal entries for financial posting of cash transfers between franchises. SOX audit trail and financial reco']
  league_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the league (e.g., NFL, NBA, MLB, NHL, MLS) under whose governance this transaction is processed and approved.']
  primary_trade_acquiring_franchise_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the franchise receiving the primary asset (player, draft pick, or cash consideration) in this transaction. In waiver claims, this is the claiming franchise.']
  primary_trade_athlete_athlete_profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the primary athlete (player) being transferred in this transaction. For multi-player trades, this represents the headline player; additional players are captured in associated transaction line records. Null for cash-only or draft-pick-only transactions.']
  season_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to league.season. Business justification: trade_transaction currently stores season_year as an INT denormalization. Adding season_id as a proper FK to league.season normalizes the season reference and enables joins to the full season record (']
  tertiary_trade_third_franchise_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the third franchise involved in a three-way trade transaction. Null for bilateral trades, waiver claims, and all other non-three-way transaction types.']
  transaction_window_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to league.transaction_window. Business justification: Trade transactions must occur within official league transaction windows (trade deadline, free agency window). trade_transaction has transaction_window_compliant (BOOLEAN) indicating compliance but no']
  approval_date date [note: 'The date on which the league office officially approved and ratified this transaction. Null if the transaction is still pending, rejected, or voided.']
  cash_consideration_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The gross monetary value of cash included in this transaction (e.g., player-for-cash trade, cash balancing payment). Subject to league-mandated cash consideration limits per CBA. Zero if no cash is exchanged.']
  cash_consideration_currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the cash consideration amount (e.g., USD, CAD, EUR). Required when cash_consideration_amount is non-zero.']
  cba_compliance_status varchar [note: 'The CBA compliance determination for this transaction, covering salary cap rules, roster limits, player movement restrictions, and cash consideration limits. under_review = league compliance office is actively reviewing; waived = league granted a formal CBA waiver.']
  clearing_date date [note: 'For waiver transactions, the date on which the player clears the waiver wire without being claimed, making them eligible for outright assignment or free agency. Null for non-waiver transaction types.']
  competitive_integrity_review_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this transaction was flagged for competitive integrity review by the league office (e.g., suspected tanking, collusion between franchises, or circumvention of salary cap rules). True = flagged for review.']
  condition_resolution_date date [note: 'The date by which all conditional terms of this transaction must be resolved or triggered. Null for unconditional transactions. Used to monitor outstanding conditional trade obligations.']
  conditional_terms_description varchar [note: 'Free-text description of any conditions attached to this transaction (e.g., \'Draft pick converts to 1st round if team makes playoffs\', \'Player option triggers salary escalation\'). Null for unconditional transactions.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time (with timezone offset) when this transaction record was first created in the league data platform. Serves as the system audit creation timestamp for data lineage and SOX compliance.']
  draft_pick_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the draft pick asset being transferred as part of this transaction. Null if no draft pick is included. For multi-pick trades, this captures the primary pick; additional picks are in associated line records.']
  effective_date date [note: 'The date on which the transaction takes operational effect — i.e., when the player or asset officially moves to the acquiring franchise\'s roster and salary cap. May differ from transaction_date or approval_date (e.g., post-trade deadline holds).']
  no_trade_clause_waived boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the player involved in this transaction had a contractual no-trade clause that was formally waived by the player to allow the transaction to proceed. True = player waived their no-trade clause.']
  no_trade_clause_waiver_date date [note: 'The date on which the player formally waived their no-trade clause to permit this transaction. Null if no no-trade clause existed or was not applicable.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for league operations staff to record supplementary context, special circumstances, or administrative notes related to this transaction that are not captured in structured fields.']
  physical_exam_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the transaction is contingent upon the acquired player passing a physical examination by the acquiring franchise\'s medical staff. True = physical required before transaction is fully ratified.']
  physical_exam_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the required physical examination for the acquired player: not_required, pending (scheduled but not completed), passed (cleared), failed (transaction may be voided), waived (franchise waived physical requirement).']
  rejection_reason varchar [note: 'Official league-provided explanation for why this transaction was rejected (e.g., salary cap violation, roster limit exceeded, CBA restriction, competitive integrity concern). Null for approved or pending transactions.']
  salary_cap_impact_acquiring decimal(18,2) [note: 'The net change to the acquiring franchise\'s salary cap space resulting from this transaction (positive = cap charge added). Used for real-time cap compliance monitoring per CBA salary cap rules.']
  salary_cap_impact_trading decimal(18,2) [note: 'The net change to the trading franchise\'s salary cap space resulting from this transaction (negative = cap relief received). Used for real-time cap compliance monitoring per CBA salary cap rules.']
  salary_obligation_currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the salary obligation transferred amount (e.g., USD, CAD).']
  salary_obligation_transferred_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total remaining salary obligation (base salary plus guaranteed money) transferred from the trading franchise to the acquiring franchise as part of this transaction. Critical for salary cap compliance tracking per CBA.']
  source_system_transaction_code varchar [note: 'The native transaction identifier from the originating operational system of record (e.g., SAP S/4HANA SD document number, Salesforce CRM opportunity ID) used for lineage tracing and reconciliation with upstream systems.']
  trade_deadline_exception_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this transaction required and received a formal league exception to proceed after the official trade deadline. True = exception granted; False = standard transaction within deadline.']
  transaction_date date [note: 'The official calendar date on which the franchises agreed to and executed the transaction. This is the principal real-world business event date, distinct from the league approval date and system audit timestamps.']
  transaction_reference_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known league-assigned alphanumeric reference number for this transaction, used in official league communications, press releases, and regulatory filings. Format: LEAGUE_CODE-TXN-YEAR-SEQUENCE.']
  transaction_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the transaction in the league approval workflow: pending_approval (submitted, awaiting league review), approved (league has ratified), rejected (league denied), voided (approved but subsequently nullified), conditional_pending (approved subject to future conditions being met), rescinded (mutually withdrawn by franchises prior to approval).']
  transaction_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time (with timezone offset) at which the transaction was formally executed and submitted to the league for approval. Used for waiver priority ordering and transaction window compliance validation.']
  transaction_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the inter-franchise movement: trade (bilateral asset exchange), waiver_claim (franchise claims player off waivers), waiver_placement (franchise places player on waiver wire), dfa_assignment (Designated for Assignment), conditional_trade (trade with future conditions), three_way_trade (multi-franchise exchange). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: trade|waiver_claim|waiver_placement|dfa_assignment|conditional_trade|three_way_trade|release_claim|emergency_acquisition — promote to reference product]']
  transaction_window_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this transaction was executed within the league-mandated transaction window (e.g., trade deadline, waiver wire period). True = compliant; False = executed outside the permitted window, requiring league exception review.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time (with timezone offset) when this transaction record was most recently modified in the league data platform. Used for change data capture, audit trails, and incremental ETL processing.']
  void_date date [note: 'The date on which this transaction was officially voided or rescinded by the league. Null for non-voided transactions.']
  void_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation of why this transaction was voided or rescinded (e.g., failed physical examination, player refusal of trade, salary cap violation discovered post-approval). Null for non-voided transactions.']
  waiver_claim_fee_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The standard waiver claim fee charged to the acquiring franchise by the league upon a successful waiver claim. Amount is defined by the applicable CBA. Zero or null for non-waiver transactions.']
  waiver_priority_order varchar [note: 'The league-assigned waiver priority rank of the claiming franchise at the time of the waiver transaction. Lower number indicates higher priority (e.g., 1 = first priority). Null for non-waiver transaction types.']

  Note: 'Record of inter-franchise player and asset movement including trades, waiver claims, and waiver wire transactions. Captures transaction date, involved franchises, assets exchanged (player references, draft pick references, cash considerations), transaction type (trade, waiver claim, waiver placement, DFA assignment), league approval status, approval date, voiding conditions, transaction window compliance flag, waiver priority order, salary obligation transfer details, and clearing dates. SSOT for all inter-franchise roster movement requiring league approval.'
}

Table league.transaction_window {
  transaction_window_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the league transaction window record. Primary key for the transaction_window data product in the league domain.']
  extended_transaction_window_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on transaction_window (extended_transaction_window_id)']
  fiscal_period_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.fiscal_period. Business justification: Transaction windows (trade deadlines, free agency periods) trigger salary cap adjustments, signing bonus accruals, and waiver claim fees that must be recorded in the correct fiscal period. Finance tea']
  league_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the league that owns and governs this transaction window. Determines which league\'s rules and CBA provisions apply.']
  obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.obligation. Business justification: Transaction windows are governed by specific CBA/regulatory obligations (transfer window rules, waiver wire obligations). CBA compliance tracking for transaction windows requires structured FK; transa']
  season_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the league season during which this transaction window is active. Anchors the window to a specific competitive year and salary cap period.']
  announcement_date date [note: 'Date on which the league officially announced this transaction window to franchises and the public. Establishes the notice period for franchise preparation.']
  approval_turnaround_hours varchar [note: 'Maximum number of hours within which the league office must approve or reject a transaction submitted during this window. Defines the Service Level Agreement (SLA) for transaction processing.']
  cap_year varchar [note: 'The salary cap year (four-digit year) applicable to transactions executed within this window. Determines which cap year\'s limits and exceptions apply for compliance validation.']
  cba_article_reference varchar [note: 'Specific article, section, or clause of the Collective Bargaining Agreement (CBA) that authorizes and governs this transaction window (e.g., \'Article VI, Section 4\'). Supports compliance audit and legal review.']
  close_date date [note: 'The official calendar date on which the transaction window closes and no further transactions may be submitted. Null for open-ended windows such as certain waiver periods.']
  close_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time (with timezone) at which the transaction window officially closes. Critical for trade deadline compliance — transactions submitted after this timestamp are invalid.']
  competitive_integrity_review_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether transactions executed during this window are subject to a competitive integrity review by the league office to prevent collusion, tanking, or other violations of fair competition standards.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this transaction window record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail and data lineage requirements.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary limits and penalty amounts defined within this transaction window (e.g., USD, EUR, CAD).']
  eligible_transaction_types varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of transaction types authorized during this window (e.g., \'trade,waiver_claim,free_agent_signing,dfa,option_exercise\'). Defines the scope of permissible roster movements for compliance validation of league_trade_transaction and waiver_claim records.']
  franchise_participation_scope varchar [note: 'Defines which franchises are eligible to participate in this transaction window. \'all_franchises\' applies league-wide; other values restrict participation to a subset of franchises.']
  governing_body_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the primary governing body that oversees and enforces the rules of this transaction window (e.g., NFL, NBA, MLB, NHL, MLS, FIFA, IOC). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: NFL|NBA|MLB|NHL|MLS|FIFA|IOC|UFC|ATP|OTHER — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  international_transfer_rules_apply boolean [note: 'Indicates whether FIFA Transfer Regulations or equivalent international governing body transfer rules apply to transactions within this window. Relevant for MLS, international soccer, and cross-border athlete movements.']
  is_free_agency_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether free agent signings are permitted during this transaction window. Governs whether franchises may negotiate and execute contracts with unrestricted or restricted free agents.']
  is_international_signing_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether international player signings are authorized during this window. Relevant for MLB International Signing Period, FIFA transfer windows, and MLS international roster slots.']
  is_trade_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether player-for-player or player-for-pick trades are permitted during this transaction window. False for windows restricted to free agency or waiver activity only.']
  is_waiver_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether waiver claims and waiver wire transactions are permitted during this transaction window. Determines whether waiver_claim records may be anchored to this window.']
  league_approval_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether transactions executed during this window require explicit league office approval before becoming effective. When true, transactions remain pending until the league approves.']
  max_cash_consideration_usd decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum cash consideration in US dollars permitted in a single transaction during this window, as governed by CBA provisions. Null if no cash limit applies. Used for compliance validation of league_trade_transaction records.']
  max_transactions_per_franchise varchar [note: 'Maximum number of transactions a single franchise may execute during this window, as defined by league rules or CBA provisions. Null indicates no per-franchise limit.']
  open_date date [note: 'The official calendar date on which the transaction window opens and franchises may begin submitting eligible transactions. Established by the league calendar and CBA provisions.']
  open_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time (with timezone) at which the transaction window officially opens. Used for compliance validation of transaction submission timestamps, particularly for trade deadline enforcement.']
  penalty_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the penalty provisions applicable to violations of this transaction window, including fine schedules, draft pick forfeiture rules, and suspension terms as defined in the CBA or league governance documents.']
  penalty_draft_pick_loss boolean [note: 'Indicates whether violation of this transaction window\'s rules may result in forfeiture of draft picks as a disciplinary penalty, per CBA or league governance provisions.']
  penalty_fine_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary fine amount imposed on a franchise for executing a transaction outside the authorized window or in violation of window rules. Expressed in the currency defined by currency_code.']
  penalty_suspension_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether franchise personnel (e.g., general managers, coaches) may be suspended for violations of this transaction window\'s rules.']
  physical_exam_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a medical physical examination of the athlete is required before a transaction executed during this window becomes effective. Common for trade transactions involving player health risk.']
  roster_freeze_applies boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a roster freeze is in effect during this transaction window, prohibiting certain roster moves. Common during playoff periods or immediately preceding the trade deadline.']
  salary_cap_applies boolean [note: 'Indicates whether salary cap compliance checks are required for transactions executed during this window. When true, all transactions must be validated against the franchise\'s cap space before league approval.']
  season_phase varchar [note: 'The phase of the competitive season during which this transaction window is active. Determines applicable roster rules, salary cap treatment, and CBA provisions for transactions executed within the window.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the operational system of record from which this transaction window record originated (e.g., SAP S/4HANA, Salesforce CRM, league operations platform). Supports data lineage and Silver layer provenance tracking.']
  sport_type varchar [note: 'The sport discipline to which this transaction window applies. Determines sport-specific CBA rules, roster size limits, and eligible transaction types. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: football|basketball|baseball|hockey|soccer|tennis|mma|other — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this transaction window record. Supports change tracking, audit trail, and downstream incremental processing in the Databricks Silver layer.']
  wada_clearance_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether World Anti-Doping Agency (WADA) clearance is required for athletes involved in transactions during this window. Applicable for international transfers and leagues with mandatory anti-doping compliance.']
  window_name varchar [note: 'Official human-readable name of the transaction window as published by the league (e.g., \'NFL Trade Deadline 2024\', \'NBA Free Agency Window 2024-25\', \'MLB International Signing Period 2024\').']
  window_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for league operations staff to record supplementary information about this transaction window, including special conditions, amendments, or exceptions not captured in structured fields.']
  window_reference_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric reference code assigned by the league office to uniquely identify this transaction window for official communications, compliance filings, and franchise notifications.']
  window_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the transaction window. \'scheduled\' = announced but not yet open; \'open\' = active and accepting transactions; \'closed\' = period has ended; \'suspended\' = temporarily halted by league order; \'cancelled\' = voided before opening.']
  window_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the transaction window by its primary business purpose. Determines which roster movement and trade activity types are authorized. DFA = Designated for Assignment. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: trade_deadline|free_agency|waiver_period|dfa_window|international_signing|supplemental_draft — promote to reference product if additional types emerge]']

  Note: 'Defines official league transaction periods (trade deadline, free agency window, waiver period, DFA window, international signing period) with open/close dates, eligible transaction types, franchise participation rules, and penalty provisions for violations. Anchors all roster movement and trade activity to authorized periods. Supports compliance validation for league_trade_transaction and waiver_claim records.'
}

Table league.team {
  team_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for team']
  conference_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the conference this team belongs to, if the league uses a conference structure (e.g., Eastern Conference, Western Conference).']
  division_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the division or conference this team is assigned to within the league (e.g., AFC East, Western Conference).']
  franchise_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the franchise entity that owns this team. Links to franchise agreements and ownership records.']
  home_venue_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the primary venue or stadium where the team plays home games.']
  league_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the league this team competes in (e.g., NFL, NBA, Premier League, La Liga).']
  parent_team_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on team (parent_team_id)']
  training_facility_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the primary training facility or practice venue used by the team.']
  championship_count varchar [note: 'Total number of league championships or titles won by the team in its history.']
  city varchar [note: 'Primary city where the team is based and represents.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code for the team\'s home country (e.g., USA, GBR, CAN).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this team record was first created in the system.']
  established_date date [note: 'Date when the team officially joined the current league or was granted franchise status.']
  fan_base_size varchar [note: 'Estimated number of registered or tracked fans associated with the team across all engagement platforms.']
  founded_date date [note: 'Date when the team was originally established or founded.']
  is_expansion_team boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the team was added to the league as an expansion franchise (true) or is an original/relocated team (false).']
  jersey_sponsor varchar [note: 'Name of the primary corporate sponsor whose branding appears on team jerseys.']
  logo_url varchar [note: 'URL or file path to the official team logo image asset.']
  market_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Estimated market valuation of the team franchise. Expressed in league\'s base currency.']
  market_value_currency varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for market value (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  mascot_name varchar [note: 'Name of the team\'s official mascot character used for fan engagement and entertainment.']
  nickname varchar [note: 'Common nickname or informal name used by fans and media to refer to the team.']
  playoff_appearances varchar [note: 'Total number of playoff or postseason appearances by the team in its history.']
  primary_color varchar [note: 'Primary brand color of the team (hex code or color name). Used for uniforms, branding, and broadcast graphics.']
  relocation_history varchar [note: 'Textual summary of any previous cities or locations where the team was based before current location.']
  rivalry_teams varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of team codes representing traditional rivals or derby opponents.']
  roster_size varchar [note: 'Current number of players on the active roster.']
  salary_cap_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum total salary amount the team is allowed to spend on player contracts under league salary cap rules. Expressed in league\'s base currency.']
  salary_cap_currency varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for salary cap amounts (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  social_media_handle varchar [note: 'Primary social media handle or username for the team (e.g., Twitter/X handle).']
  state_province varchar [note: 'State, province, or region where the team is based.']
  team_code varchar [note: 'Short alphanumeric code or abbreviation for the team (e.g., \'LAL\', \'MUN\', \'NYY\'). Used for broadcast graphics and standings displays.']
  team_name varchar [note: 'Official full name of the team (e.g., \'Los Angeles Lakers\', \'Manchester United\').']
  team_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the team within the league.']
  team_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the team by competitive level and organizational structure.']
  time_zone varchar [note: 'IANA time zone identifier for the team\'s home location (e.g., America/New_York, Europe/London).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this team record was last modified in the system.']
  website_url varchar [note: 'Official website URL for the team.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for team. Referenced by preferred_team_id.'
}

Table league.official_assignment {
  official_assignment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for the official_assignment association']
  game_result_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the official game result record for which this crew assignment was made']
  official_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the assigned game official (referee, umpire, linesman, VAR operator, etc.)']
  assignment_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the officiating bureau formally issued this crew assignment to the official. Used for lead-time tracking and conflict-of-interest window calculations.']
  conflict_of_interest_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a conflict-of-interest concern was raised or reviewed for this specific official-game pairing (e.g., official has a known affiliation with one of the competing franchises). Managed per-assignment by the officiating bureau\'s integrity unit.']
  crew_role varchar [note: 'The specific officiating role this official performed in this game (e.g., Head Referee, Linesman, VAR Operator, TMO, Umpire). Distinct from official.primary_position which reflects career-level specialization; this captures the actual role performed on the day.']
  performance_score decimal [note: 'Numeric performance grade issued by the officiating bureau\'s evaluation team for this official\'s performance in this specific game. Distinct from official.performance_rating which is a rolling composite season score. Feeds into the composite rating calculation.']
  var_tmo_intervened boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this official exercised a VAR or TMO intervention decision during this specific game. Complements game_result.var_tmo_intervention_flag at the game level by attributing the intervention to the specific official responsible.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the Assignment between an official and a game_result. It captures the formal crew assignment record created by the league officiating bureau for each game, recording which official served in which crew role for a specific game result, along with per-game performance grading, VAR/TMO intervention tracking, and conflict-of-interest clearance. Each record links one official to one game_result and carries attributes that exist only in the context of that specific assignment.. Existence Justification: In professional sports leagues, each game is officiated by a multi-person crew (head referee, linesmen, VAR operators, etc.), meaning one game_result is associated with multiple officials. Simultaneously, each official works many games per season. The league officiating department actively manages these crew assignments as a formal business process — creating, updating, and tracking them with role, performance grade, and conflict-of-interest data that belongs to neither the official nor the game result alone.'
}

Table finance.gl_account {
  gl_account_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the GL account master record in the Databricks Silver Layer. Primary key for all downstream joins and lineage tracking. Role: MASTER_RESOURCE.']
  company_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.company_code. Business justification: GL accounts in SAP FI are defined within a specific company code\'s chart of accounts. The company_code string field is a denormalized reference that should be replaced with a typed FK to company_code.']
  account_changed_by varchar [note: 'SAP user ID of the person who last modified this GL account master record (SKA1.AEDTM user). Required for SOX audit trail, segregation of duties compliance, and internal audit reviews. Supports detection of unauthorized changes to chart of accounts master data.']
  account_changed_date date [note: 'Date on which this GL account master record was last modified (SKA1.LAEDA). Used for change management tracking, SOX compliance, and data lineage in the Databricks Silver Layer. Triggers downstream reprocessing of dependent financial reporting datasets when account attributes change.']
  account_created_by varchar [note: 'SAP user ID of the person who created this GL account master record (SKA1.ERNAM). Required for SOX audit trail and segregation of duties (SoD) compliance. Enables identification of unauthorized account creation and supports internal audit reviews of chart of accounts governance.']
  account_created_date date [note: 'Date on which this GL account master record was first created in the chart of accounts (SKA1.ERDAT). Used for audit trail, SOX compliance documentation, and chart of accounts change management reporting. Supports identification of accounts created outside of standard change control processes.']
  account_group_code varchar [note: 'SAP account group (SKA1.KTOKS) that controls the number range and field selection for the GL account. Groups accounts by business function, e.g., revenue accounts, expense accounts, balance sheet accounts. Determines which fields are required, optional, or suppressed during account maintenance.']
  account_long_name varchar [note: 'Extended descriptive name of the GL account (SKAT.TXT50) providing full business context. Used in detailed financial disclosures, audit documentation, and SEC reporting narratives.']
  account_name varchar [note: 'Short descriptive name of the GL account as maintained in SAP S/4HANA (SKAT.TXT20). Used on financial reports, trial balances, and chart of accounts listings. Examples: \'Ticket Revenue\', \'Broadcast Rights Income\', \'Player Salary Expense\', \'Naming Rights Deferred Revenue\'.']
  account_number varchar [note: 'The externally-known, human-readable GL account number as defined in the SAP S/4HANA chart of accounts (SKA1.SAKNR). Uniquely identifies the posting target within a chart of accounts. Used on all journal entries, financial statements, and cost element assignments.']
  account_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the GL account master record. \'active\' accounts accept postings; \'blocked\' accounts are temporarily prevented from receiving new journal entries; \'marked_for_deletion\' accounts are pending archival after period-end close. Controls posting eligibility across all Sports Entertainment legal entities.']
  account_type varchar [note: 'Fundamental financial classification of the GL account indicating its role in the financial statements (SKA1.KTOKS mapped to account type). \'asset\' and \'liability\' and \'equity\' are balance sheet accounts; \'revenue\' and \'expense\' are profit and loss (P&L) accounts. Drives financial statement line assignment and period-end closing behavior.']
  alternative_account_number varchar [note: 'Alternative account number used for country-specific or group reporting chart of accounts mapping (SKA1.ALTKT). Enables mapping between the operational chart of accounts and a group consolidation chart of accounts or statutory reporting chart of accounts for international league entities operating under different GAAP/IFRS frameworks.']
  balance_sheet_account boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this GL account is a balance sheet account (True) or a profit and loss (P&L) income statement account (False) (SKA1.XBILK). Balance sheet accounts carry forward balances at year-end; P&L accounts are closed to retained earnings. Fundamental for period-end close processing and financial statement preparation.']
  chart_of_accounts_code varchar [note: 'Four-character SAP chart of accounts key (SKA1.KTOPL) to which this GL account belongs. Sports Entertainment may maintain multiple charts of accounts for different legal entity frameworks (e.g., US GAAP vs IFRS for international leagues). Determines the account structure and financial statement mapping.']
  commitment_management boolean [note: 'Indicates whether commitment management (purchase order commitments and funds reservations) is active for this GL account. When True, purchase orders and contracts posted against this account create budget commitments visible in FP&A reporting. Relevant for player contract commitments, venue capital expenditure, and broadcast production cost commitments.']
  controlling_area varchar [note: 'Four-character SAP Controlling area code to which this GL account\'s cost element is assigned (SKA1 CO integration). The controlling area defines the organizational unit within which cost and revenue accounting is performed. Enables cross-company-code cost allocation, internal order settlement, and EBITDA reporting across Sports Entertainment\'s multi-entity structure.']
  cost_center_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a cost center assignment is mandatory when posting to this GL account. When True, all journal entries must include a valid cost center (e.g., venue operations cost center, broadcast production cost center, athlete management cost center). Enforces cost allocation discipline for EBITDA reporting and FP&A variance analysis.']
  cost_element_category varchar [note: 'Defines the cost element category in SAP Controlling (CO) module when this GL account is also a primary cost element (SKA1.KATYP). \'primary\' cost elements correspond directly to P&L GL accounts (e.g., player salary expense, venue operating cost, broadcast production cost); \'secondary\' cost elements are used for internal allocations and settlements between cost centers; \'none\' for balance sheet accounts not used in CO. Critical for EBITDA reporting and cost center allocation in FP&A.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the account currency in which balances are maintained (SKB1.WAERS). Sports Entertainment operates across global markets with revenues in USD, EUR, GBP, CAD, AUD, and other currencies. Accounts may be maintained in local currency, group currency (USD), or hard currency. Drives foreign currency revaluation and translation for consolidated SEC reporting.']
  deletion_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this GL account has been flagged for deletion from the chart of accounts (SKA1.XLOEV). Accounts flagged for deletion cannot receive new postings and are scheduled for archival after all dependent records are cleared. Supports chart of accounts rationalization and SOX control compliance.']
  field_status_group varchar [note: 'SAP field status group code assigned to the GL account that controls which fields are required, optional, or suppressed during document entry (SKB1.FSTAG). Ensures data completeness for postings — for example, cost center is required on expense accounts, profit center on revenue accounts, and business area on intercompany settlement accounts.']
  financial_statement_item varchar [note: 'The financial statement version item code to which this GL account is assigned for external reporting (SKA1.BILKT). Maps the account to a specific line on the income statement, balance sheet, or cash flow statement as required for SEC 10-K/10-Q filings and EBITDA reporting.']
  functional_area varchar [note: 'Business functional area to which this GL account is assigned for segment and functional reporting (SKA1.FUNC_AREA). Enables profit and loss reporting by business function such as event operations, broadcast, athlete management, venue operations, merchandise, sponsorship, and corporate. Supports SEC segment reporting requirements under GAAP ASC 280.']
  house_bank_account boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this GL account is a house bank account used for cash management and bank reconciliation (SKB1.XINTB). When True, the account is linked to a physical bank account for automated bank statement processing, cash pooling, and treasury operations. Relevant for Sports Entertainment\'s multi-entity cash management across leagues and venues.']
  intercompany_account boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this GL account is designated for intercompany transactions between Sports Entertainment legal entities (e.g., between the league holding company, individual team entities, broadcast subsidiaries, and venue operating companies). When True, intercompany partner must be specified on postings for automated elimination in consolidated financial statements. Required for SOX-compliant intercompany reconciliation.']
  interest_calculation boolean [note: 'Indicates whether automatic interest calculation is activated for this GL account (SKB1.XINTB for interest indicator). Relevant for intercompany loan accounts, deferred revenue accounts (naming rights, multi-year broadcast deals), and overdue receivable accounts where interest accrual is contractually required.']
  line_item_display boolean [note: 'Indicates whether individual line items are stored and displayable for this GL account (SKB1.XKRES). When True, each posting line is retained for drill-down reporting and audit trail purposes. Required for SOX-compliant audit trails on revenue accounts (ticket revenue, broadcast rights income, sponsorship fees) and significant expense accounts (player salaries, venue operating costs).']
  open_item_management boolean [note: 'Indicates whether open item management is activated for this GL account (SKB1.XOPVW). When True, individual line items remain open until explicitly cleared against an offsetting entry (e.g., deferred revenue accounts for naming rights, advance ticket deposits, broadcast rights prepayments). Enables aging analysis and outstanding item tracking for FP&A.']
  planning_level varchar [note: 'Planning level code that controls how this GL account participates in financial planning and budgeting processes in SAP CO and Workday Adaptive Planning integration. Determines the granularity at which budget vs. actual variance analysis is performed — account level, cost center level, or profit center level. Supports FP&A EBITDA budgeting and ARR tracking.']
  posting_block boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether postings to this GL account are currently blocked at the chart of accounts level (SKA1.XSPEA). When True, no journal entries can be posted to this account regardless of company code settings. Used during account restructuring, period-end close, or SOX control enforcement.']
  profit_center_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a profit center assignment is mandatory when posting to this GL account. When True, all postings must include a valid profit center enabling segment-level profitability reporting (e.g., by league, by venue, by broadcast network). Supports SEC segment reporting under GAAP ASC 280 and internal EBITDA reporting by business unit.']
  reconciliation_account_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether this GL account serves as a reconciliation account for a subledger and, if so, which subledger type (SKA1.MITKZ). \'customer\' reconciliation accounts aggregate accounts receivable subledger balances (e.g., fan ticket receivables, sponsor invoices); \'vendor\' for accounts payable (e.g., venue contractor payables); \'asset\' for fixed asset subledger (e.g., stadium infrastructure); \'none\' for non-reconciliation accounts. Direct postings to reconciliation accounts are blocked.']
  revenue_recognition_category varchar [note: 'Classification of the revenue recognition pattern applicable to this GL account under GAAP ASC 606 / IFRS 15. \'point_in_time\' for single-event revenues (e.g., individual ticket sales, PPV events, merchandise sales); \'over_time\' for performance obligations satisfied over a period (e.g., season ticket packages, multi-year broadcast rights, naming rights); \'deferred\' for advance payments not yet earned (e.g., prepaid sponsorship fees, advance broadcast rights payments); \'not_applicable\' for non-revenue accounts. Critical for SEC revenue disclosure and audit compliance.']
  sort_key varchar [note: 'Three-digit sort key code that determines the assignment field populated in the allocation field of line items posted to this account (SKB1.ZUAWA). Controls how line items are sorted and grouped in account displays and reports. Common sort keys include posting date, document number, and external document reference for reconciliation purposes.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the operational source system from which this GL account master record was extracted (e.g., \'SAP_S4HANA_PROD\', \'SAP_S4HANA_EU\'). Supports multi-instance SAP landscapes where Sports Entertainment operates separate SAP systems for different regions or leagues. Enables data lineage tracking and conflict resolution in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  sports_revenue_stream varchar [note: 'Sports Entertainment-specific revenue stream classification for this GL account, enabling industry-specific financial analytics and KPI reporting. Examples: \'ticket_revenue\', \'broadcast_rights_income\', \'naming_rights_revenue\', \'sponsorship_fees\', \'merchandise_licensing\', \'ppv_revenue\', \'concession_revenue\', \'player_transfer_fee\', \'nil_licensing_income\', \'venue_rental\'. Used for EBITDA decomposition, ARR tracking, and league financial reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: ticket_revenue|broadcast_rights_income|naming_rights_revenue|sponsorship_fees|merchandise_licensing|ppv_revenue|concession_revenue|player_transfer_fee|nil_licensing_income|venue_rental — promote to reference product]']
  tax_category varchar [note: 'Tax category assigned to the GL account controlling tax code entry behavior during posting (SKB1.MWSKZ). Determines whether tax codes are required, optional, or not allowed on journal entries to this account. Relevant for VAT/GST on international broadcast rights, ticket sales tax, merchandise sales tax, and venue concession tax compliance.']
  tax_relevant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this GL account is subject to tax reporting and requires a tax code on postings (SKB1.XMWNO negated). When True, postings must include a valid tax code for VAT, sales tax, or withholding tax compliance. Critical for international broadcast rights distribution, PPV revenue, and merchandise sales across jurisdictions.']
  tolerance_group varchar [note: 'Tolerance group code assigned to the GL account that defines acceptable variance thresholds for payment differences, rounding, and clearing (SKB1.ZUAWA tolerance reference). Controls automatic clearing of small differences on high-volume accounts such as ticket revenue clearing, merchandise POS settlement, and concession cash accounts. Supports SOX-compliant payment processing controls.']
  valid_from_date date [note: 'Date from which this GL account is valid and available for posting. Supports time-dependent account activation for new business lines (e.g., a new OTT streaming revenue account activated at the start of a new broadcast deal, or a new NIL licensing income account activated upon regulatory approval). Enables historical accuracy in financial reporting.']
  valid_to_date date [note: 'Date until which this GL account is valid for posting. Null indicates the account has no planned expiry. Used to retire accounts associated with discontinued business lines (e.g., a closed venue, an expired broadcast rights agreement, a divested team entity) while preserving historical posting data for audit and SEC reporting.']

  Note: 'General ledger account master record defining a specific financial classification node within the enterprise chart of accounts. Each GL account represents a unique posting target (e.g., ticket revenue, broadcast rights income, venue operating expense, player salary, naming rights deferred revenue). Stores account number, description, account type (P&L vs balance sheet), currency, tax category, reconciliation account flag, cost element category, account group, and posting block status. SSOT for all account classification, financial statement line mapping, and cost element definitions across all Sports Entertainment legal entities in SAP S/4HANA FI.'
}

Table finance.cost_center {
  cost_center_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the cost center record in the Sports Entertainment lakehouse Silver layer. Primary key generated by the platform.']
  company_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.company_code. Business justification: Cost centers in SAP CO belong to a specific company code (legal entity). The company_code string field is a denormalized reference that should be replaced with a typed FK to company_code.company_code_']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Cost centers are assigned to profit centers in SAP CO for segment reporting. The profit_center_code string field is a denormalized reference that should be replaced with a typed FK to profit_center.pr']
  venue_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the physical venue or facility primarily associated with this cost center, where applicable (e.g., a venue operations cost center linked to a specific arena). Enables venue-level cost analysis and facility management reporting.']
  activity_type_code varchar [note: 'Primary SAP CO activity type associated with this cost center, representing the unit of output or service provided (e.g., \'BROADCAST_HR\' for broadcast production hours, \'VENUE_DAY\' for venue operating days). Used for activity-based cost allocation and internal service pricing.']
  allocation_method varchar [note: 'Method used to allocate or settle costs from this cost center to other cost objects at period end: direct (direct activity allocation), assessment (using assessment cycles), distribution (using distribution cycles), activity_based (ABC costing), or statistical (for reporting only, no actual settlement).']
  budget_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total approved annual budget allocated to this cost center for the current fiscal year, expressed in the cost center\'s primary currency. Sourced from Workday Adaptive Planning and loaded into SAP CO for variance reporting. Used in EBITDA forecasting and FP&A dashboards.']
  business_domain varchar [note: 'Sports Entertainment business domain that this cost center primarily supports. Enables cross-domain EBITDA analysis and cost attribution across the enterprise (e.g., \'venue\' for arena operations, \'broadcast\' for OTT/RSN production costs). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: venue|broadcast|athlete|league|sponsorship|merchandise|ticketing|fan_engagement|content|finance|workforce|compliance|digital_platform — promote to reference product]']
  business_unit varchar [note: 'Sports Entertainment business unit or division that owns this cost center (e.g., \'NFL Operations\', \'OTT Streaming\', \'Venue Management\', \'Athlete Performance\'). Used for segment-level EBITDA reporting and ARR tracking.']
  controlling_area_code varchar [note: 'SAP CO controlling area to which this cost center belongs. The controlling area defines the organizational unit within which cost accounting is performed and cross-company cost allocations are managed (e.g., \'SE01\' for Sports Entertainment North America).']
  cost_center_category varchar [note: 'Single-character or two-character SAP CO cost center category code that controls which activity types and statistical key figures can be posted (e.g., \'A\' = general, \'B\' = production, \'F\' = financial). Determines default allocation rules and reporting groupings.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'Alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the cost center within the controlling area, as defined in SAP CO. This is the externally-known business identifier used in journal entries, purchase orders, and budget allocations (e.g., \'VEN-OPS-001\', \'BCAST-PROD-02\').']
  cost_center_description varchar [note: 'Free-text description of the cost center\'s business purpose, scope of operations, and cost accountability. Provides context for financial analysts, auditors, and FP&A teams beyond what the name and code convey (e.g., \'Responsible for all operational costs of the home venue including HVAC, BMS, security, and event-day staffing\').']
  cost_center_name varchar [note: 'Full descriptive name of the cost center as maintained in SAP CO master data (e.g., \'Venue Operations — Staples Center\', \'Broadcast Production — NFL Sunday Package\'). Used in financial reports, budget dashboards, and FP&A outputs.']
  cost_center_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the cost center in SAP CO: active (accepting postings), inactive (temporarily suspended), locked (blocked for all postings), pending_activation (created but not yet effective), or archived (historical, no postings allowed). Drives period-end close controls and SOX compliance checks.']
  cost_center_type varchar [note: 'SAP CO cost center category classifying the nature of cost incurrence: overhead (administrative/support), production (direct event/broadcast output), service (shared services), profit (revenue-generating unit tracked as cost center), or investment (capital project support). Drives allocation methodology and EBITDA reporting treatment.']
  cost_element_group varchar [note: 'SAP CO cost element group defining the set of primary cost elements (GL accounts) that can be posted to this cost center. Controls which expense categories are tracked here (e.g., \'VENUE_OPEX\' for venue operating expenses, \'TALENT_COSTS\' for athlete/performer costs).']
  country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the primary country where this cost center operates. Used for statutory reporting, VAT/tax jurisdiction assignment, and GDPR/CCPA data residency compliance.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this cost center master record was first created in SAP CO. Used for audit trail, SOX compliance documentation, and data lineage tracking in the lakehouse Silver layer.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code representing the primary transaction currency for this cost center (e.g., \'USD\', \'EUR\', \'GBP\'). Drives currency translation in SAP FI/CO and multi-currency EBITDA consolidation.']
  department_code varchar [note: 'Organizational department code associated with this cost center, aligned to the SAP SuccessFactors organizational hierarchy. Enables workforce cost attribution and headcount-to-cost analysis across Sports Entertainment business units.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'Fiscal year to which the budget amount and planning data for this cost center applies (e.g., 2024, 2025). Aligns with the Sports Entertainment fiscal calendar defined in SAP FI company code settings.']
  functional_area_code varchar [note: 'SAP FI/CO functional area classifying the cost center by business function for cost-of-sales accounting and segment reporting (e.g., \'ADMIN\', \'SALES\', \'PROD\', \'R&D\'). Required for SEC income statement presentation under US GAAP.']
  geographic_region varchar [note: 'Geographic region or market where this cost center primarily operates (e.g., \'North America\', \'EMEA\', \'APAC\'). Supports regional cost allocation, transfer pricing compliance, and multi-market EBITDA reporting.']
  gl_account_group varchar [note: 'SAP FI GL account group associated with this cost center, defining the range of general ledger accounts used for cost postings. Ensures chart-of-accounts alignment and supports automated financial statement mapping for SEC reporting.']
  hierarchy_level varchar [note: 'Numeric level of this cost center within the standard cost center hierarchy (e.g., 1 = enterprise, 2 = division, 3 = department, 4 = operational unit). Used for rollup reporting in Workday Adaptive Planning and SAP CO standard hierarchy.']
  intercompany_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this cost center participates in intercompany cost settlements between Sports Entertainment legal entities (True = intercompany). Triggers intercompany elimination entries in consolidated financial statements and SEC reporting.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent change to this cost center master record in SAP CO. Supports change detection for incremental lakehouse loads, SOX audit trails, and master data governance reviews.']
  plan_lock boolean [note: 'Indicates whether budget/plan postings to this cost center are currently blocked (True = locked). Prevents changes to approved budgets after the planning cycle is closed in Workday Adaptive Planning and SAP CO.']
  posting_lock boolean [note: 'Indicates whether actual cost postings to this cost center are currently blocked (True = locked, no postings allowed). Used during period-end close, organizational restructuring, or SOX audit freeze periods to prevent unauthorized cost entries.']
  sap_changed_by varchar [note: 'SAP user ID of the person who last modified this cost center master record. Required for SOX change management controls and audit trail. Stored as the SAP logon ID.']
  sap_created_by varchar [note: 'SAP user ID of the person who created this cost center master record in SAP CO. Required for SOX segregation-of-duties controls and audit trail documentation. Stored as the SAP logon ID (not personal name).']
  segment_code varchar [note: 'SEC/IFRS 8 reportable segment to which this cost center belongs for external financial disclosure (e.g., \'LIVE_EVENTS\', \'MEDIA_RIGHTS\', \'MERCHANDISE\'). Drives segment footnote disclosures in SEC Form 10-K and quarterly 10-Q filings.']
  short_name varchar [note: 'Abbreviated display name for the cost center used in condensed financial reports, dashboards, and SAP transaction screens where space is limited (max 20 characters per SAP CO standard).']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Operational system of record from which this cost center master record was sourced (e.g., \'SAP_S4HANA\' for records originating from SAP CO, \'WORKDAY\' for records originating from Workday Adaptive Planning, \'MANUAL\' for manually entered records). Supports data lineage and reconciliation.']
  sox_relevant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this cost center is in scope for Sarbanes-Oxley (SOX) internal control testing and audit (True = SOX relevant). SOX-relevant cost centers require documented controls, segregation of duties, and periodic management certification per SOX Sections 302 and 404.']
  valid_from date [note: 'Date from which this cost center is active and eligible to receive cost postings in SAP CO. Defines the start of the cost center\'s validity period for budget planning, actual cost collection, and period-end closing.']
  valid_to date [note: 'Date until which this cost center is active and eligible to receive cost postings. After this date, the cost center is locked for new postings. Null indicates an open-ended validity period. Used for fiscal year planning and organizational restructuring.']

  Note: 'SAP CO cost center master representing an organizational unit responsible for incurring costs — e.g., venue operations, broadcast production, athlete performance, league administration, merchandise fulfillment. Tracks cost center hierarchy, responsible manager, controlling area, validity period, and budget allocation. SSOT for cost accountability across all Sports Entertainment business units.'
}

Table finance.profit_center {
  profit_center_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the profit center record in the Databricks Silver Layer. Primary key generated by the lakehouse ingestion pipeline from SAP CO master data.']
  company_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.company_code. Business justification: Profit centers in SAP CO belong to a specific company code (legal entity). The company_code string field is a denormalized reference that should be replaced with a typed FK to company_code.company_cod']
  league_id varchar [note: 'FK to league.league']
  venue_id varchar [note: 'FK to venue.venue']
  annual_capex_budget decimal(18,2) [note: 'Approved annual capital expenditure budget for this profit center in local currency. Relevant for venue operations and broadcast infrastructure profit centers. Used in FP&A and SEC capital allocation disclosures.']
  annual_opex_budget decimal(18,2) [note: 'Approved annual operating expenditure budget for this profit center in local currency, as set in Workday Adaptive Planning. Includes direct costs and allocated overhead. Used for EBITDA margin planning and cost control.']
  annual_revenue_budget decimal(18,2) [note: 'Approved annual revenue budget for this profit center in the profit center\'s local currency, as set in Workday Adaptive Planning FP&A. Used for budget vs. actual variance analysis and EBITDA forecasting.']
  arr_tracking_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this profit center tracks Annual Recurring Revenue (ARR) as a primary financial metric. Applicable to OTT streaming, digital subscription, and DTC platform profit centers where ARR is a key investor KPI.']
  broadcast_rights_type varchar [note: 'Classification of broadcast rights revenue stream associated with this profit center where applicable. Drives revenue recognition treatment under ASC 606 for media rights deals. Values: RSN (Regional Sports Network), national_tv, OTT (Over-The-Top Streaming), PPV (Pay-Per-View), international, radio, none. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: rsn|national_tv|ott|ppv|international|radio|none — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  business_unit_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the revenue-generating business unit represented by this profit center within Sports Entertainment\'s portfolio. Drives EBITDA reporting structure and profitability analysis by segment. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: league_franchise|venue|ott_platform|broadcast_network|merchandise_line|athlete_management|concert_series|sponsorship_unit|corporate — promote to reference product]']
  controlling_area_code varchar [note: 'SAP Controlling Area code to which this profit center belongs. The controlling area defines the organizational unit within which cost and profit center accounting is performed. Maps to SAP field KOKRS.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the primary operating country for this profit center. Drives regulatory compliance requirements (GDPR for EU, CCPA for California), tax treatment, and currency assignment.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this profit center master record was first created in SAP CO. Used for audit trail, data lineage, and SOX control evidence. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the profit center\'s local reporting currency (e.g., \'USD\', \'EUR\', \'GBP\'). Used for EBITDA reporting and currency translation in multi-currency consolidations. Maps to SAP field WAERS.']
  department_code varchar [note: 'Organizational department code associated with this profit center, linking it to the functional area within Sports Entertainment (e.g., \'FINANCE\', \'OPERATIONS\', \'BROADCAST\'). Maps to SAP field ABTEI.']
  elimination_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether postings to this profit center are subject to elimination during group financial consolidation. Prevents double-counting of intercompany revenues and costs in consolidated EBITDA and SEC financial statements.']
  fiscal_year_variant varchar [note: 'SAP fiscal year variant code defining the fiscal calendar applicable to this profit center (e.g., \'K4\' for calendar year, \'V3\' for April–March). Critical for period-end EBITDA and ARR reporting alignment. Maps to SAP field PERIV.']
  functional_area_code varchar [note: 'SAP functional area code classifying the profit center\'s business function for cost-of-sales accounting and P&L by function reporting (e.g., \'SALES\', \'ADMIN\', \'PRODUCTION\', \'MARKETING\'). Maps to SAP field FUNC_AREA.']
  gl_account_group varchar [note: 'SAP chart of accounts group assigned to this profit center, defining the range of GL accounts permissible for postings. Ensures correct revenue recognition and expense classification per the enterprise chart of accounts.']
  hierarchy_level varchar [note: 'Numeric depth of this profit center within the standard hierarchy tree (1 = enterprise root, 2 = segment, 3 = business unit, 4 = sub-unit). Used for hierarchical aggregation in EBITDA and FP&A reporting.']
  hierarchy_node_code varchar [note: 'Code of the profit center group/node in the standard hierarchy (SAP EC-PCA hierarchy) to which this profit center belongs. Enables roll-up reporting from individual profit centers to business segments and enterprise level. Maps to SAP field KHINR.']
  intercompany_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this profit center participates in intercompany transactions and settlements between legal entities within the Sports Entertainment group. When True, intercompany elimination rules apply during consolidation. Maps to SAP intercompany indicator.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this profit center master record in SAP CO. Supports change tracking, audit trail requirements, and incremental data pipeline processing in the Databricks Silver Layer. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  manager varchar [note: 'Name or employee ID of the person responsible for the profit center\'s financial performance and budget accountability. Used for management reporting, variance analysis, and SOX control ownership. Maps to SAP field VERAK.']
  nil_revenue_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this profit center is eligible to record NIL (Name, Image, and Likeness) revenue streams from athlete endorsement and licensing arrangements. Relevant for athlete management and league franchise profit centers under NCAA and professional league NIL rules.']
  profit_center_code varchar [note: 'The externally-known alphanumeric code that uniquely identifies the profit center within the SAP CO controlling area. Used in journal entries, cost allocations, and EBITDA reporting (e.g., \'PC-NBA-LAL\', \'PC-OTT-001\', \'PC-VENUE-MSG\'). Maps to SAP field PRCTR.']
  profit_center_description varchar [note: 'Long-form business description of the profit center\'s purpose, scope, and the revenue-generating activities it encompasses. Provides context for financial analysts, auditors, and FP&A teams. Maps to SAP field LTEXT.']
  profit_center_name varchar [note: 'Full descriptive name of the profit center as defined in SAP CO master data (e.g., \'Los Angeles Lakers Franchise\', \'OTT Streaming Platform - North America\', \'Madison Square Garden Venue Operations\'). Maps to SAP field KTEXT.']
  profit_center_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the profit center in SAP CO. \'active\' = postings allowed; \'locked\' = postings blocked (e.g., during restructuring); \'inactive\' = not yet activated; \'archived\' = historical record retained for audit. Maps to SAP field LOCK_IND and activation status.']
  region_code varchar [note: 'Geographic region code for this profit center used in regional EBITDA roll-up and management reporting (e.g., \'NA-EAST\', \'NA-WEST\', \'EMEA\', \'APAC\'). Supports multi-regional financial analysis for global sports entertainment operations.']
  revenue_category varchar [note: 'Primary revenue category for this profit center, classifying the dominant revenue stream for EBITDA reporting and segment analysis. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: ticket_sales|media_rights|sponsorship|merchandise|concessions|digital_subscription|licensing|venue_rental|other — promote to reference product]']
  sap_changed_by varchar [note: 'SAP user ID of the person who last modified this profit center master record. Required for SOX audit trail and change management controls. Maps to SAP field AENAM.']
  sap_created_by varchar [note: 'SAP user ID of the person who created this profit center master record in SAP CO. Required for SOX audit trail and segregation of duties (SoD) compliance. Maps to SAP field ERNAM.']
  segment_code varchar [note: 'Financial reporting segment classification code assigned to the profit center for IFRS 8 / ASC 280 segment reporting (e.g., \'LIVE-EVENTS\', \'BROADCAST\', \'MERCHANDISE\', \'DIGITAL-OTT\'). Drives SEC segment disclosures. Maps to SAP field SEGMENT.']
  short_name varchar [note: 'Abbreviated name of the profit center used in reports, dashboards, and financial statements where space is limited. Maps to SAP field MCTXT.']
  sox_control_owner varchar [note: 'Name or employee ID of the designated SOX internal control owner responsible for financial reporting controls associated with this profit center. Required for SOX Section 302/404 compliance documentation and audit evidence.']
  sox_risk_rating varchar [note: 'SOX financial reporting risk rating assigned to this profit center based on materiality, transaction volume, and complexity. Determines the frequency and depth of internal control testing required by the external auditor.']
  target_ebitda_margin_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Planned EBITDA margin percentage for this profit center as a target set during annual budgeting in Workday Adaptive Planning. Expressed as a percentage of revenue. Used as the primary KPI for profitability performance management.']
  valid_from date [note: 'Date from which this profit center is effective and postings are permitted in SAP CO. Aligns with fiscal year activation. Maps to SAP field DATAB.']
  valid_to date [note: 'Date until which this profit center is effective. A value of 9999-12-31 indicates an open-ended, indefinitely active profit center. Maps to SAP field DATBI.']

  Note: 'SAP CO profit center master representing a revenue-generating business unit — e.g., a specific league franchise, venue, OTT streaming platform, or merchandise line. Enables EBITDA reporting and profitability analysis at the business segment level. Stores profit center hierarchy, controlling area, company code assignment, and segment classification.'
}

Table finance.company_code {
  company_code_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for the company code record in the Sports Entertainment lakehouse Silver layer. Serves as the primary key for all downstream joins and lineage tracking.']
  accounting_standard varchar [note: 'The accounting standard framework applied to the financial statements of this company code. US_GAAP for SEC-registered US entities, IFRS for international subsidiaries, LOCAL_GAAP for statutory reporting in specific jurisdictions.']
  business_area varchar [note: 'SAP S/4HANA business area code assigned to this company code for cross-company-code segment reporting. Represents a distinct business segment such as League Operations, Broadcast, Venue Management, or Merchandise. Supports EBITDA reporting by business segment per SEC ASC 280.']
  chart_of_accounts varchar [note: 'Four-character SAP S/4HANA chart of accounts key assigned to this company code (e.g., \'SPRT\'). Defines the general ledger account structure, account groups, and account number ranges used for all financial postings. Required for SOX-compliant financial controls and SEC reporting.']
  city varchar [note: 'City of the registered office or principal place of business. Used in conjunction with street address for regulatory filings and legal correspondence.']
  company_code_code varchar [note: 'The four-character alphanumeric SAP S/4HANA company code that uniquely identifies the legal entity within the enterprise organizational structure. Aligns with the SAP FI company code field (BUKRS). Used in all intercompany postings, chart of accounts assignments, and SEC financial reporting.']
  company_code_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the company code within the SAP S/4HANA organizational structure. Controls whether new financial postings, purchase orders, and sales documents can be created against this entity. \'in_liquidation\' triggers SOX disclosure requirements.']
  consolidation_unit varchar [note: 'The consolidation unit identifier in SAP S/4HANA Group Reporting that maps this company code to the group consolidation structure. Used for automated intercompany elimination, minority interest calculation, and SEC consolidated financial statement preparation.']
  controlling_area varchar [note: 'Four-character SAP S/4HANA Controlling (CO) area code to which this company code is assigned. The controlling area is the organizational unit for cost accounting, cost center hierarchies, internal orders, and EBITDA reporting. A single controlling area may span multiple company codes for consolidated management reporting.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the jurisdiction in which the legal entity is incorporated (e.g., \'USA\', \'GBR\', \'DEU\'). Determines applicable tax law, regulatory reporting obligations (GDPR, CCPA), and functional currency assignment.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the company code master record was first created in the SAP S/4HANA system or the lakehouse Silver layer. Used for audit trail, SOX compliance, and data lineage tracking.']
  credit_control_area varchar [note: 'SAP S/4HANA credit control area assigned to this company code, governing credit limit management for sponsorship deals, broadcast rights receivables, and merchandise licensing. Controls credit exposure across the SD (Sales and Distribution) module.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code representing the local (functional) currency of the company code (e.g., \'USD\', \'EUR\', \'GBP\'). All financial postings in SAP S/4HANA FI are recorded in this currency as the primary valuation currency. Critical for EBITDA and ARR reporting.']
  deactivation_date date [note: 'The date on which the company code was deactivated or blocked in SAP S/4HANA, preventing new financial postings. Populated when an entity is dissolved, merged, or placed in liquidation. Null for active entities.']
  document_number_range varchar [note: 'SAP S/4HANA document number range interval assigned to this company code for financial document numbering. Ensures sequential, gap-free document numbering required for SOX audit trails and SEC financial reporting integrity.']
  entity_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the legal entity by its primary business function within the Sports Entertainment enterprise. Drives intercompany settlement rules, revenue recognition policies, and segment reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: league_operating_company|broadcast_network|venue_management|athlete_management|merchandise_licensing|holding_company|joint_venture — promote to reference product]']
  field_status_variant varchar [note: 'SAP S/4HANA field status variant assigned to this company code, controlling which fields are required, optional, or suppressed during financial document entry. Enforces data quality standards for SOX-compliant financial postings.']
  fiscal_year_variant varchar [note: 'Two-character SAP S/4HANA fiscal year variant code that defines the fiscal year structure for this company code (e.g., \'K4\' for calendar year, \'V3\' for April–March). Governs period-end closing, financial statement preparation, and FP&A planning cycles in Workday Adaptive Planning.']
  functional_currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 functional currency code as defined under IFRS IAS 21 or US GAAP ASC 830, representing the currency of the primary economic environment in which the entity operates. May differ from local currency for entities with predominantly USD-denominated broadcast rights or sponsorship revenues.']
  gdpr_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this company code is subject to GDPR obligations due to operating in or processing personal data of EU residents. Triggers data privacy controls, fan data consent management, and Data Protection Officer (DPO) assignment requirements.']
  group_currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 currency code for the group (parent) reporting currency used in consolidated financial statements (e.g., \'USD\' for the Sports Entertainment parent). All company code financials are translated to this currency for SEC consolidated reporting and EBITDA roll-up.']
  hard_currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 currency code for the hard currency (index currency) used in high-inflation countries per SAP S/4HANA parallel valuation. Applicable for Sports Entertainment entities operating in hyperinflationary economies per IFRS IAS 29.']
  incorporation_date date [note: 'The date on which the legal entity was formally incorporated or registered with the relevant government authority. Used in SEC filings, SOX attestations, and legal entity lifecycle management.']
  intercompany_clearing_account varchar [note: 'General ledger account number used for intercompany clearing transactions between Sports Entertainment subsidiaries (e.g., broadcast rights fees from broadcast network entity to league operating company). Critical for SOX-compliant intercompany elimination in consolidated SEC reporting.']
  language_key varchar [note: 'ISO 639-1 two-letter language code representing the primary business language for this company code in SAP S/4HANA (e.g., \'EN\', \'DE\', \'FR\'). Governs the language used for financial documents, correspondence, and system output for this entity.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the company code master record. Supports change data capture (CDC) in the Databricks lakehouse pipeline and SOX-compliant audit trail for financial master data changes.']
  legal_entity_identifier varchar [note: '20-character ISO 17442 Legal Entity Identifier (LEI) assigned by a Local Operating Unit (LOU) for publicly traded or regulated Sports Entertainment entities. Required for SEC financial reporting, derivatives reporting under Dodd-Frank, and international regulatory filings.']
  legal_name varchar [note: 'The full registered legal name of the Sports Entertainment subsidiary or operating company as filed with the relevant government authority (e.g., \'Sports Entertainment Broadcast Network LLC\', \'League Operating Company Inc.\'). Used in SEC filings, SOX attestations, and intercompany agreements.']
  minority_interest_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of the legal entity\'s equity not owned by the Sports Entertainment parent, representing non-controlling interest. Used in SEC consolidated financial statements for minority interest disclosure and EBITDA attribution. Range 0.00 to 100.00.']
  parent_company_code varchar [note: 'The SAP S/4HANA company code of the immediate parent entity in the legal entity hierarchy. Enables hierarchical traversal of the Sports Entertainment corporate structure for intercompany settlement, consolidation, and segment reporting. Null for the ultimate parent holding company.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code of the registered office address. Used for tax jurisdiction determination, regulatory filings, and ADA venue accessibility compliance mapping.']
  posting_period_variant varchar [note: 'SAP S/4HANA posting period variant assigned to this company code, controlling which fiscal periods are open or closed for financial postings. Enforces period-end close discipline and SOX-compliant financial controls by preventing backdated entries.']
  profit_center_standard_hierarchy varchar [note: 'Name of the SAP S/4HANA profit center standard hierarchy node to which this company code\'s profit centers are assigned. Enables segment-level EBITDA reporting and management accounting roll-ups across league, broadcast, venue, and merchandise business units.']
  region_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-2 state, province, or region code of the registered office (e.g., \'NY\', \'CA\', \'TX\'). Used for state tax compliance, CCPA applicability determination, and regulatory jurisdiction mapping.']
  sec_reporting_entity boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this company code is a registrant or consolidated subsidiary required to be included in SEC financial filings (10-K, 10-Q, 8-K). Drives inclusion in the consolidation perimeter for group financial reporting.']
  short_name varchar [note: 'Abbreviated or trading name of the legal entity used in internal reporting, dashboards, and ERP display fields. Corresponds to SAP S/4HANA company code short text (KTEXT). Distinct from the full legal name.']
