  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record from which this company code master record was sourced. Primarily SAP S/4HANA FI/CO for financial master data. Supports data lineage, reconciliation, and Silver layer provenance tracking in the Databricks lakehouse.']
  sox_relevant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this company code is within scope for Sarbanes-Oxley (SOX) Section 302 and 404 internal control assessments. True for all entities included in the consolidated SEC financial statements of the publicly traded Sports Entertainment parent.']
  street_address varchar [note: 'Street address of the registered office or principal place of business for the legal entity. Used in SEC filings, regulatory correspondence, and legal notices. Classified as confidential organizational contact data.']
  tax_registration_number varchar [note: 'Government-issued tax identification number for the legal entity (e.g., US Employer Identification Number (EIN), EU VAT registration number). Required for tax filings, vendor invoicing, and regulatory reporting. Stored as confidential business data.']
  vat_registration_number varchar [note: 'Value Added Tax (VAT) registration number for entities operating in VAT-applicable jurisdictions (EU, UK, etc.). Used on broadcast rights invoices, sponsorship agreements, and merchandise licensing contracts. Distinct from the domestic tax registration number.']

  Note: 'Legal entity master record within the SAP S/4HANA organizational structure. Represents each incorporated Sports Entertainment subsidiary or operating company (e.g., league operating company, broadcast network entity, venue management LLC). Stores company code, legal name, country, currency, fiscal year variant, and chart of accounts assignment. Required for SOX-compliant intercompany and SEC reporting.'
}

Table finance.journal_entry {
  journal_entry_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each journal entry record in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Primary key for this data product. Role: TRANSACTION_HEADER.']
  company_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.company_code. Business justification: Journal entries in SAP FI are posted within a specific company code (legal entity). The company_code string field is a denormalized reference that should be replaced with a typed FK to company_code.co']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the SAP Controlling (CO) cost center to which this journal entry is allocated. Enables cost allocation reporting by business unit (e.g., Venue Operations, Broadcast, Athlete Management). Corresponds to BSEG.KOSTL.']
  fiscal_period_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.fiscal_period. Business justification: Journal entries are posted to specific fiscal periods. The fiscal_year and fiscal_period INT columns are denormalized attributes that should be retrieved via JOIN to fiscal_period master table. This n']
  fixed_asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.fixed_asset. Business justification: In SAP FI-AA, asset-related journal entries (depreciation postings, acquisition postings, disposal postings, impairment charges) are linked to specific fixed asset records. journal_entry does not curr']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the primary GL account associated with this journal entry header. For multi-line entries, this represents the controlling or summary account. Detailed line-item GL accounts are embedded within the line item attributes of this product. Links to the chart of accounts master data.']
  ledger_id varchar [note: 'Two-character identifier of the SAP New GL ledger to which this entry is posted (e.g., \'0L\' = leading ledger GAAP, \'L1\' = IFRS parallel ledger, \'L2\' = local GAAP ledger). Supports parallel accounting for multi-GAAP reporting requirements.']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the SAP Controlling profit center representing the business segment responsible for this posting (e.g., NFL Division, Concert Touring, OTT Streaming). Enables EBITDA reporting by profit center and segment reporting per IFRS 8. Corresponds to BSEG.PRCTR.']
  controlling_area varchar [note: 'Four-character SAP Controlling (CO) area code grouping company codes for management accounting and internal reporting. Defines the scope for cost center, profit center, and internal order reporting within Sports Entertainment. Corresponds to BKPF.KOKRS.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code of the transaction currency in which this journal entry was originally recorded (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Part of the MONETARY_TRIPLET. Corresponds to BKPF.WAERS.']
  document_date date [note: 'The date of the originating source document (e.g., invoice date, payment date, contract date) that triggered this journal entry. May differ from posting date when documents are processed with a lag. Corresponds to BKPF.BLDAT.']
  document_number varchar [note: 'SAP-assigned ten-digit accounting document number uniquely identifying the journal entry within a company code and fiscal year. This is the externally-known business identifier used in audit trails, reconciliations, and cross-system references. Corresponds to BKPF.BELNR.']
  document_type varchar [note: 'Two-character SAP document type code classifying the nature of the journal entry (e.g., SA=GL posting, KR=vendor invoice, DZ=customer payment, AB=accrual, IC=intercompany, AA=asset posting, ZP=payment run). Drives number range assignment and account type restrictions. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: SA|KR|DZ|AB|IC|AA|ZP|RE|KZ|DR — promote to reference product]']
  entry_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp recording when this journal entry record was first created in SAP FI. Serves as the RECORD_AUDIT_CREATED field for audit trail and SOX compliance. Corresponds to BKPF.CPUDT + BKPF.CPUTM.']
  exchange_rate decimal(12,6) [note: 'Foreign currency exchange rate applied to translate the transaction currency amount to local currency at the time of posting. Sourced from SAP exchange rate tables. Corresponds to BKPF.KURSF.']
  functional_area varchar [note: 'SAP functional area code classifying the business function of this posting for cost-of-sales accounting (e.g., SALES, MKTG, ADMIN, PROD, R&D). Supports income statement presentation by function of expense per IFRS IAS 1. Corresponds to BSEG.FKBER.']
  group_currency_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total amount of the journal entry translated into the group (consolidation) currency (typically USD for Sports Entertainment). Used for consolidated EBITDA reporting, SEC filings, and group-level financial analysis. Corresponds to the aggregate of BSEG.KZBTR.']
  header_text varchar [note: 'Free-text description at the journal entry header level providing business context for the posting (e.g., \'Q3 Ticket Revenue Accrual — NFL Season\', \'Broadcast Rights Amortization — RSN Deal\', \'Intercompany Settlement — Venue Ops\'). Corresponds to BKPF.BKTXT.']
  intercompany_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this journal entry represents an intercompany transaction between two legal entities within the Sports Entertainment group (True) or an external third-party transaction (False). Critical for consolidation elimination and intercompany reconciliation.']
  internal_order_number varchar [note: 'SAP CO internal order number used to track costs and revenues for specific projects or events (e.g., a specific concert tour, stadium renovation project, or broadcast production). Enables project-level P&L and budget vs. actual analysis. Corresponds to BSEG.AUFNR.']
  last_changed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp of the most recent modification to this journal entry record. Serves as the RECORD_AUDIT_UPDATED field for change tracking and SOX audit trail. Corresponds to BKPF.AEDAT.']
  line_item_count varchar [note: 'Total number of accounting line items (debit and credit rows) embedded within this journal entry document. A balanced double-entry posting requires a minimum of two line items. Supports validation of entry completeness and complexity classification.']
  local_currency_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total amount of the journal entry translated into the company code local (functional) currency. Used for statutory financial reporting, balance sheet, and P&L in local GAAP. Part of the MONETARY_TRIPLET. Corresponds to the aggregate of BSEG.DMBTR.']
  local_currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter code for the company code local currency (functional currency) used for statutory reporting. Distinct from transaction currency when cross-currency postings occur. Corresponds to BKPF.HWAER.']
  posting_date date [note: 'The date on which the journal entry is posted to the general ledger and affects account balances. Determines the fiscal period assignment. Distinct from document date and entry date. Corresponds to BKPF.BUDAT. This is the principal business event date (BUSINESS_EVENT_TIMESTAMP category).']
  posting_key varchar [note: 'SAP two-digit posting key controlling the debit/credit indicator and account type for the primary line item of this journal entry (e.g., \'40\'=GL debit, \'50\'=GL credit, \'01\'=customer debit, \'31\'=vendor credit). Determines how the entry affects account balances. Corresponds to BSEG.BSCHL.']
  posting_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the journal entry within the SAP FI workflow. \'posted\' = fully committed to GL; \'parked\' = saved but not yet posted pending approval; \'held\' = incomplete draft; \'reversed\' = offset by a reversal document; \'simulated\' = test posting not committed; \'error\' = failed validation. Serves as the LIFECYCLE_STATUS field.']
  reference_document_number varchar [note: 'External reference number linking this journal entry to the originating source document (e.g., vendor invoice number, ticket sales batch ID, broadcast rights contract number, sponsorship deal reference). Enables cross-system traceability. Corresponds to BKPF.XBLNR.']
  revenue_category varchar [note: 'Business classification of the revenue stream associated with this journal entry, aligned to Sports Entertainment core revenue lines. Enables EBITDA reporting by revenue category and supports ARR tracking for recurring streams. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: ticket_sales|broadcast_rights|sponsorship|merchandise|licensing|ppv|ott_streaming|venue_operations|nil_rights|other — promote to reference product]']
  reversal_document_number varchar [note: 'Document number of the corresponding reversal entry if this journal entry has been reversed, or the original document number if this entry IS the reversal. Null if no reversal relationship exists. Enables audit trail for correction postings. Corresponds to BKPF.STBLG.']
  reversal_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this journal entry is a reversal document (True) or an original posting (False). Reversal entries offset prior postings for corrections, accrual reversals, or error remediation. Corresponds to BKPF.STBLG presence.']
  reversal_reason_code varchar [note: 'SAP two-digit reason code explaining why this journal entry was reversed (e.g., \'01\'=incorrect posting, \'02\'=accrual reversal, \'03\'=period-end correction). Null if not reversed. Corresponds to BKPF.STGRD.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifier of the originating operational system that generated this journal entry (e.g., SAP_FI for direct GL postings, TICKETMASTER for ticket revenue interfaces, SHOPIFY for merchandise e-commerce, WORKDAY for payroll postings, MANUAL for manual journal entries). Supports data lineage and reconciliation.']
  sox_approval_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the SOX-mandated approval workflow for this journal entry. \'pending\' = awaiting approver action; \'approved\' = authorized by designated control owner; \'rejected\' = returned for correction; \'escalated\' = routed to senior approver; \'exempt\' = system-generated entry below materiality threshold. Required for SOX Section 404 internal controls over financial reporting.']
  sox_approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the SOX approval was granted for this journal entry. Null if pending or exempt. Provides immutable audit evidence for SOX Section 404 compliance and external auditor review.']
  tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Tax amount (VAT, sales tax, or withholding tax) included in or associated with this journal entry in the transaction currency. Part of the MONETARY_TRIPLET adjustment component. Null for non-taxable postings. Corresponds to BSEG.HWSTE.']
  tax_code varchar [note: 'SAP two-character tax code applied to this journal entry for VAT, sales tax, or withholding tax calculation (e.g., \'I1\'=input tax, \'O1\'=output tax, \'W1\'=withholding). Null for non-taxable postings. Corresponds to BSEG.MWSKZ.']
  trading_partner_code varchar [note: 'Four-character company code of the intercompany trading partner entity when this is an intercompany journal entry. Used for automated intercompany elimination during group consolidation. Null for external transactions. Corresponds to BKPF.VBUND.']
  transaction_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Gross total amount of the journal entry in the transaction currency. Represents the sum of all debit line items (which must equal the sum of all credit line items for a balanced entry). Part of the MONETARY_TRIPLET. Corresponds to the aggregate of BSEG.WRBTR across line items.']

  Note: 'Core financial posting document in SAP FI representing a balanced double-entry accounting transaction with embedded line items (posting rows). Each journal entry contains one or more line items specifying GL account, amount, debit/credit indicator, cost center, profit center, and tax code. Captures document type (invoice, payment, accrual, intercompany, allocation), posting date, fiscal period, company code, reference document, posting user, reversal indicator, and SOX approval workflow status. SSOT for all financial movements across the general ledger including revenue recognition postings for ticket sales, broadcast rights, sponsorship, merchandise, and cost allocation postings. Line-item granularity is embedded within this product — no separate line product exists.'
}

Table finance.budget {
  budget_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the budget planning record in the Databricks Silver Layer. Primary key for the finance.budget data product.']
  company_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.company_code. Business justification: Budgets are scoped to a specific legal entity (company code) in SAP S/4HANA. The company_code string field is a denormalized reference that should be replaced with a typed FK to company_code.company_c']
  contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to athlete.athlete_contract. Business justification: Team salary budgets are planned against specific athlete contracts. Budget records for player payroll must reference the athlete contract driving the budget line, enabling variance analysis between bu']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Budgets in SAP CO are allocated to specific cost centers. The existing cost_center_code string field is a denormalized reference that should be replaced with a proper FK to cost_center.cost_center_id,']
  fiscal_period_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.fiscal_period. Business justification: Budgets are allocated and tracked by fiscal period. The fiscal_year and fiscal_period columns are denormalized temporal attributes that should be normalized via FK to fiscal_period master.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Budget line items are posted against specific GL accounts in the chart of accounts. The gl_account_code string field is a denormalized reference that should be replaced with a typed FK to gl_account.g']
  ledger_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.ledger. Business justification: In SAP S/4HANA, budgets are maintained against specific ledgers (leading ledger, extension ledger for parallel accounting under IFRS/GAAP). The budget table has no ledger_id FK despite the ledger bein']
  media_rights_deal_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to broadcast.media_rights_deal. Business justification: Rights fee payments are planned in finance budgets with budget lines referencing specific deals for rights fee expenditure planning and budget vs. actuals variance analysis. Finance budget controllers']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Budgets are also allocated at the profit center level in SAP CO. The profit_center_code string field is a denormalized reference that should be replaced with a typed FK to profit_center.profit_center_']
  actuals_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Actual expenditure or revenue posted to the GL account and cost center year-to-date as of the last data load from SAP FI/CO. Used for budget vs. actuals variance analysis and EBITDA reporting.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date on which the budget record received final approval in the workflow. Null if not yet approved. Critical for SOX-compliant financial authorization audit trails.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current workflow approval status of the budget record: draft (in preparation), submitted (pending review), under_review (in approval chain), approved (authorized), rejected (returned for revision), locked (closed for posting). Supports SOX-compliant budget authorization controls.']
  budget_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable descriptive name for the budget record (e.g., \'FY2025 Event Operations OPEX Base Budget\'). Used in FP&A reporting and investor relations presentations.']
  budget_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric identifier for the budget record as assigned in Workday Adaptive Planning or SAP CO. Used for cross-system reconciliation and audit traceability.']
  budget_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the budget by expenditure or revenue category: OPEX (Operating Expenditure), CAPEX (Capital Expenditure), headcount (workforce cost plan), or revenue (income plan). Drives chart of accounts mapping in SAP CO.']
  business_unit varchar [note: 'Business unit or division to which this budget is attributed (e.g., \'League Operations\', \'Broadcast Network\', \'Venue Management\', \'Fan Engagement\'). Supports segment-level EBITDA reporting and investor relations guidance.']
  committed_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Amount of the budget that has been legally committed via purchase orders, contracts, or reservations in SAP MM/SD but not yet invoiced. Represents encumbered funds for budget availability control.']
  cost_element_code varchar [note: 'SAP CO cost element code that classifies the nature of the planned expenditure (e.g., salaries, venue hire, broadcast rights fees). Enables cost element-level variance analysis.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this budget record was first created in the source system (Workday Adaptive Planning or SAP CO). Supports data lineage and SOX audit trail requirements.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary amounts in this budget record (e.g., \'USD\', \'EUR\', \'GBP\'). Sports Entertainment operates across global markets requiring multi-currency budget management.']
  effective_from_date date [note: 'The start date from which this budget record is effective and valid for financial planning and posting. Aligns with the fiscal period start date.']
  effective_to_date date [note: 'The end date through which this budget record is effective. Aligns with the fiscal period end date. Used to manage multi-year plan validity windows.']
  exchange_rate decimal(18,6) [note: 'Foreign currency exchange rate applied to convert the budget amount to the group reporting currency (USD). Sourced from SAP FI currency tables at the time of budget load. Relevant for international operations (FIFA, UEFA markets).']
  forecast_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Latest rolling forecast amount for the fiscal period as updated in Workday Adaptive Planning. Reflects the most current expectation of financial outcome, incorporating actuals-to-date and revised forward projections.']
  functional_area varchar [note: 'SAP FI functional area classification for the budget record (e.g., \'Event Operations\', \'Broadcast\', \'Athlete Management\', \'Venue Operations\'). Supports functional P&L and SEC segment reporting.']
  is_intercompany boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this budget record relates to an intercompany transaction between legal entities within the Sports Entertainment group. True = intercompany; False = third-party. Required for intercompany elimination in consolidated financial reporting.']
  lock_date date [note: 'Date on which the budget record was locked in SAP CO, preventing further manual adjustments. Ensures integrity of the approved baseline for variance reporting.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text commentary entered by the budget owner or FP&A analyst explaining assumptions, drivers, or variances for this budget record (e.g., \'Includes incremental broadcast rights cost for new RSN deal\'). Supports management reporting narratives.']
  period_granularity varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the budget record is expressed at monthly, quarterly, or annual granularity. Determines how fiscal_period is interpreted in FP&A reporting.']
  plan_type varchar [note: 'Distinguishes the planning horizon: annual_budget (single fiscal year), multi_year_plan (3–5 year strategic plan), or rolling_forecast (continuous 12–18 month forward view). Supports FP&A and investor relations cycles.']
  planned_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The original planned budget amount for the fiscal period in the transaction currency. Represents the baseline financial commitment approved at the start of the planning cycle. Core field for FP&A variance analysis.']
  prior_year_actual_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Actual amount posted for the equivalent fiscal period in the prior fiscal year. Used as a benchmark for year-over-year variance analysis and budget calibration in FP&A.']
  reporting_currency_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Planned amount translated to the group reporting currency (USD) using the exchange_rate. Enables consolidated EBITDA reporting and SEC financial disclosures across multi-currency global operations.']
  revised_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The most recently approved revised budget amount for the fiscal period, reflecting mid-year budget amendments or reforecasts. Null if no revision has been approved.']
  scenario_type varchar [note: 'Financial planning scenario classification: base (most likely), upside (optimistic), downside (pessimistic), or stress (extreme adverse). Used for EBITDA projections and investor relations guidance.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the originating system of record for this budget record: Workday Adaptive Planning (primary FP&A tool), SAP CO (controlling module), or manual (spreadsheet-based override). Supports data lineage in the lakehouse.']
  submission_date date [note: 'Date on which the budget record was formally submitted for approval in Workday Adaptive Planning. Supports FP&A calendar management and SOX audit trail.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this budget record in the source system. Used for incremental data loads into the Databricks Silver Layer and change tracking.']
  variance_explanation varchar [note: 'Structured narrative provided by the budget owner explaining material variances between planned and actual amounts. Required for FP&A monthly close reporting and investor relations commentary.']
  version varchar [note: 'Version label of the budget or forecast cycle (e.g., \'Original\', \'Revised\', \'Q1 Reforecast\', \'Q2 Reforecast\', \'Q3 Reforecast\', \'Q4 Reforecast\'). Tracks the evolution of financial plans across the fiscal year. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: original|revised|q1_reforecast|q2_reforecast|q3_reforecast|q4_reforecast — promote to reference product]']

  Note: 'Financial planning record encompassing annual budgets, multi-year plans, and rolling forecasts per cost center, profit center, or WBS element. Managed in Workday Adaptive Planning and loaded into SAP CO. Captures budget/forecast version (original, revised, Q1-Q4 reforecast), scenario type (base, upside, downside), budget type (OPEX, CAPEX), fiscal year/period, planned amounts by GL account and cost element at monthly/quarterly granularity, committed amounts, actuals-to-date, and approval workflow status. Supports FP&A variance analysis, rolling forecast cycles, EBITDA projections, and investor relations guidance for Sports Entertainment.'
}

Table finance.ar_invoice {
  ar_invoice_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the accounts receivable invoice record in the Databricks Silver Layer. Primary key for this entity.']
  company_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.company_code. Business justification: AR invoices are issued by a specific legal entity (company code) in SAP FI. The company_code string field is a denormalized reference that should be replaced with a typed FK to company_code.company_co']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the SAP CO cost center (KOSTL) responsible for the revenue-generating activity billed on this invoice. Supports expense allocation, profitability analysis (CO-PA), and FP&A reporting.']
  customer_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer master record (debtor/payer) in the AR sub-ledger. Corresponds to the SAP FI-AR customer (KUNNR) account to whom the invoice is issued — sponsors, broadcast rights licensees, merchandise wholesale buyers, or premium suite holders.']
  deal_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sponsorship.deal. Business justification: AR invoices for sponsorship billing must reference the originating deal for revenue tracking, dispute resolution, and collections. Sports finance teams run deal-level AR aging reports and reconcile in']
  fiscal_period_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.fiscal_period. Business justification: AR invoices are posted to fiscal periods for revenue recognition and financial reporting. Normalizes fiscal_year and fiscal_period INT columns.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the GL account (HKONT) in the chart of accounts to which this invoice\'s revenue is posted. Enables drill-through from AR sub-ledger to the general ledger for SOX-compliant financial controls and SEC reporting.']
  group_sale_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ticketing.group_sale. Business justification: Group sales generate AR invoices for deposits and final payments. Linking ar_invoice to group_sale enables group sales billing reconciliation, payment tracking against negotiated terms, and collection']
  journal_entry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.journal_entry. Business justification: In SAP FI, every AR invoice posting generates a corresponding journal entry (FI document). ar_invoice has reference_document_number (STRING) but no structured FK to finance.journal_entry. Adding journ']
  media_rights_deal_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to broadcast.media_rights_deal. Business justification: Media rights deals generate AR invoices for licensee rights fee payments. Finance tracks receivables per deal for cash collections, dunning, and revenue reconciliation. The existing contract_id on ar_']
  membership_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fan.membership. Business justification: Membership fee invoicing (season tickets, VIP, corporate) is a core AR process. Finance must link AR invoices to the membership record for revenue recognition, dunning management, billing dispute reso']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the SAP CO profit center (PRCTR) for segment-level P&L attribution. Enables EBITDA reporting by business unit (e.g., league operations, broadcast, venue, merchandise).']
  season_ticket_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ticketing.season_ticket_account. Business justification: AR invoices are generated for season ticket account billing (installment payment schedules, renewal invoices). Linking ar_invoice to season_ticket_account enables STH billing reconciliation, dunning m']
  ticket_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ticketing.ticket_order. Business justification: AR invoices are generated for ticket orders in B2B/corporate ticketing, group sales invoicing, and season ticket billing. Linking ar_invoice to ticket_order enables A/R collections teams to trace invo']
  amount_paid decimal(18,2) [note: 'Cumulative amount received from the customer against this invoice to date. Enables partial payment tracking and outstanding balance calculation (net_amount - amount_paid). Updated upon each payment clearing in SAP FI-AR.']
  billing_date date [note: 'The date on which the invoice was issued and sent to the customer. Represents the principal business event date for revenue recognition purposes. Maps to SAP FKDAT (billing date on SD billing document) or BLDAT (document date on FI posting).']
  clearing_document_number varchar [note: 'SAP FI clearing document number (AUGBL) assigned when the invoice is fully or partially cleared by a payment, credit memo, or write-off. Null if the invoice remains open. Provides the AR-to-payment audit trail.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this AR invoice record was first created in the source system (SAP FI-AR). Provides the audit trail creation point for SOX compliance and data lineage in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the invoice document currency (WAERS in SAP). Sports Entertainment operates across global markets; invoices may be denominated in USD, EUR, GBP, AUD, etc.']
  deferred_revenue_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Portion of the gross_amount that has been billed but not yet recognized as revenue because the associated performance obligation (e.g., season sponsorship activation, multi-year broadcast rights) has not been fully satisfied. Feeds the deferred revenue liability on the balance sheet.']
  discount_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Early-payment or contractual discount amount applied to the invoice per the payment terms (SKTO1/SKTO2 in SAP). Relevant for sponsor and broadcast rights deals with negotiated discount schedules.']
  dispute_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code identifying the reason a customer has disputed this invoice (e.g., pricing discrepancy, delivery shortfall, contract breach). Populated when payment_status = \'disputed\'. Supports AR dispute management workflow in SAP FI-AR Collections Management.']
  due_date date [note: 'The date by which payment must be received from the customer per the agreed payment terms. Drives dunning runs, cash flow forecasting, and DSO (Days Sales Outstanding) calculations in SAP FI-AR.']
  dunning_block boolean [note: 'Indicates whether dunning is blocked for this invoice (MANSP in SAP). True = dunning is suppressed (e.g., invoice under dispute or special arrangement). False = dunning proceeds normally.']
  dunning_date date [note: 'Date on which the most recent dunning notice was issued to the customer for this invoice (MADAT in SAP). Used to schedule the next dunning run and track collections escalation timeline.']
  dunning_level varchar [note: 'Current dunning level (MAHNS in SAP, 0–4) indicating how many dunning notices have been sent to the customer for this overdue invoice. Level 0 = no dunning; Level 4 = final notice / legal escalation. Drives collections workflow.']
  exchange_rate decimal(12,6) [note: 'Foreign currency exchange rate (KURSF in SAP) applied at the time of invoice posting to convert the document currency to the company code local currency. Required for multi-currency consolidation and SEC financial reporting.']
  gross_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total invoice amount before tax deductions. Represents the full billed value of goods or services rendered (e.g., sponsorship activation fee, broadcast rights license fee). Maps to SAP WRBTR (amount in document currency) on the AR line item.']
  intercompany_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this invoice is an intercompany transaction between two Sports Entertainment legal entities (True) or an external customer invoice (False). Intercompany invoices require elimination in consolidated financial statements per SOX and SEC reporting.']
  invoice_number varchar [note: 'Externally-visible, human-readable invoice document number assigned by SAP FI-AR (e.g., AR-202400012345). Used on billing documents sent to sponsors, broadcast licensees, merchandise wholesale buyers, and premium suite holders. Maps to SAP BELNR field on BKPF/VBRK.']
  invoice_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the invoice document type. \'standard\' = regular customer invoice; \'credit_memo\' = reduction of a prior invoice; \'debit_memo\' = additional charge; \'pro_forma\' = non-posting preview invoice; \'intercompany\' = settlement between Sports Entertainment legal entities.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this AR invoice record in the source system. Tracks changes to payment status, dunning level, dispute flags, and amount adjustments. Required for incremental data pipeline processing and SOX audit trail.']
  local_currency_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Net invoice amount converted to the company code\'s local (functional) currency using the exchange_rate at posting date. Used for consolidated financial reporting and EBITDA calculations in the reporting currency.']
  net_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Net payable amount after tax (gross_amount + tax_amount). This is the total amount the customer owes. Used for cash flow forecasting, DSO calculation, and revenue recognition reporting.']
  payment_block boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the invoice is blocked from being cleared by an incoming payment (ZLSPR in SAP). True = payment blocked pending resolution (e.g., dispute, credit check). False = payment can be applied.']
  payment_date date [note: 'Date on which the invoice was fully cleared/paid in SAP FI-AR (AUGDT). Null if not yet paid. Used for DSO calculation, cash flow actuals, and AR aging analysis.']
  payment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the invoice from an AR collections perspective. \'open\' = unpaid and within terms; \'partially_paid\' = partial payment received; \'paid\' = fully settled; \'overdue\' = past due_date; \'disputed\' = customer raised a dispute; \'cancelled\' = invoice voided; \'written_off\' = deemed uncollectable. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: open|partially_paid|paid|overdue|disputed|cancelled|written_off — promote to reference product]']
  payment_terms_code varchar [note: 'SAP payment terms key (ZTERM) defining the discount and net payment schedule (e.g., NT30 = net 30 days, 2/10NT30 = 2% discount if paid within 10 days). Determines due_date calculation and early-payment discount eligibility.']
  posting_date date [note: 'The date on which the invoice was posted to the general ledger (SAP BUDAT). May differ from billing_date when invoices are back-dated or posted in a subsequent period. Critical for period-close accuracy and SOX controls.']
  reference_document_number varchar [note: 'External reference number provided by the customer (e.g., purchase order number, contract reference, deal ID from Salesforce CRM). Maps to SAP XBLNR field. Enables customer-side reconciliation and dispute resolution.']
  revenue_category varchar [note: 'Business classification of the revenue stream being billed. Aligns with Sports Entertainment\'s primary revenue lines: sponsorship fees, broadcast rights licensing, merchandise wholesale, premium suite/hospitality, ticketing, and IP licensing. Supports EBITDA reporting by revenue segment. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: sponsorship|broadcast_rights|merchandise_wholesale|premium_suite|ticketing|licensing|other — promote to reference product]']
  revenue_recognition_status varchar [note: 'Indicates the ASC 606 / IFRS 15 revenue recognition status of this invoice. \'pending\' = performance obligation not yet satisfied; \'recognized\' = revenue fully recognized; \'deferred\' = billed in advance of performance; \'partially_recognized\' = multi-element arrangement partially satisfied.']
  sap_document_type varchar [note: 'SAP FI document type code (BLART) classifying the accounting document (e.g., \'RV\' = billing document transfer, \'DR\' = customer invoice, \'DG\' = customer credit memo). Preserves source system classification for reconciliation and audit.']
  tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total tax charged on the invoice (e.g., VAT, GST, sales tax). Calculated by SAP FI tax determination based on the customer\'s tax jurisdiction and the revenue category. Maps to SAP WMWST.']
  write_off_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Amount of the invoice that has been written off as uncollectable bad debt. Populated when payment_status = \'written_off\'. Feeds the bad debt expense account and allowance for doubtful accounts on the balance sheet.']

  Note: 'Accounts receivable invoice issued to external customers — sponsors, broadcast rights licensees, merchandise wholesale buyers, and premium suite holders. Stores invoice number, billing date, due date, customer account, payment terms, gross amount, tax amount, net amount, currency, payment status, and dunning level. Managed in SAP FI-AR. SSOT for revenue billing documents.'
}

Table finance.ap_invoice {
  ap_invoice_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the accounts payable invoice record in the Databricks Silver Layer. Primary key generated by the lakehouse ingestion pipeline from SAP FI-AP.']
  agent_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to athlete.agent. Business justification: AP invoices are issued to agents for commission payments on athlete contracts. Finance teams need agent reference on AP invoices for commission payment processing, 1099 tax reporting, and agent paymen']
  company_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.company_code. Business justification: AP invoices are posted within a specific legal entity (company code) in SAP FI. The company_code string field is a denormalized reference that should be replaced with a typed FK to company_code.compan']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the SAP Controlling cost center to which the invoice expense is allocated. Drives EBITDA reporting and FP&A cost attribution across business units (e.g., venue operations, broadcast, athlete management).']
  fiscal_period_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.fiscal_period. Business justification: AP invoices are posted to fiscal periods for financial reporting. Normalizes fiscal_year and fiscal_period INT columns by linking to fiscal_period master table.']
  fixed_asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.fixed_asset. Business justification: AP invoices for capital expenditures (CAPEX) are directly linked to fixed asset acquisitions in SAP FI-AA. When a vendor invoice is for a stadium renovation, broadcast equipment purchase, or other cap']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the general ledger account in the chart of accounts to which this invoice is posted. Determines financial statement line classification for SEC reporting.']
  journal_entry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.journal_entry. Business justification: In SAP FI, every AP invoice posting generates a corresponding journal entry (FI document). ap_invoice has sap_document_number (STRING) which references the SAP document but there is no structured FK t']
  nil_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to athlete.nil_agreement. Business justification: AP invoices are generated for NIL agreement payments to athletes or their agents. The ap_invoice must reference the nil_agreement it settles for payment reconciliation, royalty tracking, and NIL compl']
  officiating_assignment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to event.officiating_assignment. Business justification: Officiating assignments carry fee obligations (officiating_fee_amount, expense_allowance_amount). AP invoices for official payments must reference the specific assignment for three-way match validatio']
  participant_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to event.participant. Business justification: Event participants (athletes, performers) with appearance fees (participant.appearance_fee_amount) generate AP invoices for payment. Finance must link AP invoices to the specific participant engagemen']
  payment_run_id varchar [note: 'SAP automatic payment program run identifier (F110) that processed this invoice for payment. Links the invoice to the payment batch for reconciliation and audit.']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: ap_invoice has profit_center_code (STRING) but no FK to finance.profit_center. In SAP FI/CO, every AP invoice is assigned to a profit center for P&L reporting. Adding profit_center_id normalizes this ']
  security_incident_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to security.security_incident. Business justification: AP invoices for security incident costs (medical services, law enforcement overtime, property repair, legal fees, third-party security contractors) must be linked to the triggering incident for insura']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the invoice in the AP approval workflow. Tracks whether the invoice has been reviewed and authorized by the appropriate cost center owner or finance controller per SOX segregation of duties requirements.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the invoice was approved for payment in the AP workflow. Supports SLA monitoring for invoice processing cycle time and SOX audit trail.']
  baseline_date date [note: 'The reference date from which payment terms are calculated in SAP FI-AP. May differ from invoice date or receipt date depending on payment terms configuration.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the AP invoice record was first created in SAP FI-AP. Serves as the audit trail anchor for SOX compliance and data lineage in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code of the invoice. Sports Entertainment operates globally (USD, EUR, GBP, AUD, CAD, JPY, etc.) requiring multi-currency AP processing.']
  discount_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The cash discount amount available if payment is made within the early payment discount period as defined in the payment terms. Used for working capital optimization and DPO management.']
  due_date date [note: 'The calculated date by which payment must be made to the vendor based on payment terms. Used for cash flow forecasting, DPO (Days Payable Outstanding) reporting, and payment run scheduling.']
  exchange_rate decimal(12,6) [note: 'The exchange rate applied to convert the invoice currency to the company code local currency at the time of posting. Sourced from SAP FI exchange rate tables.']
  expense_category varchar [note: 'Financial classification of the invoice as capital expenditure (CAPEX), operating expenditure (OPEX), prepaid expense, accrual, or intercompany charge. Drives balance sheet vs. P&L treatment and EBITDA impact.']
  goods_receipt_number varchar [note: 'The SAP material document number for the goods receipt or service entry sheet that confirms delivery of goods/services from the vendor. Required for three-way match and accrual reversal.']
  gross_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total gross amount of the invoice before deduction of discounts or credits, as stated on the vendor document. Expressed in the invoice currency.']
  internal_order_number varchar [note: 'SAP CO internal order number for project-based or event-specific cost tracking (e.g., a specific concert, championship event, or venue renovation). Enables event-level P&L and ROI analysis.']
  invoice_date date [note: 'The date printed on the vendor\'s invoice document. Used as the baseline for payment terms calculation and aging analysis.']
  invoice_status varchar [note: 'Current workflow status of the invoice in the AP processing lifecycle. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: RECEIVED|UNDER_REVIEW|APPROVED|POSTED|SCHEDULED|PAID|BLOCKED|CANCELLED — promote to reference product]']
  invoice_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the invoice document type. STANDARD: regular vendor invoice; CREDIT_MEMO: vendor credit note; DEBIT_MEMO: debit adjustment; RECURRING: periodic service invoice; PREPAYMENT: advance payment request; INTERCOMPANY: intra-group settlement.']
  is_recurring boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this invoice is part of a recurring payment schedule (e.g., monthly venue lease, broadcast rights licensing fees, HVAC maintenance contracts). Supports automated accrual and cash flow forecasting.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the AP invoice record in SAP FI-AP or the Silver Layer. Required for incremental data pipeline processing and SOX change audit trail.']
  local_currency_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The net invoice amount translated to the company code\'s local (functional) currency using the exchange rate at posting date. Required for consolidated financial reporting and EBITDA calculation.']
  net_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The net payable amount after deducting applicable discounts and credits from the gross amount. This is the amount that will be disbursed to the vendor upon payment.']
  payment_block_reason varchar [note: 'Reason code explaining why the invoice payment has been blocked in SAP FI-AP. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: MANUAL_HOLD|PRICE_VARIANCE|QUANTITY_VARIANCE|DUPLICATE|MISSING_PO|PENDING_APPROVAL|DISPUTE|NONE — promote to reference product]']
  payment_date date [note: 'The actual date on which payment was disbursed to the vendor. Used for DPO (Days Payable Outstanding) calculation, cash flow reporting, and vendor relationship management.']
  payment_document_number varchar [note: 'SAP FI document number of the outgoing payment that cleared this invoice. Used for bank reconciliation, audit trail, and dispute resolution with vendors.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'The method by which the invoice will be settled. ACH: Automated Clearing House; WIRE: international wire transfer; CHECK: paper check; CARD: corporate purchasing card; EFT: electronic funds transfer; INTERCOMPANY: internal settlement.']
  payment_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the invoice has been fully paid, partially paid, cleared, or voided in SAP FI-AP. Distinct from invoice_status which tracks the approval workflow.']
  payment_terms_code varchar [note: 'SAP payment terms key (e.g., NT30, 2/10NET30) defining the payment due date calculation, early payment discount percentage, and discount period. Drives cash flow forecasting and working capital management.']
  posting_date date [note: 'The date the invoice was posted to the general ledger in SAP FI. Determines the accounting period for revenue recognition and expense allocation.']
  receipt_date date [note: 'The date the invoice was physically or electronically received by the accounts payable department. Determines the start of the payment terms clock and early payment discount eligibility.']
  sap_document_number varchar [note: 'The 10-digit SAP FI document number assigned upon posting of the invoice in SAP S/4HANA. Serves as the system-of-record identifier for audit trail and journal entry cross-reference.']
  tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total tax amount (VAT, GST, sales tax) included in the invoice. Supports tax compliance reporting and reclaim processes across jurisdictions.']
  three_way_match_status varchar [note: 'Result of the three-way match validation between the Purchase Order (PO), Goods Receipt (GR), and Invoice Receipt (IR) in SAP MM-IV. MATCHED: all three documents align within tolerance; PARTIAL_MATCH: partial quantity or price variance; UNMATCHED: significant discrepancy; NOT_APPLICABLE: non-PO invoice.']
  vendor_category varchar [note: 'Business classification of the vendor type for spend analytics and category management. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: VENUE_SERVICES|BROADCAST_EQUIPMENT|ATHLETE_AGENT|MERCHANDISE_MFG|MEDIA_RIGHTS|CONTENT_PRODUCTION|FACILITY_MGMT|TECHNOLOGY|PROFESSIONAL_SERVICES|OTHER — promote to reference product]']
  vendor_invoice_number varchar [note: 'The invoice number as printed on the vendor\'s source document. Used for three-way match reconciliation and vendor dispute resolution. Must be unique per vendor account.']
  withholding_tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Amount of withholding tax deducted from the invoice payment, applicable for athlete agent fees, NIL agreement payments, and international vendor payments subject to withholding obligations.']

  Note: 'Accounts payable invoice received from vendors and suppliers — venue service contractors, broadcast equipment providers, athlete agents, merchandise manufacturers. Stores vendor invoice number, receipt date, posting date, vendor account, payment terms, gross amount, tax amount, net amount, currency, three-way match status (PO/GR/IR), and payment block reason. Managed in SAP FI-AP.'
}

Table finance.payment {
  payment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each payment transaction record in the Sports Entertainment finance domain. Primary key for the payment data product in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  agent_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to athlete.agent. Business justification: Payment records for agent commission disbursements must reference the agent being paid. Enables agent payment history tracking, commission reconciliation, and tax withholding compliance (1099/W-9) — a']
  ap_invoice_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the primary invoice (AR or AP) that this payment clears or partially clears. Links inbound customer receipts to AR invoices and outbound vendor disbursements to AP invoices for cash application and reconciliation.']
  ar_invoice_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.ar_invoice. Business justification: The payment table handles both AP payments (invoice_id → ap_invoice already exists) and AR receipts (customer payments against AR invoices). Adding ar_invoice_id FK enables proper linkage of incoming ']
  company_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.company_code. Business justification: Payments are executed within a specific legal entity (company code). The company_code string field should be replaced with a typed FK to company_code.company_code_id.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the SAP CO cost center to which this payment is allocated for internal management accounting and EBITDA reporting. Supports expense allocation across event operations, venue management, broadcast, and athlete management divisions.']
  fan_profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer master record for inbound AR receipts. Identifies the payer for ticket sales, sponsorship receipts, merchandise revenue, and broadcast rights settlements. Null for outbound vendor disbursements.']
  fiscal_period_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.fiscal_period. Business justification: Payments are posted to fiscal periods for cash accounting and reconciliation. Normalizes fiscal_year and fiscal_period INT columns.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Payments post to specific GL accounts (bank clearing accounts, cash accounts). The gl_account_code string field should be replaced with a typed FK to gl_account.gl_account_id.']
  membership_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fan.membership. Business justification: Membership fee payments (season tickets, VIP, fan club dues) must be reconciled against the specific membership record. Finance payment processing requires this link for cash application, auto-renewal']
  payment_run_id varchar [note: 'Identifier for the SAP FI automatic payment program run (F110) that generated this payment. Groups all payments executed in a single batch payment run for reconciliation and audit. Null for manually posted payments.']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'FK to finance.profit_center']
  ticket_payment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ticketing.ticket_payment. Business justification: finance.payment (ERP payment record) must be reconciled with ticketing.ticket_payment (payment processor record) for cash reconciliation and period-end close. Linking these enables automated three-way']
  ach_trace_number varchar [note: 'The NACHA ACH trace number assigned to this payment entry for ACH transactions. Used for ACH return processing, NOC (Notification of Change) handling, and bank reconciliation. Null for non-ACH payment methods.']
  bank_key varchar [note: 'The SAP FI house bank key (HBKID) identifying the financial institution through which this payment is routed. Used for bank reconciliation, liquidity planning, and treasury management reporting.']
  block_indicator varchar [note: 'SAP FI payment block code (ZLSPR) indicating whether the payment is blocked from processing and the reason (e.g., manual block, dispute, audit hold, compliance review). Blank/null indicates no block. Critical for AP payment control and SOX compliance.']
  business_area varchar [note: 'The SAP FI business area (GSBER) representing the organizational segment (e.g., ticketing, broadcast, sponsorship, merchandise, venue) for cross-company-code financial reporting and internal balance sheet preparation.']
  check_number varchar [note: 'The physical or electronic check number assigned to this payment when payment_method is \'check\'. Used for check register management, positive pay file submission to banks, and stop-payment processing. Null for non-check payment methods.']
  clearing_document_number varchar [note: 'The SAP FI clearing document number (AUGBL) generated when this payment clears one or more open items in the AR or AP subledger. Used for cash application matching and open item management in the general ledger.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this payment record was first created in the SAP FI system or ingested into the Databricks Silver Layer. Provides the audit trail creation point required for SOX Section 404 internal controls and GDPR data lineage.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the transaction currency in which the payment is denominated (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP, JPY). Essential for multi-currency operations across global sports markets. Part of the MONETARY_TRIPLET.']
  direction varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the payment is an outbound disbursement (AP vendor payment) or an inbound receipt (AR customer collection). Fundamental discriminator for cash flow classification and treasury reporting under IAS 7.']
  discount_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The cash discount amount deducted from the gross payment when early payment terms are honored (e.g., 2/10 net 30). Corresponds to BSEG.SKFBT in SAP FI. Used for discount capture analytics and vendor relationship management.']
  document_date date [note: 'The date on the original payment instrument (check date, wire instruction date, or bank statement date). Corresponds to BLDAT in SAP BKPF. Used for instrument-level audit and bank reconciliation.']
  document_number varchar [note: 'The externally-known SAP FI payment document number assigned at the time of posting. Serves as the primary business identifier for the payment transaction used in reconciliation, audit trails, and SOX financial controls. Corresponds to BELNR in SAP BKPF.']
  due_date date [note: 'The contractual due date by which the payment obligation must be settled per invoice payment terms. Used for early payment discount eligibility, late payment penalty calculation, and days-payable-outstanding (DPO) / days-sales-outstanding (DSO) analytics.']
  exchange_rate decimal(12,6) [note: 'The foreign currency exchange rate applied to convert the transaction currency amount to the company code local currency (typically USD) at the time of payment posting. Sourced from SAP FI exchange rate tables (TCURR). Critical for multi-currency reconciliation and FX gain/loss reporting.']
  gross_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total gross amount of the payment before any deductions, discounts, or withholding taxes. Expressed in the transaction currency. Forms the basis for EBITDA reporting and revenue recognition in SAP FI. Part of the MONETARY_TRIPLET for this transaction.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp of the most recent modification to this payment record, including status changes, reversals, or data corrections. Required for SOX audit trail, change data capture (CDC) in the Databricks lakehouse pipeline, and GDPR accountability.']
  local_currency_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The payment amount converted to the company code local currency (USD) using the exchange_rate at posting date. Used for consolidated financial reporting, SEC filings, and EBITDA calculations in the functional currency.']
  net_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The net amount actually transferred after deducting cash discounts and withholding taxes from the gross amount (gross_amount - discount_amount - withholding_tax_amount). Represents the actual cash movement for treasury and bank reconciliation. Part of the MONETARY_TRIPLET.']
  payment_date date [note: 'The principal business event date on which the payment was executed or value-dated by the bank. Used for cash flow reporting, aging analysis, and period-end close. Corresponds to ZFBDT (baseline date) or VALUT (value date) in SAP FI. Distinct from the document posting date.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'The financial instrument or mechanism used to execute the payment. ACH = Automated Clearing House electronic transfer; wire = bank wire transfer (SWIFT/SEPA); check = paper or electronic check; card = credit/debit card (PCI-DSS governed); digital_wallet = PayPal, Apple Pay, or similar; direct_debit = pre-authorized bank debit. Drives bank file format selection in SAP F110.']
  payment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the payment transaction within the SAP FI workflow. \'initiated\' = payment instruction created; \'posted\' = document posted to general ledger; \'cleared\' = open items cleared; \'reversed\' = payment reversed with reversal document; \'failed\' = bank rejection or processing error; \'on_hold\' = payment blocked pending approval or dispute resolution.']
  payment_type varchar [note: 'Business classification of the payment transaction purpose. vendor_disbursement = AP payment to supplier/contractor; customer_receipt = AR collection from ticket buyer, sponsor, or broadcaster; intercompany_settlement = payment between Sports Entertainment legal entities; payroll_disbursement = athlete or staff salary payment; tax_payment = remittance to tax authority; refund = return of funds to customer. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: vendor_disbursement|customer_receipt|intercompany_settlement|payroll_disbursement|tax_payment|refund — promote to reference product]']
  posting_date date [note: 'The accounting period posting date on which the payment document was recorded in the SAP FI general ledger. Determines the fiscal period for revenue recognition and financial reporting. May differ from payment_date for cross-period transactions.']
  reference varchar [note: 'Free-form reference text transmitted with the payment to the beneficiary bank (e.g., invoice number, contract reference, event name). Appears on bank statements and remittance advice. Corresponds to BVTYP/XBLNR in SAP FI. Critical for counterparty cash application.']
  remittance_advice_sent boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a remittance advice notification has been sent to the payee or payer detailing the invoices cleared by this payment. Supports vendor and customer communication workflows in SAP FI output management.']
  reversal_document_number varchar [note: 'The SAP FI document number of the reversal posting if this payment has been reversed (e.g., returned ACH, stopped check, duplicate payment correction). Null if the payment has not been reversed. Links to the reversing document for audit trail.']
  reversal_reason_code varchar [note: 'SAP FI reversal reason code (STGRD) explaining why the payment was reversed (e.g., wrong amount, duplicate, bank return, stop payment). Null if not reversed. Used for exception reporting and fraud detection analytics.']
  special_gl_indicator varchar [note: 'SAP FI special GL indicator (UMSKZ) classifying non-standard postings such as down payments, guarantees, or security deposits associated with this payment. Blank for standard payment postings. Used for balance sheet sub-classification.']
  swift_reference varchar [note: 'The SWIFT end-to-end transaction reference (UETR or TRN) for wire transfer payments. Enables real-time payment tracking via SWIFT gpi and bank confirmation matching. Null for non-wire payment methods.']
  terms_code varchar [note: 'The SAP FI payment terms key (ZTERM) governing the due date calculation and early payment discount conditions for this transaction (e.g., NT30, 2/10NET30). Inherited from the invoice or vendor/customer master. Drives DSO and DPO analytics.']
  withholding_tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The amount of withholding tax deducted from the payment at source, applicable to athlete NIL payments, foreign vendor disbursements, and royalty settlements. Reported to tax authorities and reflected in SAP FI withholding tax tables (WITH_ITEM).']

  Note: 'Financial payment transaction recording the settlement of accounts receivable and accounts payable obligations. Captures payment document number, payment date, payment method (ACH, wire, check, card, digital wallet), bank account, cleared invoice references, payment amount, currency, exchange rate, clearing document number, and payment run ID. Covers outbound vendor disbursements, inbound customer receipts, and automated payment program runs in SAP FI. Links to AR invoices (customer receipts) and AP invoices (vendor payments) for complete cash application and reconciliation.'
}

Table finance.fixed_asset {
  fixed_asset_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the fixed asset master record in the SAP FI-AA module. Primary key for the Silver Layer lakehouse table.']
  company_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.company_code. Business justification: Fixed assets are owned by a specific legal entity (company code) in SAP FI-AA. The company_code string field should be replaced with a typed FK to company_code.company_code_id.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Fixed assets in SAP FI-AA are assigned to cost centers for depreciation allocation and cost reporting. The cost_center_code string field should be replaced with a typed FK to cost_center.cost_center_i']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Fixed assets post to specific GL accounts (asset acquisition accounts, accumulated depreciation accounts). The gl_account_code string field should be replaced with a typed FK to gl_account.gl_account_']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Fixed assets are attributed to profit centers for segment-level asset reporting and EBITDA calculation. The profit_center_code string field should be replaced with a typed FK to profit_center.profit_c']
  accumulated_depreciation decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total depreciation charged against the asset from the in-service date to the current reporting period. Contra-asset balance used to calculate net book value. Sourced from SAP FI-AA depreciation simulation/posting runs.']
  acquisition_cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'Original historical cost of the fixed asset at the time of acquisition or capitalization, in the company code currency. Includes purchase price, import duties, and directly attributable costs to bring the asset to its intended use. CAPEX SSOT figure.']
  acquisition_date date [note: 'Date on which the fixed asset was acquired, purchased, or constructed and capitalized. Used as the start date for depreciation calculation and CAPEX reporting. Corresponds to SAP FI-AA \'Capitalization Date\'.']
  annual_depreciation_charge decimal(18,2) [note: 'Planned annual depreciation expense for the current fiscal year as calculated by SAP FI-AA. Used in FP&A budgeting, EBITDA bridge analysis, and Workday Adaptive Planning depreciation forecasts.']
  asset_category varchar [note: 'High-level classification distinguishing tangible assets (stadium structures, OB trucks, scoreboards), intangible assets (broadcast rights, software licenses, NIL-related IP), right-of-use assets (IFRS 16 leases), and investment property. Drives balance sheet line-item presentation.']
  asset_class_code varchar [note: 'SAP FI-AA asset class code that determines the depreciation area, useful life defaults, and GL account determination (e.g., \'1000\' for Buildings, \'2000\' for Machinery, \'3000\' for Intangible Assets, \'4000\' for Broadcast Rights). Drives CAPEX categorization and balance sheet classification.']
  asset_class_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the SAP asset class (e.g., \'Stadium Structures\', \'Broadcast Equipment\', \'OB Trucks\', \'Digital Platform Infrastructure\', \'Acquired Broadcast Rights\', \'Scoreboards & LED Systems\'). Used in financial reporting and CAPEX dashboards.']
  asset_description varchar [note: 'Short descriptive name of the fixed asset as recorded in SAP FI-AA (e.g., \'OB Truck Unit 7\', \'Broadcast Tower – East Campus\', \'Stadium Roof Structure – Section A\'). Used on depreciation schedules and financial statements.']
  asset_location_code varchar [note: 'Physical or logical location code where the asset is deployed (e.g., venue code, broadcast facility code, data center identifier). Supports asset tracking, insurance valuation, and ADA/OSHA compliance reporting for venue assets.']
  asset_location_description varchar [note: 'Human-readable description of the asset\'s physical location (e.g., \'Main Stadium – East Stand\', \'Broadcast Tower – Roof Level 3\', \'OB Truck Depot – Training Facility\'). Used in physical asset verification and insurance schedules.']
  asset_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known SAP FI-AA asset number (main asset number) assigned at capitalization. Used as the primary business identifier on CAPEX reports, depreciation schedules, and audit trails. Format follows SAP company-code-prefixed numbering convention.']
  asset_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the fixed asset. \'under_construction\' = Assets Under Construction (AUC) not yet capitalized; \'in_service\' = actively depreciated; \'idle\' = temporarily out of service; \'impaired\' = impairment loss recognized; \'disposed\' = sold, scrapped, or retired; \'transferred\' = intercompany or intra-company transfer in progress.']
  asset_subnumber varchar [note: 'SAP FI-AA sub-number identifying a component or addition to the main asset (e.g., a scoreboard upgrade added to the main stadium structure asset). Enables component-level depreciation tracking.']
  broadcast_rights_expiry_date date [note: 'For intangible assets representing acquired broadcast rights (e.g., RSN rights, OTT streaming rights, PPV event rights), the contractual expiry date of the rights. Drives amortization end date and rights renewal planning. Null for tangible assets.']
  capex_budget_code varchar [note: 'Reference to the approved CAPEX budget line item or investment program code in Workday Adaptive Planning / SAP IM (Investment Management) that authorized the acquisition of this asset. Supports CAPEX vs. budget variance reporting.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the fixed asset master record was first created in SAP FI-AA. Provides the audit trail creation event required for SOX internal controls and data lineage in the Silver Layer lakehouse.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary amounts on this asset record (e.g., USD, GBP, EUR). Supports multi-currency reporting for global sports entertainment operations.']
  depreciation_area_code varchar [note: 'SAP FI-AA depreciation area identifier representing the valuation basis (e.g., \'01\' for book depreciation per local GAAP, \'15\' for tax depreciation, \'30\' for IFRS). Enables parallel valuation for SEC reporting and tax compliance.']
  depreciation_method_code varchar [note: 'SAP FI-AA depreciation key/method code defining the calculation method (e.g., \'LINR\' for straight-line, \'DG30\' for declining balance 30%, \'MANU\' for manual, \'UNIT\' for units-of-production). Determines how accumulated depreciation is calculated each period.']
  disposal_date date [note: 'Date on which the asset was retired, sold, scrapped, or otherwise disposed of. Triggers the derecognition of the asset from the balance sheet and recognition of any gain or loss on disposal in SAP FI-AA.']
  disposal_proceeds decimal(18,2) [note: 'Net proceeds received upon disposal or sale of the asset. Used to calculate the gain or loss on disposal recognized in the income statement. Relevant for stadium asset sales, OB truck fleet disposals, and broadcast rights expirations.']
  disposal_type varchar [note: 'Method by which the asset was disposed of. \'sale\' = sold to third party; \'scrap\' = physically scrapped; \'transfer\' = intercompany or intra-company transfer; \'donation\' = donated; \'write_off\' = written off; \'insurance_claim\' = disposed via insurance settlement (e.g., storm-damaged broadcast tower).']
  impairment_loss decimal(18,2) [note: 'Cumulative impairment loss recognized on the asset where the carrying amount exceeds the recoverable amount (per IAS 36 / ASC 360). Reduces net book value. Relevant for broadcast rights, goodwill-adjacent intangibles, and underperforming venue assets.']
  in_service_date date [note: 'Date the asset was placed into active service and depreciation commenced. May differ from acquisition_date for assets under construction. Drives the depreciation start period in SAP FI-AA.']
  insurance_policy_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the insurance policy covering this asset against loss, damage, or liability. Critical for venue assets (stadium structures), broadcast infrastructure (OB trucks, towers), and high-value equipment. Supports risk management and claims processing.']
  insured_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Declared insured replacement value of the asset as agreed with the insurer. May differ from net book value (NBV) for high-value venue and broadcast infrastructure assets. Used for insurance adequacy reviews.']
  is_fully_depreciated boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the asset has been fully depreciated (net book value equals residual value). Fully depreciated assets still in service are flagged for management review and potential useful life extension assessment per IAS 16.']
  is_leased boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the asset is a right-of-use (ROU) asset recognized under IFRS 16 / ASC 842 (e.g., leased venue, leased OB truck, leased broadcast satellite capacity). Drives separate balance sheet presentation and lease liability linkage.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent change to the fixed asset master record in SAP FI-AA. Used for incremental data loading, change detection in the Silver Layer, and SOX audit trail completeness.']
  last_physical_verification_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent physical inventory count or asset verification exercise confirming the asset\'s existence and condition. Required for SOX internal controls and annual audit procedures.']
  net_book_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Current carrying amount of the fixed asset: acquisition_cost plus subsequent_acquisition_cost minus accumulated_depreciation minus impairment_loss. Reported on the balance sheet. Refreshed each period-end close from SAP FI-AA.']
  purchase_order_number varchar [note: 'SAP MM purchase order number associated with the acquisition of the asset. Provides the procurement-to-capitalization audit trail required for SOX compliance and CAPEX authorization controls.']
  residual_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Estimated amount the entity expects to recover from the asset at the end of its useful life (salvage value). Deducted from acquisition cost to determine the depreciable base. Reviewed annually per IAS 16.']
  revaluation_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Cumulative upward or downward revaluation adjustment to the asset\'s carrying amount under the IAS 16 revaluation model (where adopted). Recorded in the revaluation surplus in equity. Applicable to stadium land and property assets.']
  serial_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturer\'s serial number or unique physical identifier for the asset (e.g., OB truck chassis number, scoreboard controller serial, broadcast transmitter serial). Used for physical verification, warranty claims, and insurance schedules.']
  subsequent_acquisition_cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'Cumulative cost of subsequent capital additions or improvements to the asset (e.g., stadium seat upgrades, scoreboard technology refresh, broadcast tower antenna replacement). Added to the asset\'s gross book value.']
  useful_life_periods varchar [note: 'Estimated useful life expressed in fiscal periods (months) for assets with sub-annual depreciation granularity. Used in SAP FI-AA period-based depreciation calculations alongside useful_life_years.']
  useful_life_years varchar [note: 'Estimated useful economic life of the asset in years, as determined at capitalization and used to calculate straight-line or other depreciation. Reviewed annually per IAS 16 (e.g., 40 years for stadium structures, 5 years for OB trucks, 3 years for broadcast rights).']
  wbs_element varchar [note: 'SAP PS Work Breakdown Structure element linked to the asset, used for CAPEX project tracking during the Assets Under Construction (AUC) phase (e.g., stadium renovation project, broadcast tower installation). Populated until the AUC is settled to a final asset.']

  Note: 'Fixed asset master record in SAP FI-AA representing capitalized physical and intangible assets — stadium structures, broadcast towers, OB trucks, scoreboards, digital platform infrastructure, and acquired broadcast rights. Stores asset class, acquisition date, acquisition cost, useful life, depreciation method, accumulated depreciation, net book value, and disposal status. SSOT for CAPEX and depreciation accounting.'
}

Table finance.revenue_recognition_schedule {
  revenue_recognition_schedule_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each revenue recognition schedule record within the finance domain. Primary key for the revenue_recognition_schedule data product.']
  company_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.company_code. Business justification: Revenue recognition schedules are scoped to a specific legal entity for SEC reporting compliance. The company_code string field should be replaced with a typed FK to company_code.company_code_id.']
  competition_round_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to event.competition_round. Business justification: Broadcast rights contracts often specify per-round fee tranches (e.g., quarterfinal, semifinal, final). Revenue recognition schedules must reference the competition round as the performance obligation']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Revenue recognition schedules are attributed to cost centers for cost allocation and reporting. The cost_center_code string field should be replaced with a typed FK to cost_center.cost_center_id.']
  fiscal_period_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.fiscal_period. Business justification: Revenue recognition schedules recognize revenue into specific fiscal periods. Normalizes fiscal_year and fiscal_period INT columns by linking to fiscal_period master.']
  fixture_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to event.fixture. Business justification: Under ASC 606/IFRS 15, each fixture is a performance obligation for broadcast rights and gate revenue. Revenue recognition schedules must reference the specific fixture whose completion triggers defer']
  journal_entry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.journal_entry. Business justification: Each recognition event generates a journal entry posting. The journal_entry_reference string field is a denormalized reference that should be replaced with a typed FK journal_entry_id → journal_entry.']
  match_result_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to event.match_result. Business justification: Match result confirmation is the triggering event for recognizing gate receipts, PPV, and broadcast revenue. Linking revenue_recognition_schedule to match_result enables finance to confirm performance']
  media_rights_deal_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to broadcast.media_rights_deal. Business justification: Media rights deals are the primary contracts requiring ASC 606/IFRS 15 revenue recognition schedules. Finance creates recognition schedules per rights deal for deferred revenue management and period r']
  nil_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to athlete.nil_agreement. Business justification: NIL agreement revenue must be recognized over the agreement term per ASC 606. The revenue_recognition_schedule must reference the nil_agreement driving the recognition schedule for proper deferred rev']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Revenue recognition schedules are attributed to profit centers for segment-level revenue reporting. The profit_center_code string field should be replaced with a typed FK to profit_center.profit_cente']
  recognized_gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Revenue recognition schedules post recognized revenue to a specific GL account. The gl_account_code string field should be replaced with a typed FK recognized_gl_account_id → gl_account.gl_account_id.']
  venue_assignment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to event.venue_assignment. Business justification: Venue revenue-sharing agreements (venue_assignment.revenue_share_pct) create deferred revenue obligations recognized over the event lifecycle. Finance must link recognition schedules to the specific v']
  accounting_standard varchar [note: 'The revenue recognition accounting standard applied to this schedule. ASC_606 for US GAAP entities; IFRS_15 for international entities; ASC_606_IFRS_15 for dual-reporting entities. Determines the specific recognition criteria and disclosure requirements.']
  constraint_applied_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the variable consideration constraint has been applied to limit the recognized amount to the extent it is highly probable (IFRS 15) or probable (ASC 606) that a significant revenue reversal will not occur. Required for ASC 606 / IFRS 15 compliance documentation.']
  contract_effective_date date [note: 'The date the underlying contract became legally binding and enforceable. Used as the ASC 606 contract inception date for determining transaction price and allocating to performance obligations.']
