  contract_expiry_date date [note: 'The date the underlying contract expires or terminates. For multi-year broadcast rights or naming rights agreements, this may be several years in the future. Nullable for evergreen contracts.']
  contract_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the underlying revenue contract driving this recognition schedule. Determines applicable recognition methodology and disclosure requirements. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: broadcast_rights|sponsorship|naming_rights|season_ticket|multi_event|licensing|merchandise|ppv|ott_subscription|nil_agreement — promote to reference product]']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this revenue recognition schedule record was first created in the system, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Provides the audit trail creation point required for SOX compliance and data lineage in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  cumulative_recognized_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total revenue recognized to date against this performance obligation since the recognition start date. Equals total_contract_value minus deferred_revenue_balance at any point in time. Used for period-end reconciliation and ASC 606 cumulative catch-up adjustment calculations.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts on this recognition schedule (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Required for multi-currency broadcast rights and international sponsorship contracts.']
  current_period_recognition_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Revenue amount to be recognized in the current accounting period for this performance obligation. Drives the period-end journal entry generation in SAP S/4HANA FI/CO. For straight-line method, equals total_contract_value divided by total periods; for milestone/event-based, equals the value of the milestone achieved.']
  deferred_revenue_balance decimal(18,2) [note: 'Current outstanding deferred revenue balance for this performance obligation — the portion of total contract value received or billed but not yet recognized as earned revenue. Drives the deferred revenue waterfall report and balance sheet liability disclosure. Updated each period-end close in SAP S/4HANA FI.']
  deferred_revenue_gl_account_code varchar [note: 'SAP S/4HANA GL account code for the deferred revenue liability balance sheet account. The deferred revenue balance is carried here until recognized. Required for balance sheet reporting and SOX-compliant financial controls.']
  discount_rate decimal(8,4) [note: 'The discount rate (as a decimal, e.g., 0.0450 for 4.50%) used to adjust for the significant financing component in the transaction price. Reflects the rate that would be used in a separate financing transaction between the entity and the counterparty at contract inception. Null when significant_financing_flag is false.']
  last_recognition_date date [note: 'The date of the most recent revenue recognition posting for this performance obligation. Used to confirm period-end close completion and to identify schedules that have not been processed in the current period.']
  modification_date date [note: 'The effective date of the most recent contract modification affecting this recognition schedule. Null when modification_flag is false. Used to determine the prospective or cumulative catch-up adjustment period.']
  modification_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this recognition schedule reflects a contract modification (e.g., renegotiated broadcast rights fee, amended sponsorship scope). When true, the modification accounting treatment (prospective or cumulative catch-up) per ASC 606-10-25-12 / IFRS 15.18 must be documented.']
  next_recognition_date date [note: 'The scheduled date for the next revenue recognition posting. Used by the FP&A team in Workday Adaptive Planning for revenue forecasting and by the accounting team to manage period-end close calendars.']
  performance_obligation_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the distinct performance obligation being recognized, as documented in the contract and required for ASC 606 / IFRS 15 disclosure. Examples: \'Exclusive broadcast rights for NFL regular season games FY2024-FY2026\', \'Naming rights for stadium facade and digital signage\', \'Season ticket package — 41 home games\'.']
  periods_recognized varchar [note: 'Number of accounting periods for which revenue has been recognized to date against this performance obligation. Used to track progress toward full recognition and to validate cumulative_recognized_amount in period-end reconciliation.']
  recognition_basis varchar [note: 'Indicates whether revenue is recognized over time (e.g., broadcast rights delivered continuously over a season) or at a point in time (e.g., a one-time PPV event, a single concert). Fundamental ASC 606 / IFRS 15 classification that determines the recognition pattern.']
  recognition_end_date date [note: 'The date on which revenue recognition is expected to be fully completed for this performance obligation. For multi-year broadcast rights, this may span several fiscal years. Nullable for open-ended or usage-based obligations without a fixed end date.']
  recognition_method varchar [note: 'The accounting method used to recognize revenue over the performance obligation period. Straight-line = equal amounts per period; Milestone = upon achievement of defined deliverables; Usage-based = proportional to actual consumption (e.g., broadcast impressions, CPM); Event-based = upon occurrence of specific events (e.g., game played); Proportional performance = based on percentage of obligation fulfilled.']
  recognition_start_date date [note: 'The date on which revenue recognition commences for this performance obligation. Typically aligns with contract effective date or the date the entity begins satisfying the performance obligation (e.g., first broadcast date, season opener, naming rights activation).']
  recognition_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the revenue recognition schedule. Draft = under review; Active = currently recognizing revenue; Suspended = temporarily halted (e.g., contract dispute); Completed = fully recognized; Cancelled = contract terminated before full recognition.']
  schedule_number varchar [note: 'Externally-visible business identifier for this recognition schedule, formatted as RRS-{YYYY}-{NNNNNN}. Used in SAP S/4HANA FI/CO journal entry references, audit workpapers, and SEC financial reporting disclosures.']
  significant_financing_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the contract contains a significant financing component requiring adjustment of the transaction price for the time value of money per ASC 606-10-32-15 / IFRS 15.60. Common in multi-year prepaid broadcast rights deals where cash is received significantly in advance of performance.']
  total_contract_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total transaction price allocated to this performance obligation as determined at contract inception per ASC 606 Step 3 (Determine Transaction Price) and Step 4 (Allocate Transaction Price). Expressed in the contract currency. Used as the basis for deferred revenue waterfall reporting.']
  total_recognition_periods varchar [note: 'Total number of accounting periods over which revenue is to be recognized for this performance obligation. Used for straight-line recognition calculations and deferred revenue waterfall projections. For event-based recognition, represents the total number of qualifying events.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this revenue recognition schedule record, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Required for change data capture in the Databricks Silver Layer and SOX audit trail completeness.']
  variable_consideration_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The estimated amount of variable consideration included in the transaction price at the most likely amount or expected value method per ASC 606. Applicable for contracts with performance bonuses, ratings escalators, or PPV revenue share components. Null when variable_consideration_flag is false.']
  variable_consideration_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the transaction price includes variable consideration components (e.g., performance bonuses, ratings-based escalators, PPV revenue share, usage-based royalties). When true, the constraint on variable consideration per ASC 606 Step 3 must be applied and reassessed each reporting period.']

  Note: 'Revenue recognition schedule governing when and how deferred revenue is recognized into earned revenue per ASC 606 / IFRS 15 standards. Applies to multi-year broadcast rights contracts, season ticket packages, multi-event sponsorship deals, and long-term naming rights agreements. Stores contract reference, performance obligation description, total contract value, deferred balance, recognition method (straight-line, milestone, usage-based), recognition start/end dates, period recognition amounts, and cumulative recognized amount. SSOT for ASC 606 compliance, deferred revenue waterfall reporting, and period-end revenue recognition journal entry generation. Distinct from recurring_revenue which tracks commercial ARR/churn metrics — this product owns the accounting treatment and timing of revenue recognition.'
}

Table finance.fiscal_period {
  fiscal_period_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for fiscal_period']
  parent_fiscal_period_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent fiscal period for hierarchical period structures (e.g., a monthly period rolling up to a quarterly period). Null for top-level periods.']
  primary_prior_fiscal_period_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on fiscal_period (prior_fiscal_period_id)']
  budget_version varchar [note: 'The budget version or scenario applicable to this fiscal period (e.g., Original Budget, Revised Budget, Forecast).']
  calendar_month varchar [note: 'The calendar month (1-12) in which this fiscal period starts, used for calendar-to-fiscal mapping.']
  calendar_quarter varchar [note: 'The calendar quarter (1-4) in which this fiscal period starts, used for calendar-to-fiscal mapping.']
  calendar_year varchar [note: 'The calendar year in which this fiscal period starts, used for calendar-to-fiscal mapping.']
  closed_date date [note: 'The date when this fiscal period was officially closed for transaction entry (yyyy-MM-dd). Null if not yet closed.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this fiscal period record was first created in the system (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX).']
  days_in_period varchar [note: 'Total number of calendar days in this fiscal period, used for pro-rata calculations and variance analysis.']
  end_date date [note: 'The last calendar date included in this fiscal period (yyyy-MM-dd).']
  fiscal_month varchar [note: 'The fiscal month number within the fiscal year (1-12). Null for non-monthly periods.']
  fiscal_period_description varchar [note: 'Additional descriptive information about the fiscal period, including special characteristics or notes.']
  fiscal_quarter varchar [note: 'The fiscal quarter number within the fiscal year (1-4). Null for non-quarterly periods.']
  fiscal_week varchar [note: 'The fiscal week number within the fiscal year (1-52 or 1-53). Null for non-weekly periods.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'The fiscal year to which this period belongs (e.g., 2024).']
  is_adjustment_period boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this is a special adjustment period (e.g., Period 13) used for year-end adjustments and accruals (True/False).']
  is_current_period boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this is the current active fiscal period (True/False).']
  is_year_end_period boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this period is the final period of the fiscal year, requiring year-end closing procedures (True/False).']
  locked_date date [note: 'The date when this fiscal period was locked for final reporting and audit (yyyy-MM-dd). Null if not yet locked.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this fiscal period record was last modified (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX).']
  period_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier code for the fiscal period, typically combining fiscal year and period number (e.g., FY2024-Q1, FY2024-P01).']
  period_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the fiscal period (e.g., Q1 2024, January 2024, FY2024 Period 1).']
  period_number varchar [note: 'Sequential period number within the fiscal year, used for ordering and reporting.']
  period_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the fiscal period. Future: not yet started; Open: active for transactions; Closed: no new transactions allowed; Locked: closed and finalized for reporting; Archived: historical record only.']
  period_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the fiscal period granularity (annual, semi-annual, quarterly, monthly, weekly, or custom).']
  reporting_currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code used for financial reporting in this period (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  sec_filing_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this fiscal period requires SEC financial reporting filings (10-Q, 10-K) for publicly traded entities (True/False).']
  sox_audit_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this fiscal period requires SOX compliance audit procedures (True/False).']
  start_date date [note: 'The first calendar date included in this fiscal period (yyyy-MM-dd).']
  working_days_in_period varchar [note: 'Number of business working days in this fiscal period, excluding weekends and holidays, used for operational metrics.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for fiscal_period. Referenced by fiscal_period_id.'
}

Table finance.payment_run {
  payment_run_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for payment_run']
  company_code_id varchar [note: 'FK to finance.company_code']
  fiscal_period_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.fiscal_period. Business justification: payment_run has fiscal_period (INT) and fiscal_year (INT) as raw denormalized columns but no FK to finance.fiscal_period. Payment runs are executed within specific fiscal periods for period-end close ']
  primary_original_payment_run_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the original payment run being reversed. Populated only when is_reversal is true. Used to maintain audit trail of corrections.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'The approval workflow status for the payment run: pending indicates awaiting authorization, approved indicates cleared for execution, rejected indicates denied by approver, bypassed indicates no approval required per policy.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the payment run was approved. Used for audit trails and compliance reporting.']
  completion_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time when the payment run execution finished, whether successfully or with errors. Used to calculate processing duration and SLA compliance.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the payment run record was first created in the system. Used for audit trails and data lineage.']
  discount_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total cash discount amount taken across all payments in this run for early payment. Represents savings achieved through prompt payment programs.']
  error_log varchar [note: 'Detailed error messages and exception logs for failed payments or processing issues. Used for troubleshooting and root cause analysis.']
  execution_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time when the payment run execution began. Critical for audit trails and reconciliation with banking systems.']
  failed_payment_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The sum of all failed payment amounts that were not disbursed. Represents funds that remain in the company account and require reprocessing.']
  failed_payment_count varchar [note: 'The number of payment transactions that failed during processing due to validation errors, insufficient funds, or system issues. Requires manual intervention.']
  is_reversal boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this payment run is a reversal of a previous run. True indicates reversal, false indicates original payment run.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the payment run record was last modified. Used for audit trails and change tracking.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes and comments about the payment run. Used to document special circumstances, manual interventions, or business context.']
  payment_currency varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all payments in this run. All disbursements within a single run are typically in the same currency.']
  payment_file_format varchar [note: 'The electronic file format standard used for bank transmission: ISO 20022 for international standard, NACHA for US ACH, BAI2 for bank reporting, MT940 for SWIFT, CAMT.053 for account statements, proprietary for bank-specific formats.']
  payment_file_name varchar [note: 'The name of the electronic payment file generated for bank transmission. Used for file tracking and reconciliation with bank acknowledgments.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'The payment instrument used for disbursements in this run: ACH for Automated Clearing House, wire for wire transfer, check for physical checks, EFT for Electronic Funds Transfer, SEPA for Single Euro Payments Area, BACS for UK Bankers Automated Clearing Services.']
  payment_run_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the payment run: draft indicates initial creation, scheduled indicates approved and queued, processing indicates active execution, completed indicates successful finish, failed indicates execution error, cancelled indicates user-terminated run.']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'The standard payment terms code applied to invoices included in this run. Defines due date calculation and discount eligibility.']
  posting_date date [note: 'The accounting date on which payment transactions are posted to the general ledger. May differ from value date for period-end processing.']
  reconciliation_status varchar [note: 'The status of bank statement reconciliation for this payment run: pending indicates awaiting bank confirmation, reconciled indicates matched with bank statement, discrepancy indicates mismatch requiring investigation, under_review indicates active reconciliation process.']
  run_number varchar [note: 'Business-facing unique identifier for the payment run, typically formatted as PR-YYYYMMDD-NNNN for external reference and audit trails.']
  run_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the payment run based on business purpose: standard for regular scheduled runs, emergency for urgent off-cycle payments, supplemental for additional payments, reversal for payment corrections, adjustment for reconciliation runs, interim for partial disbursements.']
  scheduled_date date [note: 'The date on which the payment run is scheduled to execute. Used for planning and cash flow forecasting.']
  successful_payment_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The sum of all successfully processed payment amounts. Used to reconcile actual cash outflows against planned disbursements.']
  successful_payment_count varchar [note: 'The number of payment transactions that were successfully processed and transmitted to the bank. Used to calculate success rate and identify exceptions.']
  total_payment_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The sum of all payment amounts included in this run, expressed in the payment currency. Used for cash position management and bank account reconciliation.']
  total_payment_count varchar [note: 'The total number of individual payment transactions included in this payment run. Used for reconciliation and control totals.']
  transmission_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the payment file was transmitted to the bank. Critical for cut-off time compliance and value dating.']
  value_date date [note: 'The date on which funds are to be debited from the company account and credited to payee accounts. Used for cash flow forecasting and liquidity management.']
  withholding_tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total withholding tax amount deducted from payments in this run. Required for tax compliance and reporting to tax authorities.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for payment_run. Referenced by payment_run_id.'
}

Table finance.ledger {
  ledger_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for ledger']
  company_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.company_code. Business justification: ledger has company_code (STRING) but no FK to finance.company_code. In SAP S/4HANA, ledgers are assigned to company codes (legal entities). The leading ledger and extension ledgers are configured per ']
  parent_ledger_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on ledger (parent_ledger_id)']
  accounting_principle varchar [note: 'The accounting standard or principle governing this ledger (e.g., US GAAP, IFRS, local statutory requirements).']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval workflow status for this ledger configuration before it becomes operational.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this ledger configuration was approved for operational use.']
  audit_trail_level varchar [note: 'The level of audit trail and change tracking maintained for transactions posted to this ledger.']
  balance_carryforward_method varchar [note: 'The method used to carry forward account balances from one fiscal year to the next in this ledger.']
  closed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this ledger was closed and no longer accepts new transactions.']
  consolidation_ledger_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this ledger is used for group consolidation and intercompany elimination purposes.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this ledger record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code representing the functional currency of the ledger.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'The date on which this ledger ceases to be active for new transactions. Null indicates an open-ended ledger.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'The date from which this ledger becomes active and available for financial transactions.']
  fiscal_year_variant varchar [note: 'Code identifying the fiscal year calendar structure used by this ledger (e.g., calendar year, 4-4-5 retail calendar).']
  intercompany_elimination_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this ledger includes intercompany elimination entries for consolidated financial reporting.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this ledger record was last updated or modified.']
  leading_ledger_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this ledger is the leading (primary) ledger for the organization used for statutory reporting and external financial statements.']
  ledger_code varchar [note: 'Unique business identifier code for the ledger used in financial reporting and system integration. Externally-known identifier.']
  ledger_description varchar [note: 'Detailed business description and purpose of the ledger including its scope and intended use cases.']
  ledger_name varchar [note: 'Full descriptive name of the ledger for human-readable identification and reporting purposes.']
  ledger_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the ledger indicating whether it is available for posting transactions.']
  ledger_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the ledger based on its accounting purpose and scope within the enterprise financial architecture.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free-text notes or comments about this ledger for internal reference and documentation purposes.']
  parallel_accounting_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this ledger is used for parallel accounting to support multiple accounting standards simultaneously.']
  posting_allowed_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether direct financial postings are currently permitted to this ledger.']
  retained_earnings_account varchar [note: 'The general ledger account number used for retained earnings and profit/loss carry-forward in this ledger.']
  segment_reporting_enabled_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this ledger supports segment reporting for business unit or geographic financial analysis.']
  sox_compliant_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this ledger is subject to Sarbanes-Oxley Act internal control requirements and audit trails.']
  valuation_area varchar [note: 'Code identifying the valuation area or approach used for asset and inventory valuation in this ledger.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for ledger. Referenced by ledger_id.'
}

Table compliance.regulatory_filing {

Business justification: SEC filings, league financial disclosures, and tax regulatory submissions in sports entertainment are tied to specific fiscal periods. filing_period_start_date/end_date are text; a FK to fiscal_period']
  franchise_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to league.franchise. Business justification: Franchise-specific regulatory filings (ownership disclosures, stadium safety filings, financial disclosures) require direct franchise reference. regulatory_filing has league_id and season_id but no fr']

  jurisdiction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to gaming.gaming_jurisdiction.   league_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to league.league. Business justification: Leagues submit regulatory filings to governing bodies (annual anti-doping reports, broadcast license renewals, financial disclosures). The existing league_or_sport text field is denormalized; a proper']
  league_season_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to league.season. Business justification: Regulatory filings (salary cap reports, player safety reports, anti-doping compliance reports) are season-specific obligations. Linking regulatory_filing to season enables season-level compliance repo']
  obligation_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the compliance obligation that mandated this regulatory filing, linking the submission to its originating regulatory requirement.']
  operator_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to gaming.operator. Business justification: Regulatory filings with gaming commissions are operator-specific (license applications, AML reports, incident disclosures). Compliance teams must associate each filing with the specific operator it pe']
  original_filing_regulatory_filing_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the original regulatory filing record that this filing amends or supersedes. Populated only when is_amendment is True.']
  policy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.policy. Business justification: A regulatory filing may be required by or reference a specific internal policy. Linking regulatory_filing to the governing policy enables policy-level filing tracking and demonstrates that filings are']
  acknowledgment_date date [note: 'The date on which the governing body formally acknowledged receipt of the regulatory filing.']
  acknowledgment_reference varchar [note: 'Official reference number or confirmation code issued by the governing body upon receipt and acknowledgment of the filing.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the filing was formally approved internally for submission to the governing body.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or employee identifier of the senior officer or compliance authority who approved the filing for submission (e.g., Chief Compliance Officer, General Counsel).']
  confidentiality_level varchar [note: 'Data classification level of the filing content, governing internal access controls and disclosure obligations.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this regulatory filing record was first created in the compliance register system.']
  days_overdue varchar [note: 'Number of calendar days the filing is past its due date at the time of last status update. Used for overdue alerting and escalation workflows. Zero or negative indicates on-time or early submission.']
  due_date date [note: 'The regulatory deadline by which this filing must be submitted to the governing body. Used for overdue alerting and deadline tracking.']
  external_portal_url varchar [note: 'URL of the governing body\'s electronic filing portal where this submission was made or can be accessed (e.g., SEC EDGAR, WADA ADAMS, FCC Electronic Filing System).']
  filing_category varchar [note: 'High-level business domain category of the filing, enabling cross-domain compliance reporting and portfolio management across WADA, GDPR/CCPA, PCI-DSS, ADA, CAS, and financial regulators. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: anti_doping|data_privacy|financial|broadcast|safety|accessibility|arbitration — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  filing_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the regulatory filing\'s scope, content, and purpose, providing context for compliance officers and auditors.']
  filing_period_end_date date [note: 'The end date of the reporting or compliance period covered by this regulatory filing (e.g., fiscal year end, season end).']
  filing_period_start_date date [note: 'The start date of the reporting or compliance period covered by this regulatory filing (e.g., fiscal year start, season start).']
  filing_reference_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique reference number assigned to this regulatory filing, used for tracking and correspondence with the governing body.']
  filing_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the regulatory filing, from initial draft through governing body acknowledgment and final disposition. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|submitted|acknowledged|under_review|accepted|rejected|overdue — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  filing_title varchar [note: 'Descriptive title or name of the regulatory filing as it appears on the submission document (e.g., \'Annual Anti-Doping Declaration FY2024\', \'GDPR Article 30 Records of Processing\').']
  filing_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the regulatory filing by its purpose or mandate. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: annual_report|incident_report|eligibility_certification|anti_doping_declaration|broadcast_license_renewal|financial_disclosure|privacy_impact_assessment|arbitration_submission|waiver_request|audit_response — promote to reference product]']
  governing_body_contact varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the primary contact person or department at the governing body responsible for receiving and processing this filing.']
  internal_case_reference varchar [note: 'Internal tracking number or case reference assigned by Sports Entertainment\'s compliance or legal team to this filing for cross-referencing with internal case management systems.']
  is_amendment boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this filing is an amendment or correction to a previously submitted filing. True if this is an amended submission.']
  is_recurring boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this filing is part of a recurring compliance schedule (e.g., annual, quarterly) as opposed to a one-time or ad hoc submission.']
  jurisdiction varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code representing the legal jurisdiction under which this filing is made (e.g., USA, GBR, FRA). Use \'INT\' for international/multi-jurisdictional filings.']
  penalty_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary value of the regulatory penalty or fine assessed or at risk for this filing, in the currency specified by penalty_currency_code.']
  penalty_currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the penalty amount (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  penalty_risk_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this filing carries a risk of regulatory penalty or sanction if not submitted on time or if rejected. Used for prioritization in compliance workflows.']
  recurrence_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency at which this type of regulatory filing recurs (e.g., annual for SEC annual reports, quarterly for financial disclosures). Populated when is_recurring is True.']
  regulatory_framework varchar [note: 'The primary regulatory or compliance framework under which this filing is made. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: GDPR|CCPA|PCI_DSS|WADA|SOX|ADA|OSHA|ISO_20121|CAS|FCC|SEC|FIFA|IOC|NFL|NBA|MLB|NHL — promote to reference product]']
  rejection_reason varchar [note: 'Narrative explanation provided by the governing body or internal reviewer for rejecting or returning the filing. Populated when filing_status is \'rejected\'.']
  resubmission_due_date date [note: 'Deadline by which a corrected or amended filing must be resubmitted following a rejection or request for additional information from the governing body.']
  review_deadline_date date [note: 'Internal deadline by which the filing must be reviewed and approved before submission to the governing body, supporting pre-submission quality control.']
  submission_method varchar [note: 'The channel or method used to submit the filing to the governing body (e.g., electronic portal such as SEC EDGAR or WADA ADAMS, email, postal mail).']
  submission_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The exact date and time the filing was formally submitted to the governing body. This is the principal business event timestamp for the filing transaction.']
  submitted_by varchar [note: 'Full name or employee identifier of the internal staff member or authorized representative who submitted the filing on behalf of Sports Entertainment.']
  submitter_title varchar [note: 'Official job title of the individual who submitted the filing (e.g., Chief Compliance Officer, Legal Counsel, Data Protection Officer).']
  supporting_document_count varchar [note: 'Number of supporting documents, attachments, or exhibits included with this regulatory filing submission.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this regulatory filing record was last modified in the compliance register system.']

  Note: 'Master record of all regulatory submissions made to governing bodies including FIFA, IOC, NFL, NBA, MLB, NHL, FCC, SEC, WADA, and CAS. Tracks filing type, submission date, governing body (FK to governing_body), jurisdiction, filing status, due date, submitted by, acknowledgment reference, and linked compliance obligation (FK to obligation). Serves as the authoritative register of all formal regulatory submissions and enables deadline tracking and overdue alerting.'
}

Table compliance.governing_body {
  governing_body_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each governing body or regulatory authority record in the Sports Entertainment compliance reference master.']
  acronym varchar [note: 'Widely recognized abbreviation or acronym for the governing body (e.g., FIFA, WADA, IOC, FCC, SEC, OSHA, PCI SSC).']
  annual_membership_fee decimal(18,2) [note: 'Annual fee or dues payable by Sports Entertainment to this governing body for membership, certification, or regulatory registration (in the denomination of penalty_currency_code).']
  anti_doping_authority boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this governing body has anti-doping testing, adjudication, or enforcement authority over Sports Entertainment\'s athletes (e.g., True for WADA, sport-specific anti-doping organizations).']
  applicable_business_domains varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of Sports Entertainment business domains subject to this governing body\'s authority (e.g., \'Athlete,Broadcast,Fan,Finance\' for GDPR; \'Athlete\' for WADA; \'Broadcast\' for FCC). Drives compliance scope mapping.']
  applicable_framework varchar [note: 'Primary regulatory framework, statute, or standard enforced by this body (e.g., GDPR, CCPA, PCI-DSS, SOX, WADA Code, ADA Title III, ISO 20121, CBA). Used to map compliance obligations to the correct authority.']
  arbitration_authority boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this body serves as a dispute resolution or arbitration authority for Sports Entertainment\'s legal and regulatory disputes (e.g., True for CAS — Court of Arbitration for Sport).']
  audit_cycle varchar [note: 'Frequency at which this governing body conducts or requires compliance audits, inspections, or assessments of Sports Entertainment\'s operations (e.g., annual for SOX/PCI-DSS, event_triggered for WADA testing, continuous for GDPR supervisory monitoring).']
  body_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the governing body by its organizational nature: sports_federation (FIFA, IOC, NFL, NBA, MLB, NHL, MLS), regulatory_agency (FCC, SEC, OSHA, ADA), standards_body (PCI SSC, ISO, WADA), arbitration_body (CAS), government_agency, or certification_body.']
  broadcast_regulatory_authority boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this governing body regulates Sports Entertainment\'s broadcast, OTT streaming, or media rights distribution activities (e.g., True for FCC which governs broadcast licensing and content standards).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this governing body record was first created in the Sports Entertainment compliance reference master (Silver Layer). Supports data lineage and audit trail requirements.']
  data_privacy_authority boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this governing body is a data protection supervisory authority with jurisdiction over Sports Entertainment\'s fan data processing activities (e.g., True for GDPR supervisory authorities, CCPA enforcement via California AG).']
  derecognition_date date [note: 'Date on which Sports Entertainment\'s obligation to or recognition of this governing body ceased. Null if the relationship is still active.']
  enforcement_authority_level varchar [note: 'Indicates the legal enforceability of this body\'s directives on Sports Entertainment: binding (mandatory legal compliance), advisory (recommended best practice), voluntary (opt-in participation), or conditional (binding only under specific circumstances such as event hosting).']
  filing_deadline_month varchar [note: 'Calendar month (1–12) by which annual regulatory filings, reports, or certifications must be submitted to this governing body (e.g., month 3 for SEC annual 10-K, month 12 for PCI-DSS Report on Compliance).']
  founding_year varchar [note: 'Calendar year in which the governing body was officially established or chartered (e.g., 1904 for FIFA, 1999 for WADA). Supports historical compliance context and due diligence.']
  framework_effective_date date [note: 'Date on which the current version of the regulatory framework or standard became effective and binding on Sports Entertainment.']
  framework_version varchar [note: 'Current version or edition of the regulatory framework, code, or standard enforced by this body that Sports Entertainment is obligated to comply with (e.g., WADA Code 2021, PCI-DSS v4.0, ISO 20121:2012, GDPR 2016/679).']
  governing_body_name varchar [note: 'Full official name of the governing body or regulatory authority (e.g., Fédération Internationale de Football Association, World Anti-Doping Agency).']
  governing_body_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the governing body record indicating whether Sports Entertainment is actively subject to its authority: active (current compliance obligation), inactive (no current obligation), suspended, dissolved, or under_review.']
  hq_city varchar [note: 'City where the governing body\'s principal headquarters is located (e.g., Zurich for FIFA, Montreal for WADA, Lausanne for CAS/IOC, Washington D.C. for FCC).']
  hq_country varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the country where the governing body\'s principal headquarters is physically located (e.g., CHE for FIFA/WADA/CAS in Switzerland, USA for FCC/SEC/OSHA).']
  internal_compliance_owner varchar [note: 'Name or employee identifier of the Sports Entertainment compliance officer or team lead responsible for managing the relationship and obligations with this governing body.']
  internal_legal_owner varchar [note: 'Name or employee identifier of the Sports Entertainment legal counsel responsible for regulatory correspondence, filings, and dispute management with this governing body.']
  jurisdiction_region varchar [note: 'Named geographic region or multi-country jurisdiction where the body\'s authority applies (e.g., European Union for GDPR supervisory authorities, North America for NHL Board of Governors). Complements primary_jurisdiction_country for supra-national bodies.']
  jurisdiction_scope varchar [note: 'Geographic scope of the governing body\'s authority: global (FIFA, IOC, WADA, CAS, PCI SSC), regional (GDPR supervisory authorities), national (FCC, SEC, OSHA, ADA, CCPA), state_provincial, or local.']
  last_audit_date date [note: 'Date of the most recently completed compliance audit or assessment conducted by or on behalf of this governing body against Sports Entertainment.']
  last_audit_outcome varchar [note: 'Result of the most recently completed compliance audit by this governing body: pass (fully compliant), pass_with_findings (compliant with minor remediation required), fail (non-compliant), in_progress, or not_applicable.']
  max_penalty_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum financial penalty (in the currency specified by penalty_currency_code) that this governing body can impose on Sports Entertainment per violation or incident (e.g., GDPR Article 83 up to €20M or 4% of global turnover).']
  membership_number varchar [note: 'External membership number, registration code, or license identifier assigned by the governing body to Sports Entertainment (e.g., FIFA club registration number, PCI-DSS merchant ID, FCC license number).']
  membership_status varchar [note: 'Sports Entertainment\'s formal membership or affiliation status with this governing body: member (full voting member), associate_member, observer, non_member (subject to regulation but not a member), or applicant.']
  next_audit_date date [note: 'Date of the next scheduled compliance audit, inspection, or assessment by this governing body. Used by the compliance team to prepare documentation and evidence packages.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for compliance team annotations, special conditions, escalation history, or contextual information about Sports Entertainment\'s relationship with this governing body that does not fit structured fields.']
  official_website_url varchar [note: 'Official public website URL of the governing body, used for regulatory reference, framework documentation retrieval, and compliance team research.']
  penalty_authority boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this governing body has the authority to impose financial penalties, sanctions, suspensions, or other punitive measures directly on Sports Entertainment or its athletes/operations.']
  penalty_currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the denomination of the maximum penalty amount (e.g., USD, EUR, CHF).']
  primary_contact_email varchar [note: 'Official email address of the primary liaison at the governing body for compliance correspondence, regulatory filings, and audit communications.']
  primary_contact_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the primary liaison or compliance contact person at the governing body with whom Sports Entertainment\'s compliance team interacts for regulatory matters.']
  primary_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Direct phone number for the primary liaison at the governing body, used for urgent compliance escalations and regulatory inquiries.']
  primary_contact_title varchar [note: 'Official job title of the primary contact at the governing body (e.g., Director of Compliance, Chief Regulatory Officer, Enforcement Counsel).']
  primary_jurisdiction_country varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the primary jurisdiction where the governing body is headquartered or holds primary legal authority (e.g., CHE for Switzerland-based FIFA/WADA/CAS, USA for FCC/SEC/OSHA).']
  recognition_date date [note: 'Date on which Sports Entertainment formally recognized or became subject to this governing body\'s authority, regulations, or standards.']
  regulatory_domain varchar [note: 'Primary compliance or regulatory domain this body governs (e.g., anti-doping, data_privacy, broadcast_regulation, financial_reporting, venue_safety, payment_security, sport_governance, labor_relations, accessibility, event_sustainability). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: anti_doping|data_privacy|broadcast_regulation|financial_reporting|venue_safety|payment_security|sport_governance|labor_relations|accessibility|event_sustainability — promote to reference product]']
  reporting_obligation boolean [note: 'Indicates whether Sports Entertainment has a mandatory periodic reporting or filing obligation to this governing body (e.g., True for SEC, SOX, FCC license renewals; False for advisory bodies).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this governing body record. Used for change tracking, data freshness monitoring, and compliance audit trails.']

  Note: 'Reference master for all sports and entertainment governing bodies and regulatory authorities that Sports Entertainment must comply with — FIFA, IOC, NFL League Office, NBA League Office, WADA, CAS, FCC, SEC, OSHA, ADA, GDPR supervisory authorities, PCI SSC, SOX auditors, ISO 20121 certifiers. Captures body name, acronym, jurisdiction, regulatory domain, contact authority, and applicable frameworks.'
}

Table compliance.obligation {
  obligation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for obligation']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Compliance obligations (WADA testing programs, data privacy compliance, broadcast regulatory requirements) have ongoing costs charged to specific cost centers. This FK enables compliance program cost ']
  jurisdiction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to gaming.jurisdiction. Business justification: Compliance obligations (AML thresholds, deposit limit mandates, reporting frequencies) are jurisdiction-specific in sports-entertainment gaming. The plain `jurisdiction` text field is a denormalizatio']
  league_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to league.league. Business justification: Compliance obligations (CBA reporting obligations, regulatory filing requirements) are scoped to specific leagues. League-level obligation tracking, compliance calendar management, and regulatory repo']
  obligation_governing_body_id varchar [note: 'The official identifier or article/section reference assigned by the governing body to this obligation (e.g., GDPR Article 17, WADA Code Article 2.1, PCI-DSS Requirement 6.3). Enables precise cross-referencing with official regulatory documentation.']
  obligation_governing_body_reference_governing_body_id varchar [note: 'FK to compliance.governing_body']
  applicability_scope varchar [note: 'The operational scope within Sports Entertainment to which this obligation applies (e.g., global for GDPR, athlete for WADA anti-doping, venue for ADA accessibility, broadcast for FCC regulations, digital for CCPA). Determines which business units and systems are in scope. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: global|regional|national|league|venue|event|athlete|broadcast|digital — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  compliance_category varchar [note: 'High-level thematic category grouping obligations by compliance domain (e.g., data-privacy for GDPR/CCPA, anti-doping for WADA, payment-security for PCI-DSS, accessibility for ADA, financial-reporting for SOX). Enables portfolio-level compliance reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: data-privacy|anti-doping|payment-security|accessibility|financial-reporting|workplace-safety|broadcast-regulation|athlete-eligibility|arbitration|licensing|event-safety — promote to reference product]']
  control_framework_mapping varchar [note: 'Reference to the internal or industry control framework control identifier that addresses this obligation (e.g., ISO 27001 control A.18.1, NIST CSF PR.DS-1, COSO control activity). Enables integrated compliance and risk management reporting.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this compliance obligation record was first created in the system. Provides audit trail for record provenance and supports data lineage in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  effective_date date [note: 'The date on which this compliance obligation becomes legally or contractually binding on Sports Entertainment. Marks the start of the enforcement window and triggers compliance program activation.']
  enforcement_consequence varchar [note: 'Description of the regulatory, legal, or financial consequences of non-compliance with this obligation (e.g., \'GDPR fine up to 4% of global annual turnover\', \'WADA athlete suspension up to 4 years\', \'PCI-DSS card processing suspension\'). Critical for risk prioritization.']
  escalation_threshold_days varchar [note: 'Number of days before the next_review_date or reporting deadline at which an automated escalation alert is triggered to senior compliance leadership. Ensures timely intervention before regulatory deadlines are missed.']
  evidence_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether formal documentary evidence (audit logs, test results, certifications, filings) must be collected and retained to demonstrate compliance with this obligation. Drives evidence management workflows in the compliance program.']
  evidence_retention_years varchar [note: 'Number of years that compliance evidence and documentation for this obligation must be retained per regulatory or legal requirements. Drives records management policies and data lifecycle governance.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'The date on which this compliance obligation ceases to be binding, if applicable. Null for open-ended or perpetual obligations. Used to trigger renewal workflows and archive expired obligations.']
  is_mandatory boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this obligation is legally or contractually mandatory (True) versus voluntary or best-practice (False). Mandatory obligations trigger formal compliance tracking, audit evidence collection, and regulatory reporting workflows.']
  is_recurring boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this obligation recurs on a defined schedule (True) or is a one-time requirement (False). Recurring obligations automatically generate future compliance tasks based on the review_frequency.']
  last_audit_date date [note: 'Date on which the most recent formal audit or compliance assessment was conducted against this obligation. Provides audit trail continuity and supports gap analysis between audit cycles.']
  last_audit_outcome varchar [note: 'Result of the most recent formal audit or compliance assessment for this obligation. Drives remediation planning, risk scoring updates, and regulatory reporting of compliance posture.']
  last_reviewed_date date [note: 'Date on which this obligation record was most recently formally reviewed and confirmed by the responsible compliance officer or legal counsel. Provides audit trail for obligation currency.']
  next_review_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next formal review of this obligation to assess continued applicability, regulatory changes, and compliance posture. Drives the compliance calendar and review task scheduling.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for compliance officers and legal counsel to record contextual notes, interpretive guidance, exception rationale, or correspondence summaries related to this obligation. Supports nuanced compliance management beyond structured fields.']
  obligation_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known, human-readable unique code assigned to this compliance obligation (e.g., OBL-GDPR-20240001). Used as the business identifier across regulatory filings, audit reports, and cross-system references.']
  obligation_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative describing the full scope, requirements, and expectations of the compliance obligation as imposed by the governing body or regulation. Provides context for compliance officers and legal counsel.']
  obligation_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the compliance obligation within the organization\'s compliance program. Active obligations are currently enforceable; expired obligations have passed their end date; waived obligations have been formally exempted by the governing body.']
  obligation_type varchar [note: 'Categorical classification of the obligation by its nature of required action (e.g., reporting, testing, audit, certification, training, disclosure, filing, monitoring). Drives workflow routing and task assignment. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: reporting|testing|audit|certification|training|disclosure|filing|monitoring|policy-enforcement|eligibility-review — promote to reference product]']
  penalty_currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the maximum penalty amount (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Required for multi-jurisdictional obligations where penalties are denominated in different currencies.']
  penalty_max_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum monetary penalty that can be imposed for non-compliance with this obligation, expressed in the currency specified by penalty_currency_code. Used for risk quantification, insurance coverage assessment, and executive risk reporting.']
  regulatory_article_reference varchar [note: 'Specific article, section, clause, or requirement number within the regulatory framework that mandates this obligation (e.g., \'GDPR Article 17 - Right to Erasure\', \'PCI-DSS Requirement 3.4\', \'WADA Code Article 2.1\'). Enables precise legal cross-referencing.']
  regulatory_framework varchar [note: 'The primary regulatory or governance framework that imposes this obligation (e.g., GDPR, CCPA, PCI-DSS, WADA, ADA, SOX, OSHA, CBA). Drives classification, reporting cadence, and enforcement consequences. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: GDPR|CCPA|PCI-DSS|WADA|ADA|SOX|OSHA|CBA|FIFA|IOC|NFL|NBA|MLB|NHL|FCC|SEC|ISO-20121|CAS — promote to reference product]']
  remediation_due_date date [note: 'Target date by which any identified compliance gaps or non-conformances related to this obligation must be remediated. Populated when last_audit_outcome is non-compliant or partially-compliant.']
  reporting_deadline_day varchar [note: 'Day of the month (1-31) by which periodic compliance reports or filings must be submitted to the governing body. Combined with reporting_deadline_month to define the annual filing deadline for obligations with fixed calendar deadlines.']
  reporting_deadline_month varchar [note: 'Month of the year (1-12) by which periodic compliance reports or filings must be submitted to the governing body. Combined with reporting_deadline_day to define the annual filing deadline.']
  responsible_business_unit varchar [note: 'The internal business unit, department, or division that is the primary owner accountable for fulfilling this compliance obligation (e.g., Legal, Finance, IT Security, Athlete Management, Venue Operations). Drives accountability assignment and escalation paths.']
  review_frequency varchar [note: 'How often this compliance obligation must be formally reviewed and assessed for continued compliance (e.g., annual for SOX controls, quarterly for PCI-DSS, ad-hoc for CAS arbitration cases). Drives the compliance calendar. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: daily|weekly|monthly|quarterly|semi-annual|annual|ad-hoc — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  risk_level varchar [note: 'Assessed risk severity of non-compliance with this obligation, based on likelihood of breach and magnitude of consequence. Drives prioritization of compliance resources, audit scheduling, and executive escalation thresholds.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Operational system of record from which this obligation record was originated or is primarily managed (e.g., SAP S/4HANA for SOX financial controls, Salesforce CRM for sponsor compliance, Manual for WADA program entries). Supports data lineage and silver-layer provenance tracking.']
  third_party_involved boolean [note: 'Indicates whether fulfillment of this obligation involves or requires oversight of a third-party vendor, partner, or service provider (True). Triggers third-party risk management workflows and vendor compliance assessment requirements.']
  third_party_name varchar [note: 'Name of the primary third-party vendor, partner, or service provider involved in fulfilling this obligation (e.g., WADA-accredited testing laboratory, PCI-DSS Qualified Security Assessor, GDPR data processor). Populated only when third_party_involved is True.']
  title varchar [note: 'Short, human-readable title summarizing the compliance obligation (e.g., \'Annual WADA Anti-Doping Testing Program\', \'GDPR Data Subject Access Request Fulfillment\'). Used in dashboards, reports, and regulatory correspondence.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this compliance obligation record was most recently modified. Supports change tracking, audit trails, and incremental data pipeline processing in the Databricks Silver Layer.']

  Note: 'Master catalog of all compliance obligations imposed on Sports Entertainment by governing bodies, regulations, and internal policies. Each obligation record captures the regulatory framework (GDPR, CCPA, PCI-DSS, WADA, ADA, SOX, OSHA, CBA), obligation type, effective date, expiry date, responsible business unit, enforcement consequence, review frequency, and linked governing body. Acts as the SSOT for what the organization is required to do.'
}

Table compliance.policy {
  policy_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each compliance policy record in the Sports Entertainment enterprise.']
  governing_body_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.governing_body. Business justification: The policy record has governing_body as a free-text string field. Replacing this with a FK to the governing_body reference master normalizes the relationship and enables governing body-level policy ma']
  jurisdiction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to gaming.jurisdiction. Business justification: Responsible gaming policies, AML policies, and data privacy policies are jurisdiction-specific in sports-entertainment gaming operations. The plain `jurisdiction` text field is a denormalization of ga']
  league_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to league.league. Business justification: Compliance policies (CBA policies, anti-doping policies, integrity policies, conduct codes) are scoped to specific leagues. League-specific policy management, attestation tracking, and policy gap anal']
  parent_policy_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing identifier pointing to a parent policy when this policy is a sub-policy or addendum of a higher-level framework policy (e.g., a regional GDPR addendum referencing the master Data Privacy Policy).']
  primary_superseded_by_policy_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the newer policy version that replaces this record when status is \'superseded\'. Enables version chain traceability for regulatory audit purposes.']
  ada_section_ref varchar [note: 'Specific ADA title and section referenced by this accessibility policy (e.g., \'Title III Section 302 — Public Accommodations\'). Populated only for venue accessibility policies.']
  applicability_domain varchar [note: 'Primary data or business domain to which this policy applies, aligned to Sports Entertainment\'s defined data domains. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: event|athlete|venue|broadcast|ticketing|fan|sponsorship|merchandise|league|content|finance|workforce|digital_platform|all — promote to reference product]']
  approval_authority varchar [note: 'Name or title of the individual, committee, or governing body that has authority to approve this policy (e.g., \'Board of Directors\', \'Chief Legal Officer\', \'Compliance Committee\').']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date on which the policy was formally approved by the designated approval authority. Required for regulatory audit evidence and SOX controls documentation.']
  attestation_frequency_months varchar [note: 'Frequency in months at which in-scope personnel must re-attest compliance with this policy (e.g., 12 for annual attestation). Null if requires_attestation is False.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this policy record was first created in the compliance management system. Supports audit trail and data lineage requirements.']
  data_classification varchar [note: 'Data classification level of the policy document itself, governing who may access and distribute it. Aligned to Sports Entertainment\'s data classification framework.']
  document_url varchar [note: 'Secure URL pointing to the authoritative policy document stored in the enterprise document management system or intranet (e.g., SharePoint, Dalet Galaxy DAM). Provides direct access to the full policy text.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which this version of the policy becomes binding and enforceable across Sports Entertainment operations.']
  enforcement_mechanism varchar [note: 'Primary method by which compliance with this policy is enforced (e.g., automated system controls, manual audit procedures, mandatory training attestation, contractual clauses with vendors/athletes).']
  exception_process_description varchar [note: 'Description of the formal process by which an exception or waiver to this policy may be requested and approved, including the approving authority and required documentation.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which this version of the policy ceases to be enforceable. Null for open-ended policies. Used to trigger review workflows before expiration.']
  fcc_rule_ref varchar [note: 'Specific FCC regulation or rule number referenced by this broadcast content policy (e.g., \'47 CFR Part 73 — Broadcast Content Standards\'). Populated only for broadcast content policies.']
  gdpr_lawful_basis varchar [note: 'GDPR Article 6 lawful basis for processing personal data under this policy. Populated only for data privacy policies applicable to EU residents.']
  is_mandatory boolean [note: 'Indicates whether compliance with this policy is mandatory (True) for all in-scope personnel and systems, or advisory/recommended (False). Mandatory policies require formal attestation.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this policy record in the compliance management system. Required for change audit trails under SOX and PCI-DSS.']
  last_reviewed_date date [note: 'Date on which the most recent formal review of this policy was completed and documented. Required for regulatory audit evidence.']
  next_review_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next mandatory policy review, calculated from the last review date plus the review cycle. Used to trigger compliance calendar alerts and owner notifications.']
  owner_department varchar [note: 'Organizational department or business unit responsible for policy ownership (e.g., \'Legal & Compliance\', \'Data Privacy Office\', \'Finance\', \'Venue Operations\').']
  pci_dss_requirement_ref varchar [note: 'Specific PCI-DSS requirement number(s) addressed by this policy (e.g., \'Requirement 3.4 — Protect Stored Cardholder Data\'). Populated only for payment security policies.']
  penalty_description varchar [note: 'Description of sanctions, fines, or disciplinary consequences for non-compliance with this policy (e.g., \'GDPR fines up to 4% of global annual turnover\', \'WADA 4-year ban\', \'PCI-DSS card brand fines\').']
  policy_category varchar [note: 'Secondary classification indicating whether the policy is driven by external regulatory mandate, internal governance decision, contractual obligation (e.g., CBA), or voluntary industry standard adoption.']
  policy_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the policy across all governing systems (e.g., POL-GDPR-20240001). Used in regulatory filings, audit trails, and cross-system references.']
  policy_name varchar [note: 'Official human-readable title of the compliance policy (e.g., \'GDPR Data Subject Rights Policy\', \'WADA Anti-Doping Program Policy\', \'PCI-DSS Cardholder Data Protection Policy\').']
  policy_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the policy. \'draft\' indicates the policy is being authored; \'active\' means it is currently enforced; \'under_review\' means it is being revised; \'superseded\' means a newer version has replaced it; \'retired\' means it is no longer applicable; \'suspended\' means enforcement is temporarily paused.']
  policy_type varchar [note: 'High-level classification of the policy by compliance domain. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: data_privacy|anti_doping|payment_security|venue_accessibility|broadcast_content|anti_corruption|conduct_and_ethics|labor_relations — promote to reference product]']
  regulatory_obligation_ref varchar [note: 'Specific regulatory article, section, or requirement that mandates this policy (e.g., \'GDPR Article 5\', \'PCI-DSS Requirement 12.1\', \'WADA Code Article 2.1\', \'ADA Title III Section 302\'). Links the policy to its external legal obligation.']
  requires_attestation boolean [note: 'Indicates whether in-scope employees, athletes, or contractors must formally attest (sign off) that they have read and understood this policy. Drives attestation workflow in SAP SuccessFactors.']
  review_cycle_months varchar [note: 'Mandatory review frequency expressed in months (e.g., 12 for annual, 6 for semi-annual). Drives automated review scheduling and compliance calendar entries.']
  risk_level varchar [note: 'Assessed risk level associated with non-compliance with this policy. \'critical\' indicates potential for severe regulatory sanctions, reputational damage, or operational shutdown; \'high\' indicates significant financial or legal exposure.']
  scope_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the organizational scope of the policy — which business units, geographies, employee populations, systems, or operations are covered (e.g., \'Applies to all fan-facing digital platforms processing EU resident data\').']
  training_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether mandatory training must be completed by in-scope personnel as a condition of policy compliance. Drives training assignment in SAP SuccessFactors.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Semantic version identifier for the policy document (e.g., \'1.0\', \'2.3\', \'3.0.1\'). Incremented on each approved revision to support version-controlled audit trails and regulatory submissions.']
  wada_article_ref varchar [note: 'Specific WADA Code article number referenced by this anti-doping policy (e.g., \'Article 2.1 — Presence of Prohibited Substance\'). Populated only for anti-doping policies.']

  Note: 'Master record of all internal compliance policies and codes of conduct enforced across Sports Entertainment — data privacy policies (GDPR/CCPA), anti-doping policies (WADA), payment security policies (PCI-DSS), venue accessibility policies (ADA), broadcast content policies (FCC), anti-corruption policies, and CBA-mandated conduct rules. Captures policy name, version, effective date, owner, approval authority, review cycle, and linked regulatory obligation.'
}

  fiscal_period_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.fiscal_period.   actual_start_date date [note: 'Date on which audit fieldwork or inspection actually commenced. Compared against scheduled_start_date to measure planning accuracy and identify scheduling slippage.']
  attestation_of_compliance_issued boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a formal Attestation of Compliance (AOC) or equivalent compliance certificate has been issued as an output of this audit engagement. Particularly relevant for PCI-DSS QSA audits and ISO certification audits.']

  audit_report_reference varchar [note: 'Document reference number or DAM (Digital Asset Management) identifier for the formal audit report stored in the content management system. Enables retrieval of the full report for regulatory review, legal proceedings, or CAS arbitration.']
  audit_subtype varchar [note: 'Further classification within the audit type (e.g., for PCI-DSS: QSA on-site, SAQ-D self-assessment; for SOX: internal controls over financial reporting, IT general controls; for OSHA: routine inspection, incident-triggered). Provides granular categorisation for analytics and benchmarking.']
  audit_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the audit engagement by governing framework or regulatory program. Determines the applicable standards, scope templates, and reporting obligations. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: PCI_DSS|SOX|OSHA|ADA|GDPR|WADA|ISO_20121|CCPA|internal — promote to reference product]']
  auditor_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the auditing party as internal (in-house audit team), external (independent audit firm or QSA), regulatory (government or governing body inspector), or third_party (vendor/partner audit). Affects independence requirements and report distribution.']
  certification_expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which the compliance certification or attestation resulting from this audit expires and requires renewal (e.g., PCI-DSS AOC valid for 12 months, ISO 20121 certification cycle). Drives renewal scheduling and forward pipeline management.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the audit engagement record was first created in the compliance system. Aligns with RECORD_AUDIT_CREATED canonical category for TRANSACTION_HEADER role. Supports data lineage and audit trail requirements.']
  critical_findings_count varchar [note: 'Number of audit findings classified as critical or high severity, requiring immediate remediation. Subset of total_findings_count. Drives escalation protocols and executive reporting.']
  cycle_frequency varchar [note: 'Recurrence cadence of this audit engagement type as mandated by the governing framework or internal policy (e.g., PCI-DSS QSA annual, OSHA routine quarterly, WADA ad_hoc). Drives scheduling and forward pipeline planning.']
  data_privacy_in_scope boolean [note: 'Indicates whether personal data processing activities (fan data, athlete data, employee data) are within the scope of this audit engagement, triggering GDPR/CCPA-specific audit procedures and data protection officer involvement.']
  engagement_reference varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric reference code assigned to the audit engagement at initiation (e.g., AUD-2024-00123). Used in correspondence with auditors, regulators, and management. Aligns with the BUSINESS_IDENTIFIER canonical category for TRANSACTION_HEADER role.']
  engagement_risk_rating varchar [note: 'Pre-audit risk rating assigned during planning to prioritise audit resources and determine scope depth. Based on inherent risk of the business unit, regulatory exposure, and prior audit history. Aligns with risk-based audit planning methodology.']
  engagement_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the audit engagement, progressing from scheduled through in_progress to completed. Enables forward-looking pipeline management and historical audit trail reporting. Aligns with LIFECYCLE_STATUS canonical category.']
  estimated_remediation_cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'Estimated financial cost in USD to remediate all open findings identified during the audit engagement. Used for budgeting, EBITDA impact assessment, and prioritisation of remediation investments. Populated post-report issuance.']
  final_disposition varchar [note: 'Ultimate resolution outcome of the audit engagement after all findings have been addressed. Indicates whether the engagement was fully closed with remediation, risk accepted by management, escalated to senior leadership, or referred to a regulatory body.']
  internal_audit_owner varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the internal audit manager or compliance officer responsible for coordinating and owning this audit engagement on behalf of Sports Entertainment. Accountable for scheduling, evidence collection, and management response coordination.']
  jurisdiction varchar [note: 'Geographic jurisdiction or regulatory territory applicable to this audit engagement (e.g., USA, GBR, EU, global). Determines which data privacy laws (GDPR vs CCPA), labour regulations (OSHA vs local), and broadcasting regulations (FCC vs Ofcom) apply.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the audit engagement record was most recently modified. Aligns with RECORD_AUDIT_UPDATED canonical category for TRANSACTION_HEADER role. Supports change tracking and Silver layer incremental processing.']
  lead_auditor_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the lead auditor responsible for conducting and signing off the audit engagement. May be an internal audit manager or an external QSA/firm representative. Retained for accountability and regulatory record-keeping.']
  management_response_due_date date [note: 'Deadline by which management must submit formal responses to audit findings, including remediation plans and corrective action commitments. Tracked for SLA compliance and escalation management.']
  management_response_received boolean [note: 'Indicates whether management has submitted their formal response to audit findings within the required timeframe. Tracks SLA adherence for the management response process and triggers escalation when overdue.']
  open_findings_count varchar [note: 'Number of audit findings that remain open and unresolved at the time of record update. Tracks remediation progress and supports KPI reporting on compliance posture.']
  overall_opinion varchar [note: 'The auditor\'s formal overall opinion or conclusion on the subject matter of the audit engagement. For PCI-DSS: compliant/non_compliant; for SOX: unqualified/qualified/adverse/disclaimer; for OSHA/ADA: pass/fail mapped to compliant/non_compliant. Null until report is issued.']
  payment_systems_in_scope boolean [note: 'Indicates whether payment card processing systems (POS terminals, e-commerce platforms, ticketing payment flows) are within the scope of this audit, triggering PCI-DSS specific audit procedures and QSA involvement.']
  regulatory_submission_date date [note: 'Date on which the audit report or compliance attestation was formally submitted to the relevant regulatory authority or governing body. Null when regulatory_submission_required is false or submission is pending.']
  regulatory_submission_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the audit report or findings must be formally submitted to a regulatory authority or governing body (e.g., SEC filing for SOX, FCC for broadcast compliance, WADA for anti-doping program results). Drives submission workflow and deadline tracking.']
  report_issuance_date date [note: 'Date on which the formal audit report was issued to management and relevant stakeholders. Triggers the management response clock and regulatory submission deadlines.']
  scheduled_end_date date [note: 'Planned date on which the audit fieldwork or inspection is scheduled to conclude. Used alongside scheduled_start_date for duration planning and resource scheduling.']
  scheduled_start_date date [note: 'Planned date on which the audit fieldwork or inspection is scheduled to commence. Used for forward-looking audit pipeline management and resource allocation. Aligns with BUSINESS_EVENT_TIMESTAMP canonical category (planned dimension).']
  scope_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the audit scope, defining the boundaries of the engagement including systems, processes, locations, time periods, and populations in scope. Documented at planning stage and may be refined during execution.']
  target_business_unit varchar [note: 'The organisational business unit, division, or department that is the primary subject of this audit engagement (e.g., Venue Operations, Broadcast & Media Rights, Ticketing, Fan Engagement, Finance). Enables domain-level compliance reporting and resource planning.']
  total_findings_count varchar [note: 'Total number of audit findings, observations, or deficiencies identified during the engagement across all severity levels. Provides a headline metric for management reporting and trend analysis.']
  wada_program_in_scope boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the WADA anti-doping program, including testing pools, whereabouts compliance, and Therapeutic Use Exemption (TUE) administration, is within the scope of this audit engagement.']

  Note: 'Complete audit lifecycle record from planning through execution and disposition for all compliance audits — PCI-DSS QSA audits, SOX internal controls, OSHA venue safety inspections, ADA accessibility audits, GDPR data protection audits, WADA program audits, and ISO 20121 sustainability audits. Captures planned audit details (audit type, scheduled date, auditor assignment, scope, target business unit, venue/system in scope, cycle frequency, linked obligation) and execution details (actual start/end dates, lead auditor, audit firm, findings count, overall opinion, report issuance date, management response due date, final disposition). Status progression from scheduled through in-progress to completed enables both forward-looking audit pipeline management and historical audit trail.'
}

Table compliance.audit_finding {
  audit_finding_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each individual audit finding or observation raised during a compliance audit engagement.']
  audit_engagement_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent audit engagement under which this finding was raised. Links the finding to its originating audit program.']
  compliance_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.obligation. Business justification: An audit finding documents non-conformance against a specific compliance obligation. Linking audit_finding to the obligation enables obligation-level finding tracking, repeat finding analysis, and rem']
  deal_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sponsorship.deal. Business justification: Audit findings in sports entertainment are routinely mapped to specific sponsorship deals: SOX revenue recognition findings, exclusivity violation findings, governing body compliance findings. audit_f']
  facility_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to venue.facility. Business justification: Audit findings are scoped to specific facilities (e.g., fire safety finding at Arena A vs. Practice Facility B). audit_finding has affected_business_process as plain text but no facility FK — linking ']
  franchise_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to league.franchise. Business justification: Audit findings are frequently directed at specific franchises (salary cap violations, roster compliance failures). Franchise-level compliance reporting and remediation tracking require direct franchis']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Financial audit findings in sports entertainment (e.g., revenue recognition errors on broadcast rights, improper player salary accruals) reference specific GL accounts. SOX control deficiency reportin']
  investigation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.investigation. Business justification: Critical or repeat audit findings often trigger formal investigations. This is a standard compliance escalation path. audit_finding currently has incident_report_id but no investigation_id. Adding thi']
  journal_entry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.journal_entry. Business justification: Internal audit findings citing specific journal entries as evidence of control failures (e.g., unsupported manual entries, bypassed approval workflows) require this FK. evidence_reference is a text fi']
