  policy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.policy. Business justification: An audit finding may document a violation of a specific internal policy. Linking audit_finding to the policy enables policy-level finding analysis and supports policy effectiveness measurement and rev']
  safety_incident_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to venue.safety_incident. Business justification: Audit findings frequently reference specific safety incidents as evidence or subject matter (e.g., finding that a safety incident revealed a control gap). Linking audit_finding to safety_incident supp']
  season_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to league.season. Business justification: Audit findings are season-scoped (salary cap violations, CBA compliance findings for a specific season). Season-level compliance reporting and year-over-year finding trend analysis require direct seas']
  security_incident_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to security.security_incident. Business justification: Compliance audit findings related to security control failures (access control gaps, credential misuse, screening failures) reference the specific security incident that exposed the control weakness. ']
  accepted_risk_approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the senior executive or risk committee member who formally approved the risk acceptance decision. Required for audit trail integrity under SOX and PCI-DSS.']
  accepted_risk_expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which the risk acceptance decision expires and must be reviewed and re-approved. Ensures time-bounded risk acceptance in line with PCI-DSS and SOX governance requirements.']
  accepted_risk_rationale varchar [note: 'Documented management rationale for formally accepting the residual risk associated with this finding rather than remediating it. Required when finding_status is \'accepted-risk\'. Must be approved by an authorised risk owner per governance policy.']
  actual_remediation_date date [note: 'Date on which remediation was actually completed and confirmed by the finding owner. Null if remediation is not yet complete. Used to calculate remediation cycle time and SLA adherence.']
  affected_business_process varchar [note: 'Core business process impacted by the finding (e.g., Ticket Sales and Revenue Management, Fan Engagement, Broadcast Rights Distribution, Venue Operations). Aligns findings to the business process taxonomy for operational risk reporting.']
  affected_system varchar [note: 'Name of the operational system, application, or platform where the non-conformance was identified (e.g., SAP S/4HANA FI, Ticketmaster, Salesforce CRM, Shopify Plus POS). Enables system-level risk aggregation.']
  assigned_owner varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the individual or team accountable for remediating this finding. Typically a business unit leader or compliance officer responsible for the affected control area.']
  audit_period_end date [note: 'End date of the audit period during which this finding was identified. Together with audit_period_start, defines the scope window for the audit engagement.']
  audit_period_start date [note: 'Start date of the audit period during which this finding was identified. Enables temporal scoping of findings to specific fiscal or compliance review periods.']
  auditor_name varchar [note: 'Name of the lead auditor (internal or external) who identified and documented this finding. Required for audit trail accountability and regulatory reporting.']
  auditor_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the auditor who raised the finding: internal (in-house compliance/audit team), external (third-party audit firm such as a PCI QSA or SOX external auditor), or regulatory (government or governing body inspector).']
  control_domain varchar [note: 'Functional control area to which the finding belongs (e.g., Access Control, Data Privacy, Financial Reporting, Venue Safety, Anti-Doping, Payment Processing). Enables domain-level aggregation of findings for risk reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Access Control|Data Privacy|Financial Reporting|Venue Safety|Anti-Doping|Payment Processing|Broadcast Compliance|Athlete Eligibility — promote to reference product]']
  control_reference varchar [note: 'Specific control identifier from the applicable regulatory framework or internal control catalogue that was found to be non-conformant (e.g., PCI-DSS Req 8.2.1, SOX ITGC-04, GDPR Art.32). Enables traceability to the control library.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp recording when this audit finding record was first created in the compliance management system. Supports audit trail integrity and data lineage requirements.']
  escalation_date date [note: 'Date on which the finding was formally escalated to senior management, the audit committee, or a regulatory authority. Null if no escalation has occurred.']
  escalation_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this finding requires escalation to senior management, the audit committee, or a regulatory body due to its severity, regulatory implications, or overdue remediation status.']
  evidence_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier or location of the supporting audit evidence (e.g., document ID, DAM asset reference, screenshot log path) that substantiates the finding. Supports audit trail completeness and regulatory defensibility.']
  finding_category varchar [note: 'Severity classification of the finding per standard audit grading: critical (immediate risk/breach), major (significant non-conformance), minor (limited non-conformance), or observation (improvement opportunity with no current non-conformance).']
  finding_reference varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric reference code assigned to this finding for cross-system tracking and regulatory correspondence (e.g., PCI-2024-F0012). Serves as the business identifier communicated to auditors and regulators.']
  finding_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the finding: open (identified, not yet actioned), in-remediation (remediation underway), closed (remediation verified complete), or accepted-risk (risk formally accepted by management with documented rationale).']
  finding_title varchar [note: 'Short, human-readable title summarising the nature of the audit finding (e.g., \'Inadequate access control review for POS terminals\'). Used in executive dashboards and regulatory reports.']
  identified_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the finding was formally identified and documented during the audit fieldwork. Represents the principal business event date for this finding.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp recording the most recent modification to this audit finding record. Enables change tracking and supports immutable audit trail requirements under PCI-DSS and SOX.']
  management_response varchar [note: 'Formal written response from management acknowledging the finding and committing to a remediation approach. Captured as part of the audit report finalisation process and required for SOX and PCI-DSS audit documentation.']
  non_conformance_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative describing the specific non-conformance or gap identified during the audit. Includes what was observed, what was expected per the control requirement, and the evidence reviewed.']
  owner_department varchar [note: 'Business department or organisational unit responsible for remediating the finding (e.g., Finance, IT Security, Venue Operations, Fan Engagement). Enables departmental accountability reporting.']
  prior_finding_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number of the original finding from a prior audit cycle if this is a repeat finding. Enables lineage tracking of recurring control deficiencies across audit periods.']
  recommended_remediation varchar [note: 'Auditor-recommended corrective or preventive action to address the non-conformance and prevent recurrence. Forms the basis of the remediation plan agreed with the finding owner.']
  regulatory_framework varchar [note: 'Primary regulatory or governance framework implicated by this finding (e.g., PCI-DSS, SOX, GDPR, CCPA, OSHA, ADA, WADA). Drives reporting obligations and remediation urgency. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: PCI-DSS|SOX|GDPR|CCPA|OSHA|ADA|WADA|ISO-20121|CAS|FCC — promote to reference product]']
  regulatory_notification_date date [note: 'Date on which the mandatory regulatory notification was submitted to the relevant governing body. Null if notification is not required or not yet submitted. Critical for GDPR 72-hour deadline compliance tracking.']
  regulatory_notification_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this finding triggers a mandatory notification obligation to a regulatory body (e.g., GDPR data breach notification to supervisory authority, PCI-DSS breach notification to card brands, OSHA incident reporting).']
  remediation_plan varchar [note: 'Management\'s documented plan for addressing the finding, including specific actions, milestones, and resources committed. May differ from the auditor\'s recommendation where management has chosen an alternative approach.']
  repeat_finding boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this finding is a recurrence of a previously identified finding from a prior audit cycle. Repeat findings signal systemic control failures and trigger escalated remediation requirements under PCI-DSS and SOX.']
  risk_impact_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the potential business, financial, legal, or reputational impact if this finding is not remediated. Supports risk-based prioritisation and executive reporting.']
  risk_rating varchar [note: 'Risk-based severity rating assigned to the finding reflecting the potential business impact if not remediated: high (material financial, legal, or reputational exposure), medium (moderate impact), low (limited impact), or informational (no current risk).']
  root_cause varchar [note: 'Documented root cause of the non-conformance as determined through analysis (e.g., process gap, technology failure, training deficiency, policy absence). Supports corrective action planning and recurrence prevention.']
  root_cause_category varchar [note: 'High-level classification of the root cause to enable trend analysis across findings: process (workflow/procedure gap), technology (system/tool failure), people (training/awareness), policy (missing/inadequate policy), third-party (vendor/partner failure), or environmental (external factor).']
  target_remediation_date date [note: 'Agreed target date by which the remediation action must be completed and verified. Used for SLA tracking and regulatory deadline management.']
  verification_date date [note: 'Date on which the auditor or compliance team independently verified that the remediation action was effectively implemented and the control gap closed. Required for formal finding closure.']
  verified_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the auditor or compliance officer who independently verified the effectiveness of the remediation action and authorised closure of the finding.']

  Note: 'Individual finding or observation raised during a compliance audit engagement. Captures finding reference, linked audit engagement, finding category (critical/major/minor/observation), regulatory framework implicated, description of non-conformance, root cause, recommended remediation, assigned owner, target remediation date, and finding status (open/in-remediation/closed/accepted-risk). Supports PCI-DSS, SOX, OSHA, ADA, and GDPR audit trails.'
}

Table compliance.doping_test {
  doping_test_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for doping_test']
  competition_round_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to event.competition_round. Business justification: WADA in-competition doping tests are conducted at specific competition rounds. Anti-doping officers must link each test to the competition round during which it was conducted — a mandatory WADA chain-']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Individual doping tests incur direct costs (laboratory fees, DCO fees, sample transport) charged to anti-doping program cost centers. Sports entertainment organizations track these per-test costs for ']
  franchise_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to league.franchise. Business justification: Doping tests are administered to athletes affiliated with specific franchises. Franchise-level anti-doping compliance reporting (tests per franchise, positive rate by franchise) is a standard league a']
  governing_body_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.governing_body. Business justification: doping_test has a `governing_body` STRING column storing the testing authority (WADA, USADA, national federation). Normalizing to governing_body_id → compliance.governing_body.governing_body_id elimin']
  league_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to league.league. Business justification: Doping tests are administered within a specific league\'s anti-doping program. League-level WADA compliance reporting and anti-doping program statistics require direct league reference; doping_test has']
  league_season_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to league.season. Business justification: In-competition doping tests are conducted within specific league seasons. Linking doping_test to season enables season-level anti-doping program reporting (tests per season, detection rates by season)']
  primary_doping_athlete_athlete_profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the athlete subject to the doping test or whereabouts filing obligation.']
  primary_doping_competition_event_fixture_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the sanctioned competition event during which an in-competition test was conducted. Null for out-of-competition tests and whereabouts records.']
  whereabouts_filing_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.whereabouts_filing. Business justification: A doping test (particularly out-of-competition testing) is conducted against a specific whereabouts filing period. The doping_test has whereabouts_filing_period as a string field. Replacing this with ']
  adams_reference varchar [note: 'Unique reference number assigned within the WADA Anti-Doping Administration and Management System (ADAMS) for cross-system traceability of the test or whereabouts filing.']
  b_sample_analysis_requested boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the athlete has formally requested analysis of the B-sample to contest an Adverse Analytical Finding (AAF) on the A-sample.']
  b_sample_result_status varchar [note: 'Outcome of the B-sample confirmatory analysis. A confirmed result corroborates the A-sample AAF; a not-confirmed result may lead to case withdrawal.']
  cas_case_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number of any Court of Arbitration for Sport (CAS) appeal or arbitration proceeding arising from this doping test result.']
  chain_of_custody_status varchar [note: 'Current integrity status of the sample chain of custody from collection through laboratory receipt. A broken chain may invalidate the test result.']
  collection_country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the jurisdiction where the sample was collected, used for jurisdictional reporting and WADA regional authority assignment.']
  collection_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the biological sample was collected from the athlete by the Doping Control Officer (DCO). Null for whereabouts-only records.']
  collection_location varchar [note: 'Descriptive name or address of the facility or venue where the sample was collected (e.g., doping control station at stadium, athlete hotel, training facility).']
  collection_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time at which the biological sample was collected, used for chain-of-custody sequencing and time-sensitive analysis windows.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this doping test or whereabouts filing record was first created in the compliance system, used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  dco_identifier varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the certified Doping Control Officer (DCO) who conducted the sample collection, used for chain-of-custody accountability and DCO performance auditing.']
  laboratory_accreditation_number varchar [note: 'WADA-issued accreditation number of the testing laboratory, confirming its authorization to conduct anti-doping analyses.']
  laboratory_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier or name of the WADA-accredited laboratory to which the sample was dispatched for analysis.']
  missed_filing_count varchar [note: 'Cumulative count of missed or incomplete whereabouts filings within the rolling 12-month window. Three strikes within 12 months constitutes an anti-doping rule violation under WADA Code Article 2.4.']
  missed_test_count varchar [note: 'Cumulative count of missed doping tests (failure to be present at declared whereabouts location) within the rolling 12-month window, contributing to the Article 2.4 violation threshold.']
  overnight_location varchar [note: 'Address or description of the athlete\'s regular overnight accommodation as declared in the whereabouts filing, used by DCOs to locate the athlete for unannounced testing.']
  prohibited_substance varchar [note: 'Name of the prohibited substance or method detected in the sample, as listed on the WADA Prohibited List. Null for negative results or whereabouts records.']
  prohibited_substance_category varchar [note: 'WADA Prohibited List category of the detected substance or method (e.g., S1 Anabolic Agents, S2 Peptide Hormones, M1 Blood Manipulation). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: S0|S1|S2|S3|S4|S5|S6|S7|S8|S9|P1|M1|M2|M3 — promote to reference product]']
  record_type varchar [note: 'Discriminator indicating whether this record represents a physical doping test event or a whereabouts filing submission. Drives which subset of fields are populated.']
  registered_testing_pool varchar [note: 'Indicates the Registered Testing Pool (RTP) tier to which the athlete belongs at the time of the test or filing, determining the applicable whereabouts and testing obligations.']
  result_notification_date date [note: 'Date on which the test result was formally communicated to the athlete and relevant Anti-Doping Organization (ADO), triggering any applicable provisional suspension timelines.']
  result_status varchar [note: 'Official analytical outcome of the doping test. Adverse Analytical Finding (AAF) indicates a prohibited substance detected; Atypical Finding (ATF) requires further investigation before classification. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: negative|adverse_analytical_finding|atypical_finding|pending|cancelled|passport_anomaly — promote to reference product]']
  sample_code_a varchar [note: 'Unique barcode or identifier assigned to the primary (A) sample container at the point of collection, used for laboratory tracking and chain-of-custody verification.']
  sample_code_b varchar [note: 'Unique barcode or identifier assigned to the reserve (B) sample container, retained for confirmatory analysis if the A-sample returns an Adverse Analytical Finding (AAF).']
  sample_type varchar [note: 'Biological matrix collected during the test. Values include urine, blood, Athlete Biological Passport (ABP) longitudinal blood sample, or dried blood spot. Null for whereabouts-only records.']
  sanction_end_date date [note: 'Date on which the period of ineligibility or sanction is scheduled to end, after which the athlete may apply for reinstatement of eligibility.']
  sanction_start_date date [note: 'Date on which the formal period of ineligibility or sanction commenced, used to calculate eligibility reinstatement dates.']
  sanction_status varchar [note: 'Current disciplinary sanction status resulting from this test record. Tracks progression from provisional suspension through formal sanction or appeal outcome.']
  sport_discipline varchar [note: 'Sport and discipline classification of the athlete at the time of the test (e.g., Athletics - 100m Sprint, Football - Outfield), used for sport-specific prohibited list application.']
  test_reference_number varchar [note: 'Externally assigned unique identifier for the doping test event, issued by the Doping Control Officer (DCO) or testing authority at the point of sample collection. Null for whereabouts-only records.']
  test_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the doping test as in-competition (conducted during or around a sanctioned event) or out-of-competition (conducted outside of event periods), per WADA definitions.']
  testing_authority varchar [note: 'Name of the Anti-Doping Organization (ADO) or governing body that commissioned and is responsible for the test (e.g., WADA, USADA, UKAD, league-specific anti-doping body).']
  therapeutic_use_exemption_granted boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a valid Therapeutic Use Exemption (TUE) was in place for the athlete at the time of the test, which may negate an Adverse Analytical Finding (AAF) for an otherwise prohibited substance.']
  training_location varchar [note: 'Address or name of the primary training facility declared in the whereabouts filing, including scheduled training hours for the filing period.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this record, supporting audit trail requirements and change detection in the Silver layer.']
  whereabouts_compliance_status varchar [note: 'Compliance classification for the whereabouts filing submission. A missed or incomplete filing may count toward the three-strike threshold for an Article 2.4 anti-doping rule violation.']
  whereabouts_submission_date date [note: 'Date on which the athlete submitted their whereabouts filing for the applicable quarter. Used to determine compliance with the quarterly submission deadline.']

  Note: 'WADA anti-doping program operational record encompassing both testing events and athlete whereabouts filings. For tests: captures test reference number, athlete identifier, test type (in-competition/out-of-competition), sample type (urine/blood/ABP), collection date, collection location, DCO identifier, laboratory reference, chain of custody status, result status (negative/adverse/atypical/pending), and WADA ADAMS reference. For whereabouts: captures filing period (quarter), submission date, overnight location, training and competition schedule, filing compliance status (compliant/missed/incomplete), and missed filing count toward Article 2.4 violation threshold. Core operational record for the complete anti-doping program lifecycle from whereabouts compliance through sample collection and result management.'
}

Table compliance.doping_violation {
  doping_violation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each Anti-Doping Rule Violation (ADRV) record in the compliance system. Primary key for the doping_violation data product.']
  doping_test_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific doping test (sample collection event) that triggered this violation, where applicable. Null for non-analytical violations such as whereabouts failures or tampering.']
  governing_body_id varchar [note: 'FK to compliance.governing_body']
  journal_entry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.journal_entry. Business justification: Doping violations result in prize money forfeitures, contract payment adjustments, and financial sanctions requiring journal entries. Sports entertainment finance teams must record these financial imp']
  league_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to league.league. Business justification: Doping violations result in league-level sanctions and CBA compliance actions. League-level disciplinary reporting, WADA annual reporting, and integrity dashboards require direct league reference on d']
  primary_doping_athlete_athlete_profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the athlete profile record against whom the Anti-Doping Rule Violation (ADRV) has been issued. Links to the athlete master data product.']
  season_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to league.season. Business justification: Doping violations trigger results disqualification and standings corrections for a specific season. Season-level anti-doping reporting and results management require direct season reference on doping_']
  whereabouts_filing_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.whereabouts_filing. Business justification: A doping violation can arise directly from whereabouts failures (missed filings or missed tests reaching the 3-strike threshold under WADA rules). Linking doping_violation to the specific whereabouts_']
  a_sample_result varchar [note: 'Result of the analysis of the A-sample (primary sample) by the WADA-accredited laboratory. An Adverse Analytical Finding (AAF) triggers the ADRV process; an Atypical Finding (ATF) requires further investigation.']
  appeal_filed_date date [note: 'Date on which the appeal against the hearing decision was formally filed with the Court of Arbitration for Sport (CAS) or the relevant appellate body. Null if no appeal has been filed.']
  appeal_status varchar [note: 'Current status of any appeal lodged against the hearing decision by the athlete, the anti-doping organisation (ADO), WADA, or the relevant sports federation.']
  b_sample_requested_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the athlete exercised their right to request analysis of the B-sample (confirmation sample) to verify the A-sample result. True if B-sample analysis was requested.']
  b_sample_result varchar [note: 'Result of the B-sample analysis confirming or refuting the A-sample Adverse Analytical Finding (AAF). Confirmed means the ADRV is substantiated; not_confirmed may lead to case withdrawal. Null if B-sample was not requested.']
  ban_duration_months varchar [note: 'Length of the period of ineligibility (ban) imposed on the athlete, expressed in months. Standard WADA Code sanctions are 12, 24, or 48 months depending on substance category and aggravating/mitigating factors. Zero if no ineligibility was imposed.']
  ban_end_date date [note: 'Date on which the period of ineligibility (ban) is scheduled to expire, calculated from ban_start_date plus ban_duration_months. Null if the ban is indefinite or pending final determination.']
  ban_start_date date [note: 'Date on which the period of ineligibility (ban) officially commenced. May be backdated to the date of provisional suspension or sample collection per WADA Code Article 10.11.']
  cas_decision_date date [note: 'Date on which the Court of Arbitration for Sport (CAS) issued its final arbitral award on the appeal. Null if CAS proceedings are ongoing or no appeal was filed.']
  cas_outcome varchar [note: 'Outcome of the Court of Arbitration for Sport (CAS) arbitral award: whether the original sanction was upheld, reduced, increased, overturned, remanded for rehearing, or the appeal was withdrawn. Null if no CAS appeal was filed.']
  cas_reference_number varchar [note: 'Official Court of Arbitration for Sport (CAS) case reference number assigned when the violation decision is appealed to CAS arbitration (e.g., CAS 2023/A/9876). Null if no CAS appeal has been filed.']
  case_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for compliance officers to record case-specific notes, mitigating or aggravating circumstances, legal observations, or procedural annotations relevant to the Anti-Doping Rule Violation (ADRV) case management.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the Anti-Doping Rule Violation (ADRV) record was first created in the compliance system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  decision_date date [note: 'Date on which the hearing body issued its written decision on the Anti-Doping Rule Violation (ADRV) charge, including the sanction imposed or the case dismissal.']
  disqualification_end_date date [note: 'End date of the period for which competition results are disqualified. Null if disqualification is open-ended or covers all results from the start date forward.']
  disqualification_start_date date [note: 'Start date of the period for which competition results are disqualified. Typically the date of the sample collection or the date of the non-analytical violation.']
  hearing_body varchar [note: 'Name of the tribunal, panel, or disciplinary body that conducted the hearing (e.g., FIFA Disciplinary Committee, NFL Anti-Doping Program Panel, WADA Independent Hearing Panel).']
  hearing_date date [note: 'Date on which the formal disciplinary hearing was conducted by the relevant anti-doping panel or tribunal to adjudicate the Anti-Doping Rule Violation (ADRV) charge.']
  in_competition_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the sample or violation occurred during an in-competition period (True) or out-of-competition (False). Relevant for determining which prohibited substances apply under the WADA Prohibited List.']
  laboratory_name varchar [note: 'Name of the WADA-accredited laboratory that performed the analysis of the biological sample and issued the Adverse Analytical Finding (AAF) or Atypical Finding (ATF). Null for non-analytical violations.']
  nationality_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code representing the athlete\'s nationality at the time of the violation. Used for jurisdictional reporting and WADA national anti-doping organisation (NADO) notification.']
  notification_date date [note: 'Date on which the athlete was formally notified of the Anti-Doping Rule Violation (ADRV) by the anti-doping organisation (ADO) or governing body. Triggers the provisional suspension clock under WADA Code.']
  prohibited_method varchar [note: 'Description of the prohibited method used (e.g., blood doping, gene doping, manipulation of samples), where the violation involves a prohibited method rather than a substance. Null when not applicable.']
  prohibited_substance varchar [note: 'Name of the prohibited substance or metabolite detected in the analytical finding, as identified by the WADA-accredited laboratory. Null for non-analytical violations. Aligned with the WADA Prohibited List.']
  prohibited_substance_category varchar [note: 'WADA Prohibited List category code for the detected substance or method (e.g., S1 Anabolic Agents, S2 Peptide Hormones, M1 Manipulation of Blood). Null for non-analytical violations. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: S0|S1|S2|S3|S4|S5|S6|S7|S8|S9|M1|M2|M3|P1 — 14 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  provisional_suspension_date date [note: 'Date on which the provisional suspension of the athlete took effect, pending the outcome of the hearing process. Null if no provisional suspension was imposed.']
  public_disclosure_date date [note: 'Date on which the Anti-Doping Rule Violation (ADRV) and sanction were publicly disclosed on the governing body\'s or WADA\'s official website per mandatory publication requirements. Null if not yet disclosed or exempt from disclosure.']
  public_disclosure_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the Anti-Doping Rule Violation (ADRV) and sanction have been publicly disclosed per WADA Code Article 14.3 mandatory publication requirements. False if the case involves a minor athlete or is subject to confidentiality provisions.']
  reinstatement_eligibility_date date [note: 'Date from which the athlete is eligible to apply for reinstatement and return to competition following completion of the period of ineligibility and satisfaction of all reinstatement conditions (e.g., whereabouts compliance, return-to-competition testing).']
  results_disqualified_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the athlete\'s competition results from the relevant period have been automatically disqualified as a consequence of the Anti-Doping Rule Violation (ADRV), including forfeiture of medals, points, and prizes.']
  revised_ban_duration_months varchar [note: 'Revised period of ineligibility in months as determined by the appellate body (CAS or other). Populated only when the appeal resulted in a modification of the original sanction. Null if no revision occurred.']
  sample_collection_date date [note: 'Date on which the biological sample (urine, blood) was collected from the athlete. For analytical violations, this is the principal real-world event date. Null for non-analytical violations.']
  sanction_type varchar [note: 'Type of sanction imposed following the hearing decision. Ineligibility (ban) is the primary sanction; others include reprimand, disqualification of results, financial fine, warning, or conditional suspension.']
  sport_discipline varchar [note: 'The specific sport or discipline in which the athlete competes and under which the Anti-Doping Rule Violation (ADRV) was adjudicated (e.g., Football, Basketball, Athletics, Cycling).']
  therapeutic_use_exemption_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the athlete held a valid Therapeutic Use Exemption (TUE) for the detected substance at the time of the sample collection. A valid TUE may result in the ADRV charge being withdrawn.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the Anti-Doping Rule Violation (ADRV) record was most recently modified in the compliance system. Supports audit trail requirements and change tracking for regulatory reporting.']
  violation_reference_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique alphanumeric reference code assigned to this Anti-Doping Rule Violation (ADRV) by the governing body or anti-doping organisation (ADO) for case tracking and correspondence.']
  violation_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the Anti-Doping Rule Violation (ADRV) case, tracking progression from initial notification through provisional suspension, hearing, sanction, appeal, and final resolution. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: pending|provisional_suspension|hearing_scheduled|sanctioned|appealed|overturned|closed — promote to reference product]']
  violation_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the Anti-Doping Rule Violation (ADRV) per WADA Code Article 2. Distinguishes between analytical findings (positive test results) and non-analytical violations such as whereabouts failures, tampering, refusal to submit to sample collection, possession, trafficking, administration, complicity, or prohibited association. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: adverse_analytical_finding|atypical_analytical_finding|whereabouts_failure|tampering|refusal|possession|trafficking|administration|complicity|prohibited_association — promote to reference product]']
  wada_adams_case_reference varchar [note: 'Case identifier assigned within the WADA Anti-Doping Administration and Management System (ADAMS), the global platform for managing doping control information. Used for cross-organisational case tracking and mandatory WADA reporting.']

  Note: 'Record of an Anti-Doping Rule Violation (ADRV) issued against an athlete following an adverse or atypical analytical finding or non-analytical violation (whereabouts failure, tampering, refusal). Captures violation type, linked doping test, notification date, provisional suspension date, hearing date, sanction imposed (ban duration), appeal status, CAS arbitration reference if appealed, and reinstatement eligibility date. Governed by WADA Code and CAS jurisprudence.'
}

Table compliance.whereabouts_filing {
  whereabouts_filing_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each WADA whereabouts filing record submitted by a registered testing pool athlete. Primary key for the whereabouts_filing data product in the compliance domain.']
  fixture_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to event.fixture. Business justification: Whereabouts filings reference competition locations that correspond to specific fixtures. The `competition_location` and `competition_name` plain attributes are denormalized fixture data. Anti-doping ']
  governing_body_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.governing_body. Business justification: Whereabouts filings are submitted to and managed by a governing body (WADA, national anti-doping organization). The responsible_organisation STRING field partially captures this but a proper FK to gov']
  league_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to league.league. Business justification: Whereabouts filings are administered within a specific league\'s anti-doping program. League-level WADA compliance reporting and registered testing pool management require direct league reference on wh']
  obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.obligation. Business justification: A whereabouts filing fulfills a specific WADA compliance obligation (athlete whereabouts reporting requirement). Linking whereabouts_filing to the specific obligation enables obligation-level complian']
  primary_whereabouts_athlete_athlete_profile_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the registered testing pool athlete who submitted this whereabouts filing. Links to the athlete master record in the athlete domain.']
  season_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to league.season. Business justification: Whereabouts filings are tied to a competitive season for anti-doping program management and WADA compliance reporting. Season-level whereabouts compliance statistics and missed filing tracking require']
  adams_reference_number varchar [note: 'Unique reference number assigned by the WADA ADAMS portal upon submission of the whereabouts filing. Used as the externally-known business identifier for cross-referencing with WADA and national anti-doping organisations.']
  athlete_response_deadline date [note: 'Deadline by which the athlete must respond to a missed or incomplete filing notification. Typically 14 days from notification_date per WADA ISTI. Failure to respond within this window may result in the missed filing being confirmed.']
  authorised_representative_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the authorised representative (agent, coach, or team official) who submitted the filing on behalf of the athlete, where applicable. Null if the athlete submitted directly.']
  cas_case_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number of the CAS arbitration case associated with a disputed whereabouts filing violation, if applicable. Null if no CAS proceedings have been initiated. Format follows CAS standard case numbering (e.g., CAS 2024/A/9876).']
  competition_end_date date [note: 'End date of the scheduled competition or event during the filing period. Defines the in-competition period boundary for testing jurisdiction and sample classification purposes.']
  competition_start_date date [note: 'Start date of the scheduled competition or event during the filing period. Used to plan in-competition doping control and to validate athlete availability windows.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Regulatory compliance determination for this filing period. COMPLIANT = filed on time and complete; MISSED = not filed by deadline; INCOMPLETE = filed but missing required information; LATE = filed after deadline; UNDER_REVIEW = compliance determination pending adjudication. Three MISSED or INCOMPLETE filings within 12 months trigger a WADA Code Article 2.4 violation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this whereabouts filing record was first created in the compliance data platform. Provides the audit trail creation anchor for the Silver Layer lakehouse record.']
  data_source varchar [note: 'Origin system or ingestion method through which this whereabouts filing record was loaded into the compliance data platform. ADAMS = direct integration with WADA ADAMS portal; MANUAL_ENTRY = entered by compliance staff; API_INTEGRATION = via anti-doping organisation API; BULK_IMPORT = batch file load.']
  dispute_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text description of the reason provided by the athlete or their representative for disputing a missed or incomplete filing determination. Relevant for CAS arbitration proceedings and WADA Code Article 2.4 adjudication.']
  filing_deadline date [note: 'Regulatory deadline by which the athlete must submit their whereabouts filing for the applicable quarter. Typically the last day of the preceding quarter per WADA ISTI requirements.']
  filing_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments recorded by the compliance officer or anti-doping administrator regarding this whereabouts filing. May include exceptional circumstances, force majeure events, or administrative observations relevant to compliance adjudication.']
  filing_period_quarter varchar [note: 'Calendar quarter (1=Q1 Jan-Mar, 2=Q2 Apr-Jun, 3=Q3 Jul-Sep, 4=Q4 Oct-Dec) to which this whereabouts filing applies. Under WADA Code Article 2.4, three missed filings within 12 months constitute a whereabouts failure violation.']
  filing_period_year varchar [note: 'Calendar year (e.g., 2024) to which this whereabouts filing applies. Combined with filing_period_quarter to uniquely identify the quarterly filing obligation period.']
  is_late_submission boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the filing was submitted after the regulatory deadline (submission_date > filing_deadline). Late submissions may be treated as incomplete or missed filings depending on the anti-doping organisation\'s rules.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent amendment or update to this whereabouts filing record. Supports audit trail requirements and compliance adjudication when disputes arise over filing completeness.']
  missed_filing_count_rolling_12m varchar [note: 'Running count of missed or non-compliant whereabouts filings by this athlete within the preceding 12-month rolling window at the time of this filing. When this value reaches 3, a WADA Code Article 2.4 whereabouts failure violation is triggered. Stored as a point-in-time snapshot for audit and adjudication purposes.']
  notification_date date [note: 'Date on which the athlete was formally notified of the missed or incomplete filing determination. The 14-day response window for the athlete to contest the determination begins from this date.']
  notification_sent boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the athlete has been formally notified of a missed or incomplete filing determination as required by WADA ISTI. Notification is a prerequisite before a missed filing can be counted toward the Article 2.4 violation threshold.']
  overnight_location_address varchar [note: 'Full street address where the athlete will be sleeping each night during the filing period. Required field under WADA ISTI for out-of-competition testing. Classified as restricted PII as it reveals the athlete\'s residential or accommodation location.']
  overnight_location_city varchar [note: 'City component of the athlete\'s overnight location for the filing period. Supports geographic analysis of testing pool distribution and logistics planning for out-of-competition testing.']
  overnight_location_country varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code for the athlete\'s overnight location during the filing period. Used to coordinate testing jurisdiction between national and international anti-doping organisations.']
  responsible_organisation varchar [note: 'Name of the Anti-Doping Organisation (ADO) responsible for managing this athlete\'s whereabouts filing obligation for the period (e.g., WADA, USADA, UKAD, or the relevant International Federation). Determines which body adjudicates missed filing violations.']
  review_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the compliance review process for this filing. PENDING = awaiting review; IN_REVIEW = under active compliance review; ACCEPTED = compliance determination finalised; DISPUTED = athlete or representative has contested the compliance determination; ESCALATED = referred to CAS or governing body for adjudication.']
  sixty_minute_slot_time timestamp [note: 'Specific 60-minute daily time window (HH:MM in 24-hour format, representing start of slot) during which the athlete guarantees availability for out-of-competition testing at the declared training venue. This is a mandatory WADA ISTI requirement for Registered Testing Pool athletes.']
  sport_discipline varchar [note: 'Sport and discipline for which the athlete is registered in the testing pool (e.g., Athletics - 100m Sprint, Swimming - Freestyle, Football). Used for sport-specific whereabouts rule application and reporting to governing bodies such as FIFA, IOC, or relevant International Federation.']
  submission_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the athlete or their authorised representative submitted the whereabouts filing. Used to determine whether the filing was on-time or late relative to the filing_deadline.']
  submission_method varchar [note: 'Channel or mechanism through which the whereabouts filing was submitted. ADAMS_PORTAL and ADAMS_MOBILE are the primary WADA-approved electronic submission methods. Other methods may be accepted in exceptional circumstances per anti-doping organisation policy.']
  submission_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time (ISO 8601 with timezone offset) at which the whereabouts filing was received and recorded in the ADAMS portal. Provides audit-grade precision for compliance adjudication.']
  submitted_by varchar [note: 'Role of the person who submitted the whereabouts filing on behalf of the athlete. Athletes may authorise agents, coaches, or team officials to submit on their behalf via ADAMS. Relevant for accountability and adjudication of missed filing disputes.']
  testing_pool_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the registered testing pool to which the athlete belongs. RTP = Registered Testing Pool (international-level, managed by International Federation or WADA); NRTP = National Registered Testing Pool (managed by National Anti-Doping Organisation); TP = Testing Pool (lower-tier, no whereabouts filing obligation but tracked for completeness).']
  training_schedule_end_time timestamp [note: 'Scheduled end time of the athlete\'s regular training sessions during the filing period. Together with training_schedule_start_time, defines the daily availability window for doping control officers.']
  training_schedule_start_time timestamp [note: 'Scheduled start time of the athlete\'s regular training sessions during the filing period. Combined with training_schedule_end_time, defines the 60-minute testing slot window required by WADA ISTI.']
  training_venue_address varchar [note: 'Full street address of the primary training venue for the filing period. Enables doping control officers to locate the athlete for unannounced out-of-competition testing.']
  training_venue_name varchar [note: 'Name of the primary training facility or venue where the athlete trains during the filing period. Required for out-of-competition testing planning and sample collection logistics.']
  update_count varchar [note: 'Number of times this whereabouts filing has been updated or amended after initial submission. Athletes may update filings up to the deadline without penalty; updates after the deadline may be flagged for review.']
  violation_threshold_reached boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this filing caused the athlete\'s missed filing count to reach or exceed the WADA Code Article 2.4 threshold of three missed/incomplete filings within 12 months, triggering a formal whereabouts failure violation.']

  Note: 'WADA whereabouts information filing submitted by registered testing pool athletes. Captures athlete identifier, filing period (quarter), submission date, submission method (ADAMS portal), overnight location, training schedule, competition schedule, and filing compliance status (compliant/missed/incomplete). Three missed filings within 12 months constitute a whereabouts failure violation under WADA Code Article 2.4.'
}

Table compliance.eligibility_certification {
  eligibility_certification_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each eligibility certification or exemption record within the compliance domain. Primary key for this data product.']
  arbitration_case_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.arbitration_case. Business justification: An eligibility certification may be subject to a CAS arbitration case (e.g., eligibility appeal). The eligibility_certification has cas_case_reference as a string field. Replacing this with a FK to ar']
  franchise_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to league.franchise. Business justification: Eligibility certifications are issued for athletes/staff affiliated with a specific franchise. Franchise-level eligibility compliance reporting and roster certification audits require direct franchise']
  governing_body_id varchar [note: 'FK to compliance.governing_body']
  investigation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.investigation. Business justification: An eligibility certification (TUE, reinstatement, ADA accommodation) may be issued as a result of or in conjunction with an investigation. Linking eligibility_certification to investigation enables en']
  league_id varchar [note: 'FK to league.league']
  obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.obligation. Business justification: An eligibility certification fulfills or responds to a specific compliance obligation (e.g., WADA TUE obligation, ADA accommodation requirement, league eligibility rule). Linking to the obligation mas']
  policy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.policy. Business justification: An eligibility certification is governed by a specific internal policy (e.g., WADA TUE policy, ADA accommodation policy, league eligibility policy). Linking to the policy master enables policy-level c']
  primary_eligibility_athlete_athlete_profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the athlete profile when the certified or exempt entity is an individual athlete or participant. Null when the record pertains to a venue or event.']
  primary_eligibility_event_fixture_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific event or fixture for which this certification or exemption applies. Null when the record is entity-level rather than event-specific.']
  season_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to league.season. Business justification: Eligibility certifications (player eligibility, TUEs) are season-scoped in professional sports. Season-specific eligibility compliance tracking and roster certification reporting require direct season']
  venue_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the venue when the certified or exempt entity is a facility (e.g., ADA accessibility certification, event safety permit). Null when the record pertains to an athlete.']
  ada_accommodation_requirements varchar [note: 'Detailed description of specific accessibility accommodations required or certified under ADA Title III for venue certifications or ADA undue hardship exemptions. Includes wheelchair access, assistive listening systems, accessible seating, and service animal provisions. Null for non-ADA certification types.']
  age_eligibility_verified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the athlete\'s age has been formally verified against the applicable league or competition minimum/maximum age requirements (True = verified and compliant; False = not yet verified or non-compliant). Relevant for draft eligibility, youth competition, and veteran status certifications.']
  appeal_status varchar [note: 'Current status of any appeal filed against a denial or revocation decision for this certification or exemption. Tracks the appeal lifecycle from filing through CAS or internal arbitration resolution.']
  application_date date [note: 'Date on which the certification or exemption application was formally submitted to the issuing authority. Used for SLA tracking, regulatory deadline compliance, and audit trail.']
  approved_substance varchar [note: 'Name of the specific prohibited substance or method for which a Therapeutic Use Exemption (TUE) has been granted under WADA Code Article 4.4. Null for non-TUE certification types. Must reference the WADA Prohibited List substance name.']
  broadcast_territory_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country or territory code for which a broadcast territorial exemption applies. Used for FCC content exemptions and RSN blackout rule overrides. Null for non-broadcast exemption types.']
  cba_provision_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific Collective Bargaining Agreement (CBA) article or provision that governs this eligibility certification (e.g., drug testing protocols, medical clearance requirements, grievance procedures). Null when CBA is not applicable.']
  certification_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique alphanumeric reference number assigned by the issuing authority (e.g., WADA ADAMS TUE reference, ADA compliance certificate number, league eligibility certificate number). Used for cross-system traceability and regulatory correspondence.']
  certification_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the certification or exemption record. Drives competition gating for athletes and operational permit status for venues. \'Pending\' indicates under review; \'Approved\' confirms active compliance; \'Denied\' records rejection; \'Suspended\' indicates temporary hold; \'Revoked\' indicates withdrawal; \'Expired\' indicates lapse past effective period.']
  certification_type varchar [note: 'Specific category of certification or exemption. Examples include: league_eligibility, amateur_status, age_nationality_eligibility, anti_doping_clearance, medical_clearance, ada_venue_accessibility, event_safety, therapeutic_use_exemption (TUE), broadcast_territorial_exemption, ada_undue_hardship, fcc_content_exemption. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: league_eligibility|amateur_status|age_nationality_eligibility|anti_doping_clearance|medical_clearance|ada_venue_accessibility|event_safety|therapeutic_use_exemption|broadcast_territorial_exemption|ada_undue_hardship|fcc_content_exemption — promote to reference product]']
  certified_entity_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the certified or exempt entity (athlete full name, venue name, event name, or organization name) at the time of certification issuance. Preserved for audit and historical reporting independent of master record changes.']
  certified_entity_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the entity to which this certification or exemption applies. Determines which domain FK (athlete_id, venue_id, event_id) is populated and governs applicable regulatory framework.']
  competition_eligibility_flag boolean [note: 'Derived operational flag indicating whether the certified entity is currently cleared for competition or operational use based on the current certification status and effective/expiry dates. True = eligible; False = ineligible. Used as the primary gate in athlete rostering and event participation systems.']
  compliance_framework varchar [note: 'Primary regulatory or governance framework under which this certification or exemption is issued. Enables framework-specific reporting, audit scheduling, and policy enforcement. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: WADA|ADA|FCC|GDPR|CCPA|PCI_DSS|OSHA|FIFA|IOC|CBA|SOX|ISO_20121 — promote to reference product]']
  conditions_restrictions varchar [note: 'Free-text description of any conditions, limitations, or restrictions attached to this certification or exemption (e.g., \'valid only for regular season competition\', \'subject to quarterly medical review\', \'restricted to home venue only\'). Null if unconditional.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this eligibility certification record was first created in the compliance system. Serves as the authoritative audit creation marker per SOX and GDPR accountability requirements.']
  decision_date date [note: 'Date on which the issuing authority rendered its decision (approval, denial, or conditional approval) on the certification or exemption application. Used for regulatory SLA compliance measurement.']
  denial_reason varchar [note: 'Structured reason code or free-text explanation provided by the issuing authority when a certification or exemption application is denied or revoked. Required for appeal processes and regulatory audit trails. Null when status is not denied or revoked.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which this certification or exemption becomes legally binding and operationally active. Used for competition eligibility gating and venue operational permit activation.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which this certification or exemption ceases to be valid. Null for open-ended certifications. Triggers renewal workflow and competition eligibility suspension if not renewed prior to this date.']
  is_retroactive boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this certification or exemption was granted retroactively (True) or prospectively (False). Particularly relevant for WADA Therapeutic Use Exemptions (TUEs) where retroactive approval is permitted under specific circumstances per WADA Code Article 4.4.']
  issuing_authority varchar [note: 'Name of the governing body, regulatory agency, or internal compliance function that issued or approved this certification or exemption (e.g., WADA, FIFA, NFL League Office, ADA Compliance Office, FCC, CAS, OSHA). Critical for regulatory traceability.']
  issuing_authority_reference varchar [note: 'Official reference number, case number, or docket identifier assigned by the issuing authority for this certification or exemption (e.g., WADA ADAMS TUE reference ID, CAS case number, FCC docket number). Enables direct cross-referencing with external regulatory systems.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this eligibility certification record. Used for change tracking, audit trail completeness, and incremental data pipeline processing in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  medical_justification varchar [note: 'Clinical rationale and medical necessity statement supporting a Therapeutic Use Exemption (TUE) or medical clearance certification. Documents the diagnosed condition, treatment necessity, and absence of performance-enhancing effect. Classified confidential due to sensitive health information context at the organizational compliance level.']
  nationality_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code representing the nationality or federation affiliation relevant to this eligibility certification (e.g., USA, GBR, BRA). Used for age/nationality eligibility certifications and international cap tracking.']
  prohibited_list_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the WADA Prohibited List in effect at the time of TUE application or anti-doping clearance certification (format: YYYY-MM, e.g., 2024-01). Ensures traceability to the correct annual prohibited substance list. Null for non-anti-doping certification types.']
  record_type varchar [note: 'Discriminator indicating whether this record represents a positive certification (confirming compliance status) or an exemption (granting relief from a standard requirement). Drives downstream processing logic and reporting segmentation.']
  renewal_due_date date [note: 'Date by which a renewal application must be submitted to maintain continuous certification or exemption coverage. Typically set prior to expiry_date to allow processing time. Null when renewal_required is False.']
  renewal_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this certification or exemption requires periodic renewal (True) or is granted on a permanent basis (False). Drives automated renewal reminder workflows and compliance calendar scheduling.']
  reviewing_officer varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the compliance officer, medical officer, or regulatory representative responsible for reviewing and processing this certification or exemption application within the organization. Used for internal accountability and audit trail.']
  season_year varchar [note: 'Four-digit calendar year representing the competitive season for which this certification is valid (e.g., 2024 for the 2024-25 season). Used for season-scoped eligibility gating and annual compliance reporting.']
  sport_code varchar [note: 'Standardized sport or discipline code to which this certification applies (e.g., FOOTBALL, BASKETBALL, BASEBALL, HOCKEY, SOCCER, TENNIS, MMA). Enables sport-specific eligibility rule application and cross-sport compliance reporting.']
  supporting_document_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier or URL pointing to supporting documentation stored in the DAM or document management system (e.g., medical certificates, physician letters, venue inspection reports, legal opinions). Enables audit trail linkage without embedding documents in the data product.']
  wada_adams_tue_reference varchar [note: 'Unique reference identifier assigned by the WADA Anti-Doping Administration and Management System (ADAMS) for this Therapeutic Use Exemption (TUE) record. Enables direct cross-referencing with WADA\'s global anti-doping database. Null for non-TUE records.']

  Note: 'Unified eligibility, certification, and exemption record confirming or modifying compliance status for athletes, participants, venues, and events. For certifications: covers league eligibility, amateur status, age/nationality eligibility, anti-doping clearance, medical clearance, ADA venue accessibility certification, and event safety certification. For exemptions: covers Therapeutic Use Exemptions (TUEs per WADA Code Article 4.4), broadcast territorial exemptions, ADA undue hardship exemptions, and FCC content exemptions. Captures record type (certification/exemption), issuing or reviewing authority, certified/exempt entity, effective and expiry dates, status, conditions or restrictions, medical justification (for TUEs), approved substance/method, WADA ADAMS TUE reference, and accommodation requirements. Integrates with athlete domain for competition gating and venue domain for operational permits.'
}

Table compliance.arbitration_case {
  arbitration_case_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the arbitration or dispute resolution case record within the Sports Entertainment compliance data platform.']
  company_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.company_code. Business justification: Arbitration cases carry estimated_liability_amount and settlement_amount that must be accrued against a specific legal entity. Finance requires company_code to book contingent liabilities and settleme']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Outside counsel spend and arbitration costs (outside_counsel_spend on arbitration_case) are charged to specific cost centers in sports entertainment legal/compliance departments. This FK enables legal']
  deal_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sponsorship.deal. Business justification: Arbitration cases in sports entertainment most commonly arise from sponsorship deal disputes: exclusivity breaches, non-payment, activation failures, naming rights violations. arbitration_case has spo']
  doping_violation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.doping_violation. Business justification: CAS arbitration cases frequently arise from doping violation appeals. The arbitration_case has cas_reference_number as a string but no FK to the underlying doping_violation. This FK enables the full a']
  fiscal_period_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.fiscal_period. Business justification: Arbitration case financial awards must be accrued in the correct fiscal period for GAAP/IFRS financial reporting. Period-close processes in sports entertainment require mapping estimated_liability_amo']
  fixture_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to event.fixture. Business justification: Arbitration cases frequently concern specific fixtures (result protests, eligibility disputes for a match, officiating decisions). Legal and compliance teams must link the arbitration case to the fixt']
  franchise_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to league.franchise. Business justification: Franchises are frequent parties in arbitration cases (salary arbitration, disciplinary appeals, contract disputes, territorial rights disputes). Linking arbitration_case to franchise enables franchise']
  game_result_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to league.game_result. Business justification: Arbitration cases arise from specific game results (officiating protests, result disputes, competitive integrity challenges). Direct FK from arbitration_case to game_result enables game-level legal ca']
  governing_body_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.governing_body. Business justification: arbitration_case has a `governing_body` STRING column storing the arbitration authority (CAS, FIFA, IOC, etc.). Normalizing this to a proper FK governing_body_id → compliance.governing_body.governing_']
  journal_entry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.journal_entry. Business justification: Arbitration awards and settlements (award_amount, settlement_amount on arbitration_case) require journal entries for financial accrual and payment. Legal-to-finance accrual process in sports entertain']
  league_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to league.league. Business justification: Arbitration cases (CBA disputes, disciplinary appeals, contract disputes) are filed within a specific league\'s jurisdiction. League-level legal exposure reporting and CBA dispute tracking require dire']
  primary_arbitration_policy_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the internal compliance policy, code of conduct, or regulatory framework that was allegedly violated or is at issue in this case. Links to the compliance policy master for policy enforcement tracking.']
  regulatory_filing_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_filing. Business justification: The arbitration_case has regulatory_filing_reference as a string field. Replacing this with a proper FK to regulatory_filing normalizes the relationship and enables JOIN-based access to the full filin']
  season_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to league.season. Business justification: Salary arbitration and disciplinary appeal cases are season-specific. Season-level legal liability reporting and CBA dispute resolution tracking require direct season reference on arbitration_case.']
  tournament_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to event.tournament. Business justification: Arbitration cases can be tournament-scoped (prize pool disputes, tournament eligibility challenges, seeding disputes). Legal teams link arbitration cases to the tournament in dispute — a named sports ']
  appeal_deadline_date date [note: 'Statutory or contractual deadline by which an appeal must be filed. Critical for legal calendar management and risk mitigation.']
  appeal_filed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether an appeal has been filed against the initial award or judgment. Triggers escalation workflow and updated financial provisioning.']
  award_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary amount awarded or ordered by the arbitral tribunal or court in the final award or judgment. Positive value indicates amount payable by Sports Entertainment; negative indicates amount receivable. Null if case is not yet resolved or resolved without monetary award.']
  award_summary varchar [note: 'Concise narrative summary of the final award, judgment, or decision rendered by the tribunal or court, including key findings, relief granted, and any conditions or obligations imposed.']
  case_priority varchar [note: 'Internal priority classification assigned by the legal and compliance team based on financial exposure, reputational risk, and regulatory significance. Drives resource allocation and executive escalation thresholds.']
  case_reference_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric case reference number assigned by the forum (e.g., CAS reference, court docket number, internal matter number). Used as the primary business identifier across legal counsel, governing bodies, and internal stakeholders.']
  case_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the arbitration or dispute case. Drives workflow routing, reporting dashboards, and escalation triggers. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: filed|pending|active|hearing|deliberation|awarded|settled|appealed|closed|withdrawn — promote to reference product]']
  case_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the dispute proceeding by legal forum and nature. Determines applicable procedural rules and governing body oversight. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: sports_arbitration|civil_litigation|regulatory_investigation|criminal_referral|ip_infringement|employment_dispute|cba_grievance — promote to reference product]']
  claimant_name varchar [note: 'Full legal name of the claimant (arbitration) or plaintiff (litigation) initiating the dispute. May be an individual athlete, organization, league, or regulatory body. Classified confidential as it is a party to active legal proceedings.']
  claimant_type varchar [note: 'Categorical classification of the claimant party. Enables segmentation of dispute proceedings by party type for governance reporting and trend analysis. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: athlete|club|league|broadcaster|sponsor|employee|regulatory_body|third_party — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  confidentiality_level varchar [note: 'Confidentiality classification of the case proceedings and associated documents as determined by the tribunal, court order, or settlement agreement. Governs internal access controls and external disclosure obligations.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this arbitration case record was first created in the compliance data platform. Supports audit trail requirements and data lineage tracking.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code applicable to all monetary amounts on this case record (estimated liability, award amount, settlement amount). Defaults to USD for domestic matters.']
  discovery_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the evidence discovery or document production phase of the proceedings. Tracks compliance with discovery obligations and deadlines.']
  enforcement_status varchar [note: 'Current status of enforcement of the award, judgment, or settlement terms. Tracks whether obligations have been satisfied, are being contested, or are pending enforcement action.']
  estimated_liability_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Internal legal team estimate of the maximum financial exposure or liability associated with this case, expressed in the base currency. Used for financial provisioning, SOX contingent liability disclosures, and executive risk reporting.']
  expected_resolution_date date [note: 'Projected date by which a final award, judgment, or settlement is expected. Used for financial provisioning, regulatory reporting timelines, and executive risk dashboards.']
  filing_date date [note: 'The date on which the case, claim, or complaint was formally filed with the adjudicating forum. Serves as the principal business event date for statute of limitations tracking and SLA measurement.']
  first_hearing_date date [note: 'Scheduled or actual date of the first formal hearing, oral argument, or trial session. Used for calendar management, counsel preparation tracking, and SLA compliance.']
  forum varchar [note: 'The adjudicating forum or tribunal where the case is being heard. CAS (Court of Arbitration for Sport) is the primary sports arbitration body; other forums include civil courts, regulatory agencies, and labor boards.']
  interim_measures_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of any interim or provisional measures ordered, including scope, duration, and conditions. Populated only when interim_measures_granted is True.']
  interim_measures_granted boolean [note: 'Indicates whether interim or provisional measures (e.g., injunctions, stay of eligibility suspension, asset freezes) have been granted by the tribunal or court during the pendency of the case.']
  jurisdiction varchar [note: 'Legal jurisdiction governing the case proceedings (e.g., Switzerland for CAS, specific US state for civil litigation, EU for GDPR regulatory matters). Expressed as ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code or named jurisdiction where country code is insufficient.']
  latest_hearing_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent hearing, session, or proceeding. Tracks case progression and supports scheduling of subsequent hearings.']
  lead_external_counsel varchar [note: 'Name of the primary external law firm or lead attorney representing Sports Entertainment in the proceedings. Used for matter management, conflict-of-interest checks, and outside counsel spend tracking.']
  matter_management_ref varchar [note: 'Reference identifier from the enterprise legal matter management system (e.g., SAP S/4HANA legal module or external e-billing platform) linking this case record to the full matter file, billing records, and document repository.']
  outside_counsel_spend decimal(18,2) [note: 'Cumulative legal fees and disbursements paid to external counsel for this case to date. Used for matter budget management, ROI (Return on Investment) analysis of legal spend, and EBITDA (Earnings Before Interest Taxes Depreciation and Amortization) impact reporting.']
  panel_composition varchar [note: 'Names and roles of arbitrators, panel members, or presiding judge(s) assigned to the case. Stored as a structured text description (e.g., \'Sole Arbitrator: J. Smith; Appointed by CAS\'). Classified confidential due to sensitivity of judicial assignment information.']
  regulatory_reporting_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this case requires mandatory disclosure or reporting to a regulatory body (e.g., SEC material litigation disclosure, WADA anti-doping result notification, FCC license compliance report).']
  resolution_date date [note: 'Actual date on which the case was resolved through award, judgment, settlement, withdrawal, or dismissal. Null if the case is still active.']
  respondent_name varchar [note: 'Full legal name of the respondent (arbitration) or defendant (litigation) against whom the dispute is brought. May be Sports Entertainment entity, an athlete, club, or third party. Classified confidential as it is a party to active legal proceedings.']
  respondent_type varchar [note: 'Categorical classification of the respondent or defendant party. Enables segmentation of dispute proceedings by party type for governance reporting and trend analysis. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: athlete|club|league|broadcaster|sponsor|employee|regulatory_body|third_party — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  settlement_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Agreed monetary settlement amount if the case was resolved through negotiated settlement rather than a formal award or judgment. Null if no settlement was reached.']
  settlement_terms varchar [note: 'Summary of the key terms and conditions of any negotiated settlement agreement, including confidentiality obligations, non-disparagement clauses, and performance requirements. Distinct from award_summary which covers tribunal-rendered decisions.']
  sport_or_league varchar [note: 'The specific sport, league, or competition context to which this case relates (e.g., NFL, NBA, FIFA, UFC, ATP). Enables domain-specific reporting and governing body alignment for multi-sport enterprises.']
  subject_matter varchar [note: 'Concise description of the primary legal or regulatory subject matter at issue (e.g., PED (Performance Enhancing Drug) violation, NIL (Name Image and Likeness) contract breach, broadcast IP (Intellectual Property) infringement, GDPR data privacy violation, ADA accessibility non-compliance, CBA grievance).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this arbitration case record. Supports change tracking, audit trail requirements, and incremental data pipeline processing in the Databricks Silver Layer.']

  Note: 'Unified dispute resolution and case management record for all legal proceedings — CAS (Court of Arbitration for Sport) arbitration, internal arbitration, civil litigation, regulatory investigations, criminal referrals, IP infringement, employment disputes, and CBA grievances. Captures case reference, case type and jurisdiction (sports arbitration vs civil court vs regulatory), claimant/respondent or plaintiff/defendant, filing date, panel or judge composition, external counsel, hearing and trial dates, interim measures, discovery status, estimated liability, award or judgment summary, settlement terms, enforcement status, and matter management reference. Serves as the SSOT for all dispute proceedings regardless of forum.'
}

Table compliance.privacy_request {
  privacy_request_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each privacy request or consent record within the unified data subject interaction system. Serves as the primary key for all GDPR, CCPA, and applicable privacy regulation interactions.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fan.account. Business justification: GDPR/CCPA privacy requests (deletion, portability, access) must be fulfilled at the account level — deleting payment tokens, closing accounts, and exporting account data. A DPO would expect privacy re']
  data_processing_record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.data_processing_record. Business justification: A GDPR/CCPA privacy request (access, erasure, portability) targets a specific processing activity. Linking privacy_request to the data_processing_record enables the DPO to identify which processing ac']
  governing_body_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.governing_body. Business justification: A privacy request is governed by a specific regulatory authority (GDPR supervisory authority, CCPA enforcement body). The applicable_regulation STRING field partially captures this but a proper FK to ']
  investigation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.investigation. Business justification: A privacy request (GDPR/CCPA data subject request) may trigger or be linked to a formal investigation (e.g., data breach investigation, unauthorized processing investigation). Linking privacy_request ']
  obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.obligation. Business justification: A privacy request fulfills a specific compliance obligation (e.g., GDPR Art. 17 right to erasure, Art. 15 right of access, CCPA opt-out obligation). Linking privacy_request to the specific obligation ']
  policy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.policy. Business justification: A privacy request is processed under a specific privacy policy version. The privacy_request has consent_policy_version (string) but no FK to the policy record. Linking privacy_request to the applicabl']
  primary_privacy_fan_fan_profile_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the fan or data subject who submitted the privacy request or whose consent is being recorded. Links to the fan master record in Salesforce CRM and Adobe Experience Platform.']
  ticket_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ticketing.ticket_order. Business justification: GDPR/CCPA data subject requests (right to erasure, data access) in ticketing operations are scoped to specific ticket orders containing fan PII and payment data. Privacy compliance teams require this ']
  aep_consent_profile_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier of the corresponding consent profile record in Adobe Experience Platform (AEP). Enables bidirectional synchronization between the compliance SSOT and the AEP consent management layer for real-time personalization suppression and marketing activation controls.']
