  agent_authorization_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier or document locator for the power of attorney, signed authorization form, or other legal instrument authorizing an agent to act on behalf of the data subject. Populated only when is_authorized_agent = True. Links to the compliance document management system.']
  applicable_regulation varchar [note: 'Primary privacy regulation governing this request or consent record. Determines the applicable deadline, response format, and legal basis requirements. \'BOTH\' applies when the data subject qualifies under both GDPR (EU resident) and CCPA (California resident). \'OTHER\' covers emerging state-level privacy laws (e.g., VCDPA, CPA).']
  assigned_team varchar [note: 'Internal team responsible for processing and fulfilling this privacy request. Supports workload routing, SLA monitoring, and escalation management. Values: privacy_ops (Privacy Operations team), legal (Legal department), dpo_office (Data Protection Officer\'s office), fan_services (Fan Services for consent queries), it_security (IT Security for data deletion execution).']
  consent_action varchar [note: 'Specific action taken on the consent record. Applicable when interaction_type = \'consent\'. \'granted\' = affirmative consent given; \'withdrawn\' = previously granted consent revoked; \'updated\' = consent scope modified; \'expired\' = consent lapsed per policy version expiry. Null for rights requests.']
  consent_granted_at timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the data subject affirmatively granted consent. Serves as the legal basis timestamp for processing activities relying on consent under GDPR Article 6(1)(a). Null for rights requests or consent withdrawal records.']
  consent_policy_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the privacy policy or consent notice that was presented to the data subject at the time of consent capture. Enables tracking of consent validity across policy updates and supports re-consent workflows when material policy changes occur. Format: v{major}.{minor}.']
  consent_proof_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier or URI pointing to the immutable consent proof artifact stored in the compliance document repository. Captures the exact consent interaction evidence (e.g., screenshot, API payload hash, signed consent token) required to demonstrate lawful basis under GDPR Article 7(1).']
  consent_type varchar [note: 'Category of consent being captured or withdrawn. Applicable when interaction_type = \'consent\'. Values: marketing (promotional communications), analytics (behavioral and performance analytics), third_party_sharing (data sharing with sponsors/partners), cookies (web/app cookie consent), biometric_data (facial recognition or biometric identification at venues). Null for rights requests.']
  consent_withdrawn_at timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the data subject withdrew previously granted consent. Upon withdrawal, downstream processing activities relying on this consent must cease. Integrated with Adobe Experience Platform consent management to suppress marketing and analytics processing.']
  created_at timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this privacy request record was first created in the compliance system. Audit trail field supporting data lineage and regulatory accountability requirements under GDPR Article 5(2).']
  data_categories_in_scope varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of personal data categories covered by this request or consent record (e.g., \'ticketing_history, fan_profile, payment_data, location_data, biometric_data\'). Derived from the data subject\'s request or the consent scope definition. Used for scoping the fulfillment workflow and data inventory mapping.']
  data_subject_jurisdiction varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code representing the jurisdiction of the data subject\'s residence, used to determine the applicable privacy regulation (GDPR for EU/EEA residents, CCPA for California residents, etc.) and the corresponding response obligations. Examples: GBR, DEU, USA, CAN.']
  dpo_review_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this request requires review by the Data Protection Officer (DPO) before fulfillment. Set to True for high-risk erasure requests, requests involving special category data (biometric, health), or requests that may conflict with legal retention obligations.']
  dpo_reviewed_at timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the Data Protection Officer (DPO) completed their review of the privacy request. Populated only when dpo_review_required = True. Supports audit trail for high-risk processing decisions.']
  extended_deadline date [note: 'Revised response deadline after a formal extension has been granted and the data subject notified. Replaces regulatory_deadline as the operative SLA date when an extension is invoked. Null if no extension was applied.']
  extension_notified_at timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the data subject was notified of a response deadline extension. Under GDPR, organizations may extend the 30-day deadline by a further 60 days for complex requests, but must notify the data subject within the initial 30-day window. Under CCPA, a 45-day extension is permitted with notice.']
  fulfillment_date date [note: 'Date on which the privacy request was fully fulfilled and the response was delivered to the data subject. Used to calculate actual response time against the regulatory deadline and to confirm SLA compliance. Null until the request reaches \'fulfilled\' status.']
  identity_verification_method varchar [note: 'Method used to verify the identity of the requestor. Supports audit trail requirements and helps assess verification strength. Values: email_confirmation (link-based), account_login (authenticated session), knowledge_based (security questions), document_upload (ID document), two_factor (MFA challenge).']
  identity_verification_status varchar [note: 'Status of the identity verification process for the requestor. \'pending\' = verification initiated but not complete; \'verified\' = identity confirmed; \'failed\' = verification unsuccessful (request cannot proceed); \'waived\' = verification not required per policy (e.g., consent withdrawal). Mandatory step before fulfilling access or erasure requests.']
  interaction_type varchar [note: 'Top-level discriminator indicating whether this record represents a data subject rights request (access, erasure, portability, etc.) or a consent management event (grant, withdrawal, update). Drives downstream processing workflow and regulatory deadline calculation.']
  is_authorized_agent boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the request was submitted by an authorized agent acting on behalf of the data subject rather than the data subject themselves. When True, agent authorization documentation must be collected and linked via agent_authorization_reference. Required for CCPA compliance.']
  legal_hold_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the data subject\'s records are subject to a legal hold that prevents erasure or restriction fulfillment. When True, the erasure or restriction request must be suspended pending resolution of the legal matter. Coordinated with the legal case management system.']
  processing_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field capturing internal notes, decisions, and observations recorded by the processing team during fulfillment. Includes documentation of exemptions applied, systems searched, and any complexities encountered. Confidential — not shared with the data subject.']
  reference_number varchar [note: 'Externally-communicated, human-readable reference number assigned to the privacy request or consent record at submission. Used in all correspondence with the data subject and regulatory bodies. Format: PRV-{YYYY}-{8-digit sequence}.']
  regulatory_deadline date [note: 'Calculated deadline by which the organization must fulfill or respond to the privacy request. Derived from submitted_at plus the applicable regulatory window: 30 calendar days under GDPR (extendable to 90 with notice) or 45 calendar days under CCPA (extendable to 90). Stored as a business date for SLA monitoring and escalation triggers.']
  rejection_reason varchar [note: 'Documented legal or operational basis for rejecting or partially fulfilling a privacy request. Required when response_outcome is \'rejected\' or \'partially_fulfilled\'. Must reference a valid legal exemption (e.g., \'legitimate interests override\', \'legal obligation to retain\', \'manifestly unfounded request\'). Populated in the response letter to the data subject.']
  request_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the privacy request or consent record. Tracks progression from initial submission through identity verification, processing, and final resolution. \'escalated\' indicates referral to legal or DPO for complex cases. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: submitted|verified|in_progress|fulfilled|rejected|withdrawn|escalated — promote to reference product]']
  request_type varchar [note: 'Specific type of data subject rights request submitted. Applicable when interaction_type = \'rights_request\'. Values: access_sar (Subject Access Request), erasure (Right to be Forgotten), portability (Data Portability), rectification (Correction), restriction (Processing Restriction), opt_out_of_sale (CCPA Opt-Out of Sale/Sharing). Null for consent records.']
  requestor_email varchar [note: 'Email address provided by the requestor at submission. Used for identity verification correspondence and delivery of the privacy request response. Stored as PII under GDPR Article 4 and CCPA.']
  requestor_full_name varchar [note: 'Full legal name of the individual submitting the privacy request. May differ from the fan record name when submitted by an authorized agent or legal representative on behalf of the data subject.']
  response_delivery_method varchar [note: 'Method used to deliver the privacy request response or consent confirmation to the data subject. Must align with the data subject\'s preferred contact channel and regulatory requirements for secure delivery of personal data.']
  response_outcome varchar [note: 'Final outcome of the privacy request fulfillment. \'fulfilled\' = request fully completed; \'partially_fulfilled\' = some data categories actioned, others exempted; \'rejected\' = request denied with legal basis; \'no_data_found\' = no personal data located for the subject; \'extended\' = response period extended per regulatory allowance with notice sent.']
  submission_channel varchar [note: 'Channel through which the data subject submitted the privacy request or provided/withdrew consent. Supports channel analytics and compliance audit trails. Values align with Adobe Experience Platform touchpoint taxonomy.']
  submitted_at timestamp [note: 'Exact date and time (with timezone) when the privacy request was submitted or the consent event was recorded. This is the principal business event timestamp and the anchor for all regulatory deadline calculations (GDPR 30-day, CCPA 45-day).']
  third_party_disclosures varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of third-party organizations to whom the data subject\'s personal data has been disclosed and who must be notified of erasure, restriction, or opt-out requests. Includes sponsors, broadcast partners, and analytics vendors. Required for GDPR Article 17(2) and CCPA opt-out propagation.']
  updated_at timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this privacy request record was most recently modified. Supports audit trail requirements and change detection for downstream integrations with Adobe Experience Platform and Salesforce CRM.']
  us_state_code varchar [note: 'Two-letter US state code for data subjects residing in the United States. Used to determine applicability of state-level privacy laws beyond CCPA (e.g., VCDPA for Virginia, CPA for Colorado, CTDPA for Connecticut). Populated only when data_subject_jurisdiction = \'USA\'.']
  verified_at timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the requestor\'s identity was successfully verified. Marks the start of the regulatory response clock in jurisdictions where the deadline begins upon verification rather than submission.']

  Note: 'Unified data subject interaction record under GDPR, CCPA, and applicable privacy regulations covering both rights requests and consent management. For requests: captures request type (access/SAR, erasure, portability, rectification, restriction, opt-out of sale), requestor identity, submission channel, verification status, regulatory deadline (30-day GDPR / 45-day CCPA), fulfillment date, and response outcome. For consent: captures consent type (marketing, analytics, third-party sharing, cookies, biometric data), consent channel (web, app, in-venue kiosk), consent timestamp, policy version, withdrawal timestamp, and consent proof reference. Provides the single fan-privacy SSOT for all data subject touchpoints and integrates with Adobe Experience Platform consent management.'
}

Table compliance.data_processing_record {
  data_processing_record_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each GDPR Article 30 Record of Processing Activities (RoPA) / Article 35 Data Protection Impact Assessment (DPIA) entry in the compliance register.']
  compliance_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.obligation. Business justification: A data processing record (GDPR Article 30 RoPA) is maintained to fulfill a specific compliance obligation. Linking data_processing_record to the obligation it satisfies enables obligation fulfillment ']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Data processing activities are owned by specific business units tracked as cost centers. Finance and compliance jointly review processing activities during SOX and privacy audits; cost center attribut']
  governing_body_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.governing_body. Business justification: A data processing record relates to a specific regulatory authority (GDPR supervisory authority, CCPA enforcement body, WADA for athlete data). Linking to governing_body enables regulatory authority-l']
  policy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.policy. Business justification: A GDPR Article 30 Record of Processing Activities (RoPA) must reference the governing data protection policy that authorizes and governs the processing activity. Linking data_processing_record to poli']
  sponsor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sponsorship.sponsor. Business justification: GDPR Article 30 records of processing activities must identify the data controller/processor relationship with sponsors. Sponsor personal and financial data is processed under sponsorship agreements. ']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date on which the processing activity and associated DPIA/PIA was formally approved by the authorised approver (e.g., DPO, Chief Privacy Officer, or Legal Counsel). Marks the point from which processing may lawfully commence.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or role of the individual who formally approved this processing activity record and associated DPIA/PIA (e.g., \'Chief Privacy Officer\', \'Data Protection Officer\', \'General Counsel\'). Provides accountability trail.']
  assessment_outcome varchar [note: 'Final outcome of the DPIA/PIA review process. \'Approved\' indicates processing may proceed; \'Approved with conditions\' requires specific mitigations before go-live; \'Rejected\' prohibits processing; \'Not required\' indicates no formal assessment was needed.']
  assessment_type varchar [note: 'Type of privacy impact assessment conducted for this processing activity. \'dpia\' = Data Protection Impact Assessment (mandatory under GDPR Article 35 for high-risk processing); \'pia\' = Privacy Impact Assessment (broader scope); \'screening\' = pre-DPIA threshold assessment; \'none\' = no assessment required.']
  ccpa_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this processing activity is subject to the California Consumer Privacy Act (CCPA) / CPRA, applicable to fan data processing for California residents. Triggers additional consumer rights obligations including opt-out of sale.']
  controller_processor_role varchar [note: 'Designates whether Sports Entertainment acts as a Data Controller, Data Processor, Joint Controller, or Sub-Processor for this processing activity, as defined under GDPR Article 4. Determines the applicable compliance obligations.']
  cross_border_transfer_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether personal data is transferred to a country outside the European Economic Area (EEA) or to an international organisation. Triggers mandatory documentation of transfer mechanisms under GDPR Chapter V.']
  data_domain varchar [note: 'Business data domain to which this processing activity belongs (e.g., Fan, Athlete, Ticketing). Aligns with Sports Entertainment\'s enterprise data domain taxonomy for governance and ownership assignment. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: fan|athlete|broadcast|ticketing|sponsorship|finance|workforce|digital_platform|content|venue — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  data_subject_categories varchar [note: 'Categories of individuals whose personal data is processed (e.g., \'Fans/Ticket Holders\', \'Athletes\', \'Employees\', \'Minors under 16\'). Pipe-delimited list. Mandatory field under GDPR Article 30(1)(c). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: fans|athletes|employees|minors|sponsors|media_partners|venue_staff|contractors — promote to reference product]']
  dpia_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a formal Data Protection Impact Assessment (DPIA) is mandatory for this processing activity under GDPR Article 35 (e.g., large-scale processing of special category data, systematic monitoring, biometric identification for ticketing).']
  dpo_consultation_date date [note: 'Date on which the Data Protection Officer (DPO) was formally consulted regarding this processing activity or DPIA. Provides an audit trail for GDPR Article 35(2) compliance.']
  dpo_consulted boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the Data Protection Officer (DPO) was formally consulted during the DPIA/PIA process, as required under GDPR Article 35(2). Mandatory for all high-risk processing activities.']
  identified_risks varchar [note: 'Narrative description of privacy risks identified during the DPIA/PIA assessment (e.g., \'Unauthorised access to fan biometric data\', \'Re-identification of pseudonymised athlete health records\', \'Cross-border transfer of minors\' data without adequate safeguards\').']
  includes_special_category_data boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this processing activity involves special category personal data as defined under GDPR Article 9 (e.g., health data, biometric data, racial/ethnic origin). Triggers additional safeguards and explicit consent requirements.']
  legal_basis varchar [note: 'The lawful basis under GDPR Article 6 (and Article 9 for special category data) that authorises this processing activity. For example, \'consent\' for fan marketing, \'contract\' for ticketing, \'legal_obligation\' for anti-doping compliance under WADA.']
  legitimate_interest_description varchar [note: 'Where legal basis is \'legitimate_interests\', documents the specific legitimate interest pursued and the balancing test outcome. Required to demonstrate compliance with GDPR Article 6(1)(f) and to evidence the three-part test (purpose, necessity, balancing).']
  next_review_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next periodic review of this processing activity record and associated DPIA/PIA. GDPR Article 35(11) requires DPIAs to be reviewed when there is a change in risk. Typically annual for high-risk activities.']
  pci_dss_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this processing activity involves payment card data and is therefore subject to PCI-DSS requirements. Applicable to ticketing (Ticketmaster/AXS), merchandise (Shopify Plus), and POS transactions at venues.']
  personal_data_categories varchar [note: 'Categories of personal data processed, including special category data under GDPR Article 9 (e.g., \'Fan PII: name, email, purchase history\', \'Athlete health data: injury records, biometrics\', \'Payment data: PCI-DSS card data\', \'Biometric data: facial recognition for ticketing\'). Mandatory under GDPR Article 30(1)(c).']
  processing_activity_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the personal data processing activity (e.g., \'Biometric Ticketing Enrollment\', \'Fan CRM Profiling\', \'Athlete Health Monitoring\'). Serves as the primary identity label for the record in the RoPA register.']
  processing_activity_reference varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique reference code assigned to this processing activity in the official RoPA register (e.g., \'RoPA-FAN-20240001\'). Used for cross-referencing in regulatory filings and supervisory authority correspondence.']
  processing_end_date date [note: 'Date on which this personal data processing activity ceased or is scheduled to cease. Null for ongoing processing activities. Represents the effective-until date for the processing activity\'s lifecycle.']
  processing_owner varchar [note: 'Business owner responsible for this processing activity (e.g., \'VP Fan Engagement\', \'Head of Athlete Performance\', \'Director of Ticketing Operations\'). Accountable for ensuring ongoing compliance with the documented controls and review schedule.']
  processing_purpose varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the specific purpose(s) for which personal data is processed (e.g., \'Fan personalization and targeted marketing via Adobe Experience Platform\', \'Athlete health monitoring for injury prevention\'). Must be specific and explicit per GDPR Article 5(1)(b) purpose limitation principle.']
  processing_start_date date [note: 'Date on which this personal data processing activity commenced or is scheduled to commence. Represents the effective-from date for the processing activity\'s lifecycle.']
  processing_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the processing activity record within the RoPA register. \'Active\' indicates live processing; \'Suspended\' indicates temporarily halted; \'Retired\' indicates decommissioned processing.']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this RoPA/DPIA record was first created in the compliance register. Provides the audit trail creation point for GDPR Article 5(2) accountability principle.']
  record_last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this RoPA/DPIA record was most recently modified. Supports audit trail requirements and change management tracking for GDPR Article 30 register maintenance.']
  retention_basis varchar [note: 'Legal or business justification for the defined retention period (e.g., \'SOX 7-year financial record requirement\', \'WADA anti-doping sample retention 10 years\', \'PCI-DSS transaction log 1 year\', \'GDPR consent withdrawal\'). Documents the regulatory driver for the retention schedule.']
  retention_period_days varchar [note: 'Maximum number of days personal data is retained for this processing activity before deletion or anonymisation, in accordance with GDPR Article 5(1)(e) storage limitation principle. Derived from the applicable retention schedule.']
  review_frequency varchar [note: 'Scheduled frequency for periodic review of this processing activity record and DPIA/PIA. High-risk activities (e.g., biometric ticketing, athlete health systems) typically require annual or biannual review.']
  risk_level varchar [note: 'Overall residual privacy risk level assigned to this processing activity following DPIA/PIA assessment (e.g., \'high\' for biometric ticketing, \'very_high\' for athlete health data processing). Determines escalation and supervisory authority consultation requirements.']
  risk_mitigation_measures varchar [note: 'Specific technical and organisational measures implemented or planned to mitigate identified privacy risks (e.g., \'Biometric data encrypted with hardware security module\', \'Athlete health data access restricted to medical staff via SAP SuccessFactors role-based controls\', \'DRM applied to broadcast content containing athlete biometrics\').']
  security_measures varchar [note: 'Description of technical and organisational measures implemented to protect personal data (e.g., \'AES-256 encryption at rest, TLS 1.3 in transit, role-based access control, PCI-DSS tokenisation for payment data, pseudonymisation of fan analytics data\'). Mandatory under GDPR Article 32 and Article 30(1)(g).']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Operational system of record where the personal data originates or is primarily processed (e.g., \'Salesforce CRM\', \'Ticketmaster/AXS\', \'Adobe Experience Platform\', \'SAP SuccessFactors\', \'SportsCode/Hudl\'). Enables data lineage tracing for GDPR accountability.']
  special_category_data_type varchar [note: 'Specific type of special category data processed under GDPR Article 9, where applicable. For Sports Entertainment, common types include \'health\' (athlete injury/medical records), \'biometric\' (facial recognition ticketing), and \'genetic\' (anti-doping testing under WADA). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: health|biometric|genetic|racial_ethnic|religious|political|trade_union|sexual_orientation|criminal_offence|none — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  supervisory_authority_consultation_date date [note: 'Date on which the supervisory authority was formally consulted under GDPR Article 36. Null if consultation was not required or has not yet occurred.']
  supervisory_authority_consultation_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether prior consultation with the relevant supervisory authority (e.g., ICO, CNIL) is required before commencing this processing activity, as mandated by GDPR Article 36 when residual risk remains high after mitigation.']
  third_party_recipients varchar [note: 'Names and categories of third parties to whom personal data is disclosed (e.g., \'Ticketmaster/AXS — ticketing processor\', \'Salesforce CRM — fan relationship management\', \'WADA — anti-doping authority\', \'Adobe Experience Platform — fan analytics\'). Mandatory under GDPR Article 30(1)(d).']
  transfer_destination_countries varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country codes of countries to which personal data is transferred outside the EEA (e.g., \'USA|CAN|AUS\'). Pipe-delimited. Required for GDPR Article 30 RoPA documentation and supervisory authority reporting.']
  transfer_mechanism varchar [note: 'Legal mechanism used to legitimise cross-border personal data transfers where applicable (e.g., \'standard_contractual_clauses\' for transfers to US-based Salesforce CRM or Adobe Experience Platform, \'adequacy_decision\' for transfers to UK post-Brexit). Mandatory under GDPR Article 46.']
  wada_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this processing activity involves athlete anti-doping data subject to WADA Code requirements, including whereabouts data, biological passport data, and anti-doping test results. Triggers specific data sharing obligations with WADA and national anti-doping organisations.']

  Note: 'GDPR Article 30 Record of Processing Activities (RoPA) with integrated Article 35 Data Protection Impact Assessment (DPIA/PIA) for all personal data processing activities. The authoritative register and risk assessment record capturing processing activity name, controller/processor role, data categories (fan PII, athlete health data, payment data, biometric data), purposes, legal basis, data subjects, retention period, third-party recipients, cross-border transfer mechanisms, security measures, DPIA/PIA assessment type, risk level, identified risks, risk mitigation measures, DPO consultation date, supervisory authority consultation requirement, assessment outcome, approval date, and review schedule. Mandatory for GDPR compliance and required before launching new fan platforms, biometric ticketing, athlete health systems, or any high-risk processing activity.'
}

Table compliance.investigation {
  investigation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for investigation']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Investigation costs (external forensic accountants, legal counsel, estimated_financial_impact) are charged to compliance cost centers in sports entertainment. This FK enables compliance cost tracking,']
  facility_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to venue.facility. Business justification: Compliance investigations are conducted at specific facilities (structural failure investigation at Arena A, food safety investigation at Concession Area B). investigation has incident_location as pla']
  fiscal_period_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.fiscal_period. Business justification: Investigations with financial impact (estimated_financial_impact) must be accrued and disclosed in the correct fiscal period. Period-close processes require this FK to identify open investigations req']
  franchise_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to league.franchise. Business justification: Integrity investigations (tampering, salary cap circumvention, conduct violations) are directed at specific franchises. Franchise-level integrity reporting and league oversight require structured FK; ']
  game_result_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to league.game_result. Business justification: Game integrity investigations are triggered by specific game results (suspicious outcomes, protests, VAR controversies). Direct FK from investigation to game_result enables game-level integrity case t']
  governing_body_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.governing_body. Business justification: Investigation has governing_body as STRING but should normalize to governing_body.governing_body_id FK. Investigations are conducted under the authority of specific governing bodies (WADA, FIFA, IOC, ']
  league_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to league.league. Business justification: Investigations are scoped to a specific league\'s rules and jurisdiction. League-level integrity reporting, governing body oversight, and annual integrity program reporting require direct league refere']
  obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.obligation. Business justification: Investigations may be triggered by suspected violations of external compliance obligations (WADA obligations, GDPR obligations, PCI-DSS requirements, etc.). investigation currently has no obligation_i']
  official_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to league.official. Business justification: Integrity investigations may target specific officials (match-fixing, misconduct, performance manipulation). Official-level integrity investigation tracking is a core sports integrity business process']
  operator_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to gaming.operator. Business justification: Compliance investigations can target gaming operators for license violations, AML failures, or responsible gaming breaches. The investigation uses generic subject_name/subject_type text fields; a dire']
  policy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.policy. Business justification: Investigations are conducted to determine violations of specific internal policies (code of conduct, anti-doping policy, data privacy policy, etc.). investigation currently has no policy_id. This FK l']
  season_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to league.season. Business justification: In-season integrity investigations (match-fixing, conduct violations) are tied to a specific season. Season-level integrity reporting and post-season compliance reviews require direct season reference']
  sponsor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sponsorship.sponsor. Business justification: Anti-corruption, WADA prohibited category, and data privacy investigations in sports entertainment are routinely opened against specific sponsors. investigation has subject_name and subject_reference ']
  subject_fan_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fan.fan_profile. Business justification: Compliance investigations into fan fraud, harassment, or data breach incidents reference a specific fan as the subject. The investigation has subject_name/subject_reference as free text — a direct FK ']
  tournament_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to event.tournament. Business justification: Compliance investigations can be tournament-scoped (match-fixing investigations covering an entire tournament, eligibility fraud across a competition). Integrity units link investigations to the tourn']
  actual_completion_date date [note: 'Actual date when the investigation was completed and closed. Null if investigation is still open.']
  allegation_summary varchar [note: 'Summary of the specific allegations or charges being investigated, including relevant policy or regulatory violations cited.']
  appeal_date date [note: 'Date when an appeal was formally filed against the investigation findings or sanctions. Null if no appeal filed.']
  appeal_filed_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether an appeal has been filed against the investigation findings or imposed sanctions.']
  appeal_outcome varchar [note: 'Final outcome or decision of the appeal process if an appeal was filed.']
  case_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique case number assigned to the investigation for tracking and reference purposes.']
  closed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise timestamp when the investigation was formally closed, marking the end of the investigative process.']
  confidentiality_level varchar [note: 'Data classification level indicating the sensitivity and access restrictions for investigation records and findings.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this investigation record was first created in the system for audit trail purposes.']
  estimated_financial_impact decimal(15,2) [note: 'Estimated financial impact or monetary loss associated with the incident under investigation, expressed in the organization\'s reporting currency.']
  evidence_collected_count varchar [note: 'Total number of evidence items or artifacts collected and catalogued as part of the investigation.']
  finding_outcome varchar [note: 'Final determination or outcome of the investigation indicating whether the allegations were substantiated, unsubstantiated, or remain inconclusive.']
  finding_summary varchar [note: 'Executive summary of the investigation findings, conclusions, and key facts established during the investigative process.']
  incident_date date [note: 'Date when the alleged incident or violation that triggered the investigation occurred.']
  incident_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the alleged incident, violation, or circumstances that prompted the investigation.']
  incident_location varchar [note: 'Physical or virtual location where the alleged incident or violation occurred.']
  initiated_date date [note: 'Date when the investigation was formally initiated and opened for review.']
  initiated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise timestamp when the investigation was formally initiated, capturing the exact moment the case was opened.']
  investigation_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the investigation indicating its progression through the investigative workflow.']
  investigation_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the investigation based on the nature of the alleged violation or incident being investigated.']
  last_modified_by varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the user who last modified this investigation record for audit trail purposes.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this investigation record was last updated or modified for audit trail and data lineage purposes.']
  lead_investigator_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the lead investigator assigned to the case for reference and reporting purposes.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes, comments, or contextual information related to the investigation for internal reference and case management.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Priority classification indicating the urgency and business impact of the investigation.']
  recommended_action varchar [note: 'Recommended corrective actions, sanctions, or remediation measures based on the investigation findings.']
  regulatory_filing_date date [note: 'Date when the investigation findings or incident report was submitted to the relevant regulatory authority. Null if no filing required or not yet submitted.']
  regulatory_filing_required_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether the investigation findings must be reported to external regulatory authorities or governing bodies.']
  sanction_end_date date [note: 'Date when the imposed sanction or penalty expires or is lifted. Null for indefinite sanctions.']
  sanction_imposed varchar [note: 'Description of the actual sanction, penalty, or disciplinary action imposed as a result of the investigation findings.']
  sanction_start_date date [note: 'Date when the imposed sanction or penalty becomes effective.']
  severity_level varchar [note: 'Classification of the severity or impact level of the alleged violation based on regulatory guidelines and internal risk assessment.']
  subject_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the individual or entity under investigation for identification and reporting purposes.']
  subject_reference varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the individual or entity that is the primary subject of the investigation. Links to the appropriate master entity based on subject_type.']
  subject_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the primary subject or entity under investigation.']
  target_completion_date date [note: 'Planned or target date by which the investigation is expected to be completed based on regulatory timelines or internal policies.']
  witness_count varchar [note: 'Total number of witnesses interviewed or providing statements as part of the investigation.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for investigation. Referenced by investigation_id.'
}

Table gaming.betting_market {
  betting_market_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each wagering market definition in the master catalog. Primary key for the betting_market data product.']
  broadcast_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to broadcast.broadcast_schedule. Business justification: Betting market lifecycle (open/suspend/close) is operationally tied to broadcast schedule windows. In-play markets must open at broadcast start and close at broadcast end. Integrity teams correlate ma']
  competition_round_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to event.competition_round. Business justification: Round-level betting markets (e.g., \'Top scorer in Round 5\', \'Most wins in Conference Semis\') require competition_round reference for market creation, settlement scheduling, and broadcast window alignm']
  franchise_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to league.franchise. Business justification: Team win-total, division-winner, and moneyline markets are created per franchise. A franchise_id FK enables integrity monitoring scoped to a franchise, settlement against franchise outcomes, and regul']
  league_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to league.league. Business justification: Betting markets are categorized and regulated by league for integrity monitoring, regulatory reporting, and market segmentation. The existing league_code plain attribute is a denormalized representati']
  league_season_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to league.season. Business justification: Futures and season-win-total betting markets must be scoped to a specific league season for settlement, regulatory period reporting, and GGR accounting. A sports-entertainment operator would always ti']
  match_result_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to event.match_result. Business justification: Betting market settlement — the core sportsbook operation — requires resolving each market against the official confirmed match_result. Automated settlement pipelines and integrity audit trails univer']
  parent_betting_market_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on betting_market (parent_betting_market_id)']
  playoff_bracket_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to league.playoff_bracket. Business justification: Futures markets for bracket outcomes (conference champion, championship winner, bracket round winners) must reference the specific playoff bracket for settlement. Playoff bracket betting is a major re']
  primary_betting_event_fixture_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific sports or entertainment event this market is associated with. Links the market definition to the event scheduling and planning domain for settlement and reporting.']
  rights_window_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to broadcast.broadcast_rights_window. Business justification: In-play betting markets must open and close within active broadcast rights windows. Market scheduling operations require this link to enforce that live wagering is only offered during licensed broadca']
  tournament_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to event.tournament. Business justification: Futures markets (\'Who wins the Champions League?\', \'Tournament MVP\') are scoped to a tournament. Settlement of futures requires tournament_id to determine the winner and trigger payouts. is_futures_ma']
  age_verification_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether bettors must complete age verification before placing wagers on this market. True = age verification mandatory; False = standard KYC suffices. Driven by jurisdiction-specific regulatory requirements.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this betting market definition record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail, data lineage, and regulatory compliance reporting.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which stake limits, payouts, and odds are denominated for this market (e.g., USD, GBP, EUR). Required for multi-jurisdiction operations.']
  display_order varchar [note: 'Numeric ordering value controlling the sequence in which this market is presented within its market category on betting interfaces and odds feeds. Lower values appear first. Supports merchandising and fan engagement optimization.']
  effective_from_date date [note: 'The date from which this version of the market definition is valid and operative. Used for temporal validity tracking of market parameters across version changes.']
  effective_until_date date [note: 'The date until which this version of the market definition remains valid. Null for the current active version. Enables point-in-time reconstruction of market parameters for regulatory audit and dispute resolution.']
  is_featured boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this market is designated as a featured/promoted wagering product for enhanced placement in betting interfaces, marketing campaigns, and fan engagement platforms. True = featured; False = standard placement.']
  is_futures_market boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this market is a futures wagering product where the outcome is determined at a future date (e.g., season champion, award winner). True = futures market; False = event-specific market. Affects settlement timeline and liability reporting.']
  is_live_inplay boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this market is offered as a live in-play (in-game) wagering product where odds update in real time during the event. True = live in-play market; False = pre-game market. Drives real-time odds feed routing and latency SLA requirements.']
  is_parlay_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this market can be combined into a parlay or teaser multi-leg wager. True = eligible for parlay inclusion; False = must be wagered as a standalone market. Governs parlay construction rules in the betting platform.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this betting market definition record was most recently modified. Tracks changes to market parameters, status transitions, and settlement updates for audit and compliance purposes.']
  margin_percentage decimal(6,4) [note: 'The operator\'s built-in margin (overround/vig) applied to this market, expressed as a decimal percentage (e.g., 0.0500 = 5%). Represents the house edge embedded in the odds. Confidential commercial data used for profitability analysis and pricing strategy.']
  market_category varchar [note: 'High-level grouping of the market by scope: Game = single-game outcome; Season = full-season result (e.g., division winner); Tournament = multi-round competition outcome; Player = individual athlete performance; Team = team-level statistic; Entertainment = non-sport event (e.g., awards show outcome).']
  market_close_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this wagering market was closed and no further wagers were accepted. For pre-game markets this is typically event start time; for live markets it is the point of suspension or event conclusion.']
  market_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric code uniquely identifying this market definition across odds feeds, prop bet catalogs, and regulatory reporting systems. Used as the business key in downstream integrations.']
  market_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable display name for the wagering market as presented to bettors and operators (e.g., \'NFL Week 5 Moneyline - Chiefs vs Raiders\', \'NBA Finals Game 1 Total Points Over/Under\').']
  market_open_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this wagering market was first opened and made available for bet placement. Used for audit trails, regulatory reporting, and market lifecycle analytics.']
  market_source_feed varchar [note: 'Identifier of the external odds feed provider or internal pricing engine that originates the market definition and odds for this market (e.g., \'SPORTRADAR\', \'GENIUS_SPORTS\', \'INTERNAL_TRADING\'). Supports feed reconciliation and SLA monitoring.']
  market_status varchar [note: 'Current operational lifecycle state of the wagering market. Open = accepting wagers; Suspended = temporarily halted (e.g., injury news, VAR review); Closed = no longer accepting wagers; Settled = outcome determined and payouts processed; Voided = market cancelled with stakes returned; Pending = awaiting activation.']
  market_type_code varchar [note: 'Classification of the wagering market structure. Moneyline = straight win/loss; Spread = point spread handicap; Total = over/under aggregate score; Futures = outcome of future event; Parlay = multi-leg combined bet; Teaser = adjusted spread/total parlay; Prop = proposition/player-specific bet; Live In-Play = real-time in-game market. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: moneyline|spread|total|futures|parlay|teaser|prop|live_inplay — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  max_liability_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Maximum total financial exposure the operator accepts across all wagers on this market before triggering automatic suspension or odds adjustment. Expressed in the operating currency. Confidential risk management parameter.']
  max_payout_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Maximum total payout the operator will honor for a single winning wager on this market, expressed in the operating currency. Caps operator liability for high-odds outcomes.']
  max_stake_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Maximum wager amount permitted on this market per bet placement, expressed in the operating currency. Enforced to manage operator liability exposure and comply with responsible gaming limits.']
  min_stake_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Minimum wager amount permitted on this market per bet placement, expressed in the operating currency. Enforced at point-of-sale to comply with operator risk management and regulatory minimums.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text operational notes recorded by trading or compliance staff regarding special conditions, exceptions, or context for this market (e.g., \'Suspended pending VAR review\', \'TMO decision pending\'). Not displayed to bettors.']
  odds_format varchar [note: 'Default odds presentation format for this market. American = moneyline (+/-); Decimal = European multiplier; Fractional = UK traditional; Hong Kong/Indonesian/Malay = regional formats. Drives display logic in betting interfaces and odds feeds.']
  prop_bet_category varchar [note: 'For proposition (prop) bet markets, classifies the type of proposition: Player Performance = individual athlete stat (e.g., passing yards, points scored); Team Statistic = team-level metric; Game Event = specific in-game occurrence (e.g., first score method); Entertainment = non-sport proposition; None = not a prop bet market.']
  regulatory_market_code varchar [note: 'The market identifier assigned by the relevant gaming regulatory authority for official reporting and audit purposes. Required for regulatory filings in licensed jurisdictions. May differ from internal market_code.']
  responsible_gaming_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this market has been flagged for enhanced responsible gaming controls (e.g., lower stake limits, mandatory cooling-off prompts, exclusion from promotional offers). True = enhanced controls active; False = standard controls apply.']
  season_year varchar [note: 'The calendar year of the sports season to which this market belongs (e.g., 2024 for the 2024-25 NBA season). Used for futures markets and season-long wagering products.']
  settlement_outcome varchar [note: 'The official result used to settle this market (e.g., \'HOME_WIN\', \'OVER\', \'PLAYER_A\', \'PUSH\'). Populated after event conclusion and official score confirmation. Null until market is settled. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: HOME_WIN|AWAY_WIN|DRAW|OVER|UNDER|PUSH|VOID|CANCELLED — promote to reference product]']
  settlement_rule_code varchar [note: 'Code referencing the official settlement rule set applied to determine winning outcomes for this market (e.g., REGULATION_TIME_ONLY, INCLUDING_OT, OFFICIAL_SCORE_AT_CLOSE). Governs how and when the market is settled post-event.']
  settlement_rule_description varchar [note: 'Plain-language description of the settlement rules for this market, including conditions for void, push, or partial settlement. Displayed to bettors and referenced in regulatory filings.']
  settlement_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the market outcome was officially determined and winning wagers were settled. Null if market is not yet settled. Critical for financial reconciliation and regulatory reporting.']
  sport_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code identifying the sport to which this market applies (e.g., NFL, NBA, MLB, NHL, MLS, UFC, ATP). Scopes the market to the relevant league or governing body.']
  spread_value decimal(6,2) [note: 'The handicap value applied to the favored team or participant in a spread market (e.g., -3.5 means the favorite must win by more than 3.5 points). Null for non-spread market types.']
  total_line_value decimal(6,2) [note: 'The aggregate score or statistical threshold set for an over/under (totals) market (e.g., 47.5 total points in an NFL game). Bettors wager on whether the actual result exceeds or falls below this value. Null for non-totals market types.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Incremental version counter for this market definition record. Incremented each time material changes are made to market parameters (e.g., spread adjustment, limit change). Supports change history tracking and regulatory audit requirements.']

  Note: 'Master catalog of all wagering market definitions available across sports and entertainment events — covering moneyline, spread, totals (over/under), futures, parlays, teasers, and live in-play markets. Captures market type code, sport/league scope, settlement rules, market status (open, suspended, closed, settled), minimum/maximum stake limits, and jurisdiction availability flags. SSOT for all market definitions consumed by odds feeds, prop bet catalogs, and regulatory reporting.'
}

Table gaming.odds_feed {
  odds_feed_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each odds feed record in the Silver layer lakehouse. Primary key for the odds_feed data product.']
  betting_market_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the betting market for which this odds update applies. Links to the market definition product in the gaming domain.']
  league_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to league.league_schedule. Business justification: Odds feeds are published for specific scheduled games. Linking to league_schedule enables feed suspension on postponement, scheduling-status-driven feed management, and integrity monitoring tied to th']
  live_feed_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to broadcast.live_feed. Business justification: In-play odds feeds are synchronized with live broadcast feeds — the same live signal drives both broadcast delivery and odds data ingestion. Sports integrity monitoring requires correlating odds movem']
  match_result_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to event.match_result. Business justification: Post-match, odds_feed records are resulted and archived against the official match_result. Settlement reconciliation and integrity monitoring (detecting pre-result odds manipulation) require this dire']
  media_rights_deal_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to broadcast.media_rights_deal. Business justification: Official data mandate compliance: in-play odds feeds must be sourced from and validated against the governing media rights deal. Regulators in many jurisdictions require official data provenance. A ga']
  primary_odds_event_fixture_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the sporting event (fixture or competition) to which this odds feed record is associated.']
  scoring_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to event.scoring_event. Business justification: Live in-play odds feeds are triggered and repriced by scoring events. Integrity monitoring requires correlating odds movements with specific scoring_event timestamps to detect suspicious pre-event odd']
  selection_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific selection (outcome) within the betting market for which odds are being updated (e.g., Team A to win, Over 2.5 goals).']
  superseded_odds_feed_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on odds_feed (superseded_odds_feed_id)']
  american_odds varchar [note: 'The odds expressed in American moneyline format. Positive values (e.g., +150) indicate profit on a $100 stake; negative values (e.g., -200) indicate the stake required to win $100. Standard format for US sports wagering markets.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time at which this odds feed record was first persisted in the Silver layer lakehouse. Used for data lineage and audit trail.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code applicable to stake and payout amounts for this odds feed record (e.g., USD, GBP, EUR). Supports multi-currency operations across global jurisdictions.']
  decimal_odds decimal(10,6) [note: 'The odds expressed in decimal format (European format), representing the total payout per unit staked including the stake (e.g., 2.500000 means a $1 bet returns $2.50). Widely used in European and international markets.']
  feed_channel varchar [note: 'The technical delivery channel through which this odds update was received from the provider. Supports feed reliability monitoring and SLA compliance tracking.']
  feed_latency_ms varchar [note: 'The measured latency in milliseconds between the provider_published_timestamp and the received_timestamp for this odds update. Used for SLA monitoring, provider performance benchmarking, and real-time display quality assurance.']
  feed_reference_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique identifier assigned by the odds provider for this specific odds update message. Used for deduplication, reconciliation, and audit trail against the provider\'s own records.']
  feed_source_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the odds data provider or compiler that supplied this update (e.g., \'Sportradar\', \'Kambi\', \'Genius Sports\', \'SBTech\'). Used for provider performance monitoring and SLA tracking.']
  feed_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of this odds feed record. \'active\' indicates odds are live and tradeable; \'suspended\' indicates trading is paused; \'settled\' indicates the market has been resolved; \'cancelled\' or \'void\' indicates the record is no longer valid. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: active|suspended|settled|cancelled|resulted|void — promote to reference product]']
  feed_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the odds feed protocol or API specification used by the provider for this update. Supports backward compatibility management and feed migration tracking.']
  fractional_odds_denominator varchar [note: 'The denominator component of fractional odds (UK format), representing the stake unit (e.g., denominator 1 in 3/1 means $1 staked). Combined with numerator to express the full fractional odds.']
  fractional_odds_numerator varchar [note: 'The numerator component of fractional odds (UK format), representing the profit relative to the denominator stake (e.g., numerator 3 in 3/1 means $3 profit per $1 staked). Used in UK and Irish markets.']
  handicap_value decimal(6,2) [note: 'The handicap or point spread value associated with this selection, where applicable (e.g., -1.5 goals, +3.5 points). Null for non-handicap markets. Essential for Asian handicap, European handicap, and point spread markets.']
  implied_probability decimal(8,6) [note: 'The probability of the selection winning as implied by the current odds, expressed as a decimal between 0 and 1 (e.g., 0.400000 = 40%). Derived from decimal odds as 1/decimal_odds before margin adjustment. Used for overround analysis and pricing analytics.']
  integrity_alert_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this odds update has triggered a betting integrity alert (True), such as suspicious line movement that may indicate match-fixing or insider information. Supports regulatory reporting obligations to governing bodies (FIFA, NFL, NBA, etc.).']
  is_in_play boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this odds update relates to an in-play (live) betting market where the event is already in progress (True), or a pre-match market (False). In-play markets have different regulatory requirements and risk profiles.']
  is_resulted boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the betting market has been officially resulted/settled (True) or is still open/pending (False). A resulted market has a confirmed outcome and all bets have been settled.']
  is_suspended boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the betting market was suspended at the time of this odds update (True = suspended, False = tradeable). Suspension may be triggered by significant events (e.g., red card, injury, VAR review, TMO decision) or risk management actions.']
  jurisdiction_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 or alpha-3 country/state code identifying the regulatory jurisdiction under which this odds feed record is governed. Critical for jurisdiction-specific compliance, licensing, and regulatory reporting (e.g., US state-level gaming regulations, GDPR for EU).']
  line_movement_direction varchar [note: 'Indicates the direction of the odds movement relative to the previous update for this selection. \'up\' means odds lengthened (selection less favoured); \'down\' means odds shortened (selection more favoured); \'unchanged\' means no movement; \'open\' is the first price; \'close\' is the final price before event start.']
  market_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the betting market type for this odds update (e.g., match_result, over_under, handicap, first_goalscorer, prop_bet, futures, outright). Supports market-level analytics and regulatory reporting segmentation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: match_result|over_under|handicap|first_goalscorer|prop_bet|futures|outright — promote to reference product]']
  max_stake_amount decimal(14,2) [note: 'The maximum single bet stake permitted on this selection at the time of this odds update, in the operating currency. Reflects risk management limits set by the trading desk. Confidential as it reveals internal risk appetite.']
  min_stake_amount decimal(14,2) [note: 'The minimum single bet stake permitted on this selection at the time of this odds update, in the operating currency. Enforced at point of bet acceptance.']
  odds_format varchar [note: 'The primary odds format in which this update was received from the provider. Determines which odds value field is the authoritative source. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: decimal|fractional|american|hongkong|malay|indonesian — promote to reference product]']
  overround_pct decimal(8,4) [note: 'The bookmaker\'s margin (vigorish/vig) expressed as a percentage, calculated as the sum of all implied probabilities for a market minus 100%. Represents the theoretical profit margin built into the odds. Used for pricing analytics and margin monitoring.']
  previous_decimal_odds decimal(10,6) [note: 'The decimal odds value from the immediately preceding odds update for this selection and market. Enables line movement magnitude calculation and trend analysis without requiring a self-join.']
  price_boost_indicator boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the odds for this selection have been artificially enhanced as part of a promotional price boost offer (True) or reflect standard market pricing (False). Price boosts are a common fan engagement and DTC acquisition tool.']
  provider_published_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time at which the odds provider published or generated this odds update on their end. Used to calculate feed latency (received_timestamp minus provider_published_timestamp) and for regulatory audit trails.']
  received_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time (with millisecond precision and timezone offset) at which this odds update message was received from the provider feed. Principal business event timestamp for latency measurement and audit.']
  regulatory_reporting_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this odds feed record must be included in regulatory filings or reports to gaming authorities in the applicable jurisdiction (True). Supports automated regulatory reporting workflows and SOX/CCPA/GDPR compliance.']
  responsible_gaming_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this odds feed record has been flagged for responsible gaming review (True), for example due to unusual line movement that may indicate integrity concerns, or market conditions that trigger enhanced monitoring under WADA or governing body integrity frameworks.']
  result_outcome varchar [note: 'The official result outcome for this selection once the market has been resulted. \'win\' = selection won; \'lose\' = selection lost; \'void\' = bet voided; \'push\' = stake returned (tie); \'half_win\'/\'half_lose\' = Asian handicap partial outcomes. Null until market is resulted.']
  sequence_number varchar [note: 'Sequential message number assigned by the odds provider within their feed stream for this market or session. Used to detect out-of-order delivery, gaps in the feed, and to reconstruct the correct chronological order of odds updates.']
  source_market_reference varchar [note: 'The provider\'s own internal market identifier or reference code for this betting market, as supplied in the feed message. Enables direct reconciliation with the provider\'s system without requiring a reverse lookup.']
  sport_code varchar [note: 'Standardised short code identifying the sport associated with this odds feed record (e.g., NFL, NBA, MLB, NHL, MLS, UFC, ATP). Enables cross-sport analytics and jurisdiction-specific regulatory reporting.']
  suspension_reason varchar [note: 'Descriptive reason for market suspension when is_suspended is True (e.g., \'VAR_REVIEW\', \'INJURY_STOPPAGE\', \'RISK_MANAGEMENT\', \'FEED_OUTAGE\', \'REGULATORY_HOLD\'). Null when market is not suspended. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: VAR_REVIEW|INJURY_STOPPAGE|RISK_MANAGEMENT|FEED_OUTAGE|REGULATORY_HOLD|MANUAL — promote to reference product]']
  total_line_value decimal(6,2) [note: 'The total points/goals/runs line value for over/under markets (e.g., 2.5 goals, 47.5 points). Null for non-totals markets. Used in conjunction with market_type to define the exact market being priced.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time at which this odds feed record was last modified in the Silver layer lakehouse. Supports incremental processing and change data capture.']

  Note: 'High-frequency transactional record of odds price updates received from odds compilers and third-party odds providers (e.g., Sportradar, Kambi) for each betting market. Captures market ID, selection ID, decimal/fractional/American odds format values, implied probability, line movement direction, feed source, timestamp of update, and feed status. Supports real-time odds display, line movement analytics, and audit trails for regulatory compliance.'
}

Table gaming.wager {
  wager_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each individual wager transaction placed across all sportsbook channels. Primary key for the wager entity.']
  betting_market_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the betting market on which this wager was placed (e.g., match winner, over/under, point spread). Drives market-level settlement and odds management.']
  bettor_account_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the registered bettor account that placed this wager. Links to the bettor\'s registered gaming account for KYC, responsible gaming, and regulatory reporting purposes.']
  broadcast_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to broadcast.broadcast_schedule. Business justification: In-play wagering windows are gated on broadcast schedule start/end times. Sportsbooks open/close live markets based on broadcast status, and settlement timing aligns with broadcast end. Regulators req']
  cashout_of_wager_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on wager (cashout_of_wager_id)']
  company_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.company_code. Business justification: Wagers are placed under specific licensed legal entities (company codes) for GGR tax reporting, withholding tax remittance, and regulatory compliance. Finance must attribute wager revenue to the corre']
  facility_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to venue.facility. Business justification: Retail wagers placed at in-venue sportsbook kiosks must be attributed to the specific facility for regulatory reporting, tax remittance by jurisdiction, and venue revenue reconciliation. The existing ']
  fiscal_period_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.fiscal_period. Business justification: Wagers must be attributed to fiscal periods for GGR reporting, tax remittance calculations, and period-close processes. Gaming finance teams report GGR by fiscal period to regulators and internal mana']
  game_result_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to league.game_result. Business justification: Wager settlement is triggered by the official game_result. Linking wager directly to game_result enables settlement workflow automation, integrity investigation (protest_flag, var_intervention_flag), ']
  investigation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.investigation. Business justification: Wagers flagged via `integrity_flag` for match-fixing or suspicious betting patterns trigger formal compliance investigations. Sports integrity officers must trace which specific wager(s) initiated an ']
  jurisdiction_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the regulatory jurisdiction under which this wager was accepted (e.g., Nevada, New Jersey, United Kingdom). Determines applicable tax rates, reporting obligations, and responsible gaming rules.']
  match_result_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to event.match_result. Business justification: Wager settlement — the primary sportsbook business process — resolves each wager against the official match_result. Regulatory reporting of settled wagers and integrity investigations both require dir']
  odds_feed_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to gaming.odds_feed. Business justification: A wager is placed at specific odds derived from an odds feed snapshot. wager has odds_at_placement (DECIMAL) but no FK to odds_feed. Adding odds_feed_id links each wager to the specific odds record us']
  operator_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the licensed sportsbook operator or brand under which this wager was accepted. Supports multi-brand or white-label sportsbook operations and operator-level regulatory reporting.']
  promotion_campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fan.campaign. Business justification: Sportsbook operators track which promotional campaign drove a wager (free bet offers, deposit match promotions) for marketing ROI reporting and bonus liability accounting. wager.promotion_code is a de']
  regulatory_report_id varchar [note: 'The identifier of the regulatory filing or suspicious activity report (SAR) that this wager was included in, if applicable. Used for AML compliance tracking and regulatory audit trail. Null if no regulatory report has been filed for this wager.']
  retail_location_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the physical retail sportsbook location (e.g., stadium sportsbook, casino floor) where the wager was placed. Null for digital channel wagers. Used for venue-level revenue reporting and compliance.']
  season_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to league.season. Business justification: Wagers are scoped to a league season for regulatory reporting (season-level GGR, W-2G aggregation), integrity monitoring, and financial reconciliation. Season-level wager aggregation is a standard spo']
  void_emergency_activation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to security.emergency_activation. Business justification: Wager void due to emergency event cancellation: when an emergency activation cancels an event, open wagers must be voided and stakes refunded. This FK links each voided wager to the specific activatio']
  wager_event_fixture_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the sporting or entertainment event that this wager is associated with (e.g., NFL game, UFC bout, NBA match). Used for event-level wagering aggregation and settlement.']
  wager_fixture_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the sporting or entertainment event that this wager is associated with (e.g., NFL game, UFC bout, NBA match). Used for event-level wagering aggregation and settlement.']
  bonus_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'The monetary value of any bonus or promotional credit applied to this wager. Tracked separately from real-money stake for regulatory gross gaming revenue (GGR) reporting and bonus liability accounting.']
  cashout_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'The monetary amount offered and accepted by the bettor when exercising the early cash-out option. Null if the wager was not cashed out. Represents the sportsbook\'s real-time liability reduction value.']
  cashout_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the bettor exercised the early cash-out option, if applicable. Null if the wager was not cashed out early. Used for cash-out feature analytics and liability management.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this wager record was first created in the sportsbook system. Used for audit trail, data lineage, and Silver layer ingestion tracking.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary amounts on this wager (stake, payout, settlement). Supports multi-currency sportsbook operations across international jurisdictions (e.g., USD, GBP, EUR, CAD).']
  device_fingerprint varchar [note: 'Anonymized or hashed identifier of the device used to place the wager. Used for fraud detection, multi-accounting prevention, and geolocation compliance verification. Not a direct personal identifier but may be used to identify individuals in combination with other data.']
  geolocation_country varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code where the bettor was physically located at time of wager placement. Used for international jurisdiction compliance and cross-border wagering restriction enforcement.']
  geolocation_state varchar [note: 'Two-letter US state code (or equivalent jurisdiction code) where the bettor was physically located at time of wager placement, as verified by geolocation service. Critical for confirming the bettor is within a licensed wagering jurisdiction.']
  geolocation_verified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the bettor\'s physical location was successfully verified by the geolocation service at time of wager placement. False may indicate a geolocation failure or VPN/proxy detection, requiring compliance review.']
  integrity_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this wager has been flagged for sports integrity review due to suspicious betting patterns (e.g., unusual odds movement, match-fixing indicators). Flagged wagers are reported to relevant sports governing bodies (FIFA, NFL, NBA integrity units) and gaming regulators.']
  ip_address varchar [note: 'The IP address from which the wager was placed. Used for geolocation verification to confirm the bettor is within a licensed jurisdiction at time of placement. Subject to privacy regulations in EU and California jurisdictions.']
  is_free_bet boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this wager was funded by a promotional free bet credit rather than real money from the bettor\'s account. Free bets are excluded from certain regulatory gross gaming revenue calculations.']
  is_live_bet boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this wager was placed as an in-play (live) bet after the event commenced, as opposed to a pre-game bet. Live bets carry different risk profiles and are subject to specific regulatory requirements in some jurisdictions.']
  leg_count varchar [note: 'The number of individual selections (legs) included in this wager. For straight bets this is 1; for parlays and teasers this is 2 or more. Used for parlay payout validation and risk management.']
  odds_at_placement decimal(10,4) [note: 'The decimal odds offered to the bettor at the exact moment the wager was placed. Locked in at placement time and used for payout calculation. Stored in decimal format (e.g., 2.50 = evens +100 American odds) regardless of display format shown to bettor.']
  odds_format varchar [note: 'The odds display format presented to the bettor at time of placement (e.g., \'american\' for +150/-110, \'decimal\' for 2.50, \'fractional\' for 3/2). Stored for display reconciliation and international market compliance.']
  placement_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The exact date and time (with timezone) when the wager was accepted and confirmed by the sportsbook platform. This is the principal business event timestamp and is critical for in-play betting validation, regulatory reporting, and audit trails.']
  potential_payout decimal(18,4) [note: 'The maximum gross payout amount the bettor would receive if the wager wins, calculated as stake_amount multiplied by odds_at_placement. Captured at placement time for liability management and bettor communication.']
  potential_profit decimal(18,4) [note: 'The net profit the bettor would receive if the wager wins, calculated as potential_payout minus stake_amount. Represents the bettor\'s net gain and is used in responsible gaming exposure monitoring.']
  reference_number varchar [note: 'Externally visible alphanumeric reference number presented to the bettor on their bet slip or receipt. Used for customer service inquiries, dispute resolution, and regulatory audit trails.']
  responsible_gaming_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this wager triggered a responsible gaming alert or intervention at time of placement (e.g., exceeded deposit limit, velocity threshold, or self-exclusion proximity check). Used for regulatory compliance reporting and player protection monitoring.']
  selection_description varchar [note: 'Human-readable description of the bettor\'s selection within the betting market (e.g., \'Kansas City Chiefs to win\', \'Over 48.5 total points\', \'LeBron James to score 30+ points\'). Captured at time of placement for audit and display purposes.']
  self_exclusion_override boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this wager was placed despite a self-exclusion or cooling-off period being active on the bettor\'s account. Should always be False for compliant operations; True values require immediate regulatory escalation.']
  settlement_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'The actual monetary amount paid out to the bettor upon settlement. For winning wagers this equals the gross payout; for losing wagers this is zero; for void/push wagers this equals the stake returned. Null until the wager is settled.']
  settlement_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the wager outcome was determined and the settlement amount was applied to the bettor\'s account. Null for pending wagers. Used for settlement cycle reporting and regulatory compliance.']
  stake_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'The monetary amount wagered by the bettor at time of placement, in the transaction currency. Represents the bettor\'s financial exposure and is the basis for hold calculations and regulatory reporting.']
  tax_withheld_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'The amount of tax withheld from the settlement payout at source, as required by applicable jurisdiction tax laws (e.g., US federal withholding on large winnings per IRS Form W-2G thresholds). Zero if no withholding applies.']
  ticket_terminal_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the specific betting terminal, kiosk, or point-of-sale device used to place the wager. Used for terminal-level reconciliation, fraud detection, and equipment audit trails in retail and kiosk channels.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this wager record was last modified in the sportsbook system (e.g., status change from pending to settled). Used for change data capture and audit compliance.']
  void_reason varchar [note: 'The reason code explaining why a wager was voided. Null for non-voided wagers. Used for operational reporting, bettor communication, and regulatory audit. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: event_cancelled|event_postponed|market_error|odds_error|bettor_request|regulatory_order|system_error|integrity_concern — promote to reference product]']
  wager_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the wager. \'pending\' indicates the event has not yet concluded; \'won\'/\'lost\' reflect settled outcomes; \'void\' indicates cancellation (e.g., event postponed); \'cashed_out\' reflects early cash-out by bettor; \'pushed\' indicates a tie/refund outcome.']
  wager_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the wager structure. \'straight\' is a single-selection bet; \'parlay\' combines multiple selections for higher payout; \'teaser\' adjusts point spreads; \'prop\' is a proposition bet on specific in-game events; \'futures\' bets on season-long outcomes; \'round_robin\' is a series of parlays. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: straight|parlay|teaser|prop|futures|round_robin|if_bet|reverse — promote to reference product]']

  Note: 'Core transactional record of every individual bet placed by a registered bettor across all wagering channels — retail sportsbook, mobile app, kiosk, and online platform. Captures bettor account ID, betting market ID, selection, stake amount, odds at time of placement, potential payout, wager type (straight, parlay, teaser, prop), placement channel, placement timestamp, wager status (pending, won, lost, void, cashed out), settlement amount, and settlement timestamp. Primary transactional entity for sportsbook operations and regulatory reporting.'
}

Table gaming.bettor_account {
