Captures payout type, bettor account ID, wager or contest entry reference, gross payout amount, tax withholding amount (W-2G threshold compliance), net payout amount, payout method (wallet credit, check, ACH, wire), payout timestamp, and payout status. Supports financial reconciliation, tax reporting (IRS W-2G), and regulatory GGR calculations.'
}

Table gaming.operator {
  operator_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for operator']
  company_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.company_code. Business justification: Gaming operators are licensed legal entities mapped to company codes for financial reporting, intercompany transactions, and tax registration. Finance needs operator-to-company-code attribution for co']
  facility_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to venue.facility. Business justification: Retail sportsbook operators are physically licensed to operate at specific venue facilities. Linking operator to facility enables retail license management, revenue-share reporting, and regulatory com']
  governing_body_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.governing_body. Business justification: Operators are licensed and overseen by specific governing bodies (state gaming commissions, sports integrity bodies). License issuance, renewal, and enforcement are tracked against the governing body.']
  parent_operator_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on operator (parent_operator_id)']
  aml_compliance_program boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the operator has an active AML compliance program in place.']
  api_integration_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the operator provides API integration capabilities for third-party systems and partners.']
  deposit_limit_enforcement boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the operator enforces customer-set deposit limits as part of responsible gaming measures.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date when the operator record became effective and the operator began operations or partnership.']
  geolocation_verification_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the operator uses geolocation technology to verify player location for regulatory compliance.']
  headquarters_address varchar [note: 'Physical street address of the operator\'s corporate headquarters or principal place of business.']
  headquarters_city varchar [note: 'City where the operator\'s corporate headquarters is located.']
  headquarters_country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code for the operator\'s headquarters location.']
  headquarters_postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code for the operator\'s headquarters address.']
  headquarters_state_province varchar [note: 'State or province where the operator\'s corporate headquarters is located.']
  jurisdiction_code varchar [note: 'Regulatory jurisdiction code where the operator is licensed to conduct gaming operations (e.g., NV, NJ-DGE, UK-GC).']
  kyc_verification_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the operator is required to perform KYC verification on customers per regulatory requirements.']
  license_expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the current gaming license expires and requires renewal for continued operations.']
  license_issue_date date [note: 'Date when the gaming license was originally issued to the operator by the regulatory authority.']
  license_number varchar [note: 'Official gaming license number issued by the regulatory authority for this operator.']
  license_status varchar [note: 'Current regulatory license status of the operator indicating their authorization to conduct gaming operations.']
  live_betting_supported boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the operator supports in-play or live betting on sporting events.']
  minimum_age_requirement varchar [note: 'Minimum legal age required for customers to participate in gaming activities with this operator.']
  mobile_app_available boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the operator offers a dedicated mobile application for gaming activities.']
  multi_currency_support boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the operator supports transactions in multiple currencies beyond the primary operating currency.']
  odds_feed_provider varchar [note: 'Name of the third-party vendor supplying real-time odds and betting market data to the operator.']
  operating_currency_code varchar [note: 'Primary three-letter ISO currency code used for the operator\'s gaming transactions and financial reporting.']
  operator_code varchar [note: 'Unique alphanumeric code assigned to the operator for system identification and regulatory reporting.']
  operator_name varchar [note: 'Legal business name of the gaming operator entity.']
  operator_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the gaming operator based on primary business model and gaming offerings.']
  parent_company_name varchar [note: 'Legal name of the parent corporation or holding company that owns this gaming operator.']
  payment_processor varchar [note: 'Name of the payment processing vendor handling financial transactions for the operator.']
  platform_provider varchar [note: 'Name of the technology platform provider or vendor that powers the operator\'s gaming systems.']
  primary_contact_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the primary business contact for operator communications and coordination.']
  primary_contact_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the primary business contact person for the operator.']
  primary_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Telephone number of the primary business contact for the operator.']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this operator record was first created in the system.']
  reporting_threshold_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Monetary threshold above which the operator must report transactions to regulatory authorities.']
  responsible_gaming_certification_body varchar [note: 'Name of the organization that issued the responsible gaming certification to the operator.']
  responsible_gaming_certified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the operator holds current responsible gaming certification from a recognized authority.']
  self_exclusion_program boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the operator offers a self-exclusion program for responsible gaming.']
  tax_identification_number varchar [note: 'Government-issued tax identification number for the operator entity used for tax reporting and compliance.']
  tax_withholding_rate decimal(5,4) [note: 'Default tax withholding rate applied to customer winnings by the operator as required by jurisdiction tax laws.']
  termination_date date [note: 'Date when the operator ceased operations or partnership relationship ended. Null if currently active.']
  website_url varchar [note: 'Primary website URL for the operator\'s gaming platform or corporate site.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for operator. Referenced by operator_id.'
}

Table gaming.selection {
  selection_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for selection']
  athlete_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to athlete.athlete_profile. Business justification: Sportsbooks must identify which athlete a selection references for integrity monitoring, suspension/injury-triggered market suspension, and regulatory reporting on athlete-linked wagering. selection.p']
  betting_market_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the betting market that contains this selection.']
  fixture_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the sporting event or entertainment event associated with this selection.']
  match_result_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to event.match_result. Business justification: Selection settlement (e.g., \'Home Win\', \'Over 2.5 goals\') is determined by the official match_result. The settlement pipeline must link each selection to the confirmed result for automated grading, pa']
  parent_selection_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on selection (parent_selection_id)']
  participant_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the competitor (team, athlete, or participant) represented by this selection. Nullable for non-competitor selections (e.g., over/under totals).']
  scoring_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to event.scoring_event. Business justification: Selections for \'first scorer\', \'anytime scorer\', and \'next goal\' markets are settled by referencing the specific scoring_event that determines the outcome. Automated settlement and integrity audit tra']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this selection record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for stake and payout amounts associated with this selection (e.g., USD, GBP, EUR).']
  current_odds_american varchar [note: 'Most recent odds for this selection, expressed in American moneyline format. Updated in real-time during live betting.']
  current_odds_decimal decimal(10,4) [note: 'Most recent odds for this selection, expressed in decimal format. Updated in real-time during live betting.']
  display_order varchar [note: 'Numeric sequence for ordering selections within a market for consistent presentation across betting interfaces.']
  effective_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this selection is no longer available for wagering (e.g., event start time, market close time). Null for open-ended selections.']
  effective_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this selection becomes available for wagering.']
  external_provider_code varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for this selection in the external odds feed provider system for data reconciliation and integration.']
  handicap_value decimal(10,2) [note: 'Numeric handicap or spread value applied to this selection (e.g., +3.5, -7.0). Null for non-handicap selections.']
  is_favorite boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this selection is the favorite (expected winner) in the market based on odds.']
  is_featured boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this selection is promoted or featured in marketing and user interfaces.']
  is_restricted boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this selection has betting restrictions applied (e.g., limited stake, excluded from promotions) for responsible gaming or regulatory compliance.']
  jurisdiction_code varchar [note: 'Regulatory jurisdiction code where this selection is offered, critical for compliance and responsible gaming reporting (e.g., NJ, UK, ONT).']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this selection record was last updated in the system.']
  max_stake_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Maximum wager amount allowed on this selection for responsible gaming and risk management. Null if no limit applies.']
  min_stake_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Minimum wager amount required for this selection. Null if no minimum applies.']
  odds_last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the current odds were last updated, critical for live betting and odds feed reconciliation.']
  opening_odds_american varchar [note: 'Initial odds offered for this selection when the market opened, expressed in American moneyline format (e.g., +150, -200).']
  opening_odds_decimal decimal(10,4) [note: 'Initial odds offered for this selection when the market opened, expressed in decimal format (e.g., 2.50).']
  provider_name varchar [note: 'Name of the external odds feed provider supplying data for this selection (e.g., SportsRadar, Betgenius, Don Best).']
  restriction_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation for why betting restrictions are applied to this selection (e.g., regulatory requirement, integrity concern, responsible gaming measure). Null if not restricted.']
  result varchar [note: 'Final outcome of the selection after event completion and settlement (win, loss, push/tie, void, or pending settlement).']
  selection_category varchar [note: 'Broader categorization of the selection for market segmentation and reporting (moneyline, handicap, total, proposition, futures, live in-play).']
  selection_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique business identifier for the selection used across betting systems and odds feeds.']
  selection_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the selection providing additional context for bettors (e.g., specific prop bet conditions, special rules).']
  selection_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the selection as displayed to bettors (e.g., team name, player name, outcome description).']
  selection_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the selection indicating availability for wagering and settlement state.']
  selection_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the selection indicating the nature of the wagering option (team, individual player, binary outcome, proposition bet, over/under total, point spread).']
  settlement_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the selection was officially settled and results were finalized for payout processing.']
  short_name varchar [note: 'Abbreviated name of the selection for display in constrained UI spaces (e.g., mobile betting slips).']
  total_value decimal(10,2) [note: 'Numeric total or line value for over/under selections (e.g., 45.5 points). Null for non-total selections.']
  void_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation for why the selection was voided (e.g., event cancellation, participant withdrawal, rule violation). Null if not voided.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for selection. Referenced by selection_id.'
}

Table security.credential {
  credential_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated surrogate identifier for each security credential record. Primary key for the credential master table — SSOT for credential identity across all access control operations.']
  athlete_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to athlete.athlete_profile. Business justification: Credentials (facility access, locker room, sideline passes) are issued directly to athletes. Credentialing operations require linking each credential to the athlete it was issued to for access managem']
  credential_event_fixture_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific event for which this credential is valid, if the credential is event-scoped rather than season- or venue-scoped. Null for standing/permanent credentials valid across all events at a venue.']
  credential_fixture_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific event for which this credential is valid, if the credential is event-scoped rather than season- or venue-scoped. Null for standing/permanent credentials valid across all events at a venue.']
  eligibility_certification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.eligibility_certification. Business justification: Credentials are issued based on approved eligibility certifications (TUEs, competition eligibility, broadcast access approvals). Accreditation workflows require the credential record to reference the ']
  franchise_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to league.franchise. Business justification: Credentials are issued to franchise staff, team personnel, and media. Franchise-level credential issuance audits, access control compliance reporting, and league credentialing reviews require linking ']
  media_rights_deal_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to broadcast.media_rights_deal. Business justification: Rights-holder accreditation audit: credentials issued to broadcast media personnel must be validated against an active media rights deal. Compliance teams verify that credentialed journalists, camera ']
  official_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to league.official. Business justification: Officials require security credentials for each game they officiate. Security issues and validates credentials for referees/umpires. Officiating management and pre-game credentialing workflows depend ']
  reissued_from_credential_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on credential (reissued_from_credential_id)']
  season_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the league season or tournament for which this credential is valid when multi_event_flag is True. Null for single-event credentials.']
  tournament_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to event.tournament. Business justification: Tournament-scoped credentials (World Cup press passes, Olympic accreditations) are valid across all fixtures in a tournament, not tied to a single fixture. credential.multi_event_flag signals this con']
  venue_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the venue at which this credential grants access. For multi-venue credentials, this references the primary or home venue; access to additional venues is managed via the access zone configuration.']
  access_tier varchar [note: 'Hierarchical access level granted by this credential, defining which physical zones the holder may enter. Values: field_level (playing surface and sidelines), locker_room (team areas), press_box (media and broadcast areas), general_operations (back-of-house operations), vip_suite (premium hospitality areas), restricted_zone (security command, broadcast infrastructure). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: field_level|locker_room|press_box|general_operations|vip_suite|restricted_zone|medical_area|broadcast_compound — promote to reference product]']
  access_zone_list varchar [note: 'Comma-delimited list of specific zone codes within the venue that this credential authorizes entry to (e.g., \'Z01,Z04,Z07,Z12\'). Complements access_tier with granular zone-level control as configured in the access control system.']
  accreditation_body varchar [note: 'Name of the external governing body or league office that accredited this credential (e.g., \'FIFA Accreditation Centre\', \'NFL League Office\', \'IOC Media Accreditation\'). Relevant for media passes and law enforcement credentials requiring external validation.']
  accreditation_reference varchar [note: 'External reference number or accreditation ID assigned by the governing body or league office (e.g., FIFA accreditation number, IOC media pass number). Used for cross-referencing with external accreditation systems.']
  background_check_date date [note: 'Date on which the background check for this credential holder was completed and a determination was made. Used to assess recency of screening and trigger renewal workflows.']
  background_check_status varchar [note: 'Status of the background screening conducted on the credential holder prior to issuance. Classified as confidential business-sensitive data. Values: cleared (passed), pending (in progress), failed (did not pass), waived (exempted by policy), not_required (credential type exempt).']
  barcode_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Encoded barcode string (1D or 2D/QR) printed on or embedded in the credential, scanned at access control checkpoints. Classified as confidential as it is the operational key for physical access. Managed in alignment with Ticketmaster/AXS access control systems.']
  biometric_linked boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this credential has been linked to a biometric profile (e.g., fingerprint, facial recognition) for enhanced identity verification at high-security access points. True = biometric data is enrolled and active for this credential.']
  biometric_type varchar [note: 'Type of biometric modality enrolled for this credential when biometric_linked is True. Classified as restricted PII/biometric per GDPR Article 9 and CCPA. Values: fingerprint, facial_recognition, iris_scan, palm_vein, none.']
  color_code varchar [note: 'Hex color code representing the physical color of the credential lanyard or card stock, used for rapid visual identification of access tier by security staff at checkpoints (e.g., #FF0000 for red = restricted zone, #00FF00 for green = general operations).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this credential record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail, chain-of-custody tracking, and compliance reporting. Conforms to ISO 8601 format yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  credential_number varchar [note: 'Externally visible, human-readable credential identifier printed on the physical badge or encoded in the digital pass (e.g., CRED-STAFF-000123). Used by security personnel and access control systems to reference a specific credential without exposing the internal surrogate key.']
  credential_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the credential. active: valid and in use; suspended: temporarily deactivated (e.g., investigation); revoked: permanently cancelled; expired: past validity end date; pending_activation: issued but not yet activated; lost_stolen: reported missing and deactivated for security.']
  credential_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the credential by the category of holder and access purpose. Drives access tier rules and issuance workflows. Values: staff_badge (venue/event staff), media_pass (accredited press/broadcast), vip_access_card (VIP guests and sponsors), contractor_id (third-party contractors), law_enforcement (police/security agencies), athlete_team (players, coaches, team officials).']
  emergency_contact_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the emergency contact person for the credential holder, used by venue medical and security staff in the event of an incident. Classified as restricted PII per GDPR and CCPA.']
  emergency_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Phone number of the emergency contact person for the credential holder. Classified as restricted PII per GDPR and CCPA. Used by venue medical and security staff in emergency situations.']
  escort_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the credential holder must be accompanied by an authorized escort when accessing certain zones. True = escort is mandatory. Used for contractor and visitor credentials in high-security areas.']
  holder_organization varchar [note: 'Name of the organization, team, media outlet, agency, or employer that the credential holder represents (e.g., \'ESPN\', \'Dallas Cowboys\', \'ABC Security Services\'). Used for affiliation-based access grouping and sponsor/partner credential management.']
  holder_role varchar [note: 'Functional role or job title of the credential holder within the event or venue context (e.g., \'Head Coach\', \'Camera Operator\', \'Paramedic\', \'Venue Director\'). Informs access tier assignment and is displayed on the physical credential.']
  issued_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the credential was physically produced and issued to the holder. Distinct from valid_from (when access begins) — a credential may be issued days before it becomes active.']
  issuing_authority varchar [note: 'Name of the organizational unit or external body responsible for issuing and authorizing this credential (e.g., \'NFL Security Office\', \'FIFA Accreditation Centre\', \'Venue Security Manager\'). Establishes chain-of-custody accountability.']
  issuing_authority_code varchar [note: 'Standardized short code identifying the issuing authority (e.g., \'NFL_SEC\', \'FIFA_ACC\', \'VENUE_MGR\'). Used for system-level filtering and reporting across multi-league or multi-event deployments.']
  law_enforcement_agency varchar [note: 'Name of the law enforcement agency represented by the credential holder (e.g., \'NYPD\', \'FBI\', \'US Secret Service\'). Populated only for credential_type = law_enforcement. Used for coordination with venue security command.']
  law_enforcement_badge_number varchar [note: 'Official badge or shield number of the law enforcement officer, used to verify identity against agency records. Classified as confidential. Populated only for credential_type = law_enforcement.']
  multi_event_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this credential is valid across multiple events (e.g., a season-long staff badge or a tournament-wide media pass) rather than a single event. True = valid for multiple events within the validity period.']
  photo_url varchar [note: 'Secure URL pointing to the credential holder\'s photograph stored in the Digital Asset Management (DAM) system, used for visual identity verification by security personnel. Classified as restricted biometric PII per GDPR Article 9.']
  print_template_code varchar [note: 'Code referencing the physical credential print template used for this credential type (e.g., \'TMPL-MEDIA-2024\', \'TMPL-STAFF-VIP\'). Determines layout, branding, and security features printed on the badge.']
  revocation_date date [note: 'Date on which the credential was revoked or suspended. Populated only when credential_status is revoked, suspended, or lost_stolen. Part of the chain-of-custody audit trail.']
  revocation_reason varchar [note: 'Reason code explaining why the credential was revoked or suspended. Supports security incident analysis and compliance reporting. Values: lost, stolen, employment_terminated, event_concluded, policy_violation, voluntary_surrender, duplicate_issued. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: lost|stolen|employment_terminated|event_concluded|policy_violation|voluntary_surrender|duplicate_issued — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  rfid_tag_code varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the RFID chip embedded in the credential card or wristband, used for contactless access control scanning. Classified as confidential as it is a physical access key. Null if the credential does not use RFID technology.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the operational system of record from which this credential record originated (e.g., TICKETMASTER_AXS for access control credentials, ARCHTICS for venue-managed credentials, MANUAL_ENTRY for credentials issued outside automated systems). Supports data lineage in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this credential record. Used for change tracking, audit trail, and incremental data pipeline processing in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  valid_until date [note: 'Date on which the credential expires and access is automatically revoked. For event credentials, typically the day after the event concludes. For season credentials, the last day of the season. Null indicates an open-ended standing credential subject to manual review.']
  vehicle_access_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this credential also grants vehicle access to restricted parking or loading dock areas at the venue. True = vehicle access is authorized in addition to pedestrian access.']
  vehicle_plate_number varchar [note: 'License plate number of the vehicle authorized for venue access under this credential when vehicle_access_flag is True. Classified as confidential. Null if vehicle access is not granted.']
  valid_from date [note: 'Date from which the credential becomes active and grants access. For event credentials, typically the event setup/load-in date. For season credentials, the first day of the season.']

  Note: 'Master record for all security credentials issued to personnel operating at Sports Entertainment venues and events — staff badges, media passes, VIP access cards, contractor IDs, law enforcement credentials, and athlete/team credentials. Captures credential type, holder identity reference, issuing authority, validity period, access tier, biometric linkage flag, barcode/RFID identifier, revocation status, and chain-of-custody audit fields. SSOT for credential identity across all access control operations.'
}

Table security.access_zone {
  access_zone_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each physical access control zone record within the Sports Entertainment security master catalog.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Each access zone (field-level, media, VIP, concourse) has dedicated staffing and operational costs tracked to a cost center. Venue finance teams budget and report security labor and maintenance costs ']
  facility_id varchar [note: 'FK to venue.facility.facility_id — Security zones must be linked to their physical venue facility for operational deployment planning and zone-to-venue mapping.']
  franchise_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to league.franchise. Business justification: Franchise-controlled zones (locker rooms, team benches, owner suites) are access zones tied to a specific franchise. Security staff validate credentials against franchise-specific zone ownership. Fran']
  parent_access_zone_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on access_zone (parent_access_zone_id)']
  venue_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent venue where this access zone is physically located. Used to scope zone records to a specific Sports Entertainment facility.']
  access_close_time timestamp [note: 'Standard time of day (HH:MM, 24-hour format) at which access to this zone closes relative to event day operations (e.g., \'23:59\' for post-event lockdown). Used for access control system scheduling and security staffing deployment.']
  access_control_system varchar [note: 'Name or type of the electronic access control system managing entry/exit to this zone (e.g., Ticketmaster/AXS turnstile, Lenel, C-Cure, Genetec, Honeywell, manual staffed checkpoint, or none). Determines integration points for real-time access event logging. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: ticketmaster_axs|lenel|ccure|genetec|honeywell|manual|none — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  access_open_time timestamp [note: 'Standard time of day (HH:MM, 24-hour format) at which access to this zone opens relative to event day operations (e.g., \'06:00\' for early crew access). Used for access control system scheduling and security staffing deployment.']
  ada_accessible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the access zone meets ADA accessibility requirements including wheelchair access, accessible entry/exit points, and compliant pathways (True = ADA compliant, False = not compliant or not assessed). Required for ADA regulatory reporting.']
  adjacent_zone_codes varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of zone_codes for physically adjacent access zones sharing a boundary or transition point with this zone. Used for crowd flow modeling, spillover risk assessment, and emergency egress path analysis. Note: a dedicated zone_adjacency junction product should be used for full relational modeling.']
  alcohol_permitted_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether alcohol consumption and/or service is permitted within this zone (True = permitted, False = not permitted). Used for concession operations planning, liquor licensing compliance, and fan experience management.']
  broadcast_zone_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this zone is designated as a broadcast or media production area (True = broadcast zone, False = non-broadcast). Triggers media credential requirements and coordination with broadcast operations teams and OB (Outside Broadcast) units.']
  camera_count varchar [note: 'Number of surveillance cameras installed and actively monitoring the access zone. Used for security audit compliance, coverage gap analysis, and incident response planning.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this access zone record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail, data lineage, and compliance with SOX and GDPR record-keeping requirements.']
  credential_type_required varchar [note: 'Type of credential required for access to this zone (e.g., event_ticket, staff_badge, media_credential, vip_wristband, law_enforcement_id, biometric, all_access_pass). Drives credentialing issuance rules in Ticketmaster/AXS and physical security systems. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: event_ticket|staff_badge|media_credential|vip_wristband|law_enforcement_id|biometric|all_access_pass|none — promote to reference product]']
  effective_from_date date [note: 'Date from which this access zone definition became operationally active and enforceable. Used for historical zone configuration tracking, audit trails, and compliance with venue certification requirements.']
  effective_until_date date [note: 'Date on which this access zone definition expires or is superseded. Null indicates the zone definition is currently open-ended and active. Used for zone lifecycle management and historical configuration auditing.']
  emergency_assembly_point_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this zone serves as a designated emergency assembly or muster point during evacuation scenarios (True = designated assembly point, False = not designated). Used in emergency response plans and evacuation drills.']
  entry_point_count varchar [note: 'Number of physical entry points (gates, turnstiles, doors, checkpoints) providing access into this zone. Used for crowd flow throughput modeling, staffing calculations, and emergency egress planning.']
  evacuation_route_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the primary designated evacuation route assigned to this zone in the venue emergency response plan. Used by security command center and first responders during emergency scenarios.']
  exit_point_count varchar [note: 'Number of physical exit points from this zone. Used for emergency evacuation planning, egress compliance with fire safety codes, and crowd flow modeling.']
  floor_area_sqm decimal(10,2) [note: 'Physical floor area of the access zone measured in square metres. Used to calculate occupancy density ratios, crowd flow modeling, and compliance with safety regulations requiring minimum space per person.']
  hvac_zone_reference varchar [note: 'Reference code linking this access zone to the corresponding HVAC zone in the Building Management System (BMS). Used for environmental control coordination during events and emergency ventilation management.']
  last_safety_inspection_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent physical safety inspection conducted for this access zone by internal safety officers or external regulatory inspectors. Used for OSHA compliance tracking and venue certification renewals.']
  law_enforcement_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether sworn law enforcement officers are required to be stationed at or within this zone during events (True = required, False = not required). Used for event security planning, law enforcement coordination, and regulatory compliance.']
  level_number varchar [note: 'Numeric floor or level within the venue where the zone is located (e.g., 0 = ground/field level, 1 = concourse level, 2 = upper deck, -1 = sub-ground). Used for wayfinding, evacuation routing, and zone adjacency mapping.']
  map_coordinates varchar [note: 'Geographic or venue-relative coordinate string (e.g., GeoJSON polygon or venue grid reference) defining the physical boundary of the zone on the venue floor plan. Used for digital venue mapping, crowd flow modeling, and real-time occupancy visualization.']
  max_occupancy varchar [note: 'Maximum number of persons permitted within the zone simultaneously as defined by fire safety codes, venue engineering assessments, and regulatory approvals. Used for crowd flow modeling, real-time occupancy monitoring, and ADA compliance reporting.']
  min_security_staff_count varchar [note: 'Minimum number of security personnel required to be deployed in this zone during an active event. Used for event staffing plans, security contractor scheduling, and compliance with venue safety regulations.']
  next_safety_inspection_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next mandatory safety inspection of this access zone. Used for compliance calendar management and proactive safety governance.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for operational notes, special instructions, or temporary conditions relevant to this access zone (e.g., \'Temporary barrier installed for renovation\', \'Law enforcement post required for playoff events\'). Used by security operations staff.']
  re_entry_permitted_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether credential holders who have exited this zone are permitted to re-enter during the same event (True = re-entry allowed, False = no re-entry). Drives access control system configuration in Ticketmaster/AXS.']
  screening_protocol varchar [note: 'Security screening method applied at entry points to this zone: none, visual_check, metal_detector, magnetometer, pat_down, full_search, or biometric_scan. Determines staffing requirements and equipment deployment for event security operations. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: none|visual_check|metal_detector|magnetometer|pat_down|full_search|biometric_scan — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  section_reference varchar [note: 'Venue section or block designation within which the access zone falls (e.g., \'North Stand\', \'Section 112\', \'East Wing\'). Aligns with Archtics seating inventory and venue map coordinates for operational cross-referencing.']
  smoking_permitted_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether smoking is permitted within this zone (True = permitted, False = not permitted). Used for venue policy enforcement, health and safety compliance, and fan experience communications.']
  surveillance_coverage_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the access zone is covered by CCTV or other surveillance systems (True = covered, False = not covered). Critical for security planning, incident investigation, and compliance with venue safety regulations.']
  time_restricted_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether access to this zone is restricted to specific time windows during an event (True = time-restricted, False = open throughout event). When True, access_open_time and access_close_time define the permitted window.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this access zone record was most recently modified. Used for change tracking, audit trail, and data freshness monitoring in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  vip_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this zone is designated as a VIP hospitality or premium access area (True = VIP zone, False = standard zone). Drives premium credentialing rules, enhanced security protocols, and sponsor/partner access management.']
  zone_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the access zone within a venue (e.g., FIELD-01, PRESS-BOX-A, VIP-SUITE-N). Used on credentials, wristbands, and access control hardware programming.']
  zone_color_code varchar [note: 'Hexadecimal color code used to visually represent this zone on venue maps, security dashboards, and credentialing systems (e.g., #FF0000 for red restricted zones, #00FF00 for public green zones). Standardizes visual communication across security operations.']
  zone_description varchar [note: 'Free-text narrative description of the access zone including its physical characteristics, operational purpose, notable features, and any special access conditions not captured in structured fields. Used for security briefings and operational documentation.']
  zone_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable descriptive name of the access zone (e.g., \'Field Level / Pitch Side\', \'Home Team Locker Room\', \'Broadcast Compound\', \'VIP Hospitality Suite North\'). Used in operational reports and credentialing systems.']
  zone_status varchar [note: 'Current operational lifecycle status of the access zone: active (in normal use), inactive (temporarily disabled), under_maintenance (physical works in progress), decommissioned (permanently retired), temporary (event-specific pop-up zone). Drives whether the zone is enforced in access control systems.']
  zone_tier varchar [note: 'Security classification tier of the zone defining the baseline access control level: public (general admission concourse), restricted (credentialed staff/media), secured (law enforcement / command), sterile (field/pitch/locker room — highest protection). Drives credentialing rules and screening protocols.']
  zone_type varchar [note: 'Functional category of the zone describing its operational purpose: field_pitch, locker_room, press_box, broadcast_compound, vip_hospitality, back_of_house, command_center, parking_compound, public_concourse, medical, production_truck_bay, merchandise_storage. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: field_pitch|locker_room|press_box|broadcast_compound|vip_hospitality|back_of_house|command_center|parking_compound|public_concourse|medical|production_truck_bay|merchandise_storage — promote to reference product]']

  Note: 'Master catalog of all defined physical access control zones within Sports Entertainment venues — field/pitch level, locker rooms, press box, broadcast compound, VIP hospitality, back-of-house, command center, parking compounds, and public concourse zones. Captures zone code, zone name, venue reference, zone tier (public/restricted/secured/sterile), maximum occupancy, surveillance coverage flag, and zone adjacency metadata used for crowd flow modeling.'
}

Table security.credential_assignment {
  credential_assignment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each credential assignment record. Primary key for the credential_assignment data product in the security domain.']
  access_zone_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the primary access control zone or area that this credential assignment grants entry to. Supports zone-based access management within the venue.']
  activation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sponsorship.sponsorship_activation. Business justification: Sponsor staff, brand reps, and VIP guests require credential assignments tied to specific activations (e.g., Pepsi halftime zone, Nike fan experience). Security ops issue credentials per activation to']
  athlete_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to athlete.athlete_profile. Business justification: Credential assignments are made to athletes for specific events and venues. Linking assignments to athlete profiles enables access management reporting, credential audit trails, and enforcement of sus']
  credential_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific credential (badge, pass, wristband, RFID card) being assigned to the holder. Links to the credential master record.']
  franchise_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to league.franchise. Business justification: Credential assignments for franchise staff and media require franchise attribution for franchise-level access control audits, league credentialing compliance reporting, and franchise-specific access z']
  holder_fan_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fan.fan_profile. Business justification: Credential assignments for fan holders (season ticket, VIP, fan club) must reference the fan profile to enable event-day access verification, ban enforcement checks, and post-event attendance reportin']
  league_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to league.league_schedule. Business justification: Game-day credential assignments for media, officials, and team staff are organized around the specific scheduled game. Pre-game credentialing workflows and post-game access audits require linking assi']
  league_season_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to league.season. Business justification: Season-scoped credential assignments support league-wide access control audits, franchise credential compliance reviews, and CBA-mandated media/staff access protocol reporting across the full season.']
  media_rights_deal_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to broadcast.media_rights_deal. Business justification: Media rights accreditation workflow: credential assignments for broadcast personnel are governed by the media rights deal that entitles the rights holder to field/press-box access. Rights compliance a']
  primary_credential_event_fixture_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific sports or entertainment event for which this credential assignment is valid. Null if the assignment is venue-wide or period-based rather than event-specific.']
  superseded_credential_assignment_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on credential_assignment (superseded_credential_assignment_id)']
  venue_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the venue or facility where this credential assignment is valid. Supports venue-wide credentialing independent of a specific event.']
  access_level varchar [note: 'Categorical access tier granted by this credential assignment, defining the scope of areas the holder may enter within the venue. Drives access control system enforcement at entry points. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: field|locker_room|press_box|backstage|general|restricted|all_access|vip|production — promote to reference product]']
  assignment_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the credential was formally assigned to the holder by the credentialing authority. Represents the principal business event date for this transaction.']
  assignment_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known human-readable business identifier for the credential assignment, used in credentialing workflows, printed on assignment receipts, and referenced in post-event reconciliation reports. Format: CA-{YYYY}-{NNNNNN}.']
  assignment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the credential assignment. Drives operational workflows including pickup confirmation, access control enforcement, and post-event reconciliation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: pending|issued|active|suspended|revoked|returned|expired|lost|stolen — promote to reference product]']
  assignment_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time at which the credential assignment was created in the system, capturing the exact moment of authorization. Used for audit trail and security incident investigation.']
  background_check_date date [note: 'Date on which the background check for the credential holder was completed or last updated. Used to determine if a background check is current and valid for the assignment period.']
  background_check_status varchar [note: 'Status of the background screening conducted on the credential holder prior to credential issuance. Cleared status is required for access to restricted zones such as locker rooms and field areas.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp recording when this credential assignment record was first created in the data platform. Used for audit trail, data lineage, and compliance reporting.']
  credential_scope varchar [note: 'Defines the temporal and event scope of the credential assignment — whether it is valid for a single event, multiple events, an entire season, or the venue broadly. Drives validity period enforcement and renewal workflows.']
  credential_type varchar [note: 'Physical or digital form factor of the credential being assigned. Determines issuance workflow, pickup process, and return reconciliation procedures.']
  escort_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the credential holder must be accompanied by an authorized escort when accessing credentialed areas. True when escort is mandatory; False when holder may access independently.']
  event_day_number varchar [note: 'For multi-day events (tournaments, festivals, conventions), the specific day number within the event for which this credential is valid. Null for single-day events or season-wide credentials.']
  holder_id_number varchar [note: 'Government-issued or organizational identification number (e.g., employee ID, press pass number, law enforcement badge number) used to verify the identity of the credential holder at time of issuance.']
  holder_organization varchar [note: 'Name of the organization, employer, or entity that the credential holder represents (e.g., \'ESPN\', \'ABC Security Services\', \'NFL Players Association\'). Used for credentialing workflow routing and sponsor/media tracking.']
  holder_photo_url varchar [note: 'Secure URL reference to the credential holder\'s photo image stored in the Digital Asset Management (DAM) system. Used for visual identity verification at access control points. Subject to biometric data protection regulations.']
  holder_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the individual receiving the credential, defining their role and relationship to the event or venue. Drives access zone eligibility and credentialing workflow routing. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: staff|media|contractor|law_enforcement|athlete|sponsor|vip|volunteer|official|vendor — promote to reference product]']
  issued_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time at which the physical or digital credential was formally issued and activated in the access control system. Distinct from assignment_timestamp (when the record was created) and pickup_timestamp (when the holder collected it).']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp recording when this credential assignment record was most recently modified. Used for change tracking, audit trail, and incremental data pipeline processing.']
  law_enforcement_agency varchar [note: 'Name of the law enforcement agency represented by the credential holder, applicable when holder_type is \'law_enforcement\' (e.g., \'NYPD\', \'FBI\', \'US Secret Service\'). Used for law enforcement coordination and post-event reporting.']
  meal_entitlement boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the credential assignment includes meal or catering entitlement in designated hospitality areas (e.g., press dining, staff cafeteria). Used for catering planning and cost allocation.']
  multi_zone_access boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this credential assignment grants access to multiple zones beyond the primary access zone. True when the holder is authorized for more than one zone; False for single-zone access.']
  parking_zone varchar [note: 'Designated parking zone or lot assigned to the credential holder when vehicle access is granted (e.g., \'Lot A - Media\', \'Field Level - Broadcast\', \'VIP Garage\'). Null when vehicle_access is False.']
  pickup_confirmed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the credential holder has physically collected the credential from the credentialing desk. True when pickup has been confirmed; False when credential is still awaiting collection.']
  pickup_location varchar [note: 'Name or designation of the credentialing desk, gate, or location where the credential was or is to be collected by the holder (e.g., \'Media Gate North\', \'VIP Credentialing Desk Level 2\').']
  pickup_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time at which the credential holder physically collected the credential from the credentialing desk. Null if credential has not yet been picked up.']
  return_confirmed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the credential has been returned to the credentialing authority after the event or assignment period. True when return is confirmed; False when credential is still outstanding. Critical for post-event reconciliation.']
  return_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time at which the credential was returned to the credentialing authority. Null if credential has not yet been returned. Used in post-event reconciliation to identify unreturned credentials.']
  revocation_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text description of the reason a credential assignment was revoked or suspended before its natural expiry (e.g., \'Security incident\', \'Holder no longer authorized\', \'Lost credential reported\'). Null when assignment has not been revoked.']
  revocation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time at which the credential assignment was revoked or suspended. Null when assignment has not been revoked. Used for security incident investigation and access control audit trails.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record from which this credential assignment record originated (e.g., Ticketmaster/AXS access control, Archtics venue seating and inventory, manual credentialing desk entry). Supports data lineage and reconciliation.']
  special_access_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field capturing any special access permissions, restrictions, or annotations associated with this credential assignment (e.g., \'Authorized for post-game press conference only\', \'Escort required at all times\'). Supports non-standard credentialing scenarios.']
  valid_until timestamp [note: 'Date and time at which the credential assignment expires and access is no longer granted. Null for open-ended assignments. Critical for post-event reconciliation and access revocation.']
  vehicle_access boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this credential assignment includes authorization for vehicle access to restricted venue areas such as loading docks, field-level parking, or broadcast compound. True when vehicle access is granted.']
  valid_from timestamp [note: 'Date and time from which the credential assignment becomes active and grants access. Supports time-bounded credentialing for multi-day events or seasonal venue access.']

  Note: 'Transactional record linking a specific credential to an authorized holder for a defined event or period. Captures credential ID, holder type (staff, media, contractor, law enforcement, athlete, sponsor), event or venue scope, assignment date, assigned-by officer, pickup confirmation, return confirmation, and any special access annotations. Enables per-event credentialing workflows and post-event reconciliation.'
}

Table security.access_event {
  access_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each physical access control scan event record in the Silver layer lakehouse. Primary key for this table.']
  access_zone_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the access control zone (e.g., field level, media area, backstage, VIP suite corridor) that the credential holder attempted to enter.']
  athlete_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to athlete.athlete_profile. Business justification: Access events log athlete entry/exit at facilities. WADA whereabouts compliance requires documented facility access records for athletes in the registered testing pool. Security audits and incident in']
  calendar_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the scheduled sports or entertainment event during which this access scan occurred. Links the access record to the event context for occupancy and security reporting.']
  credential_assignment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to security.credential_assignment. Business justification: An access event validates a credential that was formally assigned to a holder via credential_assignment. While access_event already has credential_id, the credential_assignment record captures the spe']
  credential_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the credential (badge, card, biometric token, or digital pass) that was presented at the reader. Distinct from a fan ticket — covers staff, media, VIP, and operational credentials.']
  fixture_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the scheduled sports or entertainment event during which this access scan occurred. Links the access record to the event context for occupancy and security reporting.']
  override_of_access_event_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on access_event (override_of_access_event_id)']
  primary_access_fixture_id varchar [note: 'FK to event.fixture.fixture_id — Event-correlated access control — security operations must correlate access scans to specific events for crowd management, capacity monitoring, and incident investigation.']
  screening_checkpoint_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to security.screening_checkpoint. Business justification: An access control scan event (access_event) occurs at a physical location — often a screening checkpoint. The access_event table currently stores checkpoint_name as a denormalized STRING. Adding scree']
  security_incident_id varchar [note: 'Reference to a security incident record if this access event triggered or was associated with a security incident. Null when no incident was raised. Supports post-event forensic review and incident management workflows.']
  venue_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the venue where the access event occurred. Used to associate the scan event with a specific Sports Entertainment facility.']
  access_direction varchar [note: 'Direction of movement at the checkpoint: \'entry\' = entering the zone or venue, \'exit\' = leaving the zone or venue, \'internal\' = movement between internal zones. Used for real-time occupancy tracking and crowd flow analysis.']
  ada_accommodation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the credential holder required an ADA (Americans with Disabilities Act) accommodation at this access point (e.g., accessible gate routing). True = ADA accommodation applied; False = standard access.']
  anti_passback_zone varchar [note: 'Identifier of the anti-passback zone group assigned to this checkpoint. Anti-passback rules prevent a credential from being used to enter a zone it has not exited. Used for tailgating detection and occupancy integrity.']
  biometric_verified_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether biometric verification (e.g., facial recognition, fingerprint) was used as part of the credential authentication for this scan event. True = biometric match confirmed; False = biometric not used or not matched.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this access event record was first written to the Silver layer lakehouse. Distinct from scan_timestamp (the real-world event time). Used for data pipeline audit trails and SLA monitoring.']
  credential_holder_category varchar [note: 'Broad category of the person holding the credential. Distinct from credential_type (which describes the credential itself); this describes the person\'s role. Used for access pattern analytics and compliance reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: athlete|staff|media|vip|vendor|law_enforcement|official — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  credential_token varchar [note: 'Tokenized or masked representation of the physical credential identifier (e.g., RFID card number, barcode, NFC token) as presented to the reader. Stored in tokenized form to comply with PCI-DSS and GDPR. Not the raw credential number.']
  credential_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the credential presented at the reader. Distinguishes between staff, media, VIP, vendor, law enforcement, athlete, and digital mobile credentials. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: staff_badge|media_pass|vip_credential|vendor_pass|law_enforcement|athlete_credential|digital_mobile — promote to reference product]']
  data_retention_expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which this access event record is scheduled for deletion or anonymization per applicable data retention policies (GDPR, CCPA). Supports automated data lifecycle management in the lakehouse.']
  denial_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code indicating why access was denied or flagged. Null when scan_result is \'granted\' or \'override_granted\'. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: INVALID_CREDENTIAL|EXPIRED_CREDENTIAL|ZONE_NOT_AUTHORIZED|BLACKLISTED|DUPLICATE_SCAN|CAPACITY_EXCEEDED|SYSTEM_ERROR — promote to reference product]']
  duplicate_scan_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this scan event was detected as a duplicate presentation of the same credential within a short time window (anti-passback detection). True = duplicate detected; False = unique scan.']
  emergency_evacuation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this scan event occurred during an active emergency evacuation protocol. True = evacuation mode active at time of scan; False = normal operations. Critical for post-incident headcount reconciliation.']
  event_phase varchar [note: 'Operational phase of the event at the time of the scan. Used to segment access patterns by event lifecycle stage for crowd management analytics and post-event review.']
  gdpr_data_subject_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the credential holder associated with this scan event is a data subject under GDPR jurisdiction (e.g., EU resident). True = GDPR data subject protections apply; False = not subject to GDPR. Drives data retention and erasure workflows.']
  law_enforcement_notified_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether law enforcement was notified as a result of this access event (e.g., blacklisted credential, flagged individual). True = law enforcement notification triggered; False = no notification.']
  network_zone varchar [note: 'Network segment or VLAN identifier of the reader device that processed this scan. Used for cybersecurity segmentation audits and forensic network analysis in the event of a security breach.']
  occupancy_count_at_scan varchar [note: 'Snapshot of the current occupancy count for the zone at the moment this scan event was processed. Captured at scan time to support real-time occupancy tracking and capacity compliance reporting.']
  officer_override_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a security officer manually overrode the system\'s access decision for this event. True = officer override applied; False = system decision was final. Critical for post-event forensic review and accountability audits.']
  override_justification varchar [note: 'Free-text or coded reason provided by the officer when performing a manual override. Required when officer_override_flag is True. Supports accountability and compliance audit trails.']
  reader_device_code varchar [note: 'Reference to the physical access control reader or scanner device that captured this scan event. Used for device-level diagnostics, tamper detection, and post-event forensic review.']
  reader_firmware_version varchar [note: 'Firmware version of the access control reader device at the time of the scan. Captured for forensic integrity, device audit trails, and vulnerability management compliance.']
  reader_location_code varchar [note: 'Alphanumeric code identifying the physical location of the reader device within the venue (e.g., \'NE-G1-R04\'). Supports device-level forensic analysis and maintenance tracking.']
  scan_duration_ms varchar [note: 'Time in milliseconds taken for the reader to process the credential and return a result. Used for throughput analysis, reader performance monitoring, and identifying bottlenecks at high-traffic checkpoints.']
  scan_result varchar [note: 'Outcome of the credential scan at the access point. \'granted\' = access permitted; \'denied\' = access refused; \'flagged\' = access held for secondary review; \'override_granted\' = officer manually permitted entry; \'timeout\' = reader did not receive a valid response. Core field for real-time occupancy tracking and compliance audit trails.']
  scan_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time (with timezone offset) when the credential was presented to the reader. The principal real-world event time for this access event. Formatted as yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  source_event_reference varchar [note: 'The original event transaction ID or record key from the source access control system (e.g., Ticketmaster/AXS transaction ID). Enables traceability back to the operational system of record for audit and reconciliation.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the access control system that originated this scan event record (e.g., AXS, Ticketmaster, Lenel, C-Cure, Genetec). Supports multi-system venue environments and data lineage tracking in the Silver layer.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this access event record was last modified in the Silver layer (e.g., enrichment, correction, incident linkage). Supports data lineage and audit trail requirements.']
  watchlist_match_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the credential or credential holder matched an entry on a security watchlist at the time of the scan. True = watchlist match detected; False = no match. Triggers escalation workflows.']
  zone_capacity varchar [note: 'Maximum permitted occupancy for the zone at the time of the scan event, as configured in the access control system. Used alongside occupancy_count_at_scan to determine capacity compliance status.']
  zone_level varchar [note: 'Security classification level of the zone being accessed at the time of the scan. Captures the sensitivity of the zone for risk-tiered reporting and compliance. Values: public, restricted, highly_restricted, field_level, backstage.']

  Note: 'High-volume transactional record of every physical access control scan event at venue entry and internal zone checkpoints — credential presented, zone accessed, timestamp, scan result (granted/denied/flagged), reader device ID, officer override flag, and denial reason code. Feeds real-time occupancy tracking, post-event forensic review, and compliance audit trails. Distinct from ticketing.gate_scan which covers fan ticket entitlement scanning.'
}

Table security.screening_checkpoint {
  screening_checkpoint_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each physical security screening checkpoint record in the Sports Entertainment venue security master. Primary key for the screening_checkpoint data product.']
  access_zone_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to security.access_zone. Business justification: A screening checkpoint controls physical entry into a specific access zone — it is the gateway to that zone. The existing access_zone_level (STRING) is a denormalized attribute describing the zone tie']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Screening checkpoint operations (staffing, equipment calibration, consumables) are budgeted and tracked per checkpoint in venue finance. Linking to cost_center enables per-gate cost reporting, staffin']
  parent_screening_checkpoint_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on screening_checkpoint (parent_screening_checkpoint_id)']
  venue_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Sports Entertainment venue where this screening checkpoint is physically deployed. Links to the venue master record.']
  activation_end_time timestamp [note: 'Standard time of day (HH:MM, 24-hour format) at which this checkpoint ceases screening operations and is deactivated. Used for egress planning and post-event security stand-down scheduling.']
  activation_schedule_type varchar [note: 'Defines the operational activation pattern for this checkpoint — whether it is staffed only on event days, permanently active, activated seasonally, or deployed on-demand for high-security events. Drives resource planning and cost allocation.']
  activation_start_time timestamp [note: 'Standard time of day (HH:MM, 24-hour format) at which this checkpoint is activated and begins screening operations relative to event start. Used for gate opening schedules and ingress management planning.']
  ada_lane_count varchar [note: 'Number of ADA (Americans with Disabilities Act) compliant accessible screening lanes available at this checkpoint. Distinct from the total lane count — used for ADA compliance reporting and accessibility planning.']
  checkpoint_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric business identifier for the screening checkpoint, used in operational communications, staffing assignments, and incident reporting (e.g., \'GATE-A-MAG-01\', \'VIP-BAG-03\'). Unique within a venue.']
  checkpoint_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable descriptive name for the screening checkpoint as displayed on operational maps, staff briefings, and incident reports (e.g., \'North Gate Magnetometer Lane 2\', \'VIP Credentialing Desk East\').']
  checkpoint_status varchar [note: 'Current operational lifecycle status of the screening checkpoint. Drives activation scheduling, staffing allocation, and security coverage gap analysis.']
  checkpoint_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the physical security screening checkpoint by its primary function and method of screening. Drives staffing requirements, equipment deployment, and throughput planning. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: magnetometer_lane|bag_check_station|pat_down_area|vehicle_inspection|credentialing_desk|explosive_trace_detection — promote to reference product if additional types are added]']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this screening checkpoint master record was first created in the system. Supports data lineage, audit trail requirements, and Silver layer ingestion tracking.']
  credential_type_accepted varchar [note: 'Type(s) of credentials or access passes accepted at this checkpoint for entry authorization (e.g., \'event_ticket\', \'media_credential\', \'staff_badge\', \'law_enforcement_id\'). Drives access control configuration in Ticketmaster/AXS. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: event_ticket|media_credential|staff_badge|law_enforcement_id|vip_pass|vendor_pass|athlete_credential — promote to reference product]']
  effective_from_date date [note: 'Date from which this screening checkpoint record is operationally valid and available for deployment. Supports checkpoint lifecycle management and historical security configuration auditing.']
  effective_until_date date [note: 'Date after which this screening checkpoint record is no longer operationally valid (e.g., due to decommissioning, venue renovation, or permanent closure). Nullable for indefinitely active checkpoints.']
  emergency_response_zone varchar [note: 'Designated emergency response zone code to which this checkpoint belongs for incident command and evacuation coordination. Aligns with the venue emergency response plan and law enforcement sector assignments.']
  equipment_asset_tag varchar [note: 'Physical asset tag or serial number of the primary screening equipment unit deployed at this checkpoint. Used for maintenance tracking, calibration scheduling, and asset lifecycle management in the facilities management system.']
  equipment_last_calibration_date date [note: 'Date on which the primary screening equipment at this checkpoint was most recently calibrated and certified for operational use. Required for regulatory compliance and safety audits.']
  equipment_next_calibration_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next mandatory calibration of the primary screening equipment at this checkpoint. Triggers maintenance work orders and ensures continuous compliance with security equipment standards.']
  event_type_applicability varchar [note: 'Specifies the category of events for which this checkpoint is activated (e.g., \'all_events\', \'sports_only\', \'concerts_only\', \'ppv_events\', \'high_profile_only\'). Supports dynamic checkpoint activation planning across the event calendar. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: all_events|sports_only|concerts_only|ppv_events|high_profile_only|private_events — promote to reference product]']
  floor_level varchar [note: 'Floor or level designation within the venue where this checkpoint is located (e.g., \'Ground\', \'Level 1\', \'Concourse\', \'Basement\'). Used for emergency evacuation planning and facility management.']
  gate_number varchar [note: 'Physical gate or entrance number associated with this screening checkpoint as labeled on venue maps and patron-facing signage (e.g., \'Gate 1\', \'Gate C\', \'Loading Dock 3\'). Used for wayfinding and incident location reporting.']
  is_ada_accessible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this screening checkpoint includes an ADA (Americans with Disabilities Act) compliant accessible lane for patrons with mobility impairments, wheelchairs, or other accessibility needs. Mandatory for venue ADA compliance reporting.']
  is_express_lane boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this checkpoint operates as an expedited screening lane for credentialed personnel, season ticket holders, VIP (Very Important Person) guests, or pre-screened patrons with reduced wait times.']
  is_vehicle_checkpoint boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this checkpoint is designated for vehicle inspection rather than pedestrian screening. Drives different equipment, staffing, and protocol requirements for parking lot and loading dock security.']
  lane_count varchar [note: 'Number of parallel screening lanes available at this checkpoint. A single checkpoint may have multiple lanes operating simultaneously to increase throughput capacity during peak ingress periods.']
  last_inspection_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent formal safety and compliance inspection of this checkpoint by internal security management or an external regulatory authority. Supports OSHA compliance tracking and audit readiness.']
  law_enforcement_post boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this checkpoint requires a dedicated law enforcement officer (LEO) post as part of the security deployment plan. Drives coordination with local police, sheriff, or federal law enforcement agencies.']
  max_queue_length_meters decimal(6,2) [note: 'Maximum physical queue length in meters that can be accommodated at this checkpoint before crowd management protocols are triggered. Used for ingress modeling and crowd density safety planning.']
  min_staff_required varchar [note: 'Minimum number of security personnel required to operate this checkpoint at baseline capacity. Used for staffing plans, shift scheduling, and SLA (Service Level Agreement) compliance with law enforcement partners.']
  next_inspection_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next mandatory safety and compliance inspection of this checkpoint. Triggers inspection work orders and ensures continuous regulatory compliance.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text operational notes or special instructions for this checkpoint, such as known physical constraints, special event configurations, or temporary modifications. Used by security supervisors during event briefings.']
  operating_sop_reference varchar [note: 'Document reference number or URL for the Standard Operating Procedure (SOP) governing operations at this checkpoint type. Ensures staff follow approved protocols and supports compliance audits.']
  optimal_staff_count varchar [note: 'Recommended number of security personnel for this checkpoint to achieve rated throughput capacity. Distinct from minimum staffing — represents the target headcount for peak event operations.']
  physical_location_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the precise physical location of this checkpoint within the venue (e.g., \'North concourse, adjacent to Section 101 entrance, ground level\'). Supports emergency response coordination and incident reporting.']
  primary_equipment_type varchar [note: 'Primary security screening equipment deployed at this checkpoint. WTMD (Walk-Through Metal Detector), X-ray (baggage X-ray scanner), ETD (Explosive Trace Detection). Drives maintenance scheduling, calibration records, and procurement planning. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: WTMD|X-ray|ETD|handheld_wand|visual_inspection|CCTV|vehicle_barrier — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  prohibited_items_list_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the prohibited items policy document governing what items are screened and confiscated at this checkpoint (e.g., \'v2.3\'). Ensures staff are operating under the current approved policy and supports audit traceability.']
  security_level varchar [note: 'Operational security threat level assigned to this checkpoint, which determines screening intensity, staffing multipliers, and equipment deployment. Aligns with DHS threat level frameworks and league-specific security mandates.']
  throughput_capacity_per_hour varchar [note: 'Rated maximum number of persons that can be screened through this checkpoint per hour under normal operating conditions. Used for crowd management planning, staffing optimization, and event ingress modeling.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this screening checkpoint master record was most recently modified. Supports change tracking, audit compliance, and incremental data pipeline processing in the Databricks Silver layer.']
  venue_zone_code varchar [note: 'Alphanumeric code identifying the specific security zone or sector within the venue where this checkpoint is located (e.g., \'ZONE-A\', \'CONCOURSE-2\', \'FIELD-LEVEL\'). Aligns with venue access control zone mapping.']

  Note: 'Master record for all physical security screening checkpoints deployed at Sports Entertainment venues — magnetometer lanes, bag check stations, pat-down areas, vehicle inspection points, and credentialing desks. Captures checkpoint code, venue zone assignment, checkpoint type, equipment deployed (WTMD, X-ray, ETD), staffing requirement, throughput capacity (persons/hour), activation schedule, and ADA-accessible lane flag.'
}

Table security.screening_event {
  screening_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each individual screening interaction record at a security checkpoint. Primary key for the screening_event data product in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  access_zone_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to security.access_zone. Business justification: A screening event occurs at a checkpoint that controls entry to a specific access zone. The existing access_zone_code (STRING) is a denormalized text reference to the zone; replacing it with a proper ']
  credential_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to security.credential. Business justification: A screening event involves a person presenting a credential for inspection. The screening_event table currently stores credential_number as a denormalized STRING, but the authoritative credential reco']
  fixture_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the sports or entertainment event during which this screening interaction took place. Enables post-event security review and cross-event trend analysis.']
  franchise_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to league.franchise. Business justification: Screening events at franchise home games must be franchise-attributed for league fan safety compliance reporting, franchise-level throughput benchmarking, and CBA-mandated safety protocol audits requi']
  league_season_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to league.season. Business justification: Season-level screening aggregation is required for league annual safety compliance submissions, prohibited items trend analysis, and CBA player/fan safety reviews covering all franchise home games.']
  membership_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fan.membership. Business justification: VIP and season-ticket members use express/priority screening lanes. Linking screening events to membership records validates lane entitlement, audits express-lane usage compliance, and supports member']
  policy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.policy. Business justification: Screening events are conducted under specific security and prohibited-items policies. Compliance audits of venue security operations require tracing each screening event to the governing policy versio']
  primary_screening_fan_profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the fan, staff member, or credentialed personnel who was screened. May be null for anonymous general admission screenings where identity is not captured.']
  primary_screening_fixture_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the sports or entertainment event during which this screening interaction took place. Enables post-event security review and cross-event trend analysis.']
  screened_athlete_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to athlete.athlete_profile. Business justification: Athletes pass through security screening checkpoints at venues. Linking screening events to athlete profiles supports WADA whereabouts compliance documentation, security audit trails, and incident inv']
  screening_checkpoint_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific security checkpoint location where this screening interaction occurred. Used to analyze throughput and incident rates by checkpoint.']
  security_incident_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to security.security_incident. Business justification: A screening event may trigger a security incident when a prohibited item is found, an individual is denied entry and becomes aggressive, or law enforcement is notified. The existing incident_report_nu']
  venue_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the venue facility where the screening occurred. Supports venue-level security performance benchmarking and ADA/OSHA compliance reporting.']
