  ada_accommodation_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the screened individual required an ADA accommodation during the screening process (e.g., wheelchair user, medical device wearer). Supports ADA compliance reporting and accessibility audit requirements.']
  alarm_resolution varchar [note: 'The resolution or explanation for an equipment alarm triggered during screening. Null when no alarm was triggered. Supports false-positive rate analysis and equipment calibration review.']
  confiscation_receipt_number varchar [note: 'Reference number issued to the individual when a prohibited item is confiscated, enabling item retrieval after the event if applicable. Null when no item was confiscated. Supports chain-of-custody documentation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this screening event record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail, data lineage, and Silver Layer ingestion tracking.']
  credential_type varchar [note: 'The type of access credential presented by the individual at the checkpoint (e.g., event ticket, staff badge, media credential). Used to validate authorized access and support post-event credentialing audits. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: ticket|season_pass|staff_badge|media_credential|vendor_pass|vip_wristband — promote to reference product]']
  crowd_density_level varchar [note: 'Operational assessment of crowd density at the checkpoint at the time of this screening interaction. Supports crowd management protocol activation analysis and post-event safety review.']
  data_source_system varchar [note: 'The operational system of record from which this screening event record was ingested into the Databricks Silver Layer (e.g., \'Ticketmaster/AXS\', \'venue_access_control_system\'). Supports data lineage and audit trail requirements.']
  entry_denied_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text or coded reason why entry was denied to the screened individual. Null when entry was not denied. Supports denied-entry trend analysis and policy enforcement review.']
  equipment_alarm_triggered_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the screening equipment (e.g., magnetometer, X-ray) triggered an alarm during this interaction. Distinguishes equipment-initiated alerts from officer-initiated secondary screenings.']
  equipment_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the specific screening equipment unit used (e.g., magnetometer unit serial number, X-ray machine ID). Supports equipment maintenance tracking, calibration compliance, and fault correlation analysis.']
  escalation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this screening interaction was escalated beyond the initial checkpoint officer, such as to a supervisor, law enforcement, or medical personnel. Key field for post-event security review and incident management reporting.']
  escalation_type varchar [note: 'The type of escalation triggered by this screening interaction. Null or \'none\' when no escalation occurred. Used to categorize escalation patterns and resource deployment analysis.']
  gate_number varchar [note: 'The physical gate or entry point identifier at the venue where the screening checkpoint is located. Used for throughput analysis and crowd flow management.']
  item_confiscated_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the detected prohibited item was physically confiscated from the individual. Distinct from denied_entry outcome — an item may be confiscated while the individual is still permitted entry. Supports confiscation log and chain-of-custody tracking.']
  lane_number varchar [note: 'The specific screening lane within a checkpoint where the interaction occurred. Multiple lanes may operate at a single gate or checkpoint. Used for lane-level throughput and staffing analysis.']
  law_enforcement_case_number varchar [note: 'The official case or incident number assigned by law enforcement when they were notified or took action related to this screening event. Null when law enforcement was not involved. Enables cross-referencing with law enforcement records.']
  law_enforcement_notified_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether local or venue law enforcement was formally notified as part of this screening interaction. Supports law enforcement coordination reporting and incident documentation.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for the screening officer to record additional observations, context, or details about the screening interaction not captured in structured fields. Used for incident documentation and post-event review.']
  prohibited_item_category varchar [note: 'High-level category of the prohibited item detected during screening. Null when no prohibited item was found. Supports trend analysis and policy review. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: weapon_firearm|weapon_edged|weapon_blunt|explosive|controlled_substance|alcohol|oversized_bag|laser_device|other — promote to reference product]']
  prohibited_item_description varchar [note: 'Free-text description of the specific prohibited item detected (e.g., \'folding knife, 3-inch blade\', \'half-pint whiskey flask\'). Null when no prohibited item was found. Used for detailed incident documentation and law enforcement reporting.']
  prohibited_item_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a prohibited item was detected during this screening interaction. True when any prohibited item was found, regardless of whether it was confiscated or the individual was denied entry. Key driver for prohibited item trend analysis.']
  queue_wait_time_seconds varchar [note: 'The estimated or measured time in seconds the individual waited in the queue before reaching the screening checkpoint. Supports fan experience analysis, crowd management planning, and SLA reporting for entry operations.']
  screened_person_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the individual being screened, distinguishing fans from credentialed personnel, VIP guests, media, athletes, and law enforcement. Supports segmented security analytics and credentialing compliance. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: fan|staff|vendor|media|vip|athlete|law_enforcement — promote to reference product]']
  screening_duration_seconds varchar [note: 'The elapsed time in seconds from the start to the completion of the screening interaction. Used for checkpoint throughput analysis, staffing optimization, and identifying bottlenecks in the fan entry process.']
  screening_method varchar [note: 'The physical or technological method used to conduct the screening. Supports equipment utilization analysis and compliance with venue security protocols. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: walk_through_magnetometer|hand_wand|pat_down|bag_x_ray|visual_inspection|explosive_trace_detection — promote to reference product]']
  screening_outcome varchar [note: 'The result of the screening interaction indicating the disposition of the screened individual. Drives escalation workflows, prohibited item trend analysis, and checkpoint throughput reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: cleared|secondary_screening|denied_entry|prohibited_item_confiscated|medical_referral|law_enforcement_referral — promote to reference product]']
  screening_reference_number varchar [note: 'Externally-visible alphanumeric reference code assigned to this screening interaction, used for incident reporting, law enforcement coordination, and audit trail purposes. Format: SCR-{YEAR}-{EVENTID}-{SEQUENCE}.']
  screening_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time when the screening interaction occurred at the checkpoint. Principal business event timestamp used for throughput analysis, timeline reconstruction, and post-event security review.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this screening event record was last modified, such as when an escalation outcome was updated or an incident report number was added. Supports audit trail and data quality monitoring.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of individual fan or personnel screening interactions at security checkpoints — capturing checkpoint ID, timestamp, screening outcome (cleared/secondary screening/denied entry/prohibited item confiscated), prohibited item type if applicable, officer ID, and escalation flag. Supports post-event security review, prohibited item trend analysis, and checkpoint throughput reporting.'
}

Table security.security_incident {
  security_incident_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each security incident record in the Sports Entertainment security management system. Primary key for the security_incident data product.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Security incident cost allocation is a core sports-venue finance process: medical response, law enforcement overtime, property damage, and legal liability costs must be charged to the responsible cost']
  escalated_from_security_incident_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on security_incident (escalated_from_security_incident_id)']
  event_day_ops_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to venue.event_day_ops. Business justification: Security incidents must be linked to the event day ops record for post-event incident reporting, insurance claim correlation, and league governing body submissions. Event day ops tracks incident_count']
  franchise_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to league.franchise. Business justification: Security incidents at games are franchise-attributed for league disciplinary review, insurance claims, and competitive integrity reporting. League annual safety compliance reports aggregate incidents ']
  game_result_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to league.game_result. Business justification: Security incidents affecting game play (field invasions, player altercations) must reference the certified game result for insurance claims, league integrity reviews, and protest adjudication. League ']
  involved_athlete_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to athlete.athlete_profile. Business justification: Security incident reports in sports venues must identify involved athletes (on-field altercations, locker room incidents, fan confrontations). League discipline, insurance claims, and broadcast disclo']
  league_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to league.league_schedule. Business justification: Security incidents at games must reference the league schedule record for game-day incident management, post-game league reporting, and schedule impact assessment (postponements, forfeitures). League ']
  league_season_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to league.season. Business justification: Season-level incident aggregation is required for league safety compliance reporting, CBA player/fan safety provision reviews, and year-over-year trend analysis submitted to league governing bodies an']
  membership_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fan.membership. Business justification: League incident reporting and insurance claims require membership tier context at time of incident. VIP/season-ticket incidents trigger escalated CRM and legal protocols. Direct FK avoids multi-join t']
  primary_security_event_fixture_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific Sports Entertainment event (game, concert, match) during which the security incident occurred. Enables event-level incident density analysis and post-event security debriefs.']
  primary_security_fan_fan_profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the registered fan profile in the CRM system (Salesforce CRM / Adobe Experience Platform) if the involved party is an identified fan. Enables cross-referencing with ticketing history, ban records, and loyalty status.']
  retail_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to merchandise.retail_location. Business justification: Theft, fraud, and counterfeit-goods incidents occur at specific retail locations inside venues. Loss prevention reporting, insurance claims, and shrinkage analysis require attributing security inciden']
  surveillance_asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to content.asset. Business justification: Security incident investigations require linking to the DAM asset containing the surveillance footage clip. Post-incident review, insurance claims, and law enforcement handoff processes all depend on ']
  tournament_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to event.tournament. Business justification: Governing bodies (FIFA, IOC, UEFA) require tournament-level security incident aggregate reports for compliance and licensing. security_incident has fixture_id but no tournament_id; deriving tournament']
  venue_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Sports Entertainment venue where the security incident occurred. Links to the venue master record for facility-level reporting and ADA/OSHA compliance tracking.']
  ban_duration_days varchar [note: 'Duration in days of the venue ban issued to the involved party. A value of -1 or null indicates a permanent ban. Populated when ban_issued is True.']
  ban_issued boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a venue ban or trespass notice was issued to the involved party as a result of this incident. Triggers downstream ban enforcement processes in Ticketmaster/AXS access control.']
  closed_at timestamp [note: 'Date and time the security incident case was formally closed following completion of all required actions, investigations, and documentation. Null if the incident remains open or under review.']
  closure_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes recorded at the time of case closure, summarizing final outcomes, follow-up actions completed, and any lessons learned for security operations improvement.']
  created_at timestamp [note: 'System-generated timestamp when the security incident record was first created in the data platform. Used for audit trail and data lineage purposes.']
  credential_type_violated varchar [note: 'Type of access credential involved in a credentialing violation incident (e.g., forged media pass, unauthorized field access badge). Populated when incident_type is credentialing_violation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: media|staff|vip|field_access|broadcast|vendor|athlete — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  crowd_density_level varchar [note: 'Estimated crowd density level in the incident zone at the time of the event. SRO (Standing Room Only) indicates maximum capacity. Used for crowd management protocol analysis and venue safety planning.']
  disposition varchar [note: 'Final outcome or action taken as a result of the security incident. Drives downstream processes including ban enforcement, medical referral, and law enforcement handoff. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: ejected|arrested|warned|banned|referred_medical|no_action|trespass_notice|cited — promote to reference product]']
  emergency_response_plan_activated boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the venue\'s formal Emergency Response Plan (ERP) was activated in response to this incident. Required for post-incident compliance review and insurance documentation.']
  evacuation_triggered boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the incident triggered a partial or full venue evacuation protocol. Drives post-incident emergency response plan review and regulatory reporting obligations.']
  gate_identifier varchar [note: 'Identifier of the entry/exit gate associated with the incident location, relevant for access control violations, prohibited item seizures, and credentialing breaches detected at ingress/egress points.']
  incident_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the security incident case, tracking progression from initial report through investigation to final disposition and closure.']
  incident_type varchar [note: 'Categorical classification of the security incident describing the nature of the event. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: fan_altercation|ejection|prohibited_item_seizure|field_invasion|credentialing_violation|threat|suspicious_package|law_enforcement_intervention|trespassing|vandalism|theft|harassment — promote to reference product]']
  insurance_claim_filed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether an insurance claim was filed in connection with this security incident. Triggers financial reporting obligations and coordination with the Finance domain.']
  insurance_claim_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number for the insurance claim filed in connection with this incident. Confidential business data used for financial reconciliation in SAP S/4HANA. Populated when insurance_claim_filed is True.']
  involved_party_id_number varchar [note: 'Government-issued or venue-issued identification number (e.g., driver\'s license, passport, fan membership ID) for the primary involved party. Restricted PII used for law enforcement coordination and ban enforcement.']
  involved_party_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the primary individual involved in the security incident (subject, suspect, or victim). Classified as restricted PII under GDPR and CCPA. Required for law enforcement reporting and case management.']
  involved_party_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the primary party involved in or subject to the security incident. Determines applicable regulatory obligations (e.g., GDPR for fan data, CBA provisions for athlete incidents). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: fan|athlete|staff|vendor|media|vip_guest|unknown — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  law_enforcement_agency varchar [note: 'Name of the law enforcement agency notified or involved in the incident response (e.g., NYPD, FBI Field Office, State Police). Populated when law_enforcement_notified is True.']
  law_enforcement_case_number varchar [note: 'Official case or report number assigned by the responding law enforcement agency. Used for cross-referencing with external law enforcement records and legal proceedings.']
  law_enforcement_notified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether local law enforcement (police, FBI, or other agency) was notified of the security incident. Required for regulatory reporting and incident severity escalation tracking.']
  league_governing_body_reported boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the incident was reported to the relevant league governing body (e.g., NFL, NBA, FIFA, NHL) per league security reporting obligations. Required for compliance with league security protocols and CBA provisions.']
  medical_referral_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the incident required a medical referral or emergency medical services (EMS) response. Triggers coordination with venue.safety_incident domain for OSHA recordkeeping when True.']
  narrative varchar [note: 'Free-text detailed description of the security incident as recorded by the responding officer, including sequence of events, actions taken, and any relevant contextual observations. Primary source for legal and insurance documentation.']
  number_of_persons_involved varchar [note: 'Total count of individuals directly involved in the security incident (subjects, victims, and witnesses). Used for incident severity assessment and resource deployment analysis.']
  occurred_at timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time the security incident occurred or was first observed, representing the principal real-world event time. Distinct from the record creation timestamp. Critical for timeline reconstruction and law enforcement reporting.']
  prohibited_item_description varchar [note: 'Free-text description of the specific prohibited item seized, including make, model, quantity, or other identifying characteristics. Supports chain-of-custody documentation and law enforcement handoff.']
  prohibited_item_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the prohibited item seized during the incident, applicable when incident_type is prohibited_item_seizure (e.g., weapon, alcohol, pyrotechnic, laser pointer, drone). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: weapon|alcohol|pyrotechnic|laser_pointer|drone|controlled_substance|recording_device|other — promote to reference product]']
  reference_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric case number assigned at the time of incident creation, used for cross-system tracking, law enforcement reporting, and insurance claims. Format: SEC-YYYY-NNNNNN.']
  reported_at timestamp [note: 'Date and time the incident was formally reported to the security operations center or duty manager, which may differ from the occurrence time. Used to measure reporting lag and response readiness.']
  response_time_minutes varchar [note: 'Elapsed time in minutes between the incident occurrence timestamp and the arrival of the first responding security officer. Key performance indicator (KPI) for security operations SLA compliance and staffing adequacy reviews.']
  section_identifier varchar [note: 'Specific section, block, or area designation within the venue zone where the incident took place (e.g., Section 112, Row G, Gate 4 Entry). Sourced from Archtics seating inventory for precise location mapping.']
  severity_tier varchar [note: 'Standardized severity classification assigned to the incident at time of reporting, used to prioritize response resources and escalation protocols. Tier 1 Critical requires immediate law enforcement; Tier 4 Low is handled by venue security staff.']
  surveillance_footage_available boolean [note: 'Indicates whether CCTV or surveillance camera footage is available and has been preserved for this incident. Critical for evidence management, legal proceedings, and post-incident review.']
  updated_at timestamp [note: 'System-generated timestamp of the most recent modification to the security incident record, supporting audit trail requirements and change tracking.']
  zone_code varchar [note: 'Alphanumeric code identifying the access control zone within the venue where the incident occurred (e.g., GA-FLOOR, VIP-SUITE, FIELD-LEVEL, CONCOURSE-A). Aligns with Archtics venue seating and zone inventory definitions.']

  Note: 'Core transactional record for all security incidents occurring at Sports Entertainment venues and events — fan altercations, ejections, prohibited item seizures, field invasions, credentialing violations, threats, suspicious packages, and law enforcement interventions. Captures incident type, severity tier, location (zone/section/gate), involved parties, responding officers, law enforcement notification flag, medical referral flag, narrative, disposition, and case closure status. Distinct from venue.safety_incident which covers physical safety/OSHA events.'
}

Table security.ejection_record {
  ejection_record_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each ejection record. Primary key for the ejection_record data product within the security domain.']
  access_entitlement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ticketing.access_entitlement. Business justification: Immediate entitlement voiding on ejection: when a fan is ejected, their access entitlement must be voided in real time to prevent re-entry. Security operations require a direct reference to the entitl']
  calendar_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Sports Entertainment event during which the ejection occurred. Used to correlate ejection volume with event type, attendance, and security staffing levels.']
  ejected_athlete_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to athlete.athlete_profile. Business justification: Athletes are ejected from games for on-field conduct violations. Ejection records must reference the athlete profile to trigger league discipline reviews, suspension determinations, and broadcast repo']
  ejecting_official_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to league.official. Business justification: When a game official initiates an ejection, the ejection record must reference the specific official for officiating performance reviews and disciplinary accountability. ejecting_officer_badge is a de']
  fan_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the fan\'s CRM account record in Salesforce CRM, used to link the ejection to the fan\'s profile for ban enforcement, repeat offender tracking, and communication history.']
  fan_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fan.fan_profile. Business justification: Ejection history must be surfaced in the fan CRM profile for repeat-offender tracking, ban enforcement, and league regulatory reporting. The existing account link is insufficient for identity resoluti']
  fixture_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Sports Entertainment event during which the ejection occurred. Used to correlate ejection volume with event type, attendance, and security staffing levels.']
  franchise_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to league.franchise. Business justification: Ejection records must be franchise-attributed for league disciplinary review, franchise-level fan conduct reporting, and CBA compliance covering fan safety provisions. League operations tracks ejectio']
  game_result_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to league.game_result. Business justification: Ejection records must be tied to the official game result for post-game disciplinary review, league integrity reporting, and game certification. League operations teams cross-reference ejections with ']
  investigation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.investigation. Business justification: Ejections involving serious misconduct (assault, racism, repeat offenders) escalate to formal compliance investigations for league conduct review and regulatory reporting. Security operations teams ne']
  league_season_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to league.season. Business justification: Season-scoped ejection aggregation is required for league annual fan conduct reports, franchise compliance reviews, and year-over-year safety trend analysis submitted to league governing bodies.']
  membership_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fan.membership. Business justification: Ejection records for season ticket holders and members must reference the active membership for ban enforcement (membership suspension/revocation), season ticket revocation decisions, and legal docume']
  policy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.policy. Business justification: Ejections are executed under specific fan conduct policies (code of conduct, zero-tolerance policy). Fan conduct enforcement, appeals processing, and legal defense of ejection decisions require tracin']
  security_incident_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the originating security incident record that triggered this ejection. Links the ejection to the broader incident context including initial report, escalation chain, and responding officers.']
  surveillance_asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to content.asset. Business justification: Ejection records require structured references to surveillance footage assets for fan ban appeals, law enforcement handoff, and ban registry documentation. The current surveillance_clip_reference is a']
  ticket_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ticketing.ticket_order. Business justification: Ejection-to-order workflow: when a fan is ejected, the ticket order must be identified to void remaining access, initiate applicable refunds, and flag the account for ban enforcement. ejection_record.']
  venue_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the venue where the ejection took place. Supports venue-level security analytics, staffing benchmarks, and ADA/OSHA compliance reporting.']
  appeal_filed_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the ejected individual has formally filed an appeal against the ejection decision. True = appeal filed; False = no appeal filed. Triggers the ejection review workflow and updates ejection_status to \'appealed\'.']
  appeal_resolution_date date [note: 'The date on which the formal appeal of the ejection was resolved. Populated when ejection_status transitions to \'upheld\' or \'overturned\'. Used for SLA (Service Level Agreement) tracking of the appeals process.']
  arrest_made_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the ejected individual was formally arrested by law enforcement at or following the ejection. True = arrest was made; False = no arrest. Relevant for legal proceedings, insurance claims, and ban severity determination.']
  ban_recommendation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the ejecting officer or supervisor has recommended a venue ban for the ejected individual. True = ban recommended; False = no ban recommended. Feeds the venue ban registry workflow for formal ban issuance.']
  ban_registry_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the formal ban record created in the venue ban registry as a result of this ejection. Populated after the ban is formally issued following the recommendation workflow. Null if no ban was issued.']
  ban_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the recommended or issued ban scope. Populated when ban_recommendation_flag is True. Drives the ban registry entry type and enforcement duration. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: single_event|season|multi_season|lifetime|league_wide — promote to reference product]']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this ejection record was first created in the system. Represents the audit trail creation time, distinct from the ejection_timestamp which captures the real-world event time. Required for data lineage and compliance audit trails.']
  crowd_density_level varchar [note: 'Assessed crowd density level in the ejection zone at the time of the incident. Used for crowd management analysis, security staffing optimization, and post-event safety reviews. Aligns with ISO 20121 event sustainability and safety management standards.']
  data_source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record from which this ejection record originated or was primarily sourced. Supports data lineage tracking and Silver Layer reconciliation in the Databricks Lakehouse.']
  ejected_party_id_number varchar [note: 'The identification document number (e.g., driver\'s license number, passport number) of the ejected individual as recorded for ban enforcement and law enforcement handoff. Stored in compliance with GDPR and CCPA data minimization principles.']
  ejected_party_id_type varchar [note: 'Type of identification document presented or used to identify the ejected individual at the time of ejection. Supports identity verification for ban enforcement and law enforcement handoff.']
  ejected_party_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the individual who was ejected, as recorded at the time of ejection from identification documents or ticketing records. Required for ban enforcement, law enforcement handoff documentation, and legal proceedings.']
  ejected_party_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the type of individual who was ejected. Determines the applicable code of conduct, ban enforcement pathway, and reporting requirements. VIP (Very Important Person) guests and media personnel follow distinct escalation protocols.']
  ejection_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized reason code classifying the primary cause of the ejection. Drives ban recommendation logic, repeat offender scoring, and regulatory reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: intoxication|fighting|field_invasion|harassment|prohibited_item|threatening_behavior|racial_abuse|ticket_fraud — promote to reference product]']
  ejection_reason_description varchar [note: 'Free-text narrative description of the specific circumstances leading to the ejection, as recorded by the ejecting security officer. Supplements the standardized reason code with contextual detail for incident review and legal proceedings.']
  ejection_reference_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric reference number assigned to this ejection at the time of occurrence, used in law enforcement handoff documentation, ban notices, and fan appeals. Format: EJ-{YYYY}-{NNNNNN}.']
  ejection_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the ejection record. \'pending\' indicates the ejection is being processed; \'confirmed\' means the ejection is finalized; \'appealed\' means the ejected party has filed a formal appeal; \'overturned\' means the ejection was reversed upon review; \'upheld\' means the ejection was sustained after appeal.']
  ejection_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time at which the ejection was executed — i.e., when the individual physically exited or was removed from the venue. This is the principal real-world business event time, distinct from when the record was created in the system.']
  law_enforcement_agency varchar [note: 'Name of the law enforcement agency to which the ejected individual was handed off (e.g., \'NYPD\', \'LAPD\', \'Stadium Authority Police\'). Populated only when law_enforcement_handoff_flag is True.']
  law_enforcement_case_number varchar [note: 'The official case or report number assigned by the receiving law enforcement agency at the time of handoff. Used for cross-referencing with police records and legal proceedings.']
  law_enforcement_handoff_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the ejected individual was handed off to law enforcement (police, stadium security authority) at the time of ejection. True = law enforcement was involved; False = internal security handled the ejection without law enforcement transfer.']
  medical_assistance_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether medical assistance was required for the ejected individual or any other party involved in the incident (e.g., intoxication requiring EMT response, injury from altercation). True = medical assistance was provided or requested; False = no medical assistance needed.']
  prior_ejection_count varchar [note: 'The number of prior ejections on record for this individual at the time of this ejection event. Used to determine escalation thresholds for ban recommendations and repeat offender classification. Sourced from the ejection history at time of record creation.']
  prohibited_item_description varchar [note: 'Description of any prohibited item(s) found on or associated with the ejected individual. Populated when ejection_reason_code is \'prohibited_item\'. Includes item type, quantity, and disposition (confiscated, surrendered, etc.).']
  repeat_offender_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the ejected individual has a prior ejection record within the Sports Entertainment system at the time of this ejection. True = prior ejection(s) on record; False = first-time ejection. Drives escalated ban recommendations and repeat offender tracking workflows.']
  seat_number varchar [note: 'The specific seat number assigned to the ejected individual within their row and section. Enables precise location correlation with ticketing records for ban enforcement and refund processing.']
  seat_row varchar [note: 'The row identifier within the seating section where the ejected individual was located. Used in conjunction with seat_section and seat_number for precise location tracking and witness identification.']
  seat_section varchar [note: 'The seating section identifier (e.g., \'114\', \'GA\', \'SRO\') where the ejected individual was located at the time of the incident, as recorded in Archtics or Ticketmaster/AXS access control systems.']
  surveillance_footage_available_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether surveillance camera footage of the ejection incident is available and has been flagged for retention. True = footage available and retained; False = no footage available. Supports legal proceedings, appeals, and security system audits.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this ejection record was most recently modified. Tracks updates to ejection status, ban recommendations, appeal outcomes, and law enforcement case numbers. Required for data lineage and Silver Layer change tracking.']
  venue_zone_code varchar [note: 'Alphanumeric code identifying the specific zone or section of the venue where the ejection incident originated, as defined in the Archtics venue seating and inventory management system (e.g., \'SEC-114\', \'FIELD-LEVEL\', \'CONCOURSE-B\'). Used for crowd management hotspot analysis.']
  venue_zone_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the venue zone where the ejection occurred. Supports access control analysis and security resource allocation by zone category. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: seating_bowl|field_level|concourse|vip_suite|media_zone|backstage|parking|entry_gate — promote to reference product]']

  Note: 'Transactional record of every fan or personnel ejection from a Sports Entertainment venue or event. Captures ejection date/time, venue zone, reason code (intoxication, fighting, field invasion, harassment, prohibited item), ejecting officer, law enforcement handoff flag, ban recommendation flag, originating security incident reference, and fan account reference for ban enforcement. Feeds the venue ban registry and repeat offender tracking.'
}

Table security.emergency_response_plan {
  emergency_response_plan_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each emergency response plan record in the Sports Entertainment security domain.']
  fixture_id varchar [note: 'Reference to a specific Sports Entertainment event for which this plan is activated or scoped. Null when the plan applies to the venue generally rather than a single event.']
  franchise_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to league.franchise. Business justification: Emergency response plans are developed per franchise home venue context. Franchise linkage enables franchise-specific plan compliance tracking, league safety audits, and regulatory submissions tied to']
  governing_body_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.governing_body. Business justification: Emergency response plans at sports venues require approval from governing bodies (DHS, league authorities, local fire marshal authorities tracked in compliance). The ERP already tracks dhs_approval_st']
  league_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to league.league. Business justification: Emergency response plans are mandated and governed at the league level (league-wide safety standards, CBA requirements). League compliance officers manage ERP approval and audit across all franchises.']
  league_season_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to league.season. Business justification: Season-scoped emergency plans are required for league annual safety compliance and DHS coordination. Season tagging enables plan version management aligned to league season lifecycle and CBA safety pr']
  obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.obligation. Business justification: Emergency response plans are created to fulfill specific regulatory obligations (fire safety, DHS venue security requirements, league safety mandates). Compliance tracking requires linking each plan t']
  policy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.policy. Business justification: Emergency response plans are mandated by and must comply with specific safety and security policies (DHS SAFETY Act, venue safety policy, league emergency protocols). Compliance audits of emergency pr']
  primary_superseded_by_plan_emergency_response_plan_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the newer emergency response plan that has replaced this version. Null if this is the current active version. Enables version chain traceability for audit and regulatory review.']
  tournament_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to event.tournament. Business justification: Tournament-level ERPs (FIFA World Cup, Olympic Games, championship tournaments) are a distinct operational construct from fixture-level ERPs. Governing bodies require tournament-scoped emergency plann']
  venue_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Sports Entertainment venue to which this emergency response plan applies. A plan may be venue-specific or cover multiple venues (see multi_venue_flag).']
  activation_trigger varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the specific conditions or thresholds that trigger activation of this emergency response plan (e.g., \'Confirmed armed individual on premises\', \'NWS Tornado Warning issued within 10-mile radius\'). Critical for incident command decision-making.']
  ada_accommodation_provisions varchar [note: 'Description of specific provisions within this plan for evacuating and sheltering guests and staff with disabilities, including wheelchair-accessible evacuation routes, Areas of Rescue Assistance (ARA), and mobility assistance protocols.']
  communication_protocol varchar [note: 'Description of the communication systems and protocols used during plan activation, including radio frequencies, PA system codes, mass notification system triggers, and social media blackout procedures.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this emergency response plan record was first created in the Sports Entertainment security data platform. Supports audit trail and regulatory document control requirements.']
  crowd_capacity_threshold varchar [note: 'Minimum crowd attendance count at which this plan\'s enhanced protocols are activated (e.g., plans may have tiered activation based on event size). Supports DHS Special Event Assessment Rating (SEAR) classification.']
  dhs_approval_date date [note: 'Date on which DHS formally approved this emergency response plan. Null if approval is pending, not required, or rejected.']
  dhs_approval_status varchar [note: 'Regulatory approval status of this plan with the U.S. Department of Homeland Security. Required for events classified under DHS Special Event Assessment Rating (SEAR) Tier I-III.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which this version of the emergency response plan becomes operationally binding. Used to determine which plan version is active for a given event date.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which this emergency response plan version expires and must be reviewed or renewed. Null for open-ended plans. Drives regulatory renewal workflows and compliance alerts.']
  fire_marshal_approval_date date [note: 'Date on which the local fire marshal formally approved this emergency response plan. Null if approval is pending, not required, or rejected.']
  fire_marshal_approval_status varchar [note: 'Regulatory approval status of this plan with the local fire marshal authority. Required for all venue events under NFPA 101 and local fire codes. Drives event permit issuance.']
  fire_marshal_contact varchar [note: 'Primary contact phone number for the local fire marshal office responsible for approving and coordinating fire evacuation components of this plan. Required for regulatory compliance.']
  incident_command_structure varchar [note: 'Description of the Incident Command System (ICS) hierarchy governing this plan, including Incident Commander role, Operations Section Chief, Safety Officer, and Public Information Officer designations per NIMS ICS standards.']
  incident_commander_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the designated Incident Commander responsible for directing emergency response operations under this plan. Confidential operational security information.']
  incident_commander_title varchar [note: 'Official job title of the designated Incident Commander (e.g., \'Director of Event Security\', \'VP Venue Operations\'). Used in regulatory filings and law enforcement coordination documents.']
  last_drill_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent tabletop exercise or full-scale drill conducted to validate this emergency response plan. Required for OSHA compliance and DHS preparedness certification.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this emergency response plan record. Used for change tracking, regulatory audit trails, and Silver Layer data lineage in the Databricks Lakehouse.']
  law_enforcement_agency varchar [note: 'Name of the primary law enforcement agency coordinating with Sports Entertainment under this plan (e.g., \'Las Vegas Metropolitan Police Department\', \'NYPD Special Events Unit\'). Required for DHS Special Event Assessment Rating (SEAR) submissions.']
  law_enforcement_contact varchar [note: 'Primary contact phone number or radio channel for law enforcement coordination during activation of this plan. Confidential operational security data restricted to authorized security personnel.']
  media_blackout_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this plan includes a media and social media blackout protocol during initial activation to prevent panic, protect operational security, and manage public communications through official channels only.']
  medical_triage_zone_count varchar [note: 'Number of designated medical triage zones established under this plan for mass casualty incident (MCI) response. Drives medical resource pre-positioning and EMS coordination planning.']
  multi_venue_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this emergency response plan applies to multiple venues simultaneously (e.g., a league-wide severe weather protocol). When True, venue_id represents the primary venue and additional venues are linked in a separate association table.']
  mutual_aid_agreement_ref varchar [note: 'Reference number or name of the mutual aid agreement with external agencies (neighboring jurisdictions, hospitals, EMS providers) that supports this plan. Enables cross-agency resource sharing during large-scale incidents.']
  next_review_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next mandatory review and update of this emergency response plan. Drives compliance calendar alerts and ensures plans remain current with regulatory changes and venue modifications.']
  plan_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric code uniquely identifying this emergency response plan across venues and regulatory submissions (e.g., ERP-FIRE-2024-001). Used in DHS filings, local fire marshal submissions, and law enforcement coordination documents.']
  plan_document_url varchar [note: 'Secure URL to the full emergency response plan document stored in the Sports Entertainment Digital Asset Management (DAM) system. Access restricted to authorized security and compliance personnel. Confidential operational security document.']
  plan_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable title of the emergency response plan (e.g., \'Active Shooter Response Plan – Allegiant Stadium 2024\'). Used in operational briefings, staff training materials, and regulatory filings.']
  plan_owner_department varchar [note: 'Organizational department responsible for this emergency response plan (e.g., \'Venue Security Operations\', \'Event Safety and Compliance\', \'Facilities Management\').']
  plan_owner_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the Sports Entertainment staff member accountable for maintaining, updating, and submitting this emergency response plan. Confidential internal personnel data.']
  plan_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the emergency response plan. \'Active\' indicates the plan is operationally deployed; \'superseded\' indicates a newer version has replaced it; \'retired\' indicates the plan is no longer applicable.']
  plan_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the emergency scenario this plan governs. Drives protocol selection, command structure, and law enforcement coordination requirements. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: active_shooter|bomb_threat|mass_casualty|fire_evacuation|severe_weather|medical_emergency — promote to reference product if additional types are needed]']
  primary_triage_zone_location varchar [note: 'Physical location description of the primary medical triage zone within the venue (e.g., \'Gate A Concourse Level 1\', \'Field Level South End Zone\'). Used by EMS and medical staff during MCI activation.']
  public_address_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code word or phrase used in venue public address announcements to signal activation of this plan without causing public panic (e.g., \'Code Alpha\' for medical emergency, \'Code Bravo\' for security threat).']
  regulatory_jurisdiction varchar [note: 'Geographic and regulatory jurisdiction governing this plan (e.g., \'Clark County, Nevada\', \'City of New York, NY\', \'Federal – DHS Tier 1\'). Determines which regulatory bodies must approve the plan.']
  sear_tier varchar [note: 'DHS Special Event Assessment Rating tier assigned to events covered by this plan. Tier 1 represents the highest national significance (e.g., Super Bowl, Olympics); Tier 3 represents significant regional events. Determines federal resource allocation and coordination requirements.']
  shelter_in_place_zone varchar [note: 'Designated venue area(s) for shelter-in-place protocols, applicable for severe weather, active threat, or hazmat scenarios. Includes interior hardened areas, concourse corridors, and below-grade spaces.']
  threat_level varchar [note: 'Severity classification of the emergency scenario addressed by this plan, aligned with DHS threat level taxonomy. Drives resource pre-positioning, law enforcement notification thresholds, and public communication protocols.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Semantic version identifier for this iteration of the emergency response plan (e.g., \'2.1.0\'). Enables tracking of plan revisions, regulatory re-submissions, and audit trails across plan lifecycle.']

  Note: 'Master record for all emergency response plans governing Sports Entertainment venues and events — active shooter protocols, bomb threat procedures, mass casualty incident (MCI) response, fire evacuation, severe weather shelter-in-place, and medical emergency escalation. Captures plan type, venue scope, version, activation triggers, command structure, law enforcement coordination contacts, medical triage zone assignments, and regulatory approval status (DHS, local fire marshal).'
}

Table security.emergency_activation {
  emergency_activation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each emergency response plan activation record. Primary key for the emergency_activation data product. Entity role: TRANSACTION_HEADER — discrete business event with a clear lifecycle.']
  broadcast_production_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to broadcast.production. Business justification: Emergency activations that interrupt live broadcasts must reference the specific broadcast production for incident command coordination, insurance claims, and regulatory reporting. emergency_activatio']
  emergency_response_plan_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific emergency response plan document that was activated. Links to the plan registry for version control and compliance tracking.']
  escalated_emergency_activation_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on emergency_activation (escalated_emergency_activation_id)']
  event_day_ops_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to venue.event_day_ops. Business justification: Emergency activations must be correlated to the specific event day ops record for after-action reporting, insurance claims, and league regulatory submissions. Venue operations and security teams joint']
  franchise_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to league.franchise. Business justification: Emergency activations at franchise home games must be franchise-attributed for league incident reporting, insurance claims, and CBA player safety compliance reviews. League tracks activations by franc']
  investigation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.investigation. Business justification: Emergency activations resulting in injuries, fatalities, or regulatory scrutiny trigger formal compliance investigations. Post-incident regulatory inquiries (crowd crush, terrorism response) require t']
  league_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to league.league_schedule. Business justification: Emergency activations during games trigger game suspension or cancellation decisions that must reference the league schedule. League operations and broadcast teams need this link to manage schedule im']
  league_season_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to league.season. Business justification: Season-level emergency activation records are required for league annual safety reports, regulatory filings, and CBA compliance reviews covering player and fan safety provisions across the full season']
  live_feed_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to broadcast.live_feed. Business justification: When an emergency interrupts a live broadcast, the specific live feed that was suspended must be recorded for after-action reports, broadcaster notification obligations, and insurance claims. emergenc']
  primary_emergency_event_fixture_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Sports Entertainment event (game, concert, match, etc.) during which this emergency activation occurred. Links activation to the event scheduling and ticketing context.']
  triggering_security_incident_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to security.security_incident. Business justification: An emergency activation is often triggered by a specific security incident — a bomb threat, mass casualty event, or severe altercation that escalates to emergency response plan activation. The existin']
  venue_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Sports Entertainment venue where the emergency activation was executed. Supports venue-level safety compliance reporting and ADA/OSHA audit trails.']
  activation_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known, human-readable business identifier for this emergency activation event (e.g., EMA-2024-000123). Used in after-action reports, law enforcement coordination, and regulatory filings. Serves as the BUSINESS_IDENTIFIER category for TRANSACTION_HEADER role.']
  activation_source varchar [note: 'Identifies how the emergency activation was triggered: manually by an authorized officer, automatically by an alarm or surveillance system, or reported by law enforcement, staff, or fans. Supports root cause analysis and system effectiveness evaluation.']
  activation_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the emergency activation. Tracks progression from initial activation through resolution or transfer to external authority. Serves as the LIFECYCLE_STATUS category for TRANSACTION_HEADER role. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: active|contained|resolved|cancelled|escalated|transferred — promote to reference product if additional states are required]']
  activation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time when the emergency response plan was formally activated. This is the principal real-world business event timestamp (BUSINESS_EVENT_TIMESTAMP category). Recorded in ISO 8601 format with timezone offset (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX).']
  after_action_report_reference varchar [note: 'Document reference number or URL linking to the formal After-Action Report (AAR) completed following this emergency activation. The AAR documents lessons learned, response effectiveness, and corrective actions. Required for OSHA, ISO 20121, and league governance compliance.']
  after_action_report_status varchar [note: 'Current completion status of the After-Action Report (AAR) for this emergency activation. Tracks compliance with mandatory post-incident review requirements.']
  all_clear_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the all-clear signal was officially issued, indicating the emergency has been resolved and normal operations may resume. Null if the activation has not yet been resolved. Used to calculate response duration.']
  broadcast_interrupted boolean [note: 'Indicates whether live broadcast operations (TV, OTT, radio) were interrupted or suspended as a result of this emergency activation. Relevant for media rights compliance, FCC reporting, and broadcast partner SLA management.']
  command_post_location varchar [note: 'Physical location description of the Incident Command Post (ICP) established for this emergency activation (e.g., \'Gate C Security Office\', \'Parking Lot B Mobile Command Unit\'). Critical for coordinating multi-agency response and documenting ICS compliance.']
  confirmed_fatalities_count varchar [note: 'Total number of confirmed fatalities recorded as a direct result of the emergency incident. Triggers mandatory OSHA fatality reporting within 8 hours, executive notification, and regulatory investigation protocols.']
  confirmed_injuries_count varchar [note: 'Total number of confirmed injuries (non-fatal) recorded as a direct result of the emergency incident. Used for OSHA 300 log reporting, insurance claims, and after-action analysis.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this emergency activation record was first created in the system. Serves as the RECORD_AUDIT_CREATED category for TRANSACTION_HEADER role. Supports data lineage and audit trail requirements.']
  drill_exercise boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this activation record represents a planned emergency drill or tabletop exercise rather than an actual emergency event. Allows filtering of drill records from operational incident analytics and regulatory reporting.']
  ems_notified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether Emergency Medical Services (EMS) was formally notified as part of this emergency activation. Mandatory documentation for mass casualty and medical emergency plan types.']
  estimated_occupancy_at_activation varchar [note: 'Estimated number of persons (fans, staff, athletes, media) present in the venue at the time of emergency activation. Critical for resource allocation, casualty ratio calculations, and post-event safety analysis. Serves as the QUANTITATIVE_RESULT category for this non-monetary TRANSACTION_HEADER.']
  evacuation_initiated boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a full or partial venue evacuation was initiated as part of this emergency activation. Drives crowd management protocol tracking and post-event safety audit requirements.']
  evacuation_type varchar [note: 'Specifies the scope of evacuation executed during this emergency activation: full venue, partial (specific sections), sector-specific (single zone), or none. Applicable only when evacuation_initiated is True.']
  event_cancelled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the Sports Entertainment event was fully cancelled (not resumed) as a result of this emergency activation. Triggers ticketing refund workflows, force majeure clauses, and insurance claim processes.']
  event_suspended boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the Sports Entertainment event (game, concert, match) was formally suspended or halted as a result of this emergency activation. Impacts ticketing refund obligations, broadcast rights, and league governance reporting.']
  false_alarm boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this emergency activation was subsequently determined to be a false alarm (no actual emergency existed). Critical for tracking false alarm rates, system calibration, and regulatory reporting to fire/police authorities.']
  fbi_notified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the Federal Bureau of Investigation (FBI) was formally notified as part of this emergency activation. Required for terrorism, active shooter, and bomb threat plan types per federal reporting obligations.']
  fire_department_notified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the fire department was formally notified as part of this emergency activation. Required for NFPA 101 and OSHA compliance documentation.']
  incident_commander_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the designated Incident Commander (IC) who assumed operational control of the emergency response under the Incident Command System (ICS). May differ from the activating authority if command was transferred.']
  incident_severity_level varchar [note: 'Standardized severity classification of the emergency incident at time of activation (Level 1 = minor/localized to Level 5 = catastrophic/mass casualty). Drives resource mobilization, agency notification requirements, and executive escalation protocols.']
  incident_trigger_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the specific incident, condition, or threat that triggered the emergency plan activation. Captures the initiating event details for after-action review and regulatory reporting.']
  insurance_claim_filed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether an insurance claim has been filed in connection with this emergency activation. Triggers financial reporting workflows and links to the finance domain for claim tracking.']
  lockdown_initiated boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a venue lockdown (perimeter secured, no entry/exit permitted) was initiated as part of this emergency activation. Critical for active shooter and terrorism threat plan types.']
  medical_transports_count varchar [note: 'Number of individuals transported by EMS or other medical transport to off-site medical facilities during or immediately following the emergency activation. Supports EMS coordination reporting and insurance documentation.']
  plan_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the type of emergency response plan activated. Determines the response protocol, agencies notified, and command structure engaged. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: evacuation|shelter_in_place|medical_emergency|fire|security_threat|natural_disaster|crowd_crush|bomb_threat|active_shooter — promote to reference product]']
  police_notified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether law enforcement (police) was formally notified as part of this emergency activation. Required for regulatory compliance documentation and after-action reporting.']
  regulatory_report_filed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a mandatory regulatory report (e.g., OSHA fatality/hospitalization report, FCC emergency alert report, local authority notification) has been filed for this emergency activation.']
  regulatory_report_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number or tracking identifier for the regulatory report filed with the applicable authority (OSHA, FCC, local emergency management). Populated only when regulatory_report_filed is True.']
  response_duration_minutes varchar [note: 'Total elapsed time in minutes from activation_timestamp to all_clear_timestamp. Captured as a stored business metric (not derived at query time) to support SLA compliance reporting and benchmarking against emergency response KPI targets.']
  shelter_in_place_initiated boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a shelter-in-place directive was issued to venue occupants as part of this emergency activation. Mutually exclusive with full evacuation in most scenarios.']
  threat_category varchar [note: 'Broad category of the threat or hazard that prompted the emergency activation. Used for trend analysis, risk modeling, and compliance reporting across venue and event portfolios. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: physical|medical|environmental|structural|cyber|crowd|weather|terrorism — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this emergency activation record was most recently modified. Serves as the RECORD_AUDIT_UPDATED category for TRANSACTION_HEADER role. Supports change tracking and Silver Layer data freshness monitoring.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of each emergency response plan activation at a Sports Entertainment venue or event. Captures activation timestamp, plan activated, activating authority, event reference, incident trigger description, command post location, agencies notified (police, fire, EMS, FBI), evacuation initiated flag, all-clear timestamp, after-action report reference, and casualties/injuries count. SSOT for emergency response execution history.'
}

Table security.plan_zone_designation {
  plan_zone_designation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for the plan_zone_designation association']
  access_zone_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking this zone designation record to the specific physical access zone being designated a role within the plan.']
  emergency_response_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking this zone designation record to the parent emergency response plan under which the zone role is defined.']
  activation_sequence_order varchar [note: 'Numeric sequence indicating the order in which this zone is activated or engaged during plan execution. Lower numbers indicate earlier activation. Enables responders to follow a structured zone-by-zone activation protocol during an incident.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which this specific zone designation became operationally active within the plan. Supports versioning of zone role assignments as plans are updated and zones are reconfigured.']
  evacuation_priority_level varchar [note: 'Numeric priority ranking (1 = highest priority) for evacuating this zone under this specific plan. Priority may differ across plans — a VIP hospitality zone may be priority 1 under an active shooter plan but priority 3 under a severe weather plan.']
  evacuation_route_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of primary and secondary evacuation routes for this plan, including ADA-accessible routes, assembly points, and crowd flow management corridors. Aligned with venue floor plan and ADA compliance requirements. [Moved from emergency_response_plan: This STRING field in emergency_response_plan is a denormalized narrative that attempts to capture zone-level evacuation routing. The proper model is to designate specific access_zone records with evacuation_priority_level and zone_role_in_plan in the plan_zone_designation association, replacing this free-text field with structured, queryable zone designations. The narrative description, if needed, can be retained as a notes field on the designation record.]']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which this zone designation expires or is superseded within the plan. Null indicates the designation remains active. Enables historical tracking of which zones served which roles under which plan versions.']
  shelter_in_place_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this access zone is designated as a shelter-in-place location under this specific emergency response plan. Belongs to the designation relationship because the same zone may be a shelter-in-place zone under a severe weather plan but an evacuation zone under a fire plan.']
  triage_zone_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this access zone is designated as a medical triage area under this specific emergency response plan. A zone may be a triage zone under an MCI plan but not under a fire evacuation plan — hence this flag belongs to the designation, not the zone master record.']
  zone_role_in_plan varchar [note: 'The specific operational role this access zone is designated to perform under this emergency response plan. A zone may serve as a triage area under one plan and an evacuation staging area under another. This attribute belongs to the designation relationship, not to either the plan or the zone alone.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the Role-based designation between emergency_response_plan and access_zone. It captures the specific operational role each physical access zone plays within a given emergency response plan — including triage staging, evacuation assembly, shelter-in-place, and command post assignments. Each record links one emergency_response_plan to one access_zone with attributes (zone role, activation sequence, triage/evacuation/shelter flags) that exist only in the context of this plan-zone pairing and cannot be stored on either entity alone.. Existence Justification: In sports venue emergency management, a single emergency response plan explicitly designates multiple access zones for specific operational roles — triage staging, evacuation assembly, shelter-in-place, command post. Conversely, a single access zone (e.g., the field level or a parking compound) can be designated under multiple emergency response plans (active shooter plan, MCI plan, severe weather plan) each assigning it a different role and activation sequence. The business actively manages these designations as a named operational concept called \'Plan Zone Designation\', and the relationship carries its own data (zone role, activation sequence, triage/evacuation flags) that belongs to neither the plan nor the zone alone.'
}
